The novelty lies in the combination of multi-scale supervision and hybrid CNN-Transformer modeling that allows for efficient localization and classification of the tumor even when voxel-level labels are scarce. Experimental assessments using multi-center datasets show superior performance when contrasted with classic fully supervised and weakly supervised techniques that highlight its clinical applicability.

title: ST-MSHT-Net: A Soft-Thresholding Multi-Stream Hybrid Temporal Network for Automatic Modulation Recognition
authors: Zhaoqi Cai, Yuan’An Liu, Dongming Yuan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: Automatic Modulation Recognition (AMR) is a key technology in cognitive radio and spectrum monitoring systems operating in complex electromagnetic environments. Although deep learning has achieved promising results, extracting discriminative features from high-dimensional signals and modeling long-term temporal dependencies under complex channel conditions remain challenging. To address these issues, this paper proposes a Soft-Thresholding Multi-Stream Hybrid Temporal Network (ST-MSHT-Net). The proposed framework adopts a six-stream multi-representation input mechanism, incorporating In-Phase/Quadrature (I/Q), Amplitude/Phase (A/P), and their two-dimensional coupled representations to enhance feature completeness. An adaptive soft-thresholding module with a learnable parameter is introduced to perform early-stage feature refinement by enforcing end-to-end sparsity constraints. Furthermore, a hybrid temporal architecture composed of LSTM-MHA-LSTM is designed to jointly capture local temporal dynamics and global contextual dependencies. Experiments conducted on the RadioML2016.10a benchmark dataset demonstrate that ST-MSHT-Net achieves a peak recognition accuracy of $\mathbf{9 3. 0 4 \%}$. Across the full SNR range from $-\mathbf{2 0 ~ d B}$ to 18 dB and the high SNR regime from 0 dB to 18 dB, the proposed model attains average accuracies of 62.6% and 91.9%, respectively, outperforming representative baseline methods.

title: Construction and Optimization of Image Recognition Fusion Model for Multi-Device Collaboration
authors: Yuqing Xie, Xiaoke Xu, Tao Zhang, Wenxue Cao
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: With the rapid development of edge computing and smart terminals, the image recognition task is gradually evolving from single-device processing to multi-device collaboration in order to adapt to the multiple requirements of real-time, accuracy and energy efficiency in complex environments. In this paper, we propose a fusion model construction and optimization method for multi-device collaboration scenarios, aiming to make full use of the collaborative capability among heterogeneous devices from multiple sources to achieve efficient division of labor and intelligent fusion of image recognition tasks. An image recognition fusion model based on collaborative architecture is constructed to support cross-device information integration at the feature and decision layers; secondly, the alignment and preprocessing mechanisms for multi-source image data are designed to improve the consistency and robustness of the fusion input; finally, comprehensive strategies such as model lightweighting, heterogeneous task scheduling, and energy-efficiency optimization are proposed to improve the overall system performance in response to resource constraints and real-time requirements. Experimental results show that the proposed method realizes the balance of recognition accuracy, inference speed and system energy consumption in multiple real-world scenarios, which verifies the potential and practical value of the fusion model in multi-device collaboration.

title: Semantic Resume Screening using Transformer Networks and Learning-to-Rank Models
authors: Aravinda Reddy Putluru, Priyanshu Arora, Ashokkumar C, Jothi Kumar C
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The exponential growth of digital recruitment platforms has resulted in organizations receiving a large volume of resumes for every job opening, making manual screening inefficient, inconsistent, and prone to bias. To address these challenges, this work presents the implementation of a transformer-based deep learning framework for automated resume shortlisting and ranking. The proposed system leverages advanced Natural Language Processing and Learning-to-Rank techniques to semantically match candidate resumes with job requirements and automatically shortlist the most relevant candidates. The framework integrates BERT-based Named Entity Recognition for robust resume parsing and structured information extraction, including skills, job roles, experience, and education. Sentence-BERT embeddings are employed to perform semantic skill and education matching, enabling context-aware similarity computation beyond traditional keyword-based approaches. A Transformer Cross-Encoder is used to evaluate role relevance by capturing fine-grained semantic interactions between candidate experience and target job roles. Education is enforced as a mandatory constraint, ensuring compliance with organizational hiring criteria. For final ranking, a RankNet-based Learning-to-Rank model is implemented to optimally order candidates based on skill relevance, experience, role alignment, and education suitability. The system uses a fixed, transparent weighting scheme to maintain interpretability and auditability. Performance evaluation using standard ranking metrics such as Precision@5, Recall@5, Mean Reciprocal Rank, and NDCG demonstrates that the proposed approach effectively prioritizes relevant candidates and aligns closely with human recruiter judgments. The implemented system significantly reduces recruitment screening time while improving ranking accuracy, fairness, and explainability, making it suitable for real-world deployment in intelligent recruitment systems.

title: MetaWeave: Interpretable Fusion of Text and Fraud Detection for E-commerce Review Systems
authors: N. Noor Alleema, Kandi Syam Vishnu Vardhan Reddy, Maramreddy Lohith Sai Reddy, S. Jagadeesh
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Online shopping is increasing, but many online reviews are fake, which misleads customers and damages businesses’ credibility. In this work, MetaWeave is described as the framework that has been developed for the more reliable detection of fake reviews. Instead of looking at text alone, as many older systems do, MetaWeave examines four types of clues: how the review is written, the time at which it was posted, how the reviewer and product are connected in the network, and also the behavior patterns of the reviewer. When these pieces are combined, the system gets a clearer picture whether a review is real or not. MetaWeave was tested on the OSF Fake Reviews dataset, containing 40,432 reviews across ten product groups. On the test split, MetaWeave reaches 95.0 percent ROC-AUC, an F1-score of 88.17 percent, and an accuracy of 88.20 percent. To avoid data leakage and to reflect real deployment conditions, a user-level data partitioning strategy is also introduced that ensures reviews from the same user do not appear in both training and testing sets. An interpretability study with SHAP and LIME is also provided to identify which linguistic, structural, and behavioral features influence the model’s predictions.

title: A Systematic Review of YOLO Architectures for Real-Time Object Detection
authors: Unnati Gayaki, Lakshmi Madireedy
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The You Only Look Once (YOLO) framework has redefined real-time object detection by introducing a single-stage architecture that balances exceptional speed with competitive accuracy, establishing it as a pivotal technology in computer vision. This systematic review traces the evolution of YOLO architectures from YOLOv1 to YOLOv11, highlighting key innovations such as anchor boxes, multi-scale predictions, and Transformer integration, which have driven performance improvements, achieving mean Average Precision (mAP) up to 59% on COCO and speeds up to 440 frames per second (FPS) for lightweight variants. YOLO's applications span autonomous vehicles, healthcare, industrial automation, surveillance, agriculture, and sports analytics. This review examines architectural advancements, performance metrics, challenges such as small object detection and computational constraints, and future directions toward adaptive and ethical AI, providing a comprehensive foundation for advancing real-time object detection.

title: The Transformer as a Parametric Filter for Sequence MIMO Equalization
authors: Kai Yu, Yulan Gao, Yijing Lin, Zhonghao Lyu, Jiwei Zhao, Ming Xiao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: In-context learning (ICL) has emerged as a promising paradigm for MIMO receiver design, enabling rapid adaptation to dynamic wireless environments via a single forward pass without the need for explicit channel state information estimation. However, the deployment of standard Transformerbased ICL receivers is constrained by the quadratic computational complexity of the softmax attention mechanism. This paper develops a computationally efficient ICL receiver based on linear attention and establishes an explicit analytical mapping between a single-layer linear attention and a context-dependent parametric filter. We derive a closed-form expression showing that the receiver performs an implicit channel estimation step through an empirical cross-correlation matrix computed from the pilot context, followed by a learned preconditioning applied to the data symbols. The proposed architecture achieves a persymbol inference complexity of $\mathcal{O}\left(d^{2}\right)$ after an initial $\mathcal{O}\left(N_{p} d^{2}\right)$ pilot-state construction, thereby eliminating the quadratic dependence on the sequence length L present in softmax attention. Simulation results indicate that the linear ICL receiver attains performance comparable to standard Transformers and practical LMMSE baselines while significantly reducing the computational overhead, making it a scalable alternative for MIMO receiver design.

title: Utilization of Meteorological Data in Site Diversity Technique for Optical Satellite Communications
authors: Mai Yoshikawa, Dimitar Kolev, Ikuko Yairi, Yuma Abe, Hiroyuki Tsuji
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: In order to establish stable communications in the vertical direction between the ground and satellites, it is important to reduce the effects of weather conditions such as clouds and rainfall on communication links. As a countermeasure technology, the utilization of site diversity techniques can be mentioned as a countermeasure to avoid the effects of clouds in satellite optical communications. In this study, we examine the results of a study on the effects of optical communication based on observation data on the ground surface and meteorological data obtained from a satellite. It was found that the correlation between various observation data obtained from the environmental data information collection equipment, cloud amount, and the possibility of optical communication is high.

title: Security Integration and Risk Management in Modern DevOps: Tools, Frameworks, and Emerging Trends
authors: Manjula Sanjay Koti, Ankit Kumar, J Rajeshwari, S. Satheesh Kumar, Khalid Anjum, Gautam S Languti
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: The emergence of DevOps has pushed teams to release updates more quickly than ever before, genuinely revolutionizing the speed of software delivery. Our study delves deeply into this important issue by examining how strong security measures, also known as DevSecOps, can be seamlessly integrated into DevOps procedures. We have combed through the most recent scholarly research to create a comprehensive picture of the frameworks, tools, and approaches that effectively integrate security into each stage of the software development process. Our analysis shows that there is an urgent need for automated security testing, cutting-edge risk models like the Preference Ranking Organization Method for Enrichment Evaluation (PROMETHEE), and cutting-edge solutions like blockchain to produce reliable audit trails. Future developments are probably going to concentrate on deeper governance integration, AIpowered threat intelligence, and the creation of standardized security maturity models for big businesses.

title: A Review of IoMT-based Sleep Apnea Monitoring Systems using Wearable Sensors
authors: S. Nandhini, Ms. Vinoth Kumar, K. Tharun Sriram, L. Saravanan
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Obstructive sleep apnea-hypopnea (OSAH) is prevalent sleep-related breathing disorder and is associated with a considerable effect on cardiovascular health and quality of life. Despite the acceptance of polysomnography (PSG) as a clinical gold standard for diagnosis, being expensive, complex to install and unable to provide long-term monitoring restrict its readiness for large-scale implementation. Recent advances in wearable sensors, embedded devices and Internet of Things (IoT) technologies made it possible to propose alternative strategies for the non-invasive and continuous sleep apnea monitoring. This paper, however is focusing on the survey for several wearable and IoT based sleep apnea monitoring systems available in literatures published recently. Furthermore, physiological sensor modalities like sensing ECG, blood O_2 saturation (SpO_2), respiratory effort and MEMS-based motion sensors are discussed in detail. Furthermore, system architectures and data processing approach as well as cloud-supported monitoring concept are presented and discussed. Critical challenges in Imaging Wireless Capsule Endoscopy: motion artifact, power consumption, data integrity and clinical problems are addressed.

title: Impact of Augmented Reality on Retail Shopping
authors: Keya Naskar, Pracheta Tejasmayee, Jagjeevan Kanaujiya, Shailesh Rastogi, Aman Pushp, Preeti Sharma
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Augmented Reality (AR) has become a revolutionary aspect in the world of retail, providing new methods of creating positive customer experiences and compelling tangible business results. AR can transform the customer-product and customer-brand interaction through the smooth integration of digital and real-life shopping setting. In the given paper, the author explores how AR has transformed the retail shopping experience, basing the discussion on the findings of the top retailers, i.e., IKEA, Sephora, Nike, L’Oréal, and Warby Parker. Based on the deep analysis of these companies, the results indicate that the key performance indicators, such as monthly sales, customer conversion rates, and general satisfaction levels have significantly improved after the adoption of AR. Indicatively, AR furniture placement tool by IKEA has assisted the firm to enable customers view products within their own homes, thus, decreasing the number of returns and enhancing buying confidence. Likewise, the Virtual Artist application available through Sephora allows customers to virtually apply makeup, which improves interaction with the product and makes the process of online shopping more convenient. The AR shoe sizing of Nike has increased the accuracy of the fit and consequently reduced the number of returns and increased customer loyalty. The virtual try-on tools created by L’Oréal have resulted in massive growth in online sales and the AR-powered glasses try-on app by Warby Parker has reinvented eyewear shopping behavior. Although AR presents unmatched opportunities, all are not well because high initial costs, technological infrastructure, and barrier to adoption continue. Nonetheless, with the ongoing development of AR technologies and their increased accessibility, it is impossible to deny their ability to transform the engagement with customers and change the face of the retail industry. The strategic importance of AR in the development of immersive, personalized, and interactive retail experiences is highlighted in this paper, which will help ensure the retail of the future is more dynamic and customer-focused.

title: Optimizing Coding Strategies for BB84-Based Quantum Key Distribution
authors: Shawn A. Prestridge, Dinesh Rajan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: Post-processing in BB84-based Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) must simultaneously ensure error correction, preserve secrecy, and satisfy strict resource constraints on embedded or edge devices. These competing requirements make the choice of coding strategy a system-level co-design problem involving both communication performance and decoder implementation cost. In this work, we present a comparative co-design framework that selects among LDPC, Polar, BCH, and Hamming codes based on real-time estimates of the quantum bit error rate (QBER), desired burn rate, and available decoding resources. We evaluate coding on the quantum channel and on the classical side-channel, quantifying their impact on residual BER, entropy loss, computational complexity, and secret-key rate. The resulting operational boundaries identify which codes are optimal in low-, moderate-, and high-error regimes. These boundaries provide a practical approach for implementing efficient BB84 reconciliation in embedded and edge QKD systems.

title: Daily Rainfall Forecasting using LSTM-MLP with Spatial Embeddings
authors: V Asha, Arpana Prasad, M T Vasumathi, Abhinav Reddy R, Abhishek G H, Avinash Manik Kamble
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Daily rainfall forecast is critical in agriculture, emergency rainfall analysis, and urban planning. However, rainfall forecast remains a challenge because of dynamic changes in rain-forecasting models. The article proposes a combination model with a blend of three advantages, including an LSTM model to predict latest trends in rain, an MLP model to examine present rain information, and a spatial embedding model to grasp rain information in different geographical sites. With the Weather AUS dataset, which contains information relevant to rainfall in different stations in Australia, the model performed very well with a 68.82% accuracy level and an ROC AUC of 0.77. The model performed well in discriminating rainy days from non-rainy days, which is important in early rainfall warning or forecast systems. The model had a stable training environment with a controlled validation loss during rainfall forecast. A combination of space, time, and table information in this model enables better rainfall forecast sites with a suitable model implementation strategy.

title: Optimizing System Design Performance and its Accuracy using Feature Importance
authors: Rangarajan Srinivasan, Raghuveeran Ramachandran, Pooja Ashok Bharde, Vivek Saravanan, Cedric Leclerc
abstract: With advancements in model accuracy and computational power, system simulation is increasingly integrated into development tools as a “virtual test bed” alongside experimental testing. However, virtual vehicle and powertrain thermal models still face challenges, particularly in ensuring accuracy across systems developed by various internal and external sources. These models, often built using different software platforms, are difficult to validate consistently, especially when integrated in a Co-simulation environment. This integration can degrade the overall accuracy of the Vehicle Simulation Platform, reducing the return on investment in model development. To address these limitations, this paper proposes the use of machine learning-based feature importance techniques at the vehicle-level simulation stage. Feature importance helps identify the most influential variables affecting system outputs. By focusing calibration and validation efforts on these key variables, the approach aims to improve correlation with physical test data across drive cycles, targeting an accuracy threshold within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{\pm 9 0 \%}$</tex>

title: Integration of Blockchain Technologies and Artificial Intelligence in Modern Education: Prospects for Digital Transformation of the Learning Process
authors: Obukhov Vadim Anatolevich, Qadamova Zulayho Erkinjon Qizi, Abdukadirova Mohizoda Ilxomidin Qizi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Science and Communications Technologies (ICISCT)
abstract: Education is going through a strange period. On the one hand, every schoolchild has a computer in their pocket that is more powerful than those that sent man to the moon. On the other hand, we are still learning in much the same way as our great-grandfathers did a hundred years ago. The same lectures for everyone, paper diplomas that can be forged, endless bureaucracy when applying to another university. Blockchain and artificial intelligence promise to change everything. The first technology can make educational documents completely reliable and instantly verifiable anywhere in the world. The second is capable of tailoring education to each individual student, taking into account their characteristics, pace of work, and preferences. We decided to find out how realistic these promises are. We analysed mathematical models, studied real projects at MIT, Arizona State University and Russian universities, and calculated the economics of implementation. The results are impressive: document verification time is reduced from weeks to minutes (a $99.7 \%$ improvement), student performance increases by $13 \%$, and projects pay for themselves in 3-5 years with a return on investment of up to $185 \%$.

title: Soil quality classification using deep learning
authors: Khaldi Ouadjih, Fouad Slaoui Hasnaoui, Semaan Georges
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: Ensuring soil quality is a major challenge in precision agriculture, particularly when decisions about irrigation and crop management depend on reliable and real-time information. Traditional physicochemical measurement methods are accurate but costly, slow, and unsuitable for continuous large-scale monitoring. Deep learning and computer vision now offer a promising alternative for rapid non-destructive assessment of soil moisture.This work proposes a lightweight real-time soilmoisture classifier based on an improved YOLO model trained to visually distinguish three soil states from RGB images: dry, moderately moist, and saturated. The model was trained on a custom, manually annotated Roboflow dataset including heterogeneous conditions (lighting, texture, soil composition, and shooting angles). The proposed YOLOv11 model reaches 95.5% accuracy, 98.4% recall, $\mathrm{mAP} \text{@} 50$ of 98.1%, and $\mathrm{mAP} \text{@} 50-95$ of $88.2 \%$, demonstrating competitive performance with existing work while ensuring real-time inference on resource-constrained devices. Despite promising results, limitations persist regarding geographic diversity and extreme lighting conditions. Future research will explore multispectral imaging, larger-scale datasets, and integration with embedded systems for autonomous irrigation control.

title: Commutation Failure Prediction for LCC-HVDC Based on CNN-LSTM-Attention Neural Network
authors: Jian Liu, Liyu Meng, Sheng Lin, Lei Liu, Jingbo Zhao, Zhengyou He
abstract: Commutation failure (CF) can cause DC current surges, impaired system transmission power and even leading to HVDC blocking. The prediction of CF combined with early control triggering is beneficial for avoiding CFs. However, current analyses of CF identification mainly focus on real-time detection or prediction based on limited factors, which are insufficient for reliable CF prediction. Therefore, a CF prediction method for the line-commutated converter high-voltage direct current (LCC HVDC) system based on CNN-LSTM-Attention is proposed. Firstly, key indicators that represent the commutation state are selected by analyzing the factors that influencing CF. Secondly, a CF prediction model is constructed based on CNN-LSTM Attention. Additionally, the input data sampling principle is clarified and the commutation state discrimination component based on DC current variation is built for the model training. On this basis, a method for predicting CF is proposed. Finally, the accuracy and efficiency of the proposed method is validated based on the CIGRE LCC-HVDC Benchmark system and the Bin-Jin HVDC system in China.

title: Industrial Processes Fault Diagnosis Method Based on Expert System-Guided Neural Network Decision-Space Sparsification
authors: Min Yin, Youqing Wang, Wei Yu, David Wilson, Xin Ma
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: Industrial fault diagnosis (FD) often faces challenges due to the scarcity of labeled data and the inability of rule-based systems to handle high-dimensional nonlinearities. This study proposes expert system-assisted neural networks (ES-Nets), a novel hybrid framework featuring ES-guided decision-space sparsification to bridge symbolic reasoning with neural networks (NNs). Unlike traditional data-driven models, this knowledge-preconditioned architecture embeds domain-specific logic into a comprehensive knowledge base before training. Specifically, the optimization process constrains the gradient descent trajectory to a knowledge-consistent subspace, effectively regularizing the parameter updates based on symbolic expert logic rather than purely on statistical gradients. Advanced embedding techniques pre-configure the model, enabling early operational performance and significantly reducing training requirements. Validation on the Tennessee Eastman (TE) process and a real-world petrochemical plant case demonstrates the framework’s superiority in accuracy and operational efficiency. This study provides an efficient and interpretable solution, facilitating effective human–machine collaboration in complex industrial environments.

title: Understanding the Effects of Projectors in Knowledge Distillation
authors: Yudong Chen, Sen Wang, Jiajun Liu, Xuwei Xu, Frank de Hoog, Brano Kusy, Zi Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Conventionally, during the knowledge distillation process (e.g., feature distillation), an additional projector is often required to perform feature transformation due to the dimension mismatch between the teacher and the student networks. Interestingly, we discovered that even if the student and the teacher have the same feature dimensions, adding a projector still helps to improve the distillation performance. In addition, projectors even improve logit distillation if we add them to the architecture too. Inspired by these surprising findings and the general lack of understanding of the projectors in the knowledge distillation process from existing literature, this paper investigates the implicit role that projectors play, but so far been overlooked. Our empirical study shows that the student with a projector (1) obtains a better trade-off between the training accuracy and the testing accuracy compared to the student without a projector when it has the same feature dimensions as the teacher, (2) better preserves its similarity to the teacher beyond shallow and numeric resemblance, from the view of Centered Kernel Alignment (CKA) [1], and (3) avoids being over-confident [2] as the teacher does at the testing phase. Motivated by the positive effects of projectors, we propose a projector ensemble-based feature distillation method to further improve distillation performance. Despite the simplicity of the proposed strategy, empirical results from the evaluation of classification tasks on benchmark datasets demonstrate the superior classification performance of our method on a broad range of teacher-student pairs and verify, from the aspects of CKA and model calibration that the student's features are of improved quality with the projector ensemble design. Code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/chenyd7/PEFD</uri>.

title: ADA-Net: Avalanche Differential Attention Network with Covariance Correlation Differential Transformer for Multimodal Snow Avalanche Susceptibility Mapping_supp1-3676659.docx
authors: Yuan-Jun Jiang
abstract: Multimodal data integration plays a critical role in accurately mapping snow avalanche susceptibility, yet existing models struggle to capture the complex nonlinear interactions among optical imagery, SAR backscatter, snowpack measurements, and meteorological variables. Traditional statistical and shallow machine learning (ML) approaches often rely on handcrafted features and linear assumptions, while recent deep learning (DL) methods employ simplistic fusion strategies that overlook higher-order dependencies and cross-modal discrepancies. To address these limitations, this study introduces a novel multimodal DL framework combining a Covariance-Correlation Differential Transformer (CCDT) for second-order feature extraction with an Avalanche Differential Attention Network (ADA-Net) for targeted cross-modal fusion. CCDT constructs differential covariance correlation matrices to reveal subtle interdependencies across 17 topographic, snowpack, and meteorological inputs, and ADA-Net’s Siamese transformer architecture amplifies critical discrepancies between modalities. Evaluated on a curated dataset of 150 avalanche events along China’s G217 and G218 highways in the Central Tianshan Mountains, our CCDT-ADA-Net framework achieves superior predictive performance (ROC-AUC = 0.9903, F1 = 0.96) and spatial accuracy, successfully classifying 97.4% of historical avalanche release areas within its high-susceptibility zone. It significantly outperforms benchmark models, including Vision Transformer, ConvLSTM, RNN, HybridMLP, and TabNet. Permutation importance analysis confirms snow density, elevation, and snow depth as dominant risk factors. This scalable, interpretable approach offers practical utility for operational avalanche forecasting and infrastructure protection in high-mountain regions.

title: Robust and Accurate LiDAR SLAM in Complex Environments via Intensity-Enhanced Features
authors: Yuqing Chen, Shuoshuo Huang, Yanyang Wang, Huosheng Hu
abstract: LiDAR-based Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) is a critical component in robotics and autonomous driving. Traditional SLAM systems relying solely on geometric features often struggle in environments with sparse or repetitive structures. To overcome these limitations, we propose a novel LiDAR SLAM framework that integrates intensity features with a dynamic weighting strategy to enhance both robustness and accuracy. Key contributions include an intensity-based curvature metric for feature extraction, an adaptive fusion scheme that combines geometric and intensity cues, and a Height-Intensity-Density Scan Context (HIDSC) descriptor for improved loop closure detection. Additionally, a factor graph-based optimization approach is employed to jointly optimize multimodal data. Experiments on the KITTI odometry benchmark, the TIERS dataset, and our custom campus dataset show that our method consistently achieves lower trajectory errors and more stable performance than representative baselines, including FAST-LIO2, LIO-SAM, Point-LIO, IGE-LIO, and Intensity-SLAM.

title: Smartphone Applications in Environmental, Biological, and Pharmaceutical Colorimetric Analysis: A Review
authors: Rimadani Pratiwi, Fadia Fatiha, Primadini Ayesha Nugraha, Ifa Latifah
abstract: Rapid technological advancements have brought about a revolution in various fields, including chemical analysis. The features of smartphones, including high-resolution cameras, reliable processing capacity, and robust connectivity, have repurposed them into an effective analytical platform. This development enables the utilization of smartphone components to perform colorimetric measurements on-site, in real-time, and at a low cost. Techniques such as Digital Image Colorimetry (DIC) leverage the smartphone camera to measure the color intensity of a chemical reaction and convert it into quantitative data utilizing the RGB (Red, Green, Blue) model, alongside other color model such as CMYK (Cyan, Magenta, Yellow, Black), and HSV (Hue, Saturation, Value). This review examines the progress and recent methodological developments in the field of smartphone-based colorimetric analysis, covering the environmental monitoring, biological samples, and pharmaceutical drugs. The study investigates the influence of smartphone camera specifications on the accuracy and sensitivity of the analytical results, while also discussing its inherent advantages and limitations.

title: A Tri-Band Frequency Selective Surface based on Multilayer Concentric Slot Geometries
authors: Yael Jasso-Martinez, Claudia E. Perez-Martinez, Rosalba Martinez-Lopez, Jose I. Martinez-Lopez, Lourdes Martinez-Lopez
abstract: This paper presents a frequency selective surface (FSS) composed of three cascaded layers, each consisting of unit cells with distinct concentric slot geometries. The concentric geometries corresponding of a square, an octagon, and a circular ring are designed to generate three passbands at the -3 dB level, centered at 6.10 GHz, 8.88 GHz, and 11.8 GHz, with corresponding fractional bandwidths of 29.3%, 8.11%, and 11.9%. This makes the structure suitable for applications in the C and X frequency bands. The proposed configuration exploits the progressive reduction of perimeter among the square, octagon, and circular geometries to achieve multiple resonances within a compact concentric structure, while the progressive separation between the resonant paths helps to reduce mutual coupling and improve frequency selectivity. The design accounts for the mutual coupling introduced by the close spacing between the concentric geometries. An equivalent circuit model is developed to describe the behavior of the FSS, comprising three cascaded sections connected through transmission lines. Each section contains three nested RLC resonant circuits with mutual inductive coupling, representing the interaction and combined response of the three geometries. To validate the simulated results, a prototype was fabricated and tested. Each layer contains 121 unit cells arranged in an 11 × 11 array. The experimental results show good agreement with both the full-wave simulations and the equivalent circuit model. The FSS demonstrates good angular stability for oblique incidence, maintaining good performance for elevation angles up to 65°.

title: Information-Theoretic Bounds on Transmission Rates for Integrated Communication and Navigation Systems
authors: Jiyang Liu, Xiaomei Tang, Feixue Wang, Xin Chen, Wenbo Xu
abstract: The emergence of 6G space-air-ground integrated networks imposes stringent requirements on resource utilization and spectral efficiency, positioning integrated communication and navigation (ICAN) as a promising enabling technology. However, existing ICAN architectures lack a unified theoretical foundation. Communication is typically characterized by the Shannon capacity, whereas navigation is evaluated via the Cramér-Rao lower bound. As a result, the two functions are still treated independently, leading to suboptimal system design and resource allocation. To bridge this gap, this paper proposes a unified framework that quantifies positioning performance through a positioning information rate (PIR) derived from differential entropy and the Fisher information matrix. This formulation enables ICAN performance to be assessed in a common information-theoretic domain. Under this paradigm, we conduct a comparative study of representative ICAN schemes and develop a corresponding resource allocation optimization framework. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed PIR metric is consistent with conventional metrics while accurately capturing system performance under a unified one-dimensional metric, thereby facilitating direct and simultaneous optimization of communication and navigation functions without separate performance evaluations.
