title: A Comprehensive Study on GDPR-Oriented Analysis of Privacy Policies: Taxonomy, Corpus and GDPR Concept Classifiers
authors: Peng Tang, Xin Li, Yuxin Chen, Weidong Qiu, Haochen Mei, Allison Holmes, Fenghua Li, Shujun Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Machine learning (ML) based classifiers that take a privacy policy as the input and predict relevant concepts are useful in different applications such as (semi-)automated compliance analysis against requirements of a specific data protection law such as the EU GDPR. Although many researchers have studied ML-based privacy policy concept classifiers, we observed multiple research gaps, e.g., the lack of a more complete GDPR taxonomy and the less consideration of hierarchical information in privacy policies. To fill such research gaps, we produced a more complete GDPR-oriented privacy policy concept taxonomy, constructed the first privacy policy corpus with explicitly hierarchical information at three levels, and conducted the most comprehensive performance evaluation study of GDPR concept classifiers for privacy policies, cover many aspects that have not been studied systematically. Our work led to multiple findings and insights, including the usefulness of considering hierarchical contextual features and different hierarchical structures, the observation that a “one size fits all” approach may not work, the reduced performance of such classifiers on our newly constructed corpus especially after the first level, and the necessity to split the training and testing sets by documents.

title: Cover Page
authors: N/A

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)

title: Triptych AI - Innovation - Cybersecurity: Challenges and Opportunities for Financial Services: The Case of Moroccan Banks
authors: Hammani Safae, El Bayane Adnane, El Mouaffaq Fadoua
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: This “The future of banking belongs to those who can integrate technology without losing customer trust” (Gomber, Koch & Siering, 2017). This statement illustrates the current dynamics of financial services, where artificial intelligence (AI), innovation, and cybersecurity constitute an essential strategic triad.This raises a key question: how can the integration of artificial intelligence and innovation improve the performance and competitiveness of banking financial services while maintaining cybersecurity? This question refers not only to the search for a balance between efficiency and security, but also to the need for a sustainable development model for banking services in a digitalized context. In the case of Morocco, this topic is of particular interest. The Moroccan banking sector, one of the most developed in Africa (World Bank, 2021), is increasingly investing in digitalization and fintech solutions. Major banks such as Attijariwafa Bank, Bank of Africa, and Crédit Agricole du Maroc have initiated major digital transformation programs, integrating AI into customer relations, fraud detection, and credit scoring.

title: FOP: A Personalized Federated Learning Framework for CLIP Integrating LoRA and Differential Privacy
authors: Zepeng Li
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: To address the problems faced by cross-modal pre-trained models such as CLIP in federated learning scenarios—high computational overhead of full-parameter fine-tuning, high risk of privacy leakage, and insufficient personalized adaptation caused by data heterogeneity—this study proposes a personalized federated learning method (FOP) that integrates low-rank adaptation (LoRA), differential privacy (DP), and global-local optimized interpolation. The FOP framework adopts a dual-prompt mechanism and dynamically balances global consensus and local features through the interpolation coefficient λ. It uses LoRA to reduce the dimensionality of prompt parameters, combines the Gaussian mechanism with gradient L2 norm clipping, and achieves privacy protection. Experiments conducted on heterogeneous datasets verify that the FOP framework outperforms existing methods such as CoOp and FedOTP in all scenarios, fully demonstrating its effectiveness in the privacy-utility trade-off and personalized adaptation.

title: An Image Recognition Method Based on Multi-Scale Wavelet Transform Convolution and Convolutional Block Attention
authors: Yuhan Zhu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: With the rapid development of digital media and artificial intelligence, intelligent image recognition has become a key link between the physical and digital worlds. Although convolutional neural networks (CNNs) have achieved strong performance in visual tasks, they still struggle with high-frequency detail loss, background interference, and hyperparameter sensitivity in fine-grained and complex-scene classification. To address these limitations, this paper proposes MSWT- CBAMFusionNet, a hybrid framework that combines multi-scale wavelet transform convolution with channel-spatial attention. The MSWT module jointly enhances fine-grained texture information in the spatial and frequency domains, while the CBAM-enhanced ResNet-50 backbone adaptively recalibrates features to suppress background noise. Furthermore, an improved Whale Optimization Algorithm (IWOA) is introduced to automatically tune key hyperparameters. Experiments on the CUB-200-2011 and Rice Leaf Disease datasets demonstrate that the proposed model achieves superior fine-grained feature extraction, stronger robustness in complex environments, and overall improved classification accuracy. Ablation studies further validate the effectiveness of each core component.

title: A Forged Speech Detection Method Based on a Hybrid Attention Mechanism
authors: Jing Yin, Peidong Luo
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: To address the security threats posed by deepfake audio and the limited generalization capabilities of existing detection methods, this paper proposes a synthetic speech detection approach incorporating a hybrid attention mechanism. This approach constructs a dual-branch network architecture. The front-end processes LFCC features and CQT spectrograms, while the back-end employs a ResNet18 backbone with an embedded CBAM module. Additionally, an Involution operator is introduced to adaptively capture feature correlations across multiple positions and scales. Combined with a Cross-Attention mechanism, this enables deep interaction and fusion of dualbranch features. Experimental results on the ASVspoof 2019 LA and ASVspoof 2021 LA datasets demonstrate that compared to baseline models, the proposed method achieves significant reductions in both the Equal Error Rate (EER) and the Minimum Tandem Detection Cost Function (min t-DCF) metrics. This effectively validates the feasibility and superiority of the proposed approach.

title: A Fast Analytical Method for No-Load Air Gap Flux Density and Voltage Estimation in Large Synchronous Generators
authors: Farshad Kiani, Hamed Tahanian
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: A rapid and efficient analytical approach is presented in this paper for the estimation of air-gap flux density and no-load induced voltage in large cylindrical-rotor synchronous generators. The approach begins by assuming a linear magnetic circuit and neglecting the effects of rotor and stator slotting. These factors are then progressively incorporated to improve model accuracy, including the influence of slotting and magnetic saturation on the air gap flux waveform. Unlike the Finite Element Method (FEM), the proposed analytical method offers significantly reduced computation time while maintaining acceptable accuracy. Comparative studies with FEM results show a low deviation in air gap flux density and RMS voltage estimation.

title: Alzheimer’s Disease Prediction from MRI Images using a Hybrid CNN–SVM Model
authors: K. Maheswari, K. Shilpa, Bayyaram Bhargavi, Bandi Bharath Raj, Badavath Sai Charan, Punyaban Patel
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Alzheimer’s disease is an aggressive, progressively worsening condition that results in the loss of memory and intellectual functions. Henceforth, prompt recognition of Alzheimer’s disease is going to play an important role in making sure that the patient receives adequate treatment. Brain Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) is a noninvasive method typically used to illustrate the presence of anatomical alterations associated with the disease in individuals. Nonetheless, interpreting the images remains a resource-intensive and subjective task that heavily relies on the evaluations of the experts involved. The swiftly growing amount of imaging data produced in healthcare has enabled the development of automated and intelligent diagnostic systems that are progressively being embraced in the quest for more precise and reliable disease diagnosis. This study presents a hybrid approach for forecasting Alzheimer's disease through the use of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) alongside Support Vector Machines (SVM). The CNN model's role is to independently identify deep features from MRI images, while the SVM classifier makes the final decision based on the features obtained. The contemporary hybrid approach combines deep learning for feature extraction with a robust machine-learning classifier, leading to improved prediction accuracy; nevertheless, it maintains the same level of reliability, making it a dependable resource for decision-making in the early diagnosis of Alzheimer’s disease

title: An AI-Driven Fusion Approach for Multilingual Retrieval via Weighted Greedy Subset Selection
authors: Rui Wang, Bin Zeng, Jian Kang, Hui Shu Yang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: With the rapid expansion of multilingual digital resources, multilingual information retrieval (MLIR) has gained significant importance in information science. While fusing results from multiple retrieval systems can enhance MLIR performance, integrating too many systems leads to computational inefficiency and information redundancy, challenging the balance between effectiveness and cost. To address this, we propose an AI-driven Multilingual Retrieval Fusion (AI-MLRF) method, which incorporates advanced natural language processing techniques and a weighted greedy subset selection strategy to optimize the fusion process. AI-MLRF consists of four core modules: semantic performance evaluation using cross-lingual pre-trained models, diversity measurement based on contrastive learning, weighted greedy subset selection, and attention-based result fusion. Experimental evaluations on benchmark datasets demonstrate that AI-MLRF outperforms state-of-the-art fusion methods, showing substantial improvements in retrieval accuracy and adaptability to multilingual scenarios. The method provides a transparent and efficient solution for multilingual retrieval result fusion, with potential applications in cross-border collaboration and academic research.

title: Research on Crop Pest Recognition Based on Improved YOLOv8
authors: Xiaofang Wu, JingChen Wu, ShiYu Zhang, YingLi Wen
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of numerous types, complex information of crop pests and low efficiency of traditional manual monitoring methods, this paper proposes an improved crop pest recognition method based on YOLOv8. Three optimization strategies are adopted: modifying the input size, adding the SE (Squeeze-and-Excitation) attention mechanism to the detection head, and adding the ECA (Efficient Channel Attention) attention mechanism to the backbone network. A dataset containing 2,470 images of ten common crop pests (including mole cricket, sugar beet weevil, fall armyworm) is constructed for model training and validation. Experimental results show that the improved model achieves significant performance improvement: the precision of the original YOLOv8 model is 80%, while the precisions of the models optimized by the three strategies reach $82 \%, 92 \%$ and 83.5% respectively. The model integrated with the SE attention mechanism exhibits the best performance, with fast inference speed and strong robustness, providing an effective technical solution for rapid and accurate identification of crop pests.

title: Energy-Aware Routing Protocol for Wireless Sensor Networks using Reinforced Sparrow Search Optimization (RSSO)
authors: Ashokkumar Muniraj, Aarthi.V.P.M.B., S.Ramya, D.Sharmiladevi, P.Gomathi, N.Mahendran
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) are severely constrained by limited battery energy, making energy-efficient routing a critical factor in extending network lifetime and ensuring reliable data delivery. This paper proposes a novel energy-aware routing protocol based on Reinforced Sparrow Search Optimization (RSSO), which enhances the conventional Sparrow Search Algorithm by integrating reinforcement-based adaptive learning to dynamically balance exploration and exploitation. RSSO optimizes both cluster head selection and multi-hop routing by jointly considering residual energy, communication cost, and transmission distance, thereby achieving balanced energy consumption and reduced routing overhead. The reinforcement mechanism enables continuous adaptation of control parameters in response to network conditions, preventing premature convergence and uneven energy depletion. Extensive MATLAB-based simulations were conducted under identical stopping criteria and repeated runs to ensure statistical reliability. Performance evaluation against HHO, EWOA, and HGW-SSO demonstrates that RSSO significantly improves network lifetime, packet delivery ratio, energy efficiency, and throughput, while exhibiting lower variance and tighter confidence intervals across multiple runs. The results confirm that RSSO provides a scalable, statistically robust, and adaptive routing solution suitable for both homogeneous and heterogeneous WSN deployments.

title: Internet of Things Applications in Classroom Environments: A Bibliometric and Thematic Analysis
authors: Mark Anthony A. Castro, John Paul P. Miranda, Christian N. Pineda, Dorothy Joy M. Tongol, Nicholas D. Alonzo, Aaron John C. Alegre
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: This study analyzes the research landscape on IoT applications in classroom environments through bibliometric and text-mining techniques. The dataset contains 919 documents published from 2011 to 2026 and shows a steady rise in scientific output, strong co-authorship, and broad participation across 256 sources and 2,838 authors, which indicates growing scholarly engagement with IoT-enabled learning spaces. The analysis identifies the most productive authors, institutions, and countries and highlights influential works that shaped the field through contributions to environmental sensing, face recognition, adaptive instruction, occupancy analytics, and digital learning systems. Annual publication trends show rapid growth from 2016 to 2024, with prominent activity in engineering, computing, and education venues that support research on smart classrooms, sensor networks, and AI-supported instruction. Furthermore, the text-mining analysis uses Latent Dirichlet Allocation and reveals three dominant themes: IoT-enabled classroom operations, smart learning environments and educational applications, and intelligent educational technologies that support data-driven teaching.

title: Structure-Aware Decoding Mechanisms for Complex Entity Extraction with Large-Scale Language Models
authors: Zhimin Qiu, Di Wu, Feng Liu, Yuxiao Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: This paper proposes a structure-aware decoding method based on large language models to address the difficulty of traditional approaches in maintaining both semantic integrity and structural consistency in nested and overlapping entity extraction tasks. The method introduces a candidate span generation mechanism and structured attention modeling to achieve unified modeling of entity boundaries, hierarchical relationships, and cross-dependencies. The model first uses a pretrained language model to obtain context-aware semantic representations, then captures multi-granular entity span features through candidate representation combinations, and introduces hierarchical structural constraints during decoding to ensure consistency between semantics and structure. To enhance stability in complex scenarios, the model jointly optimizes classification loss and structural consistency loss, maintaining high recognition accuracy under multi-entity co-occurrence and long-sentence dependency conditions. Experiments conducted on the ACE 2005 dataset demonstrate significant improvements in Accuracy, Precision, Recall, and F1-Score, particularly in nested and overlapping entity recognition, where the model shows stronger boundary localization and structural modeling capability. This study verifies the effectiveness of structure-aware decoding in complex semantic extraction tasks, provides a new perspective for developing language models with hierarchical understanding, and establishes a methodological foundation for high-precision information extraction.

title: Improved Dung Beetle Algorithm for Odor Source Localization using Fuzzy Inference
authors: Kumar Gaurav
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: This paper presents an improved Dung beetle algorithm (IDBA) for odor source localization (OSL) using a fuzzy inference method. The algorithm was evaluated in a simulation platform using laminar flow. The simulated mobile robot was used to perform experiments. The IDBA was compared with the Dung beetle algorithm (DBA) and found to be efficient in the given conditions. The overall distance travelled by the mobile agent was reduced by 6% to 35%, which highlights the efficiency of the algorithm. Moreover, the offset angle obtained from the proposed algorithm is also smaller compared to the conventional approach.

title: Data-Driven Prediction of Average Torque, Phase Resistance, and Coil Turns in 6/4 Switched Reluctance Motors Using ANN and EMDLAB
authors: Nasrin Majlesi, Ali Jamali-Fard, Tohid Sharifi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: In this paper, a data-driven approach based on an artificial neural network (ANN) with high accuracy was developed to predict the average torque, phase resistance, and number of coil turns of a switched reluctance motor (SRM). An initial 6/4 SRM model was designed in ANSYS Maxwell, and to reduce the computational cost of finite-element analysis, the open-source EMDLAB software was used to generate the data required for the ANN. A total of 1,000 SRM samples with variations in stator pole arc angle, rotor pole arc angle, and stator inner diameter were designed and simulated in EMDLAB. These three geometric parameters were used as the input data to the ANN so that it could simultaneously predict the three important outputs, including average torque, phase resistance, and number of coil turns. Evaluation of the model on the test data demonstrates the model’s ability to generalize to new data. The trained network achieves a coefficient of determination (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) close to one for all outputs, and the residual error is very small. This surrogate model significantly reduces the time required for multi-objective optimization. The results show that the proposed model is a reliable substitute for time-consuming simulations and can be effectively used in the design and optimization processes of SRM machines.

title: Optimization and Enhancing the Torque Profile of a Novel Double-Layer Radial-Flux Magnetic Gear Using a DOE Method
authors: Mohammad Mardani, Mahdi Abolghasemi, Aghil Ghaheri, Seyyed Ebrahim Afjei
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: The growing demand for high-efficiency and low-maintenance power transmission systems has accelerated interest in magnetic gearing technologies. Compared with conventional mechanical gears, magnetic gears offer silent operation, improved reliability, and inherent overload protection, making them highly attractive for modern transportation and renewable energy applications. This paper presents the design, analysis, and optimization of a novel Double-Layer Radial-Flux Magnetic Gear (DLRFMG) aimed at improving torque density and reducing torque ripple. The proposed structure employs two torque conversion layers to achieve a higher gear ratio and torque transmission capability. To optimize the performance, the Taguchi method was applied with an L27 orthogonal array, enabling multi-objective optimization of maximum applicable torque and torque ripple across all rotors with minimal experiments count. The results demonstrate significant improvements, with maximum torque enhanced by up to 72.45% and torque ripple reduced by more than 60% compared with the initial design. Analysis of variance (ANOVA) further identified the dominant influence with their corresponding percentages of the most effective control factors. The optimized DLRFMG exhibits superior torque characteristics, making it a promising solution for applications such as electric bicycles, transportation systems, and renewable energy devices.

title: Ground-to-GEO Bidirectional Optical Links between TELEO and various OGS: In Orbit Test Result, Experimental Comparison with Numerical Models for power budget and telecom link margins
authors: Sylvain Poulenard, Alvaro Jose Pascual-Gracia, Jean Frédéric Chouteau, Thibault Marduel, Frédéric Lacoste, Géraldine Artaud, Julien Sommer, Bouchra Benammar, Thierry Lanz, Etienne Samain, Loïc Eymar, Baptiste Jans, Alain Thomas, Nicolas Védrenne, Aurélie Montmerle-Bonnefois, Cyril Petit
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: TELEO is the Airbus Defence and Space demonstrator dedicated to in-orbit testing of cross atmospheric optical uplink and downlink, designed and implemented in the frame of the CNES DYSCO project. The payload, equipped with high-frequency irradiance metrology, offers the possibility of fine characterization of uplink irradiance statistical properties in the presence of adaptive optics pre-compensation. Connected to optical ground station with high-frequency metrology of irradiance and injected power, the overall system is unique to consolidate know-how on optical link budget. This work presents In-Orbit Demonstration (IOD) results and then focuses on a comparison between Airbus OneWave software output with IOD data from FrOGS beacon, from FEELINGS pre-compensated uplink, from FEELINGS corrected downlink.

title: A Lightweight and Example-Free Class-Agnostic Counting Network
authors: Hao Li, Jianhui Guo, Weiyu Sun, Yabo Zhao
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: In current class-agnostic counting research, models commonly suffer from reliance on external exemplars and high computational complexity, which limit their application in resource-constrained scenarios such as edge computing. Therefore, exploring lightweight counting methods that require no exemplar priors and enable efficient inference has become a key direction for promoting technological deployment. Toward addressing the limitations of existing class-agnostic counting methods in external exemplar dependency and model complexity, we propose LECNet, a lightweight and example-free class-agnostic counting network. The network adopts an encoder-decoder architecture, using MobileViTv2-S as the backbone encoder to extract multi-level features with local details and global context. Building upon this architecture, a pseudo-exemplar generation module integrating dual channel-spatial focus attention and adaptive pooling is designed, which autonomously extracts representative visual features from a single input image without relying on manually annotated exemplars. To enhance the semantic relevance between backbone features and pseudo-exemplar features, this paper introduces a bidirectional attention fusion module to improve the model's ability to discriminate and localize target regions. The decoder employs a lightweight design to progressively reconstruct high-resolution density maps, achieving end-to-end precise counting. Experiments on the class-agnostic FSC-147 dataset demonstrate that LECNet maintains competitive counting accuracy while substantially reducing both parameter count and computational overhead. With only 5.4M parameters and 2.8G FLOPs, it achieves an MAE of 18.26, striking an excellent balance between accuracy and efficiency. https://drive.google.com/file/d/1ymDYrGs9DSRicfZbSCDiOu0ikGDh5k6S/view?usp=sharing provides access to the FSC-147 dataset.

title: Supervised Machine Learning for Detecting Unreliable Online Reviews
authors: UndraLeela Gayathri, N. Sreenivasa Rao, Segu Mounika, JuturuBhavya Sri, Dasari Poojitha, M. Surya Bhupal Rao
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Product ratings on the internet significantly influence the buying behavior of consumers, yet the increasing number of fake or deceptive reviews is still a major problem for both consumers and retailers. This study proposes a machine-learning pipeline that is supervised and automated for the classification of the reviews into genuine and false based on both the textual and linguistic features. The whole process started with the pre-processing of a total of 40,432 product reviews that had undergone text cleaning, normalization, and removal of noise, and then each review was assigned a binary credibility label. The TF-IDF vectorization was used for cleaning the text and, with a total of 5,000 features, the numerical representation was achieved. Logistic Regression and Random Forest classifiers were trained and their performance was assessed in terms of accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score metrics. The models that have been trained can now be applied to predict the real-time credibility of reviews thus helping the platform gain more trust and consumers in their decision-making.

title: Design of Waist Exoskeleton Based on STM32
authors: Pengfei Jiang, Jinliang Yang
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Handling process is an indispensable part of the production process. To reduce the physical burden on manual handlers, this paper designs a waist exoskeleton system with STM32 as its core. The system determines the current human movement state using a 6-axis attitude sensor module, combined with angular velocity from the motor encoder and information on motor rotation angle. Leveraging the real-time motor movement angle and angular velocity values obtained by the encoder, it controls the motor to output time-varying power, thereby assisting the wearer in their movements. Through bending load experiments and walking load experiments, it is proven that the system can effectively analyze and process the feedback information from the sensors and motor encoder, and can control the motor to assist the human body in bending and walking movements.

title: Adaptive Masking DWI Denoising Algorithm Guided by b0 Images
authors: Huarui Wang, Linhai Wu, Zeyu Deng, Lihui Wang, Yueming Zhu
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Denoising diffusion-weighted imaging (DWI) is crucial for early disease diagnosis, yet existing self-supervised methods often over-smooth structures, are affected by spatially correlated noise, and depend heavily on the number of diffusion directions, resulting in degraded performance under sparse sampling. We propose a b0-guided self-supervised DWI denoising method that integrates double-shift region partitioning and region-adaptive masking. A texture-measurement matrix is first computed from the b<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</inf> image using double-shift region partitioning, enabling robust separation of flat and textured voxels while suppressing uncertain regions. Then, region-adaptive masking assigns different mask ratios to different region types, balancing noise decorrelation and structure/texture preservation. Unlike approaches relying on angular redundancy, our masking paradigm exploits spatial redundancy, reducing sensitivity to diffusion-direction count and improving directional robustness. Across three noise models (Gaussian, Rician, Nc-Chi) and multiple direction settings in both simulation and ablation studies, the proposed method consistently achieves superior PSNR, SSIM, and RMSE, and experiments on real datasets further validate its effectiveness and robustness.

title: Research on Accurate Traceability and Prediction Method of Power Failure Events of Smart Meters Based on Spatio-Temporal Big Data
authors: Xuan Kong, Rundan Zhang, Leiyang Zhao, He Cui, Yunze Li
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: Aiming at the challenge of extreme weather and new energy access to the traditional power grid fault handling mode, this paper proposes an accurate traceability and prediction method of power outage events of smart meters based on spatio-temporal big data. A physical constraint-spatio-temporal fusion-dynamic prediction framework (PSTF-Pred) is constructed. Firstly, the power grid structure is transformed into a graph neural network calculation model at the physical constraint level, and the weighted adjacency matrix is obtained through the electrical distance correction to reflect the real physical characteristics of the power grid topology. Then, in the spatio-temporal fusion layer, Spatio-Temporal Graph Attention Network (ST-GAT) is used to combine the power grid topology and time series data, and the time characteristics are extracted by the Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), and the importance relationship between nodes is dynamically learned through the spatial attention mechanism, thus effectively solving the problem of spatial over-smoothness. Finally, the dynamic prediction layer uses the fractal multi-scale model to quantify the fault propagation characteristics, and evaluates the long-range dependence by calculating the Hurst index of voltage series. Combined with short-term, medium-term and long-term prediction mechanisms, LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) time series prediction, weather data fusion and survival analysis based on fractal parameters are used to predict the power grid fault risk respectively. The experiment is based on the data of 120,000 smart meters collected by a provincial power grid from 2021 to 2023. The results show that PSTF-Pred is significantly superior to the comparison method in three indicators: average positioning error, typhoon scene error and the ratio of error exceeding 2 km, and is also significantly lower than the comparison method in the mean absolute error (MASE) of multi-scale prediction, and it is more robust in extreme scenarios.

title: Efficient and Precise Cheat Detection with Deep Learning
authors: Ilhame Khabbachi, Ghalia Mdaghri-Alaoui, Abdelhamid Zouhair
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: The digital transformation of education has accelerated significantly, driven in large part by the global health crisis of 2020. While this shift to online learning has opened new pedagogical opportunities, it has also raised pressing concerns about the authenticity of remote assessments. Educational institutions worldwide now face a key challenge: ensuring the fairness and credibility of examinations in fully digital environments—a challenge that grows more urgent as online education becomes a permanent fixture. In response, this research introduces an innovative, AI-powered solution designed to detect non-compliant behaviors during online assessments. The proposed system integrates real-time video monitoring with automated behavioral analysis to identify and deter academic misconduct. Using a student’s standard computer webcam, continuous video streams are captured and analyzed through advanced deep learning algorithms, combining object detection, classification models, and tailored data preprocessing to maximize detection accuracy. This approach contributes to safeguarding academic integrity in a rapidly evolving educational landscape while upholding the principles of fairness and transparency fundamental to effective evaluation.

title: Resource Slicing for Co-existence of eMBB and URLLC in Multi-RIS Aided Networks
authors: Bhagawat Adhikari, Ahmed Shaharyar Khwaja, Muhammad Jaseemuddin, Alagan Anpalagan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Science and Communications Technologies (ICISCT)
abstract: In this paper, a two-stage solution for multiplexing of Enhanced Mobile Broadband (eMBB) and Ultra-reliable Low Latency Communication (URLLC) is provided in multireconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) aided downlink wireless networks. An Alternating Optimization (AO) technique is proposed to maximize the eMBB sum rate as a first-stage solution. In the second stage, a matching-based heuristic algorithm is used to minimize the eMBB rate loss caused by the punctured Resource Blocks (RBs) in the URLLC transmission. The proposed solution is evaluated through extensive simulations with various scenarios including different URLLC packet sizes, number of URLLC users and the eMBB threshold rates. The proposed solution with optimized RIS phase shift provides a smaller eMBB rate loss compared to the solution with random phase shift in the various scenarios mentioned above.

title: Preliminary results of a space-qualified polarization tracking system for QKD space systems using a genetic algorithm
authors: A. Jimenez-Girela, D. Merino-Perez, P. Garcia-Parejo, A. Crespo-Ramos, E. Gonzalez Del Campo, D. Rodríguez de Llera González, A. Álvarez-Herrero
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: We present a closed-loop polarization tracking system (PTS) designed to compensate polarization perturbations in real time for satellite-based QKD links. The system employs a liquid crystal polarization control device (PCD) and a genetic algorithm that optimizes the driving voltages to minimize the detected intensity, thereby restoring the input polarization state. Experimental validation under static and dynamic perturbations demonstrates the capability of the PTS to maintain high polarization extinction ratios and low polarization-induced QBER. These results confirm the feasibility of implementing closed-loop, real-time polarization correction in dynamic free-space quantum communication scenarios.

title: Improvement Method of Convolutional Neural Network Model in Medical Image Segmentation
authors: Ziwei Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Medical image segmentation is of great significance for disease diagnosis and treatment. However, the existing convolutional neural network (CNN) model has problems such as insufficient accuracy when processing complex medical images. This paper proposes an improved CNN model, constructs a multi-scale feature fusion module based on dilated convolution, introduces a channel and space joint attention mechanism, and designs a hybrid loss function. Experiments are conducted on the BraTS brain tumor dataset and the ISIC skin lesion dataset, and the model performance is evaluated by indicators such as Dice coefficient and IoU. The results show that the Dice coefficient of the improved model on the BraTS dataset is 0.895, which is 7.2% higher than U-Net, and the IoU value is 0.821, which is 6.1% higher, on the ISIC dataset, the Dice coefficient is 0.861, which is 6.3% higher than DeepLabv3 +, and the IoU is 0.791, which is 5.7% higher. Ablation experiments show that the improved modules work together significantly, effectively enhancing the model's ability to identify and segment the target area, and providing more accurate technical support for medical image-assisted diagnosis.

title: A Lightweight LSTM-Based Model for Sequential Movie Recommendation
authors: Aya Lyousfi, Khadija Salih Alj, Kenza Bouqdir, El Habib Nfaoui
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: Sequential recommendation systems aim to learn from user interaction data to determine which item a user is most likely to engage with next. While Transformer-based models such as BERT4Rec have achieved state-of-the-art performance, they often rely on complex architectures and auxiliary metadata, such as genre embeddings, which increase computational overhead and limit applicability in real-world settings. This paper introduces LSTM4Rec, a lightweight sequential recommender system that leverages a two-layer Long Short-Term Memory network trained solely on user watch sequences without any side information. Despite its simplicity, LSTM4Rec achieves an NDCG@10 of 0.98 and Hit@10 of 99% on the MovieLens 1M dataset, surpassing a genre-enhanced BERT4Rec implementation (NDCG @ 10: 0.7689, Hit@10: 95.07%). The results demonstrate that deep bidirectional attention mechanisms are not strictly necessary for high-quality sequence modeling, and that compact, interpretable models like LSTM4Rec remain highly competitive, especially in resource-constrained environments.

title: Design and Implementation of Predictive Location Analytics using Google Maps Data and Machine Learning
authors: Chillangi Manasa, Devanga Dharani Lakshmi, D. Venkatesh, M. Mallikarjuna Rao
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Predictive location analytics is vital as a component of intelligent mobility, transportation planning, and city decisions. The given paper represents a proposal of a single machine-learning system, which will use Google Maps APIs, traffic data, and the POI distributions to predict the travel time, the 3D congestion, and the hotspots of demand. In that methodology, it combines spatial preprocessing based on geohashing, temporal window modeling, POI density modeling, and a hybrid prediction architecture based on Gradient Boosted Trees (GBT) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks. The combination of these two approaches (LSTM and GBT) is shown to be effective in predicting the travel-time in Google Maps with high accuracy by incorporating nonlinear temporal dependencies and estimating the intensity of spatial hotspots, respectively. The reliability of the system is established through graphs and statistical findings which show that the system reduces the errors by a large margin compared to the baseline models. The suggested architecture offers a well-grounded scalable architecture of predictive mobility analytics within smart-city settings.

title: Advanced Deep Learning Algorithms: Architectures, Mathematics, Applications and Open Challenges
authors: Lobna El Harrouchi, Hanae Moussaoui, Mounir Karmoudi, Nabil El Akkad
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: Deep learning has revolutionized artificial intelligence, but with very sensitive systems that exploit minute patterns inside data, able to solve impossible problems with fine nuance beyond anything that is even comprehensible by humans just a few years ago. The objective of this survey is to present an update on the state-of-the-art architectures, learning methods and computational techniques for GANs using relevant literature as source of information. We investigate Convolutional (CNNs), Recurrent (RNNs) and Long-Short-Term Memory networks, Transformers and attention mechanisms, Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), Graph Neural Networks (GNNs), diffusion models, Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) and meta-learning. Theoretical backgrounds, mathematical definitions and training process are provided for each family, followed by association of their pros and cons to typical application domains as well as popular benchmarks. We also discuss cross-cutting issues interpretability, robustness, efficiency, ethics and privacy and summarize emerging research directions. To connect theory with practice, we add a comparative summary, case studies, and notes on implementation. Our aim with this work is to provide guidance to researchers, practitioners, and policy makers looking to responsibly harness advanced deep learning in practice.

title: Optical Performance and Environmental Durability of Radiation-Resistant Polarization-Maintaining Er/Yb Co-Doped Fiber in High-Power Optical Amplifiers for Space Communications
authors: Hiroki Kobayashi, Masatoshi Mitaki, Ryoichi Nishimura, Hideo Shiratani, Kentaro Ichii, Yasutoshi Takada
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: This report presents the optical performance and environmental durability of a radiation-resistant polarization-maintaining Er/Yb co-doped fiber (EYDF) developed by Fujikura Ltd. for space communications. The EYDF features a double cladding structure with a PANDA (polarization-maintaining and absorption-reducing) design, employing germanium doping for radiation resistance and phosphorus co-doping to improve Yb-to-Er energy transfer efficiency. To verify the reliable operation of this EYDF as a high-power optical amplifier (HPOA) in space environments, Nikon Corp. assembled and evaluated an amplifier incorporating this EYDF. The HPOA achieved an output power exceeding 30 W with ratios of signal to amplified spontaneous emission exceeding 37 dB and demonstrated stable operation for over 400 h. The polarization extinction ratio consistently exceeded 17 dB during continuous 10 W operation under temperature cycling from 25 °C to 45 °C, demonstrating robustness against temperature variations. High-temperature durability tests caused no significant changes in optical characteristics. These results demonstrate that the radiation-resistant EYDF is suitable for space-based optical communication systems.

title: Visualization Analysis of Sound Wave Interference through Computer Simulation
authors: Hui Meng, Yi Wu, Xinrong Guo, Huili Dai
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: This article focuses on the visual analysis of acoustic interference phenomenon through computer simulation. In view of the limitations of traditional methods to study acoustic interference, an algorithm model based on finite difference method is constructed with the help of powerful computer simulation functions. The model divides grids in two-dimensional space, sets key parameters, and simulates sound wave propagation and interference according to discrete iterative formula of wave equation. Through experiments, a virtual two-dimensional experimental environment is built by using COMSOL Multiphysics software, and different sound source parameters are set to verify the model. The results show that under different experimental conditions, the maximum relative error between the simulated constructive interference sound pressure value and the theoretical value is 2%, and the maximum relative error of the destructive interference sound pressure value is 5%. This proves that the algorithm model can accurately simulate the phenomenon of acoustic interference, provide an effective tool for acoustic interference research and related engineering applications, and enhance the understanding and application ability of the internal mechanism of acoustic interference.

title: Intelligent Traffic Accident Detection System using Deep Learning Fusion Techniques
authors: Ajay Kumar, Sanjay Roka, Aditya Joshi, Manoj Diwakar, Neeraj Kumar Pandey, Prabhishek Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Video surveillance traffic incident detection is an important part of Intelligent Transportation Systems, as it allows for the quick response to emergency incidents and enhances the safety of road users. The use of fixed cameras to monitor traffic creates several problems for traffic incident detection, including vehicle occlusions; changes in lighting, the complexity of how vehicles interact with each other, and the need to represent both sudden collisions and gradual change in traffic flow over time. While deep learning techniques have shown success in this area, most methods have focused on short-term event modelling and do not have the capacity to capture long-term event dependencies seen in real-world roadways. To address these challenges, an intelligent traffic incident detection framework using a deep fusion architecture is introduced. The spatial feature extraction is performed at the frame level using a ResNet50 backbone. Temporal dependencies on successive frames are captured by using transformer-based encoder. Sequential information of motion over time is refined using a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network; thus, providing a temporal-coherence component of sequencing. The intelligent traffic accident detection framework was tested against the Traffic Accident Detection Dataset, built from highway surveillance camera video footage. The evaluation of the framework produced a classification accuracy of 97.93% (with equal precision and recall for accident and normal traffic classes) in realistic surveillance scenarios.

title: Multi-Modal Medical Image Fusion using Guided Filtering and Multi-Scale Anisotropic Diffusion
authors: Dev Kumar Chaudhary, Prabhishek Singh, Achyut Shankar
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Multi-modal medical image fusion (M3IF) is an important field of medical image enhancement. It will enhance the examination of medical outcomes. The proposed method is an optimized M3IF (M3IF-GF-MLAD). Guided Filtering (GF) and Anisotropic Diffusion (AD) filtering are used in this technique. The GF method decomposes the input image into base layers (BLs) and detail layers (DLs). Both BLs and DLs are fused using an adaptive weighted strategy to maintain the equal information contribution of each modality. An edge-preserving AD filtering method is implemented as post-processing refinement for sharp edges. M3IF-GF-MLAD uses conductance function for edge preservation and less blurring. The Weickert diffusion model is integrated to boost contrast and for maintaining anatomical structures. M3IF-GF-MLAD is compared with conventional fusion methods, and it surpasses all of them. As M3IF-GF-MLAD offers better results so it has the capability to solution for medical imaging applications.

title: Application of Machine Learning-Based Multi-Source Heterogeneous Information Fusion in Moving Target Tracking
authors: Shunjie Lin, Weiwei Geng, Hanhua Cao, Tianyu Zheng
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Multisource heterogeneous fusion with machine learning still lacks geometric–physical hard constraints, interpretable uncertainty estimates, and out-of-distribution robustness. Existing approaches have not explicitly embedded geometric residuals, physics-conservation constraints, or interpretable uncertainty into the network loss, allowing systematic errors to be smoothed and concealed, which introduces potential risks in real-world deployment. This paper propose DynaToken-LT, which incorporates a delay-compensation mechanism into the attention computation. The algorithm was designed to take into account the temporal characteristics of sensor streams, thereby enhancing its applicability. In the experiment, the situation where the target temporarily leaves the field of view was simulated, and the tracking performance under conditions of sensor data loss and extreme motion was tested. Experiments demonstrate that it achieves robust, low-ID-swap, and low-computation multisource fusion tracking for moving objects.

title: GAN-based Synthetic Data Augmentation to Address Class Imbalance in RetinaMNIST Dataset
authors: Sai Harsha Gangari, Jithendra Katta, Nikhil Reddy Kodumuru, Balakrishana Reddy Ragannagari, Bhagavath Sai Darapureddy, Sai Bharath Reddy Lattupalli
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Medical imaging datasets often suffer from significant class imbalance, limiting the effectiveness of deep learning models in identifying rare conditions. In this study, we focus on the RetinaMNIST dataset, a benchmark for retinal disease classification, where minority classes are underrepresented. To address this, we trained a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) enhanced with residual connections on samples from the minority Class 4 to generate synthetic retinal images. These generated images were combined with the original training set to up sample the minority class. A VGG19-based Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) was trained on both the original and augmented datasets to assess performance improvements. Results show that augmenting the training data with GAN-generated samples significantly improves minority class recall and overall model robustness, highlighting the potential of residual GAN architectures and deep feature extractors like VGG19 in medical image augmentation tasks.

title: Genetic Algorithm-Based Placement and Resource Allocation for Flying Base Stations
authors: Fady A. Abouelghit, Karim G. Seddik
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) are emerging as key components in next-generation wireless networks, offering adaptable and scalable solutions for dynamic communication environments. However, their effective deployment requires careful optimization of both spatial positioning and resource allocation. This paper addresses the joint problem of three-dimensional placement and transmit power configuration for Flying Base Stations (FBSs) operating in the presence of Macro Base Stations (MBSs). The problem is formulated as a Mixed-Integer Nonlinear Programming (MINLP) task, a class known for its computational intractability. To tackle this complexity, we propose a genetic algorithm-based framework that simultaneously optimizes spatial coordinates, power levels, and activation status for single and multiple FBS scenarios. Unlike conventional approaches that decouple these dimensions or limit their focus to isolated deployments, our methodology integrates spatial positioning, power allocation, multi-FBS coordination, and interference management with MBSs into a unified optimization framework. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm achieves nearoptimal user coverage and significantly faster convergence compared to a bounded exhaustive search baseline, confirming its effectiveness and scalability in complex deployments involving multiple FBSs and MBSs.

title: Parking Path Planning Improvement Strategy Based on AI and Functional Safety
authors: Dan Li, Yunlei Zhang, Lijuan Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: To address the potential safety risks of traditional parking path planning strategies under electrical/electronic faults, this paper proposes an AI-enhanced functional safetyimproved parking path planning strategy. By defining the functional safety boundaries during the parking process and combining an arc-line-arc method optimized with cubic polynomial curves, a phased fault detection safety mechanism for perpendicular parking is designed. When a wheel steering angle fault is detected in the previous phase, functional safety boundary indicators are first selected as inputs for the AI model. By leveraging the nonlinear relationship learned through reinforcement learning between fault scenarios and path planning equation parameters, the path parameters are dynamically adjusted, forming an AI-enhanced functional safety-improved parking path planning strategy. The proposed parking algorithm was validated through Simulink/CarSim cosimulation. A comparison between the improved and nonimproved strategies shows that when the faulty steering angle is 10 degrees, the improved path planning strategy effectively avoids collisions, demonstrating superior parking safety. Furthermore, the improved strategy results in a smaller variation amplitude of the yaw rate during movement, indicating better driving stability.

title: Research on Power Grid Investment Decision Model Based on Multi-objective Optimization Algorithm
authors: Chen Ye, Han Jiang, Yaogang Luo
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: Power grid investment decision-making is faced with the conflict and trade-off between economic, reliability and environmental protection objectives. In order to solve this problem, this paper constructs a multi-objective optimization model, which comprehensively considers the economic, reliability and environmental protection objectives of power grid investment, and introduces NSGA-II as the solution algorithm. The model optimizes the investment decision of power grid by minimizing the investment and operation cost, maximizing the reliability of power supply and minimizing the environmental impact. Through case analysis, this paper shows the application effect of the model in the actual power grid upgrading investment, and verifies its effectiveness in providing a variety of trade-off schemes and meeting different decision-making needs. The research results show that the model can provide a more scientific and reasonable solution for power grid investment decision-making and contribute to the sustainable development of power industry.

title: An Analysis of Integrated Face Recognition Attendance Analytics
authors: P.Pandiaraja, Pattidi Megha Chaitanya, Katakam Nithin Kumar, C.Selvarathi, Charles Prabu V, S.Jagan
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: In schools and other organizations, attendance control is extremely important in terms of discipline and accountability. The use of traditional methods, including manual recording in the registers or RFID-based solutions is usually ineffective, time- consuming, and subject to human error. Face recognition has emerged as a strong option of automating the check-in system in the presence of an exceptionally high rate of computer vision and artificial intelligence growth. The literature review has shown that use of biometric technologies like fingerprint or face recognition is the basis of many modern attendance systems. Although these methods have helped to enhance precision, they are however affected by the highly populated area, changing illumination or network-depending structures. Other systems do not work when the biometric sensors do not work or when the internet connection is poor resulting in incomplete records of attendance. The identified gap in the current research is the absence of a powerful, hybrid system that can effectively operate in the context of high traffic and guarantee real-time data fine-tuning even in the situations when network conditions vary. Also, the current models tend to be mobile unfriendly with regard to live monitoring and have difficulties in managing attendance at the large scale. In order to address these shortcomings, the proposed work proposes an Attendance Management System based on Face Recognition with Python and Open CV which incorporates Haar Cascade Classifier to detect faces and Local Binary Pattern Histogram (LBPH) algorithm to recognize faces. The system has automatic features of detecting and identifying individuals by a web camera, attendance marking and storing the data safely in a CSV database. It also offers a Tkinter based Graphical User Interface (GUI) that is simple to use to register, train, and monitor. The method offers real time attendance taking, reduces human intervention, and eradicates the chances of proxy attendance. The findings indicate that the system under normal lighting conditions has high recognition accuracy (approximately 95%-97%) and an average response time of less than two seconds. It is scalable, reliable and user-friendly making it applicable in deployment in schools, colleges and organizations. To sum up, the offered approach manages to fill the gap between the traditional and modern attendance systems by incorporating both the accuracy, speed, and real-time availability, and further improvement can be provided by adding the deep learning or the cloud-based synchronization to even ease the processes.

title: Measuring Multimodal Transportation Network Resilience Using Curvature-Core Decomposition and Flow Inequality Metrics
authors: Guillaume Bouleux, Giacomo Kahn, Aurélie Charles
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Control, Decision and Information Technologies (CoDIT)
abstract: Production, health, transport and goods systems have been severely affected by recent health crises, and continue to be so in the face of current climatic and geopolitical disruptions. In this unstable context, it is essential not only to ensure their robustness, but also to reinforce their resilience. Many works in the literature deal with the robustness of systems, but very few focus on proposals related to resilience. We therefore propose to approach this concept within the framework of a multimodal transport network modeled by a multilayer network. We develop a resilience strategy based on the curvature-core decomposition of the network and compare it with a random decision strategy and a decision strategy based on maximum transport capacities. The results show that the strategy based on the curvature-core of the multilayer network enables a better decision to be made, avoiding congestion and link saturation during reconstruction, and finally enabling a balance in the distribution of flows.

title: Accelerating Multimodal Large Language Model Inference through Visual Feature Compression and Decoupled Architectures
authors: Xusheng Li, Zonghai Zhu, Li Feng, Huanlai Xing
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Multimodal large models face two major efficiency bottlenecks during inference: high memory consumption and serialized execution flow. This paper proposes an inference acceleration method that integrates visual feature compression with vision–language decoupling, comprising a Text-guided Image Feature Dimensionality Reduction (TG-IFDR) mechanism and a Visual–Language Decoupled architecture (VLBridge). TG-IFDR dynamically filters visual features based on textual semantics, reducing visual KV cache by approximately 40%, while VLBridge restructures the inference pipeline to enable parallel execution of visual preprocessing and language generation. Experiments on the MSCOCO image captioning task demonstrate that the proposed method achieves significant acceleration across four mainstream models: LLaVA, BLIP-2, CogVLM2, and Qwen-VL. Using VLBridge alone increases generation throughput to 9–10× that of the baseline, and when combined with TG-IFDR, overall inference speed reaches 15–20× the baseline, delivering an order-of-magnitude acceleration. Although feature compression introduces controllable prefill latency and minor semantic degradation, this overhead can be effectively amortized in long-sequence generation scenarios. The method maintains acceptable semantic consistency while substantially improving inference efficiency, offering a feasible solution for efficient deployment of multimodal large models in real-time interactive and resource-constrained scenarios. Future work will focus on learnable visual feature selection and cross-modal attention sparsification to further optimize the trade-off between accuracy and efficiency.

title: Optimal Design for Enhanced Torque and Efficiency of a Dual-Stator Dual-Rotor Axial-Flux Switching PM Motor for Lightweight EVs
authors: Mohammad Farahzadi, Mohammadreza Naeimi, Amir Ebrahimi Shohani, Fariba Farrokh, Mohammad Sedigh Toulabi, Karim Abbaszadeh
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: This paper presents the design and optimization of a dual-stator, dual-rotor axial-flux switching permanent magnet (AFSPM) motor aimed at enhancing torque and efficiency for lightweight electric vehicle (EV) applications. A Taguchi-based optimization strategy is employed to systematically improve torque characteristics and overall performance. The proposed AFSPM motor features dual stators with 12 poles on each side and an internal 10-pole dual-rotor configuration. Phase-group concentrated windings are adopted to enhance fault-tolerant capability. The back electromotive force (EMF), cogging torque, average torque, torque ripple, and losses are analyzed using a three-dimensional finite element method (3D-FEM) to evaluate the AFSPM motor’s electromagnetic performance. A comparative study with existing AFSPM motor designs in the literature is conducted to validate the proposed structure’s superior torque density and efficiency. The optimized model demonstrates significant reductions in cogging torque and torque ripple, confirming that the proposed AFSPM motor is a promising candidate for efficient lightweight EV propulsion systems.

title: Modification of rotor lamination stacking of 425kW induction motor
authors: Arsalan Ajdani, Pouyan Shahabi, Meysam Davoud Abadi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: High-performance induction motors employ laminated rotor core designs to prevent undesirable eddy currents and minimize power loss. During the manufacturing of these laminated rotor cores, geometrical tolerances play a crucial role in maintaining proper stacking pressure. This case study focuses on determining the optimal geometrical tolerances for a 425-kW induction motor core lamination. Multi-step non-linear finite element simulations are conducted to obtain the optimal parameters for the core, fixture, and press plate design.

title: Real-time optical turbulence and wind profile monitoring with FEELINGS optical ground station
authors: Florian Quatresooz, Cyril Petit, Aurélie Bonnefois, Gaël Kermarrec, David Alaluf, Nicolas Védrenne
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: Future free-space optical communication links be-tween the ground and satellites will require systematic on-site monitoring of atmospheric turbulence to ensure reliable operation. To address this need, ONERA’s FEELINGS optical ground station has been equipped with a real-time turbulence monitoring pipeline, capable of retrieving refractive index structure parameter and wind profile information along the line of sight during optical links. The pipeline is first validated under controlled turbulent conditions before being applied to measurements collected during GEO-feeder links with the TELEO payload in September 2024. The retrieved turbulence conditions are then compared with numerical weather prediction simulations and used to assess the expected link performance.

title: Fall Detection Framework - A Study of Optimized Neural Network and Ensemble Classifiers with Feature Explainability
authors: Roopa B Hegde, Pradyumna G R, P Dhakshak
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Falls constitute a leading cause of injury specially among the elderly population, which necessitates automated high precision systems. The present study proposes an automated fall detection system integrating edge device with explainable machine learning models. Tri-axial accelerometer data from smartphone representing six activities namely fall, jump, run, sit, sleep and walk was acquired, and spectral features were extracted using a 4000 ms window to capture movement patterns. Top twenty significant features were selected employing ‘ReliefF’ algorithm to train Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and Neural Network classifiers, optimized using Bayesian optimization for hyperparameter tuning. The optimized Neural Network outperformed achieving accuracy of 99.14% and a recall of 98.26%. Further, explainable AI was employed to validate the models for reliability and clinical trust on relevant features. These findings indicate that spectral feature extraction combined with optimized machine learning models are effective, for real-time fall detection and suitable for edge deployment.

title: Integrated Edge-based Fault Analytics and Auto-Reclosing Control for Smart Three-Phase Distribution Systems
authors: M Muthukumaran, Prithive S, Sriram R, Lohitha G, Sonika S
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Traditional relays persist in holding back dependable fault management with their poor discrimination of momentary disturbances from permanent faults, lack of fine-grained phase-wise analysis, and failure to provide a uniform cloud-based visibility, especially in three-phase distribution systems. This gap creates the need for an edge-compatible, low-cost protection network architecture with real-time event-type classification and automated response mechanisms as well as remote monitoring. The designed scheme utilizes an ESP32 controller for the analysis of PT and CT readings with instant magnitude retrieval, while a constant threshold of 50 A is used to classify L-G, L-L, and three-phase faults. A persistence time of 0.05 s is used to eliminate transitory signals from sustained errors. For non-damageable faults, the schedule initiates a three-shot auto-reclosing operation, whilst for faults that do not clear, there is a transfer to lockout of appropriate quantities. MATLAB/Simulink simulation results show that all expected waveforms (voltage sag and inrush) at 0.04 s match the specified logic implemented in the program. Random hardware tests further validate consistent fault detection, transient fault restoration, and permanent fault lockout. Phase and fault recordings are transmitted to ThingSpeak every minute when a phase voltage is present, fault codes (0-3) or temporary/permanent event counts: see MATLAB analytics for verification of data availability. A practical defense and sensing layer for smart-grid/industrial/academic deployments, the system platform is also a scalable substrate for further penetration into higher voltage deployments, predictive maintenance, and more robust IoT communication stacks.

title: ConYOLO-SLAM: A Robust Dynamic SLAM System via Semantic Perception and Temporal Consistency
authors: Wen Liu, Le Miao, Xinsen Li, Zhongliang Deng
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Moving objects in dynamic environments severely undermine the static world assumption of visual SLAM systems, leading to feature mismatches and pose estimation drift. Existing semantic SLAM methods overly rely on the accuracy of single-frame segmentation networks, making them prone to failure when the network experiences missed detections or noisy segmentation boundaries. To address this issue, this paper proposes ConYOLO-SLAM, a robust dynamic SLAM system that integrates semantic awareness with temporal consistency. First, the system integrates the YOLOv8 semantic segmentation network to provide pixel-level semantic prior information for front-end feature extraction, identifying potential dynamic regions within the scene. To address the uncertainty inherent in single-frame semantic inference, we introduce a sliding-window-based temporal consistency feature filtering mechanism. This mechanism constructs a temporal buffer for feature point states and performs multi-frame validation of dynamic attributes through geometric position correlation, retaining only temporally persistent dynamic features. Experimental results on public datasets demonstrate that this approach effectively suppresses dynamic object interference, achieving significant improvements in localization accuracy and robustness in dynamic scenes compared to ORB-SLAM3.

title: Development of an Embedded Machine Learning Model for Predictive Fault Diagnosis and Energy Optimization in Electric Vehicles
authors: R.Nidhya, G.Muthuram, A.Karthikeyan, V.Ellappan
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The increasing complexity of powertrains of electric vehicles (EV) requires the incorporation of intelligent mechanisms on fault prediction and energy management. Conventional diagnostic and energy control methods do not offer real-time information or predictability, and their reliability is lower with increased cost of operation. This study builds a built-in machine learning (ML) system that can perform predictive fault diagnostics and dynamic optimum energy regulation of EVs. The experiment used real and simulated EV data sets of different driving conditions, loads, and environmental parameters. Several ML models such as the Random Forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM), and the Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks were trained to identify malfunctions in the battery and motor subsystems. The LSTM-based model had the best overall performance with an accuracy of prediction of fault of 96.8 %, precision of 95.4 %, recall of 94.9%, and energy optimization efficiency improvement of 12.6% as compared to baseline control strategies. The model had a computation latency improvement of 18.3% which proved its applicability in embedded implementation. The designed embedded ML system is efficient in predictive maintenance and adaptive energy management of EVs.

title: Transmission Duty Cycle Derivation Using AI/ML Algorithm from Telemetry Data
authors: Rakesh Kushwaha, Hiralkumar Patel
abstract: This paper presents an innovative methodology for generating transmission duty cycles for drivetrain evaluation, combining Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) techniques. Traditional methods rely on limited physical tests and deterministic models that fail to account for real-world driving complexities. The proposed framework uses large-scale vehicle telemetry data, AI-based pre-processing, and a combination of supervised and unsupervised ML algorithms to generate more accurate duty cycles. These algorithms include clustering, Principal Component Analysis (PCA), and neural networks. Additionally, reinforcement learning and predictive models are employed to simulate transmission responses under varied, unseen usage conditions. The methodology enhances the development of both conventional and electrified powertrains, providing proactive duty cycle optimization for advanced vehicle architectures, such as Automated Manual Transmission (AMT), Dual Clutch Transmission (DCT), and Electric Drive Units (EDUs). The integration of AI/ML-based strategies significantly reduces development costs and time while aligning with the Industry 4.0 paradigm and digital twin frameworks.

title: From Competency to Curiosity: Revolutionalizing IT Work
authors: Mo Nishiyama
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 ACM SIGUCCS Annual Conference
abstract: None

title: Optimal Quantum Variational Circuits for Detecting False Data Injection Attacks on Transmission Substations
authors: Taha Hammadia, Ahmad Mohammad Saber, Marthe Kassouf, Deepa Kundur, Mohsen Khalaf
abstract: In digital substations, protective relays receive their measurements over vulnerable communication channels, which makes them susceptible to cyberattacks against substation operations. This paper introduces a novel Quantum Intrusion Detection System (QIDS) for detecting False Data Injection Attacks (FDIAs) against power transformer differential relays in digital substations. These FDIAS can manipulate the targeted relay's measurements, aiming to falsely trip the power transformer protected by the relay. The proposed QIDS leverages Quantum Variational Circuits (QVCs), a quantum machine learning circuit architecture with trainable parameters, that can be trained offline to distinguish manipulated relay measurements from those arising from faults. To optimize the QIDS's performance, a Hyper-Parameter Optimization (HPO) procedure, Tree-Structured Parzen Estimator, is utilized in this paper. We test the performance of the optimized QIDS under a comprehensive set of FDIA and fault scenarios generated in an OPAL-RT HYPERSIM environment. Our results confirm that the proposed QIDS can accurately detect malicious FDIA values without compromising the relay's protective dependability. The performance of the proposed QIDS is also evaluated under measurement noise, measurement errors, sophisticated attacks, and varying measurement window sizes and sampling rates, and is further compared against existing methods. Finally, the proposed solution is implemented in an online Hardware-in-the-Loop testbed, illustrating the practicality of the solution.

title: A Novel Methodology for Implementing Planar Ultra-Wide Stopband SIW Bandpass Filters and Filtering Crossover
authors: Zhenghai Luo, Kang Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: This article presents a novel methodology for implementing a planar substrate-integrated waveguide (SIW) bandpass filter (BPF) with ultra-wide stopband based on the mode cancellation technique. To realize mode cancellation, a dual-probe feeding scheme featuring equal-amplitude, in-phase input and output signals is employed, superseding the conventional central feeding method. First, the electric and magnetic field distribution rules of various resonant modes are analytically derived to guide the placement of the input–output feeding ports and intercavity coupling windows, thus enabling effective mode cancellation and harmonic suppression. Second, a T-junction is incorporated at both the input and output ports to realize the dual probe feeding structure for mode cancellation. Moreover, the introduced T-junctions are fully integrated with the resonant cavity and provide additional suppression of higher-order harmonic modes without deteriorating the circuit performance. The proposed methodology is applicable not only to planar ultra-wide stopband BPFs but also to planar ultra-wide stopband filtering crossovers (FCRs) without intricate design procedures or degradation in device performance. Finally, the proposed methodology is validated through three planar examples, including asecond-order SIW BPF, a third-order SIW BPF, and a third-order SIW FCR. The simulated and measured results showcase that their stopbands are extended up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$4.1f_{0}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$40f_{0}+$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$40f_{0}+$</tex-math> </inline-formula> (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$f_{0}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> is the operating center frequency), respectively, demonstrating the effectiveness of the proposed design approach.

title: Demonstration of 8-kV SiC Gate Turn-Off Thyristors With High di/dt for Nanosecond Pulse Fast-Switching Applications
authors: Lingfeng Li, Juntao Li, Lianghui Li, Zhengxiang Liao, Xingliang Xu, Lin Zhang, Yinghao Meng, Yingjiang He, Zhiqiang Li
abstract: This work presents the design, fabrication, and characterization of a high di/dt silicon carbide (SiC) gate turn-off (GTO) thyristor optimized for fast operation in nanosecond pulsed-power applications. Analysis reveals that the current rise time during the turn-on process critically depends on carrier injection efficiency. Guided by this insight, the P-plus and N-base layers are carefully designed to improve the nanosecond unsaturated conductivity modulation. Optimal doping ranges (P-plus: $2.0\times 10^{{19}} \sim 1.0\times 10^{{20}}$ cm ${}^{-{3}}$ ; N-base: $2.0\times 10^{{16}} \sim 1.6\times 10^{{17}}$ cm ${}^{-{3}}$ ) are proposed with consideration of punchthrough and incomplete ionization. The accordingly optimized 8-kV GTO achieves a record di/dt of 82.7kA/( $\mu $ s $\cdot $ cm2) at a peak current density of 3.18kA/cm2 in a high-power pulse forming network, and the halved turn-on time (30.8ns) sets a new benchmark for SiC GTO pulse application. In addition, repetitive pulse operation is demonstrated for 2000pulses (20Hz, 100s) based on the above pulse condition. This study provides both a physical-level design approach and a promising solution for ultrafast pulse switching, propelling the nanosecond power pulse technology forward.

title: Attention-Emotion Assessment of ASD Children via Representation Learning based on Cross-Modal Disentanglement and Attention Alignment_supp1-3676489.pdf
authors: Dan Chen
abstract: Comprehensive cognitive-affective profiling for precision ASD assessment is hindered by inadequate computational modeling of emotion-behavior dynamics. This gap persists due to limited methods for reconciling EEG, eye-tracking, and facial expression modalities-critical for capturing ASD heterogeneity drivers. This study proposes the Cognitive-Affective Ability Assessment (CA3) framework, focusing on attention control in conjunction with valence-arousal, to address these limitations through: (1) Ability-Specific Neural Disentanglement extracting attention- and emotion-specific representations from EEG using ET and facial expression recordings as anchors; (2) Symmetrical Cross-Ability Alignment modeling contextual dependencies via a symmetric cross-attention mechanism; and (3) Uncertainty-Aware Ability Integration fusing per-ability predictions using Dirichlet modeling and Dempster-Shafer theory while quantifying confidence.
