title: Message From the General Chair
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: On behalf of the Organizing Committee, it is my great pleasure to welcome you to IEEE MECOM 2025, the IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking. We are delighted to bring together researchers, practitioners, industry leaders, and students from around the world for a vibrant forum to exchange ideas, present cutting-edge research, and foster collaboration in the rapidly evolving field of communications. IEEE MECOM continues to play a vital role in strengthening ties between academia and industry while highlighting innovation across the Middle East and beyond. We are especially pleased to host IEEE MECOM 2025 in Cairo, Egypt - a city renowned for its extraordinary history, rich cultural heritage, timeless beauty, and warm, welcoming atmosphere - providing an exceptional setting for both intellectual exchange and memorable experiences.

title: Kinetic Energy Estimation of IMU-Equipped Sediment Particles with Gaussian Process Regression and Conformal Prediction
authors: Georgios Maniatis, Jeffrey Tuhtan, Gert Toming, Edward Curley, Charlie Gadd, Richard Williams, Trevor Hoey
abstract: Direct particle-scale sediment measurements remain difficult in turbid, high-energy rivers where optical methods fail. We present an integration-free IMU workflow that maps short windows to projected speed and kinetic energy using physics-aware preprocessing, orientation-invariant Hankel embeddings, Gaussian process regression (GPR), and split conformal prediction. On event-disjoint hold-out tests, the selected GPR model (m = 10) achieves R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.628, RMSE = 0.168ms<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−1</sup>, and MAE = 0.096ms<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−1</sup>. A four-model benchmark on identical event-grouped folds (GPR, LSTM, SVR-RBF, LSBoost) gives the lowest RMSE for LSBoost (0.158ms<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−1</sup>); GPR is within 0.001ms<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−1</sup> of the strongest non-GPR comparator (LSBoost), and paired RMSE differences are non-significant (p = 0.812). Empirical conformal coverage is 87.6%/93.7%/97.9% for nominal 90%/95%/99% targets. River Calder deployments show peak kinetic energies up to 0.168 J. The framework provides uncertainty-aware kinematics and energetics for autonomous sediment-transport monitoring.

title: Reconstruct Private Embeddings: An Interaction-level Membership Inference Attack against Federated Social Matching
authors: Lingqi Jiang, Dazhong Rong, Guoyao Yu, Jianhai Chen, Qinming He, Zhenguang Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Social matching is a task that recommends potential friends to users based on their existing friendships. This user-to-user recommendation setting extends general recommender systems by shifting the focus from user-item interactions to interactions among users themselves, and general recommendation methods are often directly applied to this setting. However, we argue that in federated learning, directly applying existing federated recommendation methods to social matching can result in privacy leakage, due to the specificity of social matching: the mutual nature of friendships. We theoretically and quantitatively reveal these security vulnerabilities and propose a novel interaction-level membership inference attack targeting federated social matching. Our attack employs a reconstruction model combining affine transformations with an MLP to capture both linear and non-linear mappings between users' public and private embeddings. Trained on a portion of leaking user data, it can reconstruct benign users' private embeddings and infer their true friends. We conduct numerous experiments on multiple datasets under various experimental settings to verify the effectiveness and robustness of our attack method. Experimental results show that: (i) Our attack approach exhibits strong robustness and effectiveness across diverse experimental setups; (ii) Existing defense methods fail to effectively resist our attack while maintaining satisfactory recommendation performance. Our findings highlight the need for new privacy-preserving techniques specifically designed for federated social matching.

title: A Dynamic Impedance Matching Network for Power Ultrasonic Transduction System with Load Fluctuation
authors: Yuanhong Zhang, Xin Yang
abstract: The pronounced load fluctuation during actual operation significantly impairs the efficiency of power ultrasonic transduction systems. However, research on the matching network that is able to dynamically match the variation in the impedance of piezoelectric transducers at ultrasonic frequency is untapped. To achieve constant maximum power transfer between the power supply and the most widely-used piezoelectric transducer under variable loads, a dynamic impedance matching network is proposed here. To guarantee the matching network can accurately respond to the load fluctuation, an automatic impedance analysis method is meticulously proposed. Through integration of automatic impedance analysis and adaptive control, the equivalent capacitance and operating frequency are rapidly adjusted to optimal values, which facilitates the dynamic matching of the resistance and reactance of the piezoelectric transducer. A parameter design method is elaborated to ensure the proposed network can be matched with the piezoelectric transducer over a wide load range. Then, a control method for the switched capacitor using the supply voltage as the reference signal is proposed to improve the stability of the matching network. Finally, to verify the performance of the proposed dynamic impedance matching network and control method, both simulation and experiment are carried out based on an actual piezoelectric transducer. The results show that the proposed network can maintain the power factor of the power supply to be nearly unity, enabling the power ultrasonic transducer to operate at its rated power under different loads.

title: An $r$th-Order Generalized Five-ULA Sparse Array with High uDOFs and Low Mutual Coupling
authors: Jian Zhao, Shidong Zhang, Cuiling Fan
abstract: A uniform linear array (ULA) represents a fundamental sparse array configuration characterized by constant inter-element spacing. In this letter, for any positive integer <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$r$</tex-math></inline-formula>, we propose an <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$r$</tex-math></inline-formula>th-order generalized five-ULA sparse array (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$r$</tex-math></inline-formula>-G5USA), which includes super augmented nested array (SANA) and a class of enhanced multi-ULA sparse array (EMUSA) structure as special cases. Closed-form expressions are derived for both the array configuration and the weight function of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$r$</tex-math></inline-formula>-G5USA. Specifically, the proposed <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$r$</tex-math></inline-formula>-G5USA not only maintains a high number of uniform degrees of freedom (uDOFs) on the order of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {O}(\frac{2N^{2}}{3} + \frac{2N}{3})$</tex-math></inline-formula>, but also enables flexible control of the first <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2r-1$</tex-math></inline-formula> or <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2r-2$</tex-math></inline-formula> weight functions, reducing their values to unity. Simulation results demonstrate that <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$r$</tex-math></inline-formula>-G5USA exhibits superior performance over existing sparse arrays in terms of uDOFs, coupling leakage (CL), and direction-of-arrival (DOA) estimation accuracy.

title: Somatic Mutation Driven Cancer Stage Classification via Autoencoder Clustering and Explainable Deep Learning_supp1-3676284.pdf
authors: Haseeb Younis

title: Iterative Learning-Based Adaptive Containment Scheme of Multiagent Systems Against FDI Attacks
authors: Yang Liu, Jia-Ke Wang, Ronghu Chi, Xiaoping Liu
abstract: This paper studies an iterative learning-based adaptive containment control for a kind of repetitive multiagent systems (MASs) with a nonstrict-feedback structure. The hyperbolic tangent function and the mean value theory are combined to handle the input saturation problem. Then, we establish a relationship between the original system state and compromised system state to resolve the impact of the false data injection (FDI) attack on the system performance. Under the framework of backstepping, the nonstrict-feedback issue is addressed by using the property of the fuzzy logic system. However, it is inevitable to generate two unknown control gains during the process of addressing the input saturation and FDI attack. As a result, an estimated scheme is proposed to approximate their bound. Moreover, two auxiliary functions are introduced to the virtual controller to reduce the influence of the sign function, and achieve the asymptotic convergence. The proposed scheme guarantees that all the followers converge to a convex hull spanned by the leaders as the iteration approaches infinity. Finally, two simulation examples are considered to verify the effectiveness of the designed method.

title: A Two-Way Survey of Intrinsically Interpretable Deep Learning for Tabular Data: Bridging Methods and Systems
authors: Muhammad Ilyas, Kenenna Okafor, Fahad Sohrab, Pekka Abrahamsson
abstract: The interpretability crisis in artificial intelligence (AI) has intensified with the widespread adoption of complex black-box models in critical domains such as healthcare, finance, and cybersecurity. This issue is particularly acute for tabular data, which exhibits heterogeneity, sparsity, nontrivial feature interactions, and lacks spatial structure. This paper presents a systematic survey of intrinsically interpretable deep learning (IIDL) methods tailored for tabular data. We propose a two-dimensional framework that integrates method-centric analysis across pre-model, in-model, and post-model interpretability stages with systems-centric dimensions of timing, target, scope, and audience. Core IIDL architectures, including additive, attention-based, concept-driven, prototype-driven, and hybrid models, are critically reviewed and benchmarked using representative methods such as Neural Additive Models (NAM) and TabNet. We rigorously examine foundational assumptions underlying each architectural family, identifying critical validity constraints including additivity limitations, stability-sparsity trade-offs, supervision requirements, geometric constraints, and computational complexity barriers. Comparative analysis with post-hoc approaches such as SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) and Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations (LIME) highlights their respective robustness and interpretability trade-offs. The survey identifies open challenges in adversarial robustness, causal fidelity evaluation, human-centered interpretability, and mitigation of architectural assumption violations in deployment. Overall, this work provides a methodological and systems-level roadmap for responsible, transparent, and trustworthy AI deployment in regulated environments.

title: Self-Supervised Temporal Super-Resolution Framework with METEOR-Net for Enhancing Convective Process Reconstruction in Geostationary Satellite Cloud Data
authors: Xiang Lin, Yunying Li, Zitong Chen, Xiongwei Kou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Next-generation geostationary satellite FY-4A provides cloud data with a temporal resolution of 15 minutes, while shallow convection may evolve into deep convection in less than 30 minutes. This temporal sparsity limits the satellite’s ability to capture rapid convective processes, thereby hindering effective weather monitoring and early warning. Temporal super-resolution technology is used to address this limitation, but existing temporal super-resolution methods often directly apply video frame interpolation paradigms designed for RGB images, resulting in transfer errors. To address such issues, a novel spatiotemporal self-supervised temporal super-resolution framework is proposed, which leverages the spatiotemporal consistency between the regional and full-disk observations of the FY-4A satellite to establish a temporal super-resolution mapping relationship without the need for manually labeled data. Additionally, a Multi-channel Encoding and Temporal-flow Evolution Network for Optimized-observation Reconstruction (METEOR-Net) is proposed, which addresses the challenges of multi-channel asynchrony and complex nonlinear evolution in geostationary satellite cloud data through multi-channel collaborative encoding, temporal-flow estimation and evolution, and evolution-aware feature fusion mechanisms. To enhance the focus on convective regions, a new convective region-weighted loss function and evaluation metrics are designed. Experimental results demonstrate that this method achieves state-of-the-art performance in visual quality and convective process reconstruction accuracy in the FY-4A satellite cloud data temporal super-resolution reconstruction task, successfully enhancing the temporal resolution of FY-4A cloud data from 15 minutes to 5 minutes. This provides high-resolution satellite cloud data for future convective weather monitoring and early warning systems.

title: Nonlinear Transport in Microwave-Generated Plasma
authors: Horia-Eugen Porteanu, Ilija Stefanović, Michael Klute, Peter Awakowicz, Ralf Peter Brinkmann, Wolfgang Heinrich
abstract: Excitation of a plasma with microwaves has the advantage that the lifetime of ions and electrons is much longer than the oscillation period. At a frequency of 2.45 GHz, the electron density is constant in time; therefore, one expects a constant conductivity. In spite of this, harmonics, i.e., nonlinear transport, can be detected at pressures < 50 Pa. We identified two possible mechanisms for this nonlinear behavior, depending on the type of excitation: ICP or capacitively coupled plasma (CCP).

title: Self-Consistent Dynamic Simulations of the Charge–Discharge Cycles of a Spacecraft in Geostationary Orbit
authors: Amandine Finot, Sébastien Hess, Julien Jarrige
abstract: Because of their interaction with the surrounding plasma, spacecraft in operation experience high absolute and differential charging between surfaces made of different materials. Differential charging may ultimately lead to the formation of an electrostatic discharge (ESD) that equalizes the potential of the satellite and the plasma environment and, when triggered on a solar array, generates a plasma bubble that neutralizes, partially or totally, the cover glasses. Charging simulations are commonly used to evaluate the risk of ESD formation on satellites. However, as simulations do not consider the effect of ESDs, computed differential potentials on satellite can reach thousands of volts, while discharges are known to occur at lower values (typically around 500 V). This article presents the coupling between a charging model and a flashover propagation model, applied on a satellite with an 8-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathrm {\textbf {m}^{2}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> solar array. Simulations are presented for several charge–discharge cycles, where the flashover almost completely neutralizes the whole surface of the solar array. A parametric study is carried out to investigate the effect of the ESD ignition voltage, the environment, and the cathode spot material on the ESD occurrence frequency, the ESD duration, and the flashover current amplitude. This type of simulation can be used to further understand parameters strengthening the ESD risks.

title: A Novel Operation and Control Strategy for a P2G System Feeding Stand-alone Load
authors: Rupa Mishra, Nilanjan Tewari, Meenakshi J, Nayan Kumar, Tapas Kumar Saha
abstract: A hydrogen storage system plays a vital role in shaping future power systems dominated by renewable energy. This paper presents an integrated modeling and control strategy for a green hydrogen-based stand-alone power supply system. A dual source triple-switch buck boost converter (DSTSBBC) is designed to facilitate bidirectional energy flow between the FC and ELZ using only two actively switching devices. A finite state-machine (FSM) based control logic is used for the converter operation, ensuring that only one switch transitions at any instant. As a result, switching losses is reduced which simplifies the control structure. A supervisory controller is designed to regulates DC-link voltage by dynamically coordinating source-side power. A decoupled load-side converter is used to ensures stable voltage and frequency for a wide variety of stand-alone loads. The system is modeled and simulated in MATLAB/Simulink, and its performance is validated through a laboratory prototype using a dSPACE controller. The hardware results are demonstrating the fast transient response, aligned with FSM behavior.

title: Market-Driven Collaborative Energy Management of Cloud Data Centers
authors: Niloofar Pourghaderi, Milad Kabirifar, Payman Dehghanian, David Celeita
abstract: This paper proposes a privacy-aware collaborative energy management framework for cloud data centers (CDCs) operating within an electricity market environment. Each CDC's energy agent (CDCEA) minimizes the total CDC's energy cost by optimizing its energy profile in response to dynamic locational marginal prices and the nodal carbon intensity of the power system issued by the independent system operator (ISO), who clears the energy market. CDCEAs integrate the on-site energy resources of CDCs, such as renewable energy and energy storage systems, and leverage the spatiotemporal flexibility of computing workloads through coordination with cloud service providers (CSPs). CSPs manage privacy-preserving workload scheduling and resource allocation by collaborating with associated CDCEAs and with other CSPs, while ensuring quality of service and adherence to service-level agreements. A two-stage optimization model is developed to coordinate decision-making between the ISO and CDCEAs in energy market in the first stage, and to address the coordination of CSPs with associated CDCEAs and other CSPs in the second stage. The proposed two-stage model is solved using a three-nested distributed iterative algorithm that combines privacy-aware hierarchical and peer-to-peer ADMM methods with an analytical target cascading (ATC) approach. The method is validated using a real-world case study in Northern Virginia, USA, demonstrating its effectiveness in reducing costs and enhancing sustainability.

title: Instance-dependent uniform tail bounds for empirical processes
authors: Sohail Bahmani
abstract: We formulate a uniform tail bound for empirical processes indexed by a class of functions, in terms of the individual deviations of the functions rather than the worst-case deviation in the considered class. The tail bound is established by introducing an initial “deflation” step to the standard generic chaining argument. The resulting tail bound is the sum of the complexity of the “deflated function class” in terms of a generalization of Talagrand’s γ functional, and the deviation of the function instance, both of which are formulated based on the natural seminorm induced by the corresponding Cramér functions. Leveraging another less demanding natural seminorm, we also show similar bounds, though with implicit dependence on the sample size, in the more general case where finite exponential moments cannot be assumed. We also provide approximations of the tail bounds in terms of the more prevalent Orlicz norms or their “incomplete” versions under suitable moment conditions.

title: Heterogeneous-Sensing Bionic Skin for 3D Force-Vector Measurement and Slip Characterization
authors: Fangfang Liu, Chennan Yang, Wenhao Yang, Shizhao Li, Han Zheng, Huining Zhao, Hongli Li, Haojie Xia
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: With the rapid development of flexible electronics and smart sensing technology, electronic skin has attracted increasing attention from academia and industry as a bionic sensing system. This paper presents a biomimetic skin system that integrates heterogeneous fiber Bragg grating (FBG) distribution with a bionic fingerprint structure to enable high-precision detection and identification of three-dimensional force vectors and slip states. The system eliminates the need for additional temperature-compensation components, while still effectively reducing the impact of environmental temperature variations. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed system exhibits good linear response characteristics for both normal and shear force measurements. The normal force measurement range is 0-5 N, with a resolution of 0.042 N and a sensitivity of 1.54 V/N. The resolution of the X-direction shear force is 0.023 N with a sensitivity of 3.79 V/N, while the Y-direction shear force achieves a resolution of 0.012 N and a sensitivity of 7.78 V/N. Furthermore, a Random Forest (RF) algorithm is applied to train and classify slip signals, which enables prediction accuracies exceeding 97.2% for slip direction and velocity. These results provide new insights and methods for improving the performance and expanding the functionality of electronic skin technology, supporting its broader application in intelligent sensing.

title: Shape oscillation and 3-D microstreaming profile of a phospholipid-coated microbubble attached to a wall_supp5-3676695.avi
authors: Klazina Kooiman
abstract: Ultrasound-activated microbubbles display diverse dynamics, including shape oscillations and microstreaming, both of which are important for applications in particle transport, drug delivery, and bacterial interventions. Despite advancements, a knowledge gap persists in our understanding of the correlation between shape oscillations, corresponding microstreaming and resulting mechanical impact to neighboring boundaries for microbubbles of clinically relevant sizes. This study investigates this correlation for biotinylated phospholipid-coated microbubbles with radii of 3.0-7.3 μm (N=79) when attached to a streptavidin-coated wall, imaged at 5 million and 10 thousand frames per second to visualize both shape oscillations and microstreaming. Driven at 1.25 MHz frequency, 25,000 cycles and varying peak negative acoustic pressures (170-420 kPa), the larger sized microbubbles showed higher shape modes (up to mode 6) than the smaller ones. A shape stability model predicted the dominant shape mode with an 89% agreement with experimental findings. Four distinct 3-D microstreaming patterns were observed by employing astigmatic particle tracking velocimetry: patternless, radial, dipole and quadrupole. Modal decomposition of shape oscillations revealed that the quadrupole pattern occurred from self-interaction of the dominant shape mode, while the dipole pattern resulted from two strongly interacting shape modes. The quantified microstreaming showed maximum velocities ranging from 2-10 mm/s, which decayed with increasing distance from the microbubble center. Notably, lower shape modes and the quadrupole pattern were associated with higher wall shear stress (up to 13 Pa). These unique 3-D microstreaming profiles have the potential to enhance the efficiency of therapeutic microbubble-mediated ultrasound applications through controlled shape oscillation and mechanical impact.

title: Z-Transform Model of a Coherent Receiver for Satellite-to-Ground Laser Links Under High Doppler Rates
authors: Sophonie Pech, Fabien Destic, Arnaud Dion, Angélique Rissons
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: Following the transition of terrestrial networks to optical fibers in the 1980s, optical laser links are increasingly being explored as an alternative to Radio Frequency (RF) communications in space, especially to address congestion in the microwave bands. For downlinks, free-space propagation remains a major challenge, mainly because of atmospheric turbulence. Coherent detection is becoming the preferred approach due to its superior overall performance. In Low Earth Orbit (LEO)-to-ground downlinks, an additional issue for coherent demodulation arises from the Doppler frequency drift caused by the orbital motion of the satellite.In this study, a Z-transform modeling of a coherent receiver based on a hybrid Optical-Digital Phase-Locked Loop (ODPLL) for Doppler compensation and frequency synchronization is proposed. In the envisioned architecture, the optical signals are processed in the analog domain by optoelectrical components, with no speed limitations other than propagation delays; in turn, the control for frequency tracking is performed in the digital domain, thus easing the design. The system latency is taken into account, in view of implementing the algorithm on a Field Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) and to later integrate the photonic elements to build the hardware receiver. The simulated behavioral model achieves successful Quadrature Phase-Shift Keying (QPSK) demodulation even when submitted to strong Doppler rates, and under realistic received power levels.

title: Research on 5G Traffic Prediction Method Based on VMD-XGBoost and Bootstrap
authors: Xiang Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: In order to improve the accuracy and reliability of 5G network traffic prediction, this study proposes a 5G traffic prediction framework based on Variational Mode Decomposition (VMD) and the XGBoost model. First, VMD is used to decompose 5G traffic data into intrinsic modes, extracting multi-scale features from the data. This process reduces noise influence and enhances the model's predictive capability. Next, the prediction performance of three machine learning models-XGBoost, Random Forest (RF), and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM)-is compared. The results show that XGBoost exhibits the highest predictive accuracy on the test set under all experimental conditions. Additionally, this study applies the Bootstrap method for interval prediction using the XGBoost model, assessing the prediction uncertainty under different confidence intervals. Experimental results demonstrate that combining XGBoost with the Bootstrap method effectively provides prediction intervals, achieving high prediction coverage and narrow interval width at various confidence levels. This study provides a new perspective for 5G traffic prediction and offers a research foundation for further improvements in network resource management and optimization.

title: Research on Optimization of Deep Learning Algorithms in the Effectiveness Analysis of Unmanned Aerial Vehicle Inspection Data
authors: Chengming Jin, Jinyang Zhao, Shuliang Zhang, Jin Tao, Tianxiao Li, Haodong Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: This study focuses on the optimization problem of deep learning algorithms in the performance analysis of unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) inspection data, and proposes a novel object detection algorithm that integrates self-supervised learning and efficient network structure design. The core innovation of this algorithm lies in the design of a two-stage optimization framework: Firstly, in the pre-training stage, an improved contrastive learning strategy is adopted to conduct self-supervised pre-training by taking advantage of the unlabeled nature of the inspection data itself, enabling the model to learn general visual representations with strong robustness to rotation, scale changes, etc., effectively alleviating the reliance on a large number of manual annotations. Secondly, in terms of the detection network structure, a lightweight backbone network was constructed and integrated with channel and spatial attention mechanisms, enabling the model to adaptively focus on the key areas in the image. Meanwhile, a novel attention-guided bidirectional feature pyramid fusion module was designed. This module utilizes the attention map generated by high-level semantic information to dynamically weight and enhance the detailed information in shallow features, thereby significantly improving the feature representation ability for minor defects. Through systematic experiments on the real unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) power inspection dataset, the results show that this algorithm demonstrates significant advantages in overall detection accuracy, especially in the recognition of small targets and partially occluded targets. Meanwhile, the model maintains low complexity and computational overhead, and achieves efficient inference speed on embedded hardware. It provides a high-precision and high-efficiency solution for the intelligent analysis of unmanned aerial vehicle inspection.

title: Simulation of Industrial System Fault Detection Based on Weak Signal Injection and Wavelet Time-Frequency Analysis
authors: Sijie Tao, Xiaoping Tian, Yunlong He, Xianwu Tang, Yuyang Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Early faults in industrial systems often manifest as faint signals drowned out background noise. Traditional detection methods struggle to achieve effective early warning due to low signal-to-noise ratios. This paper proposes a novel fault detection framework integrating active weak signal injection with multi-level signal processing. First, we inject specific-frequency weak diagnostic signals into the system, transforming the complex "fault pattern recognition" problem into the more manageable "known signal detection" problem. Subsequently, a multi-level signal processing workflow—including notch filtering, wavelet denoising, and time-frequency analysis—is designed to handle complex environments featuring both strong power-frequency interference and broadband noise. Cascaded notch filters precisely suppress strong power-frequency interference, while wavelet threshold denoising suppresses broadband Gaussian noise. Finally, a time-frequency distribution map is generated via wavelet transform time-frequency analysis, enabling intuitive identification and feature extraction of the weak injected signal from the input signal. MATLAB simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method can reliably extract weak signals from complex environments with signal-to-noise ratios as low as -80 dB, achieving signal-to-noise ratio improvements exceeding 35 dB. The presence of these signals is visually and accurately identified through the time-frequency map. This research not only significantly enhances detection sensitivity under low-SNR conditions but also provides a low-cost, highly reliable, and portable early fault detection solution for industrial equipment condition monitoring.

title: Coherent OQAM Optical Transmission using a Single Balanced Photodetector for High Sensitivity Satellite Optical Communication
authors: Young-Jin Hyun, Sang-Kook Han
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: In this study, coherent offset QAM transmission (OQAM) using the minimum Nyquist frequency (MNF) is proposed. Using MNF as the frequency offset, the phase of the local oscillator rotates 90 degrees over the half of the symbol duration. Due to the inherent half-symbol time offset between the in-phase and quadrature components in OQAM, each component can be separately detected using a single balanced photodetector at distinct time instances. This eliminates the need for an optical hybrid, simplifying the receiver architecture and improving PPB performance compared to conventional homodyne QAM systems. Theoretical analysis and numerical simulations were conducted to evaluate the bit error rate (BER) performance of the proposed scheme. Furthermore, a proof-of-concept experiment was carried out to validate the feasibility of the approach.

title: Research on Substation Construction Project Progress Prediction Based on Random Forest
authors: Chao Ge, Ping Zhang, Qi Xue, Jin Pan, Yishu Fu, Jiannan Li, Xiaodong Yang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: To address the issue of insufficient accuracy in schedule management for substation construction projects, a schedule prediction model based on random forests is proposed. First, by analyzing historical project data, a multi-dimensional feature system is constructed, including resource input, environmental factors, and construction complexity. Then, the random forest algorithm is used to handle the nonlinear relationships between features and to rank feature importance in order to optimize input variables. Experimental results show that compared with traditional methods, this model can effectively learn the impact of complex factors on the project timeline, significantly improving prediction accuracy and stability. This study provides a more reliable decision support tool for substation construction management, helping to achieve refined control of project schedules. Compared with existing methods, this model demonstrates superior ability to integrate multi-dimensional heterogeneous data and capture complex nonlinear interactions, offering a novel and interpretable tool for refined schedule management in substation construction.

title: Bibliometric Analysis of Deep Learning Applications in Breast Cancer Prediction: Trends, Tools and Future Directions
authors: Syambabu Badugu, V.D. Ambeth Kumar, R Jothikumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: The application of deep learning techniques to the prediction of breast cancer led to early detection, diagnostic accuracy, and better decision-making in clinical diagnosis. The increasing quantity of literature also hinders to identify the overall research, makers and technical trends in this field. This article provides an in-depth bibliometric analysis to characterize the thematic patterns of deep learning application in breast cancer prediction. Using articles indexed by selected scientific data bases the research analyses publications trends, author’s behavior, inter-institutional collaboration, the thematic co-occurrence of key words and methodological tools between 2012 and 2024. The results reveal rapid growth in the number of publications since 2017 with convolutional neural networks being the most common technique applied. Quantitative result show that the main contributing countries are the United States, China and India and the published research topics are image base diagnosis, data augmentation and transfer learning. Weaknesses in interdisciplinary collaboration and in the absence of real-life clinical validation were also identified. This study provides a data-driven overview that can serve as an enlightenment for the domain status, research hotspots, and future trend of improving the performance of deep learning-based breast cancer prediction. The novelty of this paper lies in quantifying global worldwide research activity in order to make evidence-based recommendations for how researchers, clinicians and policymakers may better support innovative and collaborative innovations.

title: A Hybrid PM Magnetic Gear With Efficient Utilization of Rare-Earth PMs
authors: Mojtaba Malakooti Khaledi, Seyed Ahmadreza Afsari Kashani
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: The most important factor that has led to the widespread use of magnetic gears and the ability of these structures to compete with their mechanical counterparts is the use of rare-earth permanent magnets. Despite the exceptional magnetic properties of rare earth permanent magnets, their high price, unstable supply chain, and price fluctuations are factors that have posed serious challenges to their use in magnetic gears. This study, using a new magnet arrangement and the simultaneous use of two types of rare-earth and non-rare-earth magnetic materials, has attempted to achieve the desired torque density while greatly reducing the final cost of the structure. It is worth noting that the new magnet arrangement, in addition to ensuring the achievement of the desired torque density at the lowest cost, has also brought about a reduction in the cogging torque. The proposed structure has been optimized using the genetic optimization algorithm and ANSYS Maxwell 2021-R1 finite element software, and detailed descriptions of all its electromagnetic characteristics have been provided. Comparison of simulation results of the proposed optimal structure with the conventional optimal structure has shown the superiority of the proposed structure.

title: GPU-Accelerated Multilayer Turbulence Simulation for Modeling of Space-to-Ground Optical Links
authors: Hamed Akhlaghi, Michael Taylor, Antony Orth, Steve Hranilovic
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: Satellite-to-ground free-space optical links are limited by atmospheric turbulence that distorts the received wave-front and induces scintillation. We present a GPU-accelerated multilayer turbulence simulator that models the dynamic, altitude-stratified propagation for space-to-ground link geometries. The simulator is based on standard Kolmogorov phase screen and split-step Fourier propagation, but implemented in TensorFlow for significant parallelization and accelerated computation time. The simulation stores propagation operators and covariance terms as GPU tensors to avoid transfer overhead and enable high throughput at significantly reduced computation time compared with freely available turbulence propagation tools. Using a single mid-range gaming GPU the simulator was able to achieve up to 310 times speed up over existing tools, with better scaling to larger grid sizes. This acceleration enables larger-aperture, long time series, and high resolution simulations of downlink geometries for machine-learning-driven adaptive optics and signal-processing mitigation in next-generation FSO systems.

title: Research on an Efficient Data Statistics Upgrade Algorithm Based on the Spark Framework
authors: Zhenlei Bao
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: With the wide application of various data collection systems, the traditional storage and computing architecture based on relational databases is facing severe challenges in dealing with the tens of gigabytes of daily incremental data generated by tens of millions of terminal devices. The simple SQL queries of this architecture are difficult to meet the complex statistical analysis requirements and are inefficient. Therefore, this study aims to explore a data query and statistics solution that is compatible with the existing SQL ecosystem and has high scalability and high performance. This paper proposes an efficient data statistics upgrade algorithm based on the Spark framework (ESUA). ESUA aims to systematically solve the three key bottlenecks of traditional Spark in large-scale statistical tasks: load imbalance caused by fixed task partition granularity, phase blocking and redundant computing caused by static DAG scheduling strategy, and memory waste and access delay caused by non-adaptive cache mechanism. The core of the algorithm builds a multi-level model integrating task partition optimization, dynamic DAG scheduling, cache gain evaluation and cost minimization decision, with the fundamental goal of minimizing the Spark computing cost function J through theoretical modeling. ESUA adopts a four-layer architecture of “collection-scheduling-computation-aggregation”, deeply integrated into the Spark SQL framework. Theoretically, it innovatively establishes a task partition and load balancing model, solving the optimal load distribution scheme through the Lagrange multiplier method; designs a dynamic DAG scheduling optimization mechanism, adjusting the task execution path based on real-time dependency relationships to minimize the global scheduling cost; and proposes an adaptive cache and incremental computing model, optimizing the cache strategy through the “access frequency-dependency depth” priority function and significantly reducing the repetitive computing cost by using differential updates. The experimental results show that in an 8node Spark cluster environment, ESUA performs better than the traditional Spark SQL solution when processing data. In most cases, it has superior performance, especially when dealing with large data volumes, providing a feasible technical solution for building a large-scale real-time analysis platform.

title: Benchmarking Machine Learning and Deep Learning Models for 60-min Ahead Blood Glucose Prediction in Type 1 Diabetes with Simulated and Clinical Data
authors: Zelin Shen
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Blood glucose (BG) management is crucial for patients with type 1 diabetes, requiring insulin dosage adjustments based on glucose levels to maintain concentrations within the target range. Accurate BG predictions provide insights into future glucose trends, enabling appropriate preventive measures to avoid short-term consequences or long-term complications. With the advancement of continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) systems, machine learning and deep learning techniques have been increasingly applied to improve prediction accuracy. This study systematically investigates and compares the prediction performance of six representative models for 60-minute ahead BG forecasting under a unified experimental framework. Three traditional machine learning models are evaluated, including Ridge Regression, Random Forest, and LightGBM, alongside three deep learning sequence models including LSTM, GRU, and Transformer. The models are assessed using both the T1DMS simulated dataset and the OhioT1DM clinical dataset from patients with type 1 diabetes. Results reveal fundamental performance differences between simulated and clinical environments. On the simulated dataset, all models achieve remarkably strong performance with minimal differentiation, indicating that simulated data systematically underestimates real-world physiological complexity by abstracting sensor noise, behavioral irregularity, and metabolic heterogeneity. In contrast, the clinical dataset exposes significant performance stratification across models. GRU demonstrates superior performance with RMSE of 30.90 ± 1.52 mg/dL, MAE of 22.58 ± 0.90 mg/dL, and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.6804 ± 0.1036, substantially outperforming Ridge Regression with RMSE of 35.61 ± 2.78 mg/dL, MAE of 26.22 ± 2.15 mg/dL, and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.5910 ± 0.0709. Notably, GRU surpasses LSTM on clinical data despite inferior simulated performance, suggesting its simpler gating mechanism provides enhanced regularization when confronting measurement uncertainty inherent in clinical CGM data. Transformer exhibits disproportionate degradation on clinical data, indicating vulnerability to irregular temporal patterns. This study demonstrates that recurrent neural networks, particularly GRU, achieve optimal clinical BG prediction performance. The findings emphasize the critical importance of clinical data validation for developing reliable diabetes management systems.

title: Metamaterial-Inspired Triple-Band Antenna for WLAN and 5G Sub-6 GHz Applications
authors: Deptansu Bhowmik, Kalyanbrata Ghosh, Arpan Shah
publish: 2026 Symposium on Microwave, Antenna, and Propagation (SMAP)
abstract: The accelerated research and development of wireless communication technologies has created an increasing demand for compact, high-performance, and multi-band antennas which are efficiently able to support multi-platform specific wireless standards. This paper shows a coplanar waveguide (CPW)-fed metamaterial inspired antenna, achieving triple-band operation. The designed antenna resonates at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.4 \text{GHz}, 4.8 \text{GHz}$</tex>, and 5.75 GHz, corresponding to ISM, WLAN, and sub- 6 GHz 5 G frequency bands, respectively. Administered on a Rogers RT/Duroid 5880 substrate with a compact footprint of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$20 \times 20 \times 1.57 ~\text{mm}^{3}$</tex>, the antenna incorporates in itself, inductive and capacitive loading within a planar structure. Simulation results demonstrate bandwidths of 210 MHz <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(2.32-2.53 \text{GHz}), 360 \text{MHz}(4.61-4.97 \text{GHz})$</tex>, and 320 Mhz <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(5.61-5.93 \text{GHz})$</tex>, respectively, and realized gains up to 4.3 dBi. Due to its miniaturized size, planar geometry, and good network and radiation characteristics, the designed antenna is an excellent contender for integration in IoT modules, WLAN routers, 5G mobile devices, and other multi-standard wireless communication systems.

title: Production Command Decision Support System for Complex Urban Power Grid Based on Multi-objective Optimization Algorithm
authors: Xinge Hu, Mingyuan Shang, Shengya Qiao, Wangwei Ji, Xinru Zhao
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: In the process of urbanization, complex urban power grids are faced with difficulties such as severe load fluctuation and frequent access of distributed power sources. This article is devoted to designing a decision support system (DSS) for complex urban power grid production command based on multi-objective optimization algorithm (MOOA). Based on the multi-objective genetic algorithm (MOGA), a multi-objective optimization framework for complex urban power grids is proposed and embedded in DSS. The performance of the system is verified by simulation experiments. The results show that compared with the traditional single-objective optimization method, the method based on MOGA improves the availability of power supply by about 2.5% and reduces carbon emissions by 14.3%. Although the cost of power generation has increased slightly (about 5%), the overall benefit has improved significantly. The customer satisfaction survey shows that the system is highly recognized in terms of intelligence level and response speed. This article provides scientific basis and technical support for intelligent management of complex urban power grids, and will further explore the application potential of new optimization algorithms in the future.

title: Active Frequency Regulation Strategy for Electrode Boiler Load Based on Current-Type Virtual Synchronous Machine
authors: Juntao Xu, Rende Zhao, Zhiqiang Feng, Jiabin Xu, Xiao Yin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy (ICSGE)
abstract: To address the issues of slow frequency response, lack of inertia, and grid unfriendliness in traditional electrode boilers, this study proposes an active frequency regulation strategy for electrode boiler loads based on a current-type virtual synchronous machine. By incorporating a square-wave voltage regulator converter with a cascaded H-bridge between the grid and the electrode boiler load, the power output is adjusted by regulating the boiler voltage. A current-type virtual synchronous machine and PR control are employed to provide virtual inertia for the system, thereby enhancing grid stability. Second, a mathematical model of the electrode boiler is constructed to simulate dynamic load variations. Finally, simulation results demonstrate that the proposed control strategy can promptly adjust load power during sudden grid demand changes while providing inertia and damping support to the grid, delivering fast, accurate, and grid-friendly frequency regulation services.

title: Design and Application of Sliding Mode Controller for Roller Microporous System Based on Disturbance Observer
authors: Liu Yingming, Sun Chang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: In order to improve the nonlinear control performance of roll gap structure, the differential geometric feedback technology was introduced to complete the linear decoupling process. According to the sliding mode control function of disturbance observer, the sliding mode controller design of roll micro-hole system based on disturbance observer was established and its application verification was carried out. The control system can achieve ideal tracking performance and effectively compensate the errors caused by the gap, which indicates that the control algorithm designed has a strong feasibility. The sliding mode control simulation test of the disturbance observer is carried out, and the effectiveness of the synovial controller is judged according to the actual test results, which can realize the function of tracking trajectory accurately. The application verification shows that the rolling error of the modified rolling mill can be reduced, and the sliding mode control method is helpful to achieve higher qualified rate of rolling blades. It is a great significance to improve the rolling quality of sheet metal and has great practical application and popularization value.

title: Influence of Winding Arrangement on Precision of Limited Length Linear VR Resolver
authors: Mahboobe Mousavi, Farid Tootoonchian
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: This paper introduces various configurations of the three-layer overlapping winding in linear variable reluctance (LVR) resolvers. To evaluate the impact of the longitudinal end effect, each configuration is examined under three distinct scenarios: (i) both stator and mover assumed to be of infinite length, (ii) a finite mover length with an infinite stator, and (iii) finite lengths for both components. A two-dimensional, time-stepping finite element analysis (2D-TSFEA) is utilized to calculate the position error in each case. The results reveal that the optimal winding configuration varies depending on the scenario considered.

title: Electric Field Analysis of Typical GIS Defects Based on Finite Element Simulation
authors: Huajie Wu, Lingyan Lin
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Gas-insulated equipment (GIS, GIL), being critical components in UHV transmission networks and modern power systems, have insulation reliability that directly affects grid security and stable operation. This study addresses key issues including localized electric field distortion and insulation degradation caused by typical defects in UHV gas-insulated equipment. A three-dimensional electric field model was developed, incorporating four types of insulation defects: free metallic particles, metallic contaminants on insulator surfaces, conductor protrusions, and interfacial voids in insulators. Through numerical simulations, the spatial electric field distributions under normal operation and various defect conditions were accurately obtained. Results demonstrate that in defect-free equipment, the highest electric field concentration occurs at the triple junctions where shielding electrodes contact SF6 gas and insulator surfaces. The introduction of defects significantly distorts the field distribution, with free metallic particles producing the most severe field enhancement and posing the greatest threat to insulation integrity. This research systematically reveals the impact mechanisms of typical defects on electric field distribution in UHV gas-insulated equipment, evaluates the severity of different defect types, and provides critical data and theoretical support for structural optimization, insulation condition assessment, and maintenance strategy development using on-site detection techniques such as UHF method, gas composition analysis, and pulse current method.

title: Fuzzy-Assisted GA and PSO Tuning of PI Controllers for Induction Motor Speed Regulation
authors: Raouf Sirjani
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: This study focuses on improving the automatic tuning of a discrete PI speed controller used in induction motor (IM) drives. To explore different optimization strategies, two well-known meta-heuristic approaches—Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) and Genetic Algorithm (GA)—are applied and compared under identical operating conditions. Alongside these methods, a simple fuzzy supervisor with a single input is introduced to support the conventional PI controller.The performance evaluation relies on a cost function that considers integral absolute error (IAE), steady-state error (SSE), and overshoot (OS), aiming to strike a balance between fast response and steady-state accuracy. The system model was implemented in MATLAB/Simulink with a fixed time step of 5 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-5</sup> s over a 10-second experiment, including a step reference of 1500 rpm and a step load torque.According to the results, PSO tuning yields Kp = 0.1459 and Ki = 41.090, leading to OS ≈ 5.15%, SSE ≈ 16.09 rpm, and IAE ≈ 2.95 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sup> rpm•s. The GA method achieves P = 0.5964 and I = 0.7122, which yields the same overshoot and steady-state error as the other method, but with a significantly lower IAE of approximately 4.48 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> rpm•s, representing a reduction of about 98.5%. Furthermore, the fuzzy supervisor (utilizing triangular 7-MFs and a diagonal rule base) enhances the static accuracy, reducing SSE to approximately 6.56 rpm and maintaining OS at around 5.67%.The results show that meta-heuristic PI tuning is effective and reliable. In this case, GA had the lowest IAE, and the lightweight fuzzy layer made things work better in steady state without making the calculations much harder. All the important parameters and plots are provided to facilitate easy replication of the study.

title: High-precision Real-time Angle Measurement Based on TMR Magnetic Sensors
authors: Tianxu Xie, Zhuolun Cai, Chuan Yang, Yan Li, Wei Gong, Yong Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: Accurate and reliable angle and rotational speed measurement is crucial in various industrial and scientific applications. This paper develops a cost-effective, real-time system for angle and rotational speed measurement Using magnetic sensors. The setup comprises a magnetic sensor arranged around a turntable with a DC motor-driven magnet. Sensor signals are amplified and converted to digital via the ADC of an STM controller, with a high-precision timer recording relevant signal intervals. Processed data are framed per the USART protocol for transmission to a host computer. At the software layer, callback functions identify frame headers/tails for data extraction, filter signals via a low-pass filter to reduce noise, and enable real-time angular position display. System accuracy is validated through mathematical fitting to model and calibrate the sensor output-physical angle relationship, ensuring high precision and reliability with a measurement error as low as 0.6 °.

title: Software Design and Implementation of Multi-Module Collaborative Interaction System Based on STM32
authors: Zhang Luopu, Yang Pengju
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: In response to the common problems of high module coupling, poor real-time performance, and stability in multi module collaborative interaction in embedded communication systems, this paper designs a software solution for Multi-module collaboration based on STM32 controller, achieving efficient collaboration of GPRS communication, touch screen, speech recognition, and broadcasting modules. Firstly, in terms of serial communication, configure DMA channels and circular data buffers for each serial port to avoid real-time loss caused by polling waiting; In terms of interrupt handling mechanism, a multi interrupt nested architecture is implemented through a layered design that minimizes interrupt content and priority, avoiding data confusion caused by conflicts between different levels of interrupts; In terms of human-computer interaction mechanism, diversified and efficient matching of user instructions and system states is achieved through the design of multi-modal input of screen and voice, and the eventization of user input. The experimental results show that the software scheme has achieved stable communication among various modules, reducing communication delay by 80%, proving the reliability of the scheme and meeting the multi-scenario requirements of embedded systems. This study advances the technical solution for multi-module interaction and high-stability communication coordination optimization in demanding embedded scenarios, providing a reusable software architecture framework for multi-module system design in fields such as intelligent terminals and industrial control.

title: Design and Implementation of a Heterogeneous Network Communication Gateway for Industrial Control Systems Based on APL
authors: Bingjun Yan, Mingxu Gang, Bo Hu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: Aiming at the interconnection bottleneck between APL (advanced physical layer) communication network and traditional can/RS485 heterogeneous devices in industrial scene, a heterogeneous network communication gateway for industrial control system based on APL is designed and implemented. Based on the FreeRTOS embedded real-time operating system, the gateway realizes the protocol conversion and real-time transmission of multi-source heterogeneous device data by optimizing the APL data frame structure adapted to IEEE 802.3 protocol and adopting the slot multiplexing scheduling strategy. Finally, the simulation test platform is built to test and verify. The test results show that the transmission packet loss rate of the communication gateway is less than $0.01 \%$, and the transmission delay is less than 150 us, which meets the requirements of industrial real-time and reliability, and effectively solves the problem of heterogeneous network data interaction.

title: Synergizing Spatial and Frequency Domains: A Wavelet-Integrated Hybrid Attention Network for SISR
authors: Wenji Yin, Yu Han, Xiaoqi Xi, Linlin Zhu, Zhicun Zhang, Guanpeng Yan, Yanyu Guan, Chunhui Wang, Lei Li, Bin Yan
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Most single image super-resolution (SISR) methods operate in the spatial domain, yet frequency-domain analysis has proven critical in related restoration tasks. We experimentally find that the distribution gap between high-resolution (HR) and low-resolution (LR) images is more distinct in high-frequency components. This motivates a dedicated focus on high frequencies for SISR. To address high-frequency detail recovery and long-range dependency modeling, we propose the Progressive Frequency-Wavelet Hybrid Attention Transformer (PFW-HAT). Its core component, the Spatial-Frequency Synergistic Group (SFSG), cascades a Residual Hybrid Attention Group (RHAG) with a Progressive Frequency Block. RHAG captures spatial context via window attention, while the Frequency Block uses Discrete Wavelet Transform (DWT) to decouple features into structural (low-frequency) and textural (high-frequency) components. A low-frequency branch applies channel attention for global consistency, and a high-frequency branch combines channel and self-attention to sharpen details. A cross-frequency interaction mechanism, guided by a Frequency-Aware Loss, ensures spectral consistency. Experimental results validate the compelling performance of our proposed method.

title: QFed: Parameter-Compact Quantum-Classical Federated Learning
authors: Samar Abdelghani, Soumaya Cherkaoui
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: Organizations and enterprises across domains such as healthcare, finance, and scientific research are increasingly required to extract collective intelligence from distributed, siloed datasets while adhering to strict privacy, regulatory, and sovereignty requirements. Federated Learning (FL) enables collaborative model building without sharing sensitive raw data, but faces growing challenges posed by statistical heterogeneity, system diversity, and the computational burden from complex models. This study examines the potential of quantum-assisted federated learning, which could cut the number of parameters in classical models by polylogarithmic factors and thus lessen training overhead. Accordingly, we introduce QFed, a quantum-enabled federated learning framework aimed at boosting computational efficiency across edge device networks. We evaluate the proposed framework using the widely adopted FashionMNIST dataset. Experimental results show that QFed achieves a 77.6 % reduction in the parameter count of a VGG-like model while maintaining an accuracy comparable to classical approaches in a scalable environment. These results point to the potential of leveraging quantum computing within a federated learning context to strengthen FL capabilities of edge devices.

title: OW-LoRA:Orthogonal Weight-decomposed Low-Rank Adaptation
authors: Xue Yu, Xiang Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Large pre-trained models are adapted to downstream tasks under limited computational resources by using parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT), which decreases the number of parameters that need to be trained.Among existing methods, Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) is widely adopted because it introduces no additional cost during inference.However, its performance still falls behind that of full fine-tuning (FT).From a weight-space perspective, we analyze the geometric properties of weight updates in full fine-tuning. We observe that its advantage lies not only in flexible magnitude adjustment, but also in the effective organization of weight directions in high-dimensional space. Based on this insight, Orthogonal Weight-decomposed Low-Rank Adaptation (OW-LoRA) is introduced to integrate orthogonal weight decomposition with low-rank, providing an efficient approach for fine-tuning large models.In OW-LoRA, the weights from pre-training are divided into separate magnitude and direction parts, and orthogonality constraints are enforced on the directional component. This design improves the modeling of directional updates while maintaining high parameter efficiency. On the VTAB benchmark, OW-LoRA is shown to outperform other LoRA-based approaches on a wide range of tasks and model types, while also reducing the difference in performance between parameter-efficient and full fine-tuning.

title: Sentiment Analysis and Opinion Segmentation of Tourist Feedback in the Moroccan Tourism Sector Using Machine Learning and Deep Learning Approach
authors: Rachid Larhmouch, Hanae Moussaoui, Nabil El Akkad
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: With the growing digitalization of travel experiences, online reviews and social media posts have become crucial sources of insight into tourist satisfaction and perception. This study aims to analyze and segment the opinions of travelers regarding tourism in Morocco by leveraging machine learning and deep learning techniques in the field of sentiment analysis. By collecting and preprocessing usergenerated content from various platforms including travel forums, booking sites, and social networks we apply a comparative framework involving traditional machine learning classifiers (such as Support Vector Machines and Naive Bayes) and deep learning architectures (including LSTM, CNN, and BERT). The goal is not only to classify sentiments (positive, negative, neutral) but also to identify and segment key aspects of the tourism experience such as hospitality, infrastructure, cultural heritage, and security based on the opinions expressed. The experimental results demonstrate the superior performance of deep learning models, particularly transformer-based approaches, in capturing nuanced and context-dependent sentiments. This project is part of a broader effort to support the Moroccan government in the development of the Smart City initiative in Fez, by providing actionable insights into tourist experiences. The findings aim to contribute to data-driven policymaking and the enhancement of urban services in line with the smart tourism vision.

title: A Small Object Detection Algorithm for UAV Aerial Photography with Improved YOLOv11n
authors: Junjie Sun, Shiyu Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of inconsistent changes in the scale of small target features, fuzzy details, poor convergence performance of localization, as well as the susceptibility to misdetection and omission of detection in UAV aerial photography missions, an improved YOLOv11n algorithm for detecting small targets in UAV aerial photography is proposed. First, the C3k2 module is improved by Iterative Attentional Feature Fusion (iAFF) with nested attention mechanism and feature-weighted fusion, which realizes the efficient fusion of semantically and spatially inconsistent features and improves the processing capability of multi-scale information; second, the neck Secondly, the Transformer Feature Encoder (TFE) and Scale Sequential Feature Fusion (SSFF) modules are introduced into the neck feature fusion network, which improve the recognition of image details and the expression ability of detailed features by fusing feature maps at different scales. Finally, Focal Weighted Intersection over Union (Focal-WIoU) is used to replace the CIoU loss function in the original model, which enhances the localization performance of the model while accelerating the convergence speed, and accurately identifies the targets that are missed or misdetected by YOLOv11n. The experimental results show that the P, R, mAP50, and mAP50-95 are improved by 1.1%, 2.4%, 2.7%, and 1.5%, respectively, over the baseline model on the public dataset VisDrone2019. In addition, the generalization experiments on the RSOD and SIMD datasets also show that the improved model improves P, mAP50, and mAP50-95 by 1.3%, 0.5%, and 0.8%, respectively, on the RSOD dataset, and by 5%, 2.8%, and 1.6%, respectively, on the SIMD dataset, which sufficiently validates the validity and applicability of the model.

title: Student Employers: Your Work Matters.
authors: Robert Fricke

title: Stacked Intelligent Metasurfaces-based Joint Source-Channel Coding for Image Transmissions
authors: Inhyeok Choi, Shun Kojima
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: This paper proposes a joint source–channel coding (JSCC) framework based on a stacked intelligent metasurface (SIM)-enabled electromagnetic neural network (EMNN) with residual connections for image reconstruction over wireless channels. A residual connection is embedded in the multi-layer SIM architecture to alleviate the vanishing gradient problem inherent in deep EMNNs. Building on this structure, we formulate a joint optimization problem that minimizes the reconstruction error and devise a training algorithm that jointly updates the amplitudes and phases of the elements in both the transmitter and receiver SIMs via error backpropagation grounded in a physical wave-propagation model. Simulation results on the MNIST dataset show that the proposed residual SIM architecture enables high-quality image reconstruction even under low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) conditions and significantly outperforms a standard SIM design without residual connections, demonstrating enhanced robustness against channel noise.

title: Enabling Robust Radar Signal Deinterleaving via Multimodal and Continual Learning
authors: Qilin Yang, Xiangmao Chang, Jun Huang, Jingjing Gu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Realistic radar deployments operate in dense, dynamic electromagnetic environments where pulses from multiple emitters overlap in time and frequency, and the active emitter set changes as radars are activated, reconfigured, or repositioned. As a foundational step in radar signal processing, radar signal deinterleaving (RSD) faces fundamental challenges in such environments due to obscured emitter identities and the demand for real-time adaptivity. In this paper, we propose a robust deinterleaving method that integrates multimodal encoders with class-incremental learning (CIL), enabling accurate emitter identification with high precision, strong generalization, and adaptivity in evolving electromagnetic environments. Our approach jointly models intrapulse and interpulse features by transforming each radar pulse into a spectrogram and a structured descriptor set, which are encoded using pretrained backbones and then fused into a shared embedding space via contrastive learning. To support continual emitter learning at low memory and computational costs, we introduce a lightweight CIL module that integrates pseudo-feature replay, asymmetric supervised contrastive loss, and a cosine classifier with learnable scaling. On a simulated dataset built from real emitter parameters, our method maintains stable deinterleaving performance as new emitters gradually emerge under continual class expansion.

title: Pre-Matching Enhanced Hierarchical Double Auction for Collaborative Task Offloading in Vehicle-Edge-Cloud Systems
authors: Kangli Zhao, Xincao Xu, Penglin Dai, Huanlai Xing, Xiao Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Edge computing holds great potential to enhance the real-time capabilities of intelligent vehicles by deploying resources at the network edge. However, due to the dynamic and heterogeneous nature of vehicular networks, several challenges remain. These include workload imbalances caused by the interplay between mobility patterns and varying resource availability, the fluctuating decision spaces resulting from the continuous entry and exit of vehicles, and the absence of effective incentive mechanisms for resource preservation. To address these challenges, this paper formulates the task offloading and resource allocation (TSRM) problem within the vehicle-edge cloud cooperation framework by characterizing transmission and computation times accounting for the characteristics of heterogeneous resources and vehicle mobility. We prove that this problem is NP-hard, with the objective of optimizing the cumulative task service ratio to enhance overall system efficiency. We propose a Pre-Matching enhanced Hierarchical Double Auction (PMHDA) algorithm based on hierarchical auction and matching theory, which consists of two main auctions: the vehicle-edge auction (VEA) and the edge-cloud auction (ECA). In the VEA, client vehicles must decide whether to process tasks locally or delegate them to server vehicles or edge nodes. A pre-matching algorithm is designed to identify the optimal offloading candidates, taking into account vehicle mobility and dynamic network conditions. In the ECA, edge nodes determine whether to process tasks locally or forward them to other edge nodes or the cloud. Extensive simulations on real-world datasets demonstrate that PMHDA provides substantial service-rate gains over baseline methods, achieving an average improvement of 75.47% under diverse scenarios.

title: Integrating Voltage Stability into the Static Security Region: A Unified Analytical Framework
authors: Rafael Alberto García Tinoco, João Alberto Passos Filho, Jonathan Muñoz Tabora, Maximiliano Martínez
abstract: The increasing penetration of distributed and renewable generation has driven modern power systems closer to their technical limits, making voltage instability a key threat to grid reliability and reducing the operational margin for preventive control and generation redispatch. Traditional steady-state security tools, such as the Static Security Region (SSR), map feasible operating points under steady-state constraints but do not quantify proximity to voltage collapse. This paper proposes an enhanced SSR that integrates a fast, analytically derived Voltage Stability Index (VSI) based on a Thevenin equivalent and load-impedance characteristics, embedding stability-margin information directly into SSR nomograms. The resulting visualization jointly depicts feasibility and voltage-stability robustness, enabling screening and ranking of dispatch points beyond voltage-magnitude compliance. Case studies on the IEEE 9-bus and New England 39-bus systems show that the VSI-based SSR highlights dispatch-dependent low-margin regions that can occur before conventional thermal or voltage limits are reached. Overall, the method provides an efficient, interpretable tool for steady-state security assessment and preventive decision support.

title: Optimal Speed and Charging Control for Electric Buses: A Multiscale MPC-Based Framework
authors: G. Basile, A. Bozzi, S. Bracco, C. Pasquale, S. Sacone, S. Siri, A. Ferrara
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology
abstract: This article presents a control framework designed to manage the speed, dwell time, and charging schedules of electric, automated, and connected buses operating on mixed-traffic lines without reserved lanes. The control architecture is composed of two layers, each defined by distinct objectives, levels of detail, and time scales, resulting in a multiscale control framework. The high-level control layer periodically solves a multiobjective optimal control problem to ensure the adequate transport service while considering a prediction of traffic conditions along the bus line. In contrast, the low-level control layer employs a model predictive control to accurately track the high-level control actions. The MPC-based low-level controller accounts for the detailed dynamic behavior of the bus, ensuring robust execution of the high-level directives. The effectiveness of the proposed control scheme is assessed through its application to a realistic case study in Italy, demonstrating improved service reliability and energy efficiency.

title: WG-Net: Wireframe Generation From Noisy Point Cloud by Edge Primitive Fitting
authors: Yike Xu, Li Cao, Jianwei Guo, Wenjun Xie, Xiaoping Liu
abstract: 3-Dscanning has various applications in industrial design. However, the noisy point clouds being generated are difficult to be used in the applications directly. The ease of editing and lightweight nature of wireframes make it highly suitable for industrial design. To address this issue, WG-Net is proposed for generating wireframes from noisy point clouds. An edge primitive detection network that includes a multilevel feature extraction module and a feature fusion module is designed for point classification, primitive segmentation, and displacement vector prediction. Based on the classification and segmentation results of edge points, targeted fitting methods are applied to accurately fit each category of edge primitives, ensuring precise geometric representation. Finally, fitting results are integrated into a complete wireframe structure. Through extensive experiments on computer-aided design datasets, WG-Net achieved significant performance improvements compared to state-of-the-art methods. In addition, we have verified the feasibility and practical applicability of WG-Net on real scanned data.

title: CoShMDM: Contact and Shape-Aware Latent Motion Diffusion Model for Human Interaction Generation_supp2-3675725.mp4
authors: Ali Asghar Manjotho
abstract: Generating realistic two-person interaction motions from text holds immense potential in computer vision and animations. While existing latent motion diffusion models offer compact and efficient representations, they often fail to produce physically plausible contacts and are typically constrained to a single canonical body shape. As a result, the generated motion sequences exhibit substantial mesh penetrations and lack interaction realism. To address these limitations, we propose a contact and shape-aware latent motion representation and diffusion model (CoShMDM) for generating realistic two-person interactions from text. Our framework begins by constructing contact-compatible motion using SMPL-based meshes and a normal alignment-based mesh contact matrix to capture fine-grained mesh-level contacts. To account for shape diversity, we incorporate SMPL shape parameters and iteratively learn contact dynamics across different body shapes. Additionally, a reinforcement learning-based mesh penetration avoidance policy network, guided by signed distance fields, is introduced to minimize mesh penetrations while preserving contact fidelity and shape-aware motion. We further employ a dual-encoder VQ-VAE to learn disentangled latent representations for motion and contacts, which are then utilized in a text- and body-shape-conditioned diffusion model. To ensure spatial, temporal, and semantic coherence, we integrate a novel contact and motion consistency module into the diffusion transformer.
