Extensive evaluations on the InterHuman and InterX datasets demonstrate that our method outperforms state-of-the-art approaches achieving lowest FID scores (4.801 and 0.013), with 19% and 17.3% reductions in mesh penetrations, and 17.8% and 33.2% gains in contact similarity, respectively.

title: A Lightweight Design of True Random Number Generator Based on Superparamagnetic Tunnel Junction
authors: You Wang, Chaoyue Zhang, Yefan Xu, Yu Gong, Shouzhong Peng, Yue Zhang, Yijun Cui, Weiqiang Liu
abstract: With the rapid development of artificial intelligence (AI), a variety of attack techniques have emerged, threatening the data security. In this background, energy-efficient lightweight security primitives are required to guarantee the security of the Internet of Things (IoT). The conventional silicon-based true random number generator (TRNG) suffers from design complexity, high power consumption, and limited throughput, making it unsuitable for low-power and high-performance applications. Magnetic tunnel junctions (MTJs) can be used as a random source due to the random switching behaviors. However, most of the existing MTJ-based TRNG designs rely on reset–write–read operations, which limit throughput and energy efficiency. In this work, we propose a high-throughput TRNG based on the superparamagnetic tunnel junction (SMTJ). By reducing the thermal stability factor, SMTJ undergoes spontaneous random switching driven by thermal fluctuation, eliminating the need for costly reset and write operations. A compact spin-orbit torque (SOT)-controlled SMTJ model is developed and integrated by using the <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">nand</small>-SPIN structure, achieving a TRNG design with low power, high throughput, and high randomness.

title: An Integrated Design and Simulation Framework for Hybrid Electric Vehicle: A Case Study on the Logan Vehicle Platform
authors: AmirHossein Azad, David Flynn
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive design and simulation framework for a parallel hybrid electric vehicle (HEV) based on the Logan vehicle platform. The process involves addressing various challenges, including selecting the optimal configuration, modeling each element, choosing the best approach from multiple angles, and applying an effective control strategy for managing the energy system. A parallel hybrid configuration was chosen for this study. Subsequently, the electric drivetrain, which includes the internal combustion engine, electric motor, control strategy, and lithium-polymer battery pack with a five-stage constant-current charging method, was modeled and simulated using ADVISOR and MATLAB/Simulink. A modified competitive neural networkbased control method was used to manage the electric motor, enabling effective drive control. This, combined with an optimized rule-based energy management system, ensured improved system performance. High-fidelity models and real-world data were used throughout to ensure the accuracy of the results. The integrated system was evaluated using the CYC_UDDS urban driving cycle. Simulation results confirmed the system's performance and accuracy under realistic driving conditions.

title: Deep Learning-Based Cryptojacking Detection in Cloud Containerized Environments
authors: Ahmed Hussain, Javier Flores, Panos Papadimitratos
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: Cryptojacking attacks in cloud Docker environments exploit computational resources for unauthorized cryptocurrency mining, degrading performance and increasing operational costs. Existing detection methods suffer from performance overhead, the inability to detect obfuscated activities, or the detection of runtime attacks. We present a lightweight Deep Learning (DL) framework analyzing resource utilization patterns for cryptojacking detection in controlled experimental environments. Our methodology employs temporal feature extraction from cloud Virtual Machine (VM) metrics (i.e., CPU, memory, disk I/O, and network utilization) and evaluates four popular DL architectures against direct mining and obfuscated scenarios. Systematic evaluation across Bitcoin, Ethereum, Shiba Inu, and Monero demonstrates that four architectures achieve <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 0}$</tex> accuracy in our experimental setup. The optimal CNN architecture utilizes <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4, 5 4 8}$</tex> parameters, maintains <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>0.97$</tex> precision in obfuscated scenarios, and achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<0.001 \%$</tex> false positive rates. The framework enables efficient cryptojacking detection in containerized environments with minimal computational overhead while maintaining robust security capabilities under controlled evaluation conditions.

title: Electromagnetic Compatibility Study of TEC-Based Active Cooling Systems
authors: Ximing Gao, Lei Wang, Zhiwei Mao, Wei Luo, Jiguang Zhao, Bing Tian, Liang Zhao, Xiaotong Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy (ICSGE)
abstract: Quantum current sensors, owing to their nanoampere-level measurement precision, exhibit significant application potential in complex electromagnetic environments such as power systems. However, in the active thermal management systems employed for these sensors, magnetic interference generated by thermoelectric cooler (TEC) can substantially degrade the signal-to-noise ratio. To address this magneto-thermal coupling issue, this study proposes a co-design approach integrating a magnetic shielding enclosure with the active cooling system. Through multiphysics simulations and structural optimization, the design achieves dual objectives of magnetic field suppression and efficient heat dissipation.A comprehensive thermal model incorporating a TEC, heat pipes, thermal interface materials, and insulation layers was established, while a high-permeability permalloy shell was designed for magnetic shielding. The effects of the shell thickness, geometric configuration, and spatial arrangement of the cooling components on shielding performance were systematically analyzed. Results indicate that a truncated triangular pyramid opening structure can reduce TEC-induced magnetic interference from 1.6 μT to 0.078 μT, outperforming the design target by an order of magnitude. Meanwhile, the implementation of a "square-loop" heat pipe configuration combined with thermal insulation filling maintains the sensor temperature at 29 °C under a heat load of 6 W and an ambient temperature of 85 °C during continuous operation.The proposed design provides theoretical and engineering guidance for deploying high-precision quantum sensors in harsh electromagnetic environments. Future research will focus on extending the proposed design to wider temperature ranges, developing dynamic magnetic-field compensation strategies, and integrating in-situ temperature sensing for adaptive thermal control, thereby further improving system robustness and applicability in weak-magnetic-field measurement scenarios such as smart grids.

title: Impact of Bridge and Linear De-Energized Tap Changer on Transformer Short-Circuit Impedance: An Analytical Approach
authors: Mahsa Taghilou, Mojtaba Mirsalim
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: De-energized tap changers (DETCs) offer a cost-effective means of voltage regulation in transformers with steady load conditions, serving as an alternative to on-load tap changers (OLTCs). Unlike separate DETC units, integrated DETC designs are embedded into the main winding structure, where the introduction of air gaps results in non-uniform ampere-turn distributions. This structural difference alters the impedance profile, increasing radial leakage reactance. Elevated impedance can enhance eddy current and stray losses, thereby raising overall load losses and operating temperatures, which may necessitate additional cooling provisions and impact long-term reliability. This paper investigates the impact of integrated linear and bridge DETC configurations on transformer impedance. An analytical modeling framework is proposed for both configurations, with impedance behavior evaluated to highlight performance and design implications.

title: Research on Load Forecasting Algorithm in Power Grid Investment Planning
authors: Xue Ding, Yangyang Luo, Hao Zhou, Zhe Chen, Hui Luo
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: With the continuous growth of power demand and the increasing scale of power grid, accurate load forecasting is very important for the planning, construction and operation of power grid. The purpose of this article is to discuss the application and importance of load forecasting algorithm in power grid investment planning. In this article, BP neural network (BPNN) is used as the load forecasting algorithm, and a load forecasting model is constructed by collecting and processing historical load data, economic indicators, weather data and other multi-source information. By forecasting the daily load of a regional power grid in the next year and comparing it with the actual load, the performance of BPNN model in load forecasting is verified. The results show that the BPNN model can accurately predict the future load trend and capture the influence of various factors on the load, and the predicted results are not much different from the actual values. The error of the forecast results is also analyzed, and the error of most forecast days is within a reasonable range. The proposed load forecasting algorithm plays an important role in power grid investment planning. In the future, we should continue to optimize the algorithm model to provide more powerful support for power grid investment planning.

title: Application of Particle Swarm Optimization-Based Intelligent Prediction Model for Short-Term Load Forecasting in Power Systems
authors: Ding Song, Chenglin Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Accurate short-term electricity load forecasting is vital for power system scheduling. It ensures economic and efficient grid operations. However, load data exhibits strong nonlinearity and is heavily influenced by meteorological and temporal factors. These characteristics pose significant challenges for conventional forecasting methods. A novel shortterm load forecasting method is proposed in this paper. It utilizes Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) to optimize Support Vector Machine (SVM) parameters, resulting in the PSO-SVM model. This method is designed to address the limitations of conventional SVM models. In these models, accuracy is constrained by the empirical selection of the penalty parameter and the kernel function parameter. The proposed approach begins with the removal of outliers from the raw load data. The data are then normalized to enhance its stability. The global search capability of PSO is then employed to adaptively optimize the key parameters of the SVM. This process eliminates the need for manual trial-and-error selection. Finally, the method is validated using actual load data from a specific region. Performance is assessed using metrics such as Mean Squared Error (MSE), Root Mean Square Error (RMSE), and Mean Absolute Error (MAE). The experimental results show that the MSE is reduced from 0.007347 to 0.000049, the RMSE from 0.085713 to 0.006968, and the MAE from 0.059170 to 0.002989 by the PSO-optimized SVM model. These results indicate a significant improvement in predictive accuracy compared to the conventional SVM model. Superior performance is demonstrated in capturing load fluctuations. This effectively validates its advantages in short-term load forecasting for power systems. The method provides more reliable technical support for scheduling decisions.

title: Data Mining and Risk Early Warning Model Construction in the Process of Power Grid Investment Conversion
authors: Jing Xia, Zhaoxu Zhang, Nan Song, Po Hu
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: Factors such as market fluctuation, policy change and technology update bring multiple risks, which affect the investment benefit and the safety of enterprise assets. This study seeks to enhance the efficiency of decision-making and the effectiveness of risk management in power grid investment and capital allocation by leveraging data mining techniques and a risk early warning framework. A hybrid approach combining decision tree analysis with association rule mining is proposed and developed. Considering the multidimensional, complex, and nonlinear characteristics of investment data in the power grid, a decision tree is built using the C4.5 algorithm, while the Apriori algorithm is employed to uncover association rules among data. These extracted rules highlight crucial risk drivers—such as the asset-liability ratio, current ratio, and fluctuations in market demand—offering valuable data support for establishing the warning model. Furthermore, a multivariate risk early warning model is designed with a dynamic weight adjustment mechanism. By applying principal component analysis (PCA), the weights of each indicator are adaptively determined, thereby enhancing the precision of risk prediction. The early warning index system developed in this study encompasses financial, operational, and market risks. The comprehensive risk score is calculated by weighted comprehensive scoring method, and the risks are divided into three levels: low, medium and high. Results suggest that the model provides precise alerts for projects with elevated risk, and the accuracy of early warning fluctuates between 80% and 100%, which has high stability and application value. The research provides a scientific decision support tool for power grid companies, which helps them identify and avoid potential investment risks in time, and enhance their competitiveness and risk resistance.

title: Scale-Exchange Attack: Enhancing Adversarial Transferability via Multi-scale Block Transformation and Replacement
authors: Hao Chen, Qiyu Zhu, Ximeng Liu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: Deep neural networks have demonstrated exceptional performance across a wide range of visual tasks. Nevertheless, their susceptibility to adversarial examples remains a critical security flaw. These examples mislead model predictions by introducing subtle perturbations. Among these, black-box attacks are practically important yet suffer from limited transferability. To address this, we introduce the Scale-Exchange Attack Method(SEM), a strategy grounded in input transformation. The approach disrupts local feature distributions via multi-scale block transformations and selectively replaces original image blocks to preserve semantic anchors, thereby maintaining structural consistency. This process effectively perturbs attention heatmaps and improves transferability. Empirical evaluations conducted on the ImageNet dataset confirm that SEM attains superior success rates in all scenarios, and it integrates well with other methods. The method offers new insights for research on adversarial transferability and the evaluation of model robustness.

title: A Controlled Regenerative Braking Approach for Induction Motor-Based Electrified Vehicles
authors: Nikhil Suresh Chavan, Srikanthan Sridharan
abstract: During certain operating conditions in electrified vehicles, energy recovery through regenerative braking is either intentionally restricted or rendered infeasible due to practical considerations. As an alternative during such situations, this work proposes to investigate the dissipative braking capabilities of an induction motor, used for traction in a vehicle's electric drive system. Existing literature lacks comprehensive analysis of slip magnitude's impact on transient torque characteristics during braking, compared to steady-state motoring and generating operations. This paper analyses and determines the appropriate operating slip values in the regenerative braking region, such that the regenerated power is dissipated within the motor partially or completely, ensuring a controlled energy recovery. The proposed method is based on implementing a simple scalar control strategy involving dynamic variation of the motor's terminal voltage and frequency as a function of the motor speed, with a novel dynamic slip- <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$V / f$</tex> coordination framework to control energy recovery. Simulation results indicate that using an appropriate slip value during braking effectively enhances the dissipative braking capability. However, the initial braking torque achievable is observed to be reduced at higher slip magnitudes, highlighting critical transient torque trade-offs. A detailed quantitative comparison of braking performance among various operating points corresponding to low- and high-slip values is also provided to understand the trade-offs between net energy recovered and vehicle deceleration characteristics.

title: A Scalable Edge Server Deployment Strategy Based on Spectral Clustering and Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Yijing Xiang, Xia Ou, Hongbing Cheng
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: With the rapid growth of mobile devices and data traffic, mobile edge computing has become a key technology for next-generation networks. Efficient deployment of edge servers is essential for resource optimization, low latency, energy control, and load balancing, yet existing methods still lack adaptability and scalability. To address this, we propose a multi-agent reinforcement learning strategy that integrates spectral clustering with the QMIX value-decomposition framework. Spectral clustering groups candidate base stations by spatial and load features to select representative deployment nodes, while QMIX enables agents to perform dynamic task allocation and improve system adaptability. Experiments using real data from the Shanghai Telecom Bureau show that our method reduces average latency by 28.18%, 16.75%, 8.10%, and 5.43%, compared with Random, Top-K, PSO, and Sarsa, respectively. Energy consumption decreases by 6.61%, 7.44%, 3.92%, and 2.80%, while load balancing improves by 50.12%, 55.30%, 46.93%, and 35.84%. These results demonstrate a significant overall optimization effect.

title: A lightweight algorithm for detecting traffic signs
authors: Jianhui Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: In the task of traffic sign detection, traffic sign targets are of small scale and occupy a small proportion of the image. At the same time, the background is complex. If in abnormal weather conditions (such as rain, fog, etc.), it will seriously restrict the improvement of detection accuracy. Moreover, as the detection accuracy increases, the algorithms tend to become more complex and the model size increases. Aiming at the dual constraints of low detection accuracy and large model size, this paper has carried out lightweight model improvement based on YOLOv8n from the YOLO (You Only Look Once) series. Firstly, some C2f modules in the network are replaced with C2f_FasterBlock modules to simplify the complexity of the model. Then, since most traffic signs are small; to fulfill the needs of small target detection, the multi-scale detection structure is reconstructed, and P2 detection layer and detection head are added to retain the details of small targets; at the same time, the redundant P5 detection layer and detection head are pruned; thereby achieving the collaborative improvement of model lightweighting and detection accuracy. Experimental results show that the improved model achieves a precision (P) of 87.1% on the CCTSDB 2021 dataset, a recall (R) of 71.4%, mAP50 of 79.7%, and mAP50-95 of 49.9%. Compared with the original YOLOv8n, it has decreased by 0.2%, increased by 4.1%, 4.2%, and 3.2% respectively; meanwhile, the model size has been reduced to 3.7MB, meeting the lightweight deployment requirements of vehicle-mounted edge devices.

title: Sustainable IoT-Based Water Purity Monitoring System Using Oxygen, pH, and Temperature Sensors with ESP32 and Telegram Interface
authors: N Vinodalakshmi, G. A. Vatsala, G Prathyusha, P Shilpa, A Jatin Ram Chowdary, N P Ananya
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: This paper proposes a Sustainable IoT-Based Water Purity monitoring system that guarantees real-time monitoring of the water quality in sump and overhead tanks. The device uses ESP32 microcontroller with oxygen, pH, and temperature sensors to extract data at fixed periods set by the user. The measured items are automatically compared to predefined safety levels and any deviation can result in instant alerts with a Telegram Bot to inform higher authorities to take immediate maintenance and hygiene measures. Besides alerts, the system allows scheduled summaries of the current readings and trend reports and thus constant and remote monitoring. The framework is based on the principle of scalability and modularity and can easily incorporate the use of other sensors like turbidity or conductivity without the need to redesign the hardware. Reporting schedules and both alert thresholds can be customized, providing flexibility among a wide range of uses. Making use of the low price, Wi-Fi connectivity, and energy efficiency of the ESP32 and the widespread Telegram, the suggested device will be a small, affordable, and user-friendly alternative to traditional IoT-based water quality monitoring systems.

title: Comparative Review of Hybrid AI Approaches for Traffic Signal Systems
authors: Khalid Tizra, Brahim Raouyane, Abdelkrim Saouabe, Doha Banoui
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: Traffic congestion imposes major economic, environmental, and social costs on cities worldwide. This paper presents a comprehensive decision framework for selecting optimal hybrid artificial intelligence (AI) approaches for traffic signal control, based on a systematic review of 33 peer-reviewed studies (2015-2025). We determine which hybrid combinations, and under what circumstances, reliably provide performance benefits. According to our analysis, in a variety of urban contexts, Deep Q-Networks and Convolutional Neural Networks significantly reduce vehicle waiting times (25%-37%). For multi-intersection coordination, federated learning methods offer up to 47% faster convergence while preserving data privacy. Graph neural networks enable scalable network-wide coordination with 38% delay reduction, while multi-agent systems provide interpretable cooperative control achieving up to 49% improvement. We provide practical guidelines covering implementation strategies, computational requirements, and deployment constraints, addressing the persistent gap between simulation performance and real-world application. By supporting evidence-based intelligent transportation system design, this framework helps engineers and politicians choose technologies that are in line with operational, budgetary, and infrastructural requirements.

title: Defect Detection Algorithm for Steel Plate Surfaces Based on Improved YOLOv8n
authors: Zhou Wu
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: The present study proposes a model, designated as ARS-YOLOv8n, to address the issue of insufficient recognition accuracy in steel plate surface defect detection. First, the ADown module is introduced to enhance the features of steel plate surface defect targets while suppressing irrelevant area features. This enables the model to capture more defect feature information. This approach effectively addresses the challenge of low pixel proportions in small defects in steel plate surface datasets. Secondly, the C2f-iRMB module is introduced to significantly reduce the number of parameters and computational complexity, while maintaining high feature extraction capability. The integration of the slim-neck lightweight structure design, the GSConv module, and the cross-stage partial network module (VoV-GSCSP) culminates in a model that exhibits several notable advantages. Chief among these is a reduction in time complexity, a decrease in the number of parameters, and an enhancement in performance. The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed enhanced algorithm achieves a 3.1% increase in accuracy, a 4.5% increase in recall, and a 4.4% increase in mean average precision (mAP), thereby attaining an accuracy rate of 88.1%. The improved network achieves a significant enhancement in the performance of detecting surface defects on steel plates.

title: Multi-Modal Transformer-Based Retrieval for Fine-Grained Product Search in E-Commerce Platforms
authors: Lixia Sun
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Achieving precise and efficient product retrieval in large-scale e-commerce settings presents significant challenges due to visual variability and ambiguous textual descriptions. We introduce MM-TransRet, a unified multimodal transformer framework that concurrently encodes image patches and text tokens within a single backbone. Each modality is initially processed through dedicated self-attention layers, resulting in rich visual and textual embeddings. These embeddings are subsequently integrated via a gated bilinear fusion layer that adaptively emphasizes the most discriminative cues for each query. The training process employs a contrastive ranking loss, supplemented by hard-negative mining and modality-specific regularization, to foster robust, domain-aware representations. On the Amazon Products benchmark, MMTransRet achieves a Recall@1 of 0.614, mAP of 0.711, and nDCG@10 of 0.816, surpassing state-of-the-art baselines by significant margins. Analysis of training and validation loss curves reveals rapid convergence and a narrow generalization gap. Our findings demonstrate that the proposed fusion strategy and joint transformer architecture effectively capture fine-grained image-text relationships while preserving the efficiency of nearest-neighbor search, rendering MM-TransRet a practical solution for real-world e-commerce applications.

title: High-Speed Link Adaptive DFE Design Based on Improved Variable Step-Size Strategy
authors: Jingjie Zou, Qiang Chen, Wenliang Nie, Xiangfei Nie
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: To address the eye-diagram closure caused by severe inter-symbol interference (ISI) in high-speed links, this work proposes an improved variable step-size equalization algorithm based on a double arctangent function. Traditional fixed-step and variable-step LMS algorithms struggle to achieve steady-state accuracy and fast convergence at the same time. In the proposed method, the tap coefficients of the forward and feedback filters in the adaptive decision feedback equalizer (DFE) are optimized and updated simultaneously. The update process is enabled by a new step-size adaptation strategy that applies a differential operation to the double arctangent function. An exponential adjustment mechanism and a multi-parameter fine-tuning scheme are further incorporated to enhance stability. These improvements allow the equalization structure to fully exploit its combined advantages. Experimental results show that the improved algorithm converges faster and achieves better steady-state performance. In a $\mathbf{2 5 - G b p s}$ transmission link, the eye height increases by $7.7 \%$ and the eye width increases by $\mathbf{1 5. 0 \%}$.

title: Hybrid AI-based Framework for Advanced Energy Demand Forecasting
authors: Amgoth Narendra, S Alagumuthukrishnan, J S Geetha Priya, P.Deekshith Chary, Porika HimaBindhu, N Srivani
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Accurate energy demand prediction is critical for efficient power grid management, cost reduction, and ensuring system reliability. Traditional forecasting approaches often rely on historical energy demand and basic weather data, which may fail to capture complex temporal, weather-driven, and socioeconomic variations in energy demand. XGBOOST is used to predict the energy demand forecasting. Accurate energy demand forecasting is essential for efficient smart grid operation. Traditional methods trends, seasonality, while AI models such as LSTM and ensemble learners model nonlinear consumption patterns. The framework incorporates external factors including weather and calendar effects to enhance prediction accuracy. Experimental results on real-world datasets show improved forecasting performance compared to individual models. The proposed approach supports reliable energy management, peak load prediction, and sustainable integration of renewable energy sources.

title: Evaluation of the Energy–Capacity Split for System Balance and the Competitiveness of Renewable Energy in Peru after Law 32249
authors: Raul Enrique Perez Loayza, Dennis Raul Garay Aquino, Liliana Fidencia Valero Salazar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: This study evaluated how the energy and capacity split introduced after Law 32249 can support system balance and improve the competitiveness of renewable energy in Peru by remunerating electrical energy and firm capacity as separate products. The adequacy driver was defined using the 2025 to 2034 Efficient Generation Requirement under dry season conditions, and 2027 was selected as the focal milestone because the cumulative requirement reaches 600 megawatts beyond the committed projects window. A 2027 renewable led portfolio was designed to deliver 600 megawatts of firm capacity using hydropower support, wind with a conservative effective load carrying capability credit, solar made peak deliverable through a four hour battery energy storage system, and efficient combined cycle natural gas backup, yielding 3,662 gigawatt hours per year of net energy. Under a decoupled Regulated Market and Free Market contracting structure, the portfolio generated 154.3 million United States dollars per year from energy sales and 46.7 million United States dollars per year from capacity payments under a Basic Capacity Price cap.

title: Voltage Stability of Active Distribution Networks from the Perspective of Wind Power Generation Access
authors: Yuwen Xia, Liwei Tian, Haiyan Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: As the core power source form of green energy, wind power generation is increasingly being integrated into the large power grid, and its high proportion of integration poses a severe challenge to the voltage stability of active distribution networks. Based on this, this paper monitors the voltage fluctuation of active distribution networks under the perspective of wind power generation access, establishes its voltage fluctuation index variables, and uses them to form assessment characteristics of the voltage quality of active distribution networks. Firstly, by establishing the structure of wind power generation access in active distribution networks, the structural type is determined, and the research is expanded with the IEEE 33 -node system as an example. Then, an iterative optimization analysis is conducted on the IEEE 33-node system with power flow calculation as an example, determining its voltage operation matrix, and proposing its stability index expression for the matrix. Finally, through the established voltage stability index expression, the voltage stability of the IEEE 33-node system is evaluated by using the voltage matrixes obtained from simulation under different wind power generation proportions, forming indicator monitoring points for the dynamic output process of voltage under wind power generation access in active distribution networks, and determining the fluctuation threshold points. Data indicators are determined through the comparison of the time-domain shapes of various reference nodes, and the effectiveness of the proposed indicators in this paper is ultimately verified.

title: A Point Cloud Processing Method Based on Weighted Filtering
authors: Yixuan Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of noise and jump in 2D laser point clouds, this paper proposes a point cloud processing method based on weighted filtering. The method handles outliers through a local distance adjustment mechanism and achieves adaptive smoothing based on a local linear model and Gaussian weighting. Comparative experiments in typical scenarios show that compared with traditional filtering methods, the proposed method performs better in key indicators such as absolute error and range. It can effectively suppress noise while preserving contour features, providing a more robust solution for 2D point cloud processing.

title: GAN-Fusion-Based Face De-Identification with Multi-Scale Semantic Suppression and Security Performance Analysis
authors: Guangyu Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: To meet the growing need for privacy-preserving sharing of face data in medical imaging, smart surveillance and edge AI, this paper proposes a GAN-fusion face de-identification model. The network couples a multi-scale semantic suppression (MS-SS) subnetwork with an attribute-aware injection (AAI) module so that identity cues on skin texture, periocular regions and global shape are attenuated, while non-identity attributes such as pose, expression and illumination are kept stable. A dual-discriminator design-an identity-aware discriminator and an image-quality discriminator-plus a VGG19 perceptual feature loss is adopted to keep the synthesized face close to the source in structure but far from it in embedding space. On the CelebA-HQ dataset, the proposed model achieves PSNR 27.38 dB and SSIM 0.942 on anonymized images. Evaluation against a strong ArcFace recognizer shows an identity shielding rate of 97.4%, which is about 42% relative improvement over Gaussian blur (68.6%). When the FGSM adversarial perturbation with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varepsilon =0.03$</tex> is added, the attack success rate falls to 2.8%, indicating that the de-identified manifold is already hard to exploit. However, under a diffusion-based de-anonymization model similar to recent reversible/detector-guided diffusion methods, a 16.2% identity matching rate remains, suggesting that latentlevel identity hiding still needs reinforcement. The method complies with national-level requirements that demand <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 9 0 {\%}}$</tex> anonymization detection rate for released face data.

title: Semantic Alignment and Output Constrained Generation for Reliable LLM-based Classification
authors: Jixiao Yang, Sebastian Sun, Yang Wang, Yutong Wang, Xikai Yang, Chi Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: To address the limited controllability, unstable output consistency, and weakly constrained decision processes of large language models in text classification tasks, this work proposes a controllable prompt-driven text classification method that establishes an end-to-end unified modeling framework from instruction alignment to constrained decoding. Text classification is reformulated as an instruction-conditioned generative discriminative problem. Input texts and task instructions are jointly encoded to form a unified internal representation that integrates textual semantics with classification constraints. On this basis, a category semantic alignment mechanism is introduced to ensure that the model explicitly follows category boundaries and decision criteria defined by the instructions, thereby reducing classification inconsistency caused by prompt variation or implicit bias. To further improve output reliability, a structured constrained decoding strategy is designed to restrict the generation space to a predefined set of valid categories, preventing redundant text or invalid outputs from interfering with classification results. Comparative analysis under unified data and evaluation settings demonstrates that the proposed method achieves more consistent advantages in classification accuracy, discriminative stability, and overall separability. These findings indicate that deeply integrating instruction understanding and output control into the decision process of large language models effectively transforms their generative capacity into stable, interpretable, and controllable text classification capability, providing a systematic solution for building reliable intelligent text analysis systems.

title: UAV Path Planning Based on an Improved Hippopotamus Optimization Algorithm
authors: Ziheng Xiao, Yaoyao Luo, Wenhao Xu
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: To overcome the tendency of the Hippopotamus Optimization (HO) algorithm to fall into local optima and its low search accuracy in path planning, this paper proposes a multi-strategy Improved Hippopotamus Optimization (IHO) algorithm. First, Latin hypercube sampling (LHS) is used to initialize the population, enlarging the initial exploration range and making the individuals more uniformly distributed in the search space. Second, a local-escape mechanism is embedded in the defense phase: 60% of the best individuals are preserved as an elite group, while the remaining 40% are perturbed around the elites using Gaussian and Cauchy distributions to maintain diversity and avoid local optima. Third, a new collision strategy is introduced to further balance exploration and exploitation and improve search efficiency. Tests on 12 benchmark functions show that IHO achieves better optimization performance and faster convergence than the original HO, GWO, DBO, PSO and DE. Finally, when applied to UAV path planning, IHO shortens the path length by 15.36% compared with HO and shows clear advantages over the other algorithms.

title: 1-Bit Active Phase-Reconfigurable Unit Cell Ris for Next Generation Wireless Communication
authors: Nur Hasni Marzuki, Muzammil Jusoh, Muhammad Fitra Zambak, Suhail Ashgar Qureshi, Muhammad Ramlee Kamarudin, Huda A Majid, Catur Apriono, Tamer Aboufoul
publish: 2026 Symposium on Microwave, Antenna, and Propagation (SMAP)
abstract: Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (RIS) have emerged as a transformative solution for next-generation wireless networks, offering a low-power and cost-efficient means of controlling electromagnetic wave propagation. Through the integration of tunable elements within engineered metasurfaces, RIS can dynamically adjust the phase, amplitude, or polarization of incoming signals, enabling flexible and programmable wireless environments suitable for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 G}, \mathbf{6 G}$</tex>, and beyond. In this work, an active 1-bit RIS unit cell is designed and analyzed using full-wave simulation, featuring a PIN diode as the rapid switching element. The active configuration supports binary phase modulation, providing two discrete reflection states determined by the diode bias condition. The unit cell is further optimized to yield an approximately 180° phase shift between the ON and OFF states, with a tolerance of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 20^{\circ}$</tex> at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4. 6 ~ G H z}$</tex>, ensuring reliable and consistent operation at the intended frequency. Simulation results verify that the use of the PIN diode enables fast and efficient reconfigurability without compromising compactness or practical implementability. These outcomes advance ongoing RIS research and establish a solid platform for future hardware fabrication, prototyping, and experimental validation.

title: UWB-Based Dynamic Indoor Positioning via Integrated LS-Newton Optimization and Neural Network Noise Mitigation
authors: Liuqing Hu, Bochen Xiao, Shikun Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: To overcome Ultra-Wideband (UWB) positioning degradation in complex indoor environments, this study proposed an integrated framework that combines geometric localization via Least Squares (LS) estimation and Newton iteration refinement, Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM)-based statistical denoising for systematic/interference noise, and neural network–based noise classification (with an accuracy of 89.09%). The system achieves 3D accuracies of 14.96 cm under systematic noise and 29.84 cm under interference noise, demonstrating robustness in both static and dynamic scenarios across multiple indoor layouts. By unifying ranging error modeling, statistical denoising, and data-driven classification, this unified approach provides a systematic solution for centimeter-level UWB indoor positioning.

title: 3-D Thermal Modeling and Simulation of Wide Band Gap Semiconductor Devices Based CSI-fed PMSM Integrated Motor Drive System
authors: Hossein Azizi Moghaddam, Reza Mirzahosseini
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: In this paper, three-dimensional (3-D) thermal analysis and design of the cooling system of a wide bandgap (WBG) based CSI-fed PMSM integrated motor -drive system is presented. In this structure, the motor and the drive are integrated in a common enclosure, and their mutual thermal effects are considered. The sources of losses in different components of the motor and drive are calculated and updated using the coupling between the magnetic finite element simulations of the motor and the dynamic simulation of the drive system in MATLAB and PLECS software. The thermal and magnetic simulations are performed in a coupled manner to accurately evaluate the temperature distribution under real operating conditions. The results show that heat transfer in the integrated structure occurs axially and radially, and 3-D thermal modeling is essential for accurate prediction of the thermal behavior of the system.

title: GEM-ARC: AI Impelled Gamified Engagement System with Smart Reward Redemption Card for Universities and Students
authors: Ranjit Kaur, Aman Kumar, Jyotiranjan Mishra, Aakash Deep, Anuj Kumar Jha, Anuja Kumari
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: University events are a fundamental part of student life and educational experiences, but the digital platforms or systems currently used for managing them are still fragmented and non-customizable. This research presents an AI-powered ecosystem which is created especially for managing university events that resolve current constraints in a coordinated manner. Our system comprises: (1) a multi-layered personalized recommendation system which integrate reinforcement learning, content-based filtering, and collaborative filtering; (2) dynamic notification management based on their skills and interests; (3) an intelligent gamification mechanisms in which students will rewarded with coins that can redeemable using cards or digitally; (4) a chatbot processed by natural language processing (NLP) for event idea generation and query resolution; and (5) sentiment analysis for feedback evaluation. We show how these interrelated elements work together to produce a self-improving system that raises student engagement metrics, decreases administrative load, and increases event participation. Compared to traditional systems, preliminary assessments indicate a 37% decrease in administrative workload and a 42% increase in event participation. In contrast with simply integrating fragmented technologies, the architecture offered offers a novel framework which applies AI techniques to the domain of university event management, resulting in a enriched ecosystem.

title: A Co-Designed Hybrid Charger of OBC and WC With Partially Closed and Directionally Open Magnetic Circuits for EV Charging
authors: Tianyi Zhang, Guo Wei, Xin Gao, Chunbo Zhu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: In this paper, a hybrid charger integrated with the on-board charger (OBC) and wireless charger (WC) is introduced. This innovative hybrid charger applies proposed partially closed and directionally open magnetic circuits to improve the power density and efficiency of hybrid charger. The planar transformer is partitioned into tightly-coupled (TC) and loosely-coupled (LC) sections, respectively implemented by the proposed high coupling coefficient bifilar winding planar transformer (BWPT) and LC-double-DD coil. In OBC, the leakage inductance of LC-double-DD coil is cleverly utilized as the resonant inductance for the CLLC converter, while its mutual inductance significantly enhances the magnetizing inductance of BWPT. In WC, the BWPT ensures the current balance across dual receivers, while the LC-double-DD acts as the resonant inductors of LCC circuit. A 11kW-OBC and 11kW-WC experimental prototype is built to validate feasibility, results demonstrate peak charging efficiencies of 97.7% and 94.2% for OBC and WC, respectively.

title: Active Damping Implementation for Controller Performance Optimization in DSSC System
authors: He Liu, Yonghao Fang, Huiyuan Zhang, Jiayun Zhu, Zhiyuan Li, Pengfei Jia
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy (ICSGE)
abstract: As Distributed Static Series Compensators (DSSCs) become widely researched in renewable-integrated grid for power flow control and fault management, their harmonic susceptibility remains a challenge. Conventional damping methods relying on parasitic or additional resistors inherently suffer from significant resistive losses, limiting their performance. This paper presents an optimized active damping control strategy for DSSC to mitigate inherent LC filter oscillations in impedance regulation application. The model for single-phase LC-type DSSC inverter and dual-loop control is established. By transforming passive resistor-capacitor network into an active damping solution, equivalent stability is achieved without extra physical resistor. Parameter design rules are established along with primary performance analysis. Simulation results verify effective resonance suppression, stable mode transitions, and improved harmonic performance compared to conventional passive methods.

title: Adaptive Ensemble Learning for Malicious Website Detection Under Concept Drift
authors: Gaddam Srikanth Reddy, R Jothikumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: The rapid advancement of malicious websites poses significant cybersecurity risks. Traditional detection models fail to maintain accuracy under changing attack patterns due to concept drift. This paper proposes an adaptive ensemble learning framework for malicious website detection that dynamically updates classifier weights in response to detected concept drift. The framework combines multiple classifiers and selectively retrains those with performance below threshold, ensuring resilience to shifting data distributions. Evaluation on benchmark datasets shows superior detection accuracy and stability compared to static and baseline adaptive models. Results show the proposed method effectively captures adversarial evolution, maintaining robust performance with reduced retraining overhead. This approach provides a scalable, adaptive solution for real-time cybersecurity defense.

title: Robust MISO coherent optical GEO satellite feeder link with relaxed implementation constraints
authors: Youssef Minyari, Aubin Lecointre, Raphaël Le Bidan, Charly Poulliat
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: In this paper, we propose to study the potential advantages that multiple-input single-output (MISO) architectures could offer compared to the classical single-input single-output (SISO) approach for coherent free space optical (FSO) ground-to-GEO feeder links, highlighting their resilience to atmospheric turbulence and the benefits of spatial diversity. Evaluating system trade-offs under both power-limited and power-augmented scenarios, we demonstrate that MISO designs can achieve higher data rates and enable more robust optical feeder links. Choosing the best MISO configuration depends on system-level priorities, including available transmit power, optical and adaptive optics (AO) complexity, throughput-latency balance, and integration requirements. Our findings demonstrate that simple MISO architectures can effectively balance aperture size, AO requirements, interleaver depth, and transmit power, positioning them as a promising solution for efficient and resilient GEO optical feeder links.

title: Investigation of Electric Field Distribution along Porcelain Insulator with the Influence of Deteriorated Insulator
authors: Kalaiselvi Aramugam, Akash Chowdary, Subhashini Gopal Krishnan, M.K.A. Ahamed Khan, Mastaneh Mokayef
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: In power transmission system, an insulator string has a significant contribution for a safe and efficient operation. It is not possible to operate an overhead transmission system without an insulator. The main purpose of an insulator is to insulate conductive lines, it also carries the weight of the conductor in power transmission line. However, the influence of deteriorated insulators may lead to a flashover or power failure in transmission lines. The reason for this failure is a degradation in insulator strings' mechanism and insulation level for a long period of performing. By using finite element method, potential voltage distribution and electric field distributions along insulator strings with different positions under the same deteriorated level have been studied. Therefore, a model of insulator string has been developed and simulated in the COMSOL Multiphysics software. As a result, the effect of electric field distribution and potential distribution of normal porcelain insulator string and deteriorated insulator string were compared and analyzed.

title: Machine Learning Approach for Enhanced Localization Accuracy in Visible Light Positioning
authors: MD Maksudur Rahman Mazumder, Julien Le Kernec, Hanaa Abumarshoud
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: Visible light positioning (VLP) systems offer a precise indoor location without interference with traditional radio frequency (RF)-based solutions. However, existing positioning techniques face challenges, including multipath reflections, interference, and shadowing, limiting their accuracy and robustness. This study proposes a regression tree-based machine learning (ML) framework tailored specifically for accurate and robust indoor VLP localization. Regression trees were trained on received signal strength characteristics derived from simulations involving multiple light-emiting-diode transmitters, and the performance of the ML method was evaluated against conventional direct positioning (DP) approaches in scenarios with fixed and random receiver orientations, at optical power levels ranging from 10 to 100 mW. The ML approach outperformed DP significantly, reducing the root mean square error (RMSE) by up to 35% and demonstrating enhanced robustness to receiver orientation variations. This ML-based framework provides a reliable and computationally efficient solution, which makes it highly suitable for indoor real-time localization applications within internet-ofthings environments.

title: SQLight: Reasoning-Augmented and Workflow-Decoupled Text-to-SQL
authors: Zhenyang Song, Zhentian Shen
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: SQLight presents an industry-ready, lightweight Text-to-SQL paradigm that converts the original large-model-reliant DIN-SQL into a trainable small-model pipeline via "training-inference co-design". The approach consists of three stages: Supervised fine-tuning with reasoning traces to acquire base capabilities; GRPO-based reinforcement learning driven by QATCH fine-grained rewards (Cell Precision/Recall, Tuple Cardinality) plus format rewards; At test time, a light-weight Divide-and-Conquer 3-shot + result-verification workflow is used by default, while a learnable router performs dynamic difficulty scheduling. Experiments show that our 7B model achieves 57.2% execution accuracy on BIRD dev 95% of DIN-SQL+GPT-4 with 6× lower latency and 12× lower cost. The 3B model gains +11.8% accuracy after RL, surpassing 40B non-reasoning models for the first time.

title: Distributed ML Model Orchestration over multi cloud systems
authors: Dharga Kolla, Umesh Waghmode, Sai Kiran Mannava
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The rapid increase in machine learning (ML) workloads, along with the diversity of modern cloud environments, presents new opportunities and challenges for multi-cloud systems. While prior research has focused on centralized or distributed ML pipelines, limited attention has been given to key issues such as fault tolerance, data locality, dynamic policy enforcement, and vendor lock-in in multi-cloud deployments. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a novel all-in-one distributed ML orchestration framework capable of deploying, scheduling, and scaling ML models across public, private, and hybrid cloud infrastructures. A central contribution of this work is the introduction of a policy-changing orchestration mesh, defined as a cloud-native control layer that dynamically adapts networking, security, routing, and workload placement policies at runtime based on real-time system conditions such as performance metrics, cloud pricing, resource availability, and failure events. Unlike static service meshes, the proposed orchestration mesh continuously reconfigures policies to support cross-cloud workload migration, fault recovery, and cost-efficient execution. The framework integrates containerization, distributed ML training, intelligent scheduling, and service mesh technologies to enable flexible and resilient multi-cloud ML operations. Experimental results demonstrate improved throughput, reduced deployment latency, faster fault recovery, and enhanced cost efficiency, highlighting the necessity of adaptive orchestration interfaces for next-generation distributed machine learning systems.

title: Evaluating Pedestrian Detection Performance Across Weather Conditions in CARLA
authors: Hawra Alsafi, Mohamed Abusharkh
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: In the process of detecting pedestrians, it is essential to assess the quality of bounding box projections because they serve as foundational inputs for machine learning object detection models. Poor quality bounding box projections can hinder model performance and increase training errors. This leads to models that potentially exhibit degraded performance in the real-world deployment. With major safety implications for autonomous vehicles, it is imperative to quantitatively assess the quality of bounding box projections prior to model development. This study evaluates pedestrian bounding box quality in CARLA across various weather conditions during a left-hand turn scenario. Building on prior work, this study uses sequential filtering which integrates geometric constraints. Furthermore, prior work used semantic segmentation for bounding box projection validation, but the method is too restrictive. This study establishes quality metrics for synthetic datasets generated in CARLA. A novel method is introduced, and it achieves bounding box precision above 83 % across all weather conditions and accuracy ranging from 63.93 % to 69.70 %. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first quantitative pre-training assessment of bounding box quality.

title: Assessment of the Strength and Fatigue Behavior of Generator Rotor Fan Blades under Static and Cyclic Loading
authors: Alireza Shamaei-Kashani, Meysam Davoudabadi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: In this study, a novel analytical model is developed to predict the service life of generator rotor fan blades under both static and fatigue loading conditions. The static model assesses failure using a failure assessment diagram (FAD) based on fracture mechanics by calculating the fracture and load ratios. In the fatigue model, forging defects are initially represented as cracks with defined geometries based on available empirical relationships. Critical regions are then identified based on the stress distribution in the blade under cthe generator’s cyclic loading condition. The blade’s service life is subsequently estimated using Paris’ law, fracture toughness, and crack growth constants, taking into account the critical crack size and the number of load cycles. The results reveal that the lowest safety factor for the blade occurs under static loading conditions, with a value of 2.02.

title: Research and Application of Multi-Agent Cooperative Decision Algorithm Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Hongyi Hao
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: In complex dynamic environment, there are some problems in multi-agent collaborative decision-making, such as non-stationarity, credit distribution deviation and imperfect topological relationship modeling. In this article, the deep reinforcement learning (DRL) algorithm Att-GNN-MARL, which combines attention mechanism and GNN, is proposed to improve the cooperative efficiency and strategic stability of multi-agent system (MAS). Based on the centralized training decentralized execution (CTDE) framework, this study uses graph neural network (GNN) to model the spatial connection of agents, introduces attention mechanism to enhance the perception of key neighbors, and designs a counterfactual reward correction mechanism to optimize local credit allocation. In the collaborative control scenario of urban traffic signals, the experiments based on real road network on SUMO simulation platform show that the proposed algorithm reduces the average waiting time of vehicles to 54.3 seconds during rush hours, which is 22.3% less than that of QMIX. The total traffic volume reached 24,108 vehicles, which was significantly better than the comparison method. The number of rounds of strategy convergence is 640, and the learning efficiency is good. The research shows that the cooperative learning mechanism combining structural perception and dynamic attention can improve the overall performance of MAS in highly coupled tasks to some extent.

title: Automatic Classification of Agricultural Crops based on Satellite Data Time Series and Neural Networks
authors: Karimov Sardor Ilkhom Ugli, Xaydarova Inoyatxon Patidin Qizi, Abdurasulova Nasibaxon Abduvohid Qizi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Science and Communications Technologies (ICISCT)
abstract: This paper addresses the development and investigation of a hybrid neural network architecture for automatic classification of agricultural crops based on satellite data time series. The proposed model combines convolutional neural networks (CNN) with long short-term memory (LSTM) networks to effectively capture both spatial features and temporal patterns in multi-temporal satellite imagery. The objective is to improve the accuracy and robustness of agricultural land classification through the integration of remote sensing data obtained from Sentinel-2 and Landsat-8 satellites across multiple growing seasons. The work analyzes spectral characteristics of vegetation and their seasonal variations using NDVI, EVI, and SAVI indices derived from time-series data. Experimental results demonstrate that the CNN-LSTM hybrid architecture achieves superior performance compared to conventional CNN approaches, with an accuracy of $94.3 \%$ on test data from agricultural areas in Central Asia.

title:
Synergistic Thickness and Density for High-Efficiency FTO/Sb:SnO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    /CsFAPb(IBr)
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    /BaSi
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Perovskite Solar Cells
authors: Poonam Suraj Gham, Alisha Priya, Raushan Kumar
abstract: In order to maximize hole capture rates and reduce minority carrier loss in photovoltaic (PV) cells, the selection of hole transport layer (HTL) materials plays a crucial role in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{C s F A P b} \boldsymbol{(} \mathbf{I B r} \mathbf{)}_{\mathbf{3}} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{B a S i}_{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> perovskite solar cell device (PSCD). Historically, CsFAPb (IBr) 3 cells have utilized HTLs designed to minimize minority carrier loss. To further improve the performance of CsFAPb <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{I B r})_{\mathbf{3}} / \mathbf{B a S i}_{\mathbf{2}} \mathbf{P S C D}$</tex>, extensive research is focusing on HTL materials with wide energy bandgaps. This paper presents first to comparison between experimental and simulation results of exiting solar cell and then a novel wide bandgap <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{BaSi}_{2}$</tex> as an HTL nanomaterial added in existing solar cell instead of Spiro-OMeTAD HTL for make a high-efficiency solar cell. The study covers a comprehensive analysis of optimization of layer thickness and doping density, as well as the design of the proposed PV cell structure. The CsFAPb <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{IBr})_{3} / \text{BaSi}_{2} \text{PV}$</tex> cell shows exceptional performance, with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{V}_{\text{OC}}= \mathbf{1 1 9 4 ~ m V}, \mathrm{J}_{\text{SC}}=\mathbf{2 6. 8 4 ~ m A} / \text{cm}^{2}, \mathbf{F F}=\mathbf{8 4. 8 2} \%$</tex>, efficiency <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=\mathbf{2 7. 1 8}$</tex>%.

title: Cross-Integration of the "Iff" Algorithm and Digital Binary Logic to Information Fusion for a Reliable Smart Security System using Multi-Layered Discrete Sensors
authors: Manuel T. Tabada
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: This study examines the effectiveness and reliability of a smart security system. It focuses on how the If-and-Only-If (Iff) Algorithm and digital logic work together with multiple layers of sensors. The system uses sensors at doors, windows, and hallways to detect intrusions. The key is the Iff Algorithm, which ensures a precise match between sensor data and system responses. The results show that the system accurately manages data from these sensors, garnering a majority 0% error. By converting electrical signals into binary values, the system reliably created a set of sequenced binary digits, in which each digit corresponds to the installed sensor. This precise method improves the system's ability to detect intrusions and reduces the chance of false alarms, making it a reliable security solution.

title: Design of a Compact and Highly Selective Coplanar Bandpass Filter for ISM Band Applications
authors: Soufiane El Maimouni, Fouad Aytouna, Jamal Zbitou, Stephane Ginestar, Mohammed El Gibari
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: This paper proposes a compact and highly selective coplanar waveguide bandpass filter (BPF) optimized for ISM band applications around 2.4 GHz. The filter is based on coplanar waveguide (CPW) technology and is constructed on Rogers RT/duroid 5880 substrate, which has a thickness of 0.51 mm, a dielectric constant of 2.2, and a loss tangent of 0.0009. The filter demonstrates return and insertion losses of $\mathbf{2 4 ~ d B}$ and $\mathbf{1. 0 5}$ dB, respectively, with a center frequency near 2.4 GHz and an operating bandwidth ranging from 2.18 GHz to 2.64 GHz, resulting in a bandwidth of $\mathbf{4 6 0 ~ M H z}$. The design was simulated using two electromagnetic solvers based on different physical principles, both showing low insertion loss across the passband and strong signal rejection outside it. The overall size of the filter is $19.7 \times 22.05 \mathrm{~mm}^{2}$, making it a promising option for wireless communication and radio frequency signal processing applications. Notably, the 2.4 GHz ISM band is widely used, appearing in devices such as microwave ovens, Wi-Fi routers, and Bluetooth gadgets.

title: A Probabilistic Risk Index Framework and Quantitative Assessment Method for Power Systems
authors: Xide Li, Xiaofei Li, Jingyi Zhang, Shanhe Liu, Pan Gu, Chun Wu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy (ICSGE)
abstract: Traditional power system reliability assessment faces unprecedented challenges from high-penetration renewable energy—with inherent volatility, intermittency, and the rising frequency of extreme weather events—manifesting as compromised supply security, strained power-energy balance, and suboptimal renewable utilization, demanding innovative evaluation frameworks. To address these issues, this paper proposes a probabilistic power-energy balancing method that fully integrates renewable resource characteristics and the heterogeneous attributes of diverse flexible regulation resources. A refined chronological simulation model is developed to characterize the interactions of multiple system components— power supply, grid infrastructure, load demand, and energy storage. Synergistically analyzing failure probabilities and consequences, risk theory is integrated into power system assessment, yielding a dedicated risk evaluation methodology and corresponding index system. Furthermore, rapid operational risk assessment is achieved via advancements in state sampling and analysis, combining an enhanced adaptive importance sampling technique with a load-shedding model based on source-load correlations. Finally, the proposed method is validated using a 2025 planning case of a renewable-rich province, where multi-scenario accurate data are obtained to enable precise calculation of renewable curtailment, electricity demand, and other balancing metrics. The results offer scientific support and decision-making insights for the planning and development of high-renewable-penetration power systems.

title: An Efficient and Stable Dynamic Wireless Power Transfer System Based on LCLCL Boost Topology
authors: Xin Gao, Chang Liu, Shaoshuai Qi, Yonghao Zhu, Shuya Chen, Yan Yan, Shumei Cui, Chunbo Zhu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: In order to reduce the cost of DWPT systems, the transmitter (Tx) power distribution network is usually in the form of an inverter powering the Tx through an AC busbar, but existing studies have often neglected the busbar parasitic parameters. With the extension of the DWPT line, the parasitic parameters can’t be ignored. In this paper, the impact of busbar parasitic parameters is firstly analyzed by taking an example of the parallel LCC topology, and it is found that the busbar parasitic parameters lead to the efficiency degradation and output power fluctuation. In this paper, a DPWT scheme based on segmented busbar compensation and cascaded LCLCL boost topology is proposed. This method compensates the busbar parasitic inductance in segments, and also achieves load-independent busbar boosting. This will reduce the busbar current, decreases the impact of the busbar parasitic parameters on the efficiency and output power fluctuation. Moreover, the system still has ZPA characteristics.

title: Scalable and Interpretable Engineering Solutions for Fake Account Detection using GNNs and FactorVAE
authors: L. Selvam, Ramya S, S. Balamurugan, J. Relin Francis Raj, A. Vegi Fernando, S.P. Lavanya
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The proliferation of social media has introduced complex cybersecurity challenges, particularly due to the widespread presence of fake accounts that manipulate information flow, compromise network integrity, and undermine user trust. Detecting such accounts requires scalable and intelligent engineering solutions capable of analyzing multi-modal data at large scale. This paper presents a hybrid deep learning framework for fake account detection, integrating textual analysis, disentangled representation learning, and graph-based network modeling. Textual content is encoded using RoBERTa embeddings to capture semantic and stylistic features, while FactorVAE disentangles latent factors representing content, style, and intent. Network interactions are modeled through Graph Attention Networks (GAT), capturing relational and structural patterns within social networks. A hybrid MLP-GNN classifier fuses textual and network embeddings to enable high-accuracy detection across large-scale datasets. The framework incorporates explainable AI techniques, including SHAP values and attention maps, to provide interpretable predictions and enhance system reliability. Experimental evaluation demonstrates improved detection accuracy, robustness to adversarial and synthetic accounts, and generalization across multiple social media platforms. By enabling reliable identification of malicious accounts, this approach strengthens cybersecurity measures and ensures safer access to the internet. The proposed system establishes a scalable, interpretable, and efficient engineering solution for fake account detection, providing a benchmark framework for securing social media networks and protecting user interactions in online environments.

title: Research on Fault Diagnosis for Variable-Speed Bearings Using VG and GAF Algorithms
authors: Xingxing Wang, Dagao Zhang, Jianxiong Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Most existing bearing fault diagnosis methods are designed for faults under a single speed, making them poorly suited to the practical scenario where bearing speeds dynamically change during operation. Deep learning and image processing techniques have demonstrated excellent performance across various fields; meanwhile, 2D images contain far richer information than 1D signals. To address this, this paper proposes a variable-speed bearing fault diagnosis model based on the Visibility Graph (VG) and Gramian Angular Field (GAF) algorithms. First, the VG and GAF algorithms are used to encode 1D bearing signals into vg-gadf-gasf three-channel images. These images are then fed into a Multi-convolution-branch Convolutional Neural Network (MulCNN) for diagnosis. To enhance the network’s ability to distinguish homogeneous fault features under variable speeds in the high-dimensional feature space, a cross-entropy loss function constrained by feature space distance is proposed for diagnostic tasks. In the 10-class variable-speed diagnosis task on the CWRU dataset, the proposed method achieves an accuracy rate of 99.89%, showing certain advantages over existing methods. In the test on the XJTU-SY dataset, it attains an accuracy rate of 99.57%, which verifies its favorable robustness and rotational speed adaptability.

title: A Modular Framework for Transcription, Diarization, and Language-Adaptive Summarization of Multi-Party Conversations
authors: Hasna El Maizi, Rachid Fateh, Mathieu Pouliquen, Kaoutar Ahed, Yasmine Lamari, Miloud Frikel, Said Safi
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: This paper introduces a modular, language-adaptive framework for the automated analysis of multi-party conversational data. The proposed system orchestrates a sequential pipeline comprising three core modules: (1) automatic speech recognition (ASR) with integrated language identification, (2) speaker diarization, and (3) abstractive summarization. A key contribution is the dynamic adaptation of the summarization module, which leverages a large language model (LLM) guided by language-specific structured prompts selected based on the output of the ASR stage. The framework is implemented using a scalable microservices architecture that decouples the AI processing pipeline from the core application logic. We demonstrate the system’s efficacy through empirical evaluation, establishing performance benchmarks and validating the quality of its multilingual analysis capabilities. The result is a generalizable, open-source-based architecture for transforming unstructured, multilingual conversational data into structured, actionable knowledge.

title: Study of a New Outer Rotor Permanent Magnet motor with Flux-Switching effect
authors: Mehrad Haseli, Mohammad Reza Sarshar, Mojtaba Mirsalim, Seyed Hamid Montazeri
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: This paper presents an innovative outer-stator permanent magnet (PM) motor based on the flux-switching (FS) principle. The key novelty of the proposed design lies in the use of segmented stator-pole PMs, which significantly reduces the amount of PM material required compared with conventional outer FSPM machines. To further enhance the electromagnetic performance, a genetic algorithm is employed to optimize the design, aiming to maximize torque output while minimizing torque ripple. The proposed configuration is benchmarked against a conventional outer FSPM topology to emphasize its advantages. Finite element analysis (FEA) results demonstrate that segmenting the PMs reduces torque per magnet volume by 27.3% and effectively alleviates PM saturation issues. Therefore, the proposed topology offers high suitability for applications demanding high torque density.

title: A new multi-objective optimization control strategy for power systems based on adaptive dynamic programming
authors: Ou Zhang, Di Wang, Jingjiao Yin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: With large-scale renewable integration and deep source-grid-load-storage coupling, new power systems face multiobjective coordination challenges in economy, safety, and flexibility. To address the limitations of traditional methods under high uncertainty, strong nonlinearity, and multi-timescale dynamics, this paper proposes an adaptive dynamic programming (ADP)-based control strategy. It scalarizes key objectives— operational cost, power balance, and renewable utilization—into a unified performance index embedded in a value function framework. Using parameterized approximation and online learning, the method enables model-free, real-time adaptive optimization. Simulations show it outperforms rule-based and fixed-weight approaches in lowering costs, reducing curtailment, suppressing power deviations, and smoothing storage usage— enhancing overall system performance.

title: Research on Optimization of Photovoltaic Fault Diagnosis Model for High-Dimensional Feature Data: Taking XGBoost as an Example
authors: Liu Bin, He Yaoxi, Ji Fangxu, Yang Yifan, Li Wentao
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: With the rapid development of the photovoltaic industry, fault diagnosis of photovoltaic systems has become increasingly important. In view of the poor performance of traditional diagnosis models under high-dimensional feature data, this paper takes XGBoost as an example to propose an optimization algorithm for photovoltaic fault diagnosis model for high-dimensional feature data. The algorithm calculates the correlation between features and faults and assigns corresponding weights through an adaptive feature weighting module, and combines the tree structure dynamic adjustment module to preferentially split the tree based on high-weight features and control the complexity of the tree structure. The experimental simulation results show that on a 1200-dimensional feature data set containing 12 types of faults, the algorithm's diagnostic accuracy reaches 98.5%, which is 6.2 percentage points higher than the traditional XGBoost model, the precision is 98.2%, the recall is 98.7%, and the F1 value is 98.4%, all of which are better than the optimization algorithms based on PCA and Lasso, at the same time, the training time is shortened by 15.3% (48.6 seconds) and the prediction time is shortened by 12.7% (0.87 milliseconds). The ablation experiment verifies the effectiveness of the synergy of the two modules. The study shows that the optimization algorithm can effectively process high-dimensional feature data, significantly improve the accuracy and efficiency of photovoltaic fault diagnosis, and provide strong support for the stable operation of photovoltaic systems.

title: Energy-Efficient Multi-User Gesture Recognition System for Smart Home Automation
authors: S. Jayamani, Gugannath N S, Balaji S, Sabareesh S
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The rapid growth of intelligent home technologies has enabled intuitive and user-friendly automation systems. This paper presents a wireless, contactless home automation system that recognizes hand gestures in real-time using a Raspberry Pi Pico. Live hand movements are captured via a digital camera and processed through a machine learning model trained on a large dataset of hand gesture images.
