Each gesture corresponds to a specific action for household appliances, allowing users to control lights, fans, and air conditioners without physical interaction. The Raspberry Pi Pico triggers appliances via GPIO pins connected to a relay module, while an LCD provides real-time feedback on device status. The system achieves high recognition accuracy across multiple gestures and supports low-power operation suitable for continuous home deployment. Its modular design allows easy scalability to add new gestures and appliances, enhancing adaptability for diverse users, including the elderly and differently-abled. This approach demonstrates a practical, cost-effective, and efficient solution for next-generation smart homes by integrating embedded systems, computer vision, and machine learning.

title: Expose Camouflage in the Water: Underwater Camouflaged Instance Segmentation and Dataset
authors: Chuhong Wang, Hua Li, Chongyi Li, Huazhong Liu, Xiongxin Tang, Sam Kwong
abstract: With the development of underwater exploration and marine protection, underwater vision tasks are widespread. Due to the degraded underwater environment, characterized by color distortion, low contrast, and blurring, camouflaged instance segmentation (CIS) faces greater challenges in accurately segmenting objects that blend closely with their surroundings. Traditional camouflaged instance segmentation methods, trained on terrestrial-dominated datasets with limited underwater samples, may exhibit inadequate performance in underwater scenes. To address these issues, we introduce the first underwater camouflaged instance segmentation (UCIS) dataset, abbreviated as UCIS4K, which comprises 3,953 images of camouflaged marine organisms with instance-level annotations. In addition, we propose an Underwater Camouflaged Instance Segmentation network based on Segment Anything Model (UCIS-SAM). Our UCIS-SAM includes three key modules. First, the Channel Balance Optimization Module (CBOM) enhances channel characteristics to improve underwater feature learning, effectively addressing the model’s limited understanding of underwater environments. Second, the Frequency Domain True Integration Module (FDTIM) is proposed to emphasize intrinsic object features and reduce interference from camouflage patterns, enhancing the segmentation performance of camouflaged objects blending with their surroundings. Finally, the Multi-scale Feature Frequency Aggregation Module (MFFAM) is designed to strengthen the boundaries of low-contrast camouflaged instances across multiple frequency bands, improving the model’s ability to achieve more precise segmentation of camouflaged objects. Extensive experiments on the proposed UCIS4K and public benchmarks show that our UCIS-SAM outperforms state-of-the-art approaches. The code and dataset are released at https://github.com/wchchw/UCIS4K.

title: Event-Based Receding-Horizon Q-Learning Control of Nonlinear Discrete-Time Systems With Asymmetric Control Constraints
authors: Ziwen Shen, Tao Dong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: This study presents a near-optimal control architecture for nonlinear discrete-time (DT) systems with asymmetric control constraints, leveraging an event-triggered receding-horizon <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {Q}$</tex-math></inline-formula>-learning framework. To address actuator saturation effects, a non-quadratic integral term is introduced, and a constrained <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {Q}$</tex-math></inline-formula> function is established based on a receding-horizon <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {Q}$</tex-math></inline-formula>-learning framework. Then, an event-based optimal constrained control strategy and a novel event-triggered condition are obtained. Different from the existing event triggering conditions, the proposed condition includes control constraints and perturbation parameters, which means that the event triggering conditions can adaptively adjust according to the perturbations and constraints. In order to realize the control strategy, an identifier-actor-critic framework is designed: the identifier is used to estimate the nonlinear dynamic behavior of the system, the critic network is used to approximate the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {Q}$</tex-math></inline-formula> function, and the actor network is used to estimate the optimal control law. To address the asymmetric control constraints, the activation function of the actor network adopts an asymmetric constraint function. In addition, the control signal is transmitted aperiodically to reduce the calculation and transmission cost. Both critic and actor networks are updated only at the trigger moment determined by the event-triggered condition. The detailed Lyapunov analysis proves that the closed-loop system is asymptotically stable. Simulation verifies the effectiveness of the algorithm, and the results show that compared with the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {Q}$</tex-math></inline-formula>-learning algorithm and event-triggered heuristic dynamic programming (HDP) method, the proposed method has faster convergence speed and fewer control times.

title: Understanding Political Polarization with Generative AI: A Large-Scale Analysis of Voter Preferences on Social Media
authors: Loris Belcastro, Cristian Cosentino, Fabrizio Marozzo
abstract: In an era where users continuously express opinions across digital platforms, social media provides a large-scale lens on how people frame issues, justify choices, and react to events. Traditional approaches typically combine classifiers with unsupervised models to produce analytical outputs (e.g., spreadsheets, aggregate statistics), leaving human experts to interpret these outputs, connect signals across them, and translate them into explanations. The advent of Large Language Models (LLMs) has reshaped this process by enabling end-to-end pipelines that can both annotate large-scale text and generate coherent narrative reports that synthesize the evidence. This study presents an AI-based methodology for large-scale analysis of social media opinions during political events to estimate users’ propensity to support different candidates or factions. We classify and enrich posts with multidimensional annotations (e.g., candidate support, topics, sentiment) using a combination of traditional models and LLMs, inferring missing attributes from profile metadata and textual cues.We then aggregate annotations at the user level to build representative samples, validate their distributions against official population statistics, and generate concise reports that summarize the main motivations and issues driving support for each candidate or faction. We apply this methodology to X/Twitter data from swing states in the 2020 and 2024 U.S. presidential elections. For each state–candidate pair, the pipeline generates a report, which we compare against a baseline in which an LLM analyzes raw posts without any preprocessing or enrichment. The enriched configuration consistently outperforms the baseline across semantic similarity metrics, LLM-as-a-judge scores, and human preferences. Moreover, the motivations inferred from pre-election social-media data align closely with post-election expert summaries from sources such as Wikipedia and major newspapers, indicating that our approach can translate large-scale digital corpora into robust and interpretable political insight.

title: One-Way Metamirror Based on Janus Full-Space Metasurfaces
authors: Yiyuan Xu, Lili Gui, Hao Chen, Chuanshuo Wang, Xianglong Mei, Bingchen Li, Kun Xu
abstract: The growing demands for compact and asymmetric optical components in various systems have drawn significant attention to Janus metasurfaces (JMSs), which are known for their unique transmission properties that enable directional and asymmetric optical responses. However, metasurfaces that simultaneously multiplex the degrees of freedom of both the incident and outgoing directions to achieve a one-way mirror effect remain challenging to realize. In this work, we present Janus full-space metasurfaces (JFSMSs) that exhibit one-way-mirror optical behavior, whereby transmission or reflection is selected based solely on the direction of the incident wavevector relative to the metasurface. As proof of concept, a series of JFSMSs were numerically designed for holographic displays, optical encryption, vortex beam emitters, and bidirectional optical combiners. The proposed JFSMS strategy offers a promising route for advancing multifunctional and spatially multiplexed metasurfaces in the optical regime and extending related application scopes.

title: Cross-Modal Hierarchical Alignment Guided by Selective State Space Model and Domain Prior for Referring Remote Sensing Image Segmentation
authors: Yijie Zhang, Jian Cheng, Shimiao Fan, Jiabei Chen, Siyu Liu, Ziying Xia, Nyima Tashi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Referring Remote Sensing Image Segmentation (RRSIS) aims to predict the segmentation masks of corresponding ground objects based on text descriptions. Achieving accurate cross-modal alignment between text descriptions and visual content is crucial for this task. However, most existing methods rely heavily on feature-level cross-attention mechanisms based on the Transformer architecture. This approach is often inefficient and prone to information redundancy, which in turn interferes with the alignment process; moreover, due to the lack of domain prior for semantic understanding of ground objects, it remains challenging to achieve precise cross-modal alignment. To address the above issues, this paper proposes a cross-modal hierarchical alignment network (CMHA-Net) for RRSIS, which is guided by the selective state space model (SSM) and domain prior. Specifically, CMHA-Net adopts MambaVision to replace the Transformer architecture as the backbone for visual feature extraction and uses BERT to encode text. The proposed semantic selective alignment module (SSAM) leverages the domain prior provided by the pre-trained remote sensing vision foundation model (RS-VFM) to guide the selection of cross-modal features and the SSM for multi-directional weighted visual scanning, dynamically screening the visual regions with the highest semantic relevance, thereby achieving efficient cross-modal alignment. Furthermore, the prior-guided fine-grained alignment module (PFAM) leverages domain prior to facilitate dense cross-modal interaction and employs the SSM with a larger receptive field, thereby enabling fine-grained cross-modal alignment. In addition, we propose a prior-based cross-modal semantic consistency (CMSC) loss function to explicitly constrain the alignment relationship between images and text during the training process. Experiments conducted on the RefSegRS and RRSIS-D datasets demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance.

title:
Comprehensive Analysis of Highly Efficient La
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    NiMnO
                    <sub>6</sub>
                    Double Perovskite Solar Cell: Towards Lead-Free Sustainable Photovoltaics
authors: Md Sadullah, Akash Kumar Pradhan
abstract: Lead-free double perovskite materials, such as La2NiMnO6 (LNMO), have emerged as promising alternatives to toxic lead-based perovskites due to their non-toxicity, favorable band gap alignment with CH3NH3PbI3, and promising performance. Despite their potential, the optimization of lead-free double perovskite solar cells (DPSCs) remains underexplored. This study presents a numerical approach to model and optimize the performance of an environment-friendly DPSC with an FTO/ETL/LNMO/HTL structure. In this work, a comprehensive optimization process is carried out to select the most suitable electron transport layer (ETL), hole transport layer (HTL), and back metal contact (BMC) for enhanced device performance. Among the various candidates, ZnO (ETL) and CBTS (HTL) are identified as optimal choices. Furthermore, the impact of absorber layer thickness, doping density, and defect density on the overall device efficiency is thoroughly analyzed. Device optimization identified an absorber thickness of 1200 nm, acceptor doping of 5×1015 cm−3, and low defect density of 1014 cm−3 as critical for maximizing charge generation while minimizing recombination losses. Additionally, optimized resistive parameters (Rs ∼ 0.5 Ω · cm2, Rsh ∼ 105 Ω · cm2) further improved the device performance. The proposed DPSC structure, after all optimizations, achieves a remarkable power conversion efficiency (PCE) of 20.95%, with an open circuit voltage (Voc) of 0.7892 V, short circuit current (Jsc) of 31.53 mA/cm2, and fill factor (FF) of 84.18%, representing an idealized efficiency ceiling for LNMO-based oxide DPSC. Additionally, the effects of working temperature and parasitic resistance on device performance are evaluated. The results are compared with earlier experimental and theoretical studies, demonstrating the superior performance of the optimized DPSC. This work introduces a stable, non-toxic, and highly efficient DPSC model, offering a promising pathway toward sustainable and eco-friendly energy solutions.

title: Deep Learning-Based Regional Electricity Demand Prediction Using Smart Meter Data in Sri Lanka
authors: N.M.R.K. Jayathilaka, D.J.S. Warnasooriya, Kopisankar Nagarajah, T. Mukunthan, S. Jalini, V. Logeeshan, Chathura Wanigasekara
abstract: Precise short-term forecasting of regional and consumer level electricity demand is vital for energy management, especially in countries such as Sri Lanka, where significant energy challenges are faced. However, the increasing availability of smart meters’ data, existing models often lack regional specificity and do not adequately integrate external factors such as weather. This study proposes a robust deep learning framework for the prediction of real-time residential demand in Sri Lanka. A 3.5-year smart meter dataset was utilized, with 15-minute intervals, and combined with weather data. The energy demand being heavily dependent on human behavior, which is difficult to capture but it is significantly influenced by external environmental conditions. The methodology included advanced preprocessing and a generalized feature engineering pipeline, enhancing its adaptability to new regional contexts. Six models based on LSTM and GRU architectures were evaluated using incremental transfer learning strategy. Where models were trained on the Paris dataset and fine-tuned on Sri Lankan data. The analysis revealed different strengths of the model, with LSTM capturing long-term trends, GRU responding to short-term changes, and CNN-hybrids detecting sudden spikes. The GRU with the hybrid BPNN was identified as optimal, achieving the highest R² score of 0.8217 by combining the efficiency of GRU with the capacity of BPNN to model complex nonlinearities. These results were derived using datasets in which anomalies were intentionally retained to preserve real-world operational behaviour. The results confirm the potential of the framework to improve grid operational efficiency and support the development of sustainable energy systems.

title: Beyond Borders: Why Africa Matters for a Geospatially Connected Planet
authors: Abimbola Atijosan
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Magazine
abstract: Africa plays a crucial role in understanding Earth’s interconnected systems. Its geospatial data, observational networks, and growing scientific expertise inform global climate, environmental, and geophysical knowledge. Strengthening African capacity, satellite programs, and collaborative research ensures more accurate and actionable insights while promoting equity. By partnering with African institutions, the world can better monitor, predict, and respond to environmental change, making Africa central to the planet’s sustainable future.

title: Progressive Orthodontic Motion Planning based on Hierarchical Diffusion Transformer_supp1-3676039.mp4
authors: Yuanfeng Zhou
abstract: Orthodontic motion planning plays a crucial role in digital orthodontics by predicting tooth motion sequences to assist dentists in formulating treatment plans efficiently. Most prior work generates the entire intermediate tooth motion sequence given the initial and target tooth alignments. In practice, only the initial alignment of the patient is obtained. However, no existing method can predict the complete motion sequence using only the initial tooth alignment. To address this gap, we propose OrthoDiff, a novel target-free framework that uses only initial tooth alignment through a progressive generation strategy. This strategy generates tooth motion sequences by decomposing the entire motion sequence into multi-level motions, progressively constraining the inference space and reducing the complexity of target-free planning from coarse to fine. Moreover, we design a hierarchical diffusion transformer as the backbone of OrthoDiff, which treats tooth alignment as a sequence of tooth tokens and fully leverages the topological prior knowledge of the dental model. Through extensive evaluations, we demonstrate that our method significantly outperforms state-of-the-art techniques in target-free tooth motion generation. Ablation studies further confirm the efficacy of key components in our network design. Meanwhile, we also achieve state-of-the-art results in tooth target alignment prediction, benefiting from our framework. The code and data will be publicly available at https://github.com/Intelligent-Orthodontics/OrthoDiff.github.io.

title: Adaptive Multi-Energy State XRF Spectral Fusion and PSO-Wavelet Enhancement Algorithm for Trace Element Detection of Atmospheric Particulate Matter
authors: Mohai Yue, Qi Zhang, Feng Tian, Lan Rao, Fu Wang, Yun Teng, Zipeng Wang, Tongkang Dong
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: To overcome the interference caused by complex filter membrane backgrounds in energy-dispersive X-ray fluorescence (ED-XRF) analysis of trace elements in atmospheric particulate matter, this study proposes an adaptive spectral enhancement method based on multi-energy-state XRF fusion and particle swarm optimized wavelet processing. A three-energy-state XRF simulation framework is developed that accounts for nonlinear energy calibration, energy correlation effects, and filter membrane background contributions. Simulation results show that the proposed method improves both the signal-to-noise ratio and the detection limit by more than 11% compared with the PSO-Savitzky-Golay approach. The method is further validated using quartz and Teflon air filter samples, with experimental results in close agreement with theoretical predictions.

title: Joint Fleet Deployment and Ship Scheduling for Cost-Efficiency Optimization via a Two-Level Metaheuristic Algorithm
authors: Lei Gao, Ke Ke, Rui Wang, Bingfu Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Maritime transportation remains one of the most capital-intensive industries, where efficient vessel utilization critically impacts operational cost reduction and project punctuality. This paper addresses the challenge of simultaneously optimizing fleet deployment and ship scheduling, which mutually influences each other, aiming to minimize the total cost and vessel vacancy under complex operational constraints. To solve this computationally demanding problem, we propose a two-level metaheuristic-based optimization method for unsupervised exploration of the most time-efficient and cost-saving strategy with reduced computational expense. Extensive numerical experiments using simulated data validate the effectiveness of our approach, demonstrating its ability to achieve near-optimal solutions while maintaining tractable execution times.

title: Study on the Moisture-Induced Mechanism of the Buffer Layer in High-Voltage XLPE Insulated Cables
authors: Xiaoshun Dong, Zhen Chen, Liangliang Zhu, Yaxin Xu, Lin Chen, Qialihaer Aniwanbieke
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: High-voltage XLPE cables are core transmission equipment in modern power systems. Defects caused by moisture ingress into the buffer layer pose a serious threat to the safe and stable operation of these cables. Therefor, it is of great significance to identify the sources and mechanisms of moisture in the buffer layer. This paper analyzes the sources of moisture in the buffer layer from two perspectives: the decomposition of cross-linking by-products within the insulation, which generates gases, and the hygroscopic properties of the buffer-layer materials. We conducted experiments to detect and analyze XLPE by-products and to study the moisture absorption characteristics of the buffer layer. Additionally, we performed gas sampling tests on the buffer layer of in-service high-voltage cables. The results show that the moisture-absorbing performance of the water-blocking tape is significantly influenced by temperature and humidity; at a temperature of 30°C and a relative humidity of 80%, the water-absorption efficiency reaches its maximum, with an average moisture absorption mass of up to 120 mg. For an 110 kV, 800 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> cable, after degassing at 65°C for 135 hours, further conversion of peroxides inside the insulation upon commissioning can release up to 22.86 g of moisture. Moreover, there is a significant correlation among various characteristic gases within the buffer layer of in-service cables: the concentration of water vapor in the buffer layer is positively correlated with the oxygen concentration and negatively correlated with the methane concentration, confirming that both cross-linking by-product decomposition and atmospheric air contribute to moisture ingress into the buffer layer.

title: The Rise of AI-Generated Anime Avatars: Trends, Challenges, and Opportunities
authors: Fernanda Miyuki Yamada, João Paulo Gois, Hiroki Takahashi
abstract: The rise of 3-D anime-style avatars in gaming, virtual reality, and other digital media has driven significant interest in automated generation methods capable of capturing their distinctive visual characteristics. These include stylized proportions, expressive features, and nonphotorealistic rendering. This article reviews the advancements and challenges in using deep learning in 3-D anime-style avatar generation. We analyze the strengths and limitations of these methods in capturing the aesthetics of anime characters and supporting customization and animation. In addition, we identify and discuss open problems in the field, such as difficulties in resolution and detail preservation, and constraints regarding the animation of hair and loose clothing. This article aims to provide a comprehensive overview of the current state-of-the-art and identify promising research directions for advancing 3-D anime-style avatar generation.

title: Study of traditional and modern methods for diagnosing mycetoma: the case of grain culture - A systematic review
authors: Mor Diagne, Kpetchehoue Merveille Santi Zinsou, Idy Diop, Mass Gning
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: Mycetoma is a neglected tropical disease characterized by chronic granulomatous infection caused by bacteria (actinomycetes) or fungi (eumycetes). It mainly affects populations in Sudan, Senegal, and other tropical regions, causing significant medical, social, and economic impacts. Early diagnosis is crucial to limit complications, but remains difficult due to the limitations of current tools. Grain culture remains the gold standard method, although it is slow ($\mathbf{4}-\mathbf{6}$ weeks on average), resource-intensive, and dependent on specialist expertise. Molecular approaches, such as PCR, have improved accuracy (95%), but their accessibility is limited in low-resource countries. At the same time, recent advances in artificial intelligence, particularly the use of convolutional neural networks for histopathological image analysis, have shown accuracies greater than 90%, significantly reducing interpretation time. This review provides a structured classification and critical analysis of traditional methods (culture, histopathology, molecular tests) and intelligent approaches based on AI. We highlight their comparative performance, advantages, limitations, and prospects for integration in resource-limited settings. The objective is to systematically evaluate the added value of AI solutions compared to conventional methods in order to strengthen diagnostic care and reduce the impact of mycetoma on endemic populations.

title: Explainable Repair-Time Intelligence for Programmable Hearing Aids using Digital Repair Twins
authors: Sudhakavya Bodapati Venkata
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Returned programmable hearing aids are often evaluated using manual inspection and bench testing, even though many reported failures originate from software configuration changes, calibration drift, or post-deployment firmware updates. Existing diagnostic workflows lack mechanisms to reconstruct device-specific software evolution and to explain repair decisions in a traceable manner. This paper presents a repair-time intelligence framework based on Digital Repair Twins, which reconstruct the lifecycle state of an individual hearing aid at service intake by integrating manufacturing baselines, non-audio operational telemetry, and firmware deployment history derived from DevOps pipelines. Temporal deviation analysis and probabilistic failure attribution are applied to estimate the relative likelihood of software-induced versus hardware-induced faults while identifying affected subsystems. To support regulated medical device workflows, the framework provides technician-oriented explanations that link diagnostic outcomes to observable behavioral deviations and software context. A proxy evaluation using the NASA C-MAPSS degradation dataset demonstrates the feasibility of telemetry modeling, deviation detection, and explainability under repair-time constraints. Although real post-market repair data are not publicly available, the results indicate that Digital Repair Twins provide a practical and traceable foundation for improving repair efficiency in programmable medical devices.

title: ADMET-X: Machine Learning Framework for Predicting Pharmacokinetic and Toxicological Drug Properties
authors: Rohith Reddy G K, Sheik Arshad Ibrahim, Sayed Jahangir Ali, Thirumurugan M
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: High attrition rates in drug discovery are frequently driven by unfavourable absorption, distribution, metabolism, excretion, and toxicity (ADMET) properties, leading to late-stage failures despite promising efficacy. To address this challenge, we present ADMET-X, a biologically grounded and interpretable machine learning framework for comprehensive in silico prediction of pharmacokinetic and toxicological endpoints. ADMET-X integrates chemically meaningful physicochemical descriptors and structural fingerprints with ensemble learning models, including Random Forest and XGBoost, to capture complex structure–property relationships while preserving interpretability. The framework was evaluated on 31 benchmark datasets obtained from the Therapeutics Data Commons, spanning absorption, distribution, metabolism, excretion, and toxicity endpoints, using standardized training, validation, and test splits. Model performance was assessed using ROC–AUC and accuracy for classification tasks and RMSE and coefficient of determination (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) for regression tasks. ADMET-X achieved strong predictive performance across multiple endpoints, with ROC–AUC values exceeding 0.90 for key absorption, metabolism, and toxicity predictions, and competitive regression accuracy for physicochemical properties. Unlike black-box deep learning approaches, ADMET-X provides mechanistic insight through feature importance analysis, enabling transparent interpretation of predictions. Overall, the proposed framework offers a scalable, reproducible, and interpretable platform for early-stage ADMET screening and rational drug candidate prioritization.

title: Investigation of Forward Blocking Characteristics of Buried-Gate Field-Stop Barrier-Type Thyristor
authors: Guoyang Li, Hairong Li, Yucheng Li, Guangbin Zhang, Xiangyou Zeng, Zhixiong Huang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: This paper focuses on a theoretical analysis of the enhancement in overall electric field intensity and forward blocking characteristics of the Field-Stop Barrier-Type Thyristor (FS-BTH) compared to the conventional BTH. Based on Poisson's equation under the depletion approximation, analytical models are established for the P+N- abrupt junction of the conventional BTH and the PIN structure of the FS-BTH. The theoretical analysis indicates that the conventional BTH exhibits a triangular electric field distribution under lightly doped substrate conditions, where the maximum electric field intensity and blocking voltage are linearly limited by the drift region thickness. In contrast, the FS-BTH modulates the electric field profile from triangular to trapezoidal (quadrilateral) through the field-stop layer, significantly increasing the integral area of the electric field without increasing device thickness. An analytical expression incorporating the punch-through factor is derived. This formula quantitatively demonstrates how the FS-BTH achieves significant gains in overall electric field intensity and breakdown voltage for a fixed drift region thickness by "squaring" the field distribution. TCAD simulation results validate the accuracy of this theoretical model, confirming the physical superiority of the field-stop structure in optimizing the voltage-sustaining capability of power semiconductor devices.

title: Research on Machine Learning Models for Decision Analysis of UAV Inspections Based on Weather Warnings
authors: Chengming Jin, Donghui Tong, Zhenxian Yao, Xi Sun, Jianhang Sun, Shengfu Yan
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: To address the static feature bias and insufficient single-feature information in traditional models for UAV inspection decisions under weather warnings, this study proposes a machine-learning framework-driven regression (DRL) model that integrates attention feature fusion and a three-dimensional reward function. Experiments are based on UAV inspection and meteorological data from January 2023 to June 2024 for a provincial power grid (108,600 valid samples, covering five 500 kV lines). Random Forest (RF) and single-feature DRL models are used as comparison models, and their performance is verified through comparative and ablation experiments. Results show that MFF-DRL achieves an overall decision accuracy of 92.3%, an 8.7 percentage point improvement over RF and a 6.5 percentage point improvement over single-feature DRL. The misjudgment rate is as low as 3.1%, with only 1.2% of cases involving misjudgment of risk as safety. The inspection task completion rate is 89.5%, with an average decision time of 0.8 seconds. In severe adverse scenarios <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{V} \geqslant 12 ~\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R} \geqslant 0.7)$</tex>, the accuracy reaches 79.8%, a 10.9 percentage point improvement over RF. Ablation experiments confirm that the attention layer improves overall accuracy by 6.2 percentage points and significantly reduces the number of repeated inspections. While this research effectively optimizes inspection decisions, it lacks adaptability to extreme and rare weather conditions and does not cover multi-UAV collaborative scenarios. Future work aims to improve its practicality through online learning mechanisms and multi-UAV decision-making extensions.

title: Edge Intelligence for Sustainable Urban Mobility: NPU-Accelerated AI in Microcontrollers for Predictive EV Telematics and Fleet Optimization
authors: Akash Ambulkar, Rahul Khedkar, Sakshi Jadhav
abstract: The global shift towards electric vehicles (EVs) necessitates a new generation of intelligent telematics systems, particularly for large-scale applications like bus fleets. Conventional cloud-dependent telematics suffer from inherent limitations including data transmission latency, significant operational costs, and an inability to provide real-time, on-vehicle predictive analytics. This paper presents a novel approach by integrating Artificial Intelligence (AI) on Network Processing Units (NPUs) directly into low-power microcontrollers. This edge-centric paradigm enables real-time data processing and decision-making on the vehicle itself, fundamentally transforming how EV fleets are managed. Our key contributions include the development and deployment of lightweight, on-device AI models for highly accurate, dynamic range prediction that accounts for real-world variables, thereby mitigating range anxiety. The system also implements a proactive battery State of Health <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{SoH})$</tex> prediction model, shifting maintenance from a reactive to a predictive paradigm to prolong battery longevity. Furthermore, AI-driven insights are used to optimize driver behavior and route planning, leading to improved energy efficiency. Crucially, on-device processing significantly reduces the volume of data transmitted to the cloud, lowering operational costs and enhancing the speed of critical anomaly detection. Our evaluation, based on a case study of an EV bus fleet, demonstrates the tangible performance gains in terms of accuracy, cost reduction, and operational efficiency, showcasing a significant step towards sustainable and intelligent urban transportation.

title: Federated Learning and PCA-Kmeans Clustering: Optimizing Power Demand Prediction Under the Premise of Data Security
authors: Xinyue Shi, Yandi Wang, Xiaoyu Wang, Fang Shang, Ze Yuan, He Wang, Yang Lin, Qiaoyun Tian
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: This study addresses the issue of accuracy in electricity consumption forecasting within power systems and ensures the security and privacy of data. We propose an innovative federated learning-based model training method that incorporates the refinement of the AvgFedLearning algorithm, the application of PCA for feature extraction, the selection of models based on the golden section principle, and the use of the elbow method to determine the optimal number of clusters for K-means analysis, integrated with the FourierGNN prediction model. The aim is to enhance the accuracy of power demand forecasting while ensuring data security and privacy protection. The results demonstrate that, on both non-independent and identically distributed (Non-IID) and independent and identically distributed (IID) datasets, the new model has achieved a reduction of over 10% in key error metrics such as Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Root Mean Square Error (RMSE), and Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) compared to FourierGNN, while exhibiting high privacy protection characteristics. This outcome not only provides a more precise tool for power system planning in terms of electricity consumption forecasting but also effectively balances data privacy protection with forecasting accuracy, holding significant practical application value and theoretical significance for the intelligent development of the power industry.

title: Adaptive Spatio-Temporal Graph Neural Networks for Real-Time Bitcoin Prediction and Automated Trading
authors: Vignesh M, Uday Akash E M, A.Nithya
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: In this paper, we develop an end-to-end traffic- adaptive deep learning system capable of predicting Bitcoin prices in real-time and executing automated trading based on the policies of extremely volatile cryptocurrency markets. The suggested system combines several new extended neural paradigms in order to solve the issues of temporal dynamics, relations among assets, multidimensional feature representation and low-latency functionality. Particularly, Adaptive Spatio-Temporal Graph Neural Networks evolving correlations between Bitcoin and other related financial indicators and Hypercomplex Neural Networks that utilize quaternion representations of both OHLCV features (i.e. gain access to geometric dependencies between them). The Neural Ordinary Differential Equations as well as Continuous-time market dynamics, are developed to be sensitive to change of rotation and abnormal timing characteristics. A Dual-Path Spiking Neural Network additionally guarantees energy-efficient inference with low latency that can be deployed to real-time. The predictive forecasting is connected to an automated executing engine over the Zerodha Kite API so that forecasts can be translated into actual trading directly. Experimental analysis of historical Bitcoin data shows that it can better predict future price movements, has shorter inference latency, or better trading performance than standard statistical models and deep learning baseline models. The findings indicate the strength, extendability, and feasibility of the suggested scheme of intelligent cryptocurrency trading in dynamic market settings.

title: Deep Learning-Based Uncertainty-Driven Robust Time Series Forecasting for Backend Service Metrics
authors: Sijia Li, Chengda Xu, Chi Zhang, Bolin Chen, Zizhao Zhang, Zixiao Huang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: This study addresses the challenge of forecasting backend service metrics in highly dynamic and disturbed environments and proposes a robust time series prediction framework based on uncertainty estimation. The method first applies a multi-scale encoding structure to decompose and represent service metric sequences, allowing the model to capture mixed characteristics of trends, periodic patterns, and noise disturbances. It then constructs a cross-sequence dependency modeling module to describe structural coupling among multiple metrics so that the model can identify implicit interaction patterns across different service nodes in backend systems. On this basis, the framework outputs both the mean and variance of future metrics through distributed prediction heads, enabling joint characterization of predictions and their associated risk ranges. A noise response mechanism is incorporated to enhance model stability under anomalous disturbances. To improve the controllability of the overall prediction process, a risk adjustment module is designed to transform uncertainty signals into actionable adjustment factors, allowing the model to maintain consistent outputs in high volatility environments. The experiments evaluate hyperparameter sensitivity, environmental sensitivity, and data sensitivity. The results show that the proposed framework maintains high stability and robustness under multiple source disturbances and effectively suppresses error propagation caused by anomalous samples, structural changes, and system scaling. By jointly modeling point predictions and risk estimation, the study verifies the effectiveness of an uncertainty-driven robust forecasting framework for backend metric management and system reliability, providing a scalable technical path for data-driven analysis in complex systems.

title: Adaptive Risk-Aware Ride Assignment (ARARA) Algorithm to Improve Efficiency to Lower Cancellation Rates in Bengaluru
authors: Sheetal, A Alli, S Deepa, Jitha Janardhanan, N Vasantha Kumari, F Rosita Kamala
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: Ride cancellations on urban mobility platforms like Rapido, OLA, Uber and other service provide platforms are negatively impacting user experience, driver earnings, and platform efficiency due to high cancellation of rides. This study addresses the challenge by developing a machine learning based adaptive userride matching algorithm that is trained on real world ride dataset from Bengaluru. The dataset includes features such as ride time, source, destination, distance, fare, payment method, and ride status. Through data preprocessing and feature engineering, key patterns influencing ride cancellations are identified. A classification model is developed to predict the likelihood of cancellation before ride assignment by using few Machine learning models among various model XGBoost and Logistic Regression outperformed with nearly $\mathbf{9 0 \%}$ accuracy. Later to enhance the performance in allocation based on cancellation prediction the ARARA algorithm suggests that reallocates rides dynamically based on cancellation risk using inference and assignment Algorithm. Experimental results shows that how to reduce cancellation rates and improved accuracy by choosing best allocation based on top three best captains for allocation to optimize chances of cancellation. This framework can be integrated by ride platforms to enhance service reliability and optimize fleet efficiency.

title: Design and Implementation of a Novel FPGA-based Secure Electronic Voting System using Fingerprint Authentication
authors: Y. Chalapathi Rao, Doti Giribabu, Harini Nallanagula, Bandla Soumya
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The design and implementation of hardware fingerprint based EVM on Zynq-7000 FPGA is presented. The objective of designing this system is to provide security and transparency in the process of voting with fast and reliable operation. As we know, there are many EVMs in the market, but the authentication is manual or not very stringent. This causes several issues like multiple and illegal voting. To address this problem, we implemented biometric authentication on the FPGA device. The FPGA’s Block RAM saves the fingerprint data as .mem files after conversion into binary bitstreams. This allows for on-chip authentication and the fingerprint data matching occurs inside the hardware. The design is entirely coded in Verilog HDL. The operations of fingerprint matching, storing of votes and displaying of results are carried out by using various modules. After the design synthesis on the practical device only authenticated user can vote. No duplicate entries are allowed thus all the data is saved perfectly. The voter registration procedure was confirmed to have correct response and stable output at the display unit. As a result, the design has proven to function reliably as a tamper resistant voting machine. Moreover, the findings reveal the implementation of FPGAs.

title: Bio-Inspired Hexacell Oval Patch Sensor for Non-Invasive Blood Glucose Sensing
authors: Wansong Gao, Wei Chen, Abhishek Kandwal, Pawan Kumar, Qifeng Lu, Yizhe Ding
publish: 2026 Symposium on Microwave, Antenna, and Propagation (SMAP)
abstract: This work proposes an innovative sensor based on bioinspired hexacell structure mimicking certain vascular networks for non-invasive blood glucose sensing and monitoring. The design has been verified using full wave EM 3D simulations and numerically to analyze sensitivity features. The sensor has been tested by varying dielectric constants from 2.2 to 11.9 in order to analyze the shifts in frequencies that can provide sensitivity. Variations have also been introduced over the same material with glucose overlays that can further support the validations for sensitivity factor. The optimizations resulted in the fact that for the proposed sensor design, Rogers RT-duroid 5880 is the most effective dielectric material for the current work. The patch micro-cavities strengthen near-field coupling and achieve a 50Ω impedance match for this dielectric in the operating frequencies with and without glucose. Radiation efficiency stays above 82% from 9-11 GHz, and the radiation pattern shows 10.2 dBi gain, narrow beamwidth and low side-lobes. After glucose loading, the resonance shifts markedly, exhibiting the high sensitivity. Near-field hotspots are localised at patch edges and the feed gap, creating a steep gradient that can detect even minute glucose variations. The sensor offers high sensitivity, high efficiency and biosafety, providing a viable electromagnetic solution for non-invasive, real-time glucose monitoring.

title: Strict One-Shot Prototypical Networks with CosFace Pretraining for Strata Identification in Shield Tunneling
authors: Kuanfeng Fu, Xiaoming Shao, Chao Zheng, Jinjin Zhang, Tianming Zhan
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Accurate real-time identification of geological strata is critical for safe and efficient shield tunneling, yet labeled machine-operation data are typically scarce, imbalanced, and noisy. We propose a strict one-shot metric-learning framework that couples CosFace-supervised pretraining with a deployment-aligned prototypical inference pipeline. In Stage A, a lightweight multilayer perceptron (MLP) encoder is trained on the train split using a large-margin cosine classifier to carve a discriminative embedding space. In Stage B, we enforce strict one-shot: for each stratum, a single support sample selected as the medoid in the learned embedding space is fixed and reused throughout episodic fine-tuning, validation, and testing. Episodes employ class-balanced query sampling with per-epoch usage caps and replacement fallback to mitigate degeneracy under label scarcity. In inference, we compare queries only to the fixed train supports; a scalar cosine temperature is calibrated on the validation split to sharpen decision boundaries without leaking information. The entire pipeline operates on a single table with a fixed 70/10/20 split and Min–Max normalization fit on training data only, making the protocol simple and reproducible. In a metro shield data set, the proposed method achieves 90.79% accuracy and 90.05% macro-F1 on the held-out test split, improving over full-supervised MLP and ProtoNet baselines by roughly 2–3 percentage points while retaining a strict one-shot constraint and robust minority-class behavior.

title: Molecular Simulation Study on the Influence of Different Pyrolysis Products in Oil-Paper Insulation Systems on Insulation Failure Risk
authors: Yifan Zhong, Qiulin Chen, Ran Zhuo, Sicheng Zhao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: In oil-immersed transformers, the thermal stability of oil-paper insulation systems directly impacts the equipment's insulation lifespan and operational safety. When system temperatures rise, oil-paper materials undergo pyrolysis, generating gases such as water vapor and hydrogen. These microscopic molecular behaviors determine the risk of localized electric field distortion and bubble discharge. This study employs molecular dynamics simulations to construct mineral oil models incorporating water molecules and hydrogen molecules. It systematically analyzes the spatial distribution, aggregation characteristics, and migration dynamics of both molecules under temperature-rising conditions. Results indicate that as temperature increases, water molecules readily form clusters through hydrogen bonding interactions. This reduces dielectric homogeneity within the oil, heightening the likelihood of bubble nucleation and partial discharge. Conversely, hydrogen molecules remain dispersed and diffusive, exerting a weaker disruptive effect on insulation uniformity. This study reveals the distinct microscopic behaviors of different pyrolysis gases within transformer insulating oil, providing theoretical foundations for understanding thermal failure mechanisms in oil-paper insulation systems and improving thermal stability designs.

title: Research on anomaly detection and failure prediction model of lithium iron phosphate battery cluster based on consistency feature extraction
authors: Shuo Zhang, Wenbin Sun, Shikai Ye, Wenxian Lin
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: Aiming at the application of lithium iron phosphate battery (LFP) in energy storage system, a battery cluster anomaly detection and failure prediction model (CFE-ADFP) based on consistency feature extraction is proposed. In view of the fact that the inconsistency of individual performance in the battery cluster may lead to the efficiency decline and safety accidents of the energy storage system, this study aims to ensure the safe operation of the energy storage system and reduce the life cycle cost through real-time anomaly detection and failure prediction. In this paper, the basic principle of phase consistency detection algorithm is adopted, and a multi-dimensional feature fusion and task coupling learning model is constructed by combining accurate estimation of state of charge (SOC) and data-driven health state evaluation method. Through the consistency index system of dynamic weight time domain characteristics and graph structure characteristics, the model enhances the quantitative ability of early anomaly identification and degradation synchronization between monomers. At the same time, a dual-branch multi-task learning framework is designed, which uses 1D-CNN and attention mechanism to capture the abnormal patterns of time series respectively, and models the capacity degradation trajectory based on Wiener process. The experimental results show that the CFE-ADFP model is superior to the existing methods in anomaly detection and failure prediction, which provides an effective technical means for the health management of lithium iron phosphate batteries.

title: Investigation of Three-Phase Tertiary Winding for Integrated Self-Excitation in Dump Trucks
authors: Mohammad Sarvi-Maraghi, Omid Khanzadeh, Abolfazl Vahedi, Mahdi Abolghasemi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: Transportation costs of mineral products have a significant impact on their final price; therefore, the reliability of the diesel-electric propulsion systems of mining dump trucks is of special importance, whose most critical subsystem is the alternator excitation system. Given the space and weight constraints in these vehicles, integrated excitation systems are of considerable interest. In this paper, the windings employed in such systems on a 3.8kVA alternator have been studied, and to overcome the limitations of previous structures, a three-phase tertiary winding has been proposed. Two-dimensional FEM results show that, without increasing the machine volume and with a slot fill factor of 0.4, the required excitation power can be supplied by this winding. Moreover, the output-voltage THD equals 2.44%, indicating that the alternator’s output-voltage quality is preserved in the presence of this winding. These results indicate that an integrated self-excitation system with a three-phase tertiary winding is a compact and industrial solution for mining dump trucks.

title: Zero-Shot Material Transfer Methods for Complex Geometries: DepthAnythingV2 and Dual-Module Enhancement
authors: Bo Niu, Liang Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: To address issues such as depth discontinuities and texture misalignment that frequently occur in complex geometric scenes within the Zero-shot Material Transfer (ZeST) framework, we propose an improved method for zero-shot material transfer. First, we replace the Dense Prediction Transformer (DPT) depth estimator in the original framework with DepthAnythingV2 to obtain more stable geometric priors, significantly reducing depth discontinuities in irregular objects and cross-category scenes. Building upon this foundation, we design a Depth Feature Alignment Module (DFAM) to mitigate discrepancies between depth geometry and visual features in the semantic space, achieving coordinated fusion of geometry and texture through cross-attention mechanisms. Concurrently, we introduce a Depth Refinement Module (DRM) that enhances depth edge continuity and accuracy by leveraging local structure encoding and detail optimization. Embedding these modules into a ControlNet-guided diffusion-based image inpainting workflow enables precise mapping of object materials. Experimental results demonstrate that our method outperforms the ZeST framework in material consistency and structural fidelity across multiple complex object categories. It also achieves comprehensive improvements in quantitative metrics such as Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR), Learned Perceptual Image Patch Similarity (LPIPS), and Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training (CLIP). - Image Pre-training (CLIP) models. Requiring no additional training beyond fine-tuning lightweight modules, this method demonstrates strong practicality for tasks like digital asset generation and virtual scene material reconstruction.

title: End-Side License Plate Detection Model Based on Improved YOLOv8s with Depth-Separable CNNs
authors: Junhong Li, Chengxin Gao
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Aiming at the limitations of existing license plate detection algorithms in terms of detection accuracy and model lightweighting, as well as the insufficient extraction of color features, this paper proposes an end-side license plate detection model based on lightweight YOLOv8s with depth-separable convolutional neural network (CNN). In the license plate region detection stage, the number of output channels of the convolutional layer in the YOLOv8s backbone network and detection head are halved by combining migration learning and parameter fine-tuning strategies to optimize its feature extraction capability. In the license plate information parsing stage, a multi-task framework is used to extract robust features through a depth-separable convolutional backbone network, which are input into the character recognition sub-network and the color classification sub-network, respectively. The character recognition sub-network generates the logarithmic probability distribution of the character sequence through pooling, convolution and Logsoftmax, and decodes it in combination with the CTC loss function to recognize the license plate text. The color classification sub-network generates feature vectors by convolution, batch normalization, ReLU activation and global average pooling to discriminate license plate color. The experimental results show that, relative to YOLOv8n, the computation of the model proposed in this paper increases by only 0.03 GFLOPs, while the average mAP@0.5 rises by 4.3% to 79.2%, and the average mAP@[0.5:0.9] increases by 4.7% to 76.6%.

title: Intelligent Personalized Online Learning Platform Leveraging Random Forest and NLP for Enhanced Learner Engagement
authors: P. Bharathi, G. H Vandhana, S. Abirami
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The rapid growth of digital education has highlighted the need for personalized, adaptive, and intelligent online learning systems capable of catering to individual learner needs. Conventional e-learning platforms often provide static content and uniform assessments, resulting in limited engagement, suboptimal learning outcomes, and inadequate support for diverse learner abilities. This paper presents an AI-powered adaptive personalized e-learning and tutorial system that integrates Random Forest and Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques to dynamically tailor learning experiences. The system predicts learner performance using Random Forest on structured (quiz scores, engagement metrics) and unstructured (textual feedback, chatbot queries) data, enabling adaptive quiz difficulty, personalized feedback, and recommendation of learning materials. NLP algorithms, including TF-IDF–based feature extraction, extractive content summarization, and rule-based chatbot assistance, further enhance content accessibility, comprehension, and interactive support. Extensive evaluation demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed system. Compared to traditional Decision Tree and Naïve Bayes–based models, the Random Forest–driven platform exhibits higher accuracy (0.928), precision (0.921), recall (0.915), and F1-score (0.918) in predicting learner performance. Adaptive quizzes, content summarization, chatbot assistance, and recommendation modules achieve superior metrics across all parameters, including engagement, learning gain, topic mastery, and response efficiency. System-level performance analysis indicates improved response time, throughput, and resource utilization, while overall system effectiveness confirms significant gains in learner performance, engagement, and adaptive content utilization. The results highlight that the integration of ML and NLP techniques provides a robust, scalable, and intelligent environment that promotes self-paced learning, continuous assessment, and personalized educational support, making it suitable for academic, technical, and professional e-learning applications.

title: Forensic Sketch-based Face Recognition & Retrieval System using Deep Learning
authors: Janhavi Nandish, Rifa Noorain, BiBi Sadiya, Ayman Shariff, Mohammed Taj Khan M.F
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Forensic sketch-based face recognition is a challenging problem due to the significant domain gap between hand-drawn sketches and real facial photographs. Traditional manual matching approaches are time-consuming, subjective, and prone to human bias, limiting their effectiveness in modern law enforcement investigations. This paper presents an AI-driven forensic sketch-based face recognition and retrieval system using deep learning to automate and enhance suspect identification. The proposed system employs a fine-tuned InsightFace model for robust facial embedding extraction and integrates FAISS-based similarity indexing to enable efficient large-scale retrieval. A unified processing pipeline performs face detection, embedding generation, and similarity matching to retrieve the top-ranked candidates with confidence scores. Experimental evaluation on paired sketch–photo datasets demonstrates an accuracy of 82–88% with sub-second response times, achieving a 12% improvement over traditional manual methods. The system further incorporates gender-based filtering, batch processing, and real-time analytics through a user-friendly web interface. Scalability analysis confirms reliable performance with increasing database size, while ethical and legal considerations are addressed to ensure responsible deployment. The proposed solution offers a practical, scalable, and deployable framework for modern forensic investigations.

title: Intelligent Identification of Power Outage Events and Prediction of Impact Range by Integrating XGBoost and Graph Neural Network
authors: Bo Peng, Ruiping Pan, Yanyan Li, Jun Fu, Meicui Qin
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Current power grid outage identification and impact assessment still face challenges such as fragmented data sources, fragmented customer demand information, and interference from invalid outage data. These issues lead to inaccurate outage assessment results, making it difficult to meet the high standards of smart grid monitoring, early warning, and customer service. This paper proposes a modeling approach that integrates multi-source outage data with intelligent models to construct a framework for outage assessment and prediction based on extreme gradient boosting (XGBoost) and graph neural networks (GNNs). First, a multi-source data aggregation analysis is conducted, linking 95598 customer complaints with data from the Collection 2.0 and Marketing 2.0 systems, as well as marketing meter outage and restoration data, to form a unified dataset covering line outages, single distribution transformers, and substation outages. A power outage analysis model is then constructed to automatically remove invalid data such as short-term outages, terminal failures, and duplicate reports. Parameters are optimized based on provincial power outage analysis rules and inter-provincial differences. A GNN is then used to extract topological dependencies and global features. The embedded vectors and business features are then input into XGBoost to identify effective power outage events and quantify their impact. Statistical analysis is performed across dimensions such as province, city, county, station, substation, and user type. Experimental results show that this method achieves 94.8% accuracy in identifying effective power outages and reduces the average error in predicting the impact range to 1.7%, demonstrating significant advantages in both effectiveness and adaptability.

title: A Hierarchical Approach to Modeling and Controlling Complex Systems
authors: Oksana Porubay, Isamiddin Siddikov, Nilufar Abdulhamidova, Ozodbek Rakhmonov
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Science and Communications Technologies (ICISCT)
abstract: The article discusses the task of constructing effective models for automated control of large complex systems, in particular, autonomous systems (AS). The main focus is on the problem of the impossibility of direct use of functional transformations due to the high dimensionality and uncertainty of the processes described. The paper proposes a hierarchical approach to modeling based on dividing the general ASIS model into a macro model of the process and a micro model of the system. The macro model describes the relationships between goals and operations at the level of the global trajectory of the system’s functioning, while the micro model reflects the interaction of individual modules and elements that implement these goals. Oriented multigraphs of goals and modular networks are introduced as elementary model structures, which allow for automatic model synthesis, take into account the factor of human participation in the control loop, and ensure adaptation to changing external conditions. The proposed approach provides the possibility of a multi-level approximation to optimal control actions, simplifies the solution of optimization problems, and opens up prospects for application in artificial intelligence and intelligent control systems.

title: Research on Energy Efficiency Modeling and Control Algorithm of 220kV Fully Enclosed Isolation Switch for Green and Low Carbon Development
authors: Yuandong Wang, Yang Cao, Guoyan Zhang, Haiming Li, Heguang Song
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: In order to respond to the green and low-carbon development strategy and promote the intelligent operation and management of high-voltage equipment, this paper focuses on the energy efficiency optimization of 220kV fully enclosed disconnector, and studies the modeling and control algorithm. Aiming at the problems of poor adaptability and large fluctuation of energy consumption of traditional control strategy under complex working conditions, an intelligent control method combining equipment characteristics and deep Q network (DQN) is proposed. Firstly, based on the electrical characteristics and operation mechanism, the energy efficiency model of hybrid drive is constructed. Then, a DKN control strategy suitable for the operating characteristics of high-voltage equipment is designed to realize the dynamic adjustment of switching action and the improvement of energy efficiency. The experimental results show that under the same operating conditions, the average single operation energy consumption of disconnector is reduced by about 8.6%, the fluctuation range of contact resistance is obviously narrowed, and the stability of response time is also improved after adopting DKN control strategy. The prediction error of the model is controlled within 5%, which has good engineering application potential. Introducing Deep reinforcement learning (DRL) into the control of high-voltage switchgear can effectively improve operating efficiency and reduce carbon emissions, and provide a feasible technical path for the green transformation of power system.

title: Design of a Low-Power Single-Ended 11T SRAM Cell with Improved Write Margin
authors: Lakshmi Barla, Mamidipaka Hema, Kaparapu Babulu
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The primary applications of Static Random-Access Memory (SRAM) technology are in Cache Memory Systems as well as embedded types of memory; therefore, SRAM is generally required to operate at low power levels to allow for maximum stability. In current generation of deeply scaled fabrication processes, conventional SRAM cells tend to have problems associated with increased leakage power, decreased stability, and/or reduced reliability. The paper presents construction of a Single-Ended 11-Transistor (SE11T) SRAM cell using 18 nm FinFET technology. The structure of the 11T SRAM cell is essentially the existing 10T SRAM cell with an addition of one NMOS transistor to improve both read and write stability. The design of SRAM cell using FinFET technology improves control of leakage current and diminishes the amount of short-channel effect. Simulation results show that the proposed SE11T SRAM cell provides a better level of Static Noise Margin (SNM) and increased energy efficiency, than conventional SRAM cells, demonstrating the overall efficacy of the proposed SRAM cell for use in future low-power, high-reliability memory systems.

title: Distribution Network Planning and Reliability Collaborative Optimization Considering Distributed New Energy
authors: Xiaobo Wang, Xiaoyuan Chen, Jing Mao, Xingxing Zhou
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: With the large-scale access of distributed new energy, the power flow distribution of distribution network has changed significantly, and the system uncertainty has increased, which has brought many challenges to the planning and operation of distribution network, such as voltage fluctuation and reliability decline. In order to meet these challenges, this paper proposes a two-stage collaborative optimization model, which realizes the efficient operation of power system through the close cooperation of planning and operation stages. In the planning stage, optimize the grid structure, line capacity and the location and constant capacity of distributed generation (DG); The operation stage focuses on distributed energy output scheduling, energy storage charging and discharging strategy and demand response measures. By constructing a multi-objective optimization function, the total cost is minimized and the system reliability is maximized, and various constraints are considered, including DG's permeability constraint, capacity upper limit constraint, dynamic power balance constraint and voltage security constraint. The improved NSGA-II algorithm combined with Benders decomposition is used to accelerate the solution, which ensures the feasibility and efficiency of the optimization scheme. The effectiveness of the proposed method is verified by the actual case analysis of coastal industrial parks. After optimization, the reliability of the system is significantly improved, SAIDI and SAIFI are reduced by 60%, and the voltage qualification rate is increased to 98%. The total life cycle cost is reduced by 5%, and the abandonment rate of new energy is reduced by 8%, which shows that the collaborative optimization method has obvious advantages in improving the economy and reliability of distribution network, and provides new ideas and methods for distribution network planning under distributed new energy access.

title: Research on Optimization Algorithm of Transmission Line Inspection Path Based on UAV and Satellite Cooperation
authors: Jinhu Liu, Lin Chen, Shuntao Huang, Xiao Zeng, Danbing Liu, Di Li, Yadi Wang
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: In this paper, in order to solve the problem that the demand of transmission line inspection increases rapidly and the traditional inspection method is inefficient, an optimization algorithm of transmission line inspection path based on UAV and satellite cooperation is proposed. The algorithm determines the distribution of towers and potential risk areas through large-scale monitoring data provided by satellites, and UAV conducts local detailed investigation based on this information to identify equipment defects and accurately locate fault points. A task allocation mechanism including satellite global monitoring and UAV local detailed investigation is studied and constructed, and a task priority model based on historical fault data is established. At the same time, combining the advantages of Genetic Algorithm (GA) and Ant Colony Optimization Algorithm (ACO), a hybrid optimization algorithm is designed, and a dynamic weight adjustment mechanism is introduced to balance the conflict among path length, energy consumption and coverage. The experimental results show that the algorithm is superior to the traditional GA and pure ACO algorithm in path length shortening rate, high-risk task coverage rate and energy consumption stability, which significantly improves the inspection efficiency and intelligence level, and provides strong support for the efficient implementation of transmission line inspection.

title: A large model API response quality prediction model based on least squares vector machine and SHAP interpretability analysis
authors: Haorui Gao, Wenjun Zeng, Jingtao Zhang, Youqing Liang
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Large model apis have become the core infrastructure for enterprises to build AI applications and achieve intelligent interaction of AI agents. The response quality of large model apis directly affects user experience and business efficiency. Constructing models that can predict response quality in advance is a key requirement for the efficient application of large model apis. This paper proposes a regression model that integrates least squares vector Machine (LS-SVM) and SHAP interpretability analysis, providing a solution that takes into account both high accuracy and high interpretability for the prediction of API response quality of large models. The study first conducted a correlation analysis, and then carried out experiments with seven models, namely Decision tree, Random Forest, Adaboost, Extratrees, CatBoost, LightGBM, and XGBoost, as controls. From the perspective of evaluation indicators, the comprehensive prediction performance of the proposed model (Our model) is significantly better than that of most control models, and only some indicators are close to Extratrees. In terms of error indicators, this model performs outstandingly: The MSE is 0.918. Lower than 1.551 of Decision tree, 1.17 of Random Forest, 1.108 of Adaboost, 1.171 of CatBoost, 1.218 of LightGBM and 1.231 of XGBoost It is only slightly lower than Extratrees' 0.973; The RMSE is 0.958, which is lower than that of most models and smaller than Extratrees' 0.987. The MAE is 0.784, which is the lowest value among all models and is superior to Extratrees' 0.803 and other models. The MAPE is 11.722, nearly on par with Extratrees' 11.743 and lower than the rest of the models. The model's explanatory power index R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> is 0.404, which is slightly lower than Extratrees' 0.415 but higher than the other five models, indicating a strong ability to explain data variations. Overall, this model is at an excellent level in terms of error control and explanatory power. Its research results can provide reliable technical support for the quality control of enterprise large model API responses, help improve the stability of AI applications and business efficiency, and promote the efficient implementation of large model apis in actual scenarios.

title: Joint Delay–Energy Optimization of an 8-Bit Carry Lookahead Adder using Logical Effort and Voltage Scaling
authors: Qianyu Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: This paper proposes a critical-path-oriented delay–energy optimization framework for an 8-bit carry lookahead adder (CLA). Instead of optimizing the entire circuit, the optimization problem is reorganized around the dominant carry propagation path that determines most of the circuit delay. This reorganization reduces modeling complexity while preserving the main performance and energy bottleneck of the CLA. Based on this idea, a simplified and physically consistent delay–energy model is constructed using logical effort theory. The effects of diffusion capacitance and gate capacitance between adjacent stages are explicitly separated, which allows transistor sizing to be directly linked to both delay and energy. Supply voltage scaling is modeled independently from transistor sizing, and the two are jointly optimized under realistic device constraints with a strict sizing mapping rule to ensure physical realizability and consistency with SPICE simulation. Using this unified framework, the delay–energy trade-off on the critical path is analyzed and explained. The results show that aggressive minimum-delay design leads to diminishing returns, where small delay reductions cause a significant increase in energy consumption. In contrast, relaxing the delay constraint enables significant energy savings. Although only the critical path of an 8-bit CLA is optimized, the proposed framework captures the dominant delay and energy behavior of the circuit, providing clear design insights for early-stage optimization of CLAs and other CMOS arithmetic circuits.

title: Design and Analysis of a High-Sensitivity Photonic Crystal Pressure Sensor with Hexagonal Ring Resonator
authors: Ranjitha Krishna R, Usha Desai, Santhosh Kumar K B, Sushma N, Indira Bahaddur, P C Srikanth
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: This paper presents the design and performance analysis of a compact and efficient pressure sensor using a two-dimensional photonic crystal (2D-PC) structure integrated with a hexagonal ring resonator. The proposed sensor uses two quasi-waveguides coupled to a hexagonal ring cavity to detect pressure variations via refractive-index modulation. The optical behavior is modeled and simulated using the Finite-Difference Time-Domain (FDTD) method. Key performance metrics resonant wavelength shift, quality factor (Q), normalized output power, and sensitivity are analyzed. Simulation results show a resonant wavelength near 1.5708 µm with a full width at half maximum (FWHM) ≈ 0.06 nm, resulting in a high Q ≈ 2.6×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sup>. The sensor exhibits a sensitivity of 1.35 nm/MPa over the evaluated pressure range (0–6 MPa) with an approximately linear wavelength shift, confirming the device’s reliability for pressure measurement. Compact size, high spectral resolution, and compatibility with integrated photonic circuits make this design suitable for biomedical, industrial, and environmental monitoring applications.

title: Erratum to “ LC Repeater-Inspired Battery-Free, Chipless, Flexible Body Sensor Network With Lightweight Reader”
authors: En-Zhu Lyu, Yen-Ling Sung, Dong-Yu Hsu, Guan-Lin Su, Ting-Wei Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: In [1], (4), (6), and (7) in Section II-A were incorrectly typeset in the current published version of the article, compared with the accepted version from IEEE author gateway. Three issues were identified as follows.

title:
Distributivity and conditional distributivity of uninorm over a noncontinuous t-norm T
                    <sub>A</sub>
                    with applications in ensemble classifiers
authors: Liteng Zheng, Gang Li
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: This paper investigates the (conditional) distributivity of uninorms over a noncontinuous t-norm, denoted as T<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">A</inf>. Extending prior work on continuous t-norms, we demonstrate that these two properties are not equivalent in this case. This theoretical finding deepens our understanding of uninorm behavior and enriches fuzzy algebraic theory. Furthermore, it may provide a novel pathway for constructing highly interpretable and robust ensemble classifiers, as the principles of operator distributivity can inform the design of optimized aggregation rules to reconcile conflicts among multiple classification outcomes.

title: Unmanned Aerial Vehicle Path Planning Based on the MCSD-GWO Algorithm
authors: Sifan Sun
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: In response to the unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) path planning problem in mountainous battlefield environments, this paper proposes a novel algorithm called Multi-objective Clustering and Step-Driven Grey Wolf Optimizer (MCSD-GWO). By incorporating the idea of dynamic multi-objective clustering initialization, the algorithm enhances population diversity. Additionally, a step-driven dynamic weight factor is introduced to balance the algorithm’s global exploration and local exploitation capabilities, thereby avoiding premature convergence into local optima and improving convergence speed. The simulation results validate that the proposed algorithm is characterized by superior optimization performance, rapid convergence, and high stability.
