title: Machine Learning-Based Enhanced Telematics Data Simulation for Predictive Maintenance in E-Mobility Fleet Operations
authors: Akash Ambulkar, Sammit J. Deshpande
abstract: Predictive maintenance is a cornerstone for reliable e-mobility fleet management, yet acquiring sufficient failure-mode data in real fleets is both expensive and time-consuming. This paper introduces a machine learning-enhanced simulation pipeline to generate synthetic telematics datasets that mimic real-world electric vehicle operational patterns and component degradation. A generative adversarial network (GAN) is trained on limited real-world battery, motor, and sensor data from telematics units to produce diverse, physically plausible trajectories of state-of-health indicators. The simulated data populates a digital twin of an EV fleet, upon which gradient boosting and recurrent neural network models are evaluated for anomaly detection and remaining useful life (RUL) prediction. In comparison to purely heuristic simulations, our approach improves predictive maintenance scheduling accuracy by 28 % and reduces false-positive warnings by 40 %. Results indicate that ML-driven synthetic data generation can accelerate the development of reliable maintenance algorithms, ultimately enhancing fleet uptime and reducing operational costs.

title: Computer Vision Technologies for Image Object Classification
authors: Karimov Sardor Ilkhom Ugli, Abduqodirova Mohizoda Ilxomidin Qizi, Abdukadirova Gulbakhor Xomidjon Qizi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Science and Communications Technologies (ICISCT)
abstract: this paper presents modern approaches to object analysis and image classification using deep learning architectures VGG19, U-Net, and MobileNet. The features of each model are examined: high accuracy of feature extraction in VGG19, efficiency of image segmentation in U-Net, as well as compactness and computational resource optimization in MobileNet. A comparative analysis of model performance on open datasets was conducted, including Accuracy, Precision, Recall, and F1-score metrics. The results showed that the combined application of these architectures allows achieving a balance between classification accuracy, processing speed, and computational efficiency. The study demonstrates the prospects of using hybrid solutions for tasks in medicine, agriculture, video surveillance systems, and intelligent environmental monitoring.

title: An Adaptive Dual-domain Feature Alignment Network for All-Day City Image Analysis
authors: Ying Qian, Huayi Li, Pan Wang, Zihao Fu
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: The vision of smart cities relies on robust, all-day automated image analysis systems to enhance urban management and public security. However, severe illumination variations between day and night present a significant challenge for tasks such as vehicle re-identification, where the same vehicle can appear drastically different due to lighting changes and nighttime glare. This impedes the reliability of continuous urban image analytics. To address this issue, we propose an Adaptive Dual-domain Feature Alignment Network (ADFA-Net). Our approach aligns vehicle features across time domains by learning illumination-invariant representations. ADFA-Net employs a dual-stream architecture with dedicated branches for processing day and night images. Each branch incorporates a glare suppression module to enhance feature quality. Crucially, features from both streams are adaptively fused at multiple semantic levels to construct a coherent and aligned feature space. Extensive evaluations on the cross-day-night benchmarks DN-348 and DN-Wild demonstrate the effectiveness of our model. On DN-348, ADFA-Net achieves 48.7% mAP and 72.2% Rank-1 accuracy. On the more challenging DN-Wild dataset, it attains 41.4% mAP and 51.0% Rank-1 accuracy.

title: Task-Centric Model Transfer for Federated Learning Over the Edge–Cloud Continuum
authors: Swapnil Sadashiv Shinde, Carlo Fischione
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: Edge-based, task-centric Federated Learning (FL) enabled by Mobile Edge Computing (MEC) and the Internet of Things (IoT) is essential to meeting the intelligent service demands of 6 G networks. However, training task-specific FL models on resource-constrained devices remains a major challenge. This paper proposes a task-centric FL framework that leverages model transfer across distributed edge-cloud servers to enhance training efficiency and reduce overall latency. We model the end-to-end latency of FL, including model discovery, data routing, and training, and formulate a constrained optimization problem to minimize total latency through selective transfer of pre-trained FL models. Furthermore, we develop a maximum search budget-based solution that balances model search and transfer overhead with training performance. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed approach significantly reduces FL latency compared to traditional solutions, providing a practical foundation for efficient FL over the edge-cloud continuum.

title: MS-DeepLab: A Lightweight Semantic Segmentation Model for Autonomous Driving
authors: Yong Ai, Shengfu Mei, Shasha Tian, Jin Xie
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Semantic segmentation is a core technology for pixel-level object recognition in autonomous driving environmental perception, and its performance directly impacts the decision reliability and operational safety of autonomous driving systems. Most mainstream high-precision semantic segmentation models face issues of excessive parameters and low inference speeds, which prevent them from meeting the dual requirements of "lightweight deployment" and "high-precision segmentation" in autonomous driving scenarios. To tackle this challenge, this paper proposes a lightweight improved model based on DeepLabv3+, named MS-DeepLab. Specifically, we adopt MobileNetv2 to replace the original feature extraction backbone of DeepLabv3+ for lightweight compression. We also design a multidimensional shallow feature extraction mechanism and introduce a Multi-scale Convolutional Attention Module (MSCAM) to enhance feature representation capability. Finally, we construct a hybrid loss function by combining CE_Loss and Focal_Loss to mitigate the imbalance between positive and negative samples, thereby further improving segmentation accuracy. Experimental results on the Cityscapes dataset demonstrate that MS-DeepLab has only 3.14MB parameters while achieving a segmentation accuracy of 72.98% mIoU and an inference speed of 58.09 FPS. Compared with the original DeepLabv3+, the proposed model maintains a relatively high level of accuracy, while delivering an 86.90% parameter compression and an impressive 146.14% increase in inference speed. These improvements render it more compatible with the real-time deployment requirements of autonomous driving scenarios.

title: DORA: Dynamic O-RAN Resource Allocation for Multi-Slice 5G Networks
authors: Alireza Ebrahimi Dorcheh, Tolunay Seyfi, Fatemeh Afghah
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: The fifth generation (5G) of wireless networks must simultaneously support heterogeneous service categories, including Ultra-Reliable Low-Latency Communications (URLLC), enhanced Mobile Broadband (eMBB), and massive Machine-Type Communications (mMTC), each with distinct Quality of Service (QoS) requirements. Meeting these demands under limited spectrum resources requires adaptive and standards-compliant radio resource management. We present DORA (Dynamic O-RAN Resource Allocation), a deep reinforcement learning (DRL) framework for dynamic slice-level Physical Resource Block (PRB) allocation in Open RAN. DORA employs a PPO-based RL agent to allocate PRBs across URLLC, eMBB, and mMTC slices based on observed traffic demands and channel conditions. Intra-slice PRB scheduling is handled deterministically via round-robin among active UEs, simplifying control complexity and improving training stability. Unlike prior work, DORA supports online training and adapts continuously to evolving traffic patterns and cross-slice contention. Implemented in the standards-compliant OpenAirInterface (OAI) RAN stack and designed for deployment as an O-RAN xApp, DORA integrates seamlessly with RAN Intelligent Controllers (RICs). Extensive evaluation under congested regimes shows that DORA outperforms three non-learning baselines and a DQN agent, achieving lower URLLC latency, higher eMBB throughput with fewer SLA violations, and broader mMTC coverage without starving high-priority slices. To our knowledge, this is the first fully online DRL framework for adaptive, slice-aware PRB allocation in O-RAN.

title: A Mobile Dust-Proof Integrated Solution for GIS Equipment Maintenance in Various Scenarios
authors: Fugao Gong, Kai Zhao, Li Wang, Zuopeng Zhang, Lijiang Chen, Xuefeng Liu, Linglong Tan, Wei Li, Heng Guo, Yang Bai
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: With the continuous expansion of power grid scale, gas-insulated switchgear (GIS) has become increasingly prevalent in power systems, where its operational reliability directly impacts grid safety. This paper proposes a comprehensive solution to address challenges in outdoor GIS maintenance, including complex and variable environmental conditions and limited effectiveness of traditional dust-proof measures. Through systematic analysis of existing dust control shortcomings, we designed a modular dust-proof system based on the principles of “zoned dust prevention, modular adaptability, and intelligent control.” The solution features a zoned dust-proof framework, adjustable modular dust-proof sheds, accordion-style dust covers, and an integrated intelligent environmental control system. Practical applications demonstrate that this approach can reduce dust concentrations in critical areas below standard thresholds, significantly improve maintenance environment cleanliness, effectively lower equipment failure rates, while substantially enhancing maintenance efficiency and reducing dust-proofing costs. The solution holds significant potential for widespread application and promotion.

title: A Network Vulnerability Classification Method Based on CVSS
authors: Yizhou Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: In the field of information security, vulnerability assessment, classification, and management constitute core tasks for cybersecurity. Traditional vulnerability scoring and classification methods rely on manual analysis and expert review. This paper proposes an automated vulnerability assessment methodology based on large natural language processing models, which leverages deep learning techniques to achieve intelligent evaluation of the Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS). Compared to traditional expert–driven evaluation approaches, this method efficiently parses massive security text data through natural language processing. Specifically, the research innovatively integrates stemming and lemmatization techniques to significantly enhance the model's semantic comprehension of vulnerability description features. Experimental results demonstrate the model's superior assessment performance in cross–dataset tests, validating the effectiveness of deep learning-based automated vulnerability assessment approaches. Experimental results indicate that this model achieves a maximum F1 Score of 96.2% on vulnerability datasets, best reducing error by 12.94% compared to the traditional classification model.

title: Research on RAG Enhanced End-to-end Intelligent Reasoning Algorithm for Real-time Decision-making
authors: Zhenming Deng, Wen Xiong, Haichuan Zhang, Fei Chen, Wenrui Chen, Xing Wang, Lu Yan, Lei Lu
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: In this paper, an enhanced end-to-end intelligent reasoning algorithm based on RAG (retrieval-augmented generation) for real-time decision-making is proposed to meet the high requirements of AI system in real-time decision-making scenarios. Through the framework of dynamic knowledge fusion, the algorithm realizes the deep fusion of retrieval and generation by using multi-modal attention gating mechanism, dynamically adjusts the intensity of external knowledge injection, ensures the priority adoption of new knowledge, and automatically attenuates old knowledge. At the same time, the iterative reasoning mechanism based on finite state automata is designed, and the state transition is handled by LSTM model to realize multi-hop logical reasoning, and the reward shaping mechanism is introduced to optimize the reasoning process. In addition, the dual-objective loss function is used to realize the deep collaboration of retrieval, reasoning and generation modules, and knowledge distillation technology is introduced to improve the deployment feasibility and response efficiency of the system in real-time decision-making scenarios. The experimental results show that the algorithm is significantly superior to the traditional RAG method in several key indicators, with the response time reduced to 1200±50ms, the HotpotQA F1 value increased to 73.9%, the accuracy of financial risk control reached 93.6%, and the accuracy of emergency dispatch reached 89.8%, which greatly improved the accuracy of multi-hop reasoning and complex decision-making while ensuring real-time. The case study further verifies its advantages in financial risk control and emergency dispatch scenarios, and shows stronger dynamic decision-making ability.

title: Research on Hidden Danger Prediction of Power Transformer Based on TabTransformer
authors: Xi Song
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: The safe operation of power transformer is very important to the stability of power system. DGA (Dissolved Gas Analysis) is an important means to detect its hidden dangers, but the traditional methods have some limitations, such as single feature extraction, rigid threshold and low diagnostic accuracy. Therefore, this paper proposes a transformer hidden danger prediction model (TabTransformer-DGA) based on TAB Transformer. The model combines eight kinds of gas concentrations, three operating parameters and two auxiliary features to construct a multi-dimensional tabular data set, which breaks through the limitation of traditional input. The complex nonlinear relationship between features is dynamically captured by TabTransformer's self-attention mechanism, and the expression ability of diverse feature interaction patterns is enhanced by multi-head attention mechanism. Design a visualization method of multi-head attention weight, calculate the comprehensive importance score of each feature and visualize it as a heat map to improve the interpretability of the model. The experiment is based on 35,240 DGA monitoring records of a provincial company of State Grid. The results show that TabTransformer-DGA is superior to IEC 60599 three-ratio method and XGBoost method in accuracy (93.6%), macro F1 value (0.901) and composite fault recall rate (91.8%), especially in composite fault diagnosis. The model can realize high-precision and interpretable transformer hidden danger prediction, and provide support for improving the intelligent level of transformer operation and maintenance.

title: Domain-Aware Federated Unlearning With Adaptive Multi-Resolution Models for Resource-Constrained IoT Networks
authors: Hai Anh Tran, Abdelhamid Mellouk
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: The proliferation of Internet of Things (IoT) devices in distributed environments introduces new challenges for intelligent, privacy-preserving, and scalable learning systems. In such resource-constrained and heterogeneous networks, ensuring efficient collaboration across devices while respecting user privacy-especially when data must be revoked-remains an open problem. We propose FedAMP-FU, a hierarchical federated learning framework that integrates two key innovations: (i) Adaptive Multi-Resolution Models (AMRM) for efficient model scaling across devices with diverse capabilities, and (ii) Partial Forgetting via Attention Masking (PFAM), a lightweight unlearning mechanism that removes user contributions without retraining. Domain-level aggregators manage resource-aware updates and execute unlearning when needed. Experiments on real-world IoT datasets show that FedAMP-FU reduces communication overhead by up to 45 % and achieves unlearning in just 15 seconds while maintaining over 88 % accuracy and low inference latency. These results demonstrate its promise for efficient, privacy-aware learning in next-generation IoT systems.

title: From Data Freshness to Model Updates: An AoI-Driven Client Scheduling Approach
authors: Qiran Chen, Wen Chen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: In real-time federated learning (FL), local training does not necessarily start after all client information has been fully collected. In Internet of Things (IoT) networks, the rates at which devices generate or upload data are inherently heterogeneous, leading to asynchronous and uneven information arrivals. We propose a client scheduling framework aware of both age of information (AoI) and data usage that jointly captures the staleness of client models and their buffered data utilization. Building on this metric, we design two scheduling strategies—one emphasizing efficiency and the other emphasizing long-term balance—to guide client selection under communication resource constraints. Extensive simulations demonstrate that the proposed approach significantly improves the designed score compared to baseline scheduling strategies, making it suitable for real-time and resource-constrained IoT FL scenarios.

title: A Declaration of Relevance: Redefining the IT Role for the Age of AI
authors: Miranda Carney-Morris, Elizabeth Young
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 ACM SIGUCCS Annual Conference
abstract: None

title: IEEE Industry Applications Society Information
authors: N/A

title: Accelerating Wireless Distributed Learning via Hybrid Split and Federated Learning Optimization_supp1-3673745.pdf
authors: Wei Feng
abstract: Federated learning (FL) and split learning (SL) are two effective distributed learning paradigms in wireless networks, enabling collaborative model training across mobile devices without sharing raw data. While FL supports low-latency parallel training, it may converge to less accurate model. In contrast, SL achieves higher accuracy through sequential training but suffers from increased delay. To leverage the advantages of both, hybrid split and federated learning (HSFL) allows some devices to operate in FL mode and others in SL mode. This paper aims to accelerate HSFL by addressing three key questions: 1) How does learning mode selection affect overall learning performance? 2) How does it interact with batch size? 3) How can these hyperparameters be jointly optimized alongside communication and computational resources to reduce overall learning delay? We first analyze convergence, revealing the interplay between learning mode and batch size. Next, we formulate a delay minimization problem and propose a two-stage solution: a block coordinate descent method for a relaxed problem to obtain a locally optimal solution, followed by a rounding algorithm to recover integer batch sizes with near-optimal performance. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach significantly accelerates convergence to the target accuracy compared to existing methods.

title: Active Manipulation of Trapped Flux in Nb Chip at a Single Vortex Level (2025)
authors: Brenna Petrelli, Joshua Bedard, Jacob Franklin, Kyle Jackman, Lieze Schindler, Johannes Delport, Tessa Hall, Paul Rossouw, Coenrad Fourie, Daniel Yohannes, Oleg Mukhanov, Ilya Sochnikov
abstract: Trapped magnetic flux has been a long-standing problem in superconducting digital and quantum circuits, significantly affecting their reliability and limiting practical applications. Mitigation techniques like integrating moat structures into circuit layouts and increasing magnetic shielding have been in use to improve the probability of an accurate circuit operation. However, none of the methods can reliably and deterministically achieve the desired flux-free state of superconducting circuits. Active flux management can potentially solve this problem by the removal of the trapped flux from the relevant circuit areas on a chip. Here, we report numerical modelling and experimental scanning SQUID imaging results showing flux manipulation on the single vortex level using a network of pulsed electric lines in a test superconducting single layer circuit. We demonstrate the effect of vortex sweeping in areas with control lines as well as sweeping of vortices into moats via Lorentz force mechanism. This method can potentially be expanded to larger and more sophisticated digital and quantum chips.

title: Stealthy Shields: Adversarial DRL Intelligence for Securing EV Charging Communication Systems
authors: Mohammed Al-Mehdhar, Abdullatif Albaseer, Mohamed Abdallah, Saif Al-Kuwari, Ala Al-Fuqaha
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: The widespread adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) has led to a significant increase in the deployment of charging infrastructures, making them critical components of modern power grids. However, integrating advanced communication technologies into these infrastructures has also enabled sophisticated cyber threats. Although existing research has proposed various solutions, many approaches do not sufficiently address the challenges posed by advanced attack methodologies powered by deep reinforcement learning (DRL), which can adapt their strategies over time, coordinate actions across multiple EVs, and remain stealthy under protocol-compliant constraints. In this paper, we present a novel Multi-Layer Adversarial DRL (MLADRL) framework designed to tackle these challenges. Our approach first utilizes DRL to develop more destructive and coordinated attack vectors, where multiple EVs launch attacks, going beyond the single-EV attacks typically seen in the literature. We combine these DRL-generated attack samples with benign samples and handcrafted attacks to build robust datasets. Based on these datasets, we then design a DRL-based Intrusion Detection System (IDS) specifically designed to detect these advanced cyber threats. We evaluate the effectiveness of our framework across multiple attack scenarios, including varying proportions of malicious EVs and different levels of attack complexity. The results demonstrate that our MLADRL framework significantly outperforms traditional detection methods, maintaining high detection accuracy even against more destructive and complex attack strategies. The proposed DRL-based IDS, trained on the generated adversarial datasets, achieves remarkable performance. Compared to baseline methods, our DRL-based IDS attains 99.4% and 99.9% accuracy, 99.4% and 99.9% precision, and 99.6% and 99.9% recall (F1-scores: 99.6% and 99.9%) for LSTM-based and Transformer-based models, respectively.

title: Adaptive Bat-Inspired Optimization for Infrastructure-Agnostic Task Allocation in Vehicular Fogs
authors: Matheus S. Quessada, Douglas D. Lieira, Penghan Yan, Robson E. De Grande, Rodolfo I. Meneguette
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Advancements in intelligent transport systems and cloud computing services have enabled numerous opportunities for sharing computing resources and data to enhance services. Optimizing task allocation in fogs is a major challenge in efficiently utilizing the limited nearby resources. The high-mobility dynamics of urban environments heavily impact the availability of vehicles, resources, and tasks. Several works have explored the allocation problem. However, they focused on specific mobility patterns, failing to consider both the V2I and V2V scenarios, and thus did not observe a more significant aggregation of resources among vehicles. Thus, this work proposes the mechanism SIGHT, which follows the Bat meta-heuristic technique to make task allocation decisions in fogs. Our approach is designed to accommodate V2V and V2I resource allocation, enabling the adaptation of allocation decisions to highly dynamic urban computing scenarios. Extensive simulated analyses have demonstrated the efficiency of SIGHT when compared with optimization techniques, where our approach showed better performance on both infrastructure-less and infrastructure-based comparisons using DBScan and K-means clustering approaches. Results showed that SIGHT allocated up to 9% more tasks, 7 times more resources in a specific scenario (V2I, K-means), and has approximately 8% less failed allocations than other literature algorithms.

title: NeRF representation-based efficient UAV path planning framework for high-fidelity 3D ship reconstruction
authors: Haoyu Li, Tao Liu, Jiansen Zhao, Li Zhou, Zhengling Lei, Zhao Zhang, Bing Han, Duo Li
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: As an efficient and inexpensive acquisition platform, Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) are particularly suitable for image acquisition in 3D ship reconstruction tasks. However, path planning in ship reconstruction tasks is often required to consider the energy consumption of UAV batteries and the applicability of the reconstruction method, which introduces challenges to the efficient reconstruction of high-fidelity 3D ship models. To address the above challenges, this paper proposes a Neural Radiance Field (NeRF) representation-based efficient UAV path planning framework for high-fidelity 3D ship reconstruction. First, a multi-modal adaptive generation strategy for rays in NeRF is proposed by fusing the features of the point cloud and image. Second, a novel reward function and a viewpoint partitioning optimization guidance strategy for the deep reinforcement learning of the UAV path planning framework are proposed to ensure the efficiency and robustness of ship reconstruction tasks. To further optimize the 3D reconstruction result of the ship, a local adaptive distance-aware sampling policy is proposed to boost the overlap rate of the image data, which enhances the framework’s applicability for downstream reconstruction tasks. Finally, we demonstrate the efficiency and applicability of the UAV path planning framework through a series of experiments.

title: A Novel Adaptive Electrical Equivalent Circuit Model for Accurate Representation of Electrolyzer Dynamics
authors: Mohamed Khalid Ratib, Kashem M. Muttaqi, Md. Rabiul Islam, Danny Sutanto, Ashish P. Agalgaonkar
abstract: The growing integration of renewable energy sources (RESs) for green hydrogen production via water electrolysis presents substantial challenges arising from the intrinsic intermittency of RESs. Consequently, the development of accurate and responsive electrolyzer models is essential to adequately represent their dynamic behavior. Conventional modeling approaches are generally classified as physical, electrical, or empirical. Although physical models exhibit high accuracy, they are often computationally intensive, whereas electrical equivalent circuit (EEC) models, while computationally efficient, often fail to capture operation dynamics due to their fixed parameters. This paper addresses these limitations by conducting a comparative analysis between a physical model and a conventional EEC model for proton exchange membrane (PEM) electrolyzer, elucidating their respective merits and constraints. Building upon this analysis, a novel adaptive EEC model is proposed, integrating the computational efficiency of EEC models with the dynamic accuracy characteristic of physical models. The model introduces an innovative method for determining time-variant membrane ohmic resistance, which accounts for non-uniform temperature and pressure distributions as well as degradation phenomena. Simulation outcomes demonstrate that the adaptive EEC model accurately represents complex dynamic effects, providing a robust and practical framework for enhanced electrolyzer dynamic simulation under varying operational conditions, supporting more reliable integration of electrolyzers in renewable-based energy systems.

title: Shape oscillation and 3-D microstreaming profile of a phospholipid-coated microbubble attached to a wall_supp3-3676695.avi
authors: Klazina Kooiman
abstract: Ultrasound-activated microbubbles display diverse dynamics, including shape oscillations and microstreaming, both of which are important for applications in particle transport, drug delivery, and bacterial interventions. Despite advancements, a knowledge gap persists in our understanding of the correlation between shape oscillations, corresponding microstreaming and resulting mechanical impact to neighboring boundaries for microbubbles of clinically relevant sizes. This study investigates this correlation for biotinylated phospholipid-coated microbubbles with radii of 3.0-7.3 μm (N=79) when attached to a streptavidin-coated wall, imaged at 5 million and 10 thousand frames per second to visualize both shape oscillations and microstreaming. Driven at 1.25 MHz frequency, 25,000 cycles and varying peak negative acoustic pressures (170-420 kPa), the larger sized microbubbles showed higher shape modes (up to mode 6) than the smaller ones. A shape stability model predicted the dominant shape mode with an 89% agreement with experimental findings. Four distinct 3-D microstreaming patterns were observed by employing astigmatic particle tracking velocimetry: patternless, radial, dipole and quadrupole. Modal decomposition of shape oscillations revealed that the quadrupole pattern occurred from self-interaction of the dominant shape mode, while the dipole pattern resulted from two strongly interacting shape modes. The quantified microstreaming showed maximum velocities ranging from 2-10 mm/s, which decayed with increasing distance from the microbubble center. Notably, lower shape modes and the quadrupole pattern were associated with higher wall shear stress (up to 13 Pa). These unique 3-D microstreaming profiles have the potential to enhance the efficiency of therapeutic microbubble-mediated ultrasound applications through controlled shape oscillation and mechanical impact.
