title: Pilot Contamination Analysis in a Multi-Operator Cellular Network with Dedicated Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces
authors: Edi Siswanto, Gunawan Wibisono
publish: 2026 Symposium on Microwave, Antenna, and Propagation (SMAP)
abstract: The Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (RIS) can be considered a key part of the backbone of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 G}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 G}$</tex> communication mainly due to its electromagnetic properties for controlling the propagation of the signals in the wireless channel. While substantial research exists on single-operator RIS optimization, the multi-operator scenario introduces unique opportunities and complexities that require systematic investigation. The implementation of multi-operator RIS systems presents significant technical obstacles, such as inter-operator interference and the complexity of coordinating resources. A critical challenge arises during channel estimation, where the concurrent reflection of pilots by uncoordinated RIS introduces a new form of pilot contamination effect. This crossoperator RIS interference creates a performance ceiling that cannot be overcome by increasing transmit power or adding the number of training pilot signal. To mitigate this, implementing orthogonal RIS configurations between operators during pilot transmission is conveyed. Consequently, coordination between multiple operators is essential.

title: Dual-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Green Permutation Flow Shop Scheduling with Variable Machine Speeds
authors: Yongxin Lu, Yiping Yuan, Zirui Li, Yujia Song, Adilanmu Sitahong, Peiyin Mo
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: Aiming at the strong coupling between job sequencing and processing speed decision-making in the green permutation flow shop scheduling problem with processing speed constraints, and the resulting difficulty in achieving collaborative optimization, this paper proposes a dual-agent collaborative scheduling method based on reinforcement learning. The method constructs a sorting agent and a speed agent, which employ a pointer network and a graph attention network based on a probabilistic directed acyclic graph (P-DAG) to model job sequencing and processing speed decisions, respectively, and are jointly trained under the proximal policy optimization framework with shared rewards. To evaluate the effectiveness of the proposed approach, comparative experiments are conducted against MOEA/D, NSGA-II, SPEA2, and DQN. The experimental results show that, across instances of different scales, the proposed method not only achieves better and more stable optimization performance in terms of makespan and energy consumption, but also requires significantly less average solution time. This demonstrates its effectiveness, computational efficiency, and strong generalization capability in complex scheduling scenarios. (The abstract has been rewritten)

title: Self-Supervised and Diffusion-based Architectures for Document Image Restoration and Recognition
authors: K. Muthulakshmi, U.V. Anbazhagu, R. Venkatalakshmi, R. Santhana Krishnan, J. Relin Francis Raj, S. Kavitha
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The automated analysis of historical documents represents a significant engineering challenge due to severe degradation, complex layouts, and handwritten content variability. These documents form a critical component of Cultural heritage preservation and large-scale Infrastructure for digital information systems. Conventional image processing and recognition pipelines lack robustness, scalability, and reliability when applied to degraded archival data. This paper presents an integrated deep learning framework that combines self-supervised representation learning, diffusion-based image restoration, transformer-driven document understanding, and uncertainty modeling for historical document analysis. A Vision Transformer pretrained using a Masked Autoencoder enables robust feature extraction from unlabeled document images, reducing dependency on annotated datasets. Latent diffusion models with a Swin Transformer U-Net backbone perform high-fidelity document restoration, improving visual quality while maintaining structural consistency. Layout analysis is conducted using the DETR architecture, enabling accurate detection of document regions such as text blocks and paragraphs. Handwritten text recognition is achieved through a CNN–Transformer–CTC architecture, facilitating reliable sequence transcription. Explainability is incorporated using attention rollout and Grad-CAM, while diffusion-based uncertainty estimation provides confidence measures essential for trustworthy deployment. Experimental evaluation demonstrates significant performance improvements, achieving high restoration, recognition, and layout accuracy.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning-Based MPC for Lateral Maneuver of Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Souhail Bentayek, Yassine Kali, Jean-Pierre Kenné, Khalid Benjelloun, Ahmed Chebak
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: This paper presents an intelligent control framework for autonomous vehicles that integrates Model Predictive Control (MPC) with Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) to improve trajectory tracking performance under variable driving conditions. The proposed approach employs a Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (DDPG) agent to dynamically adjust the weighting parameters of the MPC cost function in real time, enabling adaptive balance between tracking precision and steering smoothness. A NARX (Nonlinear AutoRegressive with eXogenous inputs) neural network model is used to capture the nonlinear and time-varying dynamics of the vehicle, providing accurate multi-step state predictions for the MPC. Simulation results demonstrate that the DRL-enhanced MPC significantly improves lateral tracking accuracy—up to 68% reduction in error—and enhances control stability compared with conventional fixed-weight MPC. The controller exhibits robustness to abrupt changes in road friction and external disturbances. Despite its simulation-based validation, the proposed framework highlights the feasibility of real-time adaptive control and provides a promising direction toward experimental implementation on embedded automotive platforms.

title: Research on Student Value-Learning Behavior Anomaly Detection Based on LSTM Time Series Models
authors: Yuan Wang, Jinhua Peng
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: To reveal the latent modulating effect of student value orientations on learning behavior evolution, this study constructs a collaborative anomaly detection framework integrating value scales with temporal behavior logs, providing a structured characterization of subtle deviations in students' learning processes. Methodologically, we integrate static value vectors into behavioral sequence modeling through embedding mechanisms. Bidirectional LSTMs extract temporal dependencies in learning behaviors, while value-gated mechanisms modulate latent state representations across time dimensions. Anomaly scores combine reconstruction errors with individual historical distributions, enabling personalized threshold determination. Experiments using three semesters of real-world data validate the model's performance. Results demonstrate that the value-modulated temporal model outperforms baseline models across Macro-F1, AUC, and Precision@5 metrics, exhibiting higher sensitivity particularly in identifying "covert anomalies"— scenarios with minimal behavioral fluctuation but significant value deviation. Comprehensive results demonstrate that value embedding enhances the robustness of learning behavior anomaly detection, offering a novel technical approach for analyzing value-behavior linkage mechanisms in educational settings.

title: Electromagnetic Analysis of a New Delta-Type Consequent-Pole IPM Motor for PM Cost Reduction
authors: Amir Ebrahimi Shohani, Mohammadreza Naeimi, Fariba Farrokh, Mahammad Farahzadi, Karim Abbaszadeh
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: This paper presents the electromagnetic design and performance analysis of a novel delta-type consequent-pole interior permanent magnet (IPM) motor. The goal of this design is to significantly reduce the amount of permanent magnet (PM) material used, thereby lowering manufacturing costs. The proposed configuration features a consequent-pole arrangement within a delta-shaped rotor structure. This design leads to a notable decrease in the volume of PMs while still maintaining strong electromagnetic performance. Finite element analysis (FEA) results show that this new topology effectively improves torque smoothness and reduces cogging torque without a significant decrease in back electromotive force (back-EMF) voltage or rated torque. Therefore, the proposed design strikes an effective balance between performance and material costs, making it an attractive option for applications that prioritize both cost efficiency and high performance.

title: Research on Automatic Deployment and Intelligent Testing Framework of Autonomous Controllable Operating System for Power Energy System
authors: Xiang Lu, Yang Su
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: In this paper, an automatic deployment and intelligent testing framework is proposed to meet the demand of power energy system for autonomous controllable operating system. As a national key infrastructure, the power industry’s core control system has long relied on foreign operating systems, facing the security risks of supply chain. The traditional manual deployment method is inefficient and easy to introduce human error, and the existing testing methods are insufficient in detecting deep vulnerabilities. Therefore, this paper designs an automatic deployment framework of power autonomous controllable operating system, adopts a three-tier modular architecture, and realizes the dynamic loading of heterogeneous device drivers and the automatic generation of cross-platform deployment templates through eBPF technology, which significantly improves the deployment efficiency. At the same time, an intelligent test framework is built, a digital twin test environment is created, and extreme scenarios are simulated by combining chaotic engineering, so that the test coverage rate is improved to 92%. The reinforcement learning method based on PPO (Proximal Policy Optimization) algorithm is introduced to optimize the priority ranking of test cases and shorten the time for finding critical defects. The experimental results show that the framework is significantly better than the traditional scheme in deployment efficiency, system stability and test coverage, which provides a strong guarantee for the safe and stable operation of power system.

title: Noise-Robust Pepper-Pot Beam Image Processing
authors: Wenhao Ma, Guoheng Zhang, Xing Fang, Xiujia An, Lei Song, Limao Cairang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Pepper-pot emittance measurement systems are widely employed in modern accelerators for transverse beam characterization; in such systems, beam diagnostics are performed by analyzing the beam distribution recorded on a scintillation screen by a Charge-Coupled Device (CCD) camera, but raw images are strongly degraded by dark current, fixedpattern noise, hot pixels and spot ghosting. This paper presents a dedicated preprocessing and mask-generation pipeline that transforms raw beam images into a clean, connected beam-spot mask. The method combines background subtraction, Gaussian smoothing, noise-adaptive thresholding based on the background standard deviation, morphological cleaning with area filtering, and morphology-based connection of neighbouring spot fragments. Tests on experimental data show that the scheme suppresses background fluctuations, removes small spurious regions and reconstructs continuous beam footprints while preserving the main beam features. Quantitative analysis of horizontal gray-level profiles indicates a high-frequency noise reduction of 90.3%, demonstrating that the method provides reliable support for subsequent transverse beam emittance measurements.

title: An Active Current Balancing Method for Multiphase LLC Resonant Converter
authors: Seyedamirhossein Talebi, Kioumars Shahriari, Salar Sadeghian, Reza Takarl, Seyed Fariborz Zarei
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: Current sharing in power converter systems has become an effective solution for achieving high output current in modern power electronics. However, the output capability of the switching converter is limited by the voltage and current rating of semiconductor devices. For instance, MOSFETs are typically rated around 1 kV and 100 A, while insulated-gate bipolar transistors can handle up to 6.5 kV. Several approaches have been proposed to overcome these limitations. However, due to mismatches in semiconductor parameters and parasitic elements, achieving equal power distribution between modules remains difficult. This paper introduces a new technique for output current equalization in modular LLC converters. The proposed method balances the output current of N LLC modules by adjusting the transformer inductance without adding any extra components. The effectiveness of the proposed approach was verified through MATLAB simulations for N=3 LLC converter modules, delivering a total output current of 130 A.

title: A Systematic Review and Comparative Analysis of Machine Learning Models for Early Prediction of Noncommunicable Diseases
authors: Arjun M S, Pooja M R
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Recognizing non-communicable disease (NCD) as early as possible (i.e., stroke, diabetes, and heart disease) helps to improve survival through timely access to healthcare. The introduction of sophisticated machine learning (ML) techniques has fundamentally changed the ability to conduct NCD risk assessments by evaluating large quantities of clinical and physiological data. This review compares existing predictive models for NCD risk. The results show that ensemble architectures tend to provide consistent high accuracy; whereas deep learning architectures provide good performance for analysing electrocardiograms (ECGs). Beyond performance characteristics, interpretability will be important for clinical implementation of these methodologies and there are still many research areas where interpretability needs to be developed, such as using multi-modal data and learning by considering privacy.

title: Distribution Network Operation Optimization Scheduling Model Integrating Big Data and Beidou Navigation
authors: Yufeng Chai, Min Wang, Zhongying Zhao, Bin Li, Hui He
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: A distribution network operation optimization scheduling model integrating big data and Beidou navigation is proposed. The model obtains the distribution network operation data in real time through the data acquisition layer, and uses the data processing layer for cleaning, integration and analysis to establish a data warehouse to support the data requirements of the decision optimization layer. The decision-making optimization layer combines the results of big data analysis with the high-precision positioning and timing functions of Beidou navigation system, constructs an optimization model and uses particle swarm optimization (PSO) to solve it, and generates an optimal scheduling strategy, which is finally implemented by the executive layer and fed back the results for continuous improvement. The simulation results show that the model can significantly reduce the operating cost (by 15.20%), improve the equipment utilization rate (by 14.00%), and maintain a high load satisfaction rate (98%-100%). In addition, PSO algorithm shows good convergence and stability in the solution process. The research results show that the optimal scheduling model integrating big data and Beidou navigation has important theoretical significance and practical application value in improving the operation efficiency, security and reliability of distribution network.

title: An IHHO-XGBoost Model for Peak Power Load Forecasting
authors: Siqi Wu, Ziyi Cai
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: This paper proposes an XGBoost peak load model optimized by an improved Harris Hawk algorithm (IHHO). Firstly, an improved Harris hawk algorithm (IHHO) based on the good point set theory, stochastic contraction index and adaptive inertia weight is proposed, and the performance of IHHO is verified by using the single-peak and multiple-peak test functions in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 3}$</tex> benchmark test functions, and the results show that IHHO is better than the HHO. Secondly, the IHHO algorithm is combined with XGBoost to construct a parallel integration algorithm, which is applied to the construction of the peak power load model. Finally, the peak power load data of 15:00 and 23:00 daily in Delhi in 2023 are selected for the simulation experiments, which prove that the IHHO-XGBoost model proposed in this paper has a more obvious advantage compared to HHO-XGBoost, and performs better than HHOXGBoost in peak power load forecasting.

title: Research on UAV Image Retrieval Technology Based on Fusion of Deep Features and Deep Hash Codes
authors: Chen Zhiwei, Feng Chunna, Zeng Bangze
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of large calculation amount, poor real-time performance and insufficient cross-view robustness in the matching between large-size satellite reference images and real-time aerial images in UAV visual navigation, a UAV image retrieval technology fusing deep features and deep hash codes is proposed. This technology uses the Cross-View Matching Network (CVM-Net) to extract deep features with view invariance, and combines the deep hash network (HashNet) to compress high-dimensional features into compact binary hash codes to achieve efficient retrieval. Experiments on the ArcGISOnline satellite image dataset show that compared with the improved ORB algorithm, the Top-1 accuracy of the proposed technology is increased by 29.2%, and the average retrieval time is reduced by 12.7 ms; compared with the single CVM-Net, under the premise that the Top-1 accuracy only decreases by 3.1%, the retrieval and comparison time is reduced by 36.9%, which can meet the real-time positioning needs of UAVs in GPS-free environments.

title: Research on Degradation Modeling and Remaining Useful Life Prediction Algorithms Based on Electrical Equipment Condition Data
authors: Chong Liu, Guoqing Kan, Ping Tang
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: With the widespread application of electrical equipment in key fields such as energy, industry, and transportation, issues related to operational reliability and safety have attracted increasing attention. Traditional life prediction methods based on experience or physical mechanisms have limited accuracy in actual complex operating conditions and cannot meet the needs of modern intelligent operation and maintenance. This paper focuses on operational status monitoring data of electrical equipment and systematically investigates data-driven degradation modeling methods and remaining useful life (RUL) prediction algorithms. The temporal characteristics of multi-source status data and degradation evolution patterns are analyzed, and a feature extraction and preprocessing workflow is established; a predictive model integrating deep neural networks and Bayesian inference is designed, capable of effectively capturing equipment degradation trends and quantifying prediction uncertainty; Through comparative experiments using actual equipment data and open-source PHM datasets, the results show that the proposed method outperforms traditional models in terms of prediction accuracy and robustness. The research findings can provide technical support for intelligent maintenance and risk warning of electrical equipment.

title: Grey wolf Optimization-Based nonsingular fast fixed time sliding mode Control with Fixed-Time Observer for Quadrotor system subject to external perturbations
authors: Mouna Lhayani, Ahmed Abbou, Yassine El Houm
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: This paper proposes a robust control strategy for quadrotor unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), combining a nonsingular fast fixed-time sliding mode controller (NFFTTSMC) with a fixed disturbance observer (FxtDO). The NFFTTSMC ensures fast and stable convergence trajectory tracking errors while avoiding singularities. The FxtDO accurately estimates and compensates for model uncertainties and external disturbances in real time. The proposed method not only improves tracking accuracy and disturbance rejection, but also significantly reduces chattering effects, providing smoother control signals. To improve the control performance, controller gains are automatically tuned using a Grey Wolf Optimization (GWO) algorithm with an ITAE-based objective function. Simulation studies show that the proposed NFFTTSMC-FxtDO-GWO framework improves tracking accuracy, convergence, and enhances disturbance rejection. The obtained results demonstrate the effectiveness and applicability of the developing approach for quadrotor UAVs operating in uncertain and dynamic environments

title: Efficient Recognition of Construction Machinery Operation Behaviors Based on Knowledge Distillation and Edge Intelligence
authors: Hang Chen, Chunlin Liu, Hao Shen, Guangdai Luo, Shuo Zhang, Xiaoting Chen, Mianjie Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: Real-time perception of construction machinery behaviors is critical for safety and efficiency in Digital Twin systems. However, existing spatio-temporal networks (e.g., 13D) are too heavy for edge devices, while lightweight 2D networks suffer from feature confusion due to lack of temporal context. To address this, we propose a Cloud-Edge Collaborative SpatioTemporal Distillation Framework. We employ an asymmetric transfer strategy: a cloud-based ResNet-50 teacher with Temporal Shift Modules (TSM) captures long-term dependencies, which are transferred to an edge-based MobileNetV3 student via a novel Multi-scale Feature Adaptation mechanism. This aligns intermediate attention patterns, enabling the lightweight model to mimic temporal reasoning. Experiments on our Construction-5K dataset show the method achieves 89.1% accuracy on NVIDIA Jetson Xavier NX (a 2.9% gain over baseline) at 55 FPS using TensorRT FP16, balancing high accuracy with edge real-time constraints.

title: Performance Analysis of Squirrel-cage Induction Motors with Asymmetric Double Skewed Bars
authors: Amir Darjazini, Mansoure Karimi, Mehran Karimi, Mohsen Cheraghi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: High torque ripple usually causes a number of problems in electrical machines, including mechanical vibration, acoustic noise, and subsequently the equipment lifetime reduction. In induction motors, complementary methods such as proper selection of stator/rotor slot combination and slot skewing are used as the two common ways to alleviate the torque ripple issue. However, all portions of this torque ripple could not be eliminated by these conventional methods. In this regard, this paper introduces a modified asymmetric double skewed rotor (ADSR) as a possible alternative to skewed designs to decrease the torque ripple. The study, first, investigates a novel rotor structure and its manufacturing aspects. Later, the torque ripple reduction mechanism of the proposed structure will be presented, and then the optimum parameters will be determined based on the airgap harmonics elimination. Finally, three-dimensional finite element (3D-FE) models will be developed to verify the effectiveness of the optimum calculation.

title: Design and Implementation of an SM3 Algorithm Microarchitecture Based on an Event-Driven Asynchronous Mechanism
authors: Guorui Wang, Fei Fang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: To address the vulnerability of traditional synchronous hash algorithm hardware to side-channel attacks, this paper proposes a high-security asynchronous hardware architecture for the SM3 hash algorithm. The design adopts an event-driven asynchronous circuit mechanism and datafloworiented methodology, decoupling and parallelizing the message expansion and iterative compression modules to effectively reduce critical path delay. By employing multi-stage cFifo, MutexMerge, and WaitMerge control units, a fully asynchronous structure independent of a global clock is achieved. Functional simulation and evaluation on the Vivado platform demonstrate that the proposed architecture maintains complete functional correctness of the SM3 algorithm while significantly enhancing resistance to differential power analysis attacks and improving energy efficiency. This work provides a novel and secure approach for the hardware implementation of cryptographic algorithms under the asynchronous computing paradigm.

title: Intelligent Recognition System for Three-Dimensional Clothing Cutting Using Computer Vision Technology
authors: Xun Lin
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of low efficiency and large error of traditional three-dimensional clothing cutting, this paper proposes an intelligent recognition system for three-dimensional clothing cutting based on computer vision technology, and designs a multi-scale feature fusion and dynamic template matching algorithm. The algorithm realizes multi-scale feature extraction by improving the convolutional neural network, introduces the attention mechanism to enhance the key feature recognition, combines dynamic programming to optimize the template matching path, and realizes adaptive template generation and dynamic matching. A data set containing 2000 sets of images of different styles and materials is constructed for experiments. The experimental environment is Intel Core i7-12700H processor, NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 graphics card, 16GB memory, and Python 3.8 and PyTorch 1.12 framework. The results show that the accuracy of the algorithm is 96.8%, which is 23% higher than the traditional algorithm and 11% higher than the existing advanced algorithm, the recall rate is 95.5%, the F 1 value is 96.1%, and the average running time is 0.8 seconds, which is 1.2 seconds shorter than the traditional algorithm. Experiments have proved that the system and algorithm provide an efficient and accurate intelligent recognition solution for clothing three-dimensional cutting, effectively promoting the intelligent process of clothing production.

title: Feasibility and Impact of Vsat-Based Communication Systems in Indian Commercial Vehicles
authors: Arun Yadav, Prasad Chitare, Vishal Gohil
abstract: The evolution of satellite communication technologies in India has created new opportunities to bridge the connectivity gap in mobile and remote applications. This research explores the feasibility and implications of using Very Small Aperture Terminal (VSAT)-based communication systems in Indian commercial vehicles (CVs). With emerging players like JioSpaceFiber, OneWeb, and Hughes entering the SATCOM sector, and technological advancements in flat-panel antennas and low-earth orbit (LEO) networks, VSAT is gaining traction as a viable connectivity solution. Industries such as transport, logistics, mining, and e-commerce often face challenges when fleet vehicles operate in cellular blackspots. Integrating VSAT technology will enable seamless connectivity, significantly improving accessibility and operational efficiency. The paper assesses technical requirements, market-ready hardware, use-cases, and the operational impact of integrating VSAT systems in EV buses and long-haul trucks, especially in cellular blackspots across India. Cost-benefit analysis and regulatory considerations are also discussed to provide comprehensive insights for stakeholders.

title: Wireless Battery-Free Water Leak Detection with Satellite Connectivity
authors: Shishangbo Yu, Roshan Nepal, Brandon Brown, Roozbeh Abbasi, Norman Zhou, George Shaker
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: The exponential growth of Internet of Things (IoT) deployments faces critical challenges in sustainability and ubiquitous connectivity, particularly for sensors in remote and hard-to-reach locations. This paper presents a breakthrough in autonomous IoT sensing through wireless battery-free water leak detection systems with satellite connectivity, addressing both environmental sustainability and global coverage limitations. We demonstrate two innovative sensor architectures leveraging nanomaterials energy harvesting from water-sensor interactions: a Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) system achieving rapid 35-second activation time with gateway-based satellite connectivity, and a Long-Term Evolution for Machines (LTE-M) system enabling direct satellite communication without intermediate infrastructure. By harnessing nanomaterial-water reactions for power generation and constraining LTE bands to satellite-supported frequencies, our systems achieve truly autonomous operation with global connectivity. The BLE implementation offers costeffective deployment with minimal form factor <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(35 \times 35 \times 30 ~\text{mm}^{3}\right)$</tex>, while the LTE-M variant eliminates gateway requirements at the expense of longer activation time (20 minutes) and higher 5G module cost. These self-powered, maintenance-free sensors represent a paradigm shift towards sustainable and ubiquitous IoT infrastructure, eliminating battery replacement cycles and enabling deployment in previously inaccessible locations.

title: Asymmetric Rotor Hybrid Interior Permanent Magnet Coaxial Magnetic Gear for Torque Density and PM Utilization Ratio Improvement
authors: Mojtaba Malakooti Khaledi, Seyed Ahmadreza Afsari Kashani
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: Power is transferred between various rotational speeds and torque levels via magnetic gears (MGs), which operate similarly to mechanical gears. On the other hand, its contactless functioning has built-in benefits, including decreased mechanical wear, less maintenance, and increased dependability. Notwithstanding these advantages, there remain obstacles to the broad use of MGs, mainly because of the unpredictable pricing and unstable supply chain of rare-earth permanent magnets (PMs). In this paper, a unique less-rare-earth MG design is proposed to reduce dependence on these key components. Along with employing two different kinds of magnetic materials to lower the structure's overall cost, the suggested structure also uses an asymmetrical magnet arrangement in the inner rotor to enhance torque density and decrease cogging torque. This paper systematically investigates the output performance of the optimized design obtained by optimization with a genetic optimization algorithm and ANSYS Maxwell 2021-R1 software through 2D finite element analysis. The key optimization parameters include torque density, cogging torque, and magnetic material volume, which ensure the balance between material cost reduction and operational efficiency.

title: Lightweight Text-to-SQL Enhancement via Schema Ranking and Skeleton-Constrained CoT Distillation
authors: Weihong Gao, Xinying Li, Bin Qin
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Cross-database Text-to-SQL tasks require models to perform both structural planning (Skeleton Parsing) and Schema Linking on unfamiliar database schemas. The high coupling of these two subtasks significantly increases generation complexity and error rates. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a two-stage framework based on Chain-of-Thought (CoT) enhancement and decoupled generation. The first stage utilizes Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) to output a Ranked Schema sequence and a SQL Skeleton, thereby reducing the search space and providing macroscopic structural guidance. The second stage employs explicit Chain-of-Thought reasoning under the dual constraints of the skeleton and schema to complete the step-by-step filling and generation from logical planning to the final SQL. To overcome the insufficient reasoning capability of small-parameter models, we design an automated data synthesis pipeline based on large-parameter models to construct a high-quality CoT distillation dataset containing steps such as "Skeleton Fit Check" and "Constraint Inventory," and perform instruction fine-tuning on the Qwen-8B model. Experiments on the Spider dataset demonstrate that this method effectively combines the advantages of schema decoupling and explicit reasoning, with the second-stage model achieving an Execution Accuracy (EX) of approximately 85%. Results confirm that the proposed ranking strategy, skeleton constraints, and CoT distillation paradigm significantly improve generation stability and executability in complex query scenarios.

title: AI Driven Data Validation and Compliance Framework for Real-Time Cloud Pipelines
authors: Sai Kishore Chintakindhi
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The current paper provides an artificial intelligence-based end to end data validation and regulatory compliance framework in real time cloud native data pipelines. Current solutions tend to apply data quality assurance and compliance enforcement separately or as an after-processing activity which causes a fragmented trust, delayed anomaly detection, and a lack of flexibility to changing regulations. To overcome such restrictions, the framework presented here proposes a cascaded structure that consists of two complementary modules: the Aegis Fuzzy Recta Net, which is responsible of the adaptive detection and rectification of anomalies and the Lexi-Regula Transformer Engine, which is needed to interpret and enforce regulatory policies dynamically. Raw streaming data are consumed, regularized and verified and then finally undergo compliance classification in a single operational cycle. The framework is tested on the AWS Public NYC Taxi and Limousine Trips data to reflect scalability and application in real time. The experimental results demonstrate effective reduction of the severity of the anomaly and the high performance in the compliance classification in terms of precision, recall, and overall accuracy. The suggested solution allows making interpretable, regulation-conscious decisions and being robust and scalable, which is appropriate in the context of compliance-sensitive distributed cloud deployments.

title: Robot Localization Algorithm with Multi-Sensor Data Fusion Incorporating Attention Mechanism
authors: Yancheng Li, Hongli Wang, Zikun Lan, Shuai Zhou
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Traditional single sensor robot positioning has insufficient accuracy and robustness in complex environments. Although multi-sensor data fusion is a solution, it faces challenges such as data redundancy and noise interference. The aim of this study is to construct a multi-sensor localization algorithm that integrates attention mechanisms to improve the accuracy of robot localization and system robustness. Research and design a hierarchical architecture of “perception layer fusion layer decision layer”: the perception layer preprocesses multi-sensor data and extracts features, the fusion layer dynamically adjusts sensor data weights through a multi head self attention mechanism, and the decision layer combines extended Kalman filtering to complete pose estimation; Verify the performance of the algorithm through indoor and outdoor multi scene comparative experiments. The experimental results show that the algorithm has a positioning accuracy of 7.2cm (an improvement of 42.4% compared to traditional weighted average methods), a robustness index of 0.92, and maintains stable positioning in complex scenes. This study effectively solves the key problem of traditional multi-sensor fusion, providing technical support for highly autonomous navigation of robots in industrial automation, unmanned driving and other fields, and has important theoretical and practical value.

title: Research on the Application of Integrated Technologies of Large-Scale Layer 2 Network, 5G Network, and Internet of Things in the Outbound Process of Cigarette Finished Products
authors: Xin Wang, Lei Lu, Guanggeng Guo, Rui He, Shaoxue Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: To address the key issues in the outbound process of industrial cigarette finished products—including information congestion/loss caused by network fluctuations, incomplete exchange of three-code information (No.1 Engineering Code, quality traceability code, industry QR code), and insufficient efficiency of appearance defect detection—this paper proposes an integrated solution combining large-scale Layer 2 network, 5G, and Internet of Things (IoT) technologies. The core innovations are reflected in three aspects: 1) Constructing a four-layer “Perception-Network-Platform-Application” architecture to realize full-dimensional information collection and reliable transmission of cigarette cartons; 2) Optimizing device interconnection by leveraging the low-latency advantage of Large-scale Layer 2 networks and the high-speed transmission capability of 5G, solving the problem of slow response in traditional networks; 3) Integrating IoT sensing and data mining technologies to achieve synchronous verification of three-code information and appearance defects, filling the gap in quality control. Empirical results show that the solution increases outbound efficiency by 69.8 %, reduces the incorrect delivery and damage rate from 0.3 % to 0.05 %, and saves 1.25 million yuan in annual logistics costs, providing technical support for the intelligent transformation of tobacco logistics.

title: Master Data Architecture for Clinical Process Improvement
authors: Juan Gabriel Chumpitaz-Huarcaya, Wilver Auccahuasi
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The health sector is characterized by the use of a high number of technological systems, from medical devices to information systems, within the information systems we have a number of applications, such as the use of programming languages, database managers, which makes it difficult to make decisions based on the data obtained in the reports obtained from the information systems, which allows to have information in individual reports by systems and is not centralized, which makes its analysis difficult, given this situation, a method is proposed to manage reporting and data analysis processes, based on an SQL statement integration platform and which is then stored in a new database, this procedure allows greater integration of information, concluding that the proposed methodology allows to improve the performance of reports and data analysis by improving information management in clinical environments.

title: Adaptive Control of Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor Based on Fully Actuated System Method
authors: Zhixiang Zhao, Wenjin Hu, Lihong Wang, Liwei Shi, Meiyi Qing
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: The servo system is one of the key components of intelligent manufacturing, generally consisting of a servo driver and a permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM). Problems such as slow response time and large overshoot negatively affect the cooperative control among servo systems. By combining the mathematical model and state equations of PMSM, an error model of PMSM and a control strategy based on the Fully Actuated System (FAS) method are established. The crested porcupine optimization (CPO) algorithm is employed to optimize the controller parameters, thereby realizing adaptive control of PMSM. Simulation results demonstrate that the PMSM adaptive controller based on the FAS method achieves superior tracking performance and disturbance rejection. Specifically, the overshoot is reduced by 22.8% compared with PID control, and the settling time is shortened by 0.03 s.

title: Railway Track Crack Detection: A Comparative Study On Yolov7 And U-Net In Automated Inspection
authors: S Vishal, Hubert Shanthan, J Vijay Arputharaj
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: For railway networks to remain operationally safe and avoid catastrophic failures, structural integrity is essential. Track cracks can be found using labor-intensive, slow, and human error-prone manual inspection techniques. In this work, two cutting-edge deep learning models—YOLOv8 andU-Net v2—for automated railway track crack detection using high-resolution imagery from Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) are compared. In a real-world inspection scenario, we compare the different strategies of precise semantic segmentation (U-Net) and real-time object detection (YOLOv8) in order to assess their relative trade-offs. We compare performance on important metrics such as precision, recall, intersection over union (IoU), and inference speed using a custom dataset that was taken by a DJI Matrice 300 RTK drone. This work is novel because it examines how each model’s output—bounding boxes versus pixel-level masks—directly affects the usefulness for maintenance workflows from an application-focused perspective. According to our research,U-Net v2 offers the fine-grained information required for precise damage assessment, while YOLOv8 is best suited for quick, extensive screening. This study offers railway operators useful information for creating a multi-stage, hybrid inspection strategy that strikes a balance between accuracy and speed.

title: 6G Short Range Wireless Quantum Optical Communications with Sophisticated Channel Coding
authors: Peter Jung, Hamza Almujahed, Kushtrim Dini, Lukas Knopp
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: Research has shown that radio applications above <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$300 \mathbf{G H z}$</tex> require a very careful analog design that takes into account the frequency-dependent nature of ohmic resistance at these frequencies, bringing optical communication solutions for the sixth generation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(6 \mathrm{G}) \text{THz}$</tex> band located between 300 GHz and 10 THz, being well suited for short range links, into focus. The authors propose wireless quantum optical communications concepts replacing the classical radio frequency based inner physical layer transceivers used in classical channel coded short range wireless systems. Owing to low environmental interaction of such short range links, the coherence and interference ability when knowing and maintaining the carrier phase permits a beneficially robust transmission. Also, on the basis of today's wireless communication systems considering sophisticated channel codes such as the LTE turbo code and the WLAN LDPC code seems to be of greatest interest. Thus, besides illustrating the proposed wireless quantum optical communications concept, the optimum soft output quantum data detector for coherent reception with known carrier phase is discussed and both uncoded as well as novel channel coded bit error ratio performance results are presented.

title: A Phishing URL Detection Method Based on Gaussian Blur and Resnet-Atten
authors: Xingwang Li, Pengyu Chen, Wenguan Ning, Xiaoqin Li, Yu He, Juan Wen
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: With the widespread use of the Internet, phishing websites have become increasingly prevalent, posing a serious threat to users’ information security. Traditional phishing URL detection methods, which rely primarily on blacklists, textual features, or web content analysis, often fail to identify sophisticated phishing URLs with strong obfuscation techniques, especially under high-traffic conditions. This paper proposes a novel detection approach based on human visual perception. Specifically, URL strings are converted into images and processed with Gaussian blur to simulate visual cognition, after which a deep learning model, such as ResNet-Atten, is used to extract and classify image features. Experimental results demonstrate that this method exhibits strong detection performance against well-camouflaged phishing URLs and shows considerable potential for practical application in real-world network environments.

title: Flutter-based Digital Classroom App for Android & Ios
authors: P. Valarmathie, Agalya R, Harini P
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Digital learning environments require intelligent, engaging, and accessible platforms to address the limitations of conventional Learning Management Systems (LMS). This paper presents a Flutter-based digital classroom application for Android and iOS that integrates artificial intelligence and gamification to enhance student engagement and learning efficiency. The proposed system incorporates an AI academic companion using Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), a smart timetable with automated notifications, a PDF-to-audio converter for multimodal accessibility, and adaptive gamified quizzes. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that the application achieves an average app load time of 1.2 seconds on Android and 1.5 seconds on iOS, while AI Companion query response time remains below 1.0 second. The PDF-to-audio module completes conversions within 3–6 seconds, and quiz interactions exhibit latency below 200 ms. Cross-platform UI consistency reaches 98%, validating the effectiveness of Flutter for unified mobile development. Compared to traditional LMS platforms, the proposed system provides improved accessibility, real-time academic support, and higher user engagement. These results confirm that the system offers a scalable, intelligent, and efficient solution for next-generation digital education.

title: Multi-Layer Analytics for Rumour Propagation Speed in Social Networks
authors: Unnati Gayaki, Sneha Bansod
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The proliferation of rumours in social networks is a serious challenge because rumours are capable of affecting people and their behaviour in a brief period. The information flow in the contemporary communication space is simultaneous at the time when it is distributed across several connected platforms, including social media, messaging apps, and electronic communities. Consequently, single-layer network models can not be properly used to understand rumour propagation. The paper is a review article on multi-layer analytics in the speed of rumour propagation in social networks, whereby the interrelations of communication layers determine the speed of rumour propagation. The review focuses on such important modelling methods as multi-layer and multiplex network models, time dynamics and user behaviour variables that affect the propagation speed. It also emphasises on analytical measures to capture transitions of acceleration and cross-layers. Besides, the article touches upon the practical implementation of multi-layer rumour speed analysis in misinformation detection, monitoring of public opinion, and moderation of platforms. The main issues associated with complexity modeling, time congruency, user identification, and scale are reviewed, as well as the future research directions towards the enhancement of adaptive modelling and real-time analysis. On the whole, this piece of work offers a methodical insight into the role of multi-layer analytics to deepen the study of the speed at which rumours spread in the complicated social network settings.

title: Hybrid CNN-GOG Model for Robust Person Re-Identification and Trajectory Tracking
authors: Mohammad Nirou Jazi, Mohammed Ayoub Alaoui Mhamdi, Madjid Allili
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: As surveillance systems continue to expand in scale and deployment, the need for effective person reidentification (Re-ID) and dependable pedestrian trajectory tracking across different cameras has become more important. This paper presents a hybrid Re-ID framework that combines deep learning with handcrafted feature extraction to improve performance in tough real-world situations. We use two transfer learning models, ResNet-PRI and VGGPRI, to create strong deep representations. These are then combined with Gaussian of Gaussian (GOG) descriptors to build a complete appearance model. In this work, person re-identification is expressed as a similarity-based matching task using cosine similarity, without relying on explicit metric learning schemes. Experimental evaluations conducted on the CUHK01, CUHK03, and GRID benchmarks demonstrate that the proposed CNN-GOG framework attains superior Rank-1 performance and maintains stable accuracy under changes in lighting conditions, camera perspective, partial visibility, and spatial detail. These results emphasize the effectiveness and reliability of the hybrid approach for surveillance and multicamera monitoring applications.

title: An Explainable Deepfake Detection System using XceptionNet and Gradient-based Visualizations
authors: Vaishali Baviskar, Shweta Lilhare, Prasad Chaudhari, Smita Aher, Harsh Bharate, Omkar Rajewar
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The rapid increase in deepfake technology is considered a serious challenge to both digital trust and media genuineness. While deep learning models have achieved high accuracy in detecting manipulated media, most operate as "black boxes," providing predictions without justification. This lack of transparency limits their reliability in critical applications like digital forensics and journalism. This paper introduces an Explainable Deepfake Detection System designed to bridge this gap. Our system utilizes a pretrained XceptionNet model for the robust classification of images and videos. We distinguish our methodology by introducing a novel dual modal explanation pipeline that synergizes visual heatmaps (Grad-CAM) with localized textual justifications (LIME), offering superior interpretability compared to prior unimodal approaches. The system is evaluated on standard benchmarks, including FaceForensics++, Deepfake Detection Challenge (DFDC), and Celeb-DF, achieving an accuracy of 92.35%, precision of 91.27%, recall of 90.88%, and an F1-score of 91.07%. Furthermore, we address critical security vulnerabilities by evaluating how well the model handles unseen deepfake generation techniques, adversarial attacks, low quality compression, and facial occlusions, ensuring its reliability in real-world forensic scenarios.

title: Security Protection of Sensitive Data in Information Systems Based on Dynamic Encryption Technology
authors: Weilun Chen, Yan Jiao, Yiyao Chen, Wenpei Yan, Ke Li, Zonglin Cheng
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: With the acceleration of digital transformation, the leakage and unauthorized access of sensitive data in information systems are becoming increasingly prominent. Existing static encryption methods are unable to effectively address the dynamic security requirements of data throughout its transmission, storage, and usage lifecycle. More targeted security protection mechanisms are urgently needed. This paper, focusing on Dynamic Encryption (DE) technology, studies the security protection of sensitive data in information systems. We construct a security model based on dynamic key generation, automatic key rotation, and real-time monitoring of access behavior. The specific approach includes: 1) Using a Key Derivation Function (KDF) to generate dynamic keys at the user session level, effectively preventing the risks associated with key reuse; 2) Leveraging the immutable nature of blockchain, key rotation and access logs are transparently recorded; 3) A machine learning-based anomaly detection algorithm is introduced to monitor data access behavior in real-time and conduct risk assessments. Experimental deployment in a government information system demonstrates that the sensitive data leakage rate is approximately 15% of that of traditional static encryption, while improving data access latency by only 0.1 ms, achieving a balance between security and system performance. The conclusion demonstrates that the comprehensive protection model based on DE can significantly enhance the security level of sensitive data in information systems, providing a feasible path for data security management.

title: A Comparative Study of Classical Machine Learning Models for Facial Emotion Recognition with Handcrafted Features
authors: Fatima-Zahra Naciri, Insaf Bellamine, Abdelhak Boulaalam
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: Facial Emotion Recognition (FER) is essential in affective computing by enabling automatic interpretation of facial expressions across a variety of domains. Although machine learning (ML) has significantly advanced FER systems, achieving high accuracy remains challenging due to inter-individual variability and subtle emotional cues. In this study, we conduct a comparative evaluation of five classical ML classifiers — Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest, XGBoost, Naive Bayes, and Decision Tree — each combined with three widely-used handcrafted feature extraction methods: Histogram of Oriented Gradients (HOG), Local Binary Patterns (LBP), and Scale-Invariant Feature Transform (SIFT). Experiments were performed on the $C K+$ dataset after standard preprocessing, including face alignment and normalization. Results show that the HOG–SVM combination yields the best performance with an accuracy of 98.98%, outperforming all other configurations. These findings demonstrate the importance of selecting appropriate feature–classifier pairs and confirm the effectiveness of traditional ML approaches for FER, particularly in real-time or resource-constrained environments. The study also highlights the impact of preprocessing and hyperparameter tuning on model performance.

title: Design and Algorithm Research of an AI-Based Quality Inspection Intelligent System
authors: Lei Song, Xia Li, Xiaowei Meng, Xiaoyi Zhu, Wei Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: To address the challenges of traditional quality inspection technologies, which suffer from low accuracy and inefficiency and are unable to meet the demands of precision manufacturing, this paper designs a multimodal quality inspection intelligent system and proposes a multi-source feature fusion algorithm based on a dynamic attention mechanism. The system adopts a two-level “hardware-software” architecture. The hardware layer integrates high-resolution industrial cameras, high-precision sensors, and edge computing modules, while the software layer implements data preprocessing and defect detection. Experiments were conducted on a 5,000-piece bimodal dataset of automobile engine blocks. The proposed algorithm achieved an inspection accuracy of 98.2%, a 12.4 percentage point improvement over traditional RF algorithms. The inspection speed was 65 FPS, 5.4 times that of a support vector machine (SVM). The defect localization error was <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 2 ~ m m}$</tex>, a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 7. 1 \%}$</tex> reduction compared to a single ResNet-18 algorithm. The standard deviation of accuracy across 10 experiments was only 0.35%. After 30 days of application testing on an engine block production line, the system achieved an inspection accuracy rate of 97.8%, a false detection rate of 1.5%, a missed detection rate of 0.7%, and a single-piece inspection time of 0.42 seconds. This represents a 97.2% improvement in efficiency compared to manual inspection, with a failure rate of 0.2%. This system meets the demands of complex industrial working conditions and provides an effective solution for intelligent quality inspection in the manufacturing industry.

title: Forward Error Correction Considerations for Optical Satellite Links
authors: Eugenio Estinto, Scott Allwine, Luca Estinto
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: The worldwide adoption of Free-Space Optical Communications (FSOC) continues to advance due to its free spectrum and high bandwidth, including in the area of satellite communications. As with RF communications, forward error correction (FEC) is often employed in optical links to improve performance. qBeam has been researching and developing FSOC modems for nearly a decade, and has gained valuable insight into which FEC schemes are best suited for optical links. This paper will propose that treating optical communications in the same manner as RF satellite links is usually not the best approach from a FEC-selection perspective. This is due to the unique properties of optical detectors and the atmospheric turbulence effects associated with many optical links. This paper will describe two transmission channel regimes applicable to FSOC, compare the effectiveness of a few FEC approaches, and discuss ongoing qBeam optical modem efforts.

title: Plastic-YOLO: A Super-Resolution Enhanced Detection Framework for Riverine Plastic Waste Using an Optimized YOLOv11 Network
authors: Zunyu Liu, Liji Zhan, Qian Qiao, Xiaoyu Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: Riverine plastic pollution is a global threat, but accurate monitoring is hampered by environmental factors such as water surface glare, motion blur, and small scattered debris from low quality drone images. We present Plastic-YOLO, a detection framework that combines Super Resolve Reconstruction (SRR) with a optimized YOLOv11 network. The SRR module significantly improves image quality and experimentally found MambaIRv2 is the most efficient solution for reconstructing fine plastic details. We also improve the YOLOv11 backbone by three modules: MSCFN for multi-scale feature capture, LCAEM for lightweight computation and C2PSA for adaptive feature extraction. Ablation studies show that the fusion of these optimizations produces the best results. Based on high-fidelity data from rivers in Thailand and Laos, Full Plastic-YOLO achieved peak mean Average Precision (mAP<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">50</inf>) of 69.4% over baseline performance. We conclude that this SRR-enhanced and architecturally optimized approach provides highly robust, scalable and low cost solution for accurate river plastic governance. Future work will focus on unified end-to-end architectures for real-time applications.

title: On-Grid Automated Operation of Solar-Wind Integrated BESS Assisted Hybrid Microgrid for EV Charging
authors: Sheshadri Shekhar Rauth, Pushpendra Singh, Christina Sundari, Canute Sherwin, Abhishek Padmanabhan
abstract: This paper presents an automated power balancing approach for DC bus voltage regulation in an on-grid solar-wind integrated hybrid microgrid (HMG), assisted by a battery energy storage system (BESS). The latest reported work is limited to either a fixed reduction in the controllable variable (to reduce the source power generation during maximum SoC condition of the battery) or with an adaptive approach but applicable to a single source only. This work has automated the HMG-BESS operation using an adaptive strategy based on real-time battery-SoC condition, applicable to any number of controllable sources in the HMG. In HMG, to ensure a stable operation of the wind energy conversion system and solar photovoltaic system, the control strategy dynamically adjusts power flow by either operating all available sources in OFF-maximum power point mode at high SoC or reducing power supply to the grid at low SoC. Unlike existing studies focusing only on a single-source regulation approach, this work integrates multiple energy sources, enhancing the system's reliability. Opal-RT RTDS based simulation results demonstrate that the proposed automated control mechanism maintained the DC bus voltage regulated within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 4 \%$</tex> and the battery SoC within 20 % to 80 %, utilizing the maximum possible available sources and ensuring seamless microgrid operation under varying wind velocity (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7 ~\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}-11 ~\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$</tex>), solar irradiance (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$300 \mathrm{w} / \mathrm{m}^{2}-900 \mathrm{w} / \mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex>), and load <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(3.1 ~\text{kW}-16 ~\text{kW})$</tex> conditions.

title: The Influence of Core Geometry and Air Gaps on Magnetic Characteristics and Inductance of Dry-Type Series Reactor
authors: Davood Azizian, Shohreh Saberi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: The presence of air-gaps in the core limb is one of the key factors in designing the magnetic circuit and inductance calculations in core type series reactors. Variations in the length and number of air gaps not only affect the equivalent permeability and core saturation level but also influence the accuracy of modeling. In this study, an axi-symmetric two-dimensional model for dry-type series reactor was developed using finite element models. The inductance values obtained from the simulation were compared with the actual values and the model have been validated. Using the validated FE model, the effect of iron core's air gaps on the reactor inductance under different loading conditions was investigated.

title: Cluster-Aware Contrastive Learning Framework for Graph Embedding
authors: Yanqing Xu, Yangbangyan Jiang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: The study of contrastive learning (CL) has gained increasing attention in learning embeddings with better generalizability. However, over-segmentation becomes a bottleneck that limits the representative ability of CL. Particularly in the field of graph embedding, heuristic data augmentation exacerbates the over-segmentation of nodes by the CL mechanism. In response to this, we propose a new graph CL framework which (1) perturbs instances in the feature space by the Graph Perturbation Block (GPB) to avoid operations on the input space, and (2) dynamically selects positive and negative samples to involve semantic cluster information using the Progressive Contrastive Sampling (PCS) strategy. In this way, the contrastive learning method could be applied in a cluster-aware manner for node classification problems. Experimental validation on four public benchmarks demonstrates the effectiveness of the cluster-aware graph CL approach in facilitating the generation of transferable representations.

title: Near-field RCS Measurements: Based on ISAR Imaging with Scattering Center Feature Extraction
authors: Renfei Ning, Guoxu Feng
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: In this paper, a fusion method based on inverse synthetic aperture radar (ISAR) imaging and scattering center feature extraction is proposed for the key problems of wavefront curvature and multipath effect in near-field radar scattering cross-section (RCS) measurements. The study firstly establishes an electromagnetic scattering model under the incidence conditions of near-field spherical waves, and effectively solves the wavefront distortion problem in the near- and far-field transitions through a phase compensation algorithm; then, a column scanning data acquisition system is designed, and high-resolution ISAR imaging is realized by combining the two-dimensional Fourier transform with the theory of compressed sensing. At the feature extraction level, a scattering center detection method combining local signal-to-noise ratio adaptive thresholding and morphological filtering is adopted, and a parametric RCS inversion model based on geometric wrap-around theory is constructed. Simulation and experimental results show that the method can achieve better than 0.05 m resolution at 3 m near-field distance, and the deviation of the RCS value obtained by the scattering center method from the theoretical prediction is less than 7 dB, which verifies its robustness in complex electromagnetic environments. This study provides a new technical way for accurate measurement of target electromagnetic properties in compact space, which is especially suitable for application scenarios such as stealth material evaluation and dynamic target identification.

title: Design of IoT Gateway Control Platform Based on Single Chip Microcomputer
authors: Jian Zhao, Yanxiang Gong, Huiguo Cao, Shunwei Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: A microcontroller based IoT gateway control platform has been designed. The control platform uses STC89C52 microcontroller as the control core and adopts a modular design method to meet basic functions while introducing new serial peripherals and expansion interfaces, enhancing engineering applicability. The circuit structure and connection methods of each functional module have been designed in detail. According to the requirements and platform structure of the Internet of Things gateway, carefully designed testing projects were carried out for testing, making the platform easy to use and greatly improving its engineering applicability.

title: On the Benefit of Transfer Learning in Intrusion Detection Systems
authors: Mahdi Rabbani, Mohsen Tajgardan, Reza Khoshkangini, Israat Haque
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: Intrusion detection systems (IDSs) play a crucial role in identifying and protecting against malicious activities. Developing an intelligent and robust anomaly-based IDS requires effective feature extraction and classifier components to differentiate between malicious and benign instances. Also, due to the agility of the network conditions, the portability of such IDSs is essential. However, existing anomaly-based IDSs focus on model performance or transferability without integrating them for better generalized usage. This paper introduces a hybrid deep learning model, CNN-BiLSTM, which integrates convolutional neural networks (CNN) and bidirectional long-short-term memory (BiLSTM) to effectively process noisy and unbalanced network traffic by utilizing transfer learning. By leveraging pre-trained layers, the model performs effective cross-dataset and cross-attack detection, minimizing retraining efforts and adapting to unseen attack patterns. Additionally, this approach helps overcome data imbalance by reusing learned representations, which improves the detection of rare or emerging attack types. The model's performance, evaluated using state-of-the-art intrusion detection datasets, demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed transfer learning-based model for attack detection in security monitoring.

title: PaDTA: A Model-Level Disentanglement Study for Balancing Popularity Bias and Personalization in Recommendation Systems
authors: Liang Liu, Ye Fan, Tongxian Zhu, Xiang Gu, Xin Wang, Miao Tian
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Recommender systems have long faced a central dilemma: optimizing for immediate accuracy tends to over-promote popular items, sacrificing coverage of long-tail inventory and degrading user experience. Existing methods attempt to balance this trade-off within a single model, yielding only limited gains. In this paper we propose PaDTA, a novel popularity-aware dual-tower architecture that addresses the problem through model-level disentanglement. PaDTA's core is a two-tower network: one tower specializes in learning generic preferences from popular interactions, while the other focuses on mining personalized signals from long-tail interactions. The two towers are fused by a lightweight, personalized gating network that adaptively computes mixing weights from each user's historical behavior, producing customized recommendation lists for users with different popularity preferences. Experiments on public datasets show that, compared with traditional recommenders and state-of-the-art models, PaDTA significantly improves item coverage while maintaining competitive Hit Rate (HR) and Normalized Discounted Cumulative Gain (NDCG), jointly optimizing accuracy and diversity. Our work demonstrates that structural disentanglement is an effective route to resolving the fundamental tension in recommender systems.

title: AI-Powered IoT Assistant for Electric Vehicles: Fine-Tuning a Small Language Model for Intent Recognition and Response Generation
authors: Vikram Pal, Manpreet Kour, Gaurav Sethi
abstract: The integration of artificial intelligence (AI) and Internet of Things (IoT) in electric vehicles (EVs) has revolutionized vehicle automation, user interaction, and energy efficiency. This paper presents an AI-powered IoT assistant designed to enhance EV user experience by understanding and responding to natural language queries. We fine-tune a Small Language Model (SLM), Phi-3.5 Mini Instruct, using Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) with QLoRA, optimizing it for intent recognition, entity extraction, and response generation. A synthetic multi-turn conversation dataset with 50 intents and entities was generated using GPT-4o, covering various EV-related tasks such as battery monitoring, charging station navigation, and vehicle control. The model was fine-tuned using 861 training samples, 50 validation samples, and evaluated on 100 test samples. The training process, executed on four A10G GPUs, achieved a training loss of 0.18 and validation loss of 0.21. Evaluation results demonstrated significant improvements in intent recognition, with precision, recall, and F1-score increasing from the 40s to the 70s after fine-tuning. Additionally, response quality assessment using GPT-4.0 showed notable enhancements in accuracy, coherence, and specificity. These results validate the effectiveness of lightweight domain-specific fine-tuning and highlight the potential for deploying efficient AI-driven assistants in smart vehicles. Future work will focus on expanding real-world training data, optimizing for edge deployment, integrating multimodal inputs, and enhancing security mechanisms. This research contributes to the advancement of context-aware, intelligent vehicle assistants, paving the way for seamless human-AI interaction in electric mobility.

title: Similarity Consistency Learning Hashing for cross-modal retrieval
authors: Renwei Xia, Yuting Hou, Peng Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: Cross-modal hashing enables efficient retrieval across heterogeneous modalities by learning compact binary representations. Most existing methods rely on semantic labels, limiting their applicability in large-scale or weakly annotated scenarios. In this paper, we propose Similarity Consistency Learning Hashing (SCLH), an unsupervised method that learns semantically aligned hash codes by preserving cross-modal similarity consistency. SCLH constructs soft semantic guidance from original image and text features and enforces their consistency in the hash space, capturing global semantic structures with a simple and efficient framework. By jointly optimizing similarity consistency and quantization, SCLH generates discriminative binary codes for cross-modal retrieval. Experiments on benchmark datasets demonstrate that our method achieves competitive performance compared with state-of-the-art unsupervised cross-modal hashing methods.
