title: SocioMathLLM: A Multimodal Large Language Model Framework for Generating Authentic Mathematical Word Problems Driving Socialization
authors: Wu-Yuin Hwang, Ika Qutsiati Utami
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated significant advancements in automatic question generation (AQG), contributing to enhanced learning and teaching efficiency by facilitating the creation of human-like educational content and assessment materials. However, in mathematics education, where generating extensive sets of math word problems (MWPs) requires significant time and effort, the use of LLMs to design tasks that foster socialization competencies such as group collaboration, cooperation, negotiation, and ideas-sharing among students remains unexplored. This study makes the first attempt to develop a specialized multimodal LLM framework, SocioMathLLM, which integrates an innovative prompt engineering strategy to incorporate multimodal authentic contextual information (text and image), group background information, and socialization criteria (i.e., group size, task nature, social goal, social frequency, social-quality, interaction quantity, and social interdependency) to generate MWPs aimed at fostering group socialization. SocioMathLLM was evaluated through automatic metrics, human evaluation, and LLM-based evaluation. The automatic evaluation result demonstrates good performance in generating MWPs that promote socialization with an average precision of 0.906, recall of 0.943, and F1 score of 0.925. A total of 100 evaluation cases by humans and LLM also validated the robustness of SocioMathLLM, highlighting its potential for application in collaborative mathematics learning and assessment contexts.

title: DFSNet-VLM: A Hybrid Frequency-Aware and Vision-Language Framework for Remote Sensing Scene Classification and Semantic Image Explanation
authors: Muhammad John Abbas, Muhammad Attique Khan, Ameer Hamza, Shrooq Alsenan, Areej Alasiry, Mehrez Marzougui, Jungpil Shin, Yunyoung Nam
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Remote sensing has always been an area of interest for researchers due to its significance in Earth monitoring, which supports proper future planning for agriculture, construction, reforestation, and climate change. Transformer architecture achieves significant performance in remote sensing image classification; however, they come with the trade-off of higher computational complexity. In this paper, we propose a novel deep learning framework, DFSNet-VLM—Cross Domain Fusion based Texture-Sensitive Dual Stream Network — for high-precision remote sensing scene understanding. The proposed framework includes a classification model, “DFSNet,” that improves feature representation by employing both spatial and frequency domain features, which ultimately help detect global patterns and textures alongside local features. The model also promotes information exchange between both streams to complement one type of features with respect to the other by integrating cross-domain fusion blocks at multiple stages. Additionally, a pretrained VLM model, “BLIP-2,” is integrated to provide semantic descriptions of classified images. Bayesian optimization is applied to fine tune hyperparameters, reducing overfitting and improving model performance. The proposed model is evaluated on six diverse publicly available datasets and achieves improved accuracies of 97.13% on MLRSNet, 94.67% on NWPU-RESISC-45, 98.00% on EuroSAT, 92.25% on GeoSceneNet16k, 98.25% on cloud, and 96.03% on the Bijie-landslide dataset, respectively. Detailed ablation studies, comparative analysis, and Grad-CAM++-based model explainability demonstrate that the proposed model is generalizable and scalable, and that it achieves improved accuracy. In addition, the proposed model can be easily implemented in a real-time environment for diverse applications. The trained model's links are available in the data availability section.

title: RSRNav: Reasoning Spatial Relationship for Image-Goal Navigation
authors: Zheng Qin, Le Wang, Yabing Wang, Sanping Zhou, Gang Hua, Wei Tang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Recent image-goal navigation (ImageNav) methods learn a perception-action policy by separately capturing semantic features of the goal and egocentric images, then passing them to a policy network. However, challenges remain: (1) Semantic features often fail to provide accurate directional information, leading to superfluous actions, and (2) performance drops significantly when viewpoint inconsistencies arise between training and application. To address these challenges, we propose RSRNav, a simple yet effective method that reasons spatial relationships between the goal and current observations as navigation guidance. Specifically, we model the spatial relationship by constructing correlations between the goal and current observations, which are then passed to the policy network for action prediction. These correlations are progressively refined using fine-grained cross-correlation and direction-aware correlation for more precise navigation. Extensive evaluation of RSRNav on three benchmark datasets demonstrates superior navigation performance, particularly in the "user-matched goal" setting, highlighting its potential for real-world applications. Code: https://github.com/ qinzheng2000/RSRNav.git.

title: MoCom: Motion-based Inter-MAV Visual Communication Using Event Vision and Spiking Neural Networks
authors: Zhang Nengbo, Hann Woei Ho, Ye Zhou
abstract: Reliable communication in Micro Air Vehicle (MAV) swarms is challenging in environments, where conventional radio-based methods suffer from spectrum congestion, jamming, and high power consumption. Inspired by the waggle dance of honeybees, which efficiently communicate the location of food sources without sound or contact, we propose a novel visual communication framework for MAV swarms using motion-based signaling. In this framework, MAVs convey information, such as heading and distance, through deliberate flight patterns, which are passively captured by event cameras and interpreted using a predefined visual codebook of four motion primitives: vertical (up/down), horizontal (left/right), left-to-up-to-right, and left-to-down-to-right, representing control symbols (“start”, “end”, “1”, “0”). To decode these signals, we design an event frame-based segmentation model and a lightweight Spiking Neural Network (SNN) for action recognition. An integrated decoding algorithm then combines segmentation and classification to robustly interpret MAV motion sequences. Experimental results validate the framework's effectiveness, which demonstrates accurate decoding and low power consumption, and highlights its potential as an energy-efficient alternative for MAV communication in constrained environments.

title: Control of Low-Frequency Switch-Node Current Using PCB-Embedded Rogowski Coils with Reduced Sampling in GaN-Based PFC Converter_supp1-3676688.mp4
authors: P T NANDH KISHORE
abstract: Multiple currents are sensed within a power converter, requiring sensors with different bandwidths. Low-bandwidth sensors are typically used for average current control, while high-bandwidth sensors are employed for device current sensing and protection. Faster schemes of fault protection are required for GaN HEMTs. An effective solution for measuring high slew-rate switching transients in GaN HEMTs is the PCB coil-based current sensor, which offers high bandwidth and easy integrability. However, this sensor cannot be directly used to measure switch-node currents, which may be DC or line-frequency AC. This article proposes an online, analog-based reconstruction scheme for measurement of the switch-node grid current, with device current information from PCB-embedded Rogowski Coil (PCB-RC) sensors. The analog realisation facilitates current reconstruction with low latency and reduced sampling requirement. The reconstructed grid current is used for control of a GaN-based 3.3 kW Bidirectional Totem-pole PFC converter. Additionally, an overcurrent protection scheme is implemented using the reconstructed grid current, triggering safe shutdown of the converter during overload. Further, with the developed PCB-RCs, short-circuit protection of GaN HEMTs is achieved for a DC bus fault at 400 V. Therefore, PCB-RCs are demonstrated as a viable alternative to CTs and Hall-effect sensors, serving dual purposes of current control and protection.

title: Edge-Optimized Battery SOH Estimation Using LLM-Based Feature Engineering and TinyELM
authors: Younha Kim, Saebin Shin, Eunjae Cha, Xiaoyong Guo, Wansu Lim
abstract: This paper presents a computationally efficient approach for state-of-health (SOH) estimation. This approach has two novel components: large language model (LLM)-based prompt engineering for feature extraction and a edge-optimized extreme learning machine (TinyELM) for efficient SOH estimation. In our LLM-based feature engineering, we leverage GPT-5 to automatically generate potential health indicators from raw voltage and current data, then select two to four features that exhibit strong correlations with the target SOH. Building upon these features, we develop TinyELM as a streamlined and optimized variant of ELM designed for improved estimation accuracy and portability on resource-constrained edge devices. The performance of TinyELM is compared against several deep learning-based models as well as the conventional ELM model and linear-based models on three edge devices, namely, the STM32, Raspberry Pi 4, and Jetson Nano. Experimental results demonstrate that TinyELM achieves state-of-the-art performance in SOH estimation while delivering up to 28.4× faster inference than the conventional ELM and 3× faster inference than the other linear models. Furthermore, the results highlight the effectiveness of our LLM-based feature engineering, showing approximately 5× lower root mean square error on average compared to traditional and automated feature engineering approaches.

title: Direct Digital-to-Physical Synthesis: From Millimeter-Wave Transmitter to Qubit Control
authors: Najme Ebrahimi, Haoling Li, Gun Suer, Kin Chung Fong, Leonardo Ranzani
abstract: The increasing demand for high-speed wireless connectivity and scalable quantum information processing has driven parallel advancements in millimeter-wave (mm-wave) communication transmitters and cryogenic qubit controllers. Despite serving different applications, both systems rely on the precise generation of RF waveforms with stringent requirements on spectral purity, timing, and amplitude control. Recent architecture eliminates conventional methods by embedding digital signal generation and processing directly into the RF path, transforming digital bits into physical waveforms for either electromagnetic transmission or quantum state control. This article presents a unified analysis of direct digital modulation techniques across both domains, showing the synergy and similarities between these two domains. The article also focuses on four core architectures: Cartesian in-phase/quadrature (I/Q), polar, RF digital-to-analog converter (DAC), and harmonic/subharmonic modulation across both domains. We analyze their respective tradeoffs in energy efficiency, signal integrity, waveform synthesis, and error mitigations, and we highlight how architectural innovations in one domain can accelerate progress in the other.

title: Interpretable Long-Term Methane Concentration Prediction via Transformer Architectures and SHAP Analysis
authors: Jinhong Lai
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: This study employs Transformer-based models, including Informer, AutoFormer, and FEDformer, to construct an intelligent gas concentration forecasting system. Through comprehensive feature engineering that integrates lag features, rolling statistical features, and temporal interaction features, the proposed approach achieves high prediction accuracy, with an RMSE of 0.0072. Ablation experiments indicate that lag features have the most significant impact on forecasting performance, with the RMSE increasing by 27.27% after their removal. Furthermore, SHAP-based interpretability analysis validates the effectiveness of the selected features and reveals the nonlinear temporal dynamics underlying gas concentration evolution. The proposed method provides an effective technical pathway for high-precision time series forecasting in complex industrial scenarios.

title: A Smart Collaborative Platform for Streamlined Team Management and Workflow Optimization
authors: Kp Muhammed Nafih, P Madhav, K Krishnasree, Tm MeghaShaji, Ierin Babu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: Effective task management and coordination are essential in collaborative work environments, yet many existing systems suffer from fragmented communication and inefficient workflows. This paper presents FlowForge, a web-based team management platform designed to address these challenges through centralized task assignment, secure access control, and automated productivity monitoring. The system supports task prioritization, task update notifications, and performance tracking to improve accountability and workflow efficiency. FlowForge is developed using a modular architecture comprising a React.js frontend, a Node.js and Express.js backend, and a MySQL database. Role-based access is implemented to distinguish administrative, HR, and employee privileges. Preliminary evaluations demonstrate improvements in task completion rates and organizational productivity. In small-scale testing with 15 users, FlowForge reduced average task completion time by 18% and improved workflow consistency by 22% compared to manual coordination methods. A comparative evaluation with popular tools such as Trello and Asana showed faster task updates and lower coordination overhead. The platform’s scalable design allows deployment in organizations ranging from small teams to large enterprises. Future work includes the addition of multilingual support, enhanced security features, and integration with thirdparty productivity tools.

title: YOLOv8s-PSM: A novel YOLOv8s with PConv and SA for chestnut burr detection
authors: Zhangbao Chen, Chunlan Yang, Yong Hou, Yunsheng Deng
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Manual chestnuts harvesting is labor-intensive and poses high safety risks. Developing computer vision-based chestnut burr detection is of significant importance for achieving automated chestnuts picking. To tackle the challenges of diverse lighting, complex backgrounds, and the characteristics of chestnut burrs prone to foliage occlusion and clustered overlapping in natural chestnut orchards, this study focuses on designing a lightweight and high - precision chestnut burr detection model. Using YOLOv8s as the baseline model, we propose YOLOv8s-PSM, a chestnut burr detection model integrating PConv (Partial Convolution) and SA (Shuffle Attention) mechanisms. First, PConv replaces standard convolutions in the C2f module of YOLOv8s to achieve lightweight design. Second, the SA attention mechanism is embedded in the model's head to enhance localization accuracy and maturity classification precision without increasing computational complexity. Finally, utilizing the spiny spherical morphology of chestnut burr, the MPDIoU loss function is adopted to further improve localization accuracy. Experimental results demonstrate that the improved YOLOv8s-PSM model achieves superior performance on the chestnut burr detection dataset. Compared with YOLOv8s, YOLOv8s-PSM reduces parameters and FLOPs by 36% and 28% respectively, while improving mAP@0.5 and mAP@0.5:0.95 by 4.6 and 3.5 percentage points. The YOLOv8s-PSM model meets the demand for high-precision lightweight chestnut burr detection and provides technical support for automated chestnuts harvesting in natural environments.

title: Decentralized Blockchain Network for Sustainable EV Battery Recycling: Advancing the Circular Economy
authors: Aashish Kumar, Ram Kumar R, Perumal A T
abstract: The rapid adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) demands effective management of end-of-life batteries to mitigate environmental impacts. Conventional centralized recycling approaches frequently encounter inefficiencies and limited visibility, contributing to suboptimal recovery rates (typically below 60% in many regions). This paper proposes a decentralized blockchain-based network for EV battery recycling, enhancing traceability, security, and stakeholder collaboration. By utilizing smart contracts and token-based rewards, the system promotes a circular economy and lowers expenses. An expanded case study, including comparative analysis with existing pilots, demonstrates improved recycling efficiency and cost reductions. This research addresses literature gaps by offering a scalable framework for eco-friendly transportation, with contributions including enhanced stakeholder incentives, AI integration for predictive analytics and evaluations of real-world implementations. This framework outperforms traditional systems by potentially achieving 20-25% higher recycling rates.

title: Research on Multi-modal Visual Embodied Intelligent Navigation Algorithm for Complex Environment Perception
authors: Yingying Li, Qiuxue Ouyang, Chaojie Wang, Jian Tang, Zengbin Wang
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: In this paper, a multi-modal visual embodied intelligent navigation algorithm for complex environment perception is studied, aiming at solving the problem that traditional navigation methods do not perform well in complex environments such as dynamic obstacles, lighting changes, semantic fuzzy scenes and sensor noise. The algorithm integrates various modal data such as vision, lidar and inertial measurement unit (IMU), and introduces a dynamic weight allocation strategy based on attention mechanism to adaptively adjust the contribution of each sensor and improve the robustness of the system. Combining semantic segmentation with geometric SLAM technology, a semantic map is constructed to enhance the modeling accuracy of dynamic scenes, and a navigation decision-making mechanism driven by reinforcement learning (RL) is adopted to optimize path planning and obstacle avoidance strategies. Experiments are carried out in simulation and real environment. The results show that the algorithm is significantly superior to traditional methods in navigation success rate, path efficiency and security, and has good environmental adaptability and perceptual reliability, which provides an effective solution for embodied intelligent autonomous navigation in complex environment.

title: SEAL-MAC: An Energy-Anticipatory Adaptive LEACH Protocol using LSTM Forecasting for Solar-Powered IoT-WSNs
authors: Abdul Aleem, Rajesh Thumma, Md Imran V A, H. Abdul Wasay, A. Bhuvaneshwari, Amina Begum
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: A fundamental challenge in solar-powered Internet of Things-based Wireless Sensor Networks (IoT-WSNs) is to ensure long-term energy sustainability as the harvested energy is intermittent and less predictable. The traditional clustering methods like LEACH and its variants are based on residual energy or random cluster-head selection. They do not take the advantage of future energy availability, which results in inefficient energy utilization and low network lifetime. The paper suggests Solar Energy Anticipatory Leach- Medium Access Control (SEAL-MAC), an energy-anticipatory adaptive LEACH that incorporates the solar energy forecasting with the application of Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM). In SEAL-MAC sensor nodes forecast the harvested short-term energy based on history of solar data. The forecasted energy is integrated in both cluster-head selection and MAC-layer scheduling. This cross-layer model allows energy-consciousness in role assignment, duty cycling and low communication overheads in dynamic energy scenarios. Simulation findings show that SEAL-MAC achieves energy savings of up to 9.7%, network lifetime of 10.5% and a packet delivery ratio of 1.6% as compared to the state-of-the-art LEACH-based protocols. The results verify that predictive energy management can be successful in resilient long-term deployments of IoT-WSN.

title: Effectiveness Evaluation and Optimization of Navigation Mark Deployment Based on Ship Trajectory Big Data Mining
authors: Qiang Guo, Fan Zou
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: This paper focuses on the effectiveness evaluation and optimization of navigation mark deployment, using ship trajectory big data as the core. An improved Douglas-Peucker algorithm and spatiotemporal interpolation completion method are designed to preprocess AIS data. An improved DBSCAN algorithm incorporating channel constraints is used to mine trajectory features and identify typical navigation patterns and hazardous areas. A three-dimensional evaluation index system of safety, efficiency, and economy is constructed. The combined weights are determined using the AHP-entropy weight method, and a comprehensive effectiveness evaluation is achieved using the GBRT model (RMSE=0.06, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>=0.94). An improved PSO multi-objective optimization algorithm with adaptive weights and constraint processing mechanisms is proposed to achieve coordinated optimization of "safety-efficiency-cost". Experimental results show that after optimization, the ship deviation rate decreased from 18.7% to 8.3%, the navigation mark coverage efficiency increased to 91.5%, and the deployment cost decreased by 12.6%. Furthermore, it exhibits strong adaptability in complex navigation scenarios, providing technical support for intelligent optimization of navigation mark deployment.

title: CRAG-IKG: A Conflict- and Reliability-Aware GraphRAG Framework for Noisy Industrial Knowledge Graphs
authors: Changwu Wang, Yihan Fu, Changyuan Wang, Lin Wang, Qiang Zheng, Haoning Zhang, Minghui Li, Fanjie Meng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: Industrial knowledge graphs (IKGs) extracted from OCR and information extraction over heterogeneous technical documents are often noisy and conflict-rich, making GraphRAG retrieval brittle and answers hard to justify. We propose CRAGIKG, a conflict- and reliability-aware GraphRAG framework tailored to such industrial settings. Offline, we compute interpretable quality features and learn a calibrated triple prior confidence $C_{t}$, then refine file-level source reliability $R_{s}$ within conflict clusters without aggressive pruning. Online, we retrieve over a weighted KG via personalized PageRank to obtain graph relevance $G_{t}(q)$, fuse it with dense text relevance $T_{t}(q)$ and a lightweight verification score $V_{t}$, and apply conflict-aware normalization to rank competing claims and expose uncertainty with citations. Experiments on a real-world steel-process KG show improved triple discrimination, stronger conflict ranking, and higher QA correctness/faithfulness than text-only and naϊve GraphRAG baselines, with better robustness under synthetic corruption. The design is lightweight and interpretable, making it suitable for latency-constrained industrial QA with humanauditable evidence.

title: Distributed Brain-Cerebellum Architecture for Robotic Arm Control via Vision-Language Models and Generative Policies
authors: Tong Zhang, Danyong Li, Qingao Du, Mingxuan Ma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: To address the limitations of traditional robotic arm control in semantic understanding and action generalization within unstructured environments, we design and implement a control system based on a distributed "brain-cerebellum" architecture. Using complex grasping and collaborative tasks as examples, the system integrates multimodal perception, logical task decomposition, and generative motion control. The Qwen2.5-VL vision-language model serves as the decision-making core, enabling precise interpretation of natural language instructions and long-horizon task planning. In the execution layer, generative strategies—including Diffusion Policy and Flow Matching—map high-level commands to smooth and robust joint trajectories. To meet the computational constraints of edge devices, knowledge distillation and AWQ quantization are applied for model compression. This establishes a closed-loop pipeline from semantic reasoning to precise execution, allowing the robotic arm to autonomously complete complex tasks while maintaining real-time inference performance on edge terminals.

title: Liver Disease Prediction using Ensemble Learning and Deep Learning Model
authors: Rakhmatova Dilbar, Anorgul Ashirova, L.Saravanan L. Saravanan, Chori Chorshanbiev, C.Anusuya, B.Venkataramanaiah
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Humans are inventing innovative technologies in the medical field. These technologies are used for doctors as well as researchers to predict human health disorders early. Early disease monitoring can reduce death rate. Liver disease is a disorder that damages the liver due to various conditions such as alcohol drinking, obesity, genetic disorders and infections. Several liver diseases considered in proposed research such as Fatty liver disease, alcoholic disease, viral hepatitis, cirrhosis, inherited disorders and autoimmune liver disease. Deep learning is the best technique to predict liver diseases more accurately over machine learning algorithms. Convolution Neural Network(CNN) and XGboost from the artificial intelligence used in proposed work to detect liver diseases. CNN predicts liver diseases with more accuracy of 98% over existing works and XGboost. The Proposed research is useful for people to save their life from liver diseases because early monitoring and detection of liver disorders by CNN with highest prediction

title: A Measurement Inaccuracy Monitoring Method of Electricity Meter Based on Whole-Box Measurment
authors: Guangyu Liu, Yu Dai, Jianjun Zhang, Lin Gao, Hui Zhao, Baochan Huang
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: Aiming at the problem that the inaccurate metering of electricity meter in metering boxes cannot be found in time during long-term operation, a measurement inaccuracy monitoring method of electricity meter based on whole-box measurement is proposed. The common causes of inaccurate metering of electricity meter are analyzed. The method for judging the inaccuracy of electricity meter based on whole-box metering is designed, and the on-line monitoring process for inaccurate metering is constructed. The effectiveness and feasibility of the proposed method are verified through real experiments. This method can monitor the metering abnormalities of electricity meter in real time and provide technical support for the management of electric energy metering boxes.

title: An Improved Wavelet Thresholding Denoising Algorithm Based on the Squared Hyperbolic Tangent Operator
authors: Mengyu Wang, Zhengang Wan
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: To address the limitations of conventional wavelet thresholding—specifically constant deviation and poor adaptability—this paper proposes an improved strategy. For threshold selection, a hierarchical adaptive scheme is introduced. By substituting global data length with layer-specific coefficient lengths and incorporating a logarithmic damping factor, the threshold exhibits smooth decay, effectively correcting statistical estimation bias in deeper layers. Regarding function construction, a novel thresholding function based on the squared hyperbolic tangent (tanh<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) operator utilizes nonlinear additive compensation. This eliminates the constant deviation of soft thresholding, ensuring both global continuity and asymptotic unbiasedness. Simulations demonstrate that the proposed algorithm outperforms traditional and similar methods in denoising Doppler signals under varying SNRs, significantly enhancing signal fidelity.

title: Multi-Parameter Feature Extraction and Fault Intelligent Identification Method for Secondary Cable Connection Performance in Substation
authors: Zhenhua Wang, Wei Liu, Guoyang Zhao
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of single feature dimension, lack of real-time performance and weak engineering application in secondary cable connection performance detection and fault diagnosis of substation, this paper puts forward a method combining multi-parameter feature extraction with fault intelligent identification. In the aspect of feature extraction, a multi-dimensional feature library covering electrical, mechanical, environmental and communication signals is constructed, including the dynamic change rate of contact resistance, capacitive reactance characteristic parameters, time-frequency joint analysis characteristics of vibration signals, deterioration index model based on temperature and humidity changes, etc. By integrating numerical calculation and sensor data extraction methods, the comprehensive characterization of cable state is realized. In the aspect of fault identification, the dual-channel deep learning framework is adopted, the electrical features are input to 1D-CNN, and the mechanical and environmental features are input to LSTM. After the high-level features extracted from the two sub-networks are integrated in the feature fusion layer, the fault types are identified by LightGBM classifier. The improved mRMR algorithm is used to optimize the feature dimension reduction, support the incremental learning mechanism, and introduce the transfer learning strategy to improve the adaptability and generalization ability of the model. The experiment is based on the secondary cable simulation platform. The results show that this method is significantly better than the traditional method with an accuracy of 96.8%, and it has good advantages of light weight and energy efficiency in edge equipment. The annual maintenance man-hours of a single station are greatly reduced, the average time spent on troubleshooting is significantly shortened, and the proportion of preventive maintenance is greatly improved, which can effectively guarantee the reliable connection of secondary cables in substations and the safe operation of the power grid.

title: Size Reduction Microstrip Patch Antenna with Modified Double Slot at 2.35 GHz
authors: Muhammad Rafliansyah, Fannush S. Akbar, Muhsin, E.M Khusna
publish: 2026 Symposium on Microwave, Antenna, and Propagation (SMAP)
abstract: This paper proposes a compact microstrip patch antenna with a modified double slot structure for operation at 2.35 GHz targeting 5G Band n40. The dual slot configuration is introduced on the radiating patch to extend the effective surface current path, enabling antenna miniaturization without increasing substrate dimensions. A conventional patch antenna is designed as a reference and compared with the proposed slotted design through full-wave simulations. The results show an 8% reduction in linear dimensions and a 15.36% reduction in radiating area while maintaining resonance at 2.35 GHz. The reflection coefficient improves from -16 dB to -22 dB, indicating enhanced impedance matching, with a moderate reduction in realized gain due to the reduced effective aperture. The radiation pattern remains broadside and dominated by the fundamental TM <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{T M}_{10}$</tex> mode, confirming that the proposed double slot structure provides an effective and low complexity size reduction approach for compact <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 G}$</tex> antenna applications.

title: Optimizing Machine Learning Models for Breast Cancer Prediction: A Comparative Study of Advanced Hyperparameter Tuning Techniques
authors: Mrityunjay Shukla, Hardik Choudhary, S. Prita, Ramyashree, Anoop B N, Raghavendra S
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)

title: Real-Time Data Integration in Training and FineTuning Large Language Models (LLMs)
authors: Manjula Sanjay Koti, Samarth Warad, K S Nandini Prasad, B Spandana, C Vinutha, M R Jagadeesh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: The increasing use of Large Language Models (LLMs) for various tasks has created a strong need for efficient, precise, and scalable methods for training and fine-tuning. Real-time data integration offers a promising way to improve the flexibility of LLMs by including the latest information during both initial training and ongoing updates. However, this approach poses challenges, such as data preprocessing delays, retraining overhead, and consistency in distributed systems. This paper presents a framework for real-time data ingestion, preprocessing, and model updates, which aims to reduce latency while maintaining accuracy. The proposed design employs streaming data pipelines, adaptable learning rates, and incremental fine-tuning strategies to support ongoing model improvement. Experimental results indicate that real-time integration helps reduce model drift, boosts domain-specific performance, and enhances responsiveness in dynamic areas like conversational AI, recommendation systems, and decision-support tools.

title: Research on Cross-Regional Crop Pest and Disease Collaborative Diagnosis Based on Federated Knowledge Graphs: An AI Reasoning Mechanism Integrating Multi-Source Privacy Data and Domain Knowledge
authors: Wang Mu
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: To accomplish intelligent co-diagnosis of cross-regional crop pests and diseases, as well as facilitate knowledge sharing and reasoning while protecting privacy, this paper suggests a federated knowledge graph-based diagnosis approach. By aggregating multi-source and heterogeneous agricultural data, a federated knowledge graph is established through alignment and grouping of concepts. Additionally, data privacy can be protected through application of differential privacy techniques within a federated system. Algorithms of multimodal feature fusion and neural-symbolic hybrid reasoning enable improvement of model interpretability and cross-region diagnosis capability. Experiments have proven that this diagnosis model has better accuracy, efficiency, and privacy preservation compared with other centralized deep learning diagnosis models, and can thus serve as a technical means for agriculture knowledge co-sharing and intelligent diagnosis tasks.

title: Distributed Intelligent Home Appliance User Privacy Protection and Data Communication Optimization Based on Federated Learning
authors: Xuerong Wang, Subin Wang, Jin Li
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: This paper proposes a privacy-enhanced federated learning framework for smart home appliances. In the system architecture, a three-level collaborative structure of terminal layer, edge layer and cloud layer is adopted to reduce the communication burden of the terminal and enhance the scalability and privacy protection of the system. In terms of privacy protection, a layered protection mechanism combining local differential privacy (LDP) and dynamic gradient mask (DGM) is designed. Lightweight LDP is used in the terminal layer to add Gaussian noise, and DGM mechanism is introduced in the edge layer and cloud layer to dynamically adjust the mask probability, thus reducing the equipment burden while resisting inversion attacks. In communication optimization, the strategies of state-aware asynchronous aggregation (S3A) and adaptive gradient quantization (AGQ) based on device state awareness are adopted. S3A selects the devices participating in the aggregation according to the stability of the device state and dynamically adjusts the aggregation opportunity, while AGQ dynamically adjusts the quantization bit width according to the gradient information content to reduce the communication overhead and improve the training robustness. In addition, a multi-objective optimization model is constructed, and the dynamic balance among privacy protection, communication efficiency and model performance is achieved through the cloud joint optimization algorithm. Based on the published smart home data set USTC-TFC2016, the experiment simulates 100 heterogeneous smart home appliances for evaluation. The results show that under the strong privacy setting (ε = 1.2), the accuracy of this method is 82.3%, which is significantly better than the traditional LDP-FL method. The average transmission volume per round is reduced to 5.3MB, and the total traffic required to achieve 85% accuracy is only 13.7GB, which is 64% lower than FedAvg, which verifies the effectiveness of the proposed framework in privacy protection, communication efficiency and model performance.

title: Reconstructing Attribute Distributions with Similarity-Weighted Adaptive Prefixes
authors: Hao Lei, Li Mou
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: To address the challenges of control attenuation, quality degradation, and limited multi-attribute flexibility in Controlled Text Generation (CTG), this paper proposes the SWAP (Similarity-Weighted Adaptive Prefix Generation) framework. SWAP first employs Prefix-Tuning to optimize a small number of attribute-specific prefix vectors while freezing the parameters of a pretrained GPT-2 Large model, achieving parameter-efficient encoding of sentiment and topic attributes. Then, a Similarity-Weighted Decoding strategy reconstructs the class-conditional distribution at decoding time by integrating the outputs of class-conditional language models, enabling fine-grained, local adjustment of the token probabilities. Furthermore, a Transformer-based policy module trained via reinforcement learning dynamically decides when to insert additional prefixes, effectively mitigating control signal attenuation in long-text generation. Experimental results on IMDB and AG News show that, under single-attribute control, SWAP significantly outperforms baselines such as GeDi, Tailor, and Air-Decoding in terms of accuracy (Acc), perplexity (PPL), and Distinct-n diversity, while maintaining robust performance for sequences up to 512 tokens. In multi-attribute settings, SWAP further achieves higher average accuracy and lower PPL than Prefix-Tuning and Air-Decoding, demonstrating strong scalability and generalization for composite attributes. Overall, SWAP provides an efficient and effective solution for generating coherent, diverse, and strictly attribute-compliant text, with promising potential for deployment in complex, real-world CTG applications.

title: SAGE-Net: A Dual-Domain Learning Framework for High-Precision Indoor Localization and Activity Recognition
authors: Chenglin Liu, Xin Song, Siyang Xu, Jie Ren
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: With the evolution of the Internet of Things (IoT), the capability to accurately detect user position and action has become indispensable. However, robust indoor location and activity recognition remain challenging due to environmental noise and the limited representation capability of Channel State Information (CSI). To address these issues, we design SAGENet, a unified data-processing and analysis framework for highaccuracy device-free sensing. First, we construct the Autoencoderbased Dynamic Filtering (ADF) mechanism to purify the input data. The ADF implements an unsupervised strategy that effectively eliminates signal outliers based on dynamic reconstruction thresholds. We then design the Parallel Graph-Fourier Expert (P-GFE) block, which innovatively decouples feature extraction into spatial topology and spectral periodicity. These dual-domain features are harmonized by a dynamic multi-scale expert module and subsequently refined by a ResNet backbone to enable dualtask prediction. The experiments demonstrate that the proposed SAGE-Net achieves advanced performance in multiple scenarios. For indoor localization, the mean error of our proposed scheme is only 0.4163 m, which significantly outperforms state-of-the-art existing methods. For human activity recognition, it reaches an outstanding accuracy of 99.13%.

title: Research on Data Compression and Reconstruction Algorithm for Beyond-Line-of-Sight Perception of Intelligent Connected Vehicles in Vehicle-Road Cooperative Environment
authors: Jinsheng Wang, Xiang Ji, Yuanyuan Lv, Jiarong Dai, Hui Li, Rui Zheng
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Vehicle-to-everything (V2X) technology provides multi-source heterogeneous data support for beyond-line-ofsight perception of intelligent connected vehicles (ICVs), effectively overcoming the limitations of onboard sensor perception. However, massive images, LiDAR point clouds, and millimeter-wave radar data suffer from high bandwidth consumption and latency accumulation, severely impacting the real-time performance of autonomous driving decisions. Existing compression and reconstruction technologies suffer from poor multi-source data adaptability, insufficient retention of core information, and an imbalance between real-time performance and accuracy. To address these issues, this paper proposes a Multi-Source Heterogeneous Adaptive Attention Sparse Compression and Reconstruction Algorithm (MHASCR). By constructing a multi-source data mathematical representation framework and a multi-objective optimization function, it designs four core modules: adaptive heterogeneous sparse basis generation, cross-modal key information attention mechanism, bandwidth adaptive compression rate adjustment, and iterative weighted threshold reconstruction optimization, achieving a dynamic balance between compression efficiency, reconstruction accuracy, and real-time performance. Experimental results show that the algorithm performs optimally in multi-source data processing, achieving a peak signal-to-noise ratio of 38.6 dB and a structural similarity of 0.942 at an image compression ratio of 18.2. The point cloud reconstruction error is only 0.78 cm, radar data integrity is 98.7 %, the average reconstruction latency is 12.3 ms, and the key information recognition accuracy is 92.8 %. Furthermore, it can dynamically adapt to bandwidth variations of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 - 1 0 0}$</tex> Mbps, with a response time <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leq 1$</tex> frame, fully meeting the requirements of vehicle-road cooperative beyond-line-of-sight perception scenarios and providing reliable data support for autonomous driving decision-making in intelligent connected vehicles.

title: Integrating Fraud Detection into Insurance Risk Prediction via Machine Learning
authors: Hamza Errahibi, Fatima Zahra Abbour, El Mehdi Lghaouch, Chemlal Yman, Mohamed Azzouazi
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: Insurance companies face a dual challenge of accurately assessing the level of risk associated with each of their clients. At the same time, they also need to have an ability to identify instances of fraudulent activity which could undermine any future claims made by these clients and potentially jeopardise their overall financial viability. As such, traditional actuarial and rule-based approaches typically separate the challenge of assessing risk (i.e., determining whether a client is likely to present a claim) from the challenge of detecting fraud; consequently, neither method generates equitable and reliable data upon which decisions regarding the disposition of a claim can be based, nor do either of them inherently prevent discrimination (or bias) in predicting client risk. This paper proposes a new and unique framework that incorporates machine learning (ML) model(s) to take into account both methodologies as part of the same analytic pipeline. This results in both augmented predictive accuracy of risk classifications as well as significantly enhanced interpretability of the ML models themselves. Specifically, the proposed framework utilises realtime datasets provided by a variety of insurers, then jointly models the client’s risk and their fraud likelihood through a combination of ensemble learning, anomaly detection and explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) techniques. Experiments indicate that utilizing fraud features significantly enhances overall predictive accuracy (i.e., 9% improvement in risk and 18% higher recall rate for frauds) in comparison to existing ML methods. Ultimately, results produced by the proposed methodology illustrate that combining analysis of fraud with that of client risk increases the overall reliability of current insurance analytic models. Thus, offering increased transparency and neutrality for insurers’ models is based on both their risk and fraud likelihoods.

title: A Hybrid Machine Learning and Edge Computing Approaches for Landslide Detection and Early Warning Alert Systems
authors: J. Sevugan Rajesh, J.R Atchaya, V. Balusamy, I. Daniel Abishek
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Landslides have been classified as one of the most destructive and devastating forces of nature, taking away the lives of many people, damaging and destroying infrastructures, and polluting the environment. Moreover, they contribute to the long-lasting degradation of ecosystems. Effective detection and warning are the keys to mitigating the impact of landslides, but, on the other hand, existing landslide monitoring systems suffer from issues like long latency, reliance on cloud processing, and limited near real-time responsiveness. Recent research has indicated that the application of machine learning techniques along with the use of sensor networks has a huge positive impact on prediction of hazards. This project introduces a landslide detection and early warning system based on Hybrid Machine Learning and Edge Computing that uses a Raspberry Pi 3 along with an accelerometer, vibration, ultrasonic, fire, and flood sensors to continuously monitor ground and environmental conditions. The Random Forest algorithm classifies the real-time sensor data and processes it at the edge, thus making it possible to detect the hazard quickly without the need for network connectivity. The system presents real-time outputs on an LCD screen and delivers instant warning alerts through Blynk IoT to guarantee quick user awareness. The system composed of multi-sensor data fusion and edge-based machine learning, has the capabilities of providing a low-cost, scalable, and reliable solution for real-time landslide detection and multi-hazard early warning, and therefore, it's perfect for use in remote and disaster-prone areas.

title: RL-Based Optimal Low-Altitude Trajectory Tracking for UAVs with Finite-Time Weight Convergence
authors: Zekai Zhang, Xiangwang Hou, Tianyun Ding, Jingjing Wang, Jiacheng Wang, Jun Du, Xianghe Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) hold significant promise for a wide range of low-altitude applications, such as search and rescue, payload delivery, and surveillance. In such critical scenarios, achieving accurate trajectory tracking with minimal control effort and robust disturbance rejection is challenging due to system nonlinearities, external disturbances, and parameter uncertainties. This paper proposes a reinforcement learning (RL)-based optimal trajectory tracking method with disturbance rejection for UAVs to balance tracking performance and control effort. Firstly, by leveraging fundamental filtering operations and the invariant manifold principle, an unknown system dynamics estimator (USDE)-based steady-state controller is designed to ensure robust tracking performance. Second, to achieve optimization capability, an approximate optimal regulator is established by virtue of single-critic neural network to stabilize error dynamics and minimize value function. Specifically, by fully utilizing the historical data and current information, we propose a finite-time learning policy driven by neural network weight errors to approximate the Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman (HJB) solutions with faster convergence rate. Simulation results validate the effectiveness and superior performance of the proposed method.

title: Research on Intelligent Generation and Simulation Optimization of VR Scene Based on 3D Reconstruction and Deep Learning
authors: Cong He
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: At present, VR scene construction faces the core challenges such as the contradiction between realism and real-time, the difference between static and dynamic scene processing, and the traditional methods have shortcomings in the reconstruction accuracy of complex environment, the completion of occlusion areas and the efficient rendering of mobile terminals. Therefore, this study proposes an intelligent generation framework for VR scenes, which integrates 3D reconstruction and deep learning, and adopts a four-layer heterogeneous pipeline architecture: the data layer processes multi-view images or sparse point clouds through mixed data structures; The intelligent generation layer combines NeRF variant and Latent Diffusion Model (LDM), and solves the problem of occlusion completion through two-stage joint training strategy, and introduces consistency loss function to balance reconstruction accuracy and generation quality. The physical simulation layer realizes dynamic physical coupling based on LSTM prediction and NVIDIA PhysX engine to ensure that the behavior of virtual objects conforms to physical laws; The rendering layer adopts lightweight strategies such as layered rendering and texture compression to support efficient rendering of mobile 4K scenes. The experimental results show that this method performs well on ScanNet v2, industrial self-built data sets and DynamicFAUST data sets: the Chamfer Distance (CD) of 3D reconstruction is reduced to 0.51±0.09cm, the F-score is 93.7%, and the cavity repair rate is increased to 90.3%; At 4K resolution, the frame rate of complex scene rendering reaches 45fps, which is 87.5% higher than that of NeRF-only method. The success rate of physical interaction task is over 93%, and the response delay is controlled within 15ms. This research provides a low-cost and efficient VR scene generation solution for industrial design, virtual production and other fields, and promotes the depth of VR technology in practical application.

title: Edge Computing-Driven Real-Time Speech Enhancement and Command Recognition Model for Multi-Source Sensors in Coal Mines
authors: Huiming Xu, Qiangqiang Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: This paper proposes an edge computing-driven integrated model for real-time speech enhancement and command recognition in coal mines (MS-NAI-Net), adaptable to low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), multi-source interference scenarios, and edge hardware resource constraints. The core innovations of the model lie in designing multi-scale convolutional kernels to accurately capture noise features in different frequency bands, dynamically focusing on effective speech components through a spatiotemporal gating attention mechanism, reducing secondary signal distortion and processing latency through an enhancement-recognition fusion link, and achieving a lightweight design by combining depthwise separable convolutions and bottleneck structures. Experiments were conducted on a real coal mine speech dataset with over 100,000 samples in the SNR range of −10dB to 10dB, and compared with Wiener filtering, lightweight Transformer, and other algorithms for verification. Results show that MS-NAI-Net achieves a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\triangle \text{SNR}$</tex> of 12.3dB, PESQ of 3.21, and STOI of 0.89 in a typical underground SNR scenario of −5dB, with a command recognition accuracy of 98.7% and an average inference latency of 0.18s. When deployed at the edge, it consumes 85MB of memory and consumes 12W of power, all meeting the requirements of real-time performance (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leq 0.5s$</tex>) and low power consumption in underground environments. Compared with existing algorithms, it achieves synergistic optimization in performance, recognition accuracy, and deployment efficiency, providing technical support for intelligent voice communication in coal mines.

title: Cost-Benefit Structure and Investment Feasibility Analysis of Independent Energy Storage Projects
authors: Kun Wang, Hanze Zhou, Yan Shen, Hao Sun, Yuan Zhang, Gang Luo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy (ICSGE)
abstract: With the growing demand for energy storage, balancing reliability, safety, and economic performance has become a key issue in the development of storage projects. This paper conducts a full life-cycle economic analysis of electrochemical energy storage power stations, revealing the composition and dynamics of both cost and revenue structures. Using a representative independent energy storage project as a case study, the paper evaluates its investment feasibility under multiple revenue sources, including renewable energy leasing, spot market participation, frequency regulation, and capacity compensation. The results show that diversified business models can significantly improve the project’ s return on investment and enhance its commercial viability. In addition, the study identifies potential investment risks related to market volatility, battery degradation, and policy uncertainty, and proposes targeted recommendations to mitigate these factors. The research provides data-driven insights for investors and policymakers, supporting decision-making across the entire life cycle of independent energy storage projects.

title: Research on Immersive Reconstruction and Interactive Visualization System of Digital Cultural Heritage Based on AR/VR Technology
authors: Fang Lin, Zhiyue Ma
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: With the rapid development of digital technology, the protection and inheritance of cultural heritage ushered in a new opportunity. This study is devoted to building an immersive reconstruction and interactive visualization system of digital cultural heritage that integrates AR (Augmented Reality) and VR (Virtual Reality) technologies. By using 3D laser scanning, photogrammetry and other technologies to collect data, and using bilateral filtering, ICP algorithm to complete data preprocessing. In the reconstruction algorithm, AR and VR scenes are reconstructed based on feature points and deep learning respectively, and interactive visualization is achieved with the help of bone tracking and speech recognition technology. The experimental results show that the average size deviation of AR reconstruction for simple structure buildings is controlled at 2-3 cm, and that for complex structures is about 5-6 cm. The average deviation of VR reconstruction is 1-2 cm for simple structures and 3-4 cm for complex structures. In terms of interactive response time, the average time for AR gesture interaction is 0.3-0.5 seconds, and that for voice interaction is 0.5-0.7 seconds. The average VR gesture interaction is 0.2-0.4 seconds, and the voice interaction is 0.4-0.6 seconds. User experience satisfaction is high, and VR experience as a whole is slightly better. The research shows that the system has good performance in reconstruction accuracy, interactive response and user experience, and can effectively meet the needs of digital cultural heritage protection and display.

title: Deep Transfer Learning–based Multi-Class Skin Lesion Recognition and Segmentation for Clinical Decision Support
authors: Jamie G. Del Rosario, Ricardo T. Gayagoy, Ron Denzel D. Manaog, Annjela Kristy R. Nicolas, Lysa V. Comia
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Accurate and timely identification of skin lesions is critical for improving clinical outcomes in dermatological practice, particularly for conditions that exhibit subtle visual differences. This study proposes a deep transfer learning–based framework for automated multi-class skin lesion recognition and instance segmentation using the YOLOv12-seg architecture. A diverse and balanced dataset comprising 2,039 dermoscopic and clinical images across four lesion categories— Melanocytic Nevi, Vascular Lesions, Cowpox, and Dermatofibroma—was curated from multiple publicly available sources and annotated for supervised learning. The model was trained using an 87%–8%–4% train–validation–test split and evaluated using standard detection and segmentation metrics. On the held-out test set, the proposed framework achieved an overall mAP@50 of 0.816 and an mAP@50–95 of 0.514, with corresponding precision and recall values of 0.778 and 0.750, respectively. Class-wise analysis demonstrated particularly strong performance for Vascular Lesions and Melanocytic Nevi, while qualitative results confirmed accurate lesion localization and well-defined segmentation boundaries across diverse imaging conditions. The model exhibited high computational efficiency, achieving millisecond-level inference, and was successfully deployed via a Streamlit-based web application for real-time visualization and interaction. These results demonstrate the effectiveness, robustness, and practical applicability of the proposed YOLOv12-seg framework as a reliable clinical decision support tool for automated skin lesion analysis.

title: Research on Social Robot Detection Algorithms Based on Deep SC-DAE Networks
authors: Yue Tan
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Detecting social bots is one way to protect online information from the dangers of online bots. The traditional methods of detecting social bots have become less effective against new types of generative social bots because they rely mostly on manually defined feature parameters. This paper will present a model for detecting social bots that we developed using a deep spectral clustering denoising autoencoder model. This detection model uses two stages of deep learning to extract multimodal features, while creating a classifier that is highly robust and reliable. Spectral clustering constraints are applied to the derived feature representations of latent variables as part of the learning process, thereby improving both discriminative feature power and generalization ability of the model. The detection methods we presented in this paper represent a new technical approach to identifying and detecting social bots.

title: MAFR-Net: An Efficient Multi-Scale Attention Network for Flare Removal
authors: Shaofeng Liu, Jingcheng Liu, Weijie Zhang, Jian Wang, Bao Zhang, Guorong Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Lens flare represents a prevalent type of image deterioration appearing when the camera lens faces intense illumination directly, usually emerging as foggy spots, radial bands, or various optical anomalies. In real-world photography, such artifacts typically stem from one or more strong light sources. They can manifest as localized highlight streaks and frequently evolve into extensive, complex morphologies due to multi-source superposition, creating significant difficulties for current restoration algorithms to maintain a balance between local detail preservation and global context recovery, especially when dealing with intricate patterns. To resolve these obstacles, we present MAFR-Net, a streamlined framework for single-image flare removal grounded in the U-Net structure. The core component, termed the MA Block, utilizes a Floating-point Multi-scale grouped dilated Convolution (FMC) strategy to broaden the receptive field and bolster global modeling competence. The block also incorporates dual-domain attention modules (spatial and channel) to highlight degraded regions, and introduces the SimpleGate (SG) non-linearity unit to capture nonlinear feature interactions via lightweight channel-wise multiplication. These architectural components collectively enhance the model's capability to remove flare caused by single or multiple light sources. Evaluations on the Flare7K++ real-world testset indicate that MAFR-Net reaches a PSNR of 27.855 dB, surpassing existing techniques by 0.19 dB. Furthermore, its G-PSNR (24.185 dB) and S-PSNR (23.132 dB) values, which measure restoration performance in specific regions, also show improvement, validating its efficacy in handling extensive multi-source flare and localized highlight streaks.

title: CountTree: A Dataset for Tree Counting using Vision Transformers
authors: Yassin Ladham, Omar Elharrouss, Yasir Mahmood, Elarbi Badidi
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: Olive cultivation constitutes an essential agricultural sector, where quantifying olive tree populations across geographical areas holds substantial relevance for farming operations, market economics, and ecosystem preservation. This work introduces an automated framework employing artificial intelligence methodologies for enumerating olive trees captured in orbital imagery. Addressing the scarcity of appropriate training resources for neural network-based vegetation tallying, we assembled and labeled a corpus designated CountTree. For generating perimage density estimations and tree counts, we employed established neural architectures while also introducing a transformerbased methodology. Comparative assessment against baseline approaches validates our method’s effectiveness. The corpus is accessible via: CountTree.

title: A Novel Hairpin Winding Layout for High-Speed PMSM to Improve Performance and Safety in Electric Aircraft Applications
authors: Mohammad Sepehr Zamani, Aghil Ghaheri, Ebrahim Afjei
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: To make electric aircraft feasible, it is crucial to develop high-power electrical machines with high power density and efficiency while ensuring maximum safety and reliability. This paper presents a novel hairpin-winding layout for a high-speed, high-power surface-mounted permanent-magnet synchronous machine (PMSM) designed for electric aircraft propulsion. The proposed design combines the advantages of a diagonal pattern with a fractional-pitch configuration to enhance performance and reduce torque ripple, which affects propulsion safety. Finite element method (FEM) simulations demonstrate that the new winding reduces conductor weight by 5.4% and DC copper losses by 5.3%, while maintaining efficiency above 98% and meeting a 1 MW power requirement. In addition, the winding reduces the MMF amplitude of high-order harmonics, resulting in a 58.5% decrease in magnet losses, a 16% reduction in torque ripple, and an improvement in total harmonic distortion at the motor terminals. These improvements mitigate the risk of demagnetization on magnets and enhance overall reliability and safety. Although manufacturing complexity increases, the proposed winding demonstrates strong potential for next-generation electric-aircraft motors by improving efficiency, power density, performance, and operational safety.

title: Fuzzy Logic Based Severity Detection of Broken Rotor Bars Using Current and Vibration Analysis
authors: Hamidreza Aghaei, Ebrahim Nikghalb, Omid Khanzadeh
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: Broken rotor bars constitute a critical rotor fault mode in squirrel cage induction motors, whose severity quantification beyond mere detection directly informs maintenance planning and risk mitigation. A load-robust severity estimation framework is introduced, built upon motor current signature analysis while explicitly addressing operating-point dependence. First, instantaneous slip is estimated by jointly using stator-current and vibration measurements, enabling precise localization of sidebands, even under partial load where amplitudes diminish and may overlap the fundamental. Next, a windowed fast Fourier transform with leakage controlled windowing stabilizes downstream features. On these slip-aligned spectra, sideband amplitude features are extracted and normalized to operating point to form a load-independent severity index, which is mapped to actionable levels via fuzzy logic classification. Across multiple loads and 1–4 broken-bar conditions, the proposed index yields more consistent separation than a conventional amplitude baseline and improves severity classification accuracy by 18, 20.8, 3.5, and 7.8 percentage points for the four, three, two, and one broken bar cases, respectively, while slip alignment keeps the sideband-frequency error below 0.4% and windowing restores sideband visibility at low slip.

title: Comparative Analysis of Polymorphic Malware Detection Methods in Modern Cybersecurity
authors: Darshan P, P. Chinnasamy
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Detection of malware has been made more challenging as new forms of threats keep on changing over time and are no longer limited to the fixed patterns of codes. Polymorphic malware is one of such problems, that is, it changes its internal structure many times, but the malicious intent remains the same. The outcomes of this behavior are that the traditional signature-based detection methods cannot be trusted to offer good protection. To this, researchers have, over the years, come up with various detection strategies each focusing on the problem in a distinct way. Other methods are based on the analysis of program code without running it, whereas others are based on the analysis of behavior at runtime. More current solutions integrate these notions or use learning-based models to enhance the detection ability. Nevertheless, these methods differ in the effectiveness that is highly dependent on the cost of computation, scalability, and deployment limitations. This paper discusses current polymorphic malware detection techniques in terms of their advantages and limitations (as practiced) instead of focusing on their accuracy. The discussion underlines the reason behind the effectiveness of some techniques in the controlled environment and failure in the real-world environment. The study will inform the decision-making process of researchers and practitioners in modern cybersecurity systems by the trade-offs outlined.

title: Corrections to “A Class of Impulsive Eigenfunctions of Multidimensional Fourier Transform”
authors: Levent Onural
abstract: A simple mistake in a formula given in the original paper [1] resulted in errors associated with the results given in that paper; the corrections to that formula and the subsequent results are presented below. The given impulsive eigenfunctions still defined over the same manifolds as in the original paper: the coordinate axes of ${\mathbb {R}}^{N}$ are arbitrarily partitioned into two sets; each set defines a hyperplane passing through the origin and these two hyperplanes are orthogonal.

title: Real Time Perception Algorithm for Dynamic Scenes Integrating 3D Reconstruction and Visual SLAM
authors: Huimin Huang, Jiawang Liang
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: This article explores a real-time perception algorithm for dynamic scenes that integrates 3D reconstruction and visual SLAM. Visual Synchronous Localization and Mapping (VSLAM) technology uses cameras as the main sensors to collect image data, combined with multi view geometry and state estimation algorithms, to achieve mobile intelligent pose estimation and environmental map construction. However, in complex dynamic scenes, moving objects can easily interfere with feature point matching, leading to a decrease in system positioning precision. Therefore, this article proposes a deep learning (DL) network framework that integrates 3D reconstruction and VSLAM, which includes two core modules: encoder and decoder. The encoder introduces channel attention mechanism and residual convolution fusion strategy to effectively distinguish and model different semantic objects, alleviating the problem of multi-target occlusion; The decoder adopts a pixel based backprojection ray method to improve the efficiency of 3D reconstruction. The results show that this method significantly improves the precision and system stability of trajectory estimation in dynamic environments, providing new ideas for real-time perception of mobile robots in complex scenes.

title: Adaptive Heuristic Methods for Intelligent Decision Support in Electric Power Systems under Uncertainty
authors: Oksana Porubay, Mokhirakhon Khusanova, Shakhrizod Ganieva
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Science and Communications Technologies (ICISCT)
abstract: This article examines the challenge of intelligent decision-making in electric power facilities under uncertainty, employing heuristic methods. The increasing complexity of energy systems, coupled with the need for real-time responses to abnormal conditions, requires advanced control techniques beyond classical optimization. The paper presents a structured approach based on heuristic search methods, including state space graphs and AND/OR goal trees, which enables the effective decomposition and prioritization of decision-making tasks. The method incorporates expert knowledge to assign weights and estimate the relevance of subgoals, making the process adaptive and semantically meaningful. The search algorithms are illustrated using a conditional scheme of power lines and are evaluated based on their search efficiency and the optimality of the results. It is shown that the use of heuristic evaluation functions significantly reduces the size of the search tree while maintaining optimal outcomes. The approach supports the development of intelligent decision support systems (DSS) for power facilities and is applicable in real-time control under uncertainty. The proposed models are aimed at increasing system reliability and operational stability. Future work will focus on integrating this approach with AI-based forecasting and big data analysis tools to enhance dynamic decision-making in energy systems.

title: EEG2Vision: High-Fidelity Image Reconstruction from EEG via Temporal-Spectral Fusion and Cross-Modal Alignment
authors: Yan Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: Visual decoding from Electroencephalography (EEG) signals is a pivotal yet challenging task in BrainComputer Interface (BCI) research, often hindered by the low signal-to-noise ratio of EEG and the profound semantic gap between abstract brain signals and visual imagery. Existing reconstruction methods predominantly rely on temporal information, frequently overlooking crucial frequency-domain features and failing to capture fine-grained semantic details. To address these limitations, we propose EEG2Vision, a novel framework for high-fidelity image reconstruction. Our pipeline features a dual-branch encoder that integrates robust temporal features (extracted via an MAE-pre-trained ViT) with complementary frequency features (extracted via an FFTDeepCNN) to form a unified EEG representation. To enforce semantic consistency, we introduce a cross-modal alignment module that bridges EEG features with visual semantics using a FusionCLIP-Transformer, leveraging captions generated by BLIP to guide the alignment in the CLIP latent space. The aligned representations are then injected into a StyleGAN-ADA generator for conditional synthesis. Extensive experiments on the EEGCVPR40 dataset demonstrate that EEG2Vision significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieving an Inception Score (IS) of 13.64 and a Fréchet Inception Distance (FID) of 168.29. These results confirm that our approach effectively bridges the modality gap, delivering unprecedented quality and semantic correctness in EEG-to-Image reconstruction.
