title:
Characterizing GNSS Signal Behavior Under Jamming and Spoofing using Doppler, Pseudorange, and C/N
                    <sub>0</sub>
                    Metrics
authors: Bikash Kandel, Nishchal Acharya
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Science and Communications Technologies (ICISCT)
abstract: This research examines the robustness of Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) signals in clean, jammed, and spoofed environments by evaluating three critical parameters: carrier-to-noise ratio ($\mathrm{C} / \mathrm{N}_{0}$), Doppler shift, and pseudorange. According to the results, clean signals maintain the greatest $\mathrm{C} / \mathrm{N}_{0}$ (about $45 \mathrm{~dB}-\mathrm{Hz}$) with good stability, whereas spoofing results in mild disruptions (around $\mathrm{4 0 ~ d B - H z}$) with indications of manipulation and jamming causes severe degradation ($\mathrm{2 5 - 3 0 ~ d B}$-Hz) and substantial variability. According to Doppler analysis, spoofing provides implausible frequency deviations (up to $\mathrm{5 0 ~ H z}$), which are a clear indicator of fake signals, jamming produces near-static or slightly distorted shifts, and clean signals match genuine satellite dynamics (roughly 0 Hz). Pseudorange is mostly stable under all circumstances, however spoofing produces controllable biases (ranging from +3 to 4 m) that distort placement, and jamming introduces bigger inaccuracies (around +10 m). A closer look at synchronous and delay spoofing reveals different behaviours: synchronous spoofing creates anomalous Doppler variations and falsely raises $\mathrm{C} / \mathrm{N}_{0}$, while delay spoofing slightly weakens signals without significantly affecting Doppler. Overall, the results show that $\mathrm{C} / \mathrm{N}_{0}$ and Doppler offer strong discriminators for identifying and characterising GNSS jamming and spoofing, supporting the development of more robust satellite-based navigation systems, while pseudorange alone is less effective for interference detection.

title: Material Detection Algorithm Based on Image Recognition in Intelligent Workshop AI Control Platform
authors: Fanggang Niu, Yin Gong, Yong Xia, Wei Hu, Yan Zhou, Kaiwen Xue, Zhengquan Xie
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: This article studies the material detection algorithm based on image recognition in the intelligent workshop AI control platform, aiming to improve the automation and intelligence level of the production process. The dense stacking of materials in industrial scenarios leads to severe feature overlap, coupled with harsh working conditions such as changes in lighting and dust interference. Traditional detection methods are difficult to meet the requirements of high-precision counting and real-time recognition. Therefore, this article proposes a lightweight material detection algorithm based on deep learning (DL). This algorithm is based on the Mask-RCNN (Mask Region Convolutional Neural Network) framework and introduces a dehazing preprocessing module to enhance image quality. It uses MobileNetV2 combined with Feature Pyramid Network (FPN) as the backbone network, which significantly reduces the number of model parameters and improves inference efficiency while ensuring feature extraction capability. Through lightweight design, the model can be efficiently deployed on the AI management and control platform in the edge computing environment, and achieve accurate instance segmentation and material positioning of real-time collected images. The results show that the algorithm can still maintain high detection precision and robustness in complex industrial environments, significantly improving the precision of material recognition and counting.

title: Lightweight YOLOv11 Improved Algorithm and Real-Time Detection Research for VisDrone LowAltitude Surveillance Scenarios
authors: Rongyi Zhang, Shenfan Chen
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: Low-altitude UAV surveillance is constrained by limited onboard computing resources, large object scale variations, and complex background interference, which pose significant challenges to real-time object detection. To address these issues, this paper presents a lightweight improved YOLOv11 framework. Specifically, the original backbone is replaced with ShuffleNetV2 to substantially reduce model size and computational cost through efficient channel shuffling and pointwise group convolution, while preserving effective feature extraction capability. Furthermore, anchor boxes are re-generated according to the scale distribution of the VisDrone dataset, and an adaptive feature fusion module is incorporated to strengthen multi-scale representation. Experiments conducted on the VisDrone2021 dataset demonstrate that the proposed model reduces the parameter count to $38.2 \%$ of the original YOLOv11 and decreases computational complexity by $45.7 \%$. On the NVIDIA Jetson Xavier NX edge device, the inference speed reaches 32.6 FPS, while the detection accuracy remains competitive with an $\mathrm{mAP} \text{@} 0.5$ of $58.3 \%$, only 2.1 percentage points lower than the baseline. These results indicate that the proposed approach achieves a favorable trade-off between efficiency and accuracy, making it suitable for real-time deployment in low-altitude surveillance applications.

title: Design of a Magnetic Coupler with Low Mutual Inductance Fluctuations Based on Dynamic Wireless Power Transfer
authors: Xin Gao, Shijie Cong, Shaoshuan Qi, Yonghao Zhu, Shuya Chen, Yan Yan, Jiantao Zhang, Chunbo Zhu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: Dynamic wireless power transfer (DWPT) breaks the dependence of power supply on fixed locations and physical connections. It enables continuous and stable energy replenishment during equipment movement and holds great promise. Traditional magnetic coupler suffer from large mutual inductance fluctuations. To solve this issue, this paper proposes a novel magnetic coupler. Based on a single-pole long coil guide rail type, an overlapping design is employed to suppress mutual inductance fluctuations in transmitter. A single-layer DDQ structure is designed to compensate for mutual inductance drops when the Q coil is misaligned. Furthermore, finite element analysis (FEA) is performed on the parameters of the magnetic coupler, elucidating the influence of parameters on mutual inductance fluctuations, providing a technical reference for the further development of DWPT for electric vehicles (EV).

title: Human Action Recognition Using mmWave Radar with Velocity-Aware Graph Networks
authors: Yuquan Luo, Yaxin Li, Changshun Yuan, Jun Wang, Song Liang, Zihao Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: This paper presents a human action recognition framework based solely on millimeter-wave (mmWave) radar, addressing the inherent sparsity and noise of radar point clouds. We introduce a 4D skeleton representation that augments 3D joint coordinates with per-joint velocity cues. This representation is processed by a spatio-temporal graph convolutional network (ST-GCN) enhanced with a velocity-aware attention mechanism to improve robustness to radar-induced skeletal instability. We capture human motions using a custom mmWave radar and convert them into 3D skeleton sequences for recognition. Evaluations on a ten-action dataset show that the proposed method achieves an accuracy of 97.84% and a macro F 1 -score of 97.45%. The results indicate consistent cross-subject generalization and stable performance under environmental variations.

title: Anomaly Prediction and Root Cause Analysis in Power Systems based on Spatio-Temporal Graph Neural Networks
authors: Guanxiong Ma, Chan Wan, Xuan Luo, Shuang Zhao, Churan Deng, Hui Pan, Li Zhang, Jinjia Shi, Peitao Gao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy (ICSGE)
abstract: The secure and stable operation of modern power grids is heavily reliant on the continuous monitoring of a multitude of key performance indicators (KPIs) across various components. Timely prediction of anomalies and subsequent diagnosis of their root causes are crucial for preventing cascading failures. However, the complex spatio-temporal dependencies within power system data pose significant challenges for traditional methods. This paper proposes a novel framework based on Spatio-Temporal Graph Neural Networks (STGNNs) for simultaneous anomaly prediction and root cause analysis. We model the power grid as a graph, where nodes represent physical or logical entities (e.g., buses, generators, substations) and edges represent their connectivity or functional relationships. Node attributes are the multivariate time series of KPIs. The proposed STGNN model leverages graph convolutional networks (GCNs) to capture spatial correlations and recurrent neural networks (RNNs) or temporal convolutional networks (TCNs) to model temporal dynamics. By learning these joint dependencies, the model can effectively predict future system states and flag impending anomalies. Furthermore, we introduce a attention mechanism within the STGNN to quantify the contribution of each node and edge to the predicted anomaly, thereby facilitating interpretable root cause analysis. Experiments on a real-world dataset and a synthetic dataset simulating common fault scenarios demonstrate that our framework not only achieves superior anomaly prediction performance compared to baseline methods (e.g., LSTM, GRU, pure TCN) but also provides insightful and accurate root cause identification.

title: Design and Implementation of Intelligent Power Outage Pre-Scheduling System for Improving Power Supply Reliability
authors: Shuo Tan
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: At present, there are many problems in pre-scheduled outage management, such as multi-objective conflict, lagging dynamic response, and insufficient data value. Moreover, the existing system is static, localized and relies on "black box" algorithm. Therefore, an intelligent power outage pre-scheduling system is designed in this study, which adopts a three-layer dynamic closed-loop architecture of "perception-decision-execution", with digital twinning and real-time optimization as the core. The data sensing layer integrates multi-source data to construct a coupling model, and realizes the accurate evaluation of equipment real-time failure rate through LSTM; Intelligent decision layer integrates deep reinforcement learning (DRL) and multi-objective optimization (MOO) engines to generate Pareto optimal outage plan set; The feedback layer is implemented to form closed-loop learning and continuous optimization. In terms of key algorithms and models, a quantitative model of user needs is constructed to transform user sensitivity into quantitative indicators; MOO model based on NSGA-III is designed to realize multi-objective collaborative optimization, and DRL module is introduced as a dynamic adjustment mechanism to form a hybrid optimization architecture of offline planning and online adjustment. Based on the data of Meizhou Power Supply Bureau, the experiment verifies the effectiveness of the system by comparing with the traditional manual mode and business optimization software. In the scene of sudden change of new energy output, the system adjustment delay is only 48s, and the fault risk prediction error is reduced to 8.2%; On the 7th, the comprehensive index showed that its SAIFI (System Average Interruption Frequency Index) was 0.61 times/household, the comprehensive cost was 2.93 million yuan, and the compliance rate of high-priority users was 96%, all of which were better than the comparison scheme and could effectively meet the differentiated needs of different users. The system breaks through the limitation of traditional mode, improves the reliability of power supply, reduces the cost, improves the satisfaction of users, and provides an effective solution for the power outage management of complex power systems.

title: Research on Intelligent Modeling and Predictive Control Method of Force Behavior of Transmission Tower Based on Reinforcement Learning
authors: Wenbin Li, Yanting Feng, Yong Wang, Yadong Lv, Xiaokang Li, Qizhi Chen
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: The transmission tower has been exposed to complex natural environment for a long time, and it faces many challenges. The traditional analysis method has limitations. As a learning method based on trial-and-error mechanism, reinforcement learning has shown good results in optimal control of power system. In this paper, an intelligent modeling and predictive control method with deep reinforcement learning (DRL) is constructed, which solves the core problems such as abstracting the stress behavior of the tower into markov decision processes (MDP), designing the reward function and improving the online learning ability. In the research method, multi-source data are obtained through data collection and preprocessing, an intelligent modeling framework is constructed, state space, action space and reward function are designed, and a predictive control strategy based on proximal policy optimization (PPO) is adopted, and a multi-stage online learning mechanism is introduced. The experimental results show that this method is superior to the traditional method in dynamic response prediction accuracy, control performance and safety margin under various extreme load conditions, and has good migration learning ability, which can effectively cope with new disasters, provide a new intelligent control scheme for the safe and stable operation of transmission towers, and promote the application of AI in the operation and maintenance of power infrastructure.

title: Scenario Analysis of Wind and Solar Power Development for Power-Sector Decarbonization in Guangxi
authors: Yongliang Luo, Xuwen Zheng, Min E, Xianli Yang, Buncha Wattana
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: Achieving carbon neutrality has become a strategic priority for global climate governance. As a major energy consuming region in southwest China, Guangxi faces increasing pressure to decarbonize its power sector while maintaining energy security. Existing studies on regional power sector decarbonization mainly focus on economically developed eastern regions, with limited quantitative analysis of wind and solar power development potential and emission reduction pathways for energy intensive provinces in southwest China such as Guangxi. This study applies the Low Emissions Analysis Platform model to simulate the evolution of Guangxi power generation structure and carbon dioxide emissions from 2022 to 2060 under three scenarios, including a Baseline Scenario, a Policy Support Scenario, and a Deep Emission Reduction Scenario. This study is the first to construct a long term evolution pathway for Guangxi power system over the period from 2022 to 2060 using the LEAP model, and to quantify the threshold effect of wind and solar power replacing coal fired generation as well as the emission reduction elasticity under different policy intensities. The results show that under the Baseline Scenario, coal fired power remains an important component of electricity supply, accounting for approximately twenty three percent of total generation by 2060.In contrast, under the Policy Support Scenario and the Deep Emission Reduction Scenario, wind power and solar photovoltaic generation become dominant, jointly providing more than seventy six percent and eighty two percent of total electricity generation, respectively. Compared with the Baseline Scenario, carbon dioxide emissions in 2060 are reduced by approximately seventeen percent under the Policy Support Scenario and by more than thirty percent under the Deep Emission Reduction Scenario. These results demonstrate that strong policy support and large scale deployment of renewable energy are critical for achieving deep decarbonization of the power sector in Guangxi, and provide quantitative policy insights for power system transformation in southwest China as well as empirical references for large scale grid integration of wind and solar energy in regions with similar resource endowments.

title: Surface Potential based Numerical Model For PMOSFET Considering QM Effects
authors: K. V. Jyothi Prakash
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: A numerical model for calculating the surface potential of pMOSFET considering quantum mechanical (QM) effects in both inversion and accumulation region is developed by using triangular potential well approximation. The surface potential obtained from the proposed model is continuous from accumulation to strong inversion region and the model is validated by comparing the results with published data. The surface potential in accumulation region and charge density in inversion region obtained from the proposed model are in good comparison with the published result obtained by solving Schrodinger and Poisson equation self consistently. The validity of model is also verified

title: IoT-Enabled Aquaponics for Advancing Sustainable Agriculture
authors: Y Ambika Naik, Silpa Ajith Kumar, S. Hema Priyadarshini
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: Aquaponics present a practical solution to environmental challenges while targeting sustainable food production. Substantial progress has been made in hydroponics and traditional aquaculture through Internet of Things (IoT) technologies. The proposed work explores modern IoT techniques emphasizing the potential to enhance sustainable farming. The proposed technique examines the role of IoT in improvising the system efficiency and optimizes resource use by leveraging suitable sensors along with Arduino Mega to monitor turbidity (25 NTU - 950 NTU),pH(7-10), water level (2 meter), light intensity (6-930), servo motor(on/off) to feed the fish and temperature (25°C-30°C) inside the setup. A mobile app was developed using FIGMA to read the data from the aquaponics setup and display, record on hourly basis for a period of 1 month. Firebase was utilized for real time database management of the sensors. Additionally, it highlights future research opportunities to advance IoT-based aquaponics, considering challenges such as data security, energy consumption, and system complexity.

title: RMD-MobileNet: A Lightweight Residual Attention Network for Cattle Facial Recognition
authors: Zhiqiang Zheng, Chen Meng, Caili Gong, Zhi Weng
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: In the modernization of smart livestock management, efficient and precise cattle facial recognition technology has become a crucial enabler for enhancing management efficiency. To address the shortcomings of existing algorithms in balancing model complexity and recognition accuracy, this paper proposes a lightweight bovine facial recognition method: RMD-MobileNet.This approach enhances the model’s representation of key facial biometric features through a RA residual attention structure with channel-adaptive weighting mechanisms. It introduces a Multi-Scale Ghost convolution module that employs sparse feature generation strategies to effectively reduce computational complexity. Finally, The DepGraph structured pruning algorithm is introduced to systematically eliminate redundant channels and hierarchical dependencies within the network. This approach effectively maintains cow face recognition performance while further reducing the model’s parameter size and computational overhead.The dataset comprises 13,019 facial images collected from 308 cattle in real-world ranching environments. Experimental results demonstrate that RMD-MobileNet achieves a recognition accuracy of 95.48%, with model parameters compressed from 1.83 million to 1.10 million. Compared to mainstream models like ShuffleNetV2 and EfficientNet, RMD-MobileNet demonstrates significant advantages in balancing accuracy and computational efficiency. The model achieves 40.4 frames per second on the Jetson Nano embedded platform, enabling real-time application for cattle facial recognition.

title: Artificial intelligence Enhanced Chaotic Encryption vs Blockchain Frameworks for Embedded Medical Imaging Security
authors: Imane Arioua, Houssam Bouardi, Said Hraoui, Hakim Fadili
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: Advances in telemedicine and the Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) have led to the decentralization of medical imaging systems, exposing sensitive patient data to increasing security risks, while also considering the hardware constraints of embedded devices and a strict regulatory framework. This work is a systematic comparative study of two emerging security paradigms: chaotic encryption enhanced by artificial intelligence and blockchain-based access control mechanisms, deployed in embedded healthcare environments. Four evaluation criteria were used: cryptographic robustness, computational load, scalability, and compliance with current standards. The results, obtained from real medical imaging datasets, reveal significant trade-offs. AI-assisted chaotic encryption hybrid approaches demonstrate remarkable real-time privacy efficiency, with informational entropy exceeding 7.997, correlation coefficients close to 0.001, and an average encryption time of 0.1 seconds for standardsized images. Simultaneously, blockchain ensures decentralized trust and irrefutable traceability, with a throughput of up to 109,248 transactions per second and an access latency of less than 500 ms. In-depth statistical analysis validates blockchain’s superiority for auditing and regulatory compliance, thanks to its immutable ledgers, while chaotic methods guarantee robust pixel protection, with alteration rates exceeding $99.6 \%$. Ultimately, this study identifies optimal hybrid architectures that reconcile these conflicting imperatives, paving the way for secure, efficient, and compliant distributed medical imaging systems.

title: A Data Middle Platform-Based Power Grid Data Security Monitoring System
authors: Shengguo Xu, Liang Guo, Fucun Zhang, Hao Hong
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: With the deep integration of big data technology into critical sectors such as power grids, finance, and healthcare, the data environment has become increasingly complex, and security compliance requirements continue to rise. Traditional data security components suffer from issues like relative isolation and insufficient correlation analysis, primarily focusing on static statistical analysis and security auditing, while lacking real-time analysis and alerting capabilities for anomalous behaviors. This paper proposes a data mid-end-based data security monitoring method and system. By integrating multi-source data such as Data Protection Umbrella operation logs, SDDP interface data, desensitization logs, Alibaba Cloud platform login logs, and ODPS operation logs, and leveraging the real-time computing capabilities of the data mid-end and the storage capacity of the MaxCompute data warehouse, the system implements processes including data analysis, data computation, alert storage, and data storage to achieve rapid analysis and alert generation. To address the ambiguity of anomalous behavior definition and detection logic, the system combines rule-based policies, statistical threshold models, and lightweight machine learning algorithms to achieve adaptive threat detection; meanwhile, it establishes unified entity resolution mechanisms for cross-source event correlation and deploys full-link security protection for monitoring pipelines to avoid becoming new attack surfaces. Additionally, the architecture is designed with vendor-agnostic compatibility to support hybrid and multi-cloud scenarios, and a comparative analysis with mainstream SIEM and cloud-native security tools is supplemented to verify its performance advantages. Experimental results demonstrate that the system effectively addresses the insufficient correlation analysis among security components, enables real-time monitoring and early warning of anomalous behaviors, and significantly enhances data security protection capabilities.

title: Optimization of Switched Reluctance Motor (SRM) Drive for Torque Ripple Mitigation and Cost Reduction in Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle (PHEV)
authors: Sreeram K, Christy Mary Jacob, Simi Cyril, Axsah Rachel Punnen, Jeslin James
abstract: The rising demand for efficient, reliable, and cost-effective electric powertrains has significantly increased interest in Switched Reluctance Motors (SRMs) for Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle (PHEV) applications. Unlike traditional permanent magnet motors, SRMs operate without rare-earth materials, thereby reducing overall motor cost and improving environmental sustainability. Additionally, their robust and simple construction, along with excellent fault tolerance, makes SRMs well-suited for automotive environments. However, despite these advantages, conventional SRM configurations—particularly low-phase variants-are challenged by high torque ripple and increased acoustic noise. This project presents a comprehensive simulation-based analysis of a six-phase SRM, with the objective of mitigating torque ripple for PHEV applications. As part of this study, 3-phase, 4-phase, and 6-phase SRM configurations are comparatively analyzed to assess their relative performance in terms of torque generation and torque ripple suppression. The simulation results reveal that the 6-phase SRM significantly outperforms its lower-phase counterparts by offering smoother torque profiles and better dynamic response. Moreover, the increased number of phases in the 6-phase design provides inherent fault-tolerant capability, ensuring continued operation even in the event of phase failure-an essential feature for enhancing the reliability of PHEVs. This research provides comprehensive framework to assess multi-phase SRMs, focusing on their benefits of cost, reliability, and dynamics, compared to other works that assess single configurations or control techniques.

title: Research And Development Of Satellite-Mounted 30cm Aperture Optical Antenna System For Cislunar Optical Communication Systems In Japan
authors: Yoshihiko Saito, Hideaki Kotake, Shota Kimura, Katsumi Makino, Johta Awano, Morio Toyoshima
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: JAXA and NICT jointly started the research and development for cislunar deep space optical communications in FY2021. This research aims at 2.5 Gbit/s high-speed optical communication from the Moon to the Earth. In this research, we require a lightweight, rigid, and radiation-resistant optical antenna to achieve high-speed communication. At the same time, it is necessary to implement high-sensitivity optical communication system comprising an adaptive optics to compensate for the effects of thermal distortion of the antenna optics and high-sensitivity optical receiver. NICT initiated a conceptual design of a large-aperture optical antenna and an adaptive optics in FY2021. In order to achieve a compact and lightweight optical antenna system, the large aperture antenna and the adaptive optics are integrated into a single unit. We are also conducting research and development of the adaptive optics system with the aim of creating a control system that can be mounted on an FPGA. In our previous work, we reported the preliminary design results of optical components for cislunar optical communication system [1]. In this paper, we present the detailed design results that is necessary to start production of prototype-based optical antenna in FY2024 and to progress the study for the adaptive optics system.

title: Real-time Estimation of Comprehensive Inertia in Power System Subject to Severe Disturbance
authors: Haifeng Wang, Junyu Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy (ICSGE)
abstract: In new power systems, there exists an inertia sudden change phenomenon, where changes in inertia can severely impact the frequency stability of the power system. However, existing theories lack sufficient research in this area. This paper combines the Unscented Kalman Filter (UKF) with a dynamic model of the power system to propose an improved UKF algorithm based on real-time measurement data. The algorithm achieves inertia mutation detection by utilizing the modulus of the innovation sequence from the UKF. Additionally, it adaptively modifies the state covariance matrix of the algorithm according to the impact duration of the inertia mutation, thereby adjusting the estimation accuracy. This enables rapid and precise assessment of the system's comprehensive inertia under transient conditions. Finally, the accuracy of the proposed method is validated through simulation analysis.

title: Recent Progress on Developing Wearable Textile-Based Telyutenna Using Coating Technique
authors: Muhammad Farhan Maulana, Fakhruddin Ahmad Nasution, Rheyuniarto Sahlendar Asthan, Novelita Rahayu, Edwar, Achmad Munir
publish: 2026 Symposium on Microwave, Antenna, and Propagation (SMAP)
abstract: This study deals with the design of a wearable microstrip antenna developed using cotton textile as the substrate and conductive paint coating as the radiating and groundplane elements. The radiating element is uniquely shaped to represent the Telkom University logo, providing both functional wireless performance and aesthetic value for wearable accessories. The proposed Telyutenna is realized by applying a conductive coating technique onto a cotton fabric. Electromagnetic simulation and experimental measurement are performed to assess and evaluate key performance indicators. Simulation and measurement result indicate that the antenna resonates at the center frequency of 5.8 GHz. The results demonstrate that the realized textile-based Telyutenna works at the targeted frequency, confirming its implementation for wearable applications.

title: SP-SegFormer: A Retinal Segmentation Algorithm Based on SegFormer
authors: Shaoxuan Xue, Ruixi Ding
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Diagnosing diabetic retinopathy relies on manual assessment, resulting in limited resources and missed cases. Currently, most algorithms are prone to introducing noise interference when capturing long-range dependencies, making accurate segmentation difficult. Based on these issues, this paper proposes a new algorithm, SP-SegFormer, which inserts an SPBlock between the backbone part of SegFormer and the MLP decoder, using a strip pooling module to capture long-range dependencies, avoiding the introduction of noise interference from other directions. Experiments were conducted on the DRIVE dataset, resulting in a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 2 1 \%}$</tex> increase in mIoU, capture long-range dependencies while avoiding noise interference from other directions. Experiments were conducted on the DRIVE dataset, resulting in improvements of 0.21% in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{mIoU}, 0.08 \%$</tex> in Acc, 0.32% in Kappa, and 0.16% in Dice. The experimental results demonstrate that the improved SP-SegFormer algorithm yields superior performance in retinal vessel segmentation.

title: A Hybrid Framework Combining Heuristic Search and Reinforcement Learning for Multi-Objective Cold Chain Vehicle Path Optimization
authors: Yang Yang, Feifan Yan
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: In the modern logistics system, the multi-objective cold chain vehicle routing problem has attracted much attention due to its key role in ensuring food and drug safety, reducing operating costs, and responding to global sustainable development initiatives. This problem is essentially an NP hard combinatorial optimization problem, whose complexity is further exacerbated by the need to simultaneously balance multiple conflicting objectives such as transportation costs, temperature control costs, customer time window penalties, and carbon emissions. Although traditional heuristic algorithms are effective in specific scenarios, they often lack adaptability to dynamic environments; However, emerging pure deep reinforcement learning methods face challenges of low sample efficiency and insufficient generalization ability when dealing with complex constraints and large-scale instances. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an innovative hybrid reinforcement learning vehicle routing problem (HRL-VRP) framework. The core of this framework is an intelligent agent based on deep Q-networks, which learns the optimal strategy of a dynamic selection and scheduling heuristic operator through interaction with the optimization environment. These operators constitute a feature rich action space, covering genetic algorithm operators for global exploration, optimized A * search operators for local refinement, and temperature geographic dual dimensional clustering operators for problem decomposition. Through extensive experimental verification in the cold chain distribution scenario in the suburbs of Shanghai, the HRL-VRP framework has demonstrated excellent performance. The main contribution of this study is the proposal and validation of an adaptive optimization paradigm that can effectively integrate classical operations research algorithms with modern reinforcement learning techniques.

title: A Lightweight Blockchain-Integrated Mutual Authentication Scheme for Secure IoT Communications
authors: Akilan Selvaraj Saroja, Subair Ali Liayakath Ali Khan, Kayathri Devi Devprasad
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: A secure and lightweight mutual authentication protocol is vital for Wireless Body Area Networks (WBANs), which continuously transmit medical sensor data to healthcare platforms. To address this need, we propose a blockchain-assisted authentication framework that adopts a dual-protocol approach, distinguishing between emergency and routine health messages. The architecture consists of a Central Manager (CM), Sensor Device (S), Relay Node (R), and Local Gateway (L), enabling efficient and reliable mutual verification between the sensor and gateway via the relay. This is achieved through cryptographic mechanisms such as random nonces, keyed hash functions, XOR-based masking, and session key derivation. A private blockchain is employed to securely record authentication metadata, ensuring integrity, traceability, and resistance to tampering. Formal reasoning and security analysis validate the protocol’s robustness against replay, man-in-the-middle, and device compromise attacks. Furthermore, experimental evaluation highlights a significant reduction in computational overhead and communication cost compared to existing schemes, making the solution highly suitable for resource-constrained, real-time healthcare environments.

title: Research on 3D Engine Construction Technology Based on Panoramic Image and Laser Point Cloud Fusion
authors: Lezhen Zhang, Shijin Xin, Jianyong Gao, Chengjia Bao, Zicheng Deng
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: Research and solve the limitations of traditional 3D reconstruction method with single data source in geometric structure and texture information, as well as the challenges faced by multi-sensor fusion technology in practical application, such as heterogeneous data alignment, dynamic interference suppression and real-time bottleneck. In this paper, a hybrid registration method integrating semantics and geometry is proposed. The semantic features of point cloud are extracted by PointNet++ and matched with the 2D semantic tags of images for cross-modal matching, which improves the registration accuracy in dynamic scenes. At the same time, a layered rendering pipeline architecture is designed, which uses voxel grid to compress point cloud and ambient light shading map to encode texture, and realizes GPU parallel acceleration based on Vulkan API to meet the real-time rendering requirements of large-scale 3D scenes. The experimental results show that the registration error of this method is significantly better than the traditional ICP and NDT algorithms in a variety of complex scenes, and it shows higher stability and resource efficiency in large-scale point cloud rendering, and the dynamic interference suppression effect is also very significant, which effectively improves the quality and visual effect of the reconstructed scene, and provides strong support for the application of 3D scene reconstruction technology in smart cities, natural resources investigation, cultural heritage protection and other fields.

title: Smart LED Lighting Architecture: High Functional Integration with Cost-Effective Design
authors: Arunav Sharma, Srikumar Aravinda Krishnan, Aarthi Arumugam, Jeevitha Krishnan, Vairamuthu. S
abstract: The demand for smart lighting systems in automotive and industrial applications is rapidly increasing, driven by the need for enhanced safety, user convenience, and energy efficiency. However, integrating such advanced functionalities into cost-sensitive, high-volume platforms remains a significant engineering challenge. Existing solutions often rely on basic linear current regulation, which lacks robustness against voltage fluctuations, or adopt sophisticated driver architectures that are cost-prohibitive and hardwareintensive. This paper presents a novel system-level approach that combines established power regulation techniques with innovative integration strategies to deliver a high-functionality, cost-effective LED lighting system. The proposed architecture supports advanced features such as smartphone-enabled vehicle localization in key-off condition, panic braking indication through dynamic turn signal illumination, and automatic turn signal cutoff-all implemented without additional control hardware. These features are seamlessly integrated using the vehicle's existing microcontroller and electrical infrastructure. The key innovation lies not in the individual components, but in the holistic integration of power electronics, embedded control, and reliability engineering to achieve a commercially viable solution. The final implementation achieves a 50% cost reduction compared to conventional lighting systems providing similar features, while meeting stringent automotive reliability and performance standards. This work demonstrates how thoughtful system-level design can enable feature-rich, intelligent lighting in cost-constrained applications.

title: Cancelable Facial Template Protection via Deep Hashing with AdaFace and MobileNetV3-Large
authors: Yuanxin Zhou, Jinyuan Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Cancelable biometric template protection aims to secure biometric data by transforming it into a revocable, noninvertible form. This paper proposes a deep hashing approach for facial template protection using a lightweight MobileNetV3-Large backbone and the AdaFace loss function. Discriminative facial features are extracted with MobileNetV3Large, and AdaFace is used for training to improve robustness to quality variations. The real-valued embeddings are then binarized to form a cancelable facial template - compact binary codes that preserve identity information for matching while preventing recovery of the original facial image. Template revocability is ensured by incorporating user-specific transformations, and irreversibility is guaranteed by one-way hashing of features. Experiments on face recognition benchmarks demonstrate that the method maintains high recognition accuracy, achieving 2.05% EER and TARs of 96.81% <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$/ 94.92 {\%}$</tex> at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{FAR}=10^{-3} / 10^{-4}$</tex> on LFW with 256-bit protected hashes, close to the floating-point baseline of 1.87% EER. The proposed method thus offers a secure and privacy-preserving face recognition system with minimal performance degradation.

title: Safe Powernet for futuristic Electrical Vehicles
authors: Dileep K. Bhoi, Hamsa Nanjundappa
abstract: A vehicle's powernet is an integrated system that distributes power from its source to various components, including electrical loads, sensors, actuators, and electronic control units (ECUs), all connected by a physical conductive media. Communication among these components relies on standard protocols like CAN, FlexRay, Ethernet etc… The powernet's complexity is growing every passing year due to the increasing number of electrical/electronic components included in the vehicle, resulting in longer wiring harness and significantly more complex system, software and hardware designs. Recent vehicle recall data reveals that a significant portion of reported failures originate in electrical/electronic systems or the powernet. This evidence compels OEMs and Tier-1 suppliers to prioritize powernet redesign to ensure safety and robustness, while managing the increased complexity of these systems. For a considerable time, vehicle powernets operated at 12 V, powered by conventional fossil fuel engines. The increasing demand for electrical energy in vehicles led to the development of advanced alternator-generator systems. A typical mid-size vehicle contains a wiring harness that can extend up to 5 kilometers in length and weigh approximately 80 kilograms. As vehicles become more feature-rich, the energy demands have reached a tipping point, exceeding the capabilities of a 12 V battery system. This has created a need to upgrade powernets to 48 V or higher. This paper aims to evaluate the need for, and feasibility of, a safety mechanism that ensures adequate energy supply while reducing the complexity and size of the wiring harness.

title: Energy-Aware Topology Reconfiguration using Bio-Inspired Algorithms in Large-Scale Heterogeneous Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: Vignesh M, Rubesh V, Archana T, Hema MaliniA, Kalaivani C T
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Large-scale heterogeneous Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) face significant challenges in maintaining energy efficiency with non-uniform consumption of node energy and varying network conditions. Topology changes frequently lead to a decreased network lifetime and decreased communication performance. This work focuses on addressing the problem of minimizing topology reconfiguration to conserve energy while maintaining data transmission reliability. An energy-aware framework of topology reconfiguration based on bio-inspired algorithms such as Ant Colony Optimization (ACO) and Genetic Algorithm (GA) is proposed. Optimal node connectivity, balanced consumption of energies, and increased network lifetime are to be achieved with adaptive and intelligent decision-making. Network topology is adaptively changed dynamically based on parameters such as link and residual energy quality. The novelty in this work lies in combining multiple optimization strategies based on bio-inspired approaches to ensure real-time, scalable, and energy-aware management of heterogeneous WSNs topology, and hence outperform traditional static and heuristic approaches in adaptability and conserving energy.

title: A Multi-Agent Batch Learning Method Based on Offline Reinforcement Learning
authors: Qian Sun
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Single-agent reinforcement learning has achieved remarkable results in fields such as gaming and robotic control. However, due to the dynamic nature of agents, non-stationary environments, and limited data acquisition, traditional online reinforcement learning in multi-agent systems (MAS) faces challenges such as inefficient data acquisition and training deadlocks. While offline reinforcement learning can address this dilemma, existing multi-agent offline methods suffer from shortcomings such as poor policy coordination, homogeneous data utilization, and difficulty adapting to high-dimensional environments. This paper proposes a multi-agent batch learning algorithm (MA-OBLO) that optimizes performance through modules such as dynamic weighted collaborative policy updates and attention-based data filtering. Experiments show that in the collaborative transport task, MA-OBLO achieved a 92.3% success rate with 30% expert samples, representing improvements of 23.8, 11.1, and 4.7 percentage points over ILCQL, CTDE-BCQ, and MA-CQL, respectively. SMAC achieved a 68.2% win rate on the most challenging map, outperforming the competing algorithms. During peak hours, the average waiting time for intelligent transportation was 28.3 seconds, and the congestion rate was 12.5%, representing improvements of 12.9% and 18.3% over MA-CQL. Furthermore, it achieved stable performance in the fewest rounds, requiring only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1, 2 0 0}$</tex> rounds for collaborative transport, demonstrating minimal performance fluctuations, demonstrating its comprehensive advantages.

title: Artifact-Perception CLIP: Lightweight LoRA-Tuned Generative Image Detection
authors: Zhi Du, Hongjun Ren, Haiyong Chen, Yangjie Cao
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: In response to widespread concerns about synthetic media and misinformation, this paper proposes a lightweight generative fake image detection algorithm designed for edge deployment. We introduce an Artifact Perception Module (APM) that utilizes Laplacian high-pass filtering to extract high-frequency artifacts, fusing them with RGB information to capture shallow texture anomalies often missed by deep semantic networks. Furthermore, to adapt large-scale pre-trained models to resource-constrained environments, we leverage the CLIP-ViT visual encoder combined with Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA). By freezing the majority of the backbone parameters and injecting low-rank trainable matrices into the attention layers, we significantly reduce the computational overhead. The training process incorporates a joint optimization strategy: employing InfoNCE contrastive learning on few-shot real-fake pairs to learn generalized forensic features, alongside standard cross-entropy classification on a base dataset. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves an average detection accuracy of 96% across various generative models (GANs and Diffusion Models) and maintains high throughput on edge devices such as the RK3588. This work provides a robust, efficient solution for media forensics and synthetic content detection.

title: Path Planning for Medical Transport Robots Based on an Improved A* Algorithm and the Dynamic Window Approach
authors: Gaoqin Zhong, Zhiran Ji, Yu Cao, Junwen Lai, Zezhen Huang, Libin Feng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: To address issues such as insufficient real-time performance, poor obstacle avoidance flexibility, and slow emergency stop response in path planning for transport robots operating within complex, dynamic hospital environments, this paper proposes a hybrid path planning strategy that integrates an improved A* algorithm with the Dynamic Window Algorithm (DWA). This strategy is implemented within the ROS2 framework. First, the traditional $\mathbf{A}^{*}$ algorithm is optimized by introducing a dynamic weighted Manhattan distance <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">[1]</sup> as the heuristic function. This incorporates path smoothness and safety distance penalty terms, enhancing the rationality and efficiency of the global path. Second, at the local planning layer, a multi-modal perception fusion dynamic window algorithm is designed. This method dynamically embeds real-time evaluations of LiDAR, visual semantic information, and UWB positioning data into the evaluation function, enabling adaptive obstacle avoidance for diverse obstacles such as pedestrian flows, IV stands, and mobile hospital beds. Experiments demonstrate that the improved algorithm reduces planning time by approximately 35% while maintaining near-optimal path length. Emergency stop response time is $\boldsymbol{\leqslant} 180 \mathrm{~ms}$, and dynamic obstacle avoidance success rate increases to 98.7%, effectively supporting high-frequency, high-priority medication transport tasks in medical settings.

title: Multimodal Sensor Fusion-Assisted EKF and Machine Learning for UAV Positioning
authors: Lingche Chi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: To improve the positioning accuracy of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), this paper evaluates a hybrid framework combining an Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) with data-driven. Distinct from recent trends favoring large-scale deep learning, this study benchmarks performance specifically within the "smalldata" regime typical of single-session flights. We compare ensemble methods against state-of-the-art tabular deep learning architectures (e.g., TabM, RealMLP). Empirical results demonstrate that the Extra Trees algorithm outperforms over-parameterized deep models by effectively mitigating overfitting under limited sample constraints. The method significantly reduces horizontal positioning error in offline validation, offering a effective solution for precision navigation without the overhead of complex high-order physical modeling.

title: Detection and Trajectory Planning for Automatic Solar Panel Cleaning Using Drones
authors: Haoran Cui, Yanbing Yang, Yang Chen, Jiahuan Cui
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: This paper presents a two-stage autonomous UAV system for solar-panel inspection and cleaning. In the first flight stage, the UAV performs high-altitude scanning using a stereo depth camera and enhanced YOLOv11 detection network to locate small and distant solar panels. In the second flight, the UAV descends to a lower altitude, where camera collects dense point-cloud data. Principal Component Analysis (PCA) is applied to extract the panel surface plane and dominant edge direction. Based on this, the UAV generates a precise cleaning trajectory and executes it autonomously. Experiments demonstrate that the proposed two-stage framework enables robust panel detection and reliable geometry-aware trajectory generation on a UAV platform.

title: Research on Modeling and Intelligent Analysis Method of Power Grid Multi-Source Data Fusion Based on Business Scenario Driving
authors: Li Chen, Shaobo Liu, Hao Yang, Qijuan Sun, Juntai Shi
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: Under the background of global energy transformation and new power system construction, power grid data presents a multi-source heterogeneous and explosive growth trend, and traditional analysis methods are difficult to meet the demand. This paper studies and constructs a quantitative model of business scene features, expresses the scene requirements in a structured way from multi-dimensions such as space-time resolution, and puts forward a dynamic weight distribution fusion mechanism to realize adaptive weighted fusion of multi-source data according to the feature similarity and information richness of data sources and business scenes. Furthermore, a topology-data fusion graph model based on graph neural network (GNN) is proposed. The power grid equipment and its topological relationship are modeled as graph structure, and the state of equipment is aggregated and updated in multiple layers through graph accumulation, which deeply models the complex relationship between equipment. In the aspect of intelligent analysis, a multi-modal Transformer fusion model is designed, which combines the transfer learning and online learning mechanism to improve the adaptability and real-time optimization ability of the model in different scenarios. The experiment covers two typical scenarios: new energy consumption and distribution network fault location. The results show that the proposed method is significantly superior to the baseline method in terms of new energy consumption prediction accuracy and distribution network fault location accuracy. Through online learning optimization, the model can effectively adapt to data distribution changes, maintain high prediction accuracy, and provide strong support for the improvement of power grid intelligence level.

title: Short-Term Wind Farm Power Prediction Method Based on Multi-Source Data Fusion and Spatio-Temporal Graph Neural Network
authors: Shaoyi Ye, Jianfeng Che, Zheng Wang, Xiangnan Lv
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: To address the challenges of power grid dispatching brought by large-scale wind power integration, improving the accuracy of Short-Term Power Prediction (STPP) for wind farms is crucial. Traditional prediction methods struggle to effectively capture the complex nonlinear, nonstationary, and spatio-temporal coupling characteristics in wind power sequences. This paper proposes a novel short-term power prediction method for wind farms based on multi-source data fusion and Spatio-Temporal Graph Neural Network (STGNN). Results show that the proposed STGNN model significantly outperforms benchmark models in multiple metrics including Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Root Mean Square Error (RMSE), and coefficient of determination. For the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5}$</tex>-minute prediction horizon, the RMSE is reduced by 18.7 % compared to the LSTM model.

title: Design Considerations and Comparison of High-Speed Permanent-Magnet Motors for Flywheel Energy Storage Systems
authors: Fariba Farrokh, Mohammed Jamal Mohammed, Ali Jafer Mahdi, Mohammad Farahzadi, Karim Abbaszadeh
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: This paper presents the design, analysis, and performance comparison of three high-speed permanent magnet (PM) motor topologies—surface-mounted PM (SPM), interior PM (IPM), and synchronous reluctance with auxiliary PM (SynRel-PM)—intended for flywheel energy storage system (FES) applications. A unified design formulation is developed considering rotor dynamics, stator/rotor core materials, slot selection, and electromagnetic loading. The candidate machines are designed with appropriate stator slots, dimensional parameters, and sleeve specifications to ensure both mechanical integrity and electromagnetic feasibility. Finite element method (FEM) analysis is employed to evaluate cogging torque, average torque, torque ripple, and efficiency at rated conditions. Comparative results highlight that the SPM machine provides the most favorable compromise in terms of torque density, efficiency, and cost-to-performance ratio, while the IPM and SynRel-PM machines demonstrate competitive characteristics under specific constraints. These insights provide a practical guideline for selecting and optimizing high-speed PM machines in energy storage systems.

title: Distributed Iterative ML and Message Passing for Grant-Free Cell-Free Massive MIMO Systems
authors: Zilu Zhao, Christian Forsch, Laura Cottatellucci, Dirk Slock
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: Cell-Free (CF) Massive Multiple-Input Multiple-Output (MaMIMO) is considered one of the leading candidates for enabling next-generation wireless communication. With the growing interest in the Internet of Things (IoT), the Grant-Free (GF) access scheme has emerged as a promising solution to support massive device connectivity. The integration of GF and CF-MaMIMO introduces significant challenges, particularly in designing distributed algorithms for activity detection and pilot contamination mitigation. In this paper, we propose a distributed algorithm that addresses these challenges. Our method first employs a component-wise iterative distributed Maximum Likelihood (ML) approach for activity detection, which considers both the pilot and data portions of the received signal. This is followed by a Pseudo-Prior Hybrid Variational Bayes and Expectation Propagation (PP-VB-EP) algorithm for joint data detection and channel estimation. Compared to conventional VBEP, the proposed PP-VB-EP demonstrates improved convergence behavior and reduced sensitivity to initialization, especially when data symbols are drawn from a finite alphabet. The pseudo prior used in PP-VB-EP acts as an approximated posterior and serves as a regularization term that prevents the Message Passing (MP) algorithm from diverging. To compute the pseudo prior in a distributed fashion, we further develop a distributed version of the Variable-Level Expectation Propagation (VL-EP) algorithm.

title: Toward Foundation Model for Multivariate Wearable Sensing of Physiological Signals
authors: Yunfei Luo, Yuliang Chen, Asif Salekin, Tauhidur Rahman
publish: ACM Transactions on Computing for Healthcare
abstract: None

title: Construction of Teaching Experimental Platform for Speed Closed-Loop Adaptive Current Vector Control Strategy of Asynchronous Motor
authors: Linyao He
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Aiming at the teaching experiment demand of speed closed-loop adaptive current vector control for asynchronous motors, a teaching experimental platform with Buck-Boost Matrix Converter (BBMC) as the core power conversion unit is constructed, and a composite speed regulation strategy integrating PI-IP vector control and finite-time control is proposed. Firstly, based on the mathematical model of the asynchronous motor and the requirements of speed closed-loop control, the given speed signal is processed through the adaptive current vector control framework combined with the PI-IP control algorithm to generate the given voltage on the stator side of the asynchronous motor, which serves as the reference output command of the BBMC. Secondly, taking the key state variables of the BBMC (capacitor voltage and inductor current) as the control objects, the finite-time control algorithm is adopted to quickly solve the duty cycle of the power switching tubes, so as to achieve high-precision tracking of the reference voltage. Finally, through the hardware construction and software debugging of the experimental platform, the duty cycle signal is applied to the power switching devices of the BBMC, enabling the output voltage of the converter to accurately reproduce the reference command, thereby ensuring the fast and stable tracking of the actual speed of the asynchronous motor to the given speed and completing the speed closed-loop control target. Simulation analysis and actual measurement results of the experimental platform verify the effectiveness of the control strategy and the teaching applicability of the experimental platform, providing a reliable hardware carrier and technical support for the practical teaching of related courses.

title: Carbon Emission Prediction for Tobacco Logistics Parks Based on CNN-LSTM Model
authors: Jiaying Wu, Sen Bai, Guibin Cen, Taicheng Wei, Yuqin Xie
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: Carbon emission forecasting is essential for promoting sustainable development and implementing effective carbon reduction strategies, especially in high-energy-consuming sectors such as tobacco logistics. This study introduces a novel hybrid prediction model, termed Carbon-AST-CNN-LSTM, which integrates an Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) structure with a CNN-LSTM neural network to effectively capture both hierarchical and temporal features of energy consumption data. The AST component enables structured representation of factors influencing carbon emissions, while the CNN module extracts local spatial features and the LSTM module captures long-term temporal dependencies. The model was evaluated using real-world datasets from three major tobacco logistics parks in Shaanxi Province— Xi’an, Baoji, and Hanzhong. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model achieves superior predictive performance, with a Mean Absolute Error (0.089), Root Mean Squared Error (0.102), and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> Score (0.9874), significantly outperforming traditional models such as SVR, RF, LSTM, CNN, and ARIMA. This research provides a scalable, interpretable, and high-performing solution for carbon emission prediction in the logistics sector, supporting data-driven efforts toward carbon neutrality.

title: Real-Time PIM Mitigation in Cellular Networks: A Reinforcement Learning Approach
authors: Francisco Perdigon Romero, Basant Selim
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: Passive intermodulation (PIM) is a persistent issue in cellular networks, impacting uplink (UL) frequencies in multicell systems. As next-generation networks advance with higher frequencies and increased complexity, mitigating PIM becomes essential to maintain signal integrity. This paper introduces a reinforcement learning-based approach to PIM avoidance that dynamically allocates downlink (DL) resources to reduce selfinterference. By adapting to dynamic traffic conditions and incorporating buffer status into the decision-making process, the proposed method effectively balances UL and DL traffic priorities. Numerical results demonstrate the method's ability to mitigate PIM without impacting overall traffic performance, making it a robust solution for both current and future network deployments.

title: Adaptive Reinforcement Learning Decision-Making and Intelligent Control for Complex Environments: A Hybrid RL-MPC Framework
authors: Niwei Ji, Xiaoya Sheng
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Complex environments are characterized by dynamic uncertainty, multi-constraint coupling, and strong disturbances, which makes classical model-based decision and control difficult to deploy at scale. This paper proposes a hybrid reinforcement learning (RL) adaptive decision-making and intelligent control method for complex environments. The method integrates (i) a state perception and feature extraction module that fuses temporal convolution and attention to represent high-dimensional, non-stationary signals, (ii) an improved deep RL framework with dynamic reward weighting and stable policy optimization to enhance responsiveness under parameter shifts, and (iii) a model predictive control (MPC) execution layer that refines RL actions to ensure feasibility, smoothness, and real-time feedback correction. Code-level validation is conducted on two representative scenarios: intelligent manufacturing control and intelligent power-grid operation. Results show that under abrupt environment changes, decision response time is reduced to 0.6 s, achieving a 50% improvement over a strong baseline; control accuracy reaches 98.2%; robustness tests indicate 35% stronger disturbance rejection than a benchmark model; and the success rate for multi-objective tasks increases to 89.5%. The proposed framework provides a practical and generalizable solution for decision and control in complex environments, improving stability and operational intelligence.

title: Blue Carbon Management and Carbon Credit Markets: A Review of Ecological, Technological, and Digital Frameworks
authors: Vaishali Hirlekar, Balajee Maram
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Blue carbon ecosystems, including mangroves, sea-grasses, and macroalgae, are critical for climate change mitigation due to their exceptional carbon sequestration and storage capacities. Recent advances in remote sensing, machine learning, and blockchain technologies have enabled more accurate carbon accounting, scalable monitoring, and transparent carbon trading mechanisms. This paper presents a comprehensive survey of blue carbon research, focusing on ecosystem carbon pools— aboveground biomass, belowground biomass, and soil organic carbon—and their integration into carbon markets. We review methodologies for carbon quantification, including remote sensing-based assessments, predictive machine learning models, and digital measurement, reporting, and verification (MRV) systems. The paper also examines blockchain applications for carbon credit verification and market transparency, highlighting both potential and challenges. A comparative analysis of recent studies is presented in terms of methodology, spatial scale, data requirements, accuracy, and limitations. The survey identifies key research gaps, including standardization of carbon accounting, regional scalability of models, and adoption barriers for digital MRV and blockchain platforms. This work provides a holistic perspective on the current state of blue carbon science and technology, offering guidance for researchers, policymakers, and stakeholders to enhance ecosystem-based climate mitigation strategies.

title: LGBM-Based Optimal Solution for Data-Driven Transmitter Circuit Design: Prediction Performance and Efficiency Balance
authors: Xiaoli Pei
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: As semiconductor processes advance to advanced nodes, traditional experience-driven methods struggle to address the growing complexity of large-scale circuit design, leading to inefficient iterations and limited automation. To solve these issues, this study focuses on transmitter circuits and explores data-driven strategies for design automation, aiming to shift the design paradigm toward intelligence. Based on circuit physical properties and domain knowledge, five novel physics-based features were constructed to capture key circuit characteristics. Eight models covering traditional machine learning (LR, KNN, SVR, Random Forest, Extra Trees, LGBM) and deep learning (RealMLP, MLP_PLR) were trained and evaluated using metrics including R2, MAPE, prediction time, and storage size. Results show that the LGBM model achieves optimal comprehensive performance, balancing accuracy and efficiency. SHAP analysis was employed to quantify feature contributions, providing explicit guidance for circuit optimization. The proposed data-driven framework effectively reduces reliance on manual experience, enhances the automation, efficiency, and accuracy of large-scale circuit design, and offers a replicable technical route for intelligent design in related circuit fields.

title: Defect Evaluation of IGBT Bond Wire Based on on-state Voltage Drop Detection
authors: Yong Tang, Caigao Xiong, Hui Liu, Yinghua Yang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: In this paper, the early failure principle and reliability evaluation method of IGBT are analyzed, and an evaluation method of IGBT bond wire defects based on on-state voltage drop detection is proposed. Finally, the accuracy of this method is verified by experiments.

title: CT Image Denoising using Deep Learning: A Bibliometric (2020-2025) Perspective
authors: Swapna Katta, Prabhishek Singh, Deepak Garg, Manoj Diwakar
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The bibliometric analysis is a global research advancement in CT image denoising with a deep learning framework of 2020-2025. This analysis explores the changes in the different scholarly metrics including publication sources, impact of the authors, and thematic discipline by analyzing 5 years of publication information. The different Bibliometric tools that have been employed in this study include source production over time in 2020-2025, frequency of words through word cloud, global collaboration through collaboration world map, Co-authorship, and Institutional linkage through collaboration network and intellectual overall structure through co-citation and Document coupling. The journals such as IEEE Transactions on medical imaging, Medical Image Analysis can be considered as a prominent impact factor. These are known to be major contributors of publications. The document coupling and clustering approach allows major research directions, including Convolutional Neural network frameworks, generative model networks, and Transformer-based denoising techniques. This analysis indicates increasing international-collaboration, especially among the China, European countries, and United States demonstrates the growing importance, and multidisciplinary characteristics of CT image denoising research. This analysis shows a comprehensive summary of current and past trends, facilitating future researchers to identify the impactful research work, collaboration growth for advancements, and emerging research domains.

title: Software Architecture for Multi-Domain Pointing, Acquisition and Tracking in Free-Space Optical Communication
authors: Alexandru Duliu, Amita Shrestha
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: Free-Space Optical Communication (FSOC) enables the establishment of long distance optical communication links between terminals over a free-space medium (atmosphere or vacuum). It offers several advantages over radio frequency (RF) links, including enhanced security, increased data transmission rates, and in certain instances, quantum communication capabilities. Successfully establishing a free space optical communication link is a challenging task as it needs to align optical terminals at both ends with a high level of accuracy and responsiveness throughout the link. The process of aligning these elements is called Pointing Acquisition and Tracking (PAT).In this work we present a modular software architecture which is designed from the ground up to realize all aspects necessary for implementing a PAT system. We used a lightweight core architecture in order to avoid feature creep and improve maintainability and scaling.We validate the performance of our applications by estimating the open-loop tracking error when tracking aircraft and measure the close-loop tracking error for static test sources, LCTs and stars.

title: Research on Humidity Environment Prediction for Aircraft Platforms Using LSTM Networks
authors: Yujun Hao, Liangxu Cai, He Li, Qiongyao He, Yao Wu
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: The humidity environment in the critical compartments of aircraft directly affects the performance and service life of airborne equipment. High humidity easily causes component corrosion and equipment failures, so accurate prediction of the humidity environment is of great significance. The existing physical mechanism-based methods had difficulties in solving calculations, while the data-driven methods needed optimization. In this study, the aircraft cockpit was taken as the research object, and a humidity prediction method based on the Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) neural network was proposed. Through correlation analysis of field-measured data, the input variables were determined as the internal cabin temperature, external temperature, and external humidity, and the output variable was the internal cabin relative humidity. Parameters such as 100 hidden layers and 150 fully connected layers were set. The model was trained using one month of measured data (September 18 to October 18, 2024) and verified for prediction accuracy using three days of data (October 1 to October 3, 2024; note: corrected the original date logic to ensure rationality). The results showed that the maximum prediction error of the model was 7.9%, the mean absolute error was 5.1%, and the prediction followability was good. This model did not require complex physical assumptions and could accurately predict the internal cabin humidity, providing a new approach for the environmental adaptability design and maintenance support of airborne equipment, as well as a reference for determining environmental adaptability test conditions.

title: A new type of unmanned water quality detection boat based on a 32-bit ARM architecture platform
authors: Xuetao Lian, Shenqi Lin, Zongze Yang, Xue Yang, Guangya Lou, Kaihong Shen, Zhanghua Wang, Deliang Xiao, Yutao Yuan, Xujun Hu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: China began to engage in water quality testing in 1956, focusing on monitoring water environment quality and controlling water pollution. Despite significant progress over the years, it still lags behind some countries. To address the shortcomings of traditional water quality testing methods, such as limited coverage, poor real-time performance, and weak dynamic tracking, we have designed an unmanned boat for intelligent water quality testing based on STM32. With the STM32F103C8T6 microprocessor as the core and equipped with high-precision sensors, it can monitor conventional water quality data such as pH, temperature, turbidity, and dissolved oxygen in real time. It can also study the thermocline and anoxic layer at specific depths in the water and transmit the data to the cloud platform via 4G for visualization on the APP. An AI data platform has been built to quickly process and analyze the collected water quality data. This unmanned boat uses a stable and reliable remote control lifting system to precisely lift the detection equipment at different water levels, facilitating multi-parameter detection and sampling. This small boat, which features multi-parameter precision detection and intelligent cruise obstacle avoidance capabilities, can help reduce the cost of water quality testing, alleviate the pressure on social water resources, and provide a more efficient, safe and economical solution for people.

title: Integrating Federated Learning and YOLO Models for Robust Object Detection Across Edge Platforms
authors: K Vinoth, P Sasikumar
abstract: This work proposes an advanced federated learning (FL) framework that utilizes YOLOv8 and YOLOv9 architectures to address the challenges of efficient object detection (OD) in distributed and resource-constrained environments. The framework leverages the Cosine Similarity based Client Metric (CSCM) to optimize client selection and model aggregation, ensuring robust and effective global model updates across Non-Independent and Identically Distributed datasets (Non-IID). Evaluations were conducted using the UniScenes dataset, specifically curated to represent diverse real-world scenarios, encompassing objects such as persons, vehicles, two-wheeler riders, and campus shuttles. The results demonstrate significant improvements in key metrics, including precision, recall, and mean average precision (mAP), while achieving reduced communication and computational overheads compared to conventional aggregation methods like FedAvg. The system’s adaptability is further validated through deployment on various edge devices, including Raspberry Pi and NVIDIA Jetson platforms, showcasing efficient real-time OD. The research highlights the potential of federated YOLO models in enabling scalable and high-performance OD for applications such as autonomous systems, surveillance, and IoT-based solutions. Future directions for this research include exploring more complex datasets, expanding the framework’s scalability, and enhancing deployment efficiency across heterogeneous edge environments.

title: Reliability of Wide Bandgap Semiconductors for Automotive Applications
authors: Nando Kaminski, Thomas Basler, Roman Boldyrjew-Mast, Said El-Barbari, Jörg Franke, Martin Rittner, Paul Salmen, Stefan Schmitt, Oliver Senftleben, Marc Tüllmann, Markus Thoben
abstract: Wide bandgap (WBG) semiconductor devices offer tremendous advantages over their silicon counterparts. Automotive applications benefit particularly from these advantages and, consequently, the automotive sector has emerged as the lead application for silicon carbide (SiC) devices and will also play an important role for gallium nitride (GaN) power devices. SiC components in particular have been used in commercial automotive products for several years and meet the usual qualification standards in terms of robustness and reliability. However, the silicon-based standards do not cover all peculiarities that the WBG devices exhibit. Bipolar degradation and gate switching instability (GSI) are only two of the effects that do not affect silicon. Also, the degradation of the devices throughout their service life can be different from that of silicon devices. Therefore, the existing standards have to be amended to offer the same security level for WBG devices, which is essential for the success in the extremely robust and reliability-sensitive automotive sector. Furthermore, the production maturity is not yet on the same level as the silicon technology. Besides conservative device design, particular screening tests are required. If such procedures are not viable, even complex and demanding burn-in tests might be implemented to cover all relevant defects and degradation mechanisms and ensure the quality that the automotive industry demands to maintain their reliability level. This work provides a review of the current status of WBG device reliability with respect to automotive requirements and of the ongoing activities to catch up with the level of silicon devices.

title: Evaluation and Analysis of the RF Stress on the Current Collapse Effect in Si-Based RF GaN HEMTs
authors: Shaoyu Sun, Zhizhen Yin, Shengxing Song, Zhanqiang Ru, Helun Song
abstract: In recent years, with the development of high-voltage and high-power GaN radio frequency (RF) devices, the current collapse effect caused by the high electric field cannot be ignored. This work investigates the influence of RF stress on the current collapse effect of a commercial Si-based GaN RF device in an actual amplifier circuit.
