We propose a clamp circuit with an RF isolation unit that can rapidly detect the <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</small>-resistance (R<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</sub>) of the device. The measurement results show that the R<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</sub> of the device exhibits an obvious degradation under different RF output power (P<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">out</sub>) of the amplifier conditions. The TCAD simulation and experimental results indicated that the R<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</sub> degradation is related to the high drain voltage and negative gate voltage stress in the <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">off</small> state. These two factors cause a large number of ionized traps in the gate region, which leads to a positive threshold voltage shift and an increase in the channel series resistance of the device. However, when the amplifier operates under high P<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">out</sub> conditions, the R<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</sub> of the device is decreased as the P<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">out</sub> increases. The analysis results indicated that the suppression of the current collapse effect is mainly caused by the recovery of the threshold voltage. We consider that this phenomenon is caused by the high-positive gate voltage in the <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</small> state of the device, which leads to a significant decrease in the ionized trap concentration in the barrier layer.

title: Investigations of the Gate Breakdown, ESD Robustness, and Switching Characteristics in p-GaN HEMTs With Integrated ESD Designs
authors: Chih-Yao Chang, Cheng-Sheng Kao, Chih-Wen Hsiung, Hsing-Hua Hsieh, Yen-Chen Peng, Teng-Sheng Huang, Meng-Jen Tsai, Ming-Chang Tsou, Ming-Nan Chuang, Yu-Teng Tso, Tian-Li Wu
abstract: In this work, the gate breakdown and electrostatic discharge (ESD) robustness of p-GaN gate high-electron-mobility transistors (HEMTs) with integrated ESD devices are investigated in the different types of gate-to-source (G–S) ESD designs with different areas. Typical <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$I$</tex-math> </inline-formula>–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$V$</tex-math> </inline-formula> characteristics and the gate breakdown conducted by the DC sweep show that the impact of adding the ESD path at the G–S on breakdown voltage is limited. On the other hand, the weak ESD path (less than <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${V} _{\text {HBM}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> –0.5 kV) of G–S under reverse bias is enhanced <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${V} _{\text {HBM}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> nearing −8kV by adding the discharge path from source-to-gate (S–G) (Type II: Samples C and D). The improvement would depend on the ESD device area. In addition to the benefits of reverse gate ESD event, an additional S–G discharge path also facilitates ESD dissipation due to the generation of parasitic capacitance, thereby enhancing the forward ESD robustness <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${V} _{\text {HBM}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> over 4.7 kV. A further improvement under the forward ESD event can reach <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${V} _{\text {HBM}}~5.2$</tex-math> </inline-formula> kV when adding the ESD path from G–S as a bilateral ESD type (Type III: Sample E). The switching behavior comparison shows that the inclusion of the suitable G–S ESD results in only a marginal impact on the power HEMTs.

title: Quantum Inspired Vehicular Network Optimization for Intelligent Decision Making in Smart Cities
authors: Kamran Ahmad Awan, Sonia Khan, Eman Abdullah Aldakheel, Saif Al-Kuwari, Ahmed Farouk
abstract: Connected and automated vehicles require city-scale coordination under strict latency and reliability targets, yet many solutions optimize communications and mobility separately, resulting in degraded performance during outages and under edge–cloud computational contention. This work introduces QIVNOM, a quantum-inspired framework that jointly optimizes V2V/V2I communication and urban traffic control on classical edge–cloud hardware (without a quantum processor). Candidate routing–signal plans are encoded as probabilistic superpositions and updated using sphere-projected gradients with annealed sampling to minimize a regularized cost. An entanglement-style coupling regularizer links networking and mobility decisions, while Tchebycheff multi-objective scalarization with feasibility projection enforces latency and reliability constraints. Robustness is enhanced by chance constraints and Lyapunov-drift control. Plans are mapped to fog nodes through entropic optimal transport, and vehicle-level CVaR (Conditional Value at Risk) micropolicies align local safety with global guidance. In METR-LA-calibrated SUMO–OMNeT++/Veins simulations over a 5×5 km urban map with IEEE 802.11p and 5G NR sidelink, QIVNOM reduces the mean end-to-end latency to 57.3 ms (≈20% below the best baseline). Under incidents, the latency is 62 ms vs 79 ms (−21.5%), and with RSU outages, 67 ms vs 86 ms (−22.1%). Packet delivery averages 96.7% (+2.3 pts), reliability reaches 96.7% in general (RSU-outage 96.8% vs 94.1%), and corridor-closure travel metrics improve (ATT 12.8 min/33% vs 14.5 min/37%).

title: Optimization and Performance Improvement of Multisensor Piezoelectric Balance Assembly Method Based on a Complex Super-Statical Determinate Structure
authors: Jin Wang, Yaqi Wu, Yuzhe Tian, Jialing Wu, Mingyu Yuan, Shilu Mai, Zongjin Ren, Wei Liu
abstract: Multisensor piezoelectric balances are increasingly used in wind tunnel tests requiring high dynamic performance, due to their flexible structure, easy fabrication, high stiffness, and excellent dynamic performance. However, superimposed manufacturing and assembly errors degrade their force-measuring performance. To address this issue, this article proposes a novel assembly method for such balances based on a complex statically indeterminate structure. The method controls the preload of connecting bolts using a digital torque wrench and optimizes sensor height via three approaches, leveraging coordinate measuring machine data and copper sheets’ high plasticity. Mechanical and mathematical models were established, followed by simulations to analyze error impacts, and a six-component force calibration system was built for static/dynamic tests. Results show that placing copper sheets on sensors’ top/bottom (with uniform bolt preload) significantly reduces force imbalance; top-placed sheets improve static performance. Natural frequency tests confirm no negative impact on dynamic performance, verifying the method’s effectiveness and feasibility.

title: A Numerical Investigation of the Electrohydrodynamic Flow Characteristics of a Dielectric Barrier Discharge Pump
authors: Orçun Ekin
abstract: A numerical model of a two-layer dielectric barrier discharge (DBD) pump in the wire-to-plate configuration is developed and validated against the published experimental results. The study aims at successfully representing the characteristic properties of a DBD pump in a computational multiphysics model that can be easily modified to changing environmental, geometric and electrical conditions. Moreover, important output parameters, such as power conversion efficiency and discharge current, are predicted, which can be otherwise determined via experimental methods. Evolution of electric field and space charge as well as electrohydrodynamic (EHD) force components over a variety of setups were demonstrated.

title: Trajectory-Aware Air-to-Ground Channel Characterization for Low-Altitude UAVs Using MaMIMO Measurements
authors: Abdul Saboor, Zhuangzhuang Cui, Achiel Colpaert, Evgenii Vinogradov, Wout Joseph, Sofie Pollin
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive measurement-based trajectory-aware characterization of low-altitude Air-to-Ground (A2G) channels in a suburban environment. A 64-element Massive Multi-Input Multi-Output (MaMIMO) array was used to capture channels for three trajectories of an Uncrewed Aerial Vehicle (UAV), including two horizontal zig-zag flights at fixed altitudes and one vertical ascent, chosen to emulate Aerial User Equipment (AUE) operations and to induce controlled azimuth and elevation sweeps for analyzing geometry-dependent propagation dynamics. We examine large-scale power variations and their correlation with geometric features, such as elevation, azimuth, and 3D distance, followed by an analysis of fading behavior through distribution fitting and Rician K-factor estimation. Furthermore, temporal non-stationarity is quantified using the Correlation Matrix Distance (CMD), and angular stationarity spans are utilized to demonstrate how channel characteristics change with the movement of the UAV. We also analyze Spectral Efficiency (SE) in relation to K-factor and Root Mean Square (RMS) delay spread, highlighting their combined influence on link performance. The results show that the elevation angle is the strongest predictor of the received power, with a correlation of more than 0.77 for each trajectory. At the same time, the Nakagami model best fits the small-scale fading. The K-factor increases from approximately 5 dB at low altitudes to over 15 dB at higher elevations, indicating stronger Line-of-Sight (LoS) dominance. Non-stationarity patterns are highly trajectory- and geometry-dependent, with azimuth most affected in horizontal flights and elevation during vertical flight. These findings offer measurement-driven insights into low-altitude AUE channel behavior in suburban LoS-dominant environments and provide a baseline for future extensions toward higher-altitude and dense urban Non-Terrestrial Network (NTN) scenarios.

title: Ultra-compact MEMS Pirani gauge for in-situ wafer-level vacuum monitoring
authors: Peng Ding, Yongquan Su, Yichen Liu, Yongkang Liu, Shuo Chen, Shinan Wang, Yang Wang, Min Li, Qifeng Qiao, Zhenyu Wu
abstract: The ongoing trend toward sensor miniaturization has rendered ultra-compact wafer-level vacuum packaging (WLVP) indispensable. Nevertheless, a reliable monitoring tool for wafer-level vacuum (WLV) in ultra-compact devices remains unavailable. In this paper, we present an ultra-compact MEMS Pirani gauge for WLV pressure monitoring. The proposed MEMS sensor is fabricated on an 8-inch wafer with a footprint of 0.28 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. Experimental results demonstrate a pressure measurement range from 8×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-2</sup> to 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sup> Pa, with an average sensitivity of 71.812 mV/lgPa. This MEMS sensor enables in-situ WLV monitoring of the sealed cavity, including the Al-Ge eutectic bonded cavity described in this study, which achieving an effective pressure of approximately 253 Pa inside the sealed cavity. Furthermore, we demonstrated the WLV monitoring of the bonded device over a period of 525 days, confirming that the adopted bonding technology maintains a vacuum level of 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> Pa throughout the duration. With the growing demand for high-vacuum MEMS devices, the proposed MEMS sensor shows strong potential for integration with other devices via bonding techniques, enabling in-situ WLV monitoring.

title: UAV Trajectory Optimization in Air-Ground Collaborative Regional Radio Positioning Systems
authors: Ruocheng Guo, Hong Yuan, Xiao Chen, Yi Lu, Ting Liu, Guanbing Zhang
abstract: To overcome the limitations of ground-based pseudolite systems susceptible to terrain obstructions, this paper proposes an air-ground collaborative regional radio positioning system (AGCRRPS) in GNSS-denied environments, including architecture, evaluation metrics, and a systematic UAV swarm trajectory optimization method. The AGCRRPS operates through sequential pseudorange broadcasting and positioning among ground anchors, aerial UAV swarms, and ground users to ensure the stable operation of the system. The UAV swarm’s trajectory design directly impacts the propagation of system accuracy. To characterize the error propagation in this layered air-ground structure, we derive a precision mapping model suitable for multi-layer systems and the equivalent Dilution of Precision (DOP) metric for instantaneous geometric configurations. Based on this, we formulate a multi-objective optimization function that effectively evaluates regional positioning systems, incorporating three dimensions: average accuracy, inter-user balance, and temporal stability. An adaptive trajectory representation protocol that optimizes UAV trajectories while preserving primary task objectives is developed. By leveraging the Non-dominated Sorting Genetic Algorithm II (NSGA-II) as the multi-objective optimizer, we establish a systematic UAV swarm trajectory optimization approach, with simulation results demonstrating its effectiveness. Further simulations reveal the precision limits and inter-layer independence in multi-layer systems, providing theoretical insights for weak-anchor multi-tier navigation system design. Additionally, the influence of resource allocation on user performance is analyzed, offering practical guidance for anchor-UAV ratio configurations in engineering deployments.

title: NOMA-Enabled UAV Trajectory Optimization Using Deep Reinforcement Learning for Efficient IoT Data Collection
authors: Chaitanya Krishna Bulusu, Pragya Swami, Manisha Pattanaik, Mukesh Kumar Mishra, Tharmalingam Ratnarajah
abstract: Non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) is a promising technology for future networks because it can allow many sensors to connect and share the same resources at the same time. This provides a spectrally efficient method in situations where spectrum is limited but number of sensors are enormous. In this paper, we extend NOMA to unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) to solve their limited flight time issue while collecting data from sensors that are distributed randomly across an open field. The objective of this study is to design an efficient UAV trajectory planning scheme that maximizes the total collected sensor data within the available flight time. To achieve this, We model the UAV trajectory optimization as a Markov Decision Process and employ a Deep Q-Network (DQN) to adaptively select UAV movements. A novel reward function is designed to simultaneously guide the UAV toward sensor locations, avoid unnecessary detours, and enhance data collection efficiency under uplink NOMA. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed NOMA-enabled DQN framework achieves up to 93.5% average data collection, offering an improvement of approximately 19% compared to the traditional TDMA-based approach, while requiring no retraining when the sensor distribution varies.

title: Uses for AI in Developing Educational XR Experiences: Emerging Best Practices
authors: Owen McGrath
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 ACM SIGUCCS Annual Conference
abstract: None

title: Unsupervised Adaptive Asphalt Pavement Damage Recognition Algorithm Based on Fuzzy C-Means Clustering
authors: Youssef Aouni, Omar El Ogri, Souad El Moudni El Alami, Jaouad El-Mekkaoui, Mohamed Qachar, Ahmed Chigr
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: This study presents a pioneering unsupervised adaptive damage recognition algorithm for detecting asphalt pavement deterioration. The algorithm uses fuzzy C-means clustering to segment images and assign adaptive weights to pixels representing asphalt pavement damage according to their value dispersion. The detection of asphalt pavement damage is improved by a tiered organization and feedback process that categorizes noise, pavement bodies and edges. This approach is unique in that it eliminates the need for manual labelling, thereby reducing errors and processing time compared to traditional methods. High global accuracy (98.97%) and mean IoU (98.34%) on the test set were achieved by a U-net architecture that was trained with a cross-entropy loss and an Adam optimizer. This efficient approach is ideal for detecting road surface damage in asphalt surface course, and it provides precise assessments of various types of deterioration. It also introduces new methods for the automatic detection of Pavement Inspection Systems (PMS).

title: A Comprehensive Review of Adaptive Traffic Management Systems for Sustainable Smart Cities
authors: S. Boopathi, R Jothikumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: Steep urbanization and rising car ownership have reinforced traffic congestion, leading to additional energy consumption and environmental pollution in cities. Classical fixed-time traffic control systems are inadequate for addressing dynamic and complex traffic modes because they do not respond promptly. This paper provides an extensive overview of Adaptive Traffic Management Systems (ATMS) highlighting the combination of Federated Learning (FL) and Tiny Machine Learning (TinyML) as innovative enabling technologies for smart sustainable cities. FL provides privacy-preserving collaborative model training between distributed traffic nodes, while TinyML supports low-power real-time decision-making at the network edge. The convergence of these technologies creates better traffic flow, sharp cuts in carbon footprint, and reduced infrastructure energy requirements. We critically review recent implementations, point out significant research challenges like multi-modal integration and scalability issues, and provide future directions such as federated meta-learning, blockchain-protected architecture, and digital twin-based predictive control. Our results highlight the imperative role of FL and TinyML in building energy-saving, privacy-sensitive, and scalable ATMS according to United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

title: Quantum optimization for the Physician Scheduling Problem
authors: Baha Mlaouhi, Chahid Ahabchane, Iyed Benslimen
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: Scheduling medical staff in healthcare institutions is a complex and critical task that requires balancing operational constraints, personnel availability, quality of care, and staff well-being. These interdependent factors make the creation of optimal schedules particularly challenging necessitating sophisticated approaches that effectively reconcile institutional needs and medical staff expectations. This project explores an innovative alternative approach: quantum optimization. By formulating the scheduling problem as a QUBO (Quadratic Unconstrained Binary Optimization) model, we aim to leverage the capabilities of quantum algorithms, particularly the Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm (QAOA) This framework allows constraints and objectives to be reformulated as quadratic functions, facilitating their integration into Ising models and their resolution on quantum gate_based circuit systems.To this end, we implement a hybrid quantum-classical solution using QAOA on IBM’s Qiskit platform. We analyze QAOA’s performance on problem instances of up to 27 qubits, and compare it against classical solvers such as CPLEX. Through this comparative approach, we assess the practical viability of quantum optimization in medical staff scheduling and contribute to the broader investigation of quantum computing in operational planning within healthcare systems.

title: ResNet50-Based Parking Space Detection System
authors: Yibo Zhu
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: To address inefficiencies in traditional manual monitoring such as delayed information feedback and high error rates in space status assessment, this study constructs a parking space recognition system based on the ResNet50 model. By integrating transfer learning and data augmentation technologies, it realizes the automatic identification of “vacant” and “occupied” states of parking spaces. Through freezing the first 15 layers of the model to retain general features, fine-tuning the high-level network to adapt to specific tasks, and building a classification head that includes global average pooling layers and batch normalization layers, the model achieves an accuracy of 0.9625 and a loss value of 0.0971 on the validation set. It has good generalization ability and outperforms the VGG16 and VGG19 models in performance. This system enables real-time counting of parking space status, supports integration with display devices based on image recognition results, and is easy to deploy without additional hardware modifications. It provides reliable technical support for intelligent parking space management through advanced image recognition technologies.

title: A Multi-Scale Hybrid Expert Deep Learning Model for Hysteresis Modeling of Piezoelectric Actuators
authors: Xuchen Wang, Huazhou Kang, Yixiao Yang, Yuping Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: Piezoelectric actuators (PEAs) are essential components in high-precision engineering, yet their open-loop accuracy is severely compromised by inherent, rate-dependent hysteresis nonlinearity. Effectively modeling the multi-scale dynamics of this phenomenon, which encompasses both slow, global trajectory-dependent memory and rapid, localized variations, remains a significant challenge for existing data-driven methods. To address this, we propose the Wavelet-Transform Hysteresis Bidirectional Mamba (WT-HysteresisBiMamba), a novel hybrid expert architecture. Our framework first utilizes a Wavelet Transform layer to decompose the input voltage signal into a multi-resolution feature space. These features are then processed in parallel by two specialized experts: a Global Pattern Expert, which uses a bidirectional Mamba to capture long-range temporal dependencies, and a Local Variation Expert, employing a 1D Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) to extract salient local features. A dynamic gating mechanism adaptively fuses the outputs of these experts. Experimental results demonstrate that our model significantly outperforms conventional recurrent architectures like LSTM and GRU in modeling PEA hysteresis across various frequencies. The proposed model achieves superior accuracy and robustness, offering a powerful new solution for the high-fidelity system identification and control of piezoelectric actuators.

title: A new development in power transformer fault diagnosis using artificial intelligence models, a review study
authors: Reza Hojjati, Asghar Akbari Azirani
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive review of the application of Artificial Intelligence (AI) algorithms for the fault diagnosis of power transformers, focusing on three principal diagnostic methods, including Dissolved Gas Analysis (DGA), Frequency Response Analysis (FRA), and Partial Discharge (PD) monitoring. For DGA, AI-based methods are shown to enhance diagnostic accuracy and reliability by integrating results from multiple conventional interpretation techniques, generating novel diagnostic features from raw data, and generating new data representations from existing datasets for AI models input. In FRA, AI has demonstrated remarkable effectiveness in identifying the type, location, and severity of mechanical defects by analyzing FRA signatures either through the estimation of transformer ladder model parameters, the use of polar plot representations, or the extraction of statistical indices as model inputs. Finally, for PD monitoring, the application of AI to large, labeled datasets enables high accuracy classification of types of discharge. Notably, AI diagnosis techniques also facilitate the localization of PD events using data from only a single sensor, representing a significant advancement over traditional methods. The findings across these areas confirm that AI provides more robust, precise, and efficient solutions for modern transformer condition monitoring and fault diagnosis.

title: Explainable AI-Driven Feature Selection for Predicting Myocardial Infarction in T2DM Patients Utilizing Hybrid Multilayer Perceptron with Extreme Gradient Boosting
authors: T M Latha, R Venkatesan, P Rajendiran, C Sivasubramaniyan
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Myocardial Infarction (MI) is a significant health issue in the world and the cause of high death rates in most countries. Complications and deaths associated with heart attacks can be minimized through early diagnosis and accurate identification is particularly essential to people with type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM) since it would allow tracking the key risk factors effectively. This research aimed at improving Myocardial Infarction predictions based on the Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System (BRFSS) data. The proposed hybrid architecture that merges a Multilayer Perceptron (MLP), Random Forest (RF), and XGBoost to prove the robustness of the prediction of MI. A pipelined preprocessing approach of dropping duplicates, outlier detection and normalization to ensure better performance of the three individual models. Ensemble SMOTE is deployed to avoid overfitting and bias. The proposed model produced an accuracy of 96.23 and a recall of 96.24 on critical MI cases in patients with T2DM, with an AUC score of 99.89, indicating a high capacity to discriminate between the classes. The analysis with SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) helps to identify the impact of each feature on the risk of having a heart attack, which contributes to the increased transparency of the model.

title: Smart Agriculture: IoT-Based Crop Selection Using Real-Time Soil Nutrient Data
authors: Sia Duggal, Shivani Sood, Shallu Duggal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: This paper presents a smart system designed for crop prediction using machine learning. It helps farmers to choose the best crops based on soil parameters like moisture, $\mathbf{p H}$, and nutrients. We also explain an IoT- based setup that collects real-time data from the fields to support precision agriculture. Soil data and machine learning are combined in the system that gives accurate crop recommendations and hence improve yield and reduce risks. The results show that the machine learning model can predict suitable crops effectively, making farming easier and more efficient. This approach aims to support farmers in making better decisions using technology.

title: A Comprehensive Study on Mobile SOS and Personal Safety Applications Across Multiple Scenarios
authors: Kv Divya, D Chandresh, Darshan D Madiwalar, P Dinesh, Shreya R Varur
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Science and Communications Technologies (ICISCT)
abstract: Personal safety has emerged as a critical societal concern in the digital era. Rapid urbanization, increased mobility, and social vulnerability have led to the proliferation of emergency response technologies—especially mobile SOS applications that allow users to instantly alert authorities, family, or volunteer networks. This survey systematically reviews eight major SOS and personal-safety apps (Kavalan SOS, Noonlight, bSafe, Life360, Safetipin, Suraksha, EmergiFy, and Be My Eyes) to identify prevailing design trends, emergency workflows, and technological gaps across scenarios such as women’s safety, theft protection, support for visually impaired users, and fall alerts for older adults. Data were synthesized from 20+ peer-reviewed studies, industry reports, and government evaluations (2020–2025). The analysis shows a marked shift from manual panic-button models to context-aware, sensor-fused, AI-assisted systems integrated with police networks or commercial monitoring centers. Despite progress, limitations persist in connectivity dependence, privacy protection, false alarms, and adoption among marginalized groups. The paper concludes by proposing research priorities for universal, offline-capable, and ethically governed safety ecosystems that can operate seamlessly across geographies and risk contexts.

title: Federated Optimization for Distributed Wastewater Treatment Parameters Based on Improved FedAvg Algorithm and LSTM Local Models
authors: Zhiqun Ren, Yingjie Zhao, Huiting Li
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: In distributed systems for treating wastewater, the ability of traditional centralized modeling methods to fulfill the needs of real-world applications is challenged by the strong temporal features present for each processing site. To overcome this challenge, this paper presents a distributed wastewater treatment parameter optimization framework. An improved time series-oriented gradient correction mechanism is proposed to establish analogue asynchronous dynamic features at all sites. In addition, an adaptive client weighting strategy based on local loss variance is proposed to alleviate the model bias because of data heterogeneity. Lastly, the paper proposes a local-global two-level optimization mechanism: on the site side, the LSTM is able to capture the temporal dependencies of influent flow, pollutant concentration, and control instructions, while on the server side, a momentum regularization term on the global-weighted aggregation improves the stability and generalizability of the global aggregation. Experimental results show that the proposed framework has a performance advantage of up to 17.3% in RMSE and other relevant metrics over both the traditional FedAvg algorithm and a series of time series federated models.

title: Design and Analysis of a Miniaturized Active Circularly Polarized Normal-Mode Helical Antenna for Vehicular DAB+ Phased Array Systems
authors: Antonios Constantinides, Ebrahim Maki
publish: 2026 Symposium on Microwave, Antenna, and Propagation (SMAP)
abstract: This paper presents the design and simulation of a miniaturized active circularly polarized normal-mode helical antenna (NMHA) to enhance Digital Audio Broadcasting Plus (DAB+) reception in challenging mobile environments. Conventional vehicular antennas suffer from excessive size, polarization mismatch, and multipath fading; the proposed solution addresses these limitations by integrating a compact NMHA with a custom low-noise amplifier (LNA) functioning as an active impedance matching network. The antenna achieves an exceptionally low profile of 1.52 cm and a 12 cm circumference, operating in normal mode with an omnidirectional broadside radiation pattern. Its inherent circular polarization mitigates dynamic polarization mismatch and multipath interference. The integrated LNA, based on the NE3509M04 GaAs Heterojunction Field-Effect Transistor (HJ-FET), is optimized to provide a high input impedance <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\approx \mathbf{4 5 5 9}+\mathbf{j 1 1 7 2 0} \boldsymbol{\Omega}$</tex> at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 9 0. 5 ~ M H z})$</tex> to minimize antenna loading and a low output impedance (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 33.6- \mathrm{j} 0.07 \Omega)$</tex> for seamless 50Ω system integration. Scattering parameter analysis indicates an input reflection coefficient (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{S}_{11}$</tex>) of 0.997 expected due to the high input impedance and an output reflection coefficient <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\mathbf{S}_{\mathbf{2 2}}\right)$</tex> of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 1 9 6}$</tex>, corresponding to only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 3.8 \%$</tex> power reflection at the output. These results validate the proposed active NMHA as a highly sensitive and stable front-end solution, establishing its suitability as a foundational element for next-generation vehicular phased-array systems and significantly improving DAB+ reception reliability and audio quality in mobile scenarios.

title: Edge AI-Enabled Real-Time Learning Analytics for Scalable and Privacy-Aware E-Learning Platforms
authors: Mohammad Nasar, Rashid Hamed Al Azri, Mohammed Al Batahari
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: With the increasing adoption of e-learning platforms in education, large amounts of learner interaction data have been produced. When appropriately analyzed, this data can offer important insights to underpin teaching practices, track student engagement, and drive better learning results. However, almost all current learning analytics systems are based on centralized cloud-based computation, which is likely to suffer from delayed feedback (analysis results and recommendations), scalability problems, and data privacy issues. To mitigate this challenge, in this paper, describe an Edge AI-empowered real-time learning analytic system for a scalable and privacy-preserved e-learning environment. The framework makes it possible to run computations directly on data generated in learner interactions to analyze levels of engagement and early detect learners who may need support so that raw data do not have to be transmitted offsite. The practicality of the proposed method in an educational context was successfully demonstrated using a real learning analytics dataset. The findings suggest that localized analytics can increase the responsiveness of systems and help teachers act on evidence promptly, fostering more effective and human-centered digital learning environments.

title: Embedded System Controlled and Supervised Using LabVIEW
authors: Khaldi Ouadjih, Fouad Slaoui Hasnaoui, Semaan Georges
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: This work presents the design and implementation of an embedded system controlled and supervised by LabVIEW, dedicated to precision agriculture. The system integrates various environmental sensors (soil moisture, temperature, water level and flow, etc.) as well as actuators (pumps, fans) to ensure intelligent management of agricultural resources. LabVIEW was used as a local supervision platform, enabling real-time data acquisition, visualization through an intuitive graphical interface, and control of the actuators. This approach enhances the efficiency of agricultural monitoring, optimizes water and energy usage, and demonstrates the relevance of embedded systems combined with advanced software tools for smart agriculture.

title: Up to 200G Coherent COTS Evaluation against Atmospheric Dynamic Fadings
authors: Anaëlle Maho, Tarik Benaddi, Lisa Tonnelier, Dorian Montagut, Gauthier Bechet, Simon Lévêque, Mathilde Gay, Thierry Chartier, Laurent Bramerie, Michel Sotom
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: The paper evaluates the performance of commercial coherent transceivers in free-space optical communications, focusing on their robustness against atmospheric dynamic fadings. The study assesses 100G DP-QPSK and 200G DP-16QAM modulations, examining the impact of fading depth, duration, and repetition rate. Under fast fadings, 100G DP-QPSK shows minimal penalty for weak disturbances but exhibits error floors for stronger fadings. 200G DP-16QAM shows limited pre-FEC penalty but more significant post-FEC impairment. Under dynamic atmospheric conditions, FEC performance differs significantly from that observed over a static channel. It is impacted by very short and deep fadings.

title: Forecasting Technological Directions in Wireless Networks and Mobile Computing via AutoML Framework
authors: Ahmed Abolfadl, Marwa Mahmoud, Basma Afifi, Mervat Abu-Elkheir, Maggie Mashaly
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: The exponential increase in scientific publications has driven the emergence of new trends. Accurate forecasting of these developments is essential for researchers and professionals to stay updated with advancements in the field. This study presents an automated pipeline for trend prediction in the wireless networks and mobile computing domain by integrating clustering, topic modeling, and time series analysis. The process begins with the collection of 127,820 abstracts from high-impact journals and conferences, followed by extensive preprocessing and semantic embedding using the SPECTER model. AutoCluster applies meta-learning to select the most suitable clustering algorithm based on the dataset meta-features, ensuring semantically coherent groupings. AutoTopicModeling then employs a successive halving strategy to identify the best-performing topic model per cluster, followed by LLM-assisted topic labeling and optional label generalization. Finally, AutoTrendAnalysis transforms topic-labeled data into time series and applies forecasting models -ARIMA, STL, Prophet, or LSTM - to predict future topic popularity. Topics are classified as strong, weak, or noise signals based on forecast trajectories, offering interpretable insights into emerging and declining research themes. The framework is scalable, adaptive, and designed for robust trend analysis across scientific domains. Experimental results demonstrated high predictive accuracy, achieving a Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of 36.76.

title: Unlocking the Potential Adoption Process of AI-based Software from Business Ideation into Prototyping: Case Study of V0.App
authors: Arfive Gandhi, Prati Hutari Gani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Science and Communications Technologies (ICISCT)
abstract: Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) had created new entrepreneurial opportunities, especially AI-based software. Entrepreneurs can develop their business ideation faster in generation process and feedback gathering since using AI-based software, such as v0.app. However, the adoption process of AI-based software was questionable since several ethical issues and quality fulfillment. This research constructs five hypotheses to assess relationships among five suspected factors (product quality, motivation, subjective norm, security, and ability) to the intention to use AI-based software in prototyping context. The five hypotheses were accepted after statistical testing so that those suspected factors (product quality, individual motivation, and individual ability) are indeed contributing factors that influence people’s intention to use v0.app as software for business ideation. In qualitative part, most respondents agreed that UI designers and programmers will be irreplaceable. Also, they are concerned about copyright risks since the $\mathbf{v 0}$.app software may produce prototypes that are similar to each other after several users use similar general prompts Therefore, respondents realized the importance of similarity checking and specifying the used prompt based on users’ specific requirements and business ideation.

title: Multilevel PFC Converters for Efficient EV Battery Chargers
authors: Akhilesh Kumar Tiwari, Prasanta Kumar Jena, Kulshresth Singh, Padmini Sharma, Prem Kushwaha, Shankarshan Prasad Tiwari
abstract: Two-stage electric vehicle (EV) charging architectures typically include a rectification stage followed by a DC-DC conversion stage. Multilevel rectifiers (MLRs) are emerging as superior options for the rectification stage in EV chargers due to their lower voltage stress on switches, improved harmonic performance on the grid side, reduced dv/dt stress, and higher voltage handling capability. In this paper, a five-level rectifier combined with a phase-shift full-bridge (PSFB) converter is implemented for EV charging applications. The rectifier inherently balances capacitor voltages and maintains unity power factor (UPF) at the source side under various operating conditions. To validate the system's suitability for real-time EV charging, a comprehensive performance analysis is conducted, covering loss and efficiency calculations. The EV battery charger has been mathematically modelled, simulated in MATLAB and evaluated using real time validation.

title: Quad-Band Planar Magnetic Inductive Waveguide for Next-Generation Wireless Wearables
authors: Shelby V. Tommy, Jayshri Kulkarni
publish: 2026 Symposium on Microwave, Antenna, and Propagation (SMAP)
abstract: This paper presents a quad-band dual layer planar magnetic-inductive (MI) waveguide designed for next-generation wearable wireless systems. The structure has an overall size of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$158 \text{mm} \times 12 \text{mm}$</tex> and is designed on a flexible PET substrate with a relative permittivity <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\epsilon_{r})$</tex> of 2.6 and a thickness of 0.8 mm, making it suitable for integration into smart textiles. The waveguide features 13 resonating loops on the top layer and 13 complementary loops on the bottom layer, arranged in a face-to-face configuration to enable strong inter-layer magnetic coupling. This arrangement supports multiple inductively coupled modes, achieving quad-band operation at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.6,3.25,3.96$</tex>, and 4.55 GHz. Full-wave simulations, including reflection <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\mathrm{S}_{11} / \mathrm{S}_{22}\right)$</tex> and transmission <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\mathrm{S}_{21} / \mathrm{S}_{12}\right)$</tex> analyses, demonstrate efficient energy transfer, low reflection, and stable propagation under bending conditions. Additionally, specific absorption rate (SAR) evaluations on circular and rectangular human-body phantoms confirm that the design meets wearable safety limits. Compact size, multi-band performance, and mechanical flexibility make the proposed planar MI waveguide a promising solution for wearable sensing, on-body communication, and short-range wireless interconnects.

title: Enhanced Navigational & Operational Vision-Assistive Hardware with Raspberry Pi
authors: Ashwin K B, Godwin Sam Paul J, Poornima M
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: This is a wearable assistive device with AI technology, designed to empower people with visual and hearing impairments to provide real-time navigation, communication, and assistance. The device merges obstacle detection, speech-to-text, and sign language into a single compact, wearable, and ease-of-use device.NOVAH is driven by an ESP32-CAM, Raspberry Pi Zero, vibration motor, and see-through OLED screen to provide multimodal feedback (e.g., haptic alerts, on-screen text alerts, and intelligent AI-based assist). Through using computer vision, natural language understanding, and lean machine learning models, the tool runs effectively under a range of environmental conditions, ensuring that users can engage with their surroundings safely and communicate with others. Obstacle detection helps visually impaired users with timely notification, and real-time speech-to-text transcription helps users with hearing loss keep up with conversations without reliance on external devices. Also, NOVAH's sign language translation unit fills communication gaps permitting effortless two-way exchange between the hearing-impaired and non-signing people.By uniting these heterogeneous capabilities in a portable and low-cost solution NOVAH fills some of the most important gaps in current assistive technologies, which tend to target discrete functions. Initial experimental testing proves its efficacy in maximising independence, inclusivity, and quality of life making it a promising move towards accessible AI-powered wearables for sensory challenged individuals. Keywords: Obstacle Detection, Speech Transcription, Sign Language Translation, AI Wearable, ESP32-CAM, Inclusive Design

title: Design and Testing Method for a Hardware-in-the-Loop Simulation Platform of Grid-forming Energy Storage Converters
authors: Lingfeng Wei, Yibin Tao, Haoyuan Li, Guowei Chen, Yuanchuang Sun, Daquan Tao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy (ICSGE)
abstract: With the increasingly serious problem of grid stability under the high penetration rate of new energy, grid based energy storage is gradually entering the stage of large-scale application due to its advantages of stable operation in weak grids and providing inertia support to the grid. This article studies the design and testing method of a semi physical simulation platform for grid type energy storage inverters. By constructing a semi physical model for grid connected simulation testing, further verify the control strategy of the grid type energy storage system. This method can avoid the risk of hardware failure, improve testing safety, reduce testing costs, and avoid the negative impact of harmonics generated during the testing process on the power quality of the grid.

title: Design of a Low Phase-Noise X-Band Oscillator Employing a SIW Cavity Resonator and Negative-Resistance Technique
authors: Ali Osman Karali, Ahmed H. Akgiray
publish: 2026 Symposium on Microwave, Antenna, and Propagation (SMAP)
abstract: This paper presents the design and simulation of a low-phase-noise X-band oscillator based on a negative-resistance topology incorporating an SIW resonator. Compact X-band oscillators are widely used in communication and radar systems, where phase-noise performance is a critical parameter. Three SIW resonator configurations are designed, and oscillator circuits employing each resonator are evaluated through linear and nonlinear simulations. Using the negative-resistance method, the start-up conditions are analyzed, and harmonic-balance simulations are performed to determine the final oscillator performance. The proposed design achieves oscillation at 10.496 GHz with an output power of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5. 0 5 ~ d B m}$</tex> and demonstrates an FOM of -225.35 (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{dBc} / \text{Hz}$</tex>) at a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1}-\text{MHz}$</tex> offset. The oscillator utilizes the Infineon BFP620F SiGe HBT device.

title: Edge-Adaptive MIMO Beamforming using Reinforced Deep Signal Optimization for 6G Networks
authors: Ansar Isak Sheikh, N. Sripriya, M. Rajesh Khanna, Minu Meera M, R. Thiagarajan, R. Krishnamoorthy
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The swift development of wireless communication networks to 6G presents new requirements of ultra-high-speed, ultra-low-latency, and intelligent spectrum sharing. To overcome these difficulties, this paper suggests an Edge-Adaptive MIMO Beamforming system that is driven by the reinforcement of Deep Signal Optimization and in this case with high-mobility environment and dense user setting. The essence of the model uses Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) augmented with attention to adjust beam directions and makes gains on-the-fly. To achieve the goal of optimizing signal propagation environments, reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (RIS) are introduced, and hybrid analog-digital beamforming provides energy and computational efficiency. xperimental data are obtained in realistic 6G sub-THz and mmWave channels to demonstrate the efficiency of the proposed system. The proposed model outperforms all the important metrics with regard to baseline techniques like Zero-Forcing Beamforming and Hybrid Precoding. Namely, it has a spectral efficiency of 11.0 bps/Hz, beamforming gain of 38.9 dB, energy efficiency of 0.90 Gbps/W and sub-9 ms latency in high user density. Moreover, the attention-based DRP agent enhances faster convergence and generalization to the variable channel conditions. The suggested framework will adhere to the requirements of 6G by not only satisfying the harsh physical-layer specifications of 6G but also laying out scalable learning-based paradigm to deploy to an edge. The work brings a robust and adaptive beamforming approach because it combines edge intelligence, signal learning, and federated model updates to the system and thereby contributes to the overall vision of an intelligent 6G network.

title: Performance Evaluation of Deep Learning Models for Intelligent Fuse Fault Diagnosis
authors: Zhenwei E, Hanjin Xue, Donglin Li, Zhengzhi Yu, Mengchen Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: To address the challenges of traditional threshold judgment and wavelet analysis, which rely on manual experience, as well as the feature redundancy and inadequate capture of temporal information in existing deep learning models such as CNN and BiLSTM, this paper proposes a fusion model based on improved convolutional attention and bidirectional LSTM (ICA-BiLSTM). A channel-space dynamic convolutional attention (ICA) module is designed, eliminating the need for additional fully connected layers. It adaptively enhances fault-sensitive features such as voltage sag and temperature surge through local statistics. Combined with a temporal window adaptive BiLSTM layer, the window size is dynamically adjusted based on the fault evolution rate (e.g., a short circuit with instantaneous sudden change and an overload with slow accumulation), accurately capturing temporal dependencies. A simulation platform based on Matlab/Simulink was constructed to collect 8,600 samples for four conditions: normal, overload, short circuit, and poor contact. Gaussian noise of 10%-30% was added to simulate complex operating conditions. Experiments show that the model achieves a test set accuracy of 98.76 %, an improvement of 6.61, 4.44, and 2.18 percentage points over the CNN, BiLSTM, and CNN-BiLSTM models, respectively. Even with 30% noise, the accuracy remains at 95.33 %, and the single-sample inference time is 0.8ms. This meets engineering real-time and interference-resistance requirements, providing a reliable technical solution for smart fuse fault diagnosis.

title: Dynamic Route Optimization with Predictive Weather conditions
authors: S Priyadarshini, S P Suraj, Subramanya Hebbar, Damian Angelus, N Monisha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: Travel routes and weather conditions both change often, and when considered together, they greatly influence how safe and efficient transportation can be. Yet, most popular apps, like Google Maps for navigation and AccuWeather for weather updates, work separately. This means people usually have to manually check the weather against their travel plans, which can be inconvenient and time-consuming. This disconnected method often leads to wasted time, longer journeys, and even safety risks, especially when conditions evolve quickly. To address this, the study introduces an Integrated WeatherAware Route Optimisation Dashboard (IWARD) — a smart platform that merges real-time route planning with up-to-date weather forecasts. By pulling in live data through APIs, analyzing historical patterns, and using advanced machine learning techniques like Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and Reinforcement Learning, IWARD predicts localized weather and adjusts travel routes on the fly, making trips safer and more efficient.

title: Research on Foreign Body Image Recognition Technology of High Voltage Overhead Transmission Line Based on Faster R-CNN Algorithm
authors: Yige Chen, Ye Liu, Huirong Zhang, Zhiyong Chen
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: This study focuses on the image recognition of foreign bodies in high voltage overhead transmission lines, and proposes a technology based on Faster R-CNN algorithm. High-voltage overhead transmission lines are easily invaded by foreign bodies such as bird nesting and kite winding, which leads to accidents. Traditional detection methods have many limitations. Faster R-CNN has become an ideal detection algorithm because of its high accuracy and real-time performance. In this study, 10,000+UAV inspection images covering various scenes and foreign body types were collected to build a data set, and data enhancement methods were used to improve the robustness of the model. In the model architecture, ResNet-50-FPN is selected as the backbone network, and the detection performance of foreign objects is enhanced by introducing FPN structure, optimizing anchor frame parameters and adopting OHEM mechanism. In training, reasonable loss function is designed, Focal Loss is introduced to alleviate category imbalance, and various optimization strategies are applied to improve training effect. The experimental results show that the improved model achieves 89.7% in the test set, the recall rate of plastic bags is 89.6%, the accuracy rate of bird's nest is 94.2%, the model size is compressed to 72MB, and it is stable in different background complexity scenes, which is significantly superior to the original Faster R-CNN and other mainstream detection algorithms, effectively improving the accuracy and real-time performance of foreign body detection for high-voltage overhead transmission lines, and providing an efficient solution for the field of power inspection.

title: Construction of a Heart Disease Risk Prediction Model Based on Multi-Dimensional Features
authors: Fan Shang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: To address the insufficient accuracy of clinical single-indicator assessments of heart disease risk and the lack of convenient assessment tools at the primary care level, this study constructs a multi-dimensional prediction model using 70,000 cardiovascular disease physical examination and followup records from the Tianchi platform and optimizes its performance. Data preprocessing applied <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3} \sigma$</tex> treatment to continuous variables; feature engineering constructed derived features such as BMI and pulse pressure, while retaining original indicators to ensure clinical interpretability. Using 5fold stratified cross-validation, logistic regression, random forest, XGBoost, and LightGBM models were compared. After Platt Scaling probability calibration, the XGBoost model performed best, with a test set AUC of 0.8012, F1-score of 0.7425, Brier score of 0.1807, and a recall rate as high as 0.8329, reducing the risk of missed diagnosis. A feature importance analysis was conducted to assess the contribution of multidimensional features. The study shows that systolic blood pressure, diastolic blood pressure, and pulse pressure contribute over 67%, serving as core risk factors. Although limited by cross-sectional data, the model balances accuracy and practicality and can be developed into a primary care risk scoring table or embedded in medical systems, providing a digital solution for primary prevention of cardiovascular diseases.

title: Overview of Fluid Antenna Systems for Reliable 6G UAV Communications
authors: Yayun Qu, Bin Xue, Lin Zhou, Weizhe Huang
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: The evolution of 6G towards higher frequency bands and the integration of air, space, and sea domains pose significant challenges to dynamic channel matching, interference suppression, and link reliability in unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) communications. Fluid Antenna Systems (FAS) offer an innovative approach to addressing these challenges by leveraging the reconfigurable nature of antenna port positions. This article reviews the research progress of FAS for 6G UAV communications, explaining its fundamental principles and key design elements. It focuses on the unique constraints of UAV platform integration and analyzes the performance potential of FAS in enhancing spectrum efficiency, link reliability, and anti-interference capabilities. Furthermore, the article discusses current technological bottlenecks and suggests future research directions to support the development of high-performance antennas for 6G UAVs.

title: Accurate Detection Technology of Ultrasonic Partial Discharge in Airborne Overhead Lines Based on Wavelet Transform
authors: Yuqian Shi, Yongjie Zhu, Jie Mao, Ye Xu, Weibin Xue
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: As a core component of the power system, the operating status of overhead lines directly determines the safety and stability of power supply. Partial discharge (PD) is an early indicator of insulation degradation in power equipment. Timely and accurate detection of this phenomenon is of great significance for preventing equipment failures and reducing operation and maintenance costs. To address the issues of weak anti-interference capability and insufficient detection accuracy of traditional detection methods in complex environments, this paper proposes an airborne ultrasonic precise detection technology for PD in overhead line power equipment based on a wavelet transform signal enhancement algorithm. Experimental results show that the detection system can effectively suppress low-frequency noise below <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 0 k H z$</tex> and achieve accurate signal extraction within the characteristic frequency range of 20-35 kHz for PD. The closer the sensor is to the PD source, the stronger the amplitude of the frequency-domain signal; precise positioning of PD can be achieved through attitude adjustment. The system's false alarm rate and missed alarm rate are significantly reduced, and the efficiency of fault diagnosis is greatly improved, providing reliable technical support for the operation and maintenance of overhead line power equipment.

title: Efficient Model Predictive Control for 4-D Flight Trajectory Tracking Using Particle Filtering-Based Bayesian Recurrent Neural Network
authors: Yuhang Wang, Jingyao Zhang, Peng Zhao, Kaiquan Cai
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: Ultra-precise 4-D flight trajectory tracking stands as a foundational element for boosting trajectory predictability and further enhancing the operational performance of air traffic management (ATM) systems-aligning with the industry’s shift toward data-driven, efficiency-centric operations. Yet, tracking accuracy is inherently undermined by wind-induced variability. To sustain reliable tracking capabilities amid such challenges, an innovative model predictive control (MPC) architecture is introduced, incorporating a probabilistic wind estimation component built on a Bayesian recurrent neural network (BRNN) paired with a particle filter (PF) into the control development process. This combination shapes a stochastic optimal control problem (OCP) featuring preset bounds on the likelihood of trajectory deviations. Concurrently, the computational load linked to solving this intricate stochastic OCP hinders real-time deployment on flight management systems (FMS) with limited resources-a critical barrier as ATM evolves toward more responsive, on-board decision-making. To alleviate this computational strain, inequality constraints tied to system states are mapped from the state domain to the input domain through constraint conversion. The effectiveness of this novel approach is verified via simulation tests focused on 4-D flight trajectory tracking, leveraging high-fidelity data sources that reflect the ATM sector’s increasing reliance on authentic, large-scale datasets for technology validation.

title: JPEG Image Wireless Transmission System Based on CC2530
authors: Xiangyuan Yin, Sunhao Song
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: In recent years, as governments worldwide have increasingly focused on Internet of Things (IoT) technology, the research, development, and application of wireless sensor networks (WSNs) have also seen significant progress. The most widely used routing protocol for WSNs in practical applications is the ZigBee protocol. The paper proposed WSN application systems which using the ZigBee protocol for Jpeg image transmission. The image acquisition and compression of the system can be fulfilled by ov7640 and JPEG compress module(OV528). The routing and transmission section is handled by the ZigBee module(CC2530). Because of using multi-hop information transmission technology and Mesh network topology structure, the system greatly increasing the image monitoring range and reduce usage costs.

title: DHMCNN-RV1: A Deep Hybrid Morphological–Convolutional Network with Morphological Residual Enhancement for Infrared Small-UAV Detection
authors: Yifan Bu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: This paper targets infrared (IR) small-UAV detection and classification. To address the issue that morphological top-hat differencing, while enhancing local high-intensity responses, may cause feature degradation and undermine localization stability, we propose an framework, DHMCNN-RV1. The method builds upon a stronger hierarchical semantic backbone for robust feature extraction, and formulates a learnable white top-hat as an independent saliency-enhancement branch. Through residual/compensatory feature fusion, the morphology-induced saliency cues are injected into the backbone representation in a controlled manner, achieving a balance between interpretable saliency modeling and the preservation of continuous structural information. In addition, we incorporate multi-scale context aggregation and a decoupled detection head to jointly optimize classification discrimination and bounding-box regression. Grouped evaluations on a public IR UAV dataset demonstrate that the proposed approach remains adaptive and robust across different distance scales, offering a practical reference.

title: Dynamic Control of a Multi-Input DC - DC Converter for Hybrid Renewable Sources
authors: K. Venkatapathi, E. Parimalasundar
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The growing number of renewable sources that are connected to modern power systems requires the use of efficient power electronic interfaces that can accommodate several input sources with different voltage levels and dynamic characteristics. The common single input and cascade structure of the DC-DC converter is disadvantageous, complex in components, large in power loss, low in flexibility of power management and the like. The design and the analysis of a multi-input DC-DC converter for hybrids renewable energy system is the objective scope of this work. The structure of the proposed topology allows directly connecting 3 input sources rated with 24 V, 48 V and 12 V to a single conversion stage in order to obtain a regulated DC output voltage level of almost ±96V. The converter works in continuous conduction mode imposed by the dimensions of the inductors L<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf>=2mH; L<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>=5mH; and C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> =2200µF assuring low current ripple79 and good stability of the different available outputs. The switching behaviour and energy transfer mechanisms are investigated through a detailed mode-of-operations analysis at various operating conditions. The system is via a switching frequency of 25 kHz and the duty ratio that varies from 0.4-0.6 to give good voltage regulation gating. Simulated results reveal high output voltage regulation less than ±1.8% for any load variation and peak inductor current ripple does not exceed 15%. The designed converter is capable of output powers of 120 W, 230 W, and 320 W for light rated and high load conditions, respectively, with an efficiency higher than 95.6% at the rated load condition; the efficiency does not fall under 92% even at light loads. Dynamic response behaviour shows a voltage recovery time of 2.1ms during step load changes. A comparative study verifies that the proposed converter offers higher efficiency, a smaller number of components and better suitability for renewable energy systems, distributed generation and electric vehicle power management applications.

title: Digital Information Flows and Metrological Reliability of Data in the Preparation of Textile Production
authors: Munira Sadikova, Mukhammadmullo Asraev, Avazbek Karimov, Jamoldin Nazirkulov
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Science and Communications Technologies (ICISCT)
abstract: The article examines the formation and transmission of information flows at the stage of textile production preparation, as well as the influence of the metrological characteristics of raw materials on the reliability of digital data. As an example, a computer-based method for measuring the length of single fibers from a digital image is analyzed within an information exchange circuit integrating measuring equipment, LIMS, ERP and MES systems. The main types of errors – instrumental, pixel, methodological and rounding – are assessed. It is shown that the final measurement error is approximately $1.59 \%$, which ensures sufficient accuracy for automated production preparation and subsequent integration of data into the company’s digital platforms. Key mechanisms of information circulation affecting planning, raw material sorting and technological mode management are highlighted. The results confirm that metrologically validated digital fiber measurements can serve as a reliable basis for building an end-to-end information flow in the textile industry.

title: High-Quality Datasets for Embodied Intelligence: Key Challenges and Construction Pathways
authors: Qiushi Chen, Jing Yu, Lingjun Fan, Quanbo Long
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Embodied intelligence relies fundamentally on high-quality multimodal interaction data. However, existing datasets remain limited in terms of scale, realism, and task complexity. Although prior studies have independently recognized the importance of synthetic data generation, standardized evaluation, and causal modeling, few efforts have systematically integrate these core dimensions or conceptualized datasets from an ecological perspective as an "evolutionary engine" for embodied intelligence. This paper argues, for the first time, that future high-quality datasets for embodied intelligence should be developed under a synergistic framework spanning five dimensions: realism, task complexity, multimodal alignment, adaptability, and scalability. This framework not only provides an integrated perspective for dataset evaluation and construction, but also analyzes concrete pathways and methods across the stages of data acquisition, annotation, management, and usage. The ultimate goal is to promote a paradigm shift—from static training repositories toward dynamic knowledge systems that continuously drive lifelong learning and evolution in embodied intelligent agents.

title: Lightweight ResNet50 Optimization for Remote Sensing Land Use Image Recognition
authors: Yiman Shan, Xuan Hua
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Land use classification is a critical task in remote sensing image analysis, underpinning urban planning, environmental monitoring, and resource management. However, traditional methods face low efficiency and poor adaptability in processing high-resolution remote sensing imagery, making accurate and efficient land use recognition a key challenge. Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) have shown remarkable image classification performance, and lightweight model design has become a core research focus for resource-constrained deployment. In this work, an optimized lightweight ResNet50 model based on transfer learning is proposed for precise land use recognition. It integrates depthwise separable convolutions and channel width scaling to reduce redundant parameters and computational complexity. Experiments are conducted on a public high-resolution remote sensing dataset (2100 images, 21 categories with 100 images each, split 8:2 into training/validation sets) and compared with the conventional ResNet50. Results show the proposed model achieves superior performance with a training accuracy of 95%.

title: Research on Blockchain Collaborative Optimization of Virtual Power Plant Auxiliary Service Market in New Power System
authors: Yanbo Wang, Yingkun Han, Yan Ma
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: A dynamic game optimization algorithm based on hierarchical alliance chain is proposed, which aims to realize efficient cooperative optimization. Through MATLAB and Hyperledger Fabric, this paper establishes a simulation experimental platform, which is composed of 12 VHP and 8 PV companies. It is proved by experiments that this algorithm is superior in cost control, transaction efficiency and stability. In the peak load period, the total cost of the system can be reduced by 28.7% compared with the traditional centralized optimization algorithm. The result is that the algorithm achieves accurate scheduling of resources and reduces the transaction cost by the blockchain smart contract. Therefore, the rate of renewable energy can be increased by 27.1%, which can effectively promote the use of clean energy. As far as transaction efficiency is concerned, it takes only 0.72 seconds to deal with the transaction size of 1,000, and it is more than 61.1% higher than that of the old PBFT. Under the extreme circumstance of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 1 \%}$</tex> computing power attack, the abnormal transaction interception rate was 98.7%, the data consistency was maintained 99.2%, and the transaction continuity was kept high. It is proved that this algorithm provides a new and practical solution for the auxiliary service market of VSE, which can effectively promote the steady running and development of the new electric power system.

title: PUF-Assisted Secure Device Access for Industrial Control Crowdsourcing Platforms
authors: Xin Liu, Yuan Gao, Lei Li, Rui Wang, Donglan Liu, Hao Zhang, Yuntao Wang, Zhou Su
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: Industrial control crowdsourcing platforms require scalable device onboarding and repeated authenticated interactions, yet heterogeneous industrial endpoints are often resourceconstrained and vulnerable to long-term key exposure under physical compromise. This paper presents a practical, end-toend device access protocol that integrates physically unclonable functions (PUFs) with the ShangMi (SM) cryptographic suite to provide clone-resistant authentication, forward-secure channel establishment, and fine-grained authorization. The device derives SM2 private-key material on demand from on-chip PUF entropy, avoiding persistent plaintext secret storage, while the platform authenticates ephemeral SM2-ECDH handshakes via signatures under a pinned platform certificate to prevent man-in-the-middle attacks. After session authentication, a resource-level secondary authorization stage encodes device-to-resource permissions using a counting Bloom filter (CBF), enabling compact policy representation, constant-time membership checks, and auditable authorization decisions at scale. We analyze security against DolevYao adversaries, showing resistance to impersonation, replay, and key-compromise impacts under standard assumptions. A prototype and a calibrated TLS1.3 execution model are deployed, which demonstrates that our approach can consistently lower communication and end-to-end latency than TLS1.3, especially when authorization requests are frequent and network bandwidth is constrained.

title: Fine-Tuning Segment Anything Model (SAM) for Brain Tumor Image Segmentation
authors: Zahra Faska, Lahbib Khrissi, Imadeddine Mountasser, Khalid Haddouch
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: Automatic segmentation of the magnetic resonance images has become one of the leading tasks in medical image analysis to facilitate a more accurate and faster identification of brain tumors. Even though this task has been greatly boosted by U-Net-like architectures based on convolutional neural networks, recently developed foundation models for adaptable image segmentation have opened new opportunities. In this paper, we investigate MRI brain tumor segmentation using SAM. Different from the existing single-task methods, SAM enables flexible prompt-based adaptation to various segmentation tasks with very limited human effort through prompts Fine-tuned SAM was tested on an LGG dataset from Kaggle. The proposed approach achieved a mean intersection over union IoU of 0.6574 and a mean loss value of 0.0053 which clearly places it among the top performing methods in segmentation. This makes SAM as a strong, generalizable framework for medical image segmentation with high potential to be used in computer aided diagnosis systems in multiple clinical domains.

title: Predicting Future CSI Feedback for Highly-Mobile Massive MIMO Systems
authors: Yu Zhang, Ahmed Alkhateeb, Pranav Madadi, Jeongho Jeon, Joonyoung Cho, Charlie Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: Massive multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) system is promising in providing unprecedentedly high data rate. To achieve its full potential, the transceiver needs complete and instantaneous channel state information (CSI) to perform transmit/receive precoding/combining. This requirement, however, is challenging in the practical systems due to the unavoidable processing and feedback delays. As a result, such delays could degrade the system performance to a great extent, which is normally the case in the high mobility scenarios. In this paper, we develop a deep learning based channel prediction framework that proactively predicts the downlink CSI based on the past observed channel sequence. In its core, the model adopts a 3-D convolutional neural network (CNN) based architecture to efficiently learn the temporal, spatial and frequency correlations of downlink channel samples. The identified correlations are utilized to perform accurate channel predictions. Simulation results highlight the potential of the developed learning model in extracting information and predicting future downlink channels directly from the observed past channel sequence, which significantly improves the performance compared to the sample-and-hold approach, and mitigates the impact of the dynamic communication environment.

title: A Deep Learning–Enhanced Adaptive Intrusion Detection Framework for Real-Time Cyber Security Threat Analysis
authors: B. Vijay, Chandra J, Nagendra N, G. Shobana, S. Karunya, Saranya K
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The rapidly evolving nature of cyber-attacks significantly reduces the effectiveness of conventional intrusion detection systems (IDS) that rely on static rules and signatures. This work presents an adaptive deep learning– based intrusion detection framework designed to maintain reliable performance in real-time environments affected by concept drift. The proposed approach integrates one-dimensional convolutional neural networks (1D-CNN) for local feature interaction learning with a bidirectional long short-term memory (BiLSTM) network to model sequential network traffic behavior. To address evolving attack patterns, a sliding-window–based incremental learning mechanism is employed, enabling continuous model adaptation to recent traffic characteristics. The model is trained using cross-entropy loss optimized with the Adam optimizer, while dropout regularization is applied to reduce overfitting and ensure fast convergence. To enhance transparency and analyst trust, explainable artificial intelligence techniques are incorporated, including SHAP-based feature attribution and an attention mechanism for interpreting temporal dependencies. Experimental evaluation on labeled network traffic data demonstrates stable convergence, consistent detection accuracy under changing traffic conditions, and improved robustness compared to non-adaptive baseline models. These results confirm the effectiveness and practical applicability of the proposed framework for real-time and interpretable cybersecurity intrusion detection.
