title: A Novel Gigawatt-Scale System and Method for Low-Frequency Transmission and Grid Integration of Large-Scale Renewable Energy Sources
authors: Qingjian Wang, Yonghuan Liu, Yang Huang, Wei Ding, Zhengyang Pei, Ruoxi Fan, Tiantian He, Junyuan Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy (ICSGE)
abstract: This paper introduces a novel gigawatt (GW)-scale system for low-frequency (LF) transmission and grid integration of large-scale renewable energy sources, addressing the intermittency issues inherent in hybrid wind-solar power generation. By incorporating modular multilevel matrix converters (M3C), the proposed approach enables efficient LF AC power delivery. The configuration encompasses 2 GW onshore photovoltaic plants(OPVP) and three 3 GW offshore wind farms, interconnected LF submarine cables and overhead lines to M3C, which subsequently transform the output to industrial frequency (IF) for integration into a 500 kV grid. For the OPVP, LCL-based(LCL) inverter controls are employed to maintain DC voltage and inner/outer loop power regulation. In the wind farms, a coordinated strategy based on permanent magnet direct-drive turbines ensures voltage stability and optimized power extraction. To accommodate capacity scaling, two distinct M3C deployment schemes are devised: parallel LF overhead lines and valve-side paralleling. Complementary master-slave control and optimization mechanisms are developed, incorporating dynamic power sharing and phase-difference compensation to achieve balanced load distribution, circulating current mitigation, and sustained voltage equilibrium. Simulations conducted in PSCAD/EMTDC demonstrate that the system preserves voltage stability during ramp-up to 5 GW output, facilitating uniform power delivery. The findings indicate reduced energy losses, enhanced dynamic performance, and suitability for integrating diverse renewable sources, underscoring its potential for practical engineering applications.

title: Research on Sensing and Positioning Technology Based on Integrated Sensing and Communication for 6G
authors: Mingfei Wang, Xiuying Yang, Weiguang Tian, Jiahui Mi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: To address the challenges posed by random channel fading, multipath effects, and Doppler shift in user equipment (UE) sensing and positioning within 6G Integrated Sensing and Communications (ISAC) networks under high-speed mobility scenarios, this paper proposes a high-precision sensing and positioning method. The core innovation lies in enhancing positioning accuracy through scenario-adaptive modeling and joint optimization estimation and suppression of Indirect Path (IDP) interference. Specifically, millimeter-wave waveforms are employed to construct an ISAC system model tailored for high-speed mobile environments, with simultaneous establishment of communication and sensing channel models alongside signal models. By deeply exploiting channel characteristics, we integrate UE position, velocity, and channel state information (CSI) into a unified estimation framework. Through equivalent channel modeling, exploitation of convex substructures, and alternating iterative optimization, the non-convex estimation problem is transformed into a convex formulation and solved via gradient descent algorithms. System simulation results demonstrate the method’s superior performance in positioning error control: Comparative analysis of antenna configurations, subcarrier parameters, and algorithmic benchmarks confirms its effectiveness in mitigating adverse channel effects in high-mobility scenarios, thereby establishing a technical foundation for high-precision positioning applications in 6G ISAC networks.

title: Power Grid Investment Demand Forecasting Model Based on Differential Evolution Optimization Support Vector Machine
authors: Zhengxiong Long, Weifeng Hu, Yuchen Zhang
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: A Differential Evolution (DE) optimized Support Vector Machine (SVM) model for forecasting power grid investment (PGI) demand, referred to as DE-SVM, is proposed. Accurate prediction of PGI demand is crucial for investment decisions and resource optimization within electric utilities. Traditional forecasting methods, however, have limitations when dealing with the non-linear, high-dimensional, and dynamic nature of PGI demands. In response to these challenges, this study integrates the DE algorithm with SVM technology, leveraging DE to refine the SVM parameters and thereby boosting the model's forecasting precision and its capacity to generalize. Analysis based on data from 2010 through 2023 indicates that the DE-SVM framework surpasses traditional methods—including regression models, time series forecasts, grey system theory, and artificial neural networks (ANN)—in performance metrics such as MSE, MAE, and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. This approach is adept at managing the intricacies and unpredictability of PGI needs, offering a robust foundation for guiding investment decisions within the electricity sector.

title: Fast and Efficient Hardware-Accelerated Lightweight CNN for Lung Disease Detection Using ARM Compute Library
authors: Emna Aridhi, Kaouther Laabidi, Abdelkader Mami
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: Deep learning models based on Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) excel in AI-driven image classification but pose challenges for medical edge devices due to high computational and memory demands. This paper presents a lightweight CNN for detecting lung opacity, COVID-19, and viral pneumonia using X-ray images. To address these challenges, we integrated the ARM Compute Library (ARM CL) for the first time to accelerate inference on the FPGA Digilent ZedBoard. The ARM CL provides optimized functions for ARM architectures, including Single Instruction, Multiple Data (SIMD) instructions, multithreading, and memory optimization, to enhance performance. It also supports reduced precision to improve efficiency. Our embedded model achieves a $3.35 \times$ reduction in inference time compared to CPU-based implementations and a $2 \times$ improvement without ARM CL. The benchmarking against GPU and TensorFlow Lite (TFLite) models demonstrated superior energy efficiency, reaching 18.44 GOPS/W compared to 0.96 GOPS/W for GPUs and 0.09 GOPS/W for TFLite, while maintaining 99% prediction accuracy. This study highlights the effectiveness of combining lightweight CNN models with ARM CL for fast, efficient, and accurate lung disease detection on medical edge devices, thereby providing a practical framework for advancing AI-driven healthcare applications in resourceconstrained environments.

title: Vehicle to Grid Systems for Optimized Demand Response & Grid Stability Improvement Based on Deep Learning
authors: Sindhu Anbuselvan, Kamalakannan Govindasamy, Beema Thangarajan Ramesh Babu, Pavan-Kumar-Reddy Police, Mantri Nirmala, Sreedhar Naik
abstract: The rapid integration of electric vehicles (EVs) into smart grid infrastructure has elevated the need for accurate, real-time decision-making in Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) systems. Predicting V2G behavior based on electricity price, state-of charge (SOC), and grid demand is essential for optimizing energy flows and supporting grid stability. This paper proposes a novel deep learning model that combines one-dimensional convolutional layers with Gated Recurrent Units (Conv1D-GRU) to perform multi-class classification of V2G states. The model is trained and evaluated on a unique 4-year public dataset from the Indian power grid, comprising hourly records of grid demand, SOC levels, and electricity prices offering a comprehensive representation of real-world operational dynamics. Results demonstrate that the proposed Conv1D-GRU model outperforms traditional LSTM, GRU, and Conv1D-LSTM architectures. Compared to state-of-the-art models, our approach delivers superior predictive performance while maintaining a lightweight and scalable architecture. This work represents the first use of a Conv1D-GRU framework for V2G classification on a multivariate Indian grid dataset, and the results provide compelling evidence for its applicability in energy systems.

title: NCFET-based NAND Gate Design and Timing Robustness Analysis Under Ferroelectric Thickness Disturbance
authors: Zichun Xia
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: In this paper, a full-link analysis ranging from device model to gate-level performance is proposed for the design of two-input NAND gates based on NCFET-CMOS and ferroelectric layer thickness disturbance introduced by the process. The Landau-Khalatnikov ferroelectric domain dynamics model is coupled with the Virtual Source channel model under the charge conservation framework to establish a compact model that can be used in SPICE and complete the calibration within the target thickness window. Taking $\pm 20 \%$ fluctuation of ferroelectric layer thickness (tFE) as the only random variable, the characteristics of NCFET devices are analyzed by Monte Carlo sampling, with the logic functional waveform and propagation delay evaluated on two-input NAND gate circuits. According to the results, the ferroelectric thickness disturbance makes the output waveform and propagation delay of the NAND gate shift by a picosecond. Meanwhile, the large coefficient of variation (CV) reveals that the circuit robustness deteriorates with the increasing fluctuation of tFE, which indicates that the consistent ferroelectric layer thickness of the device is crucial to the circuit timing stability.

title: Reinforcement Learning for Dual Constraint Bidding in Display Advertising
authors: Quan Yu, Yuan Zhang, Yisong Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Real-time bidding (RTB) is key in online display advertising, but traditional strategies struggle with budget and ROI constraints. We propose Dual Constrained Bidding (DCB), which tailors bids to each advertiser’s specific needs using a Lagrangian binary function. Model-free reinforcement learning (RL) fine-tunes the parameters for optimal results. Experiments show DCB outperforms existing algorithms.

title: Performance Evaluation of Linear Primary Permanent Magnet Vernier Generators for Ocean Wave Energy Conversion
authors: Shahryar Haghvirdiloo, Jawad Faiz, Mehrage Ghods
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: Permanent Magnet (PM) Vernier machines are highly attractive for low-speed, direct-drive applications due to their magnetic gearing effect, which delivers high thrust density without a mechanical gearbox. However, optimizing the magnet arrangement is crucial for maximizing performance and mitigating undesirable forces. This paper presents a systematic performance analysis and direct comparison of linear PM Vernier generators featuring various magnet topologies. Specifically, existing surface-mounted consequent-pole, Halbach consequent-pole, and V-shaped consequent-pole arrangements are investigated against a newly proposed U-shaped consequent-pole PM topology. All generator designs are modeled and analyzed using the finite element analysis method, ensuring an equivalent volume of active materials for a fair comparison. The no-load performance characteristics, including back-electromotive force, cogging force, and normal force are evaluated for each design. The results demonstrate that the proposed U-shaped consequent-pole arrangement achieves significantly better overall performance in the considered metrics compared to the existing topologies, validating its potential for highly efficient and robust linear direct-drive applications.

title: Active Equalization for an Integrated Bidirectional Electric Vehicle Charger Using a Hybrid Model Predictive and Reinforcement Learning Framework
authors: Peyman Bayat, Pezhman Bayat
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: The escalating global adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) necessitates the development of advanced battery charging systems that deliver not only high efficiency but also proactive battery health management through effective charge equalization. This paper introduces a novel bidirectional battery charger architecture that seamlessly integrates a modular charge equalization circuit into its core power conversion stage. The system’s performance is optimized by an innovative adaptive predictive equalization control (APEC), which synergistically combines the predictive capabilities of model predictive control (MPC) with the adaptive level of reinforcement learning (RL). A key differentiator of this design is its unified control framework, which consolidates the traditionally separate functions of battery charging and cell equalization. This integration facilitates rapid active balancing of cell voltages, minimizes input current ripple, and provides precise reactive power support to the grid. A comprehensive simulation analysis validates the proposed APEC methodology, demonstrating outstanding performance metrics: a peak system efficiency of 94.2%, a cell equalization time of less than 15 minutes, and notable improvements in additional system parameters during both grid-to-vehicle and vehicle-to-grid operational modes. The results clearly demonstrate that the proposed charger, integrating MPC with RL, significantly outperforms existing state-of-the-art solutions, setting a new benchmark in speed, efficiency, and operational reliability for next-generation EV power electronics.

title: Gamma radiation testing of reduced-clad Erbium-doped fibers and demonstration of an ultra-compact EDFA for satellite laser communications
authors: Ahmed Osman, Giannis Psyllakis, Ilias Sourikopoulos, Theodoros M. Triantis, Leontios Stampoulidis
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: We present reduced-clad (RC) Erbium-doped fiber (EDF) gamma radiation testing performed within the scope of developing a very compact, radiation resistant 20 dBm EDFA module. We report passive radiation testing at 50 and 100 krad TID of 3 RC-EDFs. We present bread-board-level characterization with radiation-induced gain drop (RIGD) varying between 1.2 and 4.9 dB at 50 krad and 2.2 and 9.1 dB at 100 krad. We also present the assembly, integration and functional testing of a very compact EDFA prototype, which deploys one of the RC-EDFs. The unit occupies 12.6 cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> footprint and weighs < 33 grams. The module hosts pump lasers, isolators, pump/signal combiners and monitor photodiodes. The EDFA delivers 20 dBm output power over a wavelength range of 1530 to 1570 nm at room temperature. We present preliminary thermal results of the EDFA delivering >18 dBm output power when mounted on a hot plate set at temperatures up to 75°C.

title: SmartSiteAI: Predictive Commercial Zoning via Crowdsourced Location Intelligence
authors: Abdalla Adam, Abd-Elhamid M Taha
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: We present SmartSiteAI, a system for predictive commercial zoning that leverages crowdsourced location data to support decision intelligence in smart cities. Using aggregated public engagement signals, SmartSiteAI infers the expected performance of new business locations and recommends optimal commercial zones. The system integrates geospatial clustering with a supervised machine learning model to capture spatial semantics and user behavior at scale. In a case study across North Riyadh, SmartSiteAI achieved an RMSE of 0.35 and a Top-3 recommendation accuracy of 30%, demonstrating its ability to translate noisy crowdsourced signals into actionable insights. Designed for interpretability and extensibility, the framework supports explainable AI and real-time adaptation, offering a scalable approach to location-aware decision-making in smart city environments.

title: Deep Learning-based Efficient Classification of Skin Diseases using Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Tejinder Kaur, Anjali Thakur, Daljeet Kaur, Sehaj Singh Dhillon, Vijeta Chaudhary, Jotesh Gupta
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: A greater awareness of the importance of early identification in improving patient outcomes has also led to researchers looking into new technology and techniques, which is progressing the disciplines of dermatology and cancer in general. The collaborative efforts of interdisciplinary teams comprising dermatologists, computer scientists, and medical specialists demonstrate the intentional commitment to enhancing the diagnostic capabilities for skin conditions, which will ultimately benefit people and healthcare systems throughout the world. This research presents a new technique for diagnosing skin diseases that emphasizes early detection. Early identification is crucial since it allows for prompt intervention and presents chances for less intrusive and more efficient treatment techniques.

title: Multi-scenario Low-carbon Optimization Scheduling Study for Urban Power Networks Considering Electric Vehicle Charging Demand
authors: Yue Zhao, Yun Yang, Yinguo Yang, Zichao Meng, Gang Luo, Haoyu Tang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy (ICSGE)
abstract: With the rapid growth of photovoltaic generation, energy storage systems, and large-scale electric vehicle (EV) charging loads, the bulk power system is facing increasing uncertainty and operational challenges under low-carbon objectives. To effectively reduce system-level carbon emissions while accommodating large-scale EV charging demand, this paper proposes a low-carbon economic optimal dispatch method for bulk power systems considering coordinated EV charging behavior. A dynamic carbon-emission factor is first introduced to characterize the time-varying carbon intensity of electricity consumption at the system level, enabling accurate quantification of load-side carbon responsibility under different operating conditions. Based on this, a low-carbon economic dispatch model is formulated by jointly optimizing system operating cost and carbon emissions through coordinated state-of-charge (SOC) scheduling of EV loads. Case studies are carried out on a modified IEEE-33 node test system to verify the effectiveness of the proposed method. Taking a typical summer day as an example, the coordinated SOC scheduling strategy reduces the operating cost and carbon emissions of EV-integrated loads by 8.06% and 13.92%, respectively, compared with the uncoordinated charging mode, demonstrating the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed low-carbon dispatch framework for bulk power systems.

title: Inductive Learning Answer Set Programs from Instances and Solutions
authors: Xiang Du, Yisong Wang, Zhongtao Xie, Xu Wang, Hongbo Hu
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Answer set programming (ASP), which relies on answer set or stable model semantics, is a paradigm for declarative problem-solving. This paper proposes a novel inductive learning task for learning answer set programs from input output pairs, where input is an instance of a problem and output is the solutions to this problem instance. By translating such an inductive learning task into an well-established inductive learning task instance of ILASP, we obtain a computing method for the suggested inductive learning tasks. Experimental results on Hamiltonian circuits problems show that the proposed inductive learning task is effective in terms of its both expressiveness and efficiency.

title: Research on Error Correction of an Electric-Carbon Metering Device Based on Multi-Source Data Fusion
authors: Fei Lin, Yong Wang, Dan Huang, Wenqi Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Under the “dual carbon” goals, precise electric-carbon accounting is central to carbon emission management in the power system. Current electric-carbon accounting methods face limitations such as insufficient spatiotemporal accuracy and computational complexity, making it difficult to adapt to the volatility of renewable energy output and differences in regional power source structures. This paper proposes an error correction system based on multi-source data fusion, which integrates data on power generation output, inter-provincial and inter-city electricity exchanges, and carbon emission intensity to construct an accurate dynamic Electricity Carbon Emission Factor (EF) calculation method and an error correction workflow. Using Jiangsu Province as a case study, it details the calculation process for precise EFs and analyzes the spatiotemporal characteristics of the province's EFs from perspectives such as seasonal variations, differences across time periods on a typical day, and distribution across different prefecture-level cities. This system enhances the accuracy of EF measurement, provides robust support for the refined accounting of power-related carbon emissions, offers a more reliable basis for defining carbon emission responsibilities, and creates a prerequisite for optimizing the integration capacity of wind and photovoltaic(PV) power.

title: Optimizing Energy Management Through Vehicle to Grid and Grid To vehicle Integration
authors: Chamakura Krishna Reddy, Balusu Srinivasa Rao, Prasanta Kumar Jena, C. Venkata Subba Reddy, Rampelli Manojkumar, M Prashanth
abstract: This study presents a strategic analysis of Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) and Grid-to-Vehicle (G2V) technologies within a smart energy monitoring framework aimed at enhancing grid efficiency and stability. With the increasing adoption of electric vehicles (EVs), the integration of bidirectional energy exchange mechanisms has become essential for demand-side management. Simulated scenarios using realtime and historical data demonstrate that V2G operations can reduce peak load by 6.54 % and lower energy costs by 1.45 %, offering tangible benefits in grid support and cost optimization. Conversely, G2V charging patterns, while utilizing off-peak energy, lead to a 0.69 % increase in energy consumption, underscoring the need for intelligent scheduling. The study further evaluates the environmental impact of incorporating renewable energy sources, revealing that V2G contributes to emission reduction, whereas G2V shows a marginal increase due to grid dependency. The findings support the coordinated use of V2G and G2V as a viable approach for building resilient, costeffective, and sustainable energy systems.

title: Editorial: Special Issue on DLT for Security, Privacy and Trust, ACM Distributed Ledger Technologies: Research and Practice
authors: Stavros Shiaeles, Nicholas Kolokotronis, Salvatore d’Antonio, Luca Faramondi
publish: Distributed Ledger Technologies: Research and Practice
abstract: None

title: From Offline to Online: Pretrained Dynamic Optimization with Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
authors: Yongbo Ma, Weiwei Yuan, Donghai Guan, Hongsong Peng, Rujiao Wang
abstract: Dynamic optimization enables efficient decision-making in complex, time-varying, and uncertain environments, providing a wide range of application opportunities. However, the existing methods mainly focus on offline dynamic optimization problems. When faced with online optimization scenarios characterized by stronger dynamics and frequent changes that impose stricter time constraints on adaptation, their convergence performance decreases significantly. To fill this gap, we propose a pretrained dynamic optimization framework (PreDOF) for online scenarios. This framework introduces a novel learning paradigm, Evolutionary Markov Decision Process (EMDP), which is designed to harness multi-agents’ real-time decision-making ability effectively. PreDOF has two stages: 1) Offline Pre-training: multi-agent reinforcement learning is employed on large-scale evolutionary data to capture environmental change patterns. The agents are trained in each dynamic environment to guide the evolution directions; 2) Online Fine-tuning: an online agent is adjusted with the offline experience to obtain real-time optimization outcomes. The online agent is trained to join the offline multi-agent via a Weighted Joint Decision strategy, thereby accelerating online convergence and response to changes promptly. Experimental results on benchmarks demonstrate that the proposed framework outperforms the competing methods in terms of both effectiveness and convergence. Furthermore, the real-world case study also reveals the superior performance of our proposed framework in highly constrained and challenging dynamic optimization scenarios.

title: Neural Radiance Field-based Visual Rendering: a Comprehensive Review
authors: Mingyuan Yao, Yukang Huo, Yang Ran, Qingbin Tian, Ruifeng Wang, Haihua Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Neural Radiance Field (NeRF) is a groundbreaking paradigm in neural implicit representations that revolutionized 3D reconstruction, rendering, and dynamic scene modeling. To address cross-domain fragmentation and unclear technical pathways, we present a systematic framework that surveys theoretical foundations, benchmark datasets, methodological advances, and application scenarios. We begin by analyzing NeRF's core mechanisms, including radiance field modeling and differentiable volume rendering, and by defining standardized evaluation benchmarks. Then we chart evolutionary pathways in model optimization, input adaptation, and dynamic scene modeling and analyze how key methods are linked. Furthermore, we provide task-specific insights that highlight migration bottlenecks and potential remedies across digital content creation, embodied perception, and other specialized domains. We also provide comprehensive references and forward-looking guidance for further theoretical refinements and cross-disciplinary deployment of NeRF-based technologies.

title: New Approach of Switching Based Modified Nonlinear ADRC Strategy for Detection and Mitigation of Cyber Threats
authors: Vivek Kumar, Yogesh V. Hote
abstract: The article proposes a switching-based modified nonlinear active disturbance rejection control (ADRC) technique for the load frequency control (LFC) of microgrids under cyber threats, particularly time delay attacks (TDA). The proposed approach incorporates a nonlinear controller & nonlinear extended state observer (NESO), which determines the system's states to mitigate the generalized disturbance of the system. The impact of TDA on the feedback loop of the microgrid system is identified and alleviated by implementing a gradient descent approach (GDA) and a switching-based structure, respectively. The uniqueness of the proposed concept is that the method provides dual capability to improve the frequency regulation: (i) detection of TDA using a GDA-based method and (ii) mitigation of TDA using a switching mechanism. The proposed concept alleviates the delay effect from the process, irrespective of communication delays/cyber threats. However, this is a challenging task for most of the existing controllers. The simulation studies and experimental demonstration via OPAL-RT (OP5600) analyzed the proposed approach's efficacy. Moreover, the effectiveness of the proposed strategy compared to existing methods (SBL-PI, GADRC, & INLADRC) is demonstrated by a thorough study that includes multiple scenarios, such as without TDA, with TDA, parametric variations, system-model discrepancies, step changes & random perturbations in load demands and renewable energy sources, etc. The proposed strategy outperforms existing techniques in all these cases and also demonstrates real-time applicability.

title: Double-Path Quasi-Optical Cavity Enabling Zero-Coupling-Loss Pulse Compression for High-Gain Nanosecond THz Pulse Generation
authors: Zengwen Wang, Shaozhe Zhang, Houxiu Xiao, Xianfei Chen, Zhenxing Wang, Yvting Lu, Xiaotao Han
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: The quasi-optical (QO) pulse compressor based on a linear cavity represents a promising approach for generating nanosecond terahertz (THz) pulses with amplified power and tunable pulsewidth. However, its performance is significantly limited by the coupling loss, which reduces the gain factor. To overcome this limitation, we propose a novel double-path linear cavity for the pulse compressor, which can eliminate the coupling loss and enhance the gain factor. In this configuration, one QO path is used to store the THz energy and generate tunable THz pulses, while the other path is utilized to compensate for the THz coupling loss through destructive interference. A double-wafer laser-driven semiconductor switch (LDSS) with controllable reflectance functions as both the input and output coupler, enabling optimization of the intracavity field distribution for maximum gain. Through simulations and experiments, we investigate the coupling-loss compensation mechanism and the operational conditions of the double-path cavity, demonstrating significant improvement in performance. Experimental results across 230–260GHz show that nearly zero coupling loss is maintained while tuning the pulsewidth from 3 to 15ns. The gain factor reaches up to ~37 under optimal conditions and remains consistently more than double that of a conventional single-path linear cavity. Compared with the results reported in previous literature, the proposed pulse compressor achieves the state-of-the-art performance in both gain factor and pulsewidth tunability.

title: Shape oscillation and 3-D microstreaming profile of a phospholipid-coated microbubble attached to a wall_supp7-3676695.avi
authors: Klazina Kooiman
abstract: Ultrasound-activated microbubbles display diverse dynamics, including shape oscillations and microstreaming, both of which are important for applications in particle transport, drug delivery, and bacterial interventions. Despite advancements, a knowledge gap persists in our understanding of the correlation between shape oscillations, corresponding microstreaming and resulting mechanical impact to neighboring boundaries for microbubbles of clinically relevant sizes. This study investigates this correlation for biotinylated phospholipid-coated microbubbles with radii of 3.0-7.3 μm (N=79) when attached to a streptavidin-coated wall, imaged at 5 million and 10 thousand frames per second to visualize both shape oscillations and microstreaming. Driven at 1.25 MHz frequency, 25,000 cycles and varying peak negative acoustic pressures (170-420 kPa), the larger sized microbubbles showed higher shape modes (up to mode 6) than the smaller ones. A shape stability model predicted the dominant shape mode with an 89% agreement with experimental findings. Four distinct 3-D microstreaming patterns were observed by employing astigmatic particle tracking velocimetry: patternless, radial, dipole and quadrupole. Modal decomposition of shape oscillations revealed that the quadrupole pattern occurred from self-interaction of the dominant shape mode, while the dipole pattern resulted from two strongly interacting shape modes. The quantified microstreaming showed maximum velocities ranging from 2-10 mm/s, which decayed with increasing distance from the microbubble center. Notably, lower shape modes and the quadrupole pattern were associated with higher wall shear stress (up to 13 Pa). These unique 3-D microstreaming profiles have the potential to enhance the efficiency of therapeutic microbubble-mediated ultrasound applications through controlled shape oscillation and mechanical impact.

title: Shape oscillation and 3-D microstreaming profile of a phospholipid-coated microbubble attached to a wall_supp6-3676695.avi
authors: Klazina Kooiman
abstract: Ultrasound-activated microbubbles display diverse dynamics, including shape oscillations and microstreaming, both of which are important for applications in particle transport, drug delivery, and bacterial interventions. Despite advancements, a knowledge gap persists in our understanding of the correlation between shape oscillations, corresponding microstreaming and resulting mechanical impact to neighboring boundaries for microbubbles of clinically relevant sizes. This study investigates this correlation for biotinylated phospholipid-coated microbubbles with radii of 3.0-7.3 μm (N=79) when attached to a streptavidin-coated wall, imaged at 5 million and 10 thousand frames per second to visualize both shape oscillations and microstreaming. Driven at 1.25 MHz frequency, 25,000 cycles and varying peak negative acoustic pressures (170-420 kPa), the larger sized microbubbles showed higher shape modes (up to mode 6) than the smaller ones. A shape stability model predicted the dominant shape mode with an 89% agreement with experimental findings. Four distinct 3-D microstreaming patterns were observed by employing astigmatic particle tracking velocimetry: patternless, radial, dipole and quadrupole. Modal decomposition of shape oscillations revealed that the quadrupole pattern occurred from self-interaction of the dominant shape mode, while the dipole pattern resulted from two strongly interacting shape modes. The quantified microstreaming showed maximum velocities ranging from 2-10 mm/s, which decayed with increasing distance from the microbubble center. Notably, lower shape modes and the quadrupole pattern were associated with higher wall shear stress (up to 13 Pa). These unique 3-D microstreaming profiles have the potential to enhance the efficiency of therapeutic microbubble-mediated ultrasound applications through controlled shape oscillation and mechanical impact.

title: Multiobjective Simulated Annealing-Based Stopwords Substitution for Rubbish Text Attack
authors: Chen Li, Xinghao Yang, Ao Wang, Yongshun Gong, Baodi Liu, Weifeng Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: Modern natural language processing (NLP) models exhibit extreme sensitivity toward text adversarial examples, while their opposite insensitivity to text rubbish examples is greatly underestimated. Text rubbish examples usually refer to highly modified sentences that appear nonsensical to humans but can keep the model’s prediction unchanged, which are significant in model robustness evaluation, improvement, and interpretation. Existing methods usually design a single objective optimization method to simultaneously maximize the modification rate and the model confidence (MC) with some text modification strategies, such as word deletion and preposition substitution. However, the single objective optimization easily falls into local optima due to the conflicting objectives, and the simple text modification strategies greatly limit the diversity of rubbish examples. To address these problems, we propose a multiobjective simulated annealing-based stopword substitution (MOSA-S2) algorithm with three major merits. First, the MOSA-S2 replaces the input words with meaningless stopwords and employs importance-based composite perturbation to simulate word substitution, enhancing the quality and diversity of the rubbish sample generation. Second, we formulate a multiobjective simulated annealing method to adaptively determine the priority of word replacements, which can escape local optima with a controlled probability and balance multiple objectives via Pareto dominance. Third, we design a grammatically constrained variant to enhance the readability of rubbish text, while maximizing its semantic deviation from the original to mislead human judgment. We evaluate the effectiveness and efficiency of our method on six text datasets by attacking seven popular neural models. Extensive experimental results demonstrate the superiority of our MOSA-S2 and reveal the fact that modern NLP models may not fully comprehend the textual semantics, as they make the same prediction with even higher confidence for nonsensical text sequences.

title: Constant false alarm rate detection of Doppler-distributed targets in low-rank partly homogenous clutter
authors: Weiming Tian, Ruiqi Lin, Yunkai Deng, Youwang Chen, Delin Fang, Xin Xie
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: In the presence of Gaussian noise and low-rank, partly homogenous clutter, detectors based on the full-rank assumption lose the constant false alarm rate (CFAR) property, while those relying on clutter nulling perform poorly for targets within the clutter subspace. This paper addresses the CFAR detection problem of Doppler distributed targets via invariance theory. Assuming the target lies in the target subspace, its detection problem is transformed into a canonical form by both the clutter and target subspaces. Then, the maximal invariant statistics and the conditions for CFAR detection are derived via invariance theory. Following this framework, the generalized likelihood ratio test is decomposed to obtain two CFAR components for the clutter subspace echo and the noise subspace echo. When the clutter subspace is known, a CFAR detector is constructed by combining the two components with the weight that maximizes detection probability. With unknown clutter subspace, the test is further generalized into an asymptotic CFAR detector based on the clutter subspace estimate and the asymptotic distribution of the estimate. The CFAR properties and detection performances of these detectors are validated through simulations and measured clutter.

title: Frequency-Selective Polarization Converter Based on Box-Like Topology
authors: Hang Yuan, Fu-Chang Chen, Jun-Jie Yan, Kai-Ran Xiang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This paper proposes a novel transmitting-type polarization converter (PC) with high frequency selectivity and a good out-of-band suppression level. Two orthogonal TM10-mode patch resonators are utilized as the receiving and transmitting elements, respectively. A novel dual-mode patch resonator with a butterfly-shape is designed and used as an intermediate transitional element. To achieve aperture-coupling between the adjacent patches, triple slots are etched on the common ground planes, forming a box-like topology. This topology produces four polarization conversion poles (PCP) and one tunable polarization conversion zero (PCZ). To further improve the selectivity and out-of-band suppression level, two diagonally placed metal posts are introduced to connect the two orthogonal TM10-mode patch resonators, generating an extra coupling path. Based on this improved structure, two PCZs can be achieved and adjusted freely to one or both sides of the passband by changing the coupling sign between the two orthogonal TM10-mode patch resonators. The design process and coupling sign control method are described and analyzed in detail. Several polarization converters with the same bandwidth but different PCZ configurations are proposed. To validate the design method, a polarization converter with PCZ on both sides is fabricated and measured, containing a -15-dB reflecting bandwidth of 4.8-5.2 GHz, a 3-dB transmitting bandwidth of 4.78-5.22 GHz (8.8%) and two symmetrical zeros located at 4.7 and 5.3 GHz. It exhibits high selectivity (fPCZ1/flow=4.7/4.8=0.98, fPCZ2/fhigh=5.3/5.2=1.02), a good out-of-band suppression level, and good angular stability up to 45°.

title: Data-Driven Sensitivity Analysis and Hybrid Multi-Objective Optimization for Ultra-High Performance Klystron
authors: Hezhang Zhao, Limin Sun, Shifeng Li, Hu He, Hua Huang, Zhengbang Liu, Wenhao Wang, Ke He, Dongwenlong Wu, Hongge Ma
abstract: This study presents a data-driven framework integrating sensitivity analysis with hybrid multi-objective optimization to improve both the physical performance and design efficiency of high-performance klystrons. A random forest–based Normalized Output Variation (NOV) method is developed to quantitatively assess the influence of design parameters, and an adaptive parameter-range adjustment strategy is employed to enable effective variable screening and range contraction, significantly enhancing computational efficiency. The framework incorporates a hybrid multi-objective optimizer with periodic elite migration, which balances population diversity and global exploration by combining NSGA-II’s rapid convergence with OMOPSO’s local search precision, achieving 71.7% 1D design efficiency.Arepresentative design from the hybrid Pareto front is selected for 3D validation and refinement using CHIPIC particle-in-cell simulations, achieving 61% efficiency at an interaction length of 5.3 operating wavelengths. This represents the highest simulated efficiency and most compact structure among recent S- to Ka-band relativistic klystron amplifiers (RKA). The proposed framework demonstrates a robust and efficient data-driven paradigm not only for klystron design but also for the optimization of other complex vacuum electronic devices and microwave systems.

title: MAPS: A Mode-Aware Probabilistic Scheduling Framework for LPV-Based Adaptive Control
authors: Taehun Kim, Guntae Kim, Cheolmin Jeong, Chang Mook Kang
abstract: This paper proposes Mode-Aware Probabilistic Scheduling (MAPS), a practical adaptive control framework tailored for DC motor systems experiencing varying friction. MAPS uniquely integrates an Interacting Multiple Model (IMM) estimator with a Linear Parameter-Varying (LPV) based control strategy, leveraging real-time mode probability estimates to perform probabilistic gain scheduling. A key integration strategy ofMAPSlies in directly using the updated mode probabilities as the interpolation weights for online gain synthesis in the LPV controller, thereby tightly coupling state estimation with adaptive control. This seamless integration enables the controller to dynamically adapt control gains in real time, effectively responding to changes in frictional operating modes without requiring explicit friction model identification. Validation on a Hardware-in-the-Loop Simulation (HILS) environment demonstrates that MAPS significantly enhances both state estimation accuracy and reference tracking performance compared to Linear Quadratic Regulator (LQR) controllers relying on predefined scheduling variables. These results establish MAPS as a robust, generalizable solution for friction-aware adaptive control in uncertain, time-varying environments, with practical real-time applicability.

title: An Improved Robust H ∞ Observer Framework for State-of-Charge Estimation of Lithium-Ion Batteries in Electric Vehicles
authors: K. Vinothini, R. Manivannan, Chala Merga Abdissa
abstract: Accurate state-of-charge (SOC) estimation is a critical requirement for reliable battery management systems (BMS) in electric vehicle (EV) applications, especially for lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) operating under dynamic conditions. However, variations in load demand, modeling uncertainties, and external disturbances can significantly degrade the performance of conventional SOC estimation techniques. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a robust SOC estimation framework based on an H∞ observer, specifically designed to attenuate worst-case disturbances and enhance estimation robustness. The effectiveness of the proposed method is validated using experimentally measured current profiles from standard driving cycles, including the Urban Dynamometer Driving Schedule (UDDS), Supplemental Federal Test Procedure (US06), Los Angeles 1992 (LA92), and Highway Fuel Economy Test (HWFET). Initially, the proposed observer is compared with Kalman filter (KF)-based variants under identical modeling assumptions and operating conditions, achieving an overall root mean square error (RMSE) of 0.105%, which corresponds to an SOC estimation accuracy of nearly 99.9%. Furthermore, benchmarking against recent SOC estimation approaches reported in the literature demonstrates an average performance improvement of 82%. These results confirm the superior accuracy, robustness, and real-time applicability of the proposed H∞ observer for practical EV lithium-ion BMS.

title:
Near-infrared photodetector based on single-walled carbon nanotube/graphene/Al
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    /InGaAs structure
authors: Danyang Zhang, Hao Gu, Jun Chen
abstract: In recent years, near infrared photodetectors with high responsivity, superior detectivity, and fast response speed are in urgent demand for optoelectronic applications. Herein, by integrating single-walled carbon nanotubes (SWCNTs) with InGaAs, a high-performance hetero-structure photodetector with SWCNTs/Graphene/Al<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>/InGaAs structure is constructed. Systematic photoelectric characterization reveals that the device achieves a responsivity of 342.9 mA/W and a specific detectivity of 2.01 × 1010 Jones at 1064 nm. Moreover, the photodetector exhibits excellent response speed, with rise and fall times of 10.5 μs and 52.5 μs at 4 kHz. These findings prove that the hybrid structure can effectively boost the performance of photodetectors, which provides a new approach for the innovation and optimization of photodetection devices.

title: Design and Performance Evaluation of a Modular Mobile Robot for Autonomous Hospital Logistics_supp1-3674356.mp4
authors: Neri Niccolo' Dei

title: Sensorless Control of Switched Reluctance Motor for Electric Three-Wheeler Vehicles with Onboard PV Charging Capability
authors: Samyak Jain, Sushant Kumar, Shailendra Kumar, Shashank Kurm
abstract: In this paper, an estimation technique is used for estimation of rotor position by incorporating the observer based method for flux approximation. This estimated flux is then used for the rotor position estimation using the non-linear relation between the rotor position, current and resultant flux. The motor operates on the phenomenon of reluctance torque, which is developed due to the variation in the air gap flux. The converter used for the motor operation is a four-phase semi-controlled voltage source inverter (VSI). This VSI and motor assembly is supplied by a battery energy storage (BES) for driving the load at the shaft of the motor for electric vehicle application like payload and passenger transportation. The battery source is interfaced with a bidirectional chopper for power transfer from the battery to the motor and vice-versa. For improving the driving range of the vehicle the system is installed with a solar photo-voltaic (PV) panel for energy generation and battery charging for the vehicle when not in uses. This alternate source of battery charging other than the grid based charging reduces the range anxiety for the driver and improves the driving range of the electric-three wheeler vehicle. The PV panel is interfaced with a boost converter, the gate pulse of boost converter is regulated by a maximum power point tracking (MPPT) algorithm for maximum power extraction from the installed PV panel. The algorithm used for tracking the maximum power point from the PV panel is incremental conductance based technique. Various simulation results have been studied and are presented in this paper for changing speed and torque operation of the SRM drive. The results and findings confirms that the speed and position estimation is working satisfactorily for the system.

title: Research on Intelligent Identification Method of Structural Anomalies of Submarine Cable Channel Based on Deep Learning and Multi-Source Fusion
authors: Jinyao Liu, Boqing Li, Xiaoqing Lin, Zhijie Zeng
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: At present, traditional detection methods have some problems, such as low efficiency, high cost and high false alarm rate. In recent years, deep learning and multi-source information fusion technology provide a new solution for submarine cable fault identification. In this paper, an intelligent identification method of structural anomalies of submarine cable channel based on deep learning and multi-source fusion is proposed. A method called Dynamic Weighted Multi-Source Fusion Network (DWMF-Net) is proposed to improve the anomaly detection ability of submarine cables in complex marine environment. This method adopts double-branch network architecture to process images and time series signals respectively, and realizes dynamic feature alignment through cross-modal attention mechanism. Bayesian neural network (BNN) is introduced to quantify the confidence of data sources, and an adaptive weight allocation module is designed based on entropy to realize the perception and fusion optimization of uncertain environment. The experimental results show that this method can effectively identify the structural abnormality of submarine cable channel, and has strong robustness and accuracy in complex environment.

title: An Automated Machine Learning Framework for Detecting Spam in YouTube Comments
authors: Latha R S, Sreekanth G R, Kalaivani K S, Darshan T P, Dhanush V, Naveen R
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Spam comments on YouTube degrade the user experience and the authenticity of online discussions. This study introduces a machine learning-based method as an automatic way to detect spam comments by utilizing both textual and structural features. A combined dataset of 11,440 labeled comments went through normalization, tokenization, and lemmatization to be preprocessed. TF-IDF representations were supplemented with features like comment length, punctuation frequency, and URL presence. Five classifiers—Logistic Regression, Support Vector Classifier, Random Forest, Naïve Bayes, and XGBoost—were trained and tested. Chi-square feature selection was used to enhance the model’s efficiency and accuracy. XGBoost, a model, performed the best with 97.2% accuracy, demonstrated strong generalization, and robustness out of all models. The paper finds that ensemble models are very powerful in identifying spam in online comment systems and therefore, can be additionally improved by using transformer-based architectures for a future instantaneous deployment.

title: Energy Hole Avoidance Algorithm for Multi-Layer Clustered WSN Based on Node Sleeping and Uneven Layering
authors: Xiangyuan Yin, Daogen Jiang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: When large-scale Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) are required to support wireless communication in military, environmental, healthcare, and other commercial fields, multi-layer clustered routing algorithms demonstrate significant advantages over single-layer clustered routing algorithms. However, the “energy hole” problem can occur in multi-layer clustered WSNs. The “energy hole” leads to network segmentation, preventing the limited energy of WSN nodes from being fully utilized, severely reducing the efficiency of the WSN. This paper theoretically proves that under certain conditions, the “energy hole” problem can be avoided. It proposes an Energy-Hole Avoidance Algorithm for multi-layer clustered networks (EHAA-NSUL), simulation experiments demonstrate that this algorithm greatly improves the energy utilization efficiency of nodes in WSNs, and mitigates the damage caused by the “energy hole” to the WSN.

title: Neuroeducation and Artificial Intelligence: A Systematic Review of Innovative Approaches for Personalized E-learning
authors: Ridouane Oubagine, Loubna Laaouina, Adil Jeghal, Hamid Tairi
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: The intersection between Artificial Intelligence (AI) and neuro-education suggests novel approaches for personalized e-learning systems matched with learners’ cognitive, affective, and behavioral traits. We looked in Scopus, Web of Science, and IEEE Xplore for interdisciplinary publications we could review between 2022 and 2025 related to this systematic tentacle of our review. The review uses PRISMA guidelines to highlight current trends, real-world implications, and ethical dilemmas regarding integrating AI into neuro-educational frameworks. The results show that AI technologies assist personalized learning with real-time cognitive profiling capabilities, adaptive content delivery, and affect-aware interfaces. However, data privacy, algorithmic bias, and unequal access hinder adoption. Existing solutions cannot effectively address these issues. Thus, we present a novel neuro-adaptive paradigm that integrates cognitive perceiving, machine learning adaptation, and ethical governance. This is a model that provides a springboard for how to create equitable, brain-compatible learning environments. In the future, researchers should prototype in real-world contexts, undertake participatory design with educators and learners, and conduct longitudinal studies that evaluate the impact of interventions on cognition and learner well-being.

title: Hierarchical Swin U-Net for Fine-Grained Damage Assessment from Oblique UAV Imagery: The Case of Hatay
authors: Nurefşan Ak Kanigür, Gökhan Bilgin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: Rapid and accurate assessment of building damage is paramount for effective disaster response operations. This paper addresses the semantic segmentation challenges in detecting structural destruction following the devastating earthquakes in Turkey on February 6, 2023. Traditional Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) often struggle to distinguish between complex damage levels due to their limited global context understanding. To overcome this, we propose a Hierarchical Swin U-Net architecture that leverages the long-range dependency modeling capabilities of Swin Transformers.Our primary contribution is the introduction of a novel, manually annotated dataset comprising 1,260 high-resolution images with over 10,000 building instances, classified into six granular damage levels. We present a two-stage hierarchical strategy: first localizing building footprints to filter out background noise, and subsequently classifying damage severity within these regions. Furthermore, we employ a transfer learning approach using a curated version of the xView2 dataset to address domain mismatch. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method, achieving a state-of-the-art 96.28% mIoU, significantly outperforms baseline models like U-Net (30.18%) and DeepLabV3+ (82.33%), particularly in distinguishing visually similar categories such as "Heavy Damage" and "Destruction."

title: Development and Application of Dynamic Gauge Testing System for Pantograph of Metro Vehicles Facing Complex Working Conditions
authors: Jiancai Zou, Wei Xue, Shen Zhan
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: The pantograph of subway is prone to dynamic deviation under complex working conditions, which leads to pantograph-catenary failure and affects operational safety. Aiming at the problems of low accuracy and weak anti-interference ability of traditional detection methods, this paper develops a set of dynamic gauge testing system for pantographs of metro vehicles under complex working conditions. The system adopts a three-layer architecture, integrates lidar, IMU, high frame rate industrial camera and acoustic sensor, and collects the pantograph spatial position, attitude vibration, deformation and arc information in real time. The pantograph-catenary dynamic coupling model is established, and multi-sensor data fusion is realized by extended Kalman filter (EKF) to realize millimeter-level dynamic monitoring. The field test results show that the system can run stably under complex working conditions such as tunnel access, bend and strong wind, and the average error is better than the traditional method, and it has high sensitivity and fast response ability. After the deployment of this system in a railway line, the operation and maintenance data of pantograph-catenary system has been significantly improved, which shows that this system can effectively improve the operation quality and reliability of pantograph-catenary system and reduce the maintenance cost.

title: An Integrated Approach for High-Performance Speed Tracking of Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors under Unknown Load Torque
authors: Roya Delgosha, Reza Delgosha
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors (PMSMs) are prevalent today due to their advantages, including high efficiency and power. They are the primary motor in most Electric Vehicles (EVs), providing precise, rapid movements for industrial robots and CNC machines, and they act as generators in wind turbines. Therefore, identifying them and designing efficient controllers are crucial, resulting in higher performance and benefiting their capacities. According to the nonlinearities of the PMSM mathematical model and its sensitivity to external disturbances, designing a controller for this type of motor is challenging, and it degrades the performance of conventional linear controllers such as PI controllers. To address this problem, this paper evaluates the performance of an integrated control strategy that combines an advanced suboptimal tracker with an Extended Kalman Filter (EKF). The controller is based on a novel State-Dependent Riccati Equation (SDRE) technique that overcomes the limitations of traditional SDRE regulators by transforming the tracking problem into an equivalent regulation problem. Applying this unique formulation, the tracking of non-decaying reference trajectories will be possible using a discounted cost function, and controller gains are generated from a single Riccati equation. This methodology avoids the need to solve the complex Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman equation. Concurrently, the EKF can estimate the unknown load torque online and is designed to reject disturbances proactively. Combining EKF and the novel SDRE method results in a nonlinear control structure that enhances tracking accuracy and system robustness.

title: Data Processing Algorithm for Bridge Structure Health Monitoring Based on Deep Learning
authors: Jing Xin, Tong Jing
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: In view of the problem that traditional methods in bridge structure health monitoring are difficult to balance multi-scale damage identification and anti-noise performance, this paper proposes an adaptive multi-scale attention fusion convolutional recurrent network (AMSACRN) algorithm. The algorithm innovatively constructs a three-level collaborative architecture: the adaptive multi-scale feature extraction module captures damage features of different sizes through dynamic weight adjustment, the attention fusion module strengthens key features and suppresses noise, the convolutional recurrent module deeply models the spatiotemporal correlation of data. The experiment uses 86,400 sets of measured data and 16,000 sets of simulation data (including 32 damage conditions) of a cable-stayed bridge, divides the data set into <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7: 2: 1$</tex>, and compares 5 representative algorithms. The results show that the damage identification accuracy of AMSACRN is 98.5%, the precision is 98.2%, the recall is 98.7%, and the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex> score is 98.4%, which are 6.2, 6.4, 6.0, and 6.3 percentage points higher than CNN-LSTM respectively, the RMSE of vibration displacement prediction is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 0 2 3 m m}$</tex>, and the MAE is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 0 1 8 m m}$</tex>, which is more than 30% better than the comparison algorithm, the accuracy is still 94.2% under 10 dB noise, and the performance degradation is only 4.3%. The algorithm has a single sample processing time of 42 ms, which meets the real-time monitoring needs. The research provides a high-precision and strong robust intelligent data processing solution for bridge health monitoring.

title: Adversarial Attacks on LiDAR-Based Localization in Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Sarah Sedrak, Alia El Bolock, Amr ElMougy
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: Autonomous vehicles are becoming a common part of the technology scene. LiDAR-based localization is fundamental to the safe navigation of autonomous vehicles, yet remains vulnerable to security threats. This paper investigates attack vectors targeting the communication and processing pipeline of LiDAR sensors, particularly those that compromise localization integrity. Through simulation and analysis, we design multiple adversarial scenarios and evaluate their impact on localization accuracy and system reliability. Our findings demonstrate the potential for severe operational disruption, underscoring the need for robust security mechanisms. This work contributes to the growing domain of autonomous system security by emphasizing the critical role of protecting LiDAR-based localization against malicious interference.

title: Small Target Detection in UAV Imagery Using Improved YOLOv11
authors: ZhengWu Yuan, HanQing Yang, YingQi Ke, Wan Zeng
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: The rapid proliferation of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) has revolutionized various industries, becoming a pivotal technology for scientific and social advancement. However, object detection in UAV aerial imagery presents significant challenges, primarily due to drastic target scale variations and the loss of detail in small targets, which severely degrade detection accuracy. To mitigate these issues, we propose an improved target detection algorithm named DRBA-YOLO. First, the C3k2 module in the YOLOv11 backbone is replaced with a Dilated Residual C3 (DRC3) module. This enhancement optimizes the receptive field in the deeper network layers, thereby capturing richer semantic feature information. Second, we replace the traditional Feature Pyramid Network (FPN) in the neck section with a Selective Boundary Aggregation (SBA) module. Unlike the standard FPN, the SBA unit facilitates a two-way integration strategy connecting features across different resolutions., facilitating more effective information propagation and enhancing multi-scale feature fusion. Experimental results on the VisDrone2019 dataset demonstrate that DRBA-YOLO outperforms the baseline YOLOv11, improving mAP50, mAP75, and mAP50:90 by 3.9%, 4.1%, and 3.3%, respectively, indicating superior detection accuracy for UAV aerial imagery.

title: Comparative Study and Performance Analysis of Axial Flux Permanent Magnet Motors for Electric Bicycle Applications
authors: Ali Akbar Ebrahimian, Seyed Abolfazl Davodi Amrei, Seyed Ehsan Abdollahi, Abdolreza Sheikholeslami, Jafar Adabi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: Axial flux motors are desirable for automotive applications due to their higher power and torque density, compact structure, and superior efficiency. Moreover, motor topology plays a critical role in determining not only the electrical and mechanical performance but also efficiency, torque density, thermal management, cooling capability, manufacturing feasibility, and overall system reliability. Therefore, in this paper, various topologies of axial flux permanent magnet (AFPM) motors are investigated for low-power electric vehicle applications, with a particular focus on electric bicycles. The four considered configurations include: (i) single-sided AFPM, (ii) double-sided yokeless and segmented armature (YASA), (iii) double-sided outer rotor TORUS-NS, and (iv) double-sided outer rotor TORUS-NN. These topologies are compared in terms of weight, cogging torque, total harmonic distortion (THD) of the back-EMF, and power density. The results indicate that, overall, the YASA structure provides the most suitable performance for application in electric bicycles.

title: BpmnBench: A Benchmark Framework for Evaluating LLMs in Process Planning and Structuring Across Multi-Formats
authors: Yang He, Haorui Zhang, Cheng Feng
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: LLM-based agents are gradually expanding the scope of process automation applications. However, inherent limitations in context capacity and the lack of structured expression in LLMs have hindered their widespread use in complex process automation. To address this, we propose BpmnBench, a benchmark framework to evaluate the process planning capabilities of LLMs across structured representations. BpmnBench is based on 1,945 business processes from real-world RPA scenarios, creating 28,201 QA samples covering tasks with multi-domain, multi-logical types, and varying levels of complexity. We designed a four-tier, progressive difficulty evaluation framework to comprehensively assess LLMs’ abilities in understanding, generating, modifying, and transforming complex processes. Our study shows that, while LLMs demonstrate strong capabilities in static task understanding and basic structural generation, significant gaps remain in dynamic tasks, like process modification and cross-format semantic mapping. Additionally, we find that the BPMN XML 2.0 format achieves a good balance between model performance and user readability, making it suitable for complex process modelling and automated execution. We hope our benchmark provides valuable insights for future research on multi-agent collaboration and complex business process automation powered by LLMs.

title: Optical terminal development for terrestrial laser links at UWA
authors: Benjamin Dix-Matthews, Lilani Toms-Hardman, Alex Frost, Nicolas Maron, Elrina Hartman, Ayden McCann, Andrew Lance, Sabrina Slimani, Shane Walsh, Amrita Gill, Shawn Mcsorley, John Wallis, Joshua Collier, David Gozzard, Sascha Schediwy
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: The Astrophotonics group at the University of Western Australia (UWA) have made significant progress in the characterization and suppression of atmospheric turbulence effects for free-space laser transmissions. Our terrestrial laser links team is focused on the development of robust, compact, and field deployable systems capable of overcoming the significant challenges of establishing stable free-space optical links within the Earth’s atmosphere. In the past we have demonstrated both high-speed data transfer and high-precision frequency transfer over both static links, and dynamic links to airborne targets, with kilometre-scale separations.Here, we report on the development of our most advanced terrestrial optical terminal to date. We provide an overview of the terminal architecture, detail the key design parameters, and verify the performance of the terminals over an atmospheric link.

title: Design and Implementation of an RF-Based Wearable Smart Patch Using Microstrip Antenna for Early Skin Cancer Detection via Dielectric Property Analysis
authors: G.V Aswini, N Anuj, D Rakshitha, H.P Bhuvan, Abdul Faiz
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: Early detection of skin cancer can save lives and reduce treatment costs. But existing diagnostic methods are invasive, expensive, and hard to use. This paper presents a low-cost, lightweight, and wearable patch that can detect skin cancer early without surgery or pain. The patch has a 2.45 GHz microstrip antenna printed on a flexible denim substrate, making it soft and easy to wear. The passive antenna sends radio waves into the skin and measures the signals that bounce back. These signals change based on the skin’s dielectric properties, which describe how the skin stores and absorbs electric energy. Cancerous tissues show distinct dielectric characteristics compared to healthy tissues because of differences in water content and structure. The signals are amplified using an RF power detector (AD8318) and converted into digital data with a 16-bit ADS1115 ADC and this digital data is processed using an ESP32-WROOM-32 and then the data is sent to a cloud server, where a machine learning model classifies the tissue as healthy or cancerous.

title: Battling the Sun in Geostationary Optical Feeder Link Scenarios
authors: Vardan Semerjyan, Terrence Moran, Alessandro Matheoud, Gilles Ackaert, Stephen Doherty, Francesca Del Monaco
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: The operational requirements for Optical Feeder ground-to-satellite links demand continuous 24-hour functionality of Optical Ground Stations (OGS). This is particularly crucial for Geostationary Equatorial Orbit (GEO) feeder link applications, where high availability is essential. Comprehensive research has been conducted on meteorological conditions like clouds and turbulence, showing that OGS networks can achieve an aggregate availability of 99.7% (at least one OGS is not covered by cloud) through the application of site diversity techniques. However, the potential impact of solar interference on the performance of OGS networks has not been thoroughly explored. Solar blinding, or solar outage, which is influenced by the solar exclusion angle in OGS design (typically ranging from 5 to 10 degrees for telescopes with a diameter of 0.8 to 1 meter), can cause simultaneous outages across entire continental OGS networks, significantly diminishing their annual availability. This work emphasizes the critical need to account for solar blinding effects in the design and operation of optical GEO feeder link networks and evaluates strategies to mitigate them.

title: Cable Path Optimization and Multi-Objective Decision-Making Method of Transmission Line Based on Deep Learning
authors: Jiani Ji, Rongxiang Wu, Jia Wu, Zhen Lu, Yong Huang
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: In this paper, a method of transmission line cable path optimization and multi-objective decision-making based on deep learning is proposed to improve the scientificity and efficiency of transmission line path planning. Convolutional neural network (CNN) is selected as the basic structure. Through CNN, the characteristics of multi-channel raster geographic data including elevation, slope, vegetation coverage and obstacles are extracted, and the path feasibility probability map is output. Binary cross entropy with weight adjustment is used as the optimization parameter of loss function. The multi-objective decision-making model aims at minimizing economic cost, environmental cost and risk cost. Top-K candidate paths are generated by improved A* algorithm combined with CNN probability graph, and then Pareto frontier is found by NSGA-II algorithm to optimize the performance of paths under multi-objective. At the same time, the weights of each cost item are iteratively updated by using dynamic weight adjustment mechanism combined with expert feedback. The experiment was carried out in a high-performance computing environment, and the data came from the geographic information of transmission lines of a provincial power grid company. The results show that the CNN model proposed in this paper is superior to the traditional U-Net model in accuracy, recall and F1 score. Compared with the traditional A* algorithm and single-objective optimization model, it shows better comprehensive performance in economic cost, environmental cost and risk cost, achieving a good balance among multiple objectives, and providing a scientific and efficient solution for the planning and construction of transmission line cable paths.

title: Comparative Analysis of K-Nearest Neighbors and Logistic Regression Models for Climate Classification
authors: Maya G Pillai, Krishna Sharma
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: All important decision making in different fields like agricultural planning, environmental management and meteorology depend on the precise categorization of climatic conditions. In this study, in order to classify climatology based on important weather factors namely temperature, humidity, wind speed, and rainfall, compares two machine learning models: KNN (K-Nearest Neighbors) and LR (Logistic Regression). The secondary data set includes 200 climate samples classified as "Rainy" or "Dry" conditions. Through MATLAB, all simulation work including model training, testing, and result visualization, has been done. Key metrics likeF1-score, recall, accuracy, precision, and AUC are used to assess the models' performance. After using various tools algorithms and models, it has been found that K-Nearest Neighbors predicts climate trends with greater classification accuracy and reliability than Logistic Regression. The results suggest the use of simple yet effective machine learning methods in real-time meteorological applications by proving their effectiveness in climate classification.

title: Image Style Transfer Enhancement via Frequency–Spatial Attention Synthesis and Perceptual-Loss Optimization
authors: Ruocheng Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Image style transfer has advanced rapidly alongside generative adversarial networks (GANs); however, complex textures continue to reveal a persistent limitation, where fine-grained details are often attenuated, oversmoothed, or manifested as artifacts. To address this gap, this study adapts a frequency-domain self-attention solver to StarGAN-v2 and introduces AFSAS, a module that couples spatial cues with frequency-domain structure so that generation preserves texture while remaining globally coherent. In parallel, a direct structure preservation strategy is introduced by applying perceptual loss at the generation stage, rather than relegating it to an auxiliary constraint. Architectural probing identifies where AFSAS should be placed inside the generator; the additional cost is modest–parameters rise by only 9.7%–and training remains stable. Under controlled comparisons, AFSAS lowers FID by 21.1% against the baseline while increasing LPIPS diversity by 8.6 %. Extending training to 200,000 steps under the direct structure preservation setting pushes FID down to 18.11 (a 34.3% improvement over the original baseline) while keeping LPIPS high at 0.561. Taken together, frequency–spatial attention and generation-stage perceptual supervision jointly mitigate the classic GAN tension between quality and diversity, and the results suggest that frequency-aware attention can be effectively adapted to the target architecture when the loss design is aligned with the forward synthesis pathway.

title: Electric Powertrain Trade-Off Studies and Optimization for Early-Stage Design Verification
authors: Anuradha Samant, Tushit Desai
abstract: The design of electric powertrain comes with the complex task of integrating components such as electric motors, inverters, batteries, gearboxes, and control system. Optimization of individual components in isolation does not always guarantee the best optimized powertrain (subsystem) design. To achieve vehicle-level requirements, the appropriate selection of these components and the powertrain architecture becomes crucial. In such cases, system-level trade-off analysis of powertrain components plays a key role in arriving at the best powertrain design in the minimum time. Here, powertrain sizing along with the optimization framework is developed for robust and efficient design. Further, Powertrain optimization workflow is established to explore the trade-offs between the different powertrain designs and to arrive at the most optimized design.

title: AI at the Service of Moroccan University Decision-Makers: Between Technological Innovation and Institutional Constraints
authors: Sanae El Maliki, Meriem Rachid, Zouheir Boussouf, Mouna Zerhouni Khal
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: This essay examines how Moroccans universities contribute to the work of decision-making public administration and Higher Education governments university. The main purpose of this research is to find some of the places in all aspects integrating the AI system which can help with university management decisions which provide Personnel Management or recruitment, Strategic Planning and Education Services (force part-time full-time students for example). The questioning Problem is: How can AI change the way college leadership and support is done in more philosophic terms and in this context also what to do about currently obstructive elements? Thus, this will focus on issues concerning action resistance (such as dealing with change at monopoly airlines or being bankrupted by major identity thieves), budgetary limitations and need to train human resources. This study uses a qualitative methodology based on interviews with key partners in the academic sector such as administrative officials, teachers and technology experts. This will enable collecting rich data on perceptions, expectations, and challenges experienced in the process of integrating the AI. The anticipated outcomes uncover a broad range of opportunities for public managers in both human resource management and academic planning, especially with regards to the process of decision-making and the customization of the educational experience. However, several challenges are anticipated, particularly in terms of resistance to innovation, management of the digital transition, and compatibility of existing systems. This study proposes recommendations to facilitate the acceptance of AI and improve university management, while taking into account the specificities of the Moroccan context. Finally, future perspectives include studying the impact of AI on institutional performance and the widespread adoption of AI in other public sectors.

title: A Comprehensive Review of Anomaly Detection Techniques for Insider Threat Identification
authors: Bhumi Bhoi, Gordhan Jethava, Sweta Jethava
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
