abstract: Insider threats represent one of the most complex and damaging challenges in modern cybersecurity, as malicious or negligent users operate with legitimate access and can bypass traditional perimeter-based defenses. To address this issue, anomaly detection has emerged as a key mechanism for identifying deviations from normal user behavior that may indicate insider attacks. This paper presents a systematic review of anomaly detection techniques applied to insider threat identification, with a focus on machine learning, deep learning, and user and entity behavior analytics approaches. Existing methods are categorized based on their learning paradigms, data sources, and detection strategies, and their performance is critically analyzed using findings reported across public and proprietary datasets. The review highlights major strengths of behavioral and anomaly-based models in detecting subtle and evolving threats, while also identifying persistent challenges such as high false-positive rates, class imbalance, limited labeled data, and lack of interpretability. Finally, key research gaps are outlined, emphasizing the need for explainable, adaptive, and multimodal detection frameworks. This study provides a structured reference for researchers and practitioners seeking to design more reliable and practical insider threat detection systems.

title: Performance Analysis and Comparison of DenseNet169 and ResNet152 for MRI-Based Brain Tumor Classification
authors: Mohammed Maaz, Cyril Robinson Azariah J
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: Accurate identification of brain tumors from neuroimaging remains challenging despite technological advances. Deep learning approaches offer potential solutions for improving diagnostic accuracy in clinical settings. We conducted a comparative study of two convolutional neural network architectures—DenseNet169 and ResNet152—using 7,023 MRI images categorized into glioma, meningioma, pituitary adenoma, and healthy tissue. The dataset was partitioned into training (80%), validation (10%), and testing (10%) sets. Both models utilized transfer learning with ImageNet pre-trained weights and underwent standardized training for 25 epochs. DenseNet169 outperformed ResNet152 across all metrics, achieving 95.24% accuracy, 92.13% precision, 99.15% recall, and 95.51% F1-score. ResNet152 achieved 83.98% accuracy, 80.00% precision, 91.53% recall, and 85.38% F1-score. The accuracy difference of 11.26% and superior recall of DenseNet169 suggest enhanced clinical reliability. DenseNet169 demonstrates significant advantages for automated brain tumor classification, particularly in minimizing false negatives—a critical requirement for clinical applications. Further validation on diverse clinical datasets is warranted.

title: Enhancing Regression Prediction with a Two-Stage Classification-Regression Pipeline
authors: Sanjib Shah, Ashim Khadka
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Science and Communications Technologies (ICISCT)
abstract: The aim of regression models is to directly predict continuous outcomes but in many cases, they are less accurate when target values are widely distributed across the data. This paper will suggest a two step hybrid model whereby the regression problem is initially transformed into a classification problem by binning and then the problem is refined by bin-specific regression. The method uses a Support Vector Classifier (SVC) to identify the target interval and a Support Vector Regressor (SVR) to give the final continuous estimate in the range of prediction. Experiments conducted on three relatively small datasets, the Student Study, the Boston Housing and Book Read datasets show significant performance improvement as the R2 scores increase from 0.79 to $0.90,0.69$ to 0.78 and 0.69 to 0.77 respectively. These results suggest that regression accuracy can be improved by reducing the prediction space by first classifying the data but at the expense of more computational time. On the whole, the proposed classification-regression pipeline can be used as a good alternative to enhance the performance of regression especially in cases when the datasets are small and the accuracy is crucial rather than the execution time.

title: Optimal Design of CSI-fed PMSM Integrated Motor Drive System Using Coupled Electromagnetic–Thermal Multiphysics Co-simulation with Drive system
authors: Hossein Azizi Moghaddam, Reza Mirzahosseini
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: In this paper, a comprehensive process for the optimal design of an integrated motor-drive (IMD) system using coupled electromagnetic-thermal multiphysics simulation is presented. In the proposed method, the drive system is implemented in the MATLAB-PLECS environment, and the machine's two-dimensional (2D) magnetic and three-dimensional (3D) thermal simulations are performed in finite element (FE) software. The coupling between the different parts is established through an automated code in Python, such that after each simulation cycle, the temperatures obtained from the thermal analysis are used to update the temperature-dependent parameters of the motor and drive. This iterative process continues until temperature convergence is achieved. After model validation, sensitivity analysis is performed on a set of motor and drive parameters to determine their direct and mutual effects on motor and drive performance indicators. Then, nine key parameters are selected and multi-objective optimization is performed based on technical and economic criteria using different weighting factors. The results show that the optimal structure resulting from the proposed process provides significant improvements in efficiency, torque ripple, thermal stability, and cost of the system compared to the initial design.

title: Dual-Path Network with Dynamic Feature Fusion and Contrastive Representation Learning for Colonic Polyp Detection
authors: Hanhan Geng, ZhaoHeng Diao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: Accurately detecting colonic polyps is crucial for preventing and diagnosing colorectal cancer. Although remarkable progress has been achieved in recent years, most existing methods still face challenges in recognizing polyps with occult characteristics and complex morphologies. To address these challenges, this paper propose a dual-path network called Adaptive Enhancement and Contrastive Learning Stategy Network (DACSNet), which leverages adaptive spatial feature enhancement and contrastive learning to address these challenges in an end-to-end training framework. On one hand, DACSNet employs a multi-branch adaptive spatial enhancement network with dilated convolutions to model spatial context across receptive fields for capturing polyps of diverse scales and shapes. On the other hand, its metric-learning-guided branch employs selfsupervised contrastive learning to maximize the feature distance between foreground and background while minimizing intraclass variance. This stategy enables the learning of robust invariance without requiring pixel-level annotations, effectively addressing the challenge of detecting concealed polyps. Finally, the proposed network is evaluated on LDPolypVideo, CVC-ClinicDB and Kvasir-SEG, comparing it against other state-of-the-art methods, achieving precision of 84.53%, a recall of 46.72%, and a $F_{-} 1 60.16 \%$ on LDPolypVideo. Experimental results demonstrate its superior performance, with ablation experiments confirming the effectiveness of the proposed modules.

title: Control of a Modular Drive for a Six-Phase Non-Sinusoidal PMSM with Asymmetric Open-End Winding Using the Reference Current Shaping Strategy
authors: Abolfazl Halvaei Niasar
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: Electrical drives based on six-phase open-end winding permanent magnet synchronous motors (OE-PMSM) have gained significant attention in high-power applications over recent decades due to their high reliability. This paper focuses on controlling a specific type of such a drive, where each motor phase has a dedicated microcontroller. Since each phase is connected to an independent H-bridge single-phase inverter, the drive structure is entirely modular. Due to the absence of data exchange between microcontrollers, the motor is modeled in a stationary six-axis reference frame. Given the non-sinusoidal back-EMF voltages of the motor phases and the resulting torque ripple, as well as the inability to use conventional two-axis dq transformations and control methods based on them, the reference current shaping strategy in the stationary six-axis reference frame is employed. Consequently, harmonic quasi-proportional-resonant regulators are used to adjust the currents around their harmonic reference values. The theoretical analyses and the effectiveness of the proposed modeling and control methods are validated through several simulations in Simulink.

title: Towards a Quantum-Enhanced Internet of Things
authors: Bassem Arar, Abdelkrim Menina, Muhammad Idham Habibie, Riccardo Bassoli, Frank H. P. Fitzek
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: As networks continue to evolve beyond 5 G, the Internet of Things (IoT) is expected to satisfy high demands for ultra-low latency, robust security, and resiliency in dynamic environments. Emerging technologies - particularly quantum technology - are a promising solution to address these challenges. This paper proposes a modular, hybrid quantum-classical architecture that seamlessly integrates quantum technologies such as secure quantum key sharing, quantum synchronization and quantum computing - into the existing IoT infrastructure. This approach supports compatibility with existing infrastructures while enabling next-generation capabilities. We explore the architectural principles of the IoT, leverage current standardization efforts, and illustrate practical use cases, including quantumassisted time synchronization and secure communication. With a focus on scalability and interoperability, the proposed framework provides a path towards quantum-enabled IoT networks. Such a hybrid quantum-classical regime forms the basis for a resilient quantum IoT infrastructure that is aligned with future networks requirements.

title: Power Grid Portfolio Forecasting Method Based on Improved Genetic Algorithm
authors: Chen Ye, Yaogang Luo, Han Jiang
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: The traditional forecasting method of power grid investment portfolio has some problems, such as low forecasting accuracy and poor adaptability to complex environment. The improved genetic algorithm (GA) effectively solves the problems of premature convergence and low search efficiency of traditional GA by adaptively adjusting crossover and mutation probability, introducing multi-population mechanism and adopting elite retention strategy. In this paper, a comprehensive prediction model is constructed, and the crossover operator and mutation operator in GA are improved to enhance the global search ability and convergence ability of the algorithm. Through empirical research, the results show that the proposed forecasting method of power grid portfolio based on improved GA can effectively improve the accuracy and stability of forecasting and provide strong support for investment decision-making of power grid enterprises.

title: Design of Signal Processing and Control Algorithm for Distributed Sensor Networks Facing Complex Environment
authors: Zijun Liu
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: Aiming at the problems faced by Distributed Sensor Networks (DSNs) in complex environment, such as signal quality degradation, network dynamics and resource limitation, this paper proposes a DSNs collaborative optimization framework for complex environment. The framework adopts a three-layer collaborative architecture of perception-processing-control, constructs a dynamic interference prediction model, and combines variational modal decomposition (VMD) and adaptive filtering to realize lightweight signal processing. Design the topology control and multi-objective resource allocation algorithm based on virtual force field (VFF) to improve the adaptive ability of the network; A cross-layer collaborative optimization model is established, and its effectiveness is verified by distributed consistency algorithm and hardware-in-the-loop simulation platform. The experimental results show that the proposed method is superior to the traditional algorithm in signal reconstruction error, processing delay, energy consumption and network lifetime, transmission delay, energy consumption balance and other key indicators, which significantly improves the robustness, adaptability and energy efficiency of DSNs in complex environment.

title: HBNet: Low-Light Image Enhancement via HSV Decomposition and Bi-Directional Feature Interaction
authors: Weijie Zhang, Guorong Chen, Shaofeng Liu, Jian Wang, Bao Zhang, Hong Hu
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: In poorly lit environments, the simultaneous occurrence of intense noise, chromatic aberration, and the degradation of fine details renders image restoration a formidable task. Existing methods often operate directly in the RGB space, which makes it difficult to simultaneously restore luminance and preserve color fidelity. To address this, we present HBNet, an innovative framework that disentangles image components within the HSV domain and incorporates a mutual feature interaction strategy. The network consists of two cooperative branches: a chromatic branch for processing hue-saturation features with continuous cosine-sine encoding of Hue, and a luminance branch optimized for brightness restoration. To further strengthen representation learning, we integrate a Channel-Spatial Attention Fusion (CSAF) module to capture global luminance context and a Multi-Scale Contextual Feature Module (MSCFM) to adaptively aggregate spatial information. These designs enable the network to recover dark-region details while preserving natural color appearance. Comprehensive evaluations on various public benchmarks confirm that HBNet delivers state-of-the-art results. It excels in both perceptual quality and numerical indices, underscoring its potential for practical lowlight applications.

title: Responsive Home for Emotion Regulation Applications with Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Cheng Hong Hew, Mastaneh Mokayef, M. K. A. Ahamed Khan, MHD Amen Summakieh, Susama Bagchi, Sanjoy Kumar Debnath, D.Najumnissa Jamal, Kalaiselvi Aramugam
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: This paper presents a prototype of a responsive home system for emotion recognition and regulation, with a convolutional neural network (CNN). The concept of this paper is to utilize neural network technology in a real-time system. Three main parts of the system are a monitoring module, a real-time database, and emotion regulation modulation. The monitoring module uses two convolutional neural networks to detect and recognize the facial expression of the user. Then, the result is sent and stored in a cloud-hosted real-time database. The emotion regulation module is designed independently to retrieve the result and provides musical and light intervention to regulate negative emotions toward a positive mood. The performance of the model in terms of accuracy, is tested with the FER2013 test dataset and a small dataset with 400 images with background. The results show that the system achieved an accuracy of 82.42% on the FER2013 dataset and 83.0% on the small dataset. The average processing time for each image is 112.6ms, which is fast enough for a real-time system. In summary, the monitoring module with CNN successfully active the emotion regulation module when detecting sad faces. However, there is a lack of evidence to prove the effectiveness of emotion regulation.

title: An Envelope of TKEO of Difference in Positive Sequence Voltage based Solution to Detect Islanding
authors: Jitendra Kumar, Munna Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Distribution networks rapidly integrate distributed generation (DG) microgrids to fulfil load demand, support ecological balance, and lower greenhouse gas emissions. Technological developments in power electronics are increasing the dispatchability of renewable energy sources to loads, converting passive networks into bidirectionally power-flowing active networks. According to IEEE-1547-2018 requirements, microgrids must maintain voltage, frequency, and power quality while supplying connected loads, detecting unintentional failures, and islanding the microgrid in less than two seconds. There are several approaches to islanding, such as communication, hybrid, active, and passive strategies. While passive approaches leave a more significant non-detection zone (NDZ), active procedures can worsen power quality due to the infusion of deviations. While very effective, communication-based strategies are usually expensive. Hybrid techniques encompass both passive and active procedures, striking a balance between cost and effectiveness. An islanding recognition algorithm based on the Teaser Kaiser Energy Operator (TKEO) envelope of the difference in positive sequence voltage is proposed to address these issues. This method has been tested in various islanding and non-islanding scenarios, consistently demonstrating accuracy in each scenario. Its islanding detection time is shorter than that of several other current methods.

title: Neural Network-Based Prediction of AC Losses and Performance in High-Speed PMSM with Hairpin Winding
authors: Mohammad Sepehr Zamani, Aghil Ghaheri, Ebrahim Afjei
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: This study presents a hybrid modeling approach that combines 2-D Finite Element Method (FEM) simulations with a Neural Network (NN) to predict key parameters of a high-speed Permanent Magnet Synchronous Machine (PMSM), including DC losses, AC losses of individual hairpin winding layers, skin effect losses, and efficiency. These parameters are critical for designers to understand the impact of each layer’s height on performance and losses. The methodology consists of two phases: generating training data from 2-D FEM simulations and developing a NN whose configuration and parameters are optimized via Bayesian optimization to ensure high accuracy. The developed NN demonstrates excellent predictive performance on the test dataset, achieving R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> values above 99% for efficiency, DC losses, and skin-effect losses, and over 99.4% for AC losses in each hairpin winding layer. Accuracy is particularly high for layers near the air gap, which have the greatest impact on overall performance. The results confirm that the NN effectively captures the relationship between hairpin winding layer heights and output parameters in high-speed PMSM with hairpin winding, which enables reliable prediction of performance and AC losses.

title: Skeleton-Based Action Recognition Using Global Spatiotemporal Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Yunbo Li, Xin Li, Ning Yang, Junqiang Yang, Zhiying Zhou, Jianting Li, Junzhang Jiao, Yanling Ning
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: To address the demand for accurate automatic recognition of patients' abnormal behaviors in medical monitoring scenarios, this paper proposes a Global Spatiotemporal Convolutional Neural Network model that integrates Transformer and CNN for human motion recognition based on skeleton sequences. The model consists of an Embedding Layer, an Encoder Layer, and a CNN Block. It achieves feature encoding through the combination of Input Embedding and Positional Encoding, captures global dependencies via Multi-Head Attention, extracts local temporal features using 1DCNN, and ultimately completes the classification task through a fully connected layer. Experimental results on a subset of 9 health-related actions from the public NTU RGB+D dataset demonstrate that the model achieves a classification accuracy of 81.07%, outperforming comparative models such as 2sAGCN and STGCN. Ablation experiments verify that the Positional Encoding module can effectively enhance the capture of global dependencies in the dataset, significantly improving the model's classification performance and providing reliable technical support for disease early warning and health monitoring in medical scenarios.

title: Multi Agent Powered Vehicle Recommendation System
authors: Priyanka Singanamala, V D J Raditya, Sai Karthik Athota, Sai Navya Sri Chalasani, Gubba Aryan, Sivakrishna Kondaveeti
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Choosing the right vehicle is a complex problem which involves several dynamic criteria such as family, environment, budget, and luxury in which prices are volatile. In this paper, we propose DriveWise, a multi-agent vehicle recommendation system that cleverly decomposes the problem and coordinates vehicle selection to obtain optimal choice. The system is made of domain specialist agents (budget, family, luxury, eco-friendly) who evaluate the candidate cars with their own criteria. The system includes a master orchestrator agent that parses the user's query and invokes the relevant specialist agents. These agents send recommendations back to the master, which compiles them into concise and context aware recommendations to the user. DriveWise was built using OpenAI Agents SDK. This SDK enables structured agent communication and orchestration. It uses real-time pricing and inventory data for enabling up-to-date recommendations. The results highlight that the multi-agent approach outperforms classical single-model systems with relevance, interpretability, and responsiveness. In this regard, the agents dynamically collaborate and adapt in real-time to offer a scalable and efficient solution for personalized vehicle recommendation scenarios.

title: Low-Cost System for Configuring Image Sharing Servers
authors: Kitty Urbano, Wilver Auccahuasi, Sandra Meza, Percy Castro-Mejia, Maria Peña-Romero, Luis-Peña-Romero
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Sharing images and videos is a challenge today, due to the need to have computational tools based on hardware and software, making the use of image and video analysis and processing systems expensive, mainly when we analyze medical images, we present a solution based on low-cost solutions known as NAS devices, which has been configured to store and process medical images and to be able to share these images through programs for viewing images and video, as well as being able to share images through image transmission protocols and through QR codes, with which a low-cost functional PACS system is available that can provide storage, processing and sharing medical images, in the results we present the configuration and operating modes as well as in the conclusions we present the usefulness and scalability of the proposal.

title: Optimizing Cloud Data Warehousing Costs Through Redundancy Elimination and Storage-Aware Automation
authors: Junaith Meeran Haja Mohideen
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: Cloud data warehouses such as Amazon Redshift, Snowflake and Google BigQuery have become central to enterprise analytics. While these platforms deliver elasticity and high performance, unchecked data growth can lead to escalating storage costs, redundant data, and operational burdens. Existing solutions largely focus on query performance or resource scaling, overlooking how accumulated storage inefficiencies degrade both cost and speed. This paper introduces the Redshift Optimization Manager (ROM), a modular framework for continuous, proactive storage governance in Amazon Redshift. ROM automatically scans system metadata, detects unused or duplicate objects, enforces optimal compression and distribution settings, and remediates redundancy with minimal human intervention. In a controlled 4-week experiment on a 3 TB e-commerce dataset, ROM slowed warehouse growth from 30% to 10%, corresponding to an extrapolated $\sim 150 \mathrm{~TB}$ quarterly storage saving at enterprise scale. Query performance improved markedly (95th-percentile latency reduced by $\sim 43 \%$, disk spills by $\sim 47 \%$), and operational incidents dropped by $\boldsymbol{\sim} \mathbf{4 5 \%}$ thanks to a cleaner, more efficient warehouse. These results demonstrate that managing storage redundancy is as critical to sustainable data warehousing as traditional query optimization. We formalize a Redundancy Factor metric to quantify storage entropy and show that ROM’s automation can “decay” redundancy over time. This work advocates integrating storage-aware automation with cloud cost governance (FinOps) practices to achieve cost-efficient, high-performance, and resilient cloud data warehouses.

title: Speed Sensor Simulation in Software in Loop Platform
authors: Jayant Kumar Sahoo, SunkiReddy Ginugu
abstract: This work focuses on enhancing the current capability of Software In Loop (SiL) platform for digital validation of inverter Electronic Control Unit (ECU). An architecture of the inverter virtual - ECU (vECU) is proposed to eliminate the complexities involving the integration of Complex Device Driver (CDD) layer. Plant models have been developed to mimic the behavior of automotive speed sensors. A detailed discussion is provided on the challenges related to processing high frequency Pulse Width Modulation (PWM) signals for speed calculations. The vECU simulation shows an accuracy of over 99.62 % in the speed calculated in Application Software (ASW) layer modules. The proposed vECU facilitates the rapid development and digital validation of software modules, accelerating the overall development process and enabling early detection of software defects.

title: Domain-Adapted Large Language Model for Mine Safety Question Answering
authors: Hang Yu, Yanqing Li, Shixiang Chen
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Mine safety demands rapid access to accurate and reliable knowledge to reduce accidents and support critical decision-making. Existing question-answering (QA) systems struggle in this domain due to specialized terminology, complex multi-part queries, and the risk of hallucinated responses from general-purpose models. To address these challenges, we formalize the Mine Safety Question Answering task, which focuses on delivering precise and context-aware answers for safety-critical mining scenarios. We propose a domain-adapted large language model framework that combines parameter-efficient fine-tuning via LoRA with a multi-expert knowledge fusion mechanism, enabling effective injection of mining-specific expertise while controlling computational cost. To support evaluation, we construct a MineSafetyQA dataset and additionally adopt a Construction Safety QA benchmark for cross-domain validation. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines by 2–3% in accuracy and F1 score across datasets, achieving 88.0% accuracy on MineSafetyQA. Ablation studies further verify the contributions of domain adaptation and knowledge fusion, with robust performance maintained even when trained on only 50% of the data. These results highlight the effectiveness of specialized LLM adaptation for safety-critical QA and suggest broader applicability to other high-risk industrial domains.

title: Research on Multimodal Network Public Opinion Sentiment Analysis Model Based on Graph Convolutional Network
authors: Lyu Li, Tian Xin, Zhang Fang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: The internet has become an important channel for people to obtain information. How to use technologies such as deep learning and transfer learning to capture the emotional trends of greatest concern from massive amounts of network public opinion information, in order to guide positive public opinion dissemination, has become a research hotspot of great concern. Addressing the issue that relationships within modalities cannot be fully captured in multimodal network public opinion sentiment analysis, leading to insufficient effective sentiment fusion, this paper proposes a multimodal network public opinion sentiment analysis model based on a Graph Convolutional Network (GCN). The model uses Bidirectional Encoder Representation from Transformers (BERT), Facet, and COVAREP to obtain feature representations for each modality, followed by stacked bidirectional long short-term memory networks to further extract key features within the modalities. Then, a multimodal network public opinion graph is constructed, and GCN is used in the constructed network public opinion graph to generate text-image fusion features and text-audio fusion features. Finally, these two features are concatenated and classified and output through a fully connected layer. This helps the model better understand the correlation between data, thereby improving model performance. Experiments on multimodal datasets show that the multimodal model based on GCN can improve the accuracy of online public opinion analysis.

title: Efficient Prediction of Kidney Disorders using Deep Learning
authors: Mohammad Naseeruddin, M Pachhaiammal Alias Priya, Choriyev Muzaffar, Jumaniyozov Kuvondik Yo'ldashevich, Sadullayeva Moxinur, D. Stalin
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The world is moving with innovative technologies in health care to track human health parameters finding health diseases. Artificial intelligence plays an important role in prediction of health diseases. Now a days kidney diseases effect's on human health and deaths are happening throughout the world due to kidney disorders. Kidney disorders such Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD), Polycystic Kidney Disease (PKD)Kidney Stones, infections, kidney cyst, Glomerulonephritis and kidney stone. It is impossible to recognize the symptoms of the disease in its early stages. Few people are able to anticipate the symptoms of this illness and are aware of it. The illness is regarded as a possible risk factor. Therefore, this paper uses a powerful deep learning framework to prevent these diseases by lowering the risk of contracting them. Deep learning is introduced in proposed research along with machine learning to predict kidney diseases. Convolution Neural Network (CNN) from the deep learning and Support Vector Machine (SVM) from the machine learning used in proposed research to predict kidney disorders. CNN predicts kidney disorders with highest accuracy of 97% over existing methods and SVM.

title: An Edge-based Multimodal Human-Computer Interaction System for Touchless Control using Gesture and Voice
authors: Bagam Laxmaiah, K. Shilpa, Chidrawar Aishwarya, Elkari Sreeja, Chakali Saikumar, B. Bhavya
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Traditional human-computer interfaces primarily depend on physical input devices like keyboards and mice, which may cause accessibility, hygiene, and flexibility concerns in the contemporary computing atmosphere. Gesture-and voice-based interaction systems have emerged as solutions to some of these problems, but many of the existing approaches are still dependent on cloud, raise privacy issues, and are plagued by latency that reduces real-time usability. This paper describes FIDO which is an offline and multimedia human-computer interaction system that combines hand gesture recognition via vision with offline processing voice commands so as to provide touchless computer control. The system, based on edge AI capabilities, is entirely based on a local hardware and supports a mode-based assistant with the capability to carry out cursor operations, system commands, and informational mind searching, by integrating a local large language model. Contrary to traditional methods which focus on classification accuracy, the presented system is assessed with frame rate (FPS) as a leading performance metric, indicating real-time responsiveness and user experience. The experiments show consistent and graceful interaction over various frame rates from 10 to 60 FPS on non-high-end hardware, with minimal delay and reliable system performance. This method demonstrates the effectiveness of FPS-based assessment for real-time multimodal interaction systems and validates the capability of offline AI-powered interfaces to provide accessible and secure human-computer interaction.

title: VIGOR-AD: A Verification-Guided, Risk-Optimized Validation Framework for Autonomous Driving
authors: Tong Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: We propose VIGOR-AD, a hybrid validation architecture that connects high-fidelity simulation and formal verification for Autonomous Driving Systems (ADS). While simulation offers breadth and formal methods offer depth, integrating them remains challenging due to the state space explosion. This paper details the system design of VIGOR-AD, which couples a Unity3D simulation environment with the NuSMV model checker through a novel state abstraction layer. We describe the methodology for mapping continuous vehicle dynamics to discrete finite-state models, enabling the exhaustive verification of complex intersection protocols against Linear Temporal Logic (LTL) specifications. A case study on an ambulance priority protocol demonstrates the framework’s capability: formal analysis uncovers a logic flaw inaccessible to random testing, and the framework automatically concretizes the abstract counterexample into a reproducible simulation scenario. The results validate the architectural feasibility of driving simulation with formal proofs.

title: Interactive Tools and Digital Twins for Training in Industry 5.0: A Review
authors: Majda Arab, Loubna Laaouina, Adil Jeghal
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: In recent years, technological progress has accelerated dramatically, particularly in the areas of mass customization and advanced manufacturing. This evolution has been driven by the integration of artificial intelligence (AI) with human-computer interaction (HCI), creating new possibilities for efficiency and innovation. Robots, for instance, have gained increasing importance in industrial processes, where their deployment is often seen as a marker of growth and modernization. Yet, despite the growing reliance on intelligent systems, humans continue to play an essential and irreplaceable role, bringing adaptability, creativity, and decision making to the industrial environment. In this context, Industry 5.0 offers a solution where it emphasizes the need of human workers alongside the new technologies to enhance productivity without replacing any relevant element in this context, and advocates for collaboration between humans and machines, leveraging each other’s strengths to create a new version of the industrial ecosystem focused on greater effectiveness, productivity, and sustainability. This review explores the historical evolution of the industry, highlights the way how technologies like digital twins and human digital twins support in making this big change, and discusses their potential applications, implementations, and key aspects within different sectors. Additionally, the review addresses the components, benefits, and challenges associated with these concepts.

title: From Structural Morphology to BrainNet Transformer: Harmonized Morphological Networks for Multi-site Autism Diagnosis
authors: Guolin Guo, Yijie Teng, Hao Wang, Linyuan Lu
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Autism spectrum disorder (ASD) presents substantial diagnostic challenges due to its heterogeneous manifestations and the subjectivity inherent in behavioral assessments. Structural MRI provides valuable morphological information, but its diagnostic utility is hindered by pronounced interindividual variability and non-biological site effects in large multi-center cohorts. We propose Harmonized Morphological Networks (HMN), a multi-site ASD classification framework that integrates distribution-based morphological brain networks, edge-level ComBat harmonization, and graph-based deep learning. Morphological networks were constructed from regional gray-matter morphology using a distribution-based similarity measure across three cortical parcellations. ComBat was applied after network construction to harmonize edge weights while preserving biological covariates, substantially reducing residual site effects and improving cross-site consistency. Building on the harmonized networks, we adapted the BrainNet Transformer (BrainNetTF) and benchmarked it against hi-GCN and BrainNetCNN, with a domain-adversarial GNN included as a supplementary harmonization-aware baseline. Experiments on ABIDEII demonstrate that ComBat markedly mitigates site-related variability and yields modest gains for CNN/GCN-based models, while BrainNetTF achieves the highest accuracy and improved stability, and hi-GCN attains the best AUROC. These findings highlight the importance of jointly considering harmonization strategy and model architecture, and support transformer-based learning on harmonized morphological networks as a scalable approach for multi-site ASD classification.

title: Dual Low-rank Constrained Self-representation Learning for Unsupervised SAR Image Change Detection
authors: Weisong Li, Yinwei Li, Yiming Zhu
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Change detection (CD) in bi-temporal Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) images has been an important research direction in remote sensing intelligent interpretation. Recently, feature matrix decomposition methods based on superpixel analysis have demonstrated significant advantages in reducing computational complexity and suppressing detail noise by expanding the scale of analysis units. However, existing low-rank and sparse decomposition models focus solely on the low-rank structure among samples while neglecting intrinsic correlations in the feature space. Moreover, in fragmented and irregular change regions, the single sample-level low-rank assumption often fails, leading to insufficient geometric structure preservation and significant decomposition deviations. To address these issues, we propose a self-representation learning framework based on dual low-rank constraints. The framework simultaneously decomposes the change feature matrix into a projection matrix representing feature correlations and an affinity matrix describing sample similarities, while imposing global low-rank constraints on both matrices to achieve collaborative modeling of sample and feature spaces. By introducing a projection distance penalty and Laplacian local correlation constraints, the model effectively enhances its capability to characterize local geometric structures. Based on the reconstructed difference images (DIs), we further design an adaptive manifold regularization graph optimization algorithm that transforms the binary classification problem into a graph partitioning optimization problem, improving classification accuracy. Experiments on seven benchmark datasets containing complex disaster scenarios such as tsunamis and wildfires demonstrate the superior performance of our method in detecting complex change regions, achieving an average Kappa coefficient improvement of 3.83% compared to state-of-the-art methods. The source code will be available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/95xiaoli/DLR-CD</uri>.

title: MEMS Microheater With Fast Electrothermal Response for Self-Destructing Chip Applications
authors: Haoran He, Wanli Ma, Zhiqiang Fan, Kai Xu, Zhiqiang Kang, Wei Zhao, Le Zhang, Xiaojun Qiao, Wenping Geng
abstract: With the advancement of micro-nano manufacturing technologies, microheaters have demonstrated significant application value in fields such as sensing, ignition, and self-destruction systems. This paper designs and fabricates a parallel necked Ni microbridge microheater based on microelectromechanical systems (MEMS), employing a Ti/Cu/Ni metal multilayer structure on a SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>/Si/SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> substrate to achieve a compact, integrable device configuration. Finite element simulations revealed the temperature distribution and evolution patterns under different current conditions, which were subsequently validated experimentally. Test results demonstrated that the device rapidly heats up under constant current drive, reaching a maximum temperature exceeding 600°C at 1.8 A drive conditions, exhibiting excellent electrothermal response characteristics. Further integrated validation demonstrates that the proposed microheater structure can achieve stable and controllable triggering under system-level packaging conditions. Under a driving current of 3 A, the device completes the self-destruction process within 1 s, accompanied by pronounced current drop and voltage surge characteristics, indicating that the designed electrothermal structure can satisfy the ignition requirements of porous silicon energetic chips at the system level, thereby reflecting favorable structural compatibility and design rationality. [2025-0234]

title: Robust Contactless Human Respiration Monitoring amid Moving Individuals Using Wi-Fi
authors: Yanjiao Li, Jie Zhang, Qing Li, Yang Li, Hien Quoc Ngo, Trung Q. Duong, Simon L. Cotton
abstract: Respiratory rate is an important vital sign that can be used to determine human physiological state. In recent years, Wi-Fi-based contactless respiration monitoring has drawn significant attention due to the prevalence of wireless local area network (WLAN) infrastructure. Most existing approaches to respiration monitoring perform well in controlled environments, without the presence of additional moving individuals in the area of interest. A few recent studies have attempted to reduce the impact of other people moving in the vicinity of the target individual. However, these approaches exhibit notable limitations, such as restricting the number of interfering individuals to one, or requiring a direct wired connection between the Wi-Fi transmitter and receiver for synchronization. To address these issues, in this study, we develop a contactless respiration monitoring system using commodity Wi-Fi devices, which we name RoSense. Through a series of empirical studies, we observe that the channel state information (CSI) for subcarriers is significantly affected by the presence of interfering individuals, but a small subset retain relatively clear signal patterns linked to the target’s respiration. Leveraging these findings, RoSense employs a signal power-based subcarrier selection strategy to identify high-quality subcarriers. The selected subcarriers are then aligned to enhance signal gain and fused to complement the weaker periodic parts. Additionally, RoSense periodically detects the quality of subcarriers, selecting the most effective subcarriers to maximize the contribution of high-quality ones. Extensive experiments were performed in real-world settings with 10 volunteers to verify the feasibility and effectiveness of RoSense. Our results demonstrate that RoSense is able to achieve robust respiration monitoring by suppressing the impact of interfering individuals.

title: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
authors: N/A

title: Orthogonal Chirp Division Multiplexing with Cyclic Shift Keying
authors: Rongxin Zhang, Jingyi Liu, Qiqi Su, Weiling Chen, Yi Zhu
abstract: A novel orthogonal chirp division multiplexing (OCDM) scheme with cyclic shift keying (OCDM-CSK), is proposed. Compared with the conventional OCDM, OCDM-CSK embeds additional information by cyclically shifting the selected chirp-carriers according to the input bitstream. At the receiver, the shifted amounts for information recovering are estimated through the autocorrelation property of chirp signals. This design effectively exploits the structural potential of OCDM, achieving an effective trade-off between the data rate and the peak-to-average power ratio (PAPR) and ensuring robustness against burst interference.

title: LiDAR Guided Predictive Beam Optimization in Real-World mobile V2I mmWave Networks
authors: Neha Kumari, Pragya Swami, Irshad Ahmad Ansari
abstract: The proposed method utilizes light detection and ranging (LiDAR) sensory data to predict the optimal beams in highly mobile vehicle-to-infrastructure (V2I) scenarios, and it is evaluated using the DeepSense 6G dataset. Simulation results show that the Temporal Neural Network (TCN) based method outperforms the state-of-the-art model, achieving 98.3% top-1 accuracy in future beam prediction while reducing beam training overhead by 80% and achieving an average power saving of 79.53%. Remarkably, the LiDAR-assisted method operates independently of previous beam information or calibration, providing a reliable alternative to conventional techniques. These results indicate that beam alignment challenges in mmWave and terahertz communication systems can be effectively mitigated by leveraging LiDAR-guided sensing and TCN-based predictive learning.

title: Teaching cars to drive: Toward connected and automated vehicles
authors: Filippos N. Tzortzoglou, Andreas A. Malikopoulos
abstract: In recent decades, society has witnessed significant advance­ments in emerging mobility systems. These systems refer to transportation solutions that incorporate digital technologies, such as automation and connectivity, to support sustainability goals, creating safer, more efficient, and user-centered mobility. Examples include connected and automated vehicles (CAVs), shared mobility services (e.g., carpooling), electrified mobility solutions, and mobility-as-a-service platforms such as Whim, Moovit, and Uber. These innovations have the potential to greatly impact different aspects of mobility such as safety, pollution, comfort, travel time, and fairness. In this article, we explore the current landscape of CAVs. We discuss their role in daily life and their future potential, while also examining the challenges they may introduce. We further review practical challenges in CAVs research, with a focus on simulation and real-world testing of CAV algorithms. Then, existing solutions that aim to overcome these limitations are presented. Finally, we provide an accessible introduction to ­modeling CAVs using basic kinematic principles and offer an open source tutorial to motivate interested students to begin exploring the field.

title: TMTA: a Truthful Multi-Task Allocation Scheme for Enhancing Service Quality in Sparse Mobile Crowdsensing_supp1-3674086.pdf
authors: Qingyong Deng
abstract: In sparse mobile crowdsensing, the platform con-structs services based on low-cost data collection through data inference schemes, where the quality of the inferred data directly affects the service quality. Existing data inference schemes assume that workers report trustworthy data, which is not practical in SMCS. It is urgent to establish a high-quality data collection scheme for data inference that can tolerate false data to enhance inferred data quality. To address this challenge, we propose a Truthful Multi-Task Allocation (TMTA) scheme. First, we estimate the spatiotemporal correlation between areas for iden-tifying areas with high importance to the data inference process. Second, a trust-based multi-task allocation algorithm is proposed to ensure that the sensing data from high-importance areas have high trust levels. Third, a multi-armed bandit based trustworthy worker identification strategy is proposed to prioritize Multi-Task allocation for workers who can be effectively identified as trustworthy. Finally, a truthful discrete heuristic algorithm is proposed to optimize the multi-task allocation using the proposed hybrid neighbor-mode strategy, which reduces the difficulty of searching for high-utility multi-task allocations. Extensive exper-iments on two real-world air-quality datasets demonstrate that TMTA consistently outperforms six baseline methods, achieving average reductions of 24.70% in RMSE and 17.79% in sensing cost across five experimental scenarios.

title: Single-Domain Generalization: A Scattering Characteristic-Driven Cross-Attention Model for Oriented SAR Object Detection
authors: Hailiang Huang, Huangxing Lin, Jingchao Guo, Yue Huang, Xinghao Ding, Huanqiang Zeng
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Oriented object detection in synthetic aperture radar (SAR) imagery often struggles to generalize to unknown domains, due to severe distribution shifts. Single-domain generalization (single-DG) aims to tackle this challenge by learning robust representations from only one source domain. However, existing single-DG methods typically fail to capture the essential features of rotating objects, as they neglect the unique physical properties introduced by the SAR imaging process. To address this, this paper proposes the scattering characteristic-driven cross-attention model (SCCAM), which introduces a heuristic scattering representation to approximate the potential physical structures of SAR objects. Specifically, a pair of homologous images is constructed by perturbing the extremely low- and high-frequency components of the input SAR image, ensuring that they share domain-invariant information. The heuristic attribute scattering center (ASC) features are then extracted to guide a bidirectional cross-attention module, enabling progressive interactions between homologous features. This design effectively mitigates feature distribution discrepancies, thereby facilitating the learning of domain-invariant representations. Experimental results on six single-DG benchmarks demonstrate that the proposed SCCAM significantly outperforms multiple classic and state-of-the-art DG models in oriented object detection across unknown SAR domains.

title: CoShMDM: Contact and Shape-Aware Latent Motion Diffusion Model for Human Interaction Generation_supp1-3675725.pdf
authors: Ali Asghar Manjotho
abstract: Generating realistic two-person interaction motions from text holds immense potential in computer vision and animations. While existing latent motion diffusion models offer compact and efficient representations, they often fail to produce physically plausible contacts and are typically constrained to a single canonical body shape. As a result, the generated motion sequences exhibit substantial mesh penetrations and lack interaction realism. To address these limitations, we propose a contact and shape-aware latent motion representation and diffusion model (CoShMDM) for generating realistic two-person interactions from text. Our framework begins by constructing contact-compatible motion using SMPL-based meshes and a normal alignment-based mesh contact matrix to capture fine-grained mesh-level contacts. To account for shape diversity, we incorporate SMPL shape parameters and iteratively learn contact dynamics across different body shapes. Additionally, a reinforcement learning-based mesh penetration avoidance policy network, guided by signed distance fields, is introduced to minimize mesh penetrations while preserving contact fidelity and shape-aware motion. We further employ a dual-encoder VQ-VAE to learn disentangled latent representations for motion and contacts, which are then utilized in a text- and body-shape-conditioned diffusion model. To ensure spatial, temporal, and semantic coherence, we integrate a novel contact and motion consistency module into the diffusion transformer. Extensive evaluations on the InterHuman and InterX datasets demonstrate that our method outperforms state-of-the-art approaches achieving lowest FID scores (4.801 and 0.013), with 19% and 17.3% reductions in mesh penetrations, and 17.8% and 33.2% gains in contact similarity, respectively.

title: Call for Papers: IEEE Computer Society
authors: N/A

title: Mechanistic Study of FS Layer in IGBT: Exploring the Effects of Hydrogen and Phosphorus Implantation on Performance
authors: Yameng Sun, Anning Chen, Xun Liu, Yifan Song, Yang Zhou, Kun Ma, Sheng Liu
abstract: This study systematically investigates the regulation mechanisms of field-stop (FS) layers formed by hydrogen, phosphorus, and their co-implantation on insulated gate bipolar transistor (IGBT) performance through coupled TCAD simulations and wafer-level experiments. Five FS layer schemes were designed, including pure phosphorus implantation, pure hydrogen implantation with varying doses, and hydrogen–phosphorus co-implantation. The results reveal that pure phosphorus implantation creates a steep doping profile, enabling a high breakdown voltage (BV) and fast dI ${}_{C}$ /dt, though with higher Eoff. Pure hydrogen implantation achieves deep carrier modulation up to $30~\mu $ m, effectively suppressing turn-off tail current, but compromises conduction characteristics and high-temperature stability. The innovative hydrogen–phosphorus co-implantation scheme delivers an optimal tradeoff through synergistic effects; it maintains a low $V_{\textit {CEsat}}$ while achieving the fastest turn-off dV ${}_{\textit {CE}}$ /dt, lowest Etot, and superior high-temperature blocking capability and reliability. Physical analysis indicates that this scheme combines efficient carrier extraction from hydrogen implantation with strong field termination from phosphorus implantation. Experiments, including spreading resistance profiling (SRP) and double-pulse tests, validate the simulation accuracy. This work provides critical design guidelines and theoretical foundations for engineering next-generation FS layers in IGBTs for high-frequency and high-reliability applications.
