title: An Optimisation Model for the Energy-Efficient Design of Spectrometer’s Magnets for High-Energy-Physics Experiments
authors: Valentino Scalera, Daniele Centanni, Daniele Davino, Raffaele Fresa, Antonio Iaiunese, Vincenzo Paolo Loschiavo, Antonio Quercia, Massimiliano De Magistris
abstract: Large aperture high-field magnets are essential in High Energy Physics (HEP) experiments, as those performed at CERN and other primary laboratories, providing fundamental functions, in particular as detectors. Nuclear particle identification is, in fact, basically obtained by analyzing the curvature of their tracks within a given magnetic field. The design of these magnets is a quite well-consolidated process in the literature and laboratories’ practice, based on the balance of detection performance and cost, and meeting all the physical constraints. Nevertheless, a modern design approach includes, within the fundamental goals, minimizing the use of energy/power and, more generally, material resources. Such a requirement is, by the way, reflected in the new general guidelines at primary laboratories for sustainability reasons. We propose an integrated optimization model that prioritizes energy efficiency in the design of large resistive magnets. Using a case study based on the CERN SND@LHC spectrometer magnet, we show how this approach provides new insights into the optimal design trade-off. Specifically, the model identifies a design point that reduces power consumption by approximately 40% while maintaining acceptable detection performance. The analytical model’s accuracy is confirmed by FEM analysis, with detection performance predictions deviating by less than 3% from simulation results. The proposed optimization framework is, in principle, directly extendable to a broader class of magnets, both for research and industrial use.

title: Author Index
authors: N/A

title: Enhanced Predictive Current Control Strategy for PMSMs in the Presence of Parameter Uncertainty
authors: AmirHossein Azad, Hossein Shateri
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: This paper presents an advanced model predictive current control strategy aimed at improving the stator current prediction accuracy of Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors. Due to the strong reliance of predictive control algorithms on accurate system modeling, the proposed controller addresses the challenge of motor parameter uncertainties, which may arise from measurement errors or parameter drift over time. These uncertainties can impair prediction accuracy and degrade control performance. The proposed method incorporates a prediction error compensation mechanism to enhance robustness while effectively reducing current ripple. Additionally, it compensates for PWM delay and inverter dead time, factors that significantly affect real-time control performance. The control strategy is implemented and evaluated through MATLAB simulations. Results demonstrate a dynamic response with minimal current ripple and confirm the controller’s robustness under a range of parameter uncertainties, making it suitable for practical real-time applications.

title: Deep Learning–based Comparative Analysis of CNN Models for Skin Cancer Diagnosis
authors: M Prema Kumar, M. Venkata Subbarao, J T S Sindhu, A Hari Priya, A Neeharika, D Harika
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Early and accurate diagnosis of skin cancer is clinically critical, as delayed or incorrect detection, particularly of aggressive lesions such as melanoma, can significantly increase morbidity and mortality. This work presents a deep learning based comparative analysis of three convolutional neural network architectures, namely MobileNet, EfficientNet, and DenseNet, for multiclass skin lesion classification using the HAM10000 dermatoscopic image dataset comprising seven lesion categories. The models are systematically evaluated under varying training testing splits and multiple training epochs to analyze performance consistency, convergence behaviour, and generalization capability. Experimental results show that MobileNet achieves the highest overall accuracy of 94.9% at a 90:10 split, followed closely by EfficientNet with 94.8% and DenseNet with 94.2%. However, detailed class wise analysis reveals significantly lower precision and recall for clinically critical minority classes such as melanoma and actinic keratosis, despite high overall accuracy. This observation highlights the limitation of relying solely on accuracy for medical decision making. The study further provides confusion matrix based insights into misclassification patterns and discusses their clinical implications. The findings establish a foundation for translating convolutional neural network based skin lesion classifiers into reliable clinical decision support systems while identifying key challenges related to class imbalance and diagnostic risk.

title: Relevance of the NWC SAF Cloud Type product to assess the impact of clouds on Free Space Optical Communication
authors: Nicolas Sebastien, Olivier Liandrat, Guillaume Roussel, Mehdi Ben Slama, Mathieu Turpin, Sébastien Marchal, Nicolas Schmutz
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: Free Space Optical (FSO) communications offer high-bandwidth, secure satellite-to-ground links, but their performance is strongly influenced by atmospheric conditions. Clouds, in particular, can attenuate or fully block the optical signal. To ensure availability, networks of geographically distributed Optical Ground Stations (OGS) must be designed with cloud dynamics in mind. Satellite-derived products, such as the Cloud Type classification from the NWC SAF (Nowcasting Satellite Application Facility), may be used as global, long-term data for preliminary site assessment. Yet, their coarse resolution introduces uncertainty, as a single pixel may encompass varied cloud situations at ground level.To address this, we analyze the relationship between Cloud Type and Cloud Attenuation estimated with a wide-angle thermal infrared imager, the Sky InSight™, at NOAA’s Table Mountain Test Facility. By correlating these high-resolution observations with NWC SAF data (processed from GOES-16 and sourced via ICARE data service), we derive attenuation distributions for each Cloud Type. Results confirm that while Cloud Type is useful for assessing disruption risks, site availability estimates depend heavily on the chosen Cloud-Free Line Of Sight (CFLOS) attenuation threshold and system margin. This highlights the importance of complementing satellite data with ground-based measurements to refine site evaluations and support the design of resilient FSO networks.

title: Comparative Analysis of Optimizers for YOLOv11 based Object Detection
authors: K Kirtika, P Gajalakshmi, S Catherine Priya, G Palanivel
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Optimizers play an important role in the accuracy and efficiency of convolutional neural networks by controlling the updates of convolutional neural networks parameters in the training process. The literature does not have a comprehensive discussion of adaptive optimizers such as Adam, AdamW and AdaBelief on the FLIR ADAS V2 thermal imaging dataset in the context of the YOLOv11 architecture, thus this paper is a novel comparison of these adaptive optimizers. The original contributions encompass: (1) the performance evaluation of AdaBelief as an empirical experiment (with mAP@50, mAP@50-95, and training time as key metrics); (2) the discovery of the optimal hyperparameters, in particular, the fact that a momentum of 0.937 is an effective one to use; and (3), the actionable conclusions about the forging of optimal hyperparameters in place of object detecting systems which use infrared cameras. Findings demonstrate that AdaBelief attains better mAP@50 (0.696) at the lowest training cost (6.999 hrs), which is an important point of reference when operating real-time vision in the defense and autonomous devices.

title: Research on End-to-End SLAM Framework Based on Deep Feature Learning
authors: Yunfeng Wang, Jie Jin, Xiong Chen, Xuyin Gong, Maolin Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: To address the issues of traditional simultaneous localization and mapping (SLAM) that rely on manual features, poor robustness in dynamic scenarios, and complex multimodule coupling, this paper proposes an end-to-end SLAM framework based on deep feature learning. This framework consists of four major modules: the input layer, the multi-scale deep feature extraction layer, the joint estimation layer of pose and semantic map, and the loop closure detection optimization layer. The multi-scale deep feature extraction layer uses an improved ResNet combined with an attention mechanism to enhance the discrimination of key region features. The joint estimation layer of pose and semantic map filters dynamic objects through semantic segmentation to improve map robustness. The loop closure detection optimization layer achieves global consistency correction based on deep feature matching. Experiments on the TUM RGB-D and KITTI Odometry datasets show that the absolute trajectory error (ATE) of this framework in static scenarios is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 8. 2 \%}$</tex> lower than that of ORB-SLAM3. In dynamic scenarios, the map integrity reaches 89.7 % (an increase of 12.5 % compared to DF-SLAM), and the real-time frame rate remains above 25 FPS, which can meet the real-time high-precision requirements of scenarios such as autonomous driving and service robots, effectively breaking through the performance bottleneck of traditional SLAM in complex scenarios.

title: DBSCAN+UKF: Mobility-Predicted Clustering for Digital-Twin-Assisted Vehicular Task Offloading
authors: Xiaoming Yuan, Ruifan Gao, Jiaqi Zhao, Yijing Liu, Jianbo Du, Guangxia Xu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: Digital-twin (DT) enabled vehicular edge computing (VEC) networks integrate edge-side computing resources with cloud-based virtual models to achieve global perception and cooperative task offloading. Numerous offloading scheduling strategies have been proposed to address resource limitations. It is worth noting that the energy consumption of DT includes not only the computational cost of searching for offloading strategies, but also the frequent synchronization overhead required to maintain consistency between the physical and virtual worlds; however, existing studies often overlook the latter, leading to inaccurate energy evaluation and even affecting decision reliability. To address this issue, this paper proposes a lightweight predictiondriven flexible synchronization mechanism for DT-VEC, where DT updates are triggered only when the prediction error obtained by the unscented Kalman filter (UKF) exceeds a predefined threshold. Specifically, we construct a system model that accounts for the energy consumption of local/edge computing, wireless transmission, DT synchronization, and clustering, and formulate a joint optimization problem to minimize the total energy consumption. Finally, the model is combined with a deep reinforcement learning algorithm to adaptively control both offloading actions and synchronization frequency, thereby minimizing system energy consumption with almost no impact on task completion rate. Simulation results show that the proposed algorithm outperforms multiple benchmark schemes.

title: Ultra-Lightweight Knowledge Distillation for Resource-Constrained Skin Lesion Classification
authors: Md Aurongojeb Lishad, Ratul Hasan Ankon, Md. Adnan Arafat
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Skin cancer is a major global health concern, and accurate detection of skin lesions is essential for early diagnosis and treatment. Although deep learning models have achieved impressive results in medical imaging, their large size and high computational requirements restrict their deployment on embedded, mobile, and resource-limited healthcare systems. In this paper, a knowledge distillation (KD) framework is proposed for classifying skin lesions using the HAM10000 dataset. An EfficientNet-B4 model is fine-tuned as a teacher by freezing the first 20% of its layers and retraining it on HAM10000. A lightweight custom CNN student model was then designed with only 148k parameters, using depthwise separable convolutions, residual connections, and dropout layers. The student was trained with both the actual labels and the teacher’s soft predictions. A confidence-masked KD strategy was applied, transferring knowledge only when the teacher was confident. Experimental results demonstrate that KD improved the student’s macro-F1 score from 58.50% to 67.02% and accuracy from 69.20% to 80.43%, while keeping the model extremely small. This shows that knowledge distillation can greatly enhance lightweight models, making them suitable for deployment on low-power devices such as microcontrollers, Raspberry Pi boards, Android smartphones, smart watches and portable dermatoscopic diagnostic units.

title: Multi Objective Optimization Algorithm and Large-scale Data Model Analysis in Urban Power Grid Planning
authors: Shiwei Kong, Jie Han, Yi Qin, Zheng Zhang, YueXian Chen
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: Urban power grid planning is a crucial part of urban infrastructure, which is essential for ensuring stable power supply and promoting economic and social development. In this process, multi-objective optimization algorithms play a crucial role, providing scientific decision support for power grid planning by balancing cost-effectiveness, environmental protection, and power supply reliability. Meanwhile, the rapid development of big data technology has made large-scale data model analysis an important tool for revealing the complex operating rules of the power grid and predicting future electricity consumption trends. This article proposes a novel power grid data analysis platform that cleverly combines particle swarm optimization algorithm (PSO) and machine learning (ML) technology. This platform leverages the global search advantage of PSO and the complex pattern recognition capability of ML to conduct in-depth mining and analysis of power grid operation data, aiming to optimize power grid layout, improve fault prediction precision, and optimize energy allocation. The experimental results show that the platform can not only significantly shorten the power grid planning cycle and reduce operating costs, but also effectively enhance the stability and safety of the power supply system.

title: A Two-Phase Hybrid Improved RRT-ACO Approach for Efficient and Smooth UAV Path Planning
authors: Nour Elimane Elbey, Salim Bitam, Addelhamid Mellouk
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: A fixed-wing unmanned aerial vehicle is widely used in versatile applications. UAVs face several challenges, including path planning and battery capacity limitation. In this paper, we address these challenges by adapting a UAV fixed-wing energy consumption model and proposing a new two-phase approach that combines a sample-based technique with bio-inspired optimization, doubling their advantages and addressing their weaknesses. In the first phase, we improve the Rapidly-exploring Random Tree (RRT) algorithm in terms of convergence efficiency by integrating a goal-biased sampling strategy. In the second phase, we improve Ant Colony Optimization (ACO) for path optimization and refinement, where we propose a dynamic pheromone update mechanism by combining pheromone evaporation with an improved pheromone deposition rule for ensuring path quality. Additionally, to guarantee smoother paths, we utilize cubic spline interpolation, which reduces the number of waypoints and affects only a small segment of the trajectory. Performance evaluation shows that our approach can effectively plan a smooth, shorter, and collision-free path while significantly satisfying all the given constraints.

title: The Influence of the Topological Structure of Multiagent Systems on the Factual Consistency of Collaboratively Generated Text
authors: Enrui Hu
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: This paper addresses the issues of factual errors and “error amplification” in the collaborative text generation task of multi-agent systems (MAS). It designs and explores the impact of two major topological structures, Pipeline and Parallel, as well as different agent configurations on the factual consistency of generated texts, and verifies the existence of the error amplification effect, while exploring its associated patterns. This paper integrates GAIR-NLP/FacTool as the factchecking backend and designs 12 experimental configurations (2 single-agent baselines <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{+} \mathbf{1 0}$</tex> multi-agent configurations) to systematically evaluate the performance of Qwen-Plus and Qwen-Max under pure model, hybrid model, positional influence, and identity influence settings. Experimental data show that the single-agent Qwen-Max baseline performs the best with no error amplification phenomenon; in multi-agent systems, the overall performance of the Parallel topology is significantly better than that of the Pipeline topology; and experimental configurations such as identity and position also affect the factual consistency of the text to varying degrees. This paper reveals the regulatory role of multi-agent topological structures on factual consistency and provides data support and practical guidance for the design of collaborative systems with low error amplification rates. For more details about the code, please refer to: HenryHu1031/Evaluation-of-Fact-Consistency-Emergence-in-Multi-Agent-Text-Generation.

title: DeepSpeechNet: End to End Speech Recognition
authors: Ankita Hazra, MD Sharique Hussian, Moumita Pradhan
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Communication plays an important role in our daily lives. Our aim is to achieve a novel speech recognition system (SRS) for effective communication process. Here we have used efficient deep learning based model to accomplish proper decoding technique for speech recognition. The DeepSpeechNet model follows an end-to-end learning framework using a hybrid CNN-BiLSTM architecture with CTC loss. Some existing speech recognition model facing trouble for isolated word recognition, some facing low accuracy etc. Our proposed model, DeepSpeechNet can deal with such limitations and provide better approach. We emphasised on Mel Frequency Cepstral Coefficient (MFCC) feature extraction and resampled 16 kHz audio signal.The model is trained and evaluated on the LJSpeech 1.1 dataset, consisting of high quality single speaker English speech recordings. 1000 samples are used initially to train our model. DeepSpeechNet achieves 97.5% accuracy, word error rate (WER) 4.88 and character error rate (CER) 0.88. The experimental result is demonstrating the improved performance compared to existing CNN and RNN based speech recognition model.

title: A Blockchain-Integrated Smart Sensor System for Real-Time Monitoring and Automation using Telegram Bot
authors: K.G. Suhirdham, G. Mohanavel, Sonika C, S. Prabavathi, K Kannaiah, P. Santhi
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: This paper presents a blockchain-integrated smart sensor system for real-time monitoring and automation using a Telegram-based interface. The proposed architecture combines IoT sensors, edge gateways, encrypted MQTT/HTTP communication, and a private blockchain to ensure secure, scalable, and reliable data management. Sensor nodes collect real-time data and transmit it to gateways using MQTT over TLS, while HTTPS is used for backend interactions, providing end-to-end confidentiality and integrity. To support large-scale deployments, a hierarchical key management approach is adopted, where gateways manage authentication and blockchain interaction on behalf of resource-constrained sensors. The blockchain layer operates in a consortium setting and employs low-latency Proof of Authority and Practical Byzantine Fault Tolerance consensus mechanisms to achieve fast transaction finality with minimal computational overhead. The system also incorporates local buffering and asynchronous synchronization to maintain data consistency and dependable Telegram notifications under intermittent network connectivity. Performance evaluation includes a comparison with a non-blockchain Telegram-based IoT system under identical conditions, demonstrating that blockchain integration introduces moderate latency overhead while significantly enhancing data integrity, auditability, and resistance to tampering. The results indicate that the proposed system provides a practical trade-off between performance and security for real-time IoT applications.

title: Synthesis of a Nonlinear PID-Controller based on a Neural Network Representation for Adaptive Control of Dynamic Objects
authors: Umurzakova Dilnoza Maxamadjanovna, Khakimov Akhror Abdimakhamatovich, Akhmadjonov Ikhtiyorjon Ravshanjon Ugli
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Science and Communications Technologies (ICISCT)
abstract: This study introduces an innovative approach to constructing neuro-PID controllers utilizing neural network architectures for regulating dynamic systems with time-varying characteristics. The proposed framework reconceptualizes the discrete PID structure as a two-layered artificial neural network, wherein the initial layer incorporates nonlinear activation mechanisms while the subsequent layer maintains linear transfer properties. The synthesis methodology unfolds through distinct stages: preliminary computation of standard PID parameters to establish second-layer connection weights, followed by optimization of first-layer nonlinear activation profiles to elevate transient performance metrics. Global search across parameter domains employs genetic evolution strategies, ensuring solution discovery within multimodal optimization landscapes. Simulation analyses reveal that replacing traditional linear PID architectures with the developed neuro-PID framework yields significant performance enhancements for nonlinear process control, manifested through minimized steady-state errors, reduced overshoot percentages, and shortened settling durations. The methodology preserves the intuitive structure and design transparency inherent to classical PID control, positioning it as a practical solution for modernizing industrial control infrastructure across manufacturing operations, power generation plants, and related automation sectors.

title: Dual-Signal Validation for Reliable Transformer-Driven Higher-Order Question Generation
authors: Jay Nanavati, Sanskruti Patel, Unnati Patel
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Current transformer models fail to satisfy two requirements that define higher-order assessment: consistent semantic grounding and genuine cognitive depth. Most existing systems provide no mechanism to verify whether a generated question is close to the primary theme of the source passage or reaches the analytical or evaluative level expected in higher-order reasoning tasks. This paper introduces a dual-signal validation framework designed to address these limitations directly. The proposed method evaluates each candidate question using two independent signals: a semantic alignment score derived from sentence-level embeddings and a cognitive difficulty score obtained from a classifier trained on upper-tier Bloom’s taxonomy patterns. Candidate questions that fail to satisfy both signals are removed through a deterministic filtering stage. Experiments conducted across six instructional domains indicate that this post-generation validation step produces more reliable questions than conventional transformer outputs, improving both semantic fidelity and higher-order cognitive alignment without requiring extensive fine-tuning. The framework yields consistent gains of +0.06 BLEU, +0.16 ROUGE-L, and 0.4–0.6 points in human cognitive ratings relative to baseline models. These results suggest that the absence of an explicit validation mechanism represents a central weakness in existing question-generation approaches, and that dual-signal validation offers a practical and effective remedy.

title: Triple Sorting-Based Post-Training Quantization for Low-Bit Vision Transformers
authors: Jiawei Yang, Weidong Cao, Yongqiang Xie, Zhongbo Li
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Vision Transformers (ViTs) have recently achieved remarkable success in visual recognition tasks due to their strong global modeling capability. However, their massive parameter scale and complex activation distributions pose significant challenges for low-bit quantization. In particular, activations from Softmax and GELU exhibit both dense clustering near zero and heavy-tailed distributions, leading to severe precision loss under uniform quantization. To address this issue, we propose Triple Sorting-Based Post-Training Quantization (TR-PTQ), a novel framework that designs three adaptive sorting quantizers tailored to ViT activations. Specifically, (1) the Softmax Sorting Quantizer employs Top-k sorting and logarithmic quantization to retain key attention weights while mitigating long-tail redundancy; (2) the GELU-CDF Matching Quantizer aligns quantization intervals with the cumulative distribution function (CDF), allocating denser bins near zero to enhance resolution in high-frequency regions; and (3) the LayerNorm Partition Quantizer utilizes quantile-based partitioning to combine high-resolution central quantization with logarithmic tail compression. Experiments on ImageNet and COCO demonstrate that under 4-bit quantization, TR-PTQ improves DeiT-B accuracy by2.62% over RepQ-ViT, achieving 78.23% Top-1 with only 3.62% degradation from full precision, highlighting its effectiveness and robustness in low-bit ViT quantization.

title: Real-Time Surrogate Modeling and Control-Oriented Analysis of Opioid-Induced Neural Dynamics
authors: Minakshi Arya, Shubhavi Arya, Saatvik Arya
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Fentanyl-induced neural dynamics are a demanding testbed for control-oriented modeling due to nonlinear ion-channel mechanisms and steep, multi-decade dose–response behavior. A framework is presented that couples mechanistic simulators (NEURON: Hodgkin-Huxley, Izhikevich, Integrate-and-Fire) with data-driven surrogates for real-time estimation and automation-ready analysis. Using human-sourced pharmacology IUPHAR (International Union of Basic and Clinical Pharmacology) inhibition constant K<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">i</inf>: µ=0.39 nM, κ=62 nM, δ=255 nM) and 0.01–10 nM sweeps, control-relevant targets (voltage change, current change, spike frequency) are assembled and twelve regression models are evaluated. Hold-out tests show target heterogeneity: current and spike are accurately predicted (coefficient of determination R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> ≈ 0.8–1.0), whereas voltage change remains difficult (all models R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> ≤ 0). Regularized baselines (Ridge/Elastic Net/k-nearest neighbors (KNN)) achieve the best mean cross-target performance, outperforming tree/boosting ensembles in this dataset. One-way analysis of variance (ANOVA) confirms a large between dose effect for voltage change (F ≈ 7.15×103, p ≪ 10−10). Receptor-occupancy priors (Hill n=1) are integrated to constrain surrogates and outline uses in safety-aware observers, surrogate-in-the-loop simulation, and controller-in-the-loop testing; additionally, a pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic (PK/PD)-informed observer is presented that links predictions to bedside ventilation metrics (respiratory rate(RR); tidal volume(TV); end-tidal CO2(EtCO2)). The pipeline enables compact, reproducible, real-time prediction and automation-grade evaluation of nonlinear neurobiophysical responses, and transfers to other biophysical plants (e.g., cardiac myocytes, insulin–glucose regulation, neuromuscular/optogenetic systems) where fast observers are needed.

title: Design and validation of a CCSDS O3K synchronization front-end, tailor made for a non-coherent 10 Gbps GEO optical downlink
authors: Alain Thomas, Alexandre Le Bian, Alain Quentel, Géraldine Artaud, Jean-Frédéric Chouteau, Sylvain Poulenard, Thomas Anfray, Raphaël Le Bidan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: Radiofrequency spectrum congestion, SWaP constraints, link security requirements, and OISL maturity advocate for direct-to-Earth optical links, despite the challenges of trans-atmospheric optical communications. Several standards have been released, including CCSDS for non-coherent optical communications [1], SDA [2], and ESTOL [3], while others are under development [4]. For GEO direct-to-Earth optical communication links, preamplifier-based detection schemes provide high sensitivity but require adaptive optics for single-mode fiber coupling. Strong turbulence can generate deep signal fading, potentially causing loss of clock data recovery (CDR) lock, frame synchronization loss, or misdetection of in-band signaling. Rather than increasing onboard power or OGS aperture, robust solutions require appropriate framing structures and receiver algorithms resilient to signal fading. In the CCSDS non-coherent optical communications standard [1], the O3K (Optical On-Off Keying) framing scheme is well suited to this scenario and applicable to higher-performance modulation schemes such as DPSK. However, performance critically depends on CDR sensitivity, reacquisition time, and frame synchronizer strategy. This paper evaluates optical link performance, focusing on synchronization aspects from three perspectives: simulation, laboratory measurements, and real satellite-to-ground link measurements. We first present the simulation model for evaluating the communication chain between a GEO satellite and an optical ground station under strong turbulence, then provide results for different code rates and interleavings defined in the CCSDS non-coherent in-band signaling standard.

title: Research on Accurate Valuation of Farmland Assets and Plot Identification Model Based on UAV Multispectral Images
authors: Li Lin
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: Aiming at the problems existing in current farmland asset valuation and land parcel identification, such as "vague land parcel boundary, large deviation in economic value evaluation and insufficient fusion of multi-source data", this study proposes a multi-modal framework integrating UAV five-band multi-spectral images, GIS and agricultural statistical information. Firstly, an improved U-Net++ land parcel recognition model is constructed, and CBAM attention mechanism and "spectrum-texture-topology" triple loss function are introduced. In the test of 1,258 land parcels in North China Plain, 86.9% mIoU and 0.83 boundary F1-score are achieved, which are 8.6% and 15.3% higher than the baseline model, and the recall rate of small land parcels is 82.7%. Secondly, the XGBoost+1D-CNN mixed estimation model is designed to extract 23-dimensional spectrum, texture, terrain and dynamic characteristics of growth period, and realize the joint prediction of yield and value at plot level. The estimated MAPE is reduced to 14.3%, which is significantly better than the single model. In the cross-validation of black soil region in Northeast China, the model still maintains an estimation error of 84.2% mIoU and 16.8%, which verifies its cross-regional generalization ability. This study provides a high-precision and transferable technical path for digital management of farmland assets.

title: AI-Driven Carbon Footprint & ESG Reporting Platform
authors: N. Giridharan, Dhayanithi V, Sachin S, Shalini J K
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Sustainability has become a strategic priority for contemporary organizations, with carbon footprint measurement and Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) reporting playing a crucial role in achieving climate and regulatory objectives. However, conventional sustainability assessment approaches rely heavily on manual data collection, fragmented systems, and incomplete records, which limit accuracy and scalability. This paper presents an AI-based Carbon Footprint and ESG Reporting Platform that integrates data from multiple organizational sources, including energy consumption, employee commuting, supply chain activities, and project-level resource utilization, to generate a unified and comprehensive sustainability profile. The proposed platform applies artificial intelligence techniques for automated data validation, intelligent gap filling, and accurate estimation of direct, indirect, and supply chain emissions using standardized emission factors. Quantitative evaluation demonstrates improved estimation reliability and reduced data inconsistency when compared with traditional reporting methods. Additionally, sustainability scoring mechanisms, interactive dashboards, and predictive analytics provide actionable insights to support long-term strategic decision-making. By enabling real-time data integration and AI-driven analysis, the platform enhances transparency, operational efficiency, and compliance with global sustainability frameworks, thereby supporting organizations in achieving measurable emission reduction and responsible sustainability management.

title: Neuro-ElectroFusion based Intelligent Embedded System for Adaptive Biomedical Signal Enhancement
authors: Gayatri Joshi, R.Prabhu, Kotteeswaran Rangasamy, B.Mallikeswari, R.Thiagarajan, R.Krishnamoorthy
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Signal enhancement of biomedical signals is an important area of real-time biomedical monitoring, particularly in resource-constrained embedded systems. Conventional filtering methods tend to confuse noise and critical signal changes whereas pure neural methods require a lot of computation which is inapplicable to low-power wearable devices. This paper presents Neuro-ElectroFusion which is an intelligent embedded system that provides a lightweight neural processing with rule-based signal correction that would provide adaptive improvement of multi-modal biomedical signals, including EEG, ECG, and EMG. The system is based on a quantized 1D-CNN-BiLSTM architecture and a fuzzy logic unit that dynamically denoises and reconstructs signals, depending on the context and physiological state. An entropy-based event-driven sampling scheme consumes considerably less energy and latency and has no fidelity trade-offs. PhysioNet and MIT-BIH performance On PhysioNet and MIT-BIH data sets, the average SNR improvement of 13.7 dB, the reduction in RMSE of 73.4% and the real-time latency of the inference of 16.3 ms per signal window were obtained. The proposed system recorded a high accuracy of 97.68% in various noise levels, which was higher than the traditional DSP-based and TinyML models. Moreover, context-conscious Q-learning was capable of providing adaptive signal prioritization and it was noteworthy that over the baseline systems, context-aware Q-learning improved energy efficiency by 17.6%.

title: Fatigue crack growth analysis in generator shafts
authors: Pouyan Shahabi, Arsalan Ajdani, Meysam Davoudabadi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: presence of crack in rotating machinery can have a detrimental effect on this expensive component and lower their reliability significantly. Despite the recent advances in forging techniques avoidance of flaws is still unreachable specifically for large components such as turbo-generator shafts. This paper presents a general approach with safety measurements for assessment of fatigue crack growth life time of a 370 MW generator shaft using finite element stress analysis and analytical linear fracture mechanic theories. Using this procedure one can calculate the maximum permissible initial flaw diameter observed in non-destructive ultrasonic examination and compute the fatigue life time of the component.

title: Breast Cancer Prediction using a Stacked Ensemble of XGBoost and LightGBM with Logistic Regression Meta-Learning
authors: Ankita Joseph, Jossy George, Bosco Paul Alapatt
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Breast cancer remains one of the major reasons for cancer deaths in women, which is why it is key to develop and improve diagnostic systems for accurate predictions. Currently, the advent of Machine learning has helped in providing powerful algorithms to achieve advancements in cancer detection. However, the main motivation of this research is to focus on building more complex ensemble architectures, as they are known for significantly improving predictive accuracy, robustness, and generalisation, especially in performing complex tasks such as medical diagnosis. In this research, a Hybrid stacking ensemble was built using two gradient boosting techniques, XGBoost and LightGBM, with a Logistic Regression meta-learner to predict breast cancer and compare their performance with standard classifiers. The Breast Cancer Wisconsin (Diagnostic) dataset, which consists of 569 patient records, was utilised for model training and analysis. The data was preprocessed using Z-score normalisation and stratified 5-fold cross-validation. The machine learning algorithms, such as Decision Tree, Logistic Regression, and Random Forest, were compared with the hybrid model, and the metrics used for comparison were accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and ROC-AUC. The proposed hybrid model performed well, achieving a high accuracy rate of 97.37% and a recall rate of 93.00% for malignant cases. McNemar’s test (p > 0.05) confirms that this accuracy rate is statistically equivalent to the Random Forest classifier. These findings proved that the proposed model can perform optimally in predicting complex data with the same degree of precision as the standard models. Therefore, the hybrid model can be considered a robust and reliable new alternative for breast cancer prediction.

title: Development and Research of an Intelligent Adaptive Neural Network System for Distance Learning based on Artificial Intelligence Technologies
authors: Umurzakova Dilnoza Maxamadjanovna, Khasanova Madina Utkir Kizi, Qadamova Zulayho Erkinjon Qizi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Science and Communications Technologies (ICISCT)
abstract: This article presents the development and experimental verification of an intelligent adaptive neural network system for distance learning based on artificial intelligence technologies. The proposed system combines modern methods of deep learning, natural language processing and reinforcement learning to provide a personalized educational process. A new four-level architecture has been implemented, including intelligent student diagnostics, dynamic content personalization, predictive learning trajectory adaptation, and intelligent feedback assessment. The results obtained confirm the effectiveness of the intelligent adaptive approach in overcoming the limitations of traditional distance learning systems and demonstrate significant potential for widespread implementation in educational systems.

title: DANE-Net: A Feature-Driven Framework for Dynamic Parameter Optimization
authors: Jinzhe Zhao, Yiyang Hong, Kangyu Zhao, Wanping Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: This study proposes DANE-Net, a novel adaptive audio coding framework driven by deep learning to overcome the inherent limitations of conventional fixed-parameter codecs. Its core innovation is a multimodal feature fusion mechanism that extracts discriminative 13-dimensional audio features to drive dynamic optimization of encoding parameters. The framework employs a hybrid dual-branch neural network architecture, integrating 1D convolutional layers for local timefrequency pattern extraction and dense layers for statistical feature abstraction. Bidirectional LSTM layers are utilized to effectively capture temporal dependencies within audio signals. Furthermore, a dynamic bitrate mapping model (bitrate <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=64+ 20 \cdot \text{DR}+0.05 \cdot \text{SC}$</tex>) and a semantic-aware codec selection mechanism are established through comprehensive feature analysis. Experimental results validate the framework's efficacy: for music samples, it achieves 88.5% fidelity while requiring only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.06 x$</tex> the storage of reference solutions under equivalent subjective quality requirements. For speech samples, fidelity reaches 78.9%—a significant 18.9% improvement over fixedparameter codecs. Notably, the lightweight LSTM design enables encoding latency below 8 ms, operating 2.3 times faster than CNN-only implementations. This breakthrough provides a new paradigm balancing storage efficiency and audio fidelity for 5G real-time communication and high-quality streaming services.

title: Dual-Sided Adaptive Prompt Learning for Low-Resource Short Text Classification
authors: Yufei Geng
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Short text classification remains challenging due to sparse context, implicit semantics, and the reliance of promptbased methods on manually crafted templates. This paper proposes a low-resource adaptive prompt learning framework that places adaptive prompt sets on both sides of the input and generates text-specific prompts via a QKV attention mechanism, improving robustness to diverse semantic distributions. In addition, candidate label words are produced by a large language model and refined through a multi-stage filtering procedure to construct a reliable verbalizer with minimal human effort. Experiments on six low-resource TweetEval tasks show consistent improvements over strong baselines, demonstrating the effectiveness and generalization of the proposed method.

title: A Low SWaP-C Pointing and Tracking Architecture for Free Space Optical Communications between Aerial Platforms
authors: Martin Monaghan, Gerald M. Bonner, Gordon M. H. Flockhart, Craig Michie, Adam Selyem
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: Free space optical communications are emerging as an alternative to radio as the RF spectrum becomes increasingly congested and unavailable. Secure communications at far higher data rates than radio are theoretically possible in the optical domain, however this is made difficult by the presence of attenuation and misalignment failures which necessitate advanced beam steering techniques. For communications between aerial platforms such as drones, constraints on the size, weight and power of beam steering systems become prohibitive. Developing beam steering systems which are light, compact and efficient is the key to unlocking optical communications on mobile platforms. Here, we demonstrate closed loop pointing and tracking of a moving retroreflector target using cheap and lightweight components, directed by an Arduino Due microcontroller. The system is found to be capable of tracking targets moving at up to 100 milliradians per second, and is fundamentally limited by the dynamics of the mirror. Metrics of time spent optimally aligned, root-mean-square distance from target centre and statistical analyses of beam position are used to assess the feasibility of the system. We show that in a retroreflector configuration the system spends 100% of the measured interval within 0.1 milliradian of perfect alignment at angular velocities up to 90 milliradians per second.

title: Wireless Cooperative Lightweight Ad Hoc Network Control Method Considering Wireless Communication Network Congestion
authors: Xiong Xiao, Zhongfeng Wang, Shuai Shao, Ligang Li, Shijie Cui
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: With the development of wireless networks, the demand for location information in various applications has been increasing rapidly. In wireless communication scenarios, when the number of communication devices in the same area is excessive and the amount of data transmitted by these devices exceeds the carrying capacity of wireless channels, problems such as increased data transmission time, signal transmission interruptions, and data loss may occur-thereby affecting the normal operation of wireless communication functions. To address this phenomenon, a wireless cooperative lightweight ad hoc network control method is proposed. Firstly, a TDMA superframe structure model is constructed.Secondly, lightweight optimization is achieved through reference node position observation, broadcast node optimization, and intelligent time slot allocation. This process integrates a three-stage superframe design, Age of Information (Aol) quantization, and Fisher Information Matrix (FIM) performance analysis, with the Proximal Distance Descent (PDD) algorithm and a greedy algorithm deployed as core tools.Thirdly, the proposed method enables mutual transmission of relevant measurement information between devices.Finally, this approach reduces transmission time while enhancing both positioning accuracy and reliability.

title: Modular and Scenario-based Cybersecurity Curriculum with Real-Time Threat Intelligence Integration
authors: Poleboina Divya, Shaik Mohammed Zaki, Mahammad Akram, Sabbani Pavana Sutha Nithin, N. Purushotham, Javvaji Venkatarao
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Most university cybersecurity education is designed and implemented based on various well-established frameworks like CSEC2017 and the NICE Workforce Framework. While these standards are good to begin with, they are neither flexible nor provide practical exposure to rapidly changing threat landscapes. This paper, on the other hand, presents a modular, scenario-based cybersecurity curriculum that amalgamates manually curated real-time threat intelligence to develop human analytical capabilities instead of building artificial intelligence or machine learning models. The proposed system integrated theoretical modules, practical simulations, and scenario-driven learning environments through Streamlit-based application. A dynamic library of threat scenarios will be mapped against specific job roles and skill requirements; hands-on simulation labs, using open-source tools such as Metasploit, Snort, Wireshark, and Kali Linux, will enable learners to analyze and mitigate threats in real time. This allows the content administrators and instructors to update the content on a role-based, securely designed interface with SQLite data management and SHA-256 authentication. Academic and industry expert-led quarterly workshops ensure the continuous integration of emergent threat intelligence and region-specific challenges. By focusing on manual reasoning, problem-solving, and situational awareness, the proposed system closes the gap between academic training and industry readiness for future cybersecurity professionals to respond effectively to evolving digital threats.

title: Comparative Study of Dissolved Gas Analysis on Transformer Insulating Liquids (Mineral Oil and Natural Ester) Under Arcing Fault Conditions
authors: Pebi Wahyudin, Suwarno
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: Transformer insulating liquids are essential for providing electrical insulation and thermal dissipation in power transformers, and their condition strongly affects system reliability. During abnormal operating conditions, electrical arcing may occur and trigger chemical degradation of these liquids, leading to the formation of dissolved gases that can be detected through Dissolved Gas Analysis (DGA). In this study, controlled arcing faults were produced using an AC voltage source applied to a needle–plane electrode arrangement to investigate gas generation behavior in two types of transformer oils: mineral oil and natural ester oil. Variations in arcing intensity were achieved by adjusting the electrode gap and arcing duration, with applied voltages ranging from 15 kV to 25 kV and exposure periods between 10 and 30 minutes. After each arcing treatment, DGA was carried out to measure key gases such as hydrogen (H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>), methane (CH<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</inf>), acetylene (C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>), ethylene (C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</inf>), ethane (C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</inf>), Carbon monoxide (CO), and carbon dioxide (CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>). The findings show that electrical arcing substantially increases gas formation in both oils, but with distinct compositional patterns. The results show that the Duval Triangle Method consistently classifies both mineral oil and natural ester oil as D1 fault type, indicating low-energy electrical discharges. In contrast, the TDGC analysis reveals differences in gas composition, where natural ester oil exhibits higher condition levels for gases such as H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, and C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</inf>, while mineral oil demonstrates more stable gas concentrations.

title: Zero-Shot Anomaly Prediction in Distributed Systems via Meta-LearningSubtitle as needed
authors: Zhijun Wang, Ao Zhu, Yilun Wu, Ke Wu, Yueting Li, Yihan Xue
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: This study addresses the difficulty of enumerating anomaly patterns in distributed systems under highly dynamic workloads, multi-node coupling, and strong heterogeneity, and proposes a zero-shot anomaly prediction method that integrates a meta learning mechanism to overcome the limited generalization of traditional supervised models when target anomaly samples are missing. The method first builds a temporal structural joint representation network for high-dimensional monitoring data and encodes complex system behavior through service link associations, node states, and system topology. It then designs a cross-task meta knowledge extraction module to learn transferable structural invariants from multi-scenario system data so that the model can adapt quickly to new tasks and new environments. Based on this foundation, a semantic mapping space and a structural consistency constraint mechanism are introduced to form stable inference representations and support the recognition of unseen anomaly types. Finally, a zero-shot inference module combines shared meta knowledge with the current system state to achieve the prediction of unknown anomalies. Through the coordinated modeling of these components, the method produces reliable anomaly predictions even without target anomaly samples and shows strong robustness to system non-stationarity, structural drift, and task variation. The study provides a scalable data-driven solution for intelligent operations in distributed systems and offers a new technical path for addressing anomaly diversity and label scarcity.

title: Bird Audio Classification Using HuBERT: A Robust Solution Across Diverse Dataset Scales
authors: Bowen Tan, Yifan Wen, Hang Yang, Liye Mao, Jingjue Tang, Qijun Zhao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: Bird audio classification is critical for bioacoustic research and ecological monitoring, yet existing methods exhibit significant performance variations across datasets of different scales, with limited systematic cross-scale validation. To address this, we propose a HuBERT-based self-supervised learning approach for bird audio classification. By freezing the HuBERT feature extractor, introducing an intelligent attention mask mechanism, designing a mask-aware GAP strategy, and employing a multi-layer classification head, we successfully adapt HuBERT for bird vocalization classification, achieving superior performance on both large- and small-scale datasets. Experimental results demonstrate that our method achieves 91% precision and F1 score on large-scale datasets and maintains $\mathbf{7 5 \%}$ precision and F1 score on small-scale datasets, significantly outperforming traditional transfer learning and deep learning approaches. Our contributions include: (1) systematic validation of HuBERT’s efficacy in bird audio classification across diverse dataset scales; (2) novel intelligent attention mask and mask-aware GAP strategies, enabling the adaptation of a speech recognition model to bird audio classification with broad applicability; and (3) A cross-scale dataset solution is provided for bird audio classification.

title: Zero-Overhead Covert Telemetry for Small Satellites via Quantized Housekeeping Data
authors: G. Hemanth Kumar, Dilip Kumar Jang Bahadur Saini, Y. Dileep Kumar, Prajwalasimha S N, Sagar Kolekar, Neha Divekar
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The operation of Small satellites which include CubeSats faces major challenges because they must work with extremely limited resources for mass and power and hardware capabilities. The research describes a zero-overhead covert telemetry system which uses housekeeping sensor data quantization to hide low-rate covert data without requiring new hardware or changes to existing protocols. The proposed method embeds hidden information into the least significant bits of quantized housekeeping parameters which satellite systems already transmit through their regular telemetry operations. The simulation of a 3U CubeSat representative system enables the method to transmit data at 0.600 bits per second while it maintains outstanding statistical stealth through its Kullback–Leibler divergence measurement of 7.70×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−5</sup>. The system operates at a 10 dB signal-to-noise ratio to achieve a bit error rate of 2.5×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−5</sup> which does not affect the normal telemetry operations. The software-based system operates at 1 mW power consumption while it does not need any weight addition to create deniable communication for satellite systems which have restricted resources.

title: Research on Ship Target Classification Based on Deep Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Yuanchao Wu, Tingxuan Han, Yinuo Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: With the continuous improvement of intelligent shipping and maritime supervision needs, vision-based ship target classification has become a key technology to enhance maritime situational awareness capabilities. To address challenges in maritime scenarios such as large target scale variations, high inter-class similarity, and difficulty in recognizing small-sample categories, this paper proposes a ResNet-50 ship classification method based on transfer learning. By introducing a targeted data augmentation strategy, the model's ability to distinguish small-scale targets and categories with similar appearances is effectively improved. Results show that the proposed method achieves an average accuracy of 98 % on the test set, with F1 scores exceeding 0.918 for all categories and an average AUC of 0.998. This verifies the model's high precision and strong robustness in complex maritime environments. This study provides an efficient and reliable visual classification solution for intelligent ship perception, and has positive significance for promoting the development of intelligent ship environment cognition systems and improving the intelligence level of maritime traffic management.

title: Design of Intelligent Attendance System Based on STM32
authors: Wei Yu, Jinyang Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: This intelligent attendance system based on STM32 has three functions, namely facial recognition, fingerprint recognition, and password recognition. In terms of hardware, the system includes a 0.96-inch OLED screen, an FM225 facial recognition module for facial feature recognition, an SFM-V1.7 integrated semiconductor capacitor fingerprint module for fingerprint feature recognition, a 4*4 matrix buttons for system control, an SR04 ultrasonic sensor module for monitoring whether there is a person in front of the camera to control the camera's on and off. In terms of software, Keil5 is used to build a development environment for programming, and the IIC and USART serial communication protocols are utilized to achieve independent communication between each module and STM32. The built-in storage chip is used to store information, and FLASH is used to store passwords to prevent data loss during power outages. After multiple debugging and optimizations, the system could run stably and efficiently.

title: An Optimized Algorithm for Ship Detection in Load Test Data Mining Based on YOLOv8
authors: Xin Liu, Guangtai Zhang, Jin Lin, Xi Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Ship detection is a key support for maritime traffic safety and port scheduling, but complex sea conditions, ship scale differences and occlusion problems, coupled with the limitations of low infrared image resolution, high cost of remote sensing images and insufficient timeliness, make it difficult to detect objects and insufficient occlusion processing in shore-mounted visible light scenarios become the core bottlenecks. Based on YOLOv8 and combined with the idea of load test data mining, an improved YOLOv8 algorithm is proposed: First, a high-frequency enhanced residual block is designed to replace the middle-layer C2f module of the backbone network, and enhances the ability to capture high-frequency information via the “channel splitting-dual-branch extraction-fusion residual” architecture, which helps to mine detailed features such as ship edge and small target contour in load test data, and solves the problem of feature loss at multiple scales. The second is to introduce Occlusion-aware Attention-based Detection Head with occlusion perception attention at the detection head, and generate attention maps through multi-head deep separable convolution, fully connected layer and feature fusion, so as to strengthen the mining of target features in the occlusion area in the load test data and alleviate the positioning bias and feature loss. The overall average precision at 0.5 intersection over union (IoU) of the model reaches 0.842, which has excellent detection accuracy for ore ships, passenger ships, container ships and other types of ships, especially in the robustness of small target recognition and occlusion scenarios, which is better than the mainstream ship detection methods, which fully proves the effectiveness of the algorithm in the fusion application of load test data mining and ship detection. In summary, the improved framework proposed in this paper combined with data mining technology effectively improves the accuracy and overall robustness of ship detection in complex scenarios of shoreborn-borne visible light, and provides a better technical solution for actual port and waterway supervision.

title: FPGA Implementation of Chaos-Enhanced Bat Algorithm for Real-Time Constrained Engineering Optimization
authors: Rabab Ouchker, Ismail Mchichou, Mohamed Amine Tahiri, Hicham Amakdouf
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: Hardware acceleration of metaheuristic algorithms represents a critical advancement for real-time optimization in resource-constrained embedded systems. This paper presents a comprehensive FPGA implementation of the Bat Algorithm (BA) incorporating chaotic maps to enhance population diversity and avoid local minima. The proposed architecture, designed in VHDL with Q(16.16) fixed-point arithmetic, is validated on three classical benchmark functions and applied to the Welded Beam Design Problem (WBDP), a multi-constrained engineering optimization problem. Results demonstrate that integrating the chaotic logistic map significantly improves solution quality compared to standard BA, with negligible hardware overhead (5% LUTs) on a Xilinx Artix-7 FPGA. The implementation achieves solution quality comparable to the best software metaheuristics (difference ${\lt}0.3 \%$ on WBDP) while offering superior energy efficiency, demonstrating the viability of hardware-accelerated metaheuristics for real-time embedded optimization.

title: Predicting Diseases with Precision using Machine Learning
authors: T. Kavitha, Bhavishya L Kondaveeti, N Keerthana, Pranava Gopavaram, S Priyanka
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: With continuous progress in medical technology, the capacity to predict diseases more precisely has emerged as a transformative development in healthcare. By applying advanced data analytics in combination with machine learning methods, medical professionals can detect emerging health risks much earlier, allowing prompt action that enhances patient care and reduces strain on healthcare services. A major advantage of predictive modelling lies in its ability to pinpoint individuals who are at elevated risk even before symptoms appear. Such models generate personalized risk profiles, allowing people to take preventive measures to safeguard their health. At the organizational level, this knowledge facilitates greater efficiency in resource allocation, guide preventive strategies, and facilitate targeted programs for specific patient groups. Ultimately, predictive approaches enhance the effective use of healthcare resources, reduce unnecessary expenditures, and contribute to overall cost savings, while simultaneously advancing the goal of proactive and patient-centered care.

title: MATD3-Based Optimization for UAV Edge Computing Task Offloading
authors: Hongyu Pi, Xing He, Xutao Yang
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Leveraging their rapid deployability and native edge-computing capabilities, Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) are increasingly utilized for communication recovery and sensing in large-scale disasters. However, limited energy, bandwidth, and coverage make their cooperative deployment particularly challenging, especially under sparse and time-varying user demands.This paper formulates a cooperative multi-UAV mobile edge computing (MEC) scheduling problem, where each UAV jointly decides its movement and task offloading ratios to minimize a latency–energy objective. We develop a continuous-action multi-agent actor–critic algorithm (MATD3) enhanced with prioritized experience replay and policy stabilization techniques.Simulation results show that the proposed approach reduces the overall latency–energy cost by up to 27.5% compared with fully local and fully offloaded baselines, and achieves a 14.2% improvement over convex-optimization methods. The learned policy scales efficiently from 20 to 80 users with only one-tenth of the runtime of convex re-optimization, demonstrating its practicality for resilient UAV-assisted MEC.

title: CWT-GRU-based Natural Disaster Prediction Framework using Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: T.A. Sathish Shankar, Kotteswari K, P. Santhi, R. Abirami, Vikram N, K. Rajkirthi
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: In order to identify earthquakes quickly, the disaster prediction model based on Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) tracks seismic vibrations as well as ambient parameters using dispersed wireless sensor nodes. However, challenges such as poor communication, high noise levels, energy constraints, as well as the intricacy of real-time information processing hinder accurate prediction as well as efficient disaster response. Hence, this paper performs the WSN-based Earthquake Disaster Prediction (WSN-EDP) model with the help of deep learning framework. The dataset is initially collected from the standard benchmark sources such as Kaggle called Earthquake dataset. The pre-processing of this data is accomplished by the Empirical Mode Decomposition (EMD) approach. The extraction of the features is further done by the Continuous Wavelet Transform (CWT) technique. Finally, the prediction of the WSN-EDP model is performed by the deep learning model known as Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU). The proposed CWT-GRU is 6.48% and 29.06% better than the other considered existing methods with respect to prediction accuracy and MSE respectively for the developed WSN-EDP model.

title: Machine Learning and IoT-Enabled Intelligent Traffic Systems: Applications, Challenges and Research Trends
authors: Samiya Sharma, Mudita Uppal, Deepali Gupta, Sahil Bhatia, Abhinav Juneja, Reshma R
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The amalgamation of machine learning and Internet of Things has completely changed the traffic management system, as it allows designing more beneficial, safer, and sustainable municipal mobility solutions. IoT sensors, cameras and wireless networks will create huge volumes of real-time traffic data that is processed by ML techniques to manage flow of traffic, predict congestion, and improve road safety. This paper critically analyzes the scope of ML and IoT applications in intelligent transportation systems, such as environmental monitoring, smart parking, and accident avoidance. It presents bibliometric data between 2017 and 2024, which indicates that the number of publications, collaboration, and scholarly contributions has increased significantly on a national and institutional basis. The paper also analyzes major disadvantages, including privacy of data, security threat, and compatibility with devices, power consumption, and non-popularity. Ethical concerns may arise due to issue of privacy of user and data ownership. In order to improve intelligent transport systems, this work focuses on deep learning, advanced feature engineering, and real-time anomaly detection. By combining bibliometric knowledge with technical challenges, it can identify future research possibilities. The findings demonstrate how important ML and IoT are to building robust, flexible, and effective traffic control systems for smart cities.

title: Fault-Tolerant Control of Three-Phase Induction Motor Based on Predictive Feedforward Method
authors: S. Alireza Davari, Mahdi S. Mousavi, A. H. Valiloo, F. Flores-Bahamonde, M. Rahmani, Y. Zhang, Z. Zhang, J. Rodríguez
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: This research proposes a fault-tolerant control of three-phase induction motors with one phase cut out, focusing on predictive feedforward control. The paper aimed to maintain stability and robustness. Identifying zero sequence circuit models for post-fault performance is a challenge in the case of fault-tolerant drives. This research proposes a model-free feed-forward predictive control. The midpoint of the DC-link is connected to the null point of the induction motor. Simulation results evaluate the proposed method. The results demonstrated a stable performance in both steady state and transient conditions. Also, the motor does not need system identification, and it shows a robust performance in different conditions.

title: Spatiotemporal Pattern-based Flexible Machine Learning Framework for Cyberattack Detection in Smart Grid Distribution Systems
authors: Venkata Nagaraju Thatha, B. VeeraSekharReddy, Macha Harshavardan, Bingi Poojitha, K S Yash Vardhan Bharadwaj, Kondra Hemanth
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Smart grid modernization has enhanced operation efficiency and real-time monitoring, but it also makes the power grid more open to cyber-attacks. Classic detection schemes that consider static thresholds or rule-based systems are not able to model the dynamic conduct of advanced attacks such as False Data Injection (FDI)-like, Load-Altering Attacks (LAA), and Denial-of-Service (DoS) intrusions. To consider all of these, in this work we propose a spatiotemporal pattern-dependent machine learning (ML) framework which exploits spatial correlations along the grid components with temporal variations in system behavior to enhance cyberattack detection accuracy. The framework incorporates a structured preprocessing pipeline for data cleaning, normalization, and feature extraction, followed by classifier training using models such as Random Forest, Support Vector Machine, and Extreme Gradient Boosting. Experimental validation demonstrates that combining spatial and temporal features significantly improves detection performance compared to traditional methods. The proposed framework establishes a scalable, adaptable, and intelligent intrusion detection approach suitable for modern smart grid distribution environments.

title: An Autonomus Learning Framework for Intelligent Loan Eligibility Assessment
authors: G.Ratna Kumari, K. Gopal Reddy, G.Chamundeswari, Prasad Babu Bairisetty, Chiranjeevi Aggala, Ch.Venkatesh
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: In India, many people are suffering from multiple loan rejections. Even people with genuine financial reports are facing loan rejections, even for very minor amounts. It is difficult to determine the loan rejection reason using manual methods or existing ML algorithms. As per RBI guidelines, many financial companies use automated systems to identify accurate reasons. But the loan rejection rate remains high even for minor reasons, even with a high CIBIL score. In this paper, an Autonomous Learning Framework (ALF) is introduced to verify loan eligibility and accurately predict it in real time. The proposed approach is an automated system that extracts high-dimensional features from applicant data and refines eligibility based on minor features. The proposed ALF also refines the model by using continuous data collected from financial agencies for easier processing and decision-making. On the other hand, an explainable AI is integrated into the proposed approach, improving predictive performance and fairness, helping the banking sector and financial companies. The experimental results are conducted across various benchmark datasets and demonstrate high accuracy and other performance metrics.

title: Application of Voltaire Functional Series in Control of Nonlinear Polynomial Objects
authors: Umurzakova Dilnoza Maxamadjanovna, Khakimov Akhror Abdimakhamatovich, Akramova Gulyora Abdikhalikovna
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Science and Communications Technologies (ICISCT)
abstract: The article discusses the use of Voltaire functional series for the analytical synthesis of optimal regulators of systems with nonlinear polynomial dynamics. The relevance of developing control methods for objects with high degrees of nonlinearity, especially in the context of symmetric polynomial systems, is indicated. The paper proposes an approach based on an approximate analytical solution of the equations of free motion using Voltaire functional series, which makes it possible to obtain accurate approximations of solutions and simplify the design of control actions. Models with symmetric and non-symmetric characteristics are considered, as well as the transformation of the initial equations into forms convenient for synthesis. The main task is to develop a method for constructing controls that ensure asymptotic stability and minimize the functionality of generalized work. The work is relevant for the automatic control of complex nonlinear systems, where classical methods are not effective enough.

title: Supporting Students, Faculty, and Staff Through Loaner Device Programs - Bridging the Technology Gap
authors: Jeffrey P Kontio, Jessica Stockett, Theresa Dundon Friedman
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 ACM SIGUCCS Annual Conference
abstract: None

title: Fingerprint Presentation Attack Detection with Fixed Prototype Contrastive Learning and Anomaly Detection_supp1-3676709.pdf
authors: Jufu Feng
abstract: Fingerprint Presentation Attack Detection (PAD) is crucial for ensuring the reliability of fingerprint recognition systems. Mainstream methods typically frame PAD as a binary classification task. Although they achieve commendable results, these approaches often fail on unseen spoofs. This is because their decision boundaries rely heavily on trainset spoofs, leading to poor generalization. To address this issue, we reformulate PAD as an anomaly detection problem and propose the Fixed-Prototype PAD method. First, we introduce Fixed-Prototype Contrastive Learning (FPCL) for extracting PAD features. FPCL clusters the features of live fingerprints near a fixed prototype while pushing the features of spoof fingerprints away. We then design a Fixed-Prototype Anomaly Detection method to replace binary classification for decision-making. Specifically, this involves comparing the distance between test fingerprint features and the fixed live prototype to detect spoofs. Our method eliminates the dependency on the trainset spoofs during decision-making, thereby enhancing generalization capability. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach achieves state-of-the-art performance on the LivDet 2019 and LivDet 2021 benchmarks.

title: Infinite Gaussian Mixture Autoencoder for Adaptive Deep Unsupervised Clustering
authors: Ying Li, Junyu Xuan, Richard Yida Xu
abstract: Clustering is a fundamental task in machine learning and data mining. The success of deep learning, especially deep generative models, has given birth to the next generation of clustering - deep unsupervised clustering featured by the capability of clustering large-scale and high-dimensional data. One typical example is the Gaussian mixture variational autoencoder (GMVAE), where the prior of the latent variable is a mixture of Gaussian distributions (GMM). However, the mixture number manually given in GMVAE greatly impacts on the final clustering performance and also limits the adaptation ability when new clusters come. To solve this issue, we propose an Infinite Gaussian Mixture Autoencoder (IGMVAE), which replaces the plain Gaussian prior in VAE with an infinite Gaussian mixture distribution. Our method adaptively learns the number of mixtures from data, overcoming the manual setting limitation in GMVAE, and dynamically expands clusters for novel categories. Experimental results on three datasets show that our proposed IGMVAE achieves 5.5%–14.25% higher clustering accuracy compared to GMVAE. Moreover, our model is capable of generating high-quality data in a fully unsupervised manner.

title: Quad-Directional Vivaldi Antenna with Sub-Band Gain Enhancement Based on SSPP
authors: Shuangshuang Zhu, Xinlong Bi, Yang Shao, Xiaoyuan Wang, Yi Song
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This paper presents a wideband, high-gain four-directional Vivaldi antenna that employs a novel band-divided optimization strategy. The approach partitions the 2.5–4.5 GHz band into four sub-bands, each assigned to a dedicated radiation direction and individually optimized. Through synergistically integration of defected ground structures (DGS) for beam shaping and spoof surface plasmon polariton (SSPP) elements for directivity enhancement, the antenna achieves a gain above 10 dBi across the full band, with a peak of 18.3 dBi. A prototype was fabricated and measured, showing excellent agreement with simulations in terms of return loss (below –10 dB) and stable end-fire radiation patterns.

title: Early Attack Identification in the Wild
authors: Minji Kim, Dongeun Lee, Kookjin Lee, Doowon Kim, Jinpyo Kim, Sangman Lee, Jinoh Kim
abstract: While characterizing network connections in their early stage is vital for providing timely responses against network threats, existing methods become less attractive due to the requirement of complete connection information (thus unable to make timely identification) or packet payload inspection (there-fore limited to unencrypted packets under no privacy regulation). To this end, this paper takes an approach of packet stream analysis referencing statistical information of packet sequences, requiring neither packet inspection nor complete connection information.
