To enable practical packet stream analysis, there exist several challenges, such as out-of-order packet sequences introduced by network dynamics and class imbalance with a tiny fraction of attack connections. To overcome these challenges, we design two deep sequence models: (i) a bidirectional recurrent structure designed for greater resilience to out-of-order packet streams, and (ii) a pre-training-enabled sequence-to-sequence structure designed for creating consistent representations from unbalanced class distributions using self-supervised learning. We evaluate the presented deep sequence models using real and synthetic network data collections for extensive experimentation. The experimental results support the feasibility of the proposed models outperforming baseline deep learning models, yielding up to 94.8% (F1 score) only with the first five packets (k=5) from the Internet traffic collection containing a substantial fraction of network flows experiencing out-of-order delivery.

title: A hybrid Physics-Guided Machine Learning for Soil Moisture Monitoring and Drought Assessment from CYGNSS in Complex Terrain
authors: Charafa El Rhadiouini, Shuanggen Jin, Emmanuel Yeboah, Isaac Sarfo, Abraham Okrah
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Accurate soil moisture estimation is critical for drought monitoring, water resource management, and agricultural planning from the Cyclone Global Navigation Satellite System (CYGNSS). However, conventional approaches often struggle with spatial heterogeneity and seasonal variability in complex landscapes. This study develops a three-layer hybrid framework integrating Physics-guided principles, machine learning, and spatial optimization to improve soil moisture estimation across Eastern China's diverse hydroclimatic regions. The framework sequentially combines a Physics-guided empirical baseline, a machine-learning correction module, and a spatial coherence optimization layer to leverage complementary strengths across modelling paradigms. Performance was evaluated using internal CYGNSS consistency checks and external Soil Moisture of China (SMCI) in situ datasets, covering 41,948 samples across 4,025 locations during the 2021 summer monsoon season. The hybrid framework achieved R² = 0.653 for internal consistency checks and R² = 0.474 for external validation. It significantly outperformed individual model components (p < 0.001) and maintained temporal stability across the monsoon season. The framework successfully captured the drought-to-wet transition while reducing RMSE by 26.4% when compared to Physics-guided baseline models. Spatially, it showed consistent performance across diverse regions, with optimal results in agricultural areas (RMSE / 0.046 m³/m³). Error distribution improved substantially, with 60.3% of predictions within ±0.05 m³/m³ of reference values. The framework also demonstrated improved consistency with multi-scale drought indices, supporting its potential for operational soil moisture monitoring in complex landscapes and agricultural drought early warning.

title: Frequency Response Data-Driven Disturbance Observer Design for Flexible Joint Robots
authors: Deokjin Lee, Junho Song, Alireza Karimi, Sehoon Oh
abstract: Motion control of flexible joint robots (FJR) is challenged by inherent flexibility and configuration-dependent variations in system dynamics. While disturbance observers (DOB) can enhance system robustness, their performance is often limited by the elasticity of the joints and the variations in system parameters, which leads to a conservative design of the DOB. This article presents a novel frequency response function (FRF)-based optimization method aimed at improving DOB performance, even in the presence of flexibility and system variability. The proposed method maximizes control bandwidth and effectively suppresses vibrations, thus enhancing overall system performance. Closed-loop stability is rigorously proven using the Nyquist stability criterion. Experimental validation on a FJR demonstrates that the proposed approach significantly improves robustness and motion performance, even under conditions of joint flexibility and system variation.

title: Evolutionary Dynamics of Variable Games in Structured Populations_supp1-3674776.pdf
authors: Liang-Jian Deng
abstract: The game interactions among individuals in nature are often uncertain and dynamically evolving, significantly influencing the persistence of cooperation. However, it remains a formidable challenge to effectively characterize these dynamic properties in structured populations, derive theoretical conditions for cooperation, and identify the optimal game distribution for promoting cooperation. To address these issues, we propose the variable game framework in a structured population, where the game interactions between different individuals change over time. By means of the Markov chain and the pair approximation method, we derive theoretical conditions under which cooperation is favored by natural selection and when it is favored over defection under weak selection. Furthermore, we, respectively, formulate and solve two optimization problems to determine the optimal game distribution that most effectively fosters the evolution of cooperation by maximizing the gradient of cooperation selection and minimizing the fitness difference between defectors and cooperators. The theoretical predictions regarding both the conditions for cooperation and optimal game distribution are further validated by numerical calculations and extensive Monte Carlo simulations. Our findings offer novel insights into the mechanisms driving cooperative behavior in complex systems and provide theoretical guidance for designing optimal game environments that facilitate the evolution of cooperation.

title: Adaptive Task Assignment and Vehicle Routing for Electric Transfer Vehicles Under Seaport Environmental Stresses
authors: Ying Lu, Sidun Fang, Laiqiang Kong, Huaisheng Liu, Ailing Xing, Tao Niu, Guanhong Chen, Ruijin Liao
abstract: The operation of electric transfer vehicles (ETVs) in seaports faces significant challenges from harsh environmental conditions, including salt spray, temperature variations, and wind, which accelerate battery aging and increase energy consumption. Traditional scheduling approaches often neglect these environmental stresses, leading to suboptimal battery lifespan and energy efficiency. This paper proposes a novel adaptive scheduling framework that jointly optimizes task assignment, vehicle routing, and charging strategies under realistic seaport environmental conditions. First, we quantify the impact of salt spray and temperature on battery calendar/cyclic aging through electrochemical models, and formulate wind/road slope-induced energy consumption variations. A two-stage optimization model is then developed, comprising (1) a vehicle-task matching model for cost and battery aging minimization, and (2) an environmental aware routing-charging model. To solve this complex problem, we design an integer programming-critic reinforcement learning algorithm that integrates integer programming for matching decisions and a critic network for policy evaluation. Case studies using real-world data from Yingkou Port demonstrate that the proposed framework effectively adapts to environmental conditions and reduces both battery aging and total operational costs.

title: Combining Augmented Reality with Semi-autonomous, Lightmyography Based Control to Improve Usability of Prostheses
authors: Bonnie Guan, Zhe Wang, Ricardo V. Godoy, Mahonri Owen, Minas Liarokapis
abstract: Amputees often struggle with the use of new prostheses due to many factors. One such issue includes the increasing complexity of control in modern prosthesis and the difficult learning curve that a new prosthetic control system can present. As such, it is important we design control interfaces that keep the simplicity of traditional myoelectric prosthesis, while harnessing the increased dexterity of modern robotic hands. This paper expands upon a semi-autonomous control system combining human-in-the-loop control with Lightmyography decoding, together with affordances-based filtering using object detection and IMU data. An augmented reality display is designed using the capabilities of the Apple Vision Pro to give the user useful information about the control system in an attempt to reduce the mental workload associated with using the robotic hand. Overall, a user study showed that for individuals who were open to using augmented reality, workload was significantly lower (p=0.0036) based on the NASA-TLX and the usability was significantly higher (p=0.0062) based on the system usability scale, when the augmented reality was included in the framework. The framework shows promise in the training of prosthetic systems and may be able to reduce the chance of prosthetic rejection by lowering perceived workload. However, we must keep in mind that many people are not keen to use augmented reality or virtual reality headsets as they find them impractical, with the use of these headsets in everyday life being very uncommon.

title: Quantum Kernel-Driven Hybrid Neural Network for Brain Tumor Classification
authors: Alok Kumar Srivastava, Shadab Hussain, Shiru Sharma, Ashish Verma, Neeraj Sharma
abstract: Multiclass brain tumor classification from magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) remains challenging due to subtle inter-class visual similarities, particularly for anatomically small and heterogeneous tumor types such as pituitary lesions. To address these challenges, this paper presents a hybrid classical-quantum learning framework that integrates a deep convolutional feature extractor from the ResNet family with a variational quantum neural network (QNN) head for enhanced feature representation. In the present work, classical deep features extracted by the ResNet model are encoded into a six-qubit quantum circuit, enabling richer feature interactions within a high-dimensional Hilbert space. In addition, an anatomically guided Kernel-based Center Cropping (KCC) strategy is introduced as a deterministic spatial prior to emphasize anatomically relevant central brain structures, particularly benefiting challenging pituitary tumor cases. The proposed Kernel-based Center Cropping–Hybrid Quantum Neural Network (KCC-HQNN) framework is trained and evaluated on a publicly available composite four-class brain MRI dataset curated from Kaggle sources, comprising glioma, meningioma, pituitary tumor, and no-tumor categories. Experimental results across multiple ResNet variants demonstrate that the hybrid quantum–classical models consistently outperform their purely classical counterparts, achieving classification accuracy of up to 99%, while macro-averaged precision, recall, and F1 scores consistently fall in the range of approximately 0.95–0.99 across different ResNet variants. Reliability and reproducibility are further validated through stratified five-fold cross-validation and repeated experiments using multiple random initialization seeds, yielding a mean classification accuracy of 99.00 ± 0.08%, macro F1-score of 0.986 ± 0.11, and weighted F1-score of 0.989 ± 0.12, while cross-dataset evaluation on independent IEEE DataPort MRI datasets demonstrates robust generalization under dataset shift. All experiments are conducted on publicly available retrospective datasets using a quantum simulator. These results indicate that combining quantum feature processing with anatomically informed spatial processing improves multiclass discrimination of brain tumors.

title: Data-Driven Robust Resource Allocation for Vehicular Communications via Deep Support Vector Clustering
authors: Guanhua Chai, Hao Li, Changle Li, Nan Cheng, F. Richard Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: In this paper, we investigate the power control and spectrum allocation challenge in vehicle-to-everything (V2X) networks with uncertain channels, where two types of services coexist, i.e., the large-capacity services supported by vehicle-to-infrastructure (V2I) links and the ultra-reliable services supported by vehicle-to-vehicle (V2V) links. Our goal is to maximize the transmission rate of V2I whilst ensuring the V2V outage probability constraint. To deal with the channel uncertainty, we first mine the channel correlation by mapping historical channel samples into high-dimensional space via a designed deep neural network (DNN). Then, a support vector clustering (SVC) based uncertainty set is derived as a union of convex subsets according to the output of piecewise activation DNN. Afterwards, a power control algorithm is proposed for the transformed non-convex problem. Specifically, a bisection-based method is developed to tackle the fractional signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) expression, and an exploration-comparison-approximation method is proposed to relax the union of convex subsets. Additionally, we design a set of virtual V2V links to enforce the spectrum allocation problem into a feasible bipartite graph matching problem. Simulation results demonstrate the proposed method overcomes the conservatism of previous uncertainty set construction methods, and outperforms other methods on V2I transmission rate.

title: Coupled Finite Element Analysis and Phase Field Modeling of Thermomechanical Fracture in Through-Glass Vias for Advanced Packaging
authors: Han Jiang, Susu Li, Shuibao Liang, Guangbin Dou
abstract: Through-glass via (TGV) structures have been proven to be promising interconnect solutions for advanced packaging applications, while the coefficient of thermal expansion (CTE) mismatch between copper and glass induces severe stress concentrations and fracture during thermal loading. This study develops a coupled finite element and phase field modeling framework to simulate the thermomechanical fracture behavior of copper-filled TGV structures subjected to annealing. The simulations successfully capture arbitrary crack propagation paths in good agreement with experiments, revealing different failure modes: radial cracking during heating driven by circumferential stresses, and circumferential cracking during cooling driven by radial stresses. The influence of heating rate on crack formation mechanisms is systematically investigated, revealing the critical role of competition between stress accumulation and stress relaxation through copper creep. For the investigated TGV configuration, heating rates below <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$6.5~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C/min effectively suppress radial cracking during heating but cannot prevent circumferential cracking during cooling, while rates above <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$8.7~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C/min lead to dual-stage damage with significantly elevated damage levels. These findings establish heating rate as a critical control parameter for TGV manufacturing reliability and provide quantitative insights for design optimization of TGV-based advanced packaging structures.

title: A Production-Ready Framework for Unified Multi-Role Educational Environments
authors: A. Karthik, K. Vidhula Reddy, Bojja Shravya, Narra Abheram
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: This endeavor showcases the creation and execution of a state-of-the-art web-based online learning platform that encourages interactive and scalable education using a full-stack microservices architecture. The platform is powered by the latest front-end and back-end technologies to provide teachers and learners a smooth educational experience. The front end that provides responsive and easy-to-use interface was built using React.js and TailwindCSS. React Router and React Query are used for dynamic client-side routing and effective server state management. The back end that provides a RESTful API connecting to MongoDB via Mongoose ODM is built with Node.js and Express.js. The system’s strong authentication is achieved through JWT and bcrypt, while Razorpay integration allows secure payment processing for course enrollments. Other capabilities offered are file uploads through Multer, real-time email notifications through Nodemailer, and role-based access control for different types of users. The system’s customizability, safety, and ability to grow in capacity enable functionalities like user communication, progress tracking, and course management. This platform, through the adoption of modern web development techniques and security standards, presents a viable and production-ready solution for easy access to education.

title: Research on Multi-Scale Forecasting Model of Wind Power in Extreme Weather Based on Multi-Source Heterogeneous Data Fusion
authors: Xiaoli Zhu, Xuannian Lei, Haiyi Xiao, Hang Xu, Zhiyuan Zhu
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: In this paper, a multi-scale forecasting model of wind power in extreme weather based on multi-source heterogeneous data fusion is studied. The intermittence and fluctuation of wind power, especially the frequent occurrence of extreme weather events, significantly increase the uncertainty of wind power forecasting and bring challenges to the stable operation of power system. Traditional forecasting methods rely on a single data source, so it is difficult to fully describe the coupling relationship between complex meteorological conditions and wind farm dynamics, and there are limitations in extreme weather scenarios. In this study, a multi-scale prediction model combining weather radar, satellite cloud images and SCADA data is proposed. Through time-space alignment and feature extraction, extreme weather enhancement module and hierarchical multi-scale prediction model, the problems of multi-modal information integration, sensitivity enhancement of extreme weather events and multi-scale time series prediction are solved. The experimental results show that the prediction accuracy of this model is significantly better than the existing models in extreme weather such as typhoon, and it shows a good balance in calculation efficiency, which provides effective support for ensuring power grid security, optimizing energy storage configuration and reducing wind abandonment rate.

title: Who’s nearby? LBSD user discovery overview
authors: Ruiting Liu, HangYu Li, Bing Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: Location-Based Social Discovery (LBSD) services, while enhancing users’ social experiences, introduce significant location privacy risks. Existing research has predominantly focused on achieving high-precision localization by exploiting the disclosed distances returned by LBSD systems, yet it largely overlooks a fundamental and critical prerequisite in the localization pipeline: whether a target user is discoverable, that is, who is nearby. This paper argues that a user can only be detected by a probing entity if they simultaneously satisfy four conditions: they have LBSD functionality enabled, are in an active state, have not activated invisibility modes or imposed access restrictions, and reside within the distance range disclosed by the platform. Moreover, location obfuscation mechanisms employed by platforms, such as distance granularity, injected random noise, and query rate limiting, not only degrade localization accuracy but also directly govern user discoverability. To address this gap, we propose a user discovery analysis framework tailored to LBSD scenarios. This framework systematically delineates the scope of discoverable users, elucidates the intrinsic relationships among platform obfuscation strategies, probe deployment policies, and user discoverability, and provides a formal characterization of the user discovery process. By elevating user discovery from a peripheral step in localization to an independent and essential analytical task, our framework establishes a theoretical foundation for efficient subsequent localization and offers novel insights for evaluating and enhancing the privacy preserving capabilities of LBSD services.

title: Deep Learning Techniques for Real-Time Fraud Detection in Financial Transactions
authors: Sriharsha Anand Pushkala
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: With the rapid evolution of financial technology, deep learning has become a critical enabler for real-time fraud detection in financial transactions. As transaction volumes and complexities increase, the limitations of traditional rule-based and manual analytical approaches have become evident. This study explores the application of advanced deep learning architectures including convolutional neural networks (CNNs), recurrent neural networks (RNNs), autoencoders, and reinforcement learning frameworks for enhancing the speed and accuracy of fraud detection. It further examines practical implementations of these models in real-world financial systems to prevent fraudulent activities and strengthen transactional security. In addition, the paper identifies key challenges such as data privacy, model interpretability, and scalability, and outlines prospective research directions to address these issues within the continuously evolving domain of financial fraud analytics.

title: Bi-LSTM and ARIMA Approach for Soil-Specific Water Requirement Analysis
authors: S. Amudha, P. Pranav Murari, A. Chakri Govindh Yadav
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: Efficient water management is essential for sustainable agriculture, particularly in the face of climate change and limited water resources. This project presents a comprehensive approach for analyzing soilspecific water requirements using advanced predictive models, namely Bi-directional Long Short-Term Memory (Bi-LSTM) and Auto Regressive Integrated Moving Average (ARIMA). By integrating these models, we aim to achieve high accuracy in predicting water requirements for various crops based on soil properties, crop types, and climatic factors. The Bi-LSTM model is designed to capture sequential dependencies in the dataset, while ARIMA is applied for robust time series forecasting. In the PINN approach, the LSTM-based sequence-to-sequence and feedforward neural network models were trained using a cost function incorporating the hydrological model and observed data. Our simulations showed that the two investigated frameworks improved predictions compared to purely using data-driven or the hydrological model, demonstrating the potential of hybrid machine learning frameworks for accurately modeling soil moisture dynamics for guiding agricultural and hydrological applications.Knowledge of the soil water retention (SWR) data is necessary for modeling soil water movement and assessing soil water holding capacity and availability. Since direct measurement is often time-consuming and costly, pretransfer functions (PTFs) have been widely used to predict SWR data from basic soil physical properties. Natural logarithm of soil suction, In (h), is considered as an extra input parameter in this approach. It enables us to predict SWR data at any desired soil suction as it results in more extensive and useful database. To train and validate this model, a real-world dataset containing a set of parameters related to weather forecasting, soil moisture, and other related parameters was collected using smart sensors installed in a greenhouse in Chiang Mai province, Thailand. Preliminary results show that our LSTM-based model performs well in predicting soil moisture with a $0.72 \%$ RMSE error and a $0.52 \%$ cross- validation error (LSTM), and our Bi-LSTM model with a $0.76 \%$ RMSE error and a $0.57 \%$ cross- validation error. In the future, we aim to test and validate this model on other similar datasets. Finally, the proposed Bi-LSTM and ARIMA hybrid model provides a powerful tool for soil-specific water requirement forecasting. It bridges the gap between statistical accuracy and deep learning flexibility, offering a promising direction for smart irrigation systems and data- driven agricultural water management in the era of climate uncertainty.

title: A User-Side Low-Carbon Dispatch Method Considering Social Surplus Maximization
authors: Yujie Zhu, Qingshan Xu, Yongbiao Yang, Xin Shen, Yifei Wang, Hemu Ji
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy (ICSGE)
abstract: With the increasing requirements for energy green transition, in order to achieve low-carbon economic dispatch in integrated energy systems, in addition to considering carbon reduction strategies on the supply side, the carbon reduction potential on the demand side is also worth exploring. To this end, this paper investigates the optimal electricity consumption strategy for users under low-carbon demand response. For residential and commercial users, models are established considering the characteristics of rigid loads, transferable loads, and reducible loads. For industrial users, a discrete industrial manufacturing production model is developed, taking into account buffer capacity and production targets. The dispatch model is constructed with the objective of maximizing social welfare, comprehensively considering user utility, power generation costs, carbon emission costs, and compensation costs. By comparing the integrated costs and carbon reduction before and after optimization, the effectiveness of the proposed method is demonstrated.

title: Research on Forward Conduction Characteristics of Barrier-Type Thyristor with Buried Gate Structure
authors: Yucheng Li, Hairong Li, Guoyang Li, Guangbin Zhang, Xiangyou Zeng, Zhixiong Huang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: This study investigates the forward conduction characteristics of the Buried-Gate Barrier-Type Thyristor (BTH) using theoretical analysis and TCAD simulation. We established a physical model for high-level injection, elucidating the device's transition from barrier control mode to conductivity modulation mode, and verified the cubic relationship between double-injection current and voltage. Simulation results indicate that BTH exhibits low-loss characteristics similar to PIN diodes under high injection, though the gate structure results in a higher turn-on voltage. Furthermore, gate spacing is identified as a critical parameter for blocking capability; excessive spacing leads to blocking failure. Additionally, increasing substrate thickness significantly increases on-state power loss, suggesting that thinner substrates are preferable provided blocking requirements are met.

title: Robust Vehicle Re-Identification Under Viewpoint and Appearance Variations using Coordinate Attention
authors: Gaurav Singh, Aditya Joshi, Manoj Diwakar, Neeraj Kumar Pandey, Prabhishek Singh, Sanjay Roka
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Vehicle Re-Identification (Re-ID) is a key component of Intelligent Transportation Systems, although it is faced with difficulties regarding inter-class similarity and view-point variation within the class. To tackle these problems this paper proposed an effective Attentive Re-D framework. This method adopts a ResNet-50 network strengthened by the addition of Instance Batch Normalization (IBN) blocks that allow it to extract robust domain-invariant features, which remain unaffected under lighting and appearance variation. Moreover incorporate a Spatially Aware Coordinate Attention module, which enables it to extract accurate location information, thus filtering out unnecessary background noise and concentrating solely on relevant vehicle regions. Finally adopt a Generalized Mean (GeM) pooling which enhance its feature gathering strength compared to existing ones. The proposed network was trained via a multi-objective loss function, reflecting a combination of Label Smoothing Cross-Entropy, Batch Hard Triplet and Center Loss functions, aiming to achieve intra-class compactness and inter-class separability simultaneously. The proposed method was evaluated on the VeRi-776 a large-scale Re-ID benchmark dataset, which showed its effectiveness reaching a Rank-1 accuracy of 98.34% and mAP of 78.65%.

title: SP-YOLO: An Enhanced Model for High-Performance Solar Panel Defect Detection
authors: Guangyue Ma, Yu Zhao, Xiran Wang, Ce Liang
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Automated defect detection in solar panels is critical for the operational maintenance of photovoltaic power plants. However, existing deep learning-based methods still struggle to reliably identify subtle defects, handle multi-scale fault features, and meet the requirements of lightweight deployment. To address these limitations, we propose SP-YOLO, an enhanced detection framework that integrates structural optimizations and attention mechanisms to achieve efficient and accurate defect identification. Key improvements include: (1) incorporating a convolutional attention module to enhance sensitivity to fine defects; (2) adopting a bidirectional feature pyramid network for robust multi-scale feature fusion; and (3) introducing lightweight design and model pruning to reduce computational cost while maintaining high accuracy, making it suitable for deployment on edge devices such as drones. Experimental results demonstrate that our method achieves superior detection accuracy and faster inference speed on both self-collected and public benchmarks, offering a practical solution for automated solar panel inspection.

title: Research on Multi-Objective Path Optimization based on Node Weights and Robustness Modeling
authors: Mengye Meng, Jiaqi Li, Zihang Li, Wei Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: In complex network systems, path planning serves as the basis for key processes such as information dissemination and task execution, and its modeling quality directly determines the overall system performance. Traditional path selection algorithms tend to focus on the shortest path or cost minimization, while neglecting the far-reaching effects of service capability differences among nodes and structural perturbations on path effectiveness. To solve this problem, this paper proposes a novel Weighted Robust Path Optimization algorithm (WRO-Path), which aims to maximize the sum of the node weights of the selected paths and improve their robustness in dynamic environments, while guaranteeing the structural constraints and time constraints. The WRO-Path algorithm adopts a multi-stage collaborative optimization mechanism: firstly, the nodes are structurally divided and regionally preprocessed by K-means clustering, then the weight-driven path optimization model is constructed, the linear objective function and multiple path constraints are introduced for solving the problem, and ultimately, the path robustness is evaluated by using perturbation simulation and stability indexes. The experiments are conducted on multi-scale graph datasets, and the results show that the proposed algorithm outperforms the existing benchmark methods in terms of node coverage efficiency, running time and structural stability, and exhibits good adaptability and scalability. The method can be widely used in multiple types of computing scenarios such as heterogeneous computing resource scheduling, task path allocation, and graph neural network structure search.

title: Multiple Partial Discharges Detection and Localization in Air-Insulated Substations: Part 1 – UHF Sensor Array Design, Simulation Environment, and a Novel Clustering Framework
authors: Morteza Bagheri, Asghar Akbari, Hamid Jahangir
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: The accurate localization of multiple Partial Discharge (PD) sources in Air-Insulated Substations (AIS) using Ultra-High-Frequency (UHF) technology is significantly challenged by environmental noise, signal reflections, and interference. This paper, the first in a two-part series, establishes the foundational methodology to address these challenges. We present the design of an optimized biconical antenna, tailored for PD detection, and propose a diamond-Y hybrid antenna array configuration for three-dimensional localization. A detailed high-fidelity model of an HV AIS is developed in CST Studio Suite, incorporating five distinct PD sources to simulate complex real-world scenarios, including cases with no direct signal path and internal transformer PD. Furthermore, a novel framework for identifying and clustering multiple PD sources from noisy, mixed signals is introduced, based on Continuous Wavelet Transform (CWT) and wavelet denoising. Collectively, the methodologies developed in this paper create a robust and reliable platform, which is utilized in Part 2 to achieve and analyze the accurate 3D localization results of the multiple PD sources.

title: Research on Fault Prediction and Intelligent Diagnosis System for 220kV Gas - Insulated Disconnecting Switches Based on Machine Learning
authors: Jiaming Dai, Haiming Li, Junshuang Zhang, Yong Zhang, Xueli Yang
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: In this article, the fault prediction and intelligent diagnosis based on machine learning (ML) are studied for the hidden trouble existing in the operation of 220kV fully enclosed disconnector. The sensor network is deployed to collect multi-source data such as equipment current, temperature and gas density. After preprocessing and feature engineering, a diagnosis model combining random forest (RF) and convolutional neural network (CNN) is constructed. Among them, RF algorithm is used for fault prediction, and the accuracy rate is 87.3% on the test set, which can warn potential faults 4-8 hours in advance; The improved LeNet-5CNN realizes fault type recognition, and the average recognition accuracy of six common faults reaches 91.5%, which is about 10% higher than the traditional support vector machine method. The model is integrated into the intelligent diagnosis system and applied in the actual power grid. The results show that the system improves the fault handling efficiency of operation and maintenance personnel by 40%, and shortens the average power outage time of equipment from 4.2 hours to 2.5 hours. The research results provide an effective technical scheme for the intelligent operation and maintenance of 220kV high-voltage disconnector, and significantly enhance the operation reliability of Electric Power System (EPS).

title: Neuro-CRISPR-KAN: A Hybrid CNN-Transformer Architecture for Off-Target Prediction in Cystic Fibrosis
authors: Dr.S.Sarumathi, Jeganmurali B, Kameshwaran S, Mr.N.Giridharan
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Approaches for CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing to correct pathogenic mutations associated with Cystic Fibrosis are actively being explored as a therapeutic strategy, but prediction of off-target mutations, especially those from complex deletion topologies such as ΔF508, is standing in the way of clinical translation. This work proposes Neuro-CRISPR-KAN, a hybrid computational framework designed for deletion-aware CRISPR off-target prediction. The architecture leverages a two-stream feature extractor that integrates 1D-Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to capture local sequence motifs and DNABERT-2 transformers for capturing global semantic dependencies. These features drive a Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (KAN) decision core, which replaces fixed activation functions with learnable B-spline surfaces to approximate nonlinear off target risk patterns. In order to circumvent issues in positional inconsistencies, the proposed framework implements a ‘Null Tensor' encoding strategy that demonstrates improved performance compared to standard zero-padding methods due to better alignment of flanking nucleotides. Furthermore, the model translates predicted risk scores into human-readable, structured, interpretable summaries via a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) module. Experimental evaluation suggests improved predictive performance across recall-oriented safety metrics, establishing the feasibility of an interpretable computation approach to genomic safety assessment.

title: MediOptima: Data-Driven Healthcare Resource Allocation in Rural Tamil Nadu
authors: R. Praveena, K. Saranya, K. Aswathi, Ijaz Ahamed N, Shakthi S
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Rural healthcare systems carry the main burden of running into several common challenges like an unpredictable number of patients, shortage or wastage of medicines, poor allocation of healthcare workforce especially during seasonal outbreaks and public health emergencies. This paper presents a data driven healthcare resource optimization framework, MediOptima, for rural Primary Health Centres (PHCs), as a way of dealing with these operational inefficiencies. The proposed system incorporates different time, series forecasting models ARIMA, Prophet and Long Short Term Memory (LSTM), to forecast patient inflow patterns. Regression analysis is used to predict the demand for medicines by studying patient volume forecast, and staffing optimization is performed through the combination of Linear Programming and the World Health Organizations Workload Indicators of Staffing Need (WISN) methodology.An interactive Power BI dashboard is the medium through which predictive outputs are visually presented as actionable alerts to facility administrators, who then can make decisions in a timely manner. An experimental evaluation of the MediOptima model with the rural healthcare data from Tamil Nadu indicates that medicines demand prediction can be made with an accuracy as high as 97%, medicine order wastage can be reduced by 15-20%, and staff workload can be normalized by 25% as a result of the staff reallocation guided by the model.

title: Robust Alignment for Contact Lithography via Domain-Adaptive MAE and Geometric Consensus
authors: Chenyuan Song, Dingyue Zhang, Shijiao Li, Peiyuan Zhao, Shenao Zhang, Shenao Zhang, Lixin Zhang, Yangjie Cao, Mingfu Zhu, Ronghan Wei
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: In the process of contact based lithography, high-precision alignment is an important step. Traditional computer vision methods are unreliable under heavy lower-layer occlusion and binary dark-field mask interference, while standard deep learning suffers from limited robustness due to domain gaps. In this paper, we propose a lightweight hybrid framework that combines domain-adaptive feature learning with geometric consensus. we introduce a Masked Autoencoder (MAE) pretraining strategy tailored for lithography. By training a Vision Transformer (ViT-tiny) to learn the global topological structure of alignment marks. We design a simple deconvolutional head for high-resolution heatmap regression. Finally, a Random Sample Consensus (RANSAC)-based geometric post-processing module is integrated to filter outlier predictions caused by occlusion. Experimental results on synthetic datasets demonstrate that our method achieves superior accuracy compared to baselines. Our approach validating the effectiveness of structural self-supervised learning for industrial alignment tasks.

title: Smart Chatbot with Advanced Self-Evolving using AI Tools and Techniques
authors: Eldhose K. Paul, Shahana Mol, Anju John, Aparna Jiji
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The rapid advancement of artificial intelligence has transformed conversational agents from rule-based systems into intelligent, adaptive chatbots capable of learning from continuous interaction. This research proposes a smart chatbot framework with advanced self-evolving capabilities using modern AI tools and techniques. The proposed system integrates natural language understanding, contextual memory, reinforcement learning, and feedback-driven model adaptation to enable continuous performance improvement without frequent manual retraining. Unlike traditional chatbots that rely on static knowledge bases, the self-evolving mechanism allows the chatbot to dynamically refine its responses based on user behavior, sentiment trends, and contextual relevance. Experimental evaluation demonstrates improved conversational accuracy, adaptability, and user satisfaction across diverse interaction scenarios. The study highlights the potential of self-evolving chatbots in domains such as education, healthcare support, customer service, and decision assistance, offering a scalable and intelligent solution for next-generation human–computer interaction.

title: Artificial Intelligence In Robotics: Enhancing Human–Robot Interaction Through Emotion-Aware And Multilingual Nlp
authors: Enoch Arul Prakash, R Chandana, Ts Bhumika, Nagaraj Krishna
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: Human-Robot Interaction (HRI) is one of the most significant fields of robotics and artificial intelligence, which consists of Natural Language Processing (NLP). It has enabled the robots to communicate with human beings in a human way. There are two major problems in most robotic systems in the current state of NLP and HRI in spite of its impressive development speed. These restrictions complicate the implementation of robots in multicultural, multilingual communities, where trust, compassion, and inclusivity play an important role. This paper presents a new framework that combines the notion of affect recognition systems with multilingual natural language processing (NLP) to deal with such issues. The framework is made up of multilingual models using transformers that are able to comprehend and process a broad variety of different languages contextually, including mBERT and XLM-R (Cross-lingual RoBERT). These models make it possible to guarantee that robots will be able to decode the linguistic sense of the user inputs as well as the emotional nuances when the speech prosody and the text sentiment-based emotion classification methods are used. The proposed framework can produce emotionally responsive and contextually relevant responses in Hindi, Kannada and English among other languages. In this paper, I suggest a bilingual and emotion-sensitive HRI architecture that combines transformer-based NLP and emotion detection in prosody. Baseline prototype experiments perform better than 80 percent in sentiment and emotion recognition.

title: Research on AC Resistance Characteristics of Large Cross-section Cables and Correction of Skin Effect Coefficient
authors: Yu Lu, Canshu Qiu, Mingjie Ye, Guiping Chen, Hangxi He, Linbin Li, Shiyuan Lyu, Gang Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy (ICSGE)
abstract: In response to the issue that the empirical value of the skin effect coefficient (k<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">s</inf>) in the CIGRE report differs from the actual measurement, leading to inaccurate assessment of the AC resistance of cables, this paper builds a test platform for the AC resistance of large cross-section cables and conducts experimental research on eight types of copper core cables (with cross-sections ranging from 800 to 3000mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) and one 1600mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> aluminum core cable. By combining the instantaneous power method with Simpson numerical integration, the AC resistance at 40℃ to 90℃ was measured and the actual k<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">s</inf> value was inversely calculated. The AC resistance calculation results in the CIGRE report were innovatively compared, and the deviation of the recommended values in the CIGRE standard and the application potential of aluminum core cables were analyzed through the comparison results. The results show that there is a significant deviation in the recommended value of k<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">s</inf> =1 for aluminum conductors in the CIGRE standard. The k<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">s</inf> of the actual bidirectional twisted structure is 15% to 20% lower than that of the solid conductor, and that of the fan-shaped five-section structure is 8% to 12% lower than that of the compact circular structure. Ignoring structural differences can lead to an error in AC resistance calculation exceeding ±15%. Therefore, new k<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">s</inf> suggested values should be proposed for cables of certain structures.

title: Real-Time Deep Learning Framework for Emotion-Aware Smart Classrooms: A Practical Approach to Behavioural Feedback
authors: Balvinder Singh Gurudatta, Lalji Prasad
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The smart classroom is growing to adaptive and personalized learning classrooms, with learning capacity to read the emotions of learners being the key component of the enhancement of learning and school enrolment. The paper gives emotion sensitive intelligent classroom framework based on deep learning, uninterrupted, which integrates facial expression, speech emotion recognition, and body physiological sensing to comprehend the impact of learners. The convolutional neural networks (CNNs) give rise to, in turn, extract spatial messages of the face-based input, but the Long Short-Term Memory networks acquire temporal emotional variation of multimodal data streams. The multimodal fusion way of decision combines various presentations in order to strengthen the effectiveness of various classroom contexts that are predetermined by various lightings, sounds, and the location of several students. The computational complexity is reduced to allow real time execution on resource-constrained edge devices by model pruning, quantization or knowledge distillation techniques. In addition, the system includes an adaptive feedback system that will provide practical feedback to the instructors to end up making dynamic adjustments concerning the teaching pace and activity. The given framework demonstrates how to do better at engaging students, supporting their emotional well-being, and the individual learning process as well as significant ethical issues that should be linked to privacy and equity in educational AI.

title: Automation of Data Generation for Textile Information Systems
authors: Munira Sadikova, Ahror Kayumov, Abrorjon Kholmatov, Abdumalikjon Ruzaliyev, Abdumuxtor Umarov
publish: 2025 International Conference on Information Science and Communications Technologies (ICISCT)
abstract: The development of digital technologies in the textile industry requires a transition from disparate information sources to integrated information systems based on correctly generated data. The most vulnerable stage remains the preparation of primary data on raw materials, operations, and process parameters, traditionally dominated by manual entry and fragmented accounting. This paper examines methods for automating data generation processes for textile information systems and analyzes the key factors affecting their quality. It demonstrates that automation eliminates repetitive operations, reduces the likelihood of human error, and ensures continuous information exchange between departments. Approaches to constructing structured textile data sets, their subsequent validation, and integration into corporate digital platforms, including ERP and MES, are considered. Particular attention is paid to the role of automated procedures in improving the accuracy and consistency of data necessary for the effective operation of modern digital models of textile enterprises.

title: Research on Circuit Performance Characteristics of High-Frequency Switch-Controlled Audio Amplifiers Based on Fourier Analysis
authors: Wendong Li, Qianqian Chen, Yunlin Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: This paper systematically investigates key technologies of Class-D audio amplifiers in recent years, covering structural operation principles and high-frequency applicability. Through error analysis of the audio amplification circuit under high-frequency switch control and harmonic spectrum monitoring, the fidelity performance and high frequency applicability of the designed audio amplifier are evaluated. By increasing the circuit's driving frequency to the megahertz level, time-domain and frequency-domain waveforms at different frequencies are obtained via Fourier analysis. Simulation results show the amplifier performs stably in the low-to-medium frequency ranges without filtering parameter adjustments, improved during medium-to-high frequency transitions, but experiences severe distortion at high frequencies. Future research should explore influencing factors for medium-to-high frequency performance improvement and optimize circuit filtering parameters to address high-frequency distortion issues.

title: Research on operator fusion and graph optimization technology of real-time video behavior recognition model based on NPU instruction set
authors: Jianxiang Luo, Qinghang Wu, Guisheng Zhang, Yefeng Xia
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: In view of the problems faced by edge devices in real-time video behavior recognition, such as high delay, high power consumption and poor adaptability to dynamic input, this paper proposes a collaborative optimization method of operator fusion and graph optimization based on NPU (Neural Processing Unit) instruction set. Firstly, the matching mechanism of fusion revenue evaluation function and dynamic template is constructed, and the platform adaptive fusion rule base is designed to support the efficient fusion execution of 3D CNN and Transformer models on mainstream NPUs such as Huawei Ascend 910B, Horizon Journey 6 and MLU370. Secondly, graph optimization techniques such as spatio-temporal subgraph decomposition, dynamic shape caching and data flow pipeline arrangement are introduced to solve the memory and scheduling bottleneck caused by dynamic resolution input. The experimental results show that under the condition of 320×320@30fps, the reasoning delay of the optimized model on three kinds of edge devices is reduced to 28.8ms, 34.1ms and 18.9ms respectively, the memory occupation is reduced by up to 41%, the power consumption is reduced to 3.7W, and the F1 value of anomaly detection is increased to 0.814, which is significantly better than mainstream frameworks such as TensorRT and TVM. This study effectively promotes the application of NPU in real-time video understanding at the edge.

title: Research on AI Concentration Recognition and Distribution Prediction Model for Toxic and Harmful Gases in Laboratories
authors: Ruo Jia, Yu Guo
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Toxic and harmful gases in laboratories tend to accumulate at high concentrations. Traditional monitoring methods that rely on fixed-point detection and empirical thresholds are difficult to characterize the concentration field. This paper constructs an AI concentration recognition and distribution prediction model. Concentration data is collected through a multi-sensor network, and the global input is formed by standardization and feature concatenation. A feedforward neural network is introduced to achieve nonlinear mapping of concentrations at key positions. The spatio-temporal correlation between monitoring nodes is modeled by combining sliding time Windows and graph convolution. On this basis, the dynamic reconstruction and prediction of the concentration field of toxic and harmful gases in the laboratory are realized. Based on the renovated laboratory platform, different leakage and ventilation conditions were set up, and the model proposed in this paper was compared with the fixed threshold rule model and the traditional regression model. The results show that the RMSE and MAE of the model proposed in this paper on the test set are <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.18 \text{mg} / \mathrm{m}^{3}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.12 \text{mg} / \mathrm{m}^{3}$</tex> respectively, the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{2}$</tex> is 0.94, the hit rate of safety early warning is approximately 95%, and the duration of exceeding the standard under typical leakage conditions is shortened by about 60% compared with no optimization strategy. It can provide quantitative support for laboratory leakage identification and risk control optimization.

title: Comparative performance analysis of PID and SMC controllers in a model-in-the-loop framework for quadrotor UAVs
authors: Meryem Lahboub, Anass Mansouri, Hamid Hassani, Ali Ahaitouf
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: This paper presents a systematic performance comparison between a classical Proportional Integral Derivative (PID) controller and a Super-Twisting Sliding Mode Controller (SMC) for quadrotor attitude stabilization, using a MATLAB/Simulink Model-in-the-Loop (MIL) simulation framework. To thoroughly assess the controller’s capabilities, three simulation scenarios are designed: (i) step response evaluation to assess transient performance through key metrics including settling time, overshoot, and steady-state error; (ii) robustness testing using multi-step command sequences and composite wind disturbances, evaluated using Integral of Absolute Error (IAE) metric; and (iii) sinusoidal trajectory tracking to investigate precision and smoothness, quantified through IAE. Results show that while the PID controller exhibits smoother control inputs under nominal conditions, it underperforms in terms of accuracy and disturbance rejection. At the same time, SMC offers enhanced precision and robustness across all scenarios, despite higher control effort and noticeable chattering. Nevertheless, these effects are mitigated through careful tuning of the super-twisting reaching law. The analysis confirms the superior adaptability of SMC in complex and disturbed environments, while highlighting the classic trade-offs between precision, robustness, and actuation smoothness. These findings open future perspectives for controller validation across more realistic conditions.

title: Linguistic Prior-Enhanced Open Information Extraction: Contrastive Learning and Optimal Transport Approach
authors: Xiaoyu Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: Open Information Extraction (OpenIE) refers to the task of transforming unstructured text into structured triples containing subject, predicate, and object.. Current detection-based OpenIE methods have achieved remarkable progress on public benchmarks. However, existing models have not fully exploited the implicit linguistic prior knowledge embedded in text sequences. In particular, syntactic dependencies and part-of-speech (POS) tags serve as essential signals for integrating heterogeneous features and resolving semantic ambiguities like polysemy. To tackle these challenges, this paper proposes a contrastive learning and optimal transport-based OpenIE method that incorporates linguistic prior knowledge, referred to as COT-OIE. Specifically, we reconstruct the extraction model into a contrastive optimal transport framework to guide feature space optimization. Furthermore, part-of-speech (POS) information and dependency syntactic information are introduced and embedded as linguistic priors. They participate in and guide the computation of multi-head cross-attention, enabling fusion of heterogeneous features and capturing more contextual information from text sequences. To further enhance the model’s discriminative ability for feature representation, we design a global objective loss that simultaneously considers the contrastive learning phase and the optimal transport phase. Experimental findings indicate that the proposed approach surpasses leading baseline models on two standard OpenIE datasets.

title: An Empirical study of Integrating Multi-Omics Data with Explainable Graph Convolutional Networks for Biomarker Discovery and Advancing Disease Risk Stratification
authors: Suresh Kulandaivelu, Mohan Mani
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: A unification of multiple biological streams of information, e.g., proteomic, epigenomic, and genomic information typically represented as multi-omics datasets is needed to effectively classify early disease risk. Such data diversity and complexity, degrade both forecast precision and interpretability. To describe multi-omics data in biologically interpretable network form, the study discusses the need for establishing a graph-based representation framework. In this graph-based representation, genes, proteins, and other molecular markers are treated as nodes, and their interactions as edges. The system can capture the complex regulatory connectivity between biomarkers, allowing for accurate stratification of disease risk. The study takes into account the necessity of creating Explainable Graph Convolutional Networks (X-GCN) with hierarchical attention mechanisms, which are critical to transparent and interpretable identification of effective biomarkers that affect patient risk levels. The study also emphasizes the significance of transforming the model predictions into efficient candidate biomarker panels. These panels are of importance for clinical use and need to be handed over to the experts for scrutiny and experimentally verified to make them useful. The study argues that such improvements are necessary to improve the interpretability, clinical usefulness, and predictive power of multi-omics disease risk prediction models by critically reviewing the existing literature.

title: Real-Time AVPC Scheduling with Collaborative Vehicle-Infrastructure Perception and Adaptive RL
authors: Gordon Owusu Boateng, Xinhao Liu, Zhao Wang, Qian Dong, Xiansheng Guo, Azzam Mourad, Mohsen Guizani
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: The growing adoption of Autonomous Electric Vehicles (AEVs) and EV-charging infrastructure necessitates efficient real-time Automated Valet Parking and Charging (AVPC) scheduling frameworks that can accommodate both parking and charging demands. Accurate parking resource scheduling relies on precise resource occupancy detection and resource type classification via environmental perception. However, most existing perception models for the parking lot environment are limited by their sole reliance on static infrastructure-side data and lack of optimal parking resource scheduling solutions for the dynamic AVPC scenario. This paper proposes a novel adaptive Reinforcement Learning (RL) framework for AVPC resource scheduling by leveraging collaborative vehicle-infrastructure perception to enhance situational awareness and scheduling decision-making accuracy. We introduce a multi-sensor fusion model that integrates perception data from AEV-mounted sensors (LiDAR, radar, and cameras) with infrastructure-installed sensors (overhead cameras, Wi-Fi, and Bluetooth beacons). The fused perception output is then used by an adaptive Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) agent with a fusion-aware reward shaping mechanism to guide the global scheduling of AEVs to parking and charging resources. The goal is to jointly optimize parking resource proximity, suitability, and energy cost while improving scheduling success rate. Simulation results using real-world parking lot data demonstrate that the proposed approach significantly outperforms existing baselines, improving convergence and scheduling success rate by at least 18.92% and 10.61%, respectively.

title: Design and Implementation of a QoS-Driven Resource Allocation Algorithm for LoRa Multi-Channel Gateway Networks
authors: Qingzheng Li, Dongsheng Zhao, Yawen Dai
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: This paper addresses the challenges of access conflicts, intermodulation interference, and QoS guarantees in high-density IoT scenarios using LoRa, proposing a multi-channel resource allocation method oriented towards Quality of Service (QoS). Through spectrum testing, intermodulation experiments, and SF interference analysis, a channel and power optimization model under engineering constraints is constructed, and initial parameter allocation is implemented based on NSGA-II. A reliable downlink control and dynamic adjustment mechanism is achieved by combining an improved DQ protocol, enabling the gateway to finely optimize node parameters based on real-time link quality. Experimental results show that this method significantly reduces packet loss rate and improves system throughput and latency stability under high load conditions, validating the effectiveness and engineering deployability of the proposed scheme in practical LoRa ad hoc networks.

title: A Twin-Layered Learning Model for Early Hypertension Detection
authors: Duggirala Sarath Babu, Rayapudi Aruna
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Hypertension is one of the abnormal blood conditions in humans that can affect the heart. Early detection of hypertension can reduce complications and long-term risks. In this paper, a Twin-Layered Learning Model (TLLM) is presented to detect hypertension using dual-stage deep learning (DSDL) for accurate early-stage detection. In the TLLM, the first layer consists of feature-extraction blocks, such as convolutional and recurrent layers, that process multi-source physiological signals, including ECG, health details, and blood pressure, to create deep temporal–spatial embeddings. The second layer, a tiny refined block, refines the tiny filters and features and integrates with the attention-based classifier to improve critical strength and decrease false-positive rates (FPR). The model also integrates adaptive normalization, cross-domain feature alignment, and a hybrid loss function to improve robustness by filtering noise from the refined data— experiments conducted on a benchmark dataset that demonstrate superior performance across classification metrics.

title: GraphRCA-Chorus: Choreographed Multi-Agent Graph Transformers for Root Cause Analysis in Microservices
authors: Jialei Fu, Zeyu Wang, Peiqing Lu, Kejian Tong
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: The rapid proliferation of microservice architectures has significantly increased the complexity of failure diagnosis and root cause analysis (RCA). Traditional centralized approaches struggle to scale with the dynamic topology and heterogeneous data streams inherent in modern cloud-native environments. We introduce GraphRCA-Chorus, a novel choreographed multi-agent framework that leverages decentralized graph-transformer architectures and Llama-3 large language models to autonomously localize faults. By treating metrics, logs, and traces as a unified temporal graph, our system enables agents to negotiate hypotheses through a self-organizing consensus protocol without central orchestration. Experimental results on benchmark datasets demonstrate that GraphRCA-Chorus outperforms state-of-the-art baselines by 7.6% in top-1 accuracy while reducing mean time to detection, offering a robust solution for automated reliability assurance in complex distributed systems.

title: Recommender Systems in E-Commerce: Algorithmic Taxonomy, Comparative Evaluation, and a Framework for Next-Generation Personalization
authors: Houda El Bachri, Hayat Sahlaoui, El Arbi Abdellaoui Alaoui, Abdelaaziz Hessane, Said Agoujil
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: Recommender systems have become a cornerstone of modern e-commerce, empowering platforms to personalize user experiences, enhance engagement, and optimize sales by addressing the challenge of information overload. This review advances the state of the field through four key contributions. First, it introduces a structured taxonomy that systematically organizes recent algorithmic advances across five paradigms—traditional, deep learning, reinforcement learning, transformer-based, and graph-based recommender systems—accompanied by detailed performance metrics and trade-off analyses. Second, it proposes a reference pipeline architecture that synthesizes these paradigms into a coherent and modular production framework, integrating their complementary strengths to achieve robustness, adaptability, and scalability. Third, a comparative evaluation is presented to identify the conditions under which each paradigm excels, considering data characteristics, operational constraints, and business objectives. Finally, the review provides evidence-based deployment guidelines distilled from empirical results and realworld case studies, offering practical insights for transitioning from research prototypes to large-scale production systems. By unifying methodological perspectives and bridging theory with practice, this work charts a comprehensive roadmap for the design of next-generation recommender systems capable of transforming personalization in e-commerce and beyond.

title: Graph-Transformer Reconstruction Learning for Unsupervised Anomaly Detection in Dependency-Coupled Systems
authors: Chong Zhang, Chihui Shao, Junjie Jiang, Yinan Ni, Xiaoxuan Sun
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: To address the practical challenges of diverse anomaly patterns, strongly coupled dependencies, and high labeling costs in large-scale complex infrastructures, this paper presents an unsupervised anomaly detection method that integrates graph neural networks with Transformer models. The approach learns normal system behavior and identifies deviations without relying on anomaly labels. Infrastructure components are abstracted as nodes in a dependency graph, where nodes are characterized by multiple source observability signals. A graph encoder aggregates neighborhood information to produce structure-enhanced node representations. Self-attention mechanisms are introduced along the temporal dimension to capture long-range dynamic dependencies. This design enables joint modeling of structural relations and temporal evolution. A reconstruction-based training strategy is adopted to constrain the learning of normal patterns. Reconstruction error is used to derive anomaly scores for detection. To ensure reproducibility and ease of deployment, complete specifications of data organization, training procedures, and key hyperparameter settings are provided. Comparative experiments on public benchmarks demonstrate overall advantages across multiple evaluation metrics and confirm the effectiveness of the proposed framework in representing anomaly propagation and temporal drift characteristics in complex systems.

title: Predicting Crude Oil Futures using Feed Forward Neural Networks and Technical Indicators: A Comparative Study on WTI and Brent
authors: Kumar Chandar S, Hitesh Punjabi, Mayur Malik
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: In the domains of economic management and energy analysis, forecasting the price of crude oil is increasing popularity. It is essential to the facilitating rapid and cost-effective development with improved quality. Accurate prediction of the crude oil market is essential for steady and fast economic development because of its enormous influence on the global economy and society. Moreover, precise crude oil price prediction aids the traders in making accurate decision to maximize profits. In this work, a machine learning method for forecasting future global price data for crude oil is provided based on past data. The proposed model consists of three phases: primarily, historical data of selected crude oil data are gathered and normalized using data normalization technique. Secondly, technical indicators are derived from the crude oil data. Finally, a Feed Forward Neural Network (FFNN) is designed and trained using these technical indicators to forecast the price of crude oil in the future. Daily, weekly, and monthly data from Brent crude oil and West Texas Intermediate (WTI) are used to evaluate the generated model's prediction ability. To find the most effective FFNN configuration, the model's efficacy is evaluated by adjusting hidden layer number and hidden neurons. Performance of the model is also analyzed by varying number of training and testing samples. The experimental outcomes demonstrates that the designed model exhibits excellent performance for both WTI and Brent data. Notably, the model proves to be effective in predicting crude oil prices, when technical indicators are used as input variables.

title: Thermal Image Guided Reinforcement Learning for UAV Thermal Soaring
authors: Haobo Liu, Ke Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: Long-endurance flight of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) can be achieved by exploiting atmospheric energy sources such as thermal updrafts. In this paper, we integrate a thermal image sensor modality in the control loop, providing the agent with a local map of ground surface thermal intensity (e.g., an infrared temperature image of the terrain) to guide thermal detection. Unlike prior methods that rely only on point sensors, this allows the agent to proactively steer toward areas likely to contain updrafts instead of waiting to stumble upon lift. A high-fidelity simulation environment is developed with a CFD model of a thermal updraft using the Boussinesq approximation. The SAC agent learns a policy π(a|s) that optimizes climbing performance – balancing climb rate and energy use – by mapping the observed state (including thermal imagery) to control actions. Simulation results demonstrate that the learned policy enables the UAV to autonomously locate and exploit thermals, achieving sustained climbs. The result shows that thermal image guidance can enhance soaring efficiency, and thermal capture rate. The proposed approach shows promise for extending UAV endurance through bio-inspired autonomous thermal soaring.

title: Research on a Lightweight Full-Stack Edge Execution Optimization Framework Based on Serverless and WebAssembly
authors: Yu Mao, Zhishen Chen, Xiangjun Ma
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: This paper proposes a lightweight full-stack execution framework integrating Serverless architecture with WebAssembly runtime optimization to enhance performance and energy efficiency in edge deployments. The system employs modular task decomposition and Light-Container Isolation (LCI) technology to achieve cross-node function reuse on AWS Lambda and Cloudflare Workers platforms. An Reinforcement Learning Scheduler (RL-Scheduler) predicts request distribution in real-time, dynamically allocating CPU cycles and memory limits. Targeted testing demonstrates a 52% reduction in cold start time, a 33% decrease in average execution latency, and a 21% reduction in energy consumption under 3,000 concurrent tasks. Results confirm the framework effectively enhances execution autonomy and cross-platform portability for edge Serverless systems in multi-tenant environments.

title: A High-Precision Detection Framework for Crane Wire Rope Defects Based on Multi-Scale Feature Collaboration and Axial Topology-Awareness
authors: Fuquan Nie, Fukai Yu, Yuxuan Nie
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Detecting defects in crane wire ropes is crucial for ensuring safety in heavy industries. To address the inefficiencies of traditional methods and the limitations of current deep learning models in extracting multi-scale features, adapting axial topology, and balancing efficiency and accuracy, we introduce an enhanced YOLO11 framework named YOLO-MA in this study. This framework incorporates a Multi-scale Feature Fusion Network (MFFN) to retain defect features across all scales through channel partitioning and cascading receptive fields. Additionally, it introduces an Axial Topologyaware Detection Head (ATDH) that integrates a linear topology prior to enhance anti-interference capabilities and positioning accuracy. Experimental results demonstrate that YOLO-MA achieves an mAP50 of 94.3%, mAP50-90 of 63.2%, a recall rate of 88.9%, an inference speed of 536 frames per second (FPS), and superior parameter efficiency. YOLO-MA presents an effective solution for detecting defects in industrial components with varying aspect ratios.

title: Enhancing Small and Elongated Defect Detection based on Radial Convolution and Content-Adaptive Upsampling
authors: Zhe Xu, Yuan Xie
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: Wind turbine blade defect detection is critical for ensuring operational safety. However, existing lightweight object detection models struggle with small and elongated defects due to limited receptive fields and boundary loss during multi-scale fusion. This paper proposes an enhanced YOLO11-based framework, introducing two modules: Radial Feature Convolution (RFConv) and Content-Adaptive Lightweight Upsampler (CALU). RFConv expands the receptive field with multi-directional convolutions, improving small and elongated defect detection. CALU preserves boundary details during upsampling by learning content-aware sampling offsets. Experiments on a wind turbine blade defect dataset show that the proposed method outperforms YOLO11, achieving higher recall and mean Average Precision with only 2.41 million parameters, demonstrating its effectiveness for practical blade inspection.

title: An Adaptive Cyber Threat Intelligence System for Monitoring and Countering Underground Economy Operations
authors: Karanam Manohara Rao, Bala K
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The fast development of the subterranean cyber economies has further increased the magnitude and complexity of the online threats, and it is becoming difficult to detect and respond to them using the conventional detection and response systems. This paper describes an Adaptive Cyber Threat Intelligence System (ACTIS), which is aimed at monitoring, analysing and countering malicious activities that are brought about due to the operations of the underground economy. The proposed framework is a combination of data-driven analytics, anomaly detection based on machine learning, and real-time threat intelligence fusion to give valuable insights to security operations centres (SOCs) and policy-makers. ACTIS uses multi-source data, such as dark web forums, marketplaces on hacker websites, as well as illegal communication channels, to derive threat indicators and patterns of behaviour. An active adaptation engine constantly optimises the detection models in accordance with changing attack vectors and attacker strategies, and keeps the system resilient to new attacker threats. Experimental findings indicate that the suggested system has high detection rates, lower false-positive rates, and improved response rates in comparison to traditional intelligence systems.

title: Multi-Objective Optimization Model for Operation Efficiency Analysis and Optimization of 220kv Spare Transformer
authors: Chuansheng Luo, Lei Huang, Guangliang Zhang, Anrun Wang, Guanjun Song
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: In this paper, a multi-objective optimization model is constructed to optimize the operation efficiency of 220kV spare transformer, aiming at minimizing the life cycle cost, maximizing the comprehensive energy efficiency and optimizing the system reliability. The model comprehensively considers the objectives of economy, energy efficiency and reliability in transformer operation, and introduces practical operation constraints, such as load rate range, switching operation frequency limit and power quality requirements. Using the improved NSGA-II as the solver, the convergence speed of the algorithm and the quality of solution set distribution are improved through key improvements such as dynamic crossover rate design, elite retention strategy and improved penalty function method. Based on the actual operation data of a 220kV hub substation in a provincial power grid in East China, the model is verified. The results show that compared with the traditional single-objective optimization scheme, the multi-objective optimization method proposed in this paper has significantly improved the key indicators such as life cycle cost, comprehensive energy efficiency and system reliability, and the life cycle cost has decreased by 16.6%, the comprehensive energy efficiency has increased by 0.9%, the system reliability score has increased by 9.6%, and the annual power loss has decreased by 12.7%. The coverage rate of N-2 redundancy is increased from 75% to 95%, which effectively balances the conflict between economy, energy efficiency and reliability, and provides strong support for the scientific management of spare transformers.

title: Demystifying Black-Box Credit Scoring Models Through Glass-Box Interpretability: A Random Forest and XAI Framework
authors: Samragee Chopra, Abha Sharma, Piyush Kumar Rath, Denny Ben
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: Identifying fraudulent behavior in financial systems remains a difficult task, primarily due to the uneven distribution of data, where legitimate transactions vastly outnumber fraudulent ones.
