Standard machine learning classifiers often fail to catch these rare cases, increasing the chances of financial threats. This work applies a Random Forest model to the German credit risk dataset, incorporating feature refinement, rebalancing techniques, and interpretable AI methods—namely SHAP and LIME—to strengthen prediction reliability and clarity. Detecting fraud in financial systems continues to be a major concern, largely because of the imbalance in transaction data—fraudulent cases are far less common than legitimate ones. As a result, traditional machine learning methods often fail to catch these rare events, which increases the likelihood of financial losses. In this work, a Random Forest classifier is used on the German credit risk dataset, along with techniques like feature selection, class balancing, and explainable AI approaches such as SHAP and LIME help boost model performance while also making its decisions more transparent. The evaluation shows that the model achieves better fraud detection results, especially with higher recall, helping reduce the number of missed fraudulent transactions. Additionally, the use of SHAP and LIME makes the model’s decisions easier to interpret, providing clearer insights for financial analysts. This study offers an effective and explainable machine learning solution aimed at strengthening fraud prevention strategies in the financial sector.

title: NL2SQL Generation Technology for Multi-Source Heterogeneous Data Based on RLHF and Knowledge Graph
authors: Dong Wang, Yize Le
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Relying on simple rule matching methods can only mechanically transform simple semantics, resulting in low NL2SQL generation efficiency. This paper studies NL2SQL generation technology for multi-source heterogeneous data based on RLHF and knowledge graph. By extracting key indicator information from the structured tables of the power grid business system and constructing an entity relationship network, and simultaneously performing text preprocessing, information extraction, and semantic understanding on unstructured documents, multi-source heterogeneous data is integrated into a structured knowledge graph, forming the foundation for a semantic engine that supports semantic understanding. The Transformer model architecture is used to generate initial SQL statements. Combined with human expert feedback to train a reward model to evaluate the generation quality, the policy network parameters are optimized through a policy gradient algorithm, so that the generated SQL statements meet the business requirements in terms of semantic consistency, syntactic correctness, and execution accuracy. Based on the constructed database characteristic knowledge base, the initial SQL is parsed into an abstract syntax tree and then matched with the target system adjustment rules. Combined with the semantic engine to maintain the query intent, the reward model is used to select the optimal compatible SQL, ultimately achieving accurate NL2SQL conversion across database systems. Experiments show that the proposed technique maintains an accuracy of over 90% with minimal fluctuations under varying data complexity, and an average semantic matching rate exceeding 96%. Compared to the comparison methods, its response speed consistently leads, effectively improving NL2SQL generation efficiency.

title: Global-Local Collaborative Learning: A Dynamic and Attentive Framework for Tiny Object Detection in Aerial Imagery
authors: Yixin Li, Longdi Feng
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: The ubiquitous deployment of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) necessitates robust low-altitude security systems; however, detecting extremely tiny objects against complex, dynamic aerial backgrounds remains a formidable challenge in computer vision. Existing detection paradigms often suffer from "feature vanishing" in deep networks, failing to reconcile the intrinsic trade-off between high recall and real-time inference on resource-constrained edge devices. Here, we present DBC-YOLO, an enhanced detection framework driven by global-local collaborative learning. We first introduce Dynamic Convolution to reconstruct the backbone, empowering the network with content-adaptive weight generation to robustly capture multi-scale, deformable targets. Furthermore, we engineer a "divide-and-conquer" hybrid attention neck: integrating Bi-Level Routing Attention (BRA) to selectively sparsify background noise in shallow layers, while synergizing with Coordinate Attention (CA) to reconstruct precise spatial localization in deep layers. Additionally, a high-resolution P2 detection head is constructed to directly leverage fine-grained shallow features, effectively resolving tiny object feature degradation. On the authoritative VisDrone2019-DET benchmark, DBC-YOLO achieves a qualitative leap in performance: attaining an APs of 35.5% (+4.6%) for tiny objects and an mAP50 of 68.8%. Crucially, despite the architectural enhancements, the model maintains a robust inference speed of 104 FPS, fulfilling the strict real-time requirements of aerial surveillance. Notably, our model outperforms the computationally intensive Vision Transformer (ViT) architecture in accuracy while achieving a 2.5-fold increase in inference speed and reducing computational costs by approximately 25%. These findings bridge the gap between high-precision perception and low-latency computing, providing a premier solution for next-generation intelligent emergency response systems.

title: AGC Optimal Dispatch Method for Large Hybrid Variable-Speed and Fixed-Speed Pumped Storage Power Stations
authors: Jingqiu Kang, Ning Ma, Zhenshan Chen, Di Yao, Hui Deng, Xiao Qi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy (ICSGE)
abstract: Currently, fixed-speed units dominate the pumped storage power stations in China. Although variable-speed units are being progressively deployed owing to their flexible regulation capabilities, the optimal automatic generation control (AGC) dispatch for hybrid power stations with both fixed and variable speed units remains a critical challenge. To address this issue, this paper proposes an optimal AGC dispatch method for large-scale hybrid pumped storage power stations. Firstly, by analyzing the differences in operational characteristics between fixed and variable speed units, an operational constraint system for various working conditions is established, leading to the formulation of an AGC optimal dispatch model that synergizes the advantages of both unit types. Secondly, a distributionally robust optimization algorithm based on Benders decomposition is designed to accelerate the solution of this two-stage optimization problem. Finally, a simulation model is developed in MATLAB for a case study power station with 10 fixed-speed and 2 variable-speed units to validate the proposed method's effectiveness.

title: Fault Diagnosis and Rapid Isolation Method of Complex Power Grid Based on Deep Learning
authors: Liyun Pan, Lei Wang, Xinqiao Gu, Junxian Yan, Rui Ma
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: Aiming at the problem of fault diagnosis and rapid isolation of complex power grid, this paper proposes a method based on deep learning. With the development of global energy internet, the scale and complexity of power system increase, and the difficulty of fault diagnosis and isolation increases. Traditional methods have some problems, such as insufficient multi-modal data fusion, decoupling of spatio-temporal features and weak linkage of isolation strategies. In this paper, a data representation with multisource information is constructed, and the spatio-temporal features are jointly extracted by the graph product-Transformer hybrid network, and the dynamic optimal isolation path is generated by introducing the deep reinforcement learning (DRL) framework to realize the linkage of diagnosis and control. The experiment is based on the extended IEEE 118-bus system. The results show that the proposed method is superior to the traditional method in fault diagnosis accuracy and isolation effect, and has the advantages of high practicability and control accuracy, which provides a new idea for complex power grid fault response.

title: Thermo-physical Coupling Modeling and Intelligent Control Algorithm for Silk-making and Drying Process
authors: Tiegong Gao, Ting Jiang, Guangqiu Wen, Yiqing Huang, Wenqiang Wu, Bin Liu
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: In this paper, aiming at the complex thermophysical interaction in the tobacco drying process, the thermo-physical coupling modeling and intelligent control algorithm are studied. Firstly, a three-field coupling model including heat conduction, moisture diffusion and tobacco deformation was constructed. Unsteady heat transfer equation, diffusion model modified by deformation tensor and generalized Maxwell model were used to characterize the internal temperature field, moisture diffusion and tobacco deformation characteristics respectively, and the parameters were identified and optimized by multi-scale model fusion and improved NSGA-II algorithm. Secondly, a dual-channel intelligent control architecture combining physical model apriori and deep learning correction is designed, and a deep reinforcement learning control strategy based on SAC (Soft Actor-Critical) algorithm and an online adaptive mechanism are introduced to realize precise control of the silk drying process. The experimental results show that, compared with traditional PID control, pure physical model control and pure LSTM control, the hybrid control method proposed in this paper has obvious advantages in water content control accuracy, energy consumption reduction and system robustness. The standard deviation of water content decreased below 0.055% and energy consumption is reduced by 21%, which provides effective technical support for the intelligent upgrade of cigarette shredding and drying process and has important engineering application value.

title: Support Vector Regression with Superpixel-Guided Sampling for trimap generation
authors: Weiguang Han, Guilin Yao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: This paper proposes a trimap optimization method that integrates superpixel sampling with support vector regression (SVR), enhanced by a non-local strategy. The incorporation of superpixel sampling improves the quality and robustness of training samples, while the combination of SVR and non-local modeling enables more accurate foreground probability estimation. The core principle of the method is to learn a regression function such that the deviation between predicted and ground-truth values for the majority of samples remains within a predefined tolerance threshold $\varepsilon$, thereby achieving an effective balance between global consistency and preservation of local details. The experiments are conducted on the online matting training dataset introduced by Rhemann et al. Quantitative and qualitative evaluations demonstrate that the proposed approach consistently outperforms conventional trimap optimization methods, confirming its effectiveness and practical applicability in real-world scenarios.

title: Federated and Explainable AI Models for Privacy-Preserving Predictive Analytics in Healthcare System
authors: Abdul Aziz, Bhaduri R., Srinivasa Rao Kurakula, Addul Rahmath Mohammad, Syed Sadique Basha, Sivakoteswararao Katta
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Healthcare systems increasingly rely on predictive analytics to improve patient outcomes, yet concerns about data privacy and model transparency remain significant barriers to adoption. This paper presents a novel framework integrating Federated Learning (FL) with Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) to enable privacy-preserving predictive analytics in distributed healthcare environments. Our approach allows multiple healthcare institutions to collaboratively train machine learning models without sharing sensitive patient data, addressing critical privacy regulations such as HIPAA and GDPR. We incorporate explainability techniques including SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) and LIME (Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations) to provide clinically interpretable insights into model predictions. The framework is evaluated on real-world healthcare datasets for disease prediction and risk stratification tasks. Results demonstrate that federated models achieve comparable accuracy to centralized approaches while maintaining data privacy, with explainability layers enhancing clinical trust and adoption. Our work bridges the gap between advanced predictive analytics and practical healthcare deployment, offering a scalable solution that balances model performance, privacy preservation, and interpretability. This research contributes to the advancement of trustworthy AI in healthcare systems.

title: GAPS-RL: A Graph Attention Predictive Scheduling Framework for Dynamic Road Construction Management
authors: Hanming Yao
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Road construction projects generally face serious problems of project delays and cost overruns, mainly due to the inability of traditional static planning methods to effectively respond to the dynamic changes in the real environment and uncertainty. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a novel two-stage deep learning framework called GAPS-RL, aimed at achieving proactive project management. The first stage is the Spatiotemporal Prediction Module (STPM), which innovatively integrates Graph Attention Network (GAT) and Attention Long Short Term Memory (LSTM) network. It accurately predicts the future multi-step progress trajectory of all construction activities by learning the structural information of project task dependency graphs and the time evolution data of each task. The second stage is the Multi Objective Scheduling Module (MOSM), which utilizes the Near End Policy Optimization (PPO) algorithm in Reinforcement Learning (RL). This RL agent uses the probabilistic predictions generated in the first stage as its forward-looking state representation, thereby learning a dynamic resource allocation strategy to collaboratively optimize multiple objectives such as project completion time, total cost, and resource utilization. Finally, this article conducted experiments based on a comprehensive road construction project synthesis dataset. The results indicate that the GAPS-RL framework significantly outperforms traditional critical path method (CPM) and series of independent machine learning benchmark models in all key performance indicators.

title: Differentially Private Accelerated Distributed Algorithm for Aggregative Optimization
authors: Bing Liu, Dongxing Li, Li Chai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: This article studies the distributed aggregative optimization (DAO) problem, wherein each agent’s local objective function depends not only on its own decision variables but also on an aggregate term involving all agents’ decisions. In such settings, frequent information exchange among agents raises serious privacy concerns, as sensitive information may be inferred from shared data. To address this issue, we propose a differentially private accelerated distributed gradient tracking algorithm that integrates techniques from distributed dynamic average consensus, the heavy-ball momentum method, and differential privacy (DP). Specifically, to preserve privacy, the exchanged information is perturbed with independent Laplace noise. Moreover, our algorithm uses a noise deduction mechanism to prevent the accumulation of errors caused by noise during the estimation of aggregate variables and local gradients, thereby ensuring the algorithm’s accuracy. Under the assumption that the global objective function is strongly convex and has Lipschitz-continuous gradients, we rigorously prove that the proposed algorithm achieves linear convergence in the mean-square error sense. In addition, we derive explicit suboptimality bounds and formally establish that the algorithm satisfies <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\epsilon $</tex-math> </inline-formula>-DP. Finally, numerical simulations are provided to validate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: GECS-Net: Two-View Correspondence Learning via Geometric Embedding and Cross-Stage Consensus
authors: Zihan Li, Zhihao Zhao, Chengyou Wang, Cheng Zhao
abstract: Two-view correspondence learning is fundamental to geometric computer vision. However, existing methods often fail in complex scenarios due to inadequate spatial modeling and stage-wise inconsistency. We propose geometric embedding and cross-stage-network (GECS-Net), a robust framework that unifies geometry-aware embedding with a progressive cross-stage consensus strategy. Specifically, GECS-Net synergizes a geometric embedding attention (GEA) module using rotary positional embeddings (RoPE) to encode relative spatial constraints, and a cross-stage graph feature extraction (CSGFE) module to enforce structural consensus via sparse graph neural networks. Evaluations on YFCC100M and SUN3D show that GECS-Net significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieving an 82.2% F-score on YFCC100M and improving mAP@5<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula> by 8.29% in unknown outdoor scenes, demonstrating superior robustness and generalization.

title: PNProRL: Self-Supervised Neural Relighting via Photometric Perception and Progressive Optimization
authors: Chenhao Guo, Zhulun Yang, Xin Ding, You Yang, Qiong Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Portrait relighting shows great potential in photography, film, and AR by simulating diverse lighting effects. Existing state-of-the-art methods often rely on expensive paired OLAT or synthetic data, which limits scalability. Moreover, accurately modeling the interaction between physics-guided rendering, neural rendering, and real-world remains challenging. To address these issues, we propose a novel multi-stage self-supervised relighting framework. It progressively refines intrinsic scene properties via a simple-to-complex training strategy, removing the need for expensive paired data while adapting to various lighting conditions. One core design introduces a novel pre-training method approach using diverse shading-based masking for self-reconstruction, which improves the model's perception of complex lighting variations. Furthermore, we introduce two perceptual modules that leverage the linear superposition of light to narrow the gap between physics-guided and neural rendering, and better align relit results with real-world observations. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our unified framework achieves new state-of-the-art performance in portrait relighting, surpassing recent methods in photorealism, synthesis quality, and identity preservation. It provides a practical paradigm for high-fidelity relighting under diverse lighting.

title: Improved distance estimation method with adaptive fitness function constraints based on density and connectivity in WSN localization scenarios
authors: Songyut Phoemphon
abstract: Wireless sensor networks (WSNs) often experience localization inaccuracies because of signal propagation asymmetry. This asymmetry causes signal strength variations and leads to discrepancies among the shortest-path estimates between sensor nodes; specifically, the shortest path from Si to Sj may differ from that from Sj to Si. To address this challenge, an enhanced localization method that incorporates bidirectional distance estimation between anchor nodes with known locations and unknown nodes and assigns a weight value to each path is proposed. This strategy is combined with a single-hop distance estimation technique. Furthermore, particle swarm optimization (PSO) is employed to estimate the positions of unknown nodes, and fuzzy logic is used to compute adaptive weights for each anchor node on the basis of the accuracy of the distance approximation results. Experimental evaluations are conducted under two anchor deployment scenarios (nonuniform and perimeter cases) and two degrees of irregularity (DOI) values (0.00 and 0.01). The results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior localization accuracy, outperforming the existing approaches (DEDC, PM, RANP-PSO, IGWO-CS, GAPSODV-Hop, and DV-Hop) by average margins of 12.74%, 16.14%, 34.06%, 36.28%, 38.29%, and 44.41%, respectively. These findings highlight the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed approach in terms of increasing the localization accuracy achieved in WSNs affected by signal variations.

title: Autonomous Navigation for Mobile Robots in Emergency Rescue
authors: Hongda Chen, Shanshan Sun, Zefeng Wang, Wei Shen, Jikai Yu, JeanMarie Nianga, Laurent Peyrodie, Huajun Wang
abstract: Emergency rescue robots are currently widely used in disaster relief scenarios such as earthquake response, mine collapses, and industrial explosions. Due to environmental challenges such as terrain variability and weak GPS signals, robots experience reduced localization accuracy and prolonged path planning, failing to meet the demands of emergency rescue operations. To address these issues, this study employs the Ackermann quick-response emergency mobile robot as the experimental platform. Initially, the application of the UKF algorithm significantly enhanced the state estimation accuracy of the robot. Combined with the AMCL algorithm, it achieved high-precision localization with an average accuracy error less than ±0.036 m. Subsequently, the DAC-A* algorithm was proposed to address the issues with the traditional A* algorithm, such as excessive turning points and large turning angles, which delay the robot's arrival at the rescue site. Simulations conducted in Matlab demonstrate that compared to the traditional A* algorithm, the DAC-A* algorithm reduces the time to reach the rescue destination by nearly 40%, substantially improving rescue efficiency. Lastly, this research provides reliable technical support for disaster response, bearing significant practical significance and application value. Future enhancements through further algorithm optimization and exploration of more application scenarios could better meet the real needs of emergency rescue.

title: Spatial Balancing for RGB-Thermal Semantic Segmentation in Autonomous Driving: A Study from Analysis to Improvement
authors: Haotian Li, Henry K. Chu, Yuxiang Sun
abstract: Semantic segmentation based on RGB-Thermal (RGB-T) data fusion has made great progress in the field of autonomous driving. However, we find that most existing RGB-T semantic segmentation methods exhibit inferior performance in image central regions, in which segmentation performance is critical for driving safety. We refer to this phenomenon as spatial bias. To discover the reason for spatial bias, we design a series of experiments. The results challenge the common knowledge that more training data lead to better segmentation performance, and reveal a close causal relationship between segmentation performance and object complexity as well as image quality. We also provide a theoretical interpretation for the causal relationship using information theory and feature space analysis. Based on the findings, we propose a Gaussian-guided regional balancing masking method to balance segmentation performance across different image regions. Moreover, we introduce a spatial-weighted loss to further enhance the overall segmentation performance. Experimental results on two public datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of our method in mitigating spatial bias and improving balanced performance.

title: Scaling AI Access Across State Universities: A Multi-Institutional Platform Approach
authors: Bernd Decker, Uta Christoph, Sarah Grzemski
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 ACM SIGUCCS Annual Conference
abstract: None

title: Secure Semantic Communication over Wiretap Channels: Rate-Distortion-Equivocation Tradeoff_supp1-3676713.pdf
authors: Denis Kozlov

title: Two-Phase Cell Switching in 6G vHetNets: Sleeping-Cell Load Estimation and Renewable-Aware Switching Toward NES
authors: Maryam Salamatmoghadasi, Metin Ozturk, Halim Yanikomeroglu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: This paper proposes a two-phase framework to improve the sustainability in vertical heterogeneous networks (vHetNets) that integrate various types of base stations (BSs), including terrestrial macro BSs (MBSs), small BSs (SBSs), and a high-altitude platform station-super MBS (HAPS-SMBS). In Phase I, we address the critical and often-overlooked challenge of estimating the traffic load of sleeping SBSs, a prerequisite for practical cell switching operations, by introducing three methods with varying data dependencies: (i) a distance-based estimator (no historical data), (ii) a multi-level clustering (MLC) estimator (limited historical data), and (iii) a long short-term memory (LSTM)-based temporal predictor (full historical data). In Phase II, we incorporate the most accurate estimation results from Phase I into a renewable energy-aware cell switching strategy, explicitly modeling solar-powered SBSs in three operational scenarios that reflect realistic hybrid grid-renewable deployments. This flexible design allows the framework to adapt switching strategies based on renewable availability and storage conditions, making it more practical and robust for real-world networks. Using a real call detail record dataset from Milan, simulation results show that the LSTM method achieves a mean absolute percentage error (MAPE) below 1% in Phase I, while in Phase II, the threshold-based solar integration scenario achieves up to 23% network energy saving (NES) relative to conventional cell switching. Overall, the proposed framework bridges the gap between theoretical cell switching models and practical, sustainable 6G radio access network (RAN) operation, enabling significant energy saving without compromising quality of service.

title: "Why to vaccinate her? She’s just a girl": Exploring Challenges and Opportunities in Cervical Cancer Care in India
authors: Sara Moin, Pushpendra Singh
publish: ACM Transactions on Computing for Healthcare
abstract: None

title: Preparing the next generation of AO-compensated Ground-to-GEO Bidirectional Optical Links by fine analysis of the current systems limitations and numerical test of advanced AO concepts
authors: Aurélie Montmerle Bonnefois, Cyril Petit, Perrine Lognoné, Timothée Vène, Florian Quatresooz, Kyliann Robert, Jérôme Henrion, Geoffrey Maulion, Yann Lai-Tim, Axel Vincent-Randonnier, Jean-Marc Conan, Laurent Mugnier, Laurie Paillier, Nicolas Védrenne, Laurent Coret, Sylvain Poulenard, Thomas Anfray, Xavier Gnata, Sarah Montigaud, Jean-Christophe Richard
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: Now that the first generation of adaptive optics dedicated to bidirectional ground-to-GEO optical links has been successfully tested, in-depth analysis of experimental performance is needed for current system optimization and future model-based concepts fine-tuning. Here we first present the principle of our ground station digital twin AOSIM and how it compares very well to experimental data obtained from bidirectional ground-to-GEO links between ONERA’s OGS FEELINGS and ADS GEO Payload TELEO; then we show some example of ways we used AOSIM for optimizing our current system with regard to Shack-Hartmann Wavefront Sensor and control matrix; and finally, we discuss perspectives in using AOSIM for fine-tuning of AO next generation concepts, through the example of optimal Point-Ahead-Angle pre-compensation of the uplink.

title: Multi-Dimensional Identification and Mechanism Exploration of Key Factors Affecting Scientific Configuration of Hydropower-Wind-Photovoltaic-Energy Storage in Southwest China
authors: Yu Zhang, Li Fan, Wenju Liang, Qiang Lu, Wei Tang, Qingkun Tan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy (ICSGE)
abstract: To address wind/photovoltaic (PV) curtailment in the development of hydropower-wind-PV-energy storage (HWPE) and achieve full absorption of new energy in Southwest China (i.e., Sichuan, Chongqing, and Tibet), this study explores the key factors governing its scientific configuration. Based on the "resource-load-system-economy" dynamic balance, 14 key factors are identified and categorized into four dimensions, with emphasis on four core factors, namely the hydropower regulation coefficient. By employing the control variable method, orthogonal experimental design, and Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) model, the impact of ±20% variations in each factor on the configuration ratio is quantified. Results show that higher values of the hydropower regulation coefficient and the new energy guaranteed output coefficient reduce the demand for energy storage and increase wind/PV share; higher load peak-valley difference rate requires more energy storage and limits wind/PV; energy with higher kWh profit has higher configuration priority. Targeted recommendations are proposed, and the proposed quantitative method can provide technical support for the scientific configuration of HWPE in Southwest China.

title: RIS Partitioning and Link Selection for Maximizing Connectivity of NOMA UAV Networks
authors: Mohammed Saif, Shahrokh Valaee
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: Recent works propose a simple reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) partitioning and link selection scheme for maximizing connectivity of unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) networks, under a common modeling assumption that one RIS serves a single user. This paper considers a general model using nonorthogonal multiple access (NOMA) and multiple RISs, showing its high effectiveness at maximizing connectivity of UAV networks via scheduling multiple users per RIS rather than the one-to-one user-RIS scheduling scheme considered in the previous works and the RIS free network. An alternative scheme of NOMA user clustering and closed-form solution for RIS-aided link selection and RIS partitioning is proposed, which is practical for dense networks where the number of users greatly exceeds the number of RISs and UAVs.

title: Automatic High Beam Control System with Integrated Weather and Steering Detection for Smart Vehicles
authors: Sreeram K, Meenakshi Manoj, Arjun Dinesh, Rohan Varkey Roy
abstract: This paper presents a cost-effective, sensor-integrated automatic high beam control system to improve safety for drivers and improve driving comfort in real-time conditions. Compared to existing systems that involve single parameter control, the proposed design comprises of a multi-sensory solution combining a lux meter (BH1750), rain sensor (YL-83) and a rotary encoder to ensure better management of vehicle lights. This dynamically switches between high and low beams depending on ambient light intensity, sense precipitation and activates fog lamps, and utilizes angle of the steering, to initiate corner lamps for increased visibility. An ESP32 microcontroller manages the inputs and regulates the lights through relays for real-time decisions without user intervention. The system was validated on a test vehicle and using the simulation software program TinkerCAD. Modeling and prototype results indicate that car drivers would have better visibility during the night and poor weather conditions like rain and light fog. The driver's potential glare to other road users would be reduced, due to the corner illumination. Thus, this paper presents a cost-effective, modular system, capable of being integrated into a wide range of vehicles for a relatively low cost. The paper also encompasses future enhancements, including AI-based adaptation and vehicle network (CAN bus) integration.

title: Research on Optimizing Occlusion Duration Based on Multi-Intelligent Optimization Algorithms and Trajectory Modeling
authors: Yuhong Chen, Yongjia Gao, Yao Zheng
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: To address the calculation and optimization of maximum effective shielding duration in shielding systems, this paper integrates trajectory modeling with intelligent optimization algorithms such as genetic algorithms and ant colony optimization to conduct parameter optimization studies across diverse scenarios. Under defined parameter conditions, trajectory models for each core unit are constructed. Projection formulas determine shielding effectiveness, while the binary search method solves for effective shielding intervals within specific ranges to obtain foundational shielding duration data. For the multivariate scenario involving a single execution unit and a single shielding unit, decision variables include movement direction, velocity, and the release/activation timing of the shielding unit. A particle swarm optimization algorithm is introduced to construct an objective planning model. Iterative updates of particle positions and velocities balance search randomness with optimization efficiency, enabling rapid identification of optimal solutions. For scenarios with a single execution unit and multiple masking units, a genetic algorithm is employed. Through population initialization, fitness evaluation and selection, crossover, and mutation operations, the timing parameters of multiple masking units are optimized to maximize the combined masking effect. For scenarios with multiple execution units and a single shielding unit, an ant colony optimization (ACO) approach is employed. Utilizing pheromone concentration and heuristic information to guide parameter selection, ACO achieves collaborative optimization across multiple execution units. The targeted application of different intelligent algorithms effectively increases the total shielding duration across various scenarios, validating their effectiveness in complex multi-variable optimization problems.

title: Artificial Intelligence (AI) based Rabies Detection in Dogs
authors: S. S. Thamilselvi, Dharshini A, Joshitha R
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Rabies has remained a big health issue globally especially in areas that have domestic dogs as the main sources of transmission to humans. Rabies infection in dogs can be controlled through early identification of the disease to contain the outbreak of the disease, reduce exposure of people, and carry out timely intervention measures. Traditional laboratory based diagnostic techniques, including direct fluorescent antibody (DFA) and RT-PCR, proved to be sound, although invasive, costly and impractical in the environment with limited resources or in the field. In order to address these constraints, the paper introduces a multimodal artificial intelligence (AI)-based system of non-invasive detection of rabies in dogs. The suggested system will combine video, image, and audio information to record behavioral, physiological, and vocal signs of infection. Video analysis goes through abnormal behaviors involving aggression, restlessness, or paralysis, whereas image processing is based on such physical abnormalities as excessive salivation and facial asymmetry. The analysis of audio signals identifies uncharacteristic vocalizations, such as the change of bark frequency and strained howling, which give another diagnostic depth. A combination of these three modalities, the system will increase the detection accuracy and reliability at a low-cost, compact, and field-deployable system. The objective of this approach is to support the rabies control programs in the world, early diagnosis, and minimize the risk of human exposure. The findings reveal that multikind AI can be a useful alternative to the conventional laboratory diagnostics, particularly in the rural or resource-limited setting, and, therefore, enhance animal and human health outcomes.

title: A Novel Hybrid V- and Spoke-type PM Magnetic Gear
authors: Mojtaba Malakooti Khaledi, Seyed Ahmadreza Afsari Kashani
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: Magnetic gears (MGs) do not need to undergo routine maintenance in terms of cooling and lubrication, in contrast to typical mechanical gears. However, they do have certain restrictions, such as low torque density and high cogging torque. A coaxial magnetic gear is suggested in this study in order to simultaneously achieve the lowest cogging torque, the maximum torque density, and the lowest consumption of magnetic material. The proposed structure was optimized using a genetic optimization algorithm, and its magnetic and mechanical performances were evaluated using ANSYS Maxwell 2021-R1 and ANSYS Workbench 2021-R1 finite element software. A comprehensive comparison of the proposed structure with the conventional optimal structure shows the superiority of the proposed structure.

title: Optimal Configuration and Operation Strategy of Power Grid Relay Protection Based on Big Data Analysis
authors: Shichang Hou, Ming Jiao, Yuxi Wang, Liang Wei, Zhibo Yu
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: This paper proposes an optimal configuration and operation strategy of power grid relay protection based on big data analysis. Traditional relay protection relies on manual experience or static setting calculation, and it is difficult to adapt to the dynamic changes and complex fault characteristics of power grid. In this paper, multi-source data such as SCADA, PMU, fault recording and equipment status are integrated to build a power grid operation feature library, and key features are extracted through feature engineering technology. A multi-objective dynamic optimization model is established, with minimizing the weighted sum of misoperation probability, configuration cost and fault removal time as the objective function, and considering the constraints of selectivity, sensitivity and reliability, NSGA-III algorithm is adopted to solve the optimal setting scheme set, and a manual decision-making preference selection mechanism is introduced. In the aspect of operation strategy, based on real-time power flow sensitivity, the directional locking logic and multi-agent collaborative control system are designed for distributed power supply access. Taking IEEE 39-node system as the test platform, the distributed photovoltaic power station is connected, and the effectiveness of the method is verified by the actual power grid operation data and simulation data. The results show that, compared with the traditional scheme, the fault identification accuracy of this method is improved to 97.3%, the average removal time is reduced to 232ms, the average number of misoperation is reduced to 12 times, and the rejection rate is reduced to 3.8% in the DER reverse current scenario, which shows stronger adaptability and reliability, and has obvious economic advantages. The payback period of investment is only 5.7 months, which provides a new idea of data-driven for the intelligent and dynamic development of smart grid relay protection.

title: Research on Risk Dependency Structures and Resource Allocation Optimization in New Energy Technology Collaboration within Enterprise Distributed Innovation
authors: Xiaoqin Gao, Juan Chen, Min Huang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: As new energy technology systems grow increasingly complex, corporate innovation activities in photovoltaics, energy storage, wind power, and energy management exhibit distinct distributed characteristics. Cross-enterprise collaboration and cross-regional innovation have become pivotal drivers for industrial upgrading. However, the lengthy new energy industrial chain, numerous participants, and high technical coupling lead to significant knowledge stickiness in knowledge transfer processes, thereby impairing collaborative innovation efficiency. To address this issue, this paper constructs a probabilistic resource allocation optimization model for distributed innovation networks in new energy enterprises. First, Least Squares Support Vector Machine (LSSVM) is employed to predict the success rate of collaborative innovation tasks. Model parameters are jointly optimized using grid search and Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) under RBF, Polynomial, and Sigmoid kernel functions to adapt to different types of technological coupling and knowledge stickiness structures. Subsequently, a Gaussian Copula is introduced to characterize the risk dependency structure among technological maturity, external market volatility, and R&D collaboration complexity between innovation nodes. This enables the calculation of conditional value at risk (CoVaR) to quantify collaborative innovation risks under high uncertainty in new energy technologies. During resource allocation, a cost-loss model based on Bayesian decision theory is established, and the simulated annealing algorithm is employed to solve for the optimal combination of R&D and collaborative resources. Simulation results demonstrate that under conditions of rapid new energy technology iteration and significant knowledge stickiness, this method reduces resource waste by 17% – 23% compared to traditional empirical allocation approaches while enhancing cross-enterprise innovation collaboration efficiency. This study provides a quantifiable and interpretable decision-making method for distributed innovation management in the new energy industry, holding significant implications for building an efficient industrial collaborative innovation ecosystem.

title: A Multi-Site Water Level Prediction Algorithm Integrating Bayesian Optimization and Temporal Attention Mechanisms
authors: Xinyue Cheng, Junwei Yan, Zikang Zhou, Wenxuan Zhou
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Water level forecasting plays an increasingly critical role in water resource development and utilization, flood prevention and emergency response, efficient vessel scheduling, and safe navigation. This study proposes a multi-site joint water level prediction model—Bayesian-optimized Attention-enhanced Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BAT-BiLSTM). The model incorporates a multi-head temporal attention mechanism to strengthen the extraction of salient time-dependent features and employs a bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) structure to enhance the modeling of both forward and backward dependencies in water level variations, and further utilizes Bayesian optimization to automatically tune hyperparameters, thereby improving the model’s generalization capability and predictive accuracy. Using historical water level records from four representative hydrological stations along the Jianghan Canal in Hubei Province for training and validation, the experimental results demonstrate that BAT-BiLSTM effectively captures the complex dynamic correlations among multiple stations. It achieves high accuracy and robustness in multi-site joint forecasting tasks, providing a technically valuable reference for regional hydrological prediction and water resource management.

title: Autonomous Cloud Security Hardening using Generative AI and Reinforcement Learning in Zero Trust Architectures
authors: Nagaraju Goshikonda
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Cloud environments get much more complex, the traditional security model is no longer equipped to handle such attacks. ZTA or zero trust architecture is often referred to as a security model for the mind, assuming that any and every system cannot be trusted. However, traditional ZTA implementations tend to be rather stable. This study proposes a framework that can develop a self-directed automated cloud security system through Zero Trust Architecture (ZTA), using Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) and Reinforcement Learning (RL). The system automatically responds to attacks by observing the behavior of the sub-systems. After observing it response, it can update and modify the attack policy in a real time. With GenAI to formulate the policies, and Reinforcement Learning (RL) for decision making through feedback, this system strengthens security posture, improves threat detection and enforces Zero Trust compliance. The system can respond to new threats and vulnerabilities. This makes the cloud infrastructures more resilient. The first assessment revealed that the proposed system is better than existing models in terms of flexibility, efficiency, and protection against cyber threats.

title: ARU-Net-MC: Enhanced Residual U-Net with Multi-Head Attention for Lung Nodule Segmentation
authors: Rajesh Tulasi, Vijaya Chandra Jadala
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Early detection of lung cancer through CT imaging significantly improves patient survival rates. Precise segmentation of pulmonary nodules is essential for computer-aided diagnostic systems but remains challenging due to variations in size, contrast, and anatomical complexity. This study introduces ARU-Net-MC, an innovative architecture that synergistically integrates hybrid residual blocks with channel- spatial attention, multi-head attention gates, and a progressive dilated ASPP module. The proposed framework addresses key limitations of existing methods through three novel contributions: (1) hybrid residual blocks with integrated channel-spatial attention for enhanced feature representation, (2) multi-head attention gates for interpretable spatial and channel feature refinement with boundary preservation, and (3) progressive dilated ASPP with adaptable receptive fields for multi-scale context integration. We implement a composite loss function combining Dice, focal, and gradient losses for improved boundary definition. Comprehensive evaluation on the LIDC- IDRI dataset demonstrates state-of-the-art performance with a Dice coefficient of 0.928±0.021, surpassing recent approaches including Swin-UNet (0.912) and nnU-Net (0.920). The method shows particular effectiveness on challenging cases such as small nodules (Dice: 0.903) and ground-glass opacities (Dice: 0.891). Ablation studies confirm each component’s contribution, while attention visualizations provide interpretable insights into model decisions, supporting clinical implementation and trust in AI-assisted diagnosis.

title: Adaptive Federated Multi Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning for Clinical Imaging Applications
authors: Voruganti Naresh Kumar, K. Ashish, G. Vardhan, A. Mohan, D. Sandhya Rani, A. Divya Bharathi
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: This paper introduces FedMediX, an adaptive federated multi agent deep reinforcement learning (MARL) framework for radiological data analysis. FedMediX accurately predicts multiple diseases from different types of diagnostic scans by involving a collaborative deep learning model trained across hospitals without sharing patient data. It is also able to automatically identify the type of medical image and explain the prediction using heatmaps. To address privacy constraints among multiple hospitals and non-stationary growth of medical data, federated learning is incorporated with a multi-agent reinforcement learning controller. To handle the non-IID data and enhance robustness, the federated learning involves a hybrid aggregation strategy incorporating attention mechanisms, trust scoring, and robust filtering. With a shared backbone, it automatically classifies the imaging modalities before disease-specific inference. This framework enables dynamic client selection and includes a Grad-CAM-based explainable AI providing visual justification for model predictions. Evaluation results on datasets confirm improved accuracy, faster convergence, and enhanced robustness of the proposed system.

title: DS-YOLO: Dynamic Snake Convolution and Multi-Scale Attention for Deformable Gas Leak Detection
authors: Feifei Dai, Wei Zhou
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Natural gas pipeline leak detection is critical for industrial safety, yet gas plumes exhibit amorphous, semitransparent, and irregularly shaped visual characteristics that pose significant challenges for automated detection. Existing YOLOv8-based methods employ rigid square convolutional kernels with fixed grid-like receptive fields, struggling to capture the elongated, curved morphology and subtle visual signals of gas leaks. We propose DS-YOLO, which enhances detection of deformable targets by integrating Dynamic Snake Convolution (DSConv) in the backbone and Efficient Multi-scale Attention (EMA) in the neck. DSConv explicitly models the topological geometric features of tubular plumes through adaptive deformable receptive fields, while EMA selectively amplifies leak-related signals and suppresses background noise. Experiments demonstrate that DS-YOLO significantly outperforms the baselines, with ablation studies validating the effectiveness of both modules. Our code will be found at https://github.com/PapersAuthors/DS-YOLO

title: Transformer-Integrated CNN-BiLSTM Approach for Audio Forgery Detection via MFCC Feature Analysis
authors: Ravula Sai Deekshitha, Pavan Kumar. P, Laxman Bodhan, Mankala Satish, R.Rajashekar, Kumbala Pradeep Reddy
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: The deep fake technologies are expanding on a large scale, especially in the fake audio generation domain, this generation of deep fake content poses a significant threat in the data security, privacy, & social trust. it involves the use of machine learning techniques that helps in the creation of the realistic & convincing fake voices and makes the individuals exposed to cyber-attacks by giving information which is not true and threatens them. The proposed work introduces a hybrid deep learning model that integrates two different algorithms they are CNN convolutional neural networks along with BILSTM to detect deep fake audios accurately. CNNs are employed for their mastery in spatial feature extraction, especially when processing (MFCCs) Mel-frequency cepstral coefficients from audio transmission signals. MFCCs capture the time-frequency features of audio, making them ideal for detecting frequent irregularities in patterns of the speech. At the same time, the BiLSTM component, is designed which processes audio sequences using bidirectional flow to capture richer temporal features & detecting subtle inconsistencies commonly present in deep fake audio. This temporal modelling helps detect abrupt transitions or unnatural fluctuations common in deep fake speech. The CNN-BiLSTM hybrid model forms a time-distributed network capable of robust and real-time detection.

title: Improved Super-Twisting for Robotic Manipulators: Toward Reduced Chattering and Enhanced Tracking Performance
authors: Yassine Kali, Maarouf Saad
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: This paper presents an integration of a wellestablished second-order sliding mode with prescribed performance control for robotic manipulators, aiming to enhance control performance while explicitly limiting the level of chattering. In the proposed framework, both the joint-space trajectory tracking error and the switching manifold are systematically transformed to satisfy predefined performance constraints at each stage of the controller design. The transformation applied to the switching manifold functions analogously to a boundary layer method, yet ensures the finite-time stability and robustness characteristics of the original super-twisting approach, as the same reaching law is used. The proposed methods are validated via simulations on a 2-link serial manipulator and benchmarked against the standard super-twisting algorithm to highlight performance enhancements.

title: A Lightweight Algorithm for Small Object Detection Based on Multi-Scale Feature Enhancement
authors: Shengjin Hou, Chuhua Huang, Xiaolong Ma
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Detecting small objects in unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) imagery remains challenging due to limited target resolution, cluttered backgrounds, and large scale variation. To address these issues, we propose SPD-YOLO, a lightweight detector built upon the YOLOv11n architecture. SPD-YOLO integrates three key components: (i) a Spatial–Channel Self-Attention (SCSA) module to enhance feature representation, (ii) a DySample-based dynamic upsampling module to preserve multi-scale details, and (iii) a redesigned detection head tailored for small targets. Experiments on VISDrone2019 demonstrate that SPD-YOLO outperforms existing lightweight detectors, improving mAP@50 from 32.5% to 42.5% and mAP@50–95 from 19.0% to 25.8%, while keeping the model compact at only 2.38M parameters. Additional experiments on the FAPKOR dataset further confirm the robustness of SPD-YOLO under diverse backgrounds and varying object scales. These results indicate that SPD-YOLO achieves a favorable balance between accuracy and efficiency, making it suitable for real-time small-object detection on UAV platforms with limited computational resources.

title: SLP-RT-DETR: Lightweight Detection Method for Small-Scale Infrared UAV
authors: Lelun Xu, Zhao Li, Wei Liu, Jianzhong Wang, Tianji Zhu, Jianjun Yi
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: UAV target detection technology is a critical research direction in the UAV field. Traditional template matching methods based on handcrafted features suffer from low accuracy and poor robustness, while neural network-based detection methods exhibit strong feature representation capabilities but face challenges in balancing detection accuracy and real-time performance. To address these issues, we propose a lightweight improved model based on the RT-DETR framework for small-scale UAV scenarios. The proposed model incorporates depth-wise separable convolution in feature extraction and multi-scale feature fusion modules to reduce model parameters and computational complexity. Additionally, we improve the IoU loss function by introducing angle and position losses to mitigate the target-background imbalance problem. Furthermore, the feature extraction and fusion architecture is optimized to preserve more fine-grained information, enhancing detection accuracy for small-scale targets. Experimental results on a self-constructed infrared UAV dataset demonstrate that compared to the original RT-DETR, our SLP-RT-DETR achieves a 39.31% parameter reduction while improving mAP@0.5:0.95 by 0.7%, effectively advancing both accuracy and real-time performance for small-scale infrared UAV target detection.

title: Research on Wind Power Generation Prediction Based on GA-BP
authors: Kun He, Jinling Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Wind power generation is a vital element of modern social progress. The accurate forecast of wind power generation can assist in the planning of power generation. With the aim of enhance the precision of wind power prediction, this study put forward a new method based on a genetic algorithm-optimized BP neural network. First, we define the topology of the BP neural network, then we use the genetic algorithm to find the optimal initial weights and thresholds. Finally, we train and update the BP neural network to build a GA-BP prediction model. The final experimental result shows that the training and test sets have different degrees of improvement in various evaluation indicators compared with the BP model. The results indicate that the GA-BP architecture is superior to the BP neural network in the prediction of wind power generation.

title: Chromatic Clarity: AI-Enhanced Image Transformation for Color Vision Deficiency
authors: Sakshi Vyavahare, Muskan Ali, Yashraj Nimbalkar, Yashashree Nimbalkar, Anuradha Yenkikar, Nilesh Sable
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: Color Vision Deficiency (CVD), often referred to as color blindness, impacts a considerable segment of the population and poses difficulties in various domains, including education, design, and digital media. This paper introduces an AI-driven system designed to enhance visual accessibility by simulating and correcting images for individuals affected by protanopia, deuteranopia, and tritanopia. Initially, Red Blue Green (RGB) images are transformed into the LMS color space to replicate the color perception of those with CVD. Subsequently, Daltonization techniques are applied to enhance color contrast in the simulated images. A Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) featuring residual blocks is utilized to automate the correction process, ensuring that image details are retained while improving color differentiation. The resulting output comprises modified images customized for each type of CVD, providing enhanced visual clarity and color distinction. This system is deployed as a real-time web application, demonstrating the practical application of deep learning and image processing in improving accessibility for individuals with color vision deficiencies.

title: Bridging Forecasting and Optimization: A Decision-Focused Learning Framework for Power
authors: Jinhao Guo, Haolin Sui
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: This paper presents a decision-focused learning (DFL) framework designed to enhance power system decision-making under uncertainty by tightly integrating forecasting and optimization. Unlike traditional statistical-based learning approaches that minimize prediction error independently from downstream decisions, our method embeds decision loss directly into the training of the forecasting model. We investigate both indirect and direct DFL strategies, where the indirect approach learns predictions that are later used in optimization, while the direct approach produces decisions directly through end-to-end differentiable learning. The proposed framework is validated on the IEEE 118-node system, a realistic power grid scenario featuring high renewable penetration. Experimental results demonstrate that the direct DFL model achieves a decision cost of $240,349 and online decision time of just 3 minutes and 10 seconds–significantly outperforming benchmark methods in both economic efficiency and real-time responsiveness. The findings highlight that integrating optimization objectives into learning not only improves decision quality but also enhances robustness under uncertainty, offering a promising direction for intelligent power scheduling systems in dynamic environments.

title: Comparing In-Switch P4-Based and Controller-Based Network Control Load Balancing
authors: Marco Ramos, Rui Silva, Daniel Corujo, Rui L. Aguiar, Ayman Radwan
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: This paper targets the need for more efficient networking solutions to support the Internet's growth, by proposing a decentralized SDN (Software-Defined Networking) management framework that uses real-time traffic monitoring and P4-programmable switches to optimize resource allocation. By embedding part of the control logic in the data plane, the system improves flexibility, reduces latency, and lowers communication overhead. A stateful monitoring function modeled as an Extended finite-state machine (EFSM) enables switches to track local traffic autonomously. The approach is validated through simulations with BMv2 switches in Mininet, analyzing performance under varying traffic conditions.

title: ReasonTele: Enhancing LLM Task Planning in Telecommunication Network Operations and Maintenance via CoT-Driven Data Synthesis
authors: Jun Liu, Wei Wu, Xiaofeng Zhang, Zhuo Chen, Hong Li, Xin Chen, Xiaozhao Xuan, Zuozhong Zhang, Bingzhan Zhang, Kuang Xu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: Automating task planning via Large Language Models (LLMs) has emerged as a critical approach for managing the escalating complexity of telecommunication network operations and maintenance. However, the practical deployment of LLMs in this domain is significantly hindered by the scarcity of high-quality specialized data and the absence of standardized evaluation benchmarks. To address these limitations, we propose ReasonTele, a closed-loop framework designed to surmount domain capability bottlenecks via Chain-of-Thought (CoT) driven data synthesis. Specifically, the framework iteratively synthesizes high-fidelity data from sparse expert seeds by incorporating a domain-knowledge-driven four-step planning process and a verify-correct feedback mechanism. Subsequently, we perform domain-adaptive fine-tuning using explicit CoT data to guide the LLM in internalizing expert reasoning logic, thereby substantially enhancing its task planning proficiency. Furthermore, to precisely quantify LLM performance, we establish a comprehensive fivedimensional evaluation protocol, ranging from basic specification compliance to deep logical consistency. Experimental results demonstrate that our ReasonTele LLM significantly outperform state-of-the-art baselines, such as GPT-4o, in comprehensive planning metrics, validating the effectiveness of our proposed method.

title: On-ground tests of LASIN free space optical communication system dedicated to CO3D constellation
authors: Nicolas De Guembecker, Alvaro Jose Pascual Gracia, Thomas Anfray, Jérémie Lochard, Alexandre Jullien, Olivier Cambon, Mehdi Ghezal, Lyonel Barthe, Laurent Coret, Jean-Frédéric Chouteau, Philippe Cheoux-Damas, Lionel Perret, Géraldine Artaud
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: This paper presents an update on LASIN (LASer through the INstrument) free space optical communication system that is embarked on one satellite of the CO3D Earth observation constellation manufactured by Airbus [1].

title: Switch Trust Evaluation Model via Multi Scale Temporal Sliding Windows and Hybrid Resampling
authors: Hailong Luo, Hongyong Jia, Wei Zhao
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Trust evaluation of switches is vital for network security, yet existing models often fail to distinguish stealthy port scanning from normal traffic due to feature overlap and jitter. To address this, we propose a novel model incorporating a multi scale temporal sliding window (MST-SW) mechanism to suppress transient noise via historical context. Furthermore, we employ a SMOTE-Tomek hybrid resampling strategy to correct data imbalance and optimize classification boundaries. By utilizing a cost sensitive XGBoost algorithm with class weight penalties, the model significantly improves detection recall for low trust levels, achieving state-of-the-art performance in experiments. The code is available at: https://anonymous.4open.science/r/TrustModel.

title: Integration of a Polarization based QKD Receiver Module to an Optical Ground Station
authors: R. Singh, T. Roger, C. Perumangatt, R. I. Woodward, O. Crampton, C. Simmons, A. Green, R. Donaldson, R. S. Tessinari, R. M. Stevenson, A. J. Shields
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: Satellite-to-ground quantum key distribution (QKD) has the potential to extend the reach of quantum secured communication to global scale. Taking the first step towards QKD using the UK quantum communication hub’s optical ground station (HOGS), this work presents results from a free-space QKD experiment conducted using the HOGS facility. A compact receiver module was integrated to HOGS and polarization encoded quantum bits, sent from a 2U QKD source, were detected, generating secret keys with total loss of up to 38 dB.

title: Research on Path Planning of Mountain Obstacle Avoidance Robots Based on an Improved Ant Colony Algorithm
authors: Zhou Jingyi
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of the traditional ant colony algorithm in large-scale complex mountain environments, such as low global search efficiency, slow convergence speed, excessive path turns, insufficient smoothness, and difficulty in meeting the obstacle avoidance requirements of mountain robots. The algorithm accelerates convergence by dynamically updating pheromones on paths at different levels, introduces a distance function (incorporating mountain slope factors) and a direction function as heuristic factors to enhance obstacle avoidance capability, adopts an improved adaptive pseudo-random transfer strategy to avoid local optima, and optimizes path smoothness while reducing turns using cubic uniform B-spline curves. Experiments in two scales of mountain simulation environments show that compared with the traditional algorithm, the proposed algorithm reduces path turns by 55.6% and 59.4% respectively, and increases convergence speed by 87.5% and 100% respectively, verifying its superiority. Path planning experiments for indoor AGVs using the QBot2e platform further confirm the algorithm's engineering practical value.

title: End Winding Eddy Current Loss Calculation of Large Synchronous Generators Considering Circulating Currents
authors: Moein Farhadian, Fazel Pourmirzaei Deylami, Hamed Tahanian
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: Eddy current loss accounts for a substantial portion of stator winding loss in large synchronous generators (SGs). Although accurate estimation of Eddy current loss in active part of winding is straightforward, in the end region, Eddy current loss calculation faces more complications. This paper deals with winding end part Eddy current loss calculation taking into account circulating currents. The circulating currents are injected into different strands in 3D finite element (FE) model by applying proper boundary conditions. The results of end winding loss calculation considering circulating current shows notable differences in loss distribution compared to equal current injection to winding strands.

title: Research on an Intelligent Packaging Design System Integrating Image Recognition and User Emotional Analysis
authors: Yimei Lv, Xianjun Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 4th International Conference of Safe Production and Informatization (IICSPI)
abstract: This paper addresses the issue that traditional food packaging designs are unable to precisely match users' emotional needs. It has researched and constructed an intelligent food packaging design system that integrates image recognition and user emotion analysis. This system uses an improved convolutional neural network (CNN) model to automatically recognize the visual elements (colors, patterns, and text) of food packaging. The recognition accuracy of the test set reaches 92%. A multimodal fusion algorithm (combining facial expressions, text evaluations, and physiological signals) is adopted to build a user emotion analysis model, and the emotion prediction accuracy is increased to 85%. Based on the mapping relationship of “visual elements - emotional needs - food type”, personalized packaging schemes are generated through the intelligent decision-making module. Experimental verification shows that compared with traditional design schemes, the packaging schemes generated by this system have significant improvements in user emotional resonance (increased by 37%) and purchase intention scores (increased by 29%), providing technical support and practical references for the intelligent and emotional design of food packaging.

title: Transfer-based DRL for Network Slicing Resource Allocation in Radio Access Network
authors: Yuanzheng Ou, Jianbin Zheng, Tinghong Rong
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: Network slicing technology partitions physical resources into multiple virtual end-to-end slices to meet the diverse and heterogeneous service requirements of users. Therefore, achieving fair and efficient dynamic resource allocation has become the key to optimizing network performance. Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) has been demonstrated as a feasible solution for excavating the dynamic characteristics of network slices to enable dynamic resource allocation. However, the complexity of the network environment and the vast amount of data lead to low learning efficiency of the agent. Furthermore, in multi-slice deployment scenarios, applying DRL techniques easily induces the curse of dimensionality problem within the large-scale action space. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a Transfer Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (Transfer-DDPG) algorithm. It accelerates the learning speed of the agent by introducing prior knowledge of optimal action selection and achieves efficient handling of the large-scale action space in multislice scenarios by reconstructing the network architecture and the policy learning process. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm can efficiently achieve resource allocation in multi-slice environments and significantly improves the learning efficiency of the agent.

title: Kaiser–Nuttall Hybrid Window-Based All-Phase FFT Harmonic Detection
authors: Wenyuan Zhao, Zhengang Wan
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Conventional FFT-based harmonic detection suffers from severe spectral leakage, pronounced picket-fence effect, and limited accuracy in estimating amplitude, frequency, and phase of higher-order harmonics. This paper proposes an all-phase fast Fourier transform (apFFT) harmonic detection method using a Kaiser–Nuttall hybrid convolution window. The hybrid window, formed by convolving the Nuttall window with the Kaiser window, preserves ultra-low sidelobe characteristics while introducing tunable parameter control. An all-phase convolution window is achieved by convolving the forward window with its time-reversed counterpart, enabling zero-phase spectral analysis. A four-spectral-line interpolation model is then employed for joint estimation of harmonic frequency, amplitude, and phase. Theoretical analysis and simulations show that the proposed method effectively mitigates spectral leakage and picket-fence effects, substantially reducing relative errors in amplitude, frequency, and phase for multi-harmonic and high-order cases, demonstrating strong engineering applicability.

title: Alzheimer’s Disease EEG Identification Based on Hjorth Parameters and Machine Learning
authors: Jitong Wang, Ruofan Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)
abstract: This study focuses on Alzheimer’s disease and compares the classification performance of multiple machinelearning models on electroencephalographic (EEG) signals. CatBoost achieved an accuracy of 96.88%, surpassing many traditional learners and demonstrating its superiority for EEG data. By comparing leave-one-subject-out (LOSO) and K-fold cross-validation, we reveal that K-fold suffers from substantial label-leakage at both subject and sample levels, biasing the model toward learning individual rather than disease-specific features. After deliberately perturbing the labels, LOSO accuracy drops markedly, providing a more realistic estimate of generalization. Although computationally expensive, LOSO is essential for ensuring reliability and generalizability in medical-EEG classification.

title: Research on Fault Response Optimization and Adaptive Control Strategy of Power Metering System Based on Reinforcement Learning
authors: He Cui, Leiyang Zhao, Rundan Zhang, Xuan Kong, Liying Liao
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: With the rapid development of smart grids, the role of electricity metering systems in power monitoring, billing, and operation and maintenance has become increasingly prominent. During operation, the system often faces various issues such as communication interruptions, hardware failures, and data anomalies. Traditional fault response mechanisms have obvious shortcomings in terms of accuracy, timeliness, and intelligence. This paper proposes an optimization and adaptive control strategy for fault response in electricity metering systems based on reinforcement learning, aiming to enhance the system's fault identification capabilities and response efficiency in complex environments. The paper first analyzes the typical fault characteristics and response mechanisms of electricity metering systems and constructs a reinforcement learning model suitable for this scenario. Subsequently, a response optimization strategy integrating deep reinforcement learning algorithms is designed and implemented, and an adaptive control mechanism is further integrated to achieve dynamic adjustment and online learning of system strategies. Through simulation experiments and comparative tests, the advantages of the proposed method in terms of fault response time, control stability, and strategy robustness are verified. The research results provide an effective new path and theoretical support for the intelligent operation and maintenance of electricity metering systems in smart grids.

title: Research on Design and Performance Optimization of Drive System of Infinitely Variable Speed Motor Based on Fuzzy Control
authors: Huimeng Guo, Wei Zhao, Lina Sun
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: Traditional stepless speed regulation technology mostly adopts PID control algorithm, but it has some defects such as large overshoot and long adjustment time under complex working conditions. As a control method based on fuzzy logic, fuzzy control can deal with uncertain and nonlinear problems and is suitable for systems that are difficult to establish accurate mathematical models. In this study, an infinitely variable speed drive scheme based on dynamic adaptive fuzzy control (DAFC) is proposed, and a parameter adaptive fuzzy controller combining on-line monitoring of motor state and improved particle swarm optimization (PSO) is constructed to realize dynamic optimization of fuzzy rule base. At the same time, a multi-objective collaborative optimization model integrating response speed, steady-state error and system efficiency is established, and Pareto optimal solution tracking is realized by dynamically adjusting the weighting coefficient. The experimental results show that the performance of DAFC is significantly better than that of traditional PID and conventional fuzzy control under the conditions of speed step response test and sudden load, and its rise time, overshoot and adjustment time are greatly improved, and it shows good comprehensive performance under different working conditions, which meets the real-time requirements of the system and provides a new idea for the intelligent development of motor drive system.

title: Designing an Adapted High Gain L-Band Ultra Wide-Band Tapered Slot Vivaldi Antenna
authors: Aunjan Sushil, Anik Dev, Susmita Sen
publish: 2026 Symposium on Microwave, Antenna, and Propagation (SMAP)
abstract: The growing demand for robust wireless systems in long-range communication, synthetic aperture radar (SAR), and spectrum-monitoring operations necessitates antenna designs that offer wide instantaneous bandwidth with enhanced beam focusing property and adaptive capabilities, particularly in the challenging L-band (1-2 GHz). The Tapered Slot Vivaldi Antenna (TSVA) emerges as a promising solution due to its end-fire radiation and inherent wideband capability. This work demonstrates the optimized topology, analysis, and experimental corroboration of an innovative, compact, and adaptive high-gain ultra-wideband (UWB) Vivaldi antenna optimized for L-band operation. The core antenna employs an exponentially tapered profile and FR-4 is selected as the substrate material because of its affordability and dielectric constant of 4.3. To alleviate the inherent limitations of low-frequency operation, a compact design acquired through the implementation of an optimized, slot-feed mechanism, which ensures smooth current transition and consistent impedance matching from 1.3 GHz to 1.85 GHz. Moreover, the incorporation of precisely configured exponential slots along the radiating edges effectively suppresses diffraction phenomena, reduces cross-polarization levels, and improves gain consistency across the entire operating frequency range. The designed antenna exhibits a remarkable voltage standing wave ratio (VSWR) ranging from 1.3 to 1.85 GHz, signifying superior impedance matching characteristics. In addition, the obtained return loss, denoted by an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S_{11}$</tex> value of −43 dB at resonance, substantiates exceptional power coupling efficiency and an intricately optimized configuration. The antenna maintains a consistent gain between 6.1 dBi and 8.2 dBi throughout the operational bandwidth, with compact overall dimensions of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{215} \mathbf{ mm} \boldsymbol{\times} \mathbf{165 mm}$</tex>. The amalgamation of high gain, superior impedance matching, and excellent radiation efficiency establishes the proposed antenna as a robust and high-performance solution for satellite communication, GPS, radio astronomy and other long-range wireless systems operating within the L-band spectrum.

title: Automated Testing Framework for Retrieval-Augmented Generation Systems: Evaluation Metrics and Optimization Strategies
authors: Jun Zhang, Rui He, Xiaodong Zheng, Yang Lin, Shixiang Lu, Yeheng Qu
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: The rapid development of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems has addressed critical limitations in large language models (LLMs), including factual hallucinations, knowledge obsolescence, and domain-specific incompetence.
