However, the complexity of RAG architectures introduces new challenges in performance evaluation. This paper proposes a comprehensive automated testing framework that systematically assesses both retrieval and generation components through quantifiable metrics. Our experimental results demonstrate that the weighted integration of context recall (CR) and answer correctness (AC) yields 17.1% higher discriminative power than single metrics, while semantic-aware document chunking improves answer relevance by 13.0%. This study not only provides actionable strategies for fine-tuning RAG systems but also codifies standardized benchmark protocols tailored to their industrial deployment.

title: Real-Time Wave Energy Forecasting with Physics-Informed Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks
authors: Ho Jun Ang, Hayati Binti Pg Hj Mohd Yassin, Daphne Teck Ching Lai
publish: 2026 International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Intelligent Control and Artificial Intelligence (EEICAI)
abstract: Wave forecasting is essential for laboratory coastal studies and wave-energy research. This paper presents a Physics-Informed Kolmogorov–Arnold Network implemented on a Rasp-berry Pi for real-time wave height prediction in an IoT-enabled laboratory flume. The system integrates an ESP32 data acquisition unit, ultrasonic and optical sensing, and WiFi communication for continuous transmission. A 2.0 m by 0.5 m by 0.4 m acrylic and steel flume was designed and validated using finite-element analysis. Using buoy datasets for model development, the proposed method achieved RMSE equal to 0.146, MAE equal to 0.125, and a Physics-Loss value of 0.9189, outperforming baseline models such as LSTM and MLP. The compact, low-power architecture supports embedded forecasting aligned with Brunei’s BNCCP 2035 and ASEAN’s Renewable Energy Roadmap.

title: Structured KV Cache Selection with RoPE Correction for Efficient Multi-Query Large Table Question Answering
authors: Xinlu Wang, Zhuo Zhang, Qianyi Cheng
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: The application of Large Language Models to complex reasoning tasks over large-scale, structured data, such as enterprise tables, is becoming increasingly prevalent. However, this application is critically hindered by the prohibitive VRAM consumption associated with the intermediate activation (KV cache), as well as high inference latency. In such scenarios, where a large table is subjected to multiple reasoning queries, the substantial computational cost of the prefill stage is redundantly incurred for each new query. Furthermore, the KV cache required to store the full table context creates a severe memory bandwidth bottleneck during the decoding phase.To address these limitations, we introduce StructKV, a novel framework that effectively decouples the static table context from the dynamic query. StructKV operates by pre-computing the KV cache for the entire table at a structured, cell-level granularity, which is then offloaded to CPU memory. At inference time, StructKV selectively loads only the KV cache corresponding to the query-relevant sub-table into VRAM. Moreover, the framework applies a corrective adjustment to the rotary position encoding for the selected sub-table based on its original position information, thereby enhancing inference accuracy. Experiments show Struc-tKV achieves over 6.1x KV cache compression, delivers up to 2.7x speedup in per-token latency, and improves reasoning accuracy by 2 percentage points.

title: The Neuro-Linguistic Frontier: A Review of Brain-to-Text Technologies and Their Application in Restoring Communication Aids
authors: S Geetha, M Thrisha, Varsha Gowda, K Varsha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: Brain-to-text communication is a groundbreaking use of Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCIs). It opens new doors for improving human communication, particularly for those with speech and motor difficulties. In the last ten years, we have seen major progress in turning neural activity into text and speech using electroencephalography (EEG), electrocorticography (ECoG), and deep learning models. This paper offers a thorough review of current research in brain-to-text. It looks at contributions ranging from traditional signal processing methods to modern neural decoding techniques that use encoder-decoder models and large datasets. The review divides past studies into categories: brainwave acquisition and processing, EEG-based text translation, imagined speech recognition, speech synthesis from neural activity, and clinical neuroprosthetic applications. A comparative analysis points out the advantages of invasive and non-invasive methods, the increasing importance of deep learning, and the difficulties in reaching high accuracy, real-time performance, and the ability to generalize across different subjects. Existing research gaps include the limited number of open datasets, issues related to privacy and security, and the need for light, wearable solutions suitable for real-world use. By highlighting these trends and challenges, this survey lays the groundwork for future studies and positions our project as a step toward creating efficient, real-time brain-to-text communication systems.

title: O-RAN RIC-Enabled Dynamic Resource Allocation with Reinforcement Learning for Green Communication
authors: Mostafa Mouawad, Ahmed El-Ashmawy, Ahmed Abdelmoaty
publish: 2025 IEEE Middle East Conference on Communications and Networking (MECOM)
abstract: Open Radio Access Networks (O-RAN) have revolutionized the telecommunications landscape by disaggregating traditional RAN architectures and introducing programmable intelligence through the RAN Intelligent Controller (RIC). As mobile network operators worldwide embrace O-RAN to reduce vendor lock-in and enable innovative services, energy efficiency has emerged as a critical challenge, with RAN infrastructure accounting for over 70 % of network energy consumption. The integration of machine learning algorithms through NearReal Time RIC applications (xApps) presents unprecedented opportunities to optimize network operations dynamically. This paper addresses the joint resource provisioning optimization problem in O-RAN by formulating it as a Markov Decision Process (MDP) within the RIC framework. We propose a novel dynamic switching strategy for Radio Units (RUs) that intelligently balances traffic load distribution while maintaining power efficiency through reinforcement learning-based xApp deployment. Our system model considers user-RU association, traffic utilization, and power consumption to maximize network performance via Near-Real-Time RIC decision-making. The approach uses continuous-time Markov chains to model system states and employs both value iteration and policy iteration algorithms to derive optimal switching policies implementable as practical xApps. Comprehensive simulation results demonstrate that our proposed method achieves superior rate utilization while maintaining energy efficiency through intelligent RU sleep state management, providing a practical framework for sustainable O-RAN deployments

title: GenNav - AI Enhanced Autonomous Vehicle Safety Using Generative Models
authors: P. Venkadesh, S.V. Divya, K. Kasthuri, R.M. Madhushree, M. Aishwarya
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: The GenNav is a novel approach that combines predictive modelling with generative models to enhance the safety of self-driving automobiles. GenNav applies modern machine learning methods to realistically recreate challenging driving situations, such as sudden obstacles and unpredictable pedestrian behaviour-issues that earlier systems often failed to manage. Many existing autonomous vehicle safety models rely on fixed algorithms and limited datasets, making it difficult for them to respond effectively to unusual or rapidly changing road conditions. This weakness increases the risk of safety failures and reduces public confidence in autonomous technology.A major limitation in current systems is their inability to simulate rare or unexpected events, which leads to poor decision-making in critical moments. GenNav addresses this problem by combining generative models with predictive simulations to create a more flexible and responsive navigation framework. By using continuous real-time sensor data, it builds dynamic driving scenarios that help vehicles operate more safely in complex environments [1]. This approach aims to reduce accidents and strengthen public trust in autonomous transportation [2].The system blends sensor inputs with crowdsourced information to improve decision-making and uses Explainable AI to provide clear reasoning for its actions. Through extensive real-world and virtual testing, GenNav demonstrates strong reliability and aligns with regulatory standards, supporting a safer and smarter autonomous ecosystem.

title: Clustering Student Profiles with K-Means and HAC for Future Adaptive Virtual Learning Agents
authors: Ghalia Mdaghri-Alaoui, Ilhame Khabbachi, Abdelhamid Zouhair, El Mokhtar En-Naimi
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: This study leverages the OULAD (Open University Learning Analytics Dataset) to develop a cluster-based approach aimed at supporting individualized learning experiences. The analysis incorporates three dimensions of learner data: educational involvement, demographic characteristics, and behavioral patterns. K-Means and Hierarchical Agglomerative Clustering (HAC) techniques were applied independently to each dimension to generate meaningful and interpretable learner profiles. These profiles subsequently served as target variables for training various supervised classification algorithms, including Support Vector Machines, Random Forests, and Neural Networks. Model effectiveness was assessed through metrics such as accuracy, F1-score, and cross-validation procedures. The findings demonstrate that combining supervised and unsupervised learning yields accurate and useful student classification. The derived profiles form the basis for a future multi-agent virtual reality learning environment, in which specialized agents can adapt behavior, personalize learning paths, and coordinate pedagogical support to deliver a tailored immersive learning experience.

title: AI-Driven Behavior Prediction in Autonomous Vehicles: A Short Review
authors: Khaoula El Akrout, Abdellatif Ezzouhairi
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Embedded Systems and Artificial Intelligence (ESAI)
abstract: This paper presents a systematic literature review of artificial intelligence approaches for autonomous vehicle behavior prediction from 2016 to 2025, addressing a critical gap in synthesizing emerging neuro-symbolic methods compared to existing surveys. The review distinguishes itself through its comprehensive analysis of regional adaptation challenges and focus on explainability within intelligent transportation systems-an underexplored intersection in information systems research. A structured methodology categorizes approaches into three paradigms: deep learning for perception and trajectory forecasting, reinforcement learning for dynamic decision-making, and hybrid neuro-symbolic methods combining learning with explicit reasoning. Analysis reveals that while deep learning achieves state-of-the-art accuracy in trajectory prediction, it suffers from interpretability issues, and reinforcement learning excels in real-time adaptation but faces safety validation challenges. Hybrid approaches emerge as the most promising direction, balancing performance with explainability and regulatory compliance. The review further examines how cultural factors, regional regulations, and infrastructure variations necessitate adaptive AI systems for global deployment. Concrete conclusions highlight neuro-symbolic integration as essential for achieving trustworthy autonomous systems that can operate effectively across diverse international contexts.

title: DEDUCT: An Efficient Model for Secure and Redundant-Free Cloud Text Storage
authors: K Harsha Vardhan Reddy, K Kiran, K Rajesh, K Narendra, S. Dilli Babu, Sunil Kumar Malchi
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: This paper proposes a secure, efficient, and scalable textual data deduplication framework designed for cloud storage environments. The framework integrates probabilistic similarity detection techniques with cloud-oriented data management to eliminate both exact and near-duplicate textual documents at scale. Incoming text data is first preprocessed and tokenized, after which compact similarity signatures are generated using a MinHash-based approach. Locality-sensitive hashing is employed to group similar documents efficiently, significantly reducing the computational overhead associated with pairwise comparisons. To further enhance lookup efficiency, Bloom filter–based indexing is utilized, enabling fast membership checks with minimal memory consumption. The proposed framework is deployed in a cloud-simulated environment that closely models real-world data ingestion, indexing, and storage workflows. Experimental evaluation conducted on large and diverse textual datasets with varying duplication levels demonstrates high deduplication effectiveness, substantial storage reduction, low processing latency, and stable performance as data volume increases. Compared to traditional exact-matching techniques, the proposed approach achieves improved accuracy and scalability while maintaining operational efficiency. The results confirm that combining lightweight probabilistic data structures with similarity estimation provides a practical and secure solution for large-scale textual data deduplication in modern cloud storage systems.

title: Multi-Filter Deep Learning Framework for Partial Discharge Localization in Generator Stators
authors: Mehdi Nourollahi, Mohammad Hamed Samimi, Arash Abyaz, Ahmad Kalhor
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: Partial discharges (PDs) are early indicators of insulation degradation in generator stator windings, and their timely localization is essential to ensure reliable operation and reduce costly outages. This paper presents a deep learning-based framework for PD localization, leveraging a high-fidelity finite element (FEM) simulation model of a 12-bar stator. A multi-filter acquisition strategy is employed to capture complementary spectral components of PD signals prior to feature learning. Five neural network architectures are investigated: multi-layer perceptron (MLP), convolutional neural network (CNN), temporal convolutional network (TCN), CNN combined with long short-term memory (CNN+LSTM), and Transformer. Results demonstrate that the Transformer model achieves the highest classification accuracy (87.1%) for PD localization, benefiting from its self-attention mechanism to capture long-range dependencies and reduce misclassification. The CNN+LSTM hybrid model also provides strong performance, while simpler models such as MLP and CNN show limited capability in handling nonlinear, overlapping PD features. The findings confirm that attention-based architectures, combined with multi-filter strategies, offer a powerful approach for practical condition monitoring of high-voltage rotating machines, paving the way for reliable AI-assisted maintenance in industrial applications.

title: Performance Prediction for Ultra-High Voltage Insulating Pull Rods Using an Improved WOA-BP Neural Network
authors: Qiu Zhonghua, Wu Binfan, Wu Tian, Peng Yong, Zhu Xiang, Meng Yue, Zhang Yuqiu, Wang Lingzhi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Smart Grid and Energy (ICSGE)
abstract: The rapid development of ultra-high voltage (UHV) transmission technology has imposed increasingly strict requirements on insulation carrying tools. However, the traditional performance evaluation through finite element simulation is hindered by long computation time and low efficiency, and thus cannot meet the modern design needs. To address this issue, we propose a high-performance prediction method using a neural network optimized by an improved whale optimization algorithm (IWOA). The IWOA first utilizes its global search ability to optimize the initial weights, thresholds, and architecture of the backpropagation (BP) neural network, thereby alleviating the tendency of traditional BP to easily get stuck in local optima and converge slowly. The standard WOA is further enhanced by introducing Gaussian chaotic mapping and adaptive update strategies to improve convergence accuracy and stability. The resulting hybrid model uses structural parameters as input and directly predicts key performance indicators, such as maximum stress, deformation, and electric field strength. Experiments show that compared with the traditional BP model and the WOA-BP prediction model, the IWOA-BP model has significant advantages, with a determination coefficient (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) exceeding 0.992, and has higher prediction accuracy. This model provides an efficient and reliable tool for the rapid performance evaluation and optimization of UHV insulation tools, has important theoretical value, and has broad application prospects in improving design efficiency and ensuring grid stability.

title: Fault Detection and Classification in Induction Motors :An Explainable Convolutional Neural Networks Approach
authors: Ali Vahidi, Amirata Taghizadeh, Mohammadreza Toulabi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: Effective fault diagnosis in induction motors is crucial for maintaining operational safety and efficiency in industrial settings. While deep learning models, particularly Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), have shown great promise, their inherent black box nature often hinders their adoption due to a lack of transparency and trust. This paper addresses this challenge by presenting an end-to-end, explainable diagnostic framework that leverages thermal imaging for non-invasive fault classification. We develop a tailored CNN architecture that automatically learns discriminative features from thermal images to distinguish between various motor faults. To make the model's reasoning transparent, we integrate Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) through the Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) technique, which generates visual heatmaps highlighting the exact image regions influencing the model's predictions. Simulation results demonstrate the framework's high effectiveness, achieving 97.3% accuracy across 11 operational conditions. Critically, the XAI visualizations confirm that the model's decisions are based on physically relevant thermal signatures, successfully identifying both concentrated hotspots and more subtle, distributed fault patterns. This combined approach provides a solution that is not only accurate but also trustworthy for industrial predictive maintenance.

title: Performance Optimization Simulation of Vacuum Dehumidifier Based on Fluid Dynamics and Heat Transfer Model
authors: Yonggang Liu, Ziyu Liu
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: Vacuum moisture regain machine (VCM) is a key equipment in tobacco industry processing. However, in actual operation, the cooling water circulation tank of VCM often suffers from the problem of "water shortage", resulting in insufficient cooling water entering the condenser, which in turn reduces the efficiency of the equipment and has a negative impact on product quality. Meanwhile, traditional methods are difficult to effectively identify unqualified stem tobacco among tobacco with similar shapes and colors. To address these issues, this paper proposes a performance optimization scheme based on fluid dynamics (FD) and heat transfer model (HTM). This scheme combines FD and heat transfer theory to simulate steam flow characteristics and heat exchange processes, and deeply analyzes the effects of condenser heat transfer efficiency and cooling water temperature on VCM performance. At the same time, we innovatively introduced deep learning (DL) technology to construct a specialized unqualified tobacco detection model, which can effectively distinguish different types of tobacco and accurately identify leaf stem tobacco among them. The results show that the method proposed in this article can effectively improve the efficiency of VCM work and enhance the accuracy of identifying unqualified tobacco.

title: Comparative Accuracy of IEC Ratio Approaches and Duval Triangle/Pentagon in Transformer Fault Diagnosis from DGA Data
authors: Fateme Aliyari, Mohammad Hamed Samimi, Majid Sanaye-Pasand
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: Accurate interpretation of Dissolved Gas Analysis (DGA) results is still challenging on heterogeneous datasets. Therefore, the robustness of standard diagnostic methods across diverse data is still ambiguous. This paper performs a comparative evaluation of four widely used DGA interpretation techniques—IEC 60599 Ratio Method, Duval Triangle, Duval Pentagon, and the 90th Percentile Gas Population—using a comprehensive General Dataset consisting of 2,538 transformer oil samples. The study analyzes the diagnostic performance of these methods in terms of accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. Also, confusion matrices are used to assess class separability, overlapping regions, and frequently misclassified fault types. Results show that the Duval Triangle method achieves the best overall fault classification accuracy and F1-score, providing balanced performance across each fault type. Although the Duval Pentagon is a more recent and structurally advanced model, its performance was weaker on diverse data, indicating limited generalization. In contrast, the IEC 60599 Ratio Method, despite its rigid boundaries and limited detection range, provides higher certainty and lower ambiguity.

title: Advanced Deep Learning Techniques for Lung Cancer Early Prediction
authors: Leema Raina F, C. Anbarasi
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Computer tomography (CT) images of the lungs were vital in the early diagnosis of lung cancer, thus lowering the mortality rate and patient outcomes. As more and more medical imaging data was made available, machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) strategies have become useful in creating computer-aided diagnosis (CAD) systems to predict lung cancer. The paper aimed to provide a synthesized literature review of the most recent and representative research using traditional ML, U-Net-based segmentation structures, transfer learning, and more sophisticated learning structures, including multimodal and federated learning. The reviewed literature suggested that traditional ML methods based on radiomic features that were manually determined had an average classification performance but that the DL models greatly improved the performance of detecting and diagnosing tissue by automatically learning features. Specifically, 3D CNNs and attention-enhanced encoder-decoder networks performed better because they were capable of capturing volumetric and contextual data of CT scans. The transfer learning methods also enhanced the model robustness with limited training data and multimodal PET/CT models and federated learning models have resolved the issue of diagnostic reliability and patient data privacy. All in all, the integrated results demonstrated that deep learning-based CAD systems were consistently better than conventional methods and offered the way forward in the accurate and early-stage prediction of lung cancer and the reliable support of clinical decisions.

title: Research on Fault Prediction and Intelligent Control Strategy of Hydraulic Equipment Based on Edge Computing
authors: Jing Wang, Kun Wang, Jinyi Li, Lin Zhou
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: Edge computing (EC) provides a new way to solve this problem with its advantages of low latency and high efficiency. The hydraulic fault prediction framework constructed in this paper adopts a three-layer architecture of sensor layer, EC layer and cloud collaboration layer. Sensor layer collects high-frequency fault feature data, EC layer is responsible for real-time feature extraction and lightweight model reasoning, and cloud collaboration layer realizes continuous model iteration and performance improvement. Intelligent control strategy realizes fault-tolerant control in fault state by dynamically adjusting control parameters. The experiment was carried out on the Bosch Rexroth hydraulic experimental platform. The results show that the edge lightweight LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) model is superior to the cloud LSTM model and the traditional SVM method in fault prediction accuracy, reasoning delay and energy consumption, and the adaptive fuzzy PID control strategy is significantly superior to the traditional PID control in pressure fluctuation control, recovery time and energy consumption. In addition, this scheme also shows significant advantages in improving equipment reliability and reducing operating costs, which provides strong support for intelligent maintenance and control of hydraulic equipment.

title: Smart Agriculture: Crop Prediction, Fertilizer Recommendation and Water Requirement using Machine Learning
authors: Ch.Rajendra Prasad, Yalabaka Srikanth, Ramu Moola, Srinivas Samala, P. Ramchandar Rao, Thottempudi Pardhu
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Smart agriculture has an important role to play to help enhance the productivity of crops and make the most of agricultural assets using machine learning techniques. This paper is designed to present an integrated system of smart agriculture that focuses on three key components of the system namely: prediction of crops, recommending fertilizers, and estimating water requirements of crops. The proposed system takes historical data on crop harvests, soil nutrient content and climatic situation and uses it as an analysis tool to forecast the best crops to be grown in a certain area. On the basis of soil nutrient deficiencies and based on crop-specific rate, suitable fertilizer types and quantities are recommended for the balanced management of nutrients. In addition, the system estimates water requirements of the crop in liters per hectare taking into account environmental factors such as temperature, humidity, soil moisture, crop type and weather conditions. Multiple machine learning algorithms, such as Decision Tree, Random Forest, Naive Bayes and K-Nearest Neighbors are implemented and tested for performance comparison Experimental results show that Random Forest algorithm has better accuracy and stability in all the modules. The proposed approach is in favor of precision agriculture, thanks to decision making, better use of resources and sustainable practices in farming especially during climate changes and/or resource scarcity.

title: Timeliness Optimization Model of Distribution Line Fault Repair Based on Data Drive
authors: Qun Yu, Junwen Long, Kai Yang, Wenju Cheng, Ying Liu
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: As the "last mile" of power system, the stability of distribution network is very important to social production and residents' life. However, the traditional fault repair mode of distribution network can not meet the needs of modern power system because of inaccurate fault location, inefficient resource scheduling and delayed response time. In this paper, a data-driven timeliness optimization model of distribution line fault repair is proposed. By building a distribution network data center and integrating multi-source data, the fault features can be accurately extracted and dynamically updated. The LSTM network with improved spatio-temporal attention mechanism is used to predict the failure probability, and the multi-source data fusion method based on Bayesian estimation is used to locate the failure point, and the dynamic resource scheduling and path planning are realized through the reinforcement learning model based on markov decision processes. The experimental results show that the model is superior to the traditional method and static scheduling model in the key indexes such as average fault repair time, positioning error and resource utilization rate, which significantly improves the emergency repair efficiency. Under extreme weather conditions such as typhoon, the model can still maintain high accuracy and reliability of fault prediction, effectively identify most fault events, and maintain a low false alarm rate. When dealing with concurrent faults, dynamic scheduling strategy can significantly reduce the total driving distance and time consumption, and further accelerate the speed of fault repair.

title: Machine Learning-Based Credit Scoring for Personalized and Inclusive Lending in Consumer-Centric Financial Systems
authors: K. Balaji, P.Seshagiri Rao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Computational Innovations and Sustainable Technologies (ICCIST)
abstract: Traditional approaches to credit-scoring are largely based on rule systems that can be excessively fixed and limited to the ability to reflect individual financial behavior. The article analyzes the effectiveness of machine-learning (ML)- based credit ranking with the hypothesis that they can improve predictive capability and fairness of consumer credit lending. The performance of these algorithms, including supervised methods of learning, e.g., logistic regression, random forests as well as the deep learning, is contrasted to the conventional credit models. Model transparency is provided by SHAP values and other methods explainable by AI. Findings show that practice based on the use of ML outperform traditional methods in risk assessment, especially, through the inclusion of supplementary forms of data in traditional databases based on transaction behavior, virtual footprints, and psychometric signals. Furthermore, ethical standards and moral confidence in ML informed credit decision-making will require regulation-proof and explanatory modelling. Through the research, it is recommended to implement policy measures intended to cause financial institutions, fintech companies, and regulating bodies to implement ML-based credit-scoring technologies, with fairness and predictive effectiveness being reciprocal drivers of financial access and consumer-friendly lending practices.

title: Research on Behavior Pattern Recognition and Interactive Regulation of Air Conditioning Based on Big Data Analysis
authors: Maomao Ding, Zhongwei Cai, Tianpeng Xia, Lu Liu, Junxiang Duan, Jiamei Yan
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: This paper studies the behavior pattern recognition and interactive regulation strategy of air conditioning based on big data analysis. Firstly, data preprocessing, including time stamp unification, outlier processing and feature extraction, lays the foundation for subsequent analysis. Then, K-means clustering algorithm is used to classify users' air-conditioning behavior, and four main behavior modes are identified: normal mode, energy-saving mode, comfort mode and fluctuation mode. Based on these modes, the corresponding interactive control strategies are studied and formulated, aiming at ensuring the comfort of users and realizing energy conservation and emission reduction. The experimental results show that the overall energy consumption is reduced by 15% after the control strategy is applied, and the energy consumption in energysaving mode is the most significant, reaching 20%. In addition, the control strategy also helps users in fluctuation mode to reduce frequent adjustments and improve the stability of use. This study proves the effectiveness of big data analysis in air conditioning behavior pattern recognition and interactive regulation, and provides new ideas and methods for energysaving control in commercial air conditioning field.

title: Investigation of Fade Mitigation Methods on Experimental FSO LEO-to-Ground Links
authors: Cuong T. Nguyen, Hoang D. Le, Dimitar R. Kolev, Alberto Carrasco-Casado, Toshihiro Kubo-Oka, Hiroyuki Tsuji, Morio Toyoshima, Anh T. Pham
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Space Optical Systems and Applications (ICSOS)
abstract: In recent years, the standardization of free-space optics (FSO)-based low-earth orbit (LEO) satellite communication systems has aroused growing interest. This is thanks to the ability to provide high data rates and global coverage of the optical satellite systems. However, one of the key challenges lies in selecting effective techniques to combat channel fading and improve system reliability. To address this, current standards consider forward error correction (FEC) codes and/or interleaving at the physical layer, along with automatic repeat request (ARQ) protocols at the link layer. This work aims to investigate and compare the performance of various fade mitigation strategies, which aligns with current standardization efforts. In particular, we conduct both information-theoretic analysis and simulation for key link-layer performance metrics, i.e., frame loss probability (FLP), goodput, and average system delay. Through this study, we provide insightful discussions and practical design guidelines that benefit the development of reliable optical satellite systems.

title: Towards Blind Restoration of Finger Vein Images: A Collaborative Framework for Mixed Degradation and Local Disruption
authors: Yingshuai Song, Huimin Lu
publish: 2025 5th International Symposium on Artificial Intelligence and Big Data (AIBDF)
abstract: Refers to the vein image the influence of mixed degradation and local structural damage problem, this paper proposes a collaborative DPENet repair network based on GAN. The dual-path mechanism of multi-scale semantic fusion was designed in the network. Through global dependency modeling and local structure weighting, the gated fusion was used to realize the collaborative enhancement of contrast and detail. At the same time, a channel-aware context reasoner is introduced to reconstruct the damaged texture that conforms to the vascular prior. Experiments on HKPU and SDUMLA-HMT datasets show that DPENet is superior to the mainstream methods in terms of PSNR and FID indicators, and the visual clarity and contrast of veins are significantly improved.

title: Model for Identifying Multiple Fault Types in Rotating Machinery Based on Data Fusion of Multi-Sensor Vibration Signals
authors: Chongnan Tang, Hailin Miao, Yahui Yu
publish: 2025 Asia Conference on Energy Conversion Systems and Power Electronics (AECSPE)
abstract: Rotating machinery is prone to multiple faults under complex working conditions, and the diagnostic accuracy of a single sensor is not good. Therefore, this article proposes a multi-fault identification model based on multi-sensor vibration signal data fusion. The model collects multichannel vibration signals by arranging acceleration sensors in different directions of the bearing seat, extracts time-frequency features by combining wavelet packet energy entropy with statistical features, and completes feature level fusion with principal component analysis (PCA). The results show that the average recognition accuracy of the proposed method is 93.7% and F1-score is 92.9% on the self-built experimental platform and CWRU public data set, which is obviously superior to traditional SVM, random forest and single depth model. In the environment of 5 dB noise interference, the F1-score of the model still remains 89.4%, showing good robustness. The research proves that multi-sensor fusion and deep learning collaborative modeling are effective in improving the accuracy of multi-fault identification of rotating machinery.

title: Dynamic Adaptation-Based Multi-Agent Emotional Regulation Mechanism in Public Opinion
authors: Qinglan Wei, Neng Rao, Xuebing Wen, Hongjiang Xiao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Embedded Systems, Mobile Communication and Computing (EMC²)
abstract: Emotion serves as the core driving force behind the evolution of public opinion. However, current public opinion governance faces critical challenges, including insufficient accuracy in mixed emotion recognition, obscure mechanisms of multi-path emotional evolution, and significant lags in intervention strategies. Taking the complex medical-education public opinion event ("PUMC 4+4") as an experimental paradigm, this study proposes a dynamic emotional regulation framework based on Multi-Agent Simulation (MAS). Technically, we first construct a domain-adaptive group emotion calculation model utilizing semantic decoupling and establish a multi-level propagation metric system based on Gestalt theory. Second, a digital twin society is built to simulate evolutionary paths under empathy-oriented, rationality-oriented, and achievement-oriented strategies. Validation against real-world Weibo data confirms the model's ecological fidelity (Pearson r > 0.8). Experimental results indicate a significant "Scale Effect": while empathy-oriented strategies effectively mitigate early-stage negative emotions in large-scale networks, rationality-oriented approaches are superior for long-term trust reconstruction in professional circles. This study transitions public opinion governance from static monitoring to dynamic, explainable decision support.

title: Rotor Stack Length Optimization of Variable Reluctance Resolvers to Mitigate Inclined Rotor Fault
authors: Davood Karamalian, Behrooz Majidi, Mohammadreza Moradian
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: Resolvers are essential components in close-loop control of electric motors which are widely used in electric vehicles, robotics, and aerospace applications. Among all types of resolvers, variable reluctance resolvers benefit from a robust configuration which makes them suitable in harsh, dusty and high-humid environments. However, their precision can be affected by mechanical faults such as rotor inclination. To mitigate the effects of this fault, this study investigates the optimization of rotor stack length to enhance resolver’s accuracy under this faulty condition. In this paper, first an overview of resolver operating principles is presented, followed by an analysis of rotor inclination fault and its origins. Then Finite Element Method (FEM) simulation is used to model the inclined rotor fault. The resolver's performance is then analyzed using MATLAB by varying this parameter to determine the optimal rotor stack length which can create minimal position error. A prototype resolver with optimized rotor length is prototyped and tested. The results indicate that increasing the rotor stack length by 1mm reduces position error up to 38.14%.

title: ECCST 2025 Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Communication, Computer Science and Technology (ECCST)

title: Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 Symposium on Microwave, Antenna, and Propagation (SMAP)

title: Reduced RF chains using Fixed Phase Shifters for Over-the-Air Computation
authors: Aravind Seshadri, Ajit K. Chaturvedi
abstract: As wireless networks scale to accommodate the large number of devices, over-the-air computation (Aircomp) has emerged as a promising solution. It enables efficient data aggregate estimation by using the waveform superposition property of the wireless medium. In practical communication systems, multiple antennas at the receiver can improve performance. However, the high cost and power consumption of radio frequency (RF) chains limit the use of one RF chain per antenna, prompting researchers to explore alternative architectures. These designs often face a trade-off between performance and power consumption. In this work, we propose an algorithm for a switch-phase shifter-based architecture. The algorithm approximates the solution of the fully digital system by using a two-vector decomposition method, enabling comparable performance with fewer RF chains. Further, this proposed approach allows us to analyze the impact of the number of RF chains and phase shifters on the system’s performance relative to the fully digital structure, offering valuable guidelines for efficient hardware design.

title: L-CLIPScore: a Lightweight Embedding-based Captioning Metric for Evaluating and Training_supp1-3665006.pdf
authors: xu yang
abstract: We propose a novel embedding-based captioning metric termed as L-CLIPScore that can be used for efficiently evaluating caption quality and training captioning model. L-CLIPScore is calculated from a lightweight CLIP (L-CLIP), which is a dual-encoder architecture compressed and distilled from CLIP. To compress, we apply two powerful techniques which are weight multiplexing and matrix decomposition for reducing the parameters of encoders and word embedding matrix, respectively. To distill, we design a novel multi-modal Similarity Regulator (SR) loss to transfer more vision-language alignment knowledge. Specifically, SR loss amplifies the multi-modal embedding similarity if the given image-text pair is matched and diminishes the similarity if the pair is non-matched. By compressing and distilling by this novel SR loss, our L-CLIP achieves comparable multi-modal alignment ability to the original CLIP while it requires fewer computation resources and running time. We carry out exhaustive experiments to validate the efficiency and effectiveness of L-CLIPScore when using it as the judge to evaluate caption quality. We also discover that when using L-CLIPScore as the supervisor to train the captioning model, it should be mixed up by an n-gram-based metric and meanwhile analyze why using L-CLIPScore only will cause fail training

title: Optimal Scheduling of Multiple Data Center Microgrids Considering the Spatiotemporal Equilibrium Allocation of Carbon Emission Allowance
authors: Chengling Hu, Changzheng Shao, Hanxin Zhang, Kaigui Xie
abstract: The optimal scheduling of data center microgrids (DCMGs) considering the coupled trading of energy and carbon emission allowance (CEA) has been extensively studied. While CEA is typically allocated and assessed on an annual basis, DCMGs' operation and energy trading are conducted on a daily schedule. This discrepancy in temporal scales poses challenges for aligning daily operational decisions with long-term carbon compliance goals. This paper investigates the optimal scheduling of multiple DCMGs, considering the spatiotemporal equilibrium allocation of CEA. First, a CEA spatiotemporal decomposition model is developed. Then, a Lyapunov optimization-based low carbon scheduling model is introduced, which decouples long term constraints and significantly reduces computational complexity while ensuring both annual carbon compliance and economic efficiency. Furthermore, a two-layer game-theoretic energy–carbon coupling trading model is presented to conduct collaborative optimization for multiple DCMGs. Finally, a test system is used to demonstrate the significant effects of emission reduction and the economic benefits of the proposed methods.

title: High-Resolution Optical Satellite Image Guided DEM Super-Resolution via Topographic-Aware Transformer
authors: Yubin Tang, Enping Yan, Yujiu Xiong, Jiawei Jiang, Hua Sun, Dengkui Mo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Digital elevation models (DEMs) are recognized as fundamental to numerous geospatial applications, yet the spatial resolution of widely available DEMs is often insufficient for fine-scale analysis. Deep learning-based super-resolution (SR) has been developed as a powerful technique for enhancing DEM resolution. However, challenges remain in modeling complex, long-range topographic dependencies and in effectively fusing guidance information from auxiliary data such as high-resolution(HR) optical imagery, which can result in the loss of fine details and the generation of geomorphologically inconsistent surfaces. In this study, an attention-transformer-guided terrain super-resolution (ATTSR) is proposed for high-fidelity, image-guided DEM super-resolution. The ATTSR framework is designed with a dual-branch Swin Transformer backbone to capture global spatial context, and a novel topographic-aware attention (TAA) module is introduced for the adaptive fusion of image and DEM features. Furthermore, a terrain gradient constraint is incorporated into a collaborative loss function to explicitly preserve topographic structure. The performance of ATTSR is evaluated on three diverse datasets for SR tasks from 30 m to 10 m and from 90 m to 30 m. Compared to state-of-the-art methods, the elevation root mean square error (RMSE) is reduced by 7%–36%, with corresponding improvements observed in slope and aspect fidelity. These results demonstrate that ATTSR provides a state-of-the-art solution for image-guided DEM SR, advancing the generation of accurate, large-scale HR terrain products.

title: Two-Phase Account Group Migration Service with Dynamic Load Awareness for Optimizing Sharding Blockchain
authors: Kaimin Zhang, Xingwei Wang, Bo Yi, Lin Qiu, Enliang Lv, Jingjing Guo, Lu Wang
abstract: Sharding, as a Layer-1 scaling technique, is widely recognized as a promising solution to address the scalability limitations faced by blockchains. However, distributing accounts across shards generates numerous cross-shard transactions (CSTs) and inter-shard workload imbalances, potentially compromising scalability. Existing state-of-the-art methods typically optimize transaction distribution for subsequent epochs by periodically reallocating accounts using graph partitioning or community detection to balance loads and minimize CSTs. Nevertheless, these methods often involve computationally expensive account migration and overlook real-world transaction skewness, exacerbating workload imbalances. To address the above problems, we propose a two-phase account group migration service with dynamic load awareness in this paper. This service can monitor and analyze system state to determine the necessity of account migration. Once triggered, it employs a two-phase algorithm that combines account grouping with global group-level re-partitioning to balance workloads and minimize CSTs. Additionally, group-level migration further helps reduce computational overhead. We evaluate the designed service by replaying large-scale real Ethereum transactions. Experimental results demonstrate that, compared with the baselines, our method can not only improves system throughput and reduces transaction confirmation latency, but also enhances overall scalability.

title: Multi-parameter Detection Technology for Temperature and Partial Discharge in Liquid-solid Composite Dielectrics based on DFB-FL
authors: Jixian Qiao, Chao Du, Weichao Zhang, Lisheng Zhong, Qibing Shao, Hong Zhao
abstract: Traditional fiber optic sensors used for operation and maintenance inspection of power equipment face challenges in achieving multi-parameter detection due to factors such as sensitivity, safety, detection range, reusability, and difficulty in spatial separation from the demodulation system. Distributed feedback fiber laser (DFB-FL) exhibit extremely high detection sensitivity to strain due to its ultra-narrow emission linewidth. Moreover, its small size, high safety, excellent reusability, and independent demodulation systems can effectively solve the aforementioned problems. Based on the ultimate frequency noise and acoustic pressure model, the minimum measurable acoustic pressure of DFB-FL in the partial discharge (PD) acoustic emission frequency band is derived. The relationship between the center wavelength of DFB-FL and external temperature changes is explored based on the temperature sensitivity characteristics of DFB-FL. A multi-parameter detection system for PD acoustic emission and temperature based on DFB-FL is developed. The results of 80 kVA oil-immersed transformer temperature and PD detection show that the developed DFB-FL multi-parameter detection system can be effectively applied to measure the top oil temperature and hot spot temperature of oil-immersed transformers. Meanwhile, under the same PD ultrasonic excitation source, the average maximum response SNR of the system is 13.71 dB higher than that of a piezoelectric transducer.

title: Design and Implementation of Secondary Authentication Algorithm for 5G IoT Devices Based on PUF and RedCap Technique
authors: Zhixiong Chen, Meiying Wang, Yong Li, Weiping Shao, Jinyi Qiu
abstract: Malicious terminals that illegally access the 5G power service network can steal or tamper with the sensitive information of the power system. However, research on how to ensure the identity legitimacy of lightweight 5G RedCap (Reduced Capability) terminals and further enhance the security of the 5G channel side in the power sector remains insufficient. To address this gap, this paper proposes a low-cost secondary authentication protocol based on Physical Unclonable Function (PUF) hardware chips. Firstly, a grid-oriented 5G security architecture and a system model for RedCap terminal authentication are established. Secondly, aiming at the challenge that resource-constrained terminals struggle to support traditional cryptographic protocols, a lightweight secondary authentication scheme and a confidential identity database scheme are proposed. These schemes are built on the PUF and a lightweight encryption algorithm, with the integration of "master-slave" dual PUF chips. Finally, the proposed schemes are analyzed and validated through security software evaluation and hardware terminal testing. The results demonstrate two key advantages: In terms of effectiveness, compared with similar schemes, the proposed scheme reduces computation costs by 75% to 80%, communication costs by 20% to 50%, and the cost of RedCap terminals by approximately 60%. In terms of reliability, besides resisting basic attacks, it also effectively defends against ESL (Ephemeral Secret Leakage) attacks, modeling attacks and stolen verifier attacks, among others.

title: All-in-One Image Restoration via Causal-Deconfounding Wavelet-Disentangled Prompt Network
authors: Bingnan Wang, Bin Qin, Jiangmeng Li, Fanjiang Xu, Fuchun Sun, Hui Xiong
abstract: Image restoration represents a promising approach for addressing the inherent defects of image content distortion. Standard image restoration approaches suffer from high storage cost and the requirement towards the known degradation pattern, including type and degree, which can barely be satisfied in dynamic practical scenarios. In contrast, all-in-one image restoration (AiOIR) eliminates multiple degradations within a unified model to circumvent the aforementioned issues. However, according to our causal analysis, we disclose that two significant defects still exacerbate the effectiveness and generalization of AiOIR models: 1) the spurious correlation between non-degradation semantic features and degradation patterns; 2) the biased estimation of degradation patterns. To obtain the true causation between degraded images and restored images, we propose Causal-deconfounding Wavelet-disentangled Prompt Network (CWP-Net) to perform effective AiOIR. CWP-Net introduces two modules for decoupling, i.e., wavelet attention module of encoder and wavelet attention module of decoder. These modules explicitly disentangle the degradation and semantic features to tackle the issue of spurious correlation. To address the issue stemming from the biased estimation of degradation patterns, CWP-Net leverages a wavelet prompt block to generate the alternative variable for causal deconfounding. Extensive experiments on two all-in-one settings prove the effectiveness and superior performance of our proposed CWP-Net over the state-of-the-art AiOIR methods.

title: HFP-SAM: Hierarchical Frequency Prompted SAM for Efficient Marine Animal Segmentation
authors: Pingping Zhang, Tianyu Yan, Yuhao Wang, Yang Liu, Tongdan Tang, Yili Ma, Long Lv, Feng Tian, Weibing Sun, Huchuan Lu
abstract: Marine Animal Segmentation (MAS) aims at identifying and segmenting marine animals from complex marine environments. Most of previous deep learning-based MAS methods struggle with the long-distance modeling issue. Recently, Segment Anything Model (SAM) has gained popularity in general image segmentation. However, it lacks of perceiving fine-grained details and frequency information. To this end, we propose a novel learning framework, named Hierarchical Frequency Prompted SAM (HFP-SAM) for high-performance MAS. First, we design a Frequency Guided Adapter (FGA) to efficiently inject marine scene information into the frozen SAM backbone through frequency domain prior masks. Additionally, we introduce a Frequency-aware Point Selection (FPS) to generate highlighted regions through frequency analysis. These regions are combined with the coarse predictions of SAM to generate point prompts and integrate into SAM’s decoder for fine predictions. Finally, to obtain comprehensive segmentation masks, we introduce a Full-View Mamba (FVM) to efficiently extract spatial and channel contextual information with linear computational complexity. Extensive experiments on four public datasets demonstrate the superior performance of our approach. We will make our code publicly available upon the acceptance.

title: DepMatch: Boosting Semi-supervised Semantic Segmentation by Exploring Depth Difference Knowledge
authors: Jianjian Yin, Xiruo Jiang, Tao Chen, Gensheng Pei, Yazhou Yao, Fumin Shen, Heng-Tao Shen
abstract: Existing semi-supervised semantic segmentation (SSS) methods fail to explore the potential of depth information in unlabeled data, as they suffer from i) inter-class depth similarity, and ii) intra-class depth discrepancy. To address these challenges, this paper proposes DepMatch, a simple yet effective approach that leverages depth difference knowledge to guide consistency learning. Specifically, a Class-wise Depth Disparity Perception (CDDP) module is designed to exploit depth difference information, driven by class prediction priors, facilitating robust feature learning. Depth-feature discrepancy set is first constructed and then reliable pixel pairs are selected for inter-class depth disparity knowledge distillation. Simultaneously, exponential normalization is applied to intra-category depth disparity for suppressing large outlier variations, and an entropy-based adaptive weight is derived to prioritize feature learning of high entropy areas. Moreover, we propose the Uncertain Logit Disparity Regulation (ULDR) module, which leverages the depth variations at class boundaries to promote the mutual regulation of uncertain pixel logit information, enhancing the model’s spatial understanding. Experiments on five public benchmarks show that DepMatch can be seamlessly incorporated as a plug-and-play plugin into popular SSS frameworks, achieving significant performance improvements across various visual encoders. The source code and models are made available at https://github.com/NUST-Machine-Intelligence-Laboratory/DepMatch.

title: PSAvatar: A Point-Based Shape Model for Real-Time Head Avatar Animation With 3D Gaussian Splatting_supp1-3676544.mp4
authors: KANGLIN LIU
abstract: Despite much progress, achieving real-time highfidelity head avatar animation is still difficult and existing methods have to trade-off between speed and quality. 3DMM based methods often fail to model non-facial structures such as eyeglasses and hairstyles, while neural implicit models suffer from deformation inflexibility and rendering inefficiency. Although 3D Gaussian has been demonstrated to possess promising capability for geometry representation and radiance field reconstruction, applying 3D Gaussian in head avatar creation remains a major challenge since it is difficult for 3D Gaussian to model the head shape variations caused by changing poses and expressions. In this paper, we introduce PSAvatar, a novel framework for animatable head avatar creation that utilizes discrete geometric primitive to create a parametric shape model and employs 3D Gaussian for fine detail representation and high fidelity rendering. The parametric shape model is a Point-based Shape Model (PSM) which uses points instead of meshes for 3D representation to achieve enhanced representation flexibility. Specifically, PSM first converts the FLAME mesh to points by sampling on the surfaces as well as off the meshes to enable the reconstruction of not only surface-like structures but also complex geometries such as eyeglasses and hairstyles. By aligning these points with the head shape in an analysis-by-synthesis manner, the PSM makes it possible to utilize 3D Gaussian for fine detail representation and appearance modeling, thus enabling the creation of high-fidelity avatars. We show that PSAvatar can reconstruct high-fidelity head avatars of varieties of subjects and the avatars can be animated in real-time.

title: Diagnosis of Rotor Asymmetries in Inverter-fed Wound Rotor Induction Machines via PSH Analysis of Start-up Vibration
authors: Jose E. Ruiz-Sarrio, Pedro Llovera-Segovia, Carlos Madariaga-Cifuentes, Angela Navarro-Navarro, Vicente Biot-Monterde, Jose A. Antonino-Daviu
abstract: Wound Rotor Induction Machines (WRIMs) are widely utilized in industrial applications with high starting torque. The rotor health of such machines influences the machine efficiency and ultimately, the overall safety and reliability. The monitoring of the housing vibrations represent a highly available manner of holistic diagnosis. Nevertheless, the evaluation of rotor health through vibration signals primarily focuses on steady-state operation and has received limited investigation when compared with electrical signature analysis. The present paper introduces a novel technique to diagnose rotor winding asymmetries in WRIMs by analysing the transient evolutions of Principal Slot Harmonic (PSH) families. Particularly, an inverterfed start-up is analysed, which poses increased challenges for rotor defect diagnosis when compared to the line-fed scenario. The methodology exploits the resonance phenomena that arises from the interactions of time evolving forces and structural frequencies. The automatically extracted energy profile of the objective components provides adequate diagnosis and severity identification of three different asymmetry levels at rated load conditions.

title: Integrated Simulation of an E-Bike Powertrain and Vehicle Dynamics Using MATLAB/Simulink
authors: Danial Gharibi, Mojtaba Mirsalim
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electrical Machines and Drives (ICEMD)
abstract: This paper presents the design and simulation of an electric bicycle system developed in the MATLAB/Simulink environment. The proposed system integrates electrical, mechanical, and control subsystems in a modular framework, enabling detailed analysis of their interactions. A cyclist model is incorporated to realistically represent human limitations in torque and power delivery, allowing accurate assessment of rider-vehicle dynamics. The modeling procedure uses built-in Simulink components such as Vehicle Body Total Road Load, along with custom-developed blocks to capture braking behavior and cyclist contributions. To validate the system, several test scenarios were implemented, including acceleration, maximum speed analysis, hill-climbing on graded slopes, and evaluation under the JPN10 drive cycle. These outcomes highlight the modular Simulink model’s capability to provide a reliable and flexible platform for analyzing electric bicycle performance and optimizing system design.

title: Scalar Sign Function-Based NFTSMC for 2-DOF Robotic Arms
authors: Lotfi Chaouech, Moez SOLTANI, Abdelkader CHAARI
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Control, Decision and Information Technologies (CoDIT)
abstract: This paper develops a novel Non-Singular Fast Terminal Sliding Mode Control (NFTSMC) scheme for twolink robotic manipulators using scalar sign function formulation. The proposed controller simultaneously addresses three critical challenges in robotic control: singularity prevention through carefully designed sliding surface exponents, complete chattering elimination via the mathematical properties of the scalar sign function, and guaranteed finite-time convergence despite dynamic uncertainties and external disturbances. The control architecture combines equivalent dynamics compensation, robust switching terms, and stabilization components, all implemented through the scalar sign function framework. Rigorous Lyapunov stability analysis establishes finite-time convergence properties, with the scalar sign function providing exact discontinuity approximation without compromising theoretical guarantees. Simulation studies on a 2-DOF robotic arm demonstrate the controller's effectiveness in achieving precise trajectory tracking while maintaining smooth control signals. The approach offers significant advantages in computational efficiency and implementation simplicity compared to conventional sliding mode controllers, making it particularly suitable for real-time robotic applications requiring both high precision and robustness.

title: Machine Learning Model for Estimating Building Material and Labor Costs with Integrated Chatbot Support
authors: Vignesh K, B. Baranitharan, Rishikesh Sourabh, Abhijeet Kumar, Gaurav Kumar, Ayush Kumar Verma
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI-Driven Smart Systems and Ubiquitous Computing (ICAUC)
abstract: Accurate construction cost estimates continue to be a pressing concern within the construction industry. In regions such as India where the cost of materials and rate of labor vary significantly from location, this issue becomes increasingly more difficult to navigate. The implementation of a real-time Machine Learning-based system developed by research team will allow the construction industry to gain access to an accurate estimate of building costs based on selected geographical location and structural design components. The application of the CatBoost and XGBoost regression algorithms allow the trained Machine Learning model to identify potential complexities between building metrics and environmental influences. The FastAPI backend operates with a React.js frontend that requires an authenticated user using Firebase to access the real-time dashboard that receives inputs from the user and produces an instant forecast report. Result demonstrated that using this approach produces high levels of accuracy when predicting construction costs across the total number of cities vetted in research, and that the overall performance metrics reflect improved reliability. This approach improves the accuracy of determining budgets for future construction projects for both contractors and architects.
